Observe how around 100,000 lambs are referred to twice in the text as ["it."]{.class_s4N2-0} Again, we are struck with the remarkable foresight of Moses. Could he have foreseen Matthew 27:25 (["His blood be upon us and upon our children"]{.class_s4N2-0})?[ ]{.class_s4N2-0}Could he have known that darkness would come prematurely at the death of the Lamb, and that before six p.m. (a Jewish "evening"), the sun's light would fail? Why didn't he tell them to cut the head off this lamb? Every lamb for the next 1,000 years is decapitated (Lev. 1:8, 12). Could Moses have known that ["a bone of him shall not be broken"]{.class_s4N2-0}? (John 19:36; Psa. 34:20).

(The corrupt [Septuagint]{.class_s4N4-0}---with the Samaritan text---destroys the cross-reference to Israel by writing "the sons of Israel" in the passage. This was done also in verse 3.)

Lightfoot and Clarke alter the text to read "between the evenings" in an effort to make it match Calvary, but again, the "help" is useless. We got the sense without altering anything.

["And they shall take of the blood\...the two side posts and on the upper door post"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 7). Here sits another one of the most astounding texts in the entire Bible, and one of the greatest proofs of inspiration possible. And what do the "great Conservative scholars" and "godly translators" say about the text? [Nothing. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Absolutely nothing.

Out of Keil, Delitzsch, Bantzsch, Ellicott, Dummelow, Schaff, Clarke, Bullinger, Lange, Williams, Johnson, Bruce, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown we find: "there was a lattice work over the door"; there wasn't any blood "on the threshold," etc. Two of them change the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}[]{#c1BJ.xhtml_page_240 .class-1}text, one of them makes a reference to an Apocryphal book, and two of them play "hooky" and skip the verse with[ no comment at all.]{.class_s4N4-0}

But what have we here? Blood in [three ]{.class_s4N4-0}places?! One thief over [here ]{.class_s4N4-0}and another over [there]{.class_s4N4-0} and Someone in the middle who is HIGHER than the transgressors on either side of Him? How did Moses know that the sinless One would be on the [middle ]{.class_s4N4-0}cross? Or how would he have known there would be any "middle cross"? Or for that matter, why not blood around the WINDOW? Why not two spots of blood on either side of the door and none OVER it? Why not [one ]{.class_s4N4-0}over it?

No, we cannot run from the text; we must face it. Whoever gave Moses his instructions (see vs. 1) knew something about ["the Lamb of God, which taketh away the sin of the world"]{.class_s4N2-0} (John 1:29)[ ]{.class_s4N2-0}that no man could have guessed by intuition, grasped by mental telepathy, or hit upon by accidental guesswork. [Moses had a revelation from God,]{.class_s4N4-0} exactly as John (Rev. 1:1--2) and Paul (Gal. 1:10--12) and Peter (Matt. 16:17) and David (2 Sam. 23:2). (See the 152+/- "coincidences" that mark the life of Joseph under Gen. 37--50, in the [Commentary on Genesis.]{.class_s4N4-0})

["And they shall eat the flesh in that night, roast with fire"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 8). This last statement is reinforced with:[ "Eat not of it raw, nor sodden at all with water, but roast with fire"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 9). What normal man, in his right mind, would think that Moses had enough foresight to anticipate the Lamb's cry ["I thirst"]{.class_s4N2-0} (John 19:28) apart from a direct revelation of God Almighty Himself? Furthermore, an "accident" occurs in the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}text which reveals the fingerprints of an omnipotent hand on the passage, which has still been preserved in the []{#c1BJ.xhtml_page_241 .class-1}[AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1611) in spite of the amateurish bungling of such confused "Bibles" as the [NASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(any edition). Note!

1\. To be redeemed and spared from God's wrath, one must have ["a lamb" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(note the indefinite article in vs. 3---["a lamb"]{.class_s4N2-0}).

2\. But not just[ any lamb]{.class_s4N4-0} will do; one must receive ["the lamb" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(see the definite article in vs. 4, [which the corrupt ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}\[1901\] [has erased)]{.class_s4N4-0}.

3\. But not even ["the"]{.class_s4N2-0} Lamb of God can avail the sinner unless he personally appropriates it for himself. Hence, ["your lamb" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 5) narrows the application of salvation to personal reception of a personal Lamb.

A lamb, THE lamb, YOUR lamb!

Ah, the unsearchable riches of the[ King James ]{.class_s4N4-0}Bible! How infallible are its translations, and its manuscripts past finding out. This remarkable translation of the articles (and possessives) will not even be found in the Masoretic text; it is an advanced revelation given by the Holy Spirit in the English text of 1611, and later removed in the corrupt perversions of the [RV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1885), [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1901), [NASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1963), [RSV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1952), [New English Bible ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1970), [NRSV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1980), and similar imposters. (The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} has missed all of the spiritual applications in vss. 1--9; this is quite characteristic of writers and teachers who are always talking about "looking beyond the [literal word ]{.class_s4N4-0}to grasp the great[ spiritual truths ]{.class_s4N4-0}of the Scripture." Most of them couldn't find a bowling ball in a bath tub.)

However, our typology keeps on running too close to allow the scholars (Philip Schaff---[Conservative,]{.class_s4N4-0} and Dean Luther Weigle---[Liberal]{.class_s4N4-0}) to play games. Christ has no water on the cross (vss. 8--9), and furthermore, He []{#c1BJ.xhtml_page_242}suffers the eternal punishment of FIRE in the place of the sinner (Matt. 27:46). Throughout the Bible, from cover to cover, the wrath of God (which fell on the sacrifices) is described as being FIRE: see Numbers 11:1--3; Psalm 89:46; Jeremiah 4:4; Isaiah 30:27; Matthew 3:12; Ezekiel 20:47; and any other 200 places. (Note Zech. 9:11 for a real picture of it.)

["His head with his legs, and with the purtenance thereof" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 9). This is NOT the manner of the regular burnt offering prescribed in Leviticus; furthermore, this is not called a "burnt offering." It is said to be ["the sacrifice of the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord]{.class_s4N9-0}['S passover"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 27), and it is not offered by a priest or a priest class. It is INDIVIDUAL, as salvation is an individual matter.

["And ye shall let nothing of it remain until the morning" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 10). The lamb is referred to by the singular (["it"]{.class_s4N2-0}) seven times in these first eleven verses, even though nearly 100,000 lambs are being slain to feed over 1,500,000 people. Students of dispensations will note that in the Millennial morning, with Christ on earth, "the blood of the Lamb" is no longer applied to sinners, nor is any mention made of "believing on Christ" in order to be saved (Matt. 5--7 with comments in the [Commentary on Matthew]{.class_s4N4-0}). There is blood shed in the Millennium, but it is [not ]{.class_s4N4-0}the blood of the Lamb of God.

The unleavened bread (vs. 8) speaks of separation from impurity, and the bitter herbs are a reminder of the 215 years spent in Egypt (or 400, depending upon the timetable adopted).

12:11 "And thus shall ye eat it; with your loins girded, your shoes on your feet, and your staff in your []{#c1BJ.xhtml_page_243}hand; and ye shall eat it in haste: it is the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}'S passover.

12 For I will pass through the land of Egypt this night, and will smite all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, both man and beast; and against all the gods of Egypt I will execute judgment: I am the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}.

13 And the blood shall be to you for a token upon the houses where ye are: and when I see the blood, I will pass over you, and the plague shall not be upon you to destroy you, when I smite the land of Egypt.

14 And this day shall be unto you for a memorial; and ye shall keep it a feast to the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}throughout your generations; ye shall keep it a feast by an ordinance for ever.

15 Seven days shall ye eat unleavened bread; even the first day ye shall put away leaven out of your houses: for whosoever eateth leavened bread from the first day until the seventh day, that soul shall be cut off from Israel.

16 And in the first day there shall be an holy convocation, and in the seventh day there shall be an holy convocation to you; no manner of work shall be done in them, save that which every man must eat, that only may be done of you.

17 And ye shall observe the feast of unleavened bread; for in this selfsame day have I brought your armies out of the land of Egypt: therefore shall ye observe this day in your generations by an ordinance for ever."

The Bible readers should observe that the ["firstborn"]{.class_s4N2-0} is consigned to death regardless of his moral principles or conduct (Rom. 6:23). He is a perfect []{#c1BJ.xhtml_page_244}picture of the human race, born ["dead in trespasses and sins" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(Eph. 2:1--3), and the firstborn of the [Hebrews ]{.class_s4N4-0}is going to die just like the firstborn of the [Egyptians]{.class_s4N4-0} if the blood is not applied (1 John 2:1--2). Any Egyptian could have followed Abel's example; all that we can say about the Egyptians who lost their children this night is: ["to him that knoweth to do good, and doeth it not, to him it is sin" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(James 4:17). By a strange coincidence (unobserved by Keil, Delitzsch, Lange, Clarke, Henry, Williams, Bullinger, Dummelow, Pink, Bantzsch, and the other 300 commentators), the word ["firstborn"]{.class_s4N2-0} occurs exactly FOUR times in each verse in 11:5, again in 12:29, and again in 13:15. (On the last reference, [Young's Concordance]{.class_s4N4-0} has failed, pg. 352.) There are FOUR "firstborns" in the Scripture.

1\. The oldest son in a family (Gen. 10:15, 41:51, 48:14, 18).

2\. The Lord Jesus Christ, by Divine birth and human birth (Luke 2:7; Matt. 1:25; Col. 1:15, 18; Rom. 8:29).

3\. Israel in Egypt (Exod. 4:22--23).

4\. ["The firstborn of death"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Job 18:13).

Everything about verse 11 breathes of urgency. ["Loins girded,"]{.class_s4N2-0} as the soldier of Ephesians 6:10--14. The man is ready for action with the robe tucked under his girdle (1 Kings 18:46). ["Your shoes on your feet,"]{.class_s4N2-0} as the soldier of Ephesians chapter 6 is ready to bear ["the gospel of peace"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rom. 10:15) to any place (Eph. 6:15). ["Your staff in your hand," ]{.class_s4N2-0}as Jacob took it with him in his flight (Gen. 32:10). ["Ye shall eat it in haste" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(cf. Isa. 28:16), and you cannot eat it too quickly. It is a figure of salvation: the sooner you apply the blood, the better, and the sooner you leave Egypt, the better.

Neither Bullinger, Williams, Dummelow, Clarke, nor Johnson are able to find any [spiritual application]{.class_s4N4-0} in the passage; if the reader is looking for spiritual truth, he will be wasting his time examining them. When it comes to [criticizing the]{.class_s4N4-0} [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}text and altering it to suit their individual fancies (or the fancies of their teachers), these men are very adept; however, when it comes to finding the [correct doctrinal application,]{.class_s4N4-0} the[ spiritual lesson taught,]{.class_s4N4-0} and the [exact intention]{.class_s4N4-0} of the Holy Spirit, these men---as 90% of their kin in any age---are bankrupt. (Note the remarks on the "mining operations" under 6:3.)

["For I will pass through the land of Egypt\...and against all the gods of Egypt I will execute judgment" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 12). The Lord has already demonstrated His power to deal with ["the gods of Egypt"]{.class_s4N2-0} (see chaps. 6--11); however, this final blow hits not only cattle (as in 9:6 and 9:25), but frogs, cats, dogs, sheep, pigs, horses, camels, snakes, birds, bees, flies, crocodiles, mice, and [human beings. ]{.class_s4N4-0}If a family is childless and has no ["firstborn,"]{.class_s4N2-0} they usually have a pet, and if this pet is the firstborn of some litter (or if this pet has a litter), the family observes that one of the animals drops dead (vs. 29). Those who worshipped [Sobek ]{.class_s4N4-0}panicked when they saw dead crocodiles floating downstream; those who worshipped [Pasht ]{.class_s4N4-0}had a fit when their pet cat died; those who worshipped [Taouris ]{.class_s4N4-0}were deeply disturbed when the hippopotami in the river keeled over; those who worshipped [Horus ]{.class_s4N4-0}nearly had a heart attack when the lions and falcons began to check out; and those who worshipped [Kneph ]{.class_s4N4-0}mourned with weeping and wailing when their lambs croaked (lambs are supposed to "bleat," not [croak! ]{.class_s4N4-0}).

Dummelow---who believes all of the "fundamentals"---leaps verse 13 like he thought it wasn't in the "originals," and the [Wycliffe ]{.class_s4N4-0}commentator follows suit. Presented with the greatest passage in the Old Testament on salvation by blood, Dr. Johnson and Dr. Dummelow seem to contract some rare disease which paralyzes their pens. It strongly resembles the disease which afflicted 100% of the commentators when they ran into Rebekah's views on "mixed marriages" (see Gen. 27:46, and comments in the [Commentary on Genesis]{.class_s4N4-0}).

Where these "lapses in spirituality" occur, the Lord has usually placed something delightful for the real Bible believer to enjoy. This time, he is referred to the [Wycliffe Commentary,]{.class_s4N4-0} which has just (two verses before) explained that the [LXX "pascha"]{.class_s4N4-0} probably meant "to leap over." (So said, so done!) Dr. Johnson "leaps over" verse 13 like it was something from [Bel and the Dragon. ]{.class_s4N4-0}To make sure he does a real ["LXX pascha" ]{.class_s4N4-0}on this broad jump, he also clears verse 14. [But how could any "spiritual'' commentator afford to overlook verse 13?]{.class_s4N4-0} (That is the never-ending theme song of these "commentators" who alter the [King James ]{.class_s4N4-0}text: i.e., "the final authority is not the Bible but the 'living Word,' the Lord Jesus who\...etc., etc." That is, the Bible to them is a DEAD word.)

Now, the Passover is a ["token"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 13), ["a memorial"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 14), ["a feast"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 14), ["an ordinance"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 17), ["an holy convocation"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 16), a divine ["service"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 26), and a ["sacrifice"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 27)..

["And the blood shall be to you for a token\...and when I see the blood, I will pass over you\...when I smite the land of Egypt" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 13). You might have known the "13" would cause a panic. The verse has []{#c1BJ.xhtml_page_247}almost as many readings as there are denominations. The reason for this is quite apparent to any Christian (in any age) who is trusting the shed blood of Christ for his redemption (Acts 20:28; Col. 1:14; Rom. 5:9--10).

At the present time, there are two religions: [God's]{.class_s4N4-0} and [man's. ]{.class_s4N4-0}New Testament salvation is an operation originating in the mind of God (Heb. 12:2) and coming from God [downward ]{.class_s4N4-0}to man by supernatural revelation (Gal. 1:11--12). This revelation is that no "religion" can save any man and that salvation is from [God: ]{.class_s4N4-0}it is bound up in the finished work which God has done for man (Heb. 10:8--12). This salvation can only be accepted by man as a [free gift]{.class_s4N4-0} coming from God (Rom. 3:24), and it is given graciously and [freely ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Rom. 6:23) without any strings attached (Eph. 2:8--9). Its recipient cannot [earn ]{.class_s4N4-0}it (Eph. 2:8--9); he cannot [work for it]{.class_s4N4-0} (Rom. 4:5); he cannot [beg, borrow, ]{.class_s4N4-0}or [steal ]{.class_s4N4-0}it (Psa. 51:1--17); and he cannot [pay for it]{.class_s4N4-0} after he gets it (Rom. 11:35--36). Furthermore, he cannot[ take credit]{.class_s4N4-0} for any part of it (1 Cor. 1:30--31). This defines man as a bankrupt beggar who is totally disabled and unfit for work; man is in the condition of a dying leper with an incurable disease (Rom. 3:10--20).

[This is the picture which unregenerate mankind instinctively rejects. ]{.class_s4N4-0}They want their photograph taken by Charles Darwin or John Dewey---not Paul. They want a portrait painted by Charles Dickens or Bertrand Russell---not one painted by Peter or Matthew. They want the pencil sketch of Sigmund Freud or Karl Marx---not the Leica reproduction of James or John. Apart from the grace of God, man will not, and cannot, "tell it like it is," or even HEAR it like it is. The unregenerate, Hell-bound Popes, Priests, ambassadors, therapists, []{#c1BJ.xhtml_page_248}healers, civil rights workers, Cardinals, philosophers, artists, surgeons, historians, musicians, Presidents, Communists, Socialists, Kings, and psychiatrists of any age will automatically accept Matthew chapters 5--7 before Matthew chapter 23, John chapter 10 before John chapter 8, 1 Corinthians chapter 13 ahead of Romans chapter 1, James chapter 2 ahead of Revelation chapter 17, and "Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs" instead Isaiah chapter 53.

Liberals, Conservatives, Catholics, atheists, Fundamentalists, and agnostics may differ in the[ amount ]{.class_s4N4-0}of the Bible they reject; but 90% of the Fundamental scholars, in any age, will reject some of the New Testament. Liberals and agnostics go further and reject most of the New and Old Testaments, and atheists will only quote one or two verses in the whole Bible that have the [appearance ]{.class_s4N4-0}of denying the rest of the Bible: Marx---Acts 4:32, George Bernard Shaw---Micah 6:7, Tom Paine ---Matthew 7:12, etc.

But the great mass of humanity will instinctively reject [salvation by grace, ]{.class_s4N4-0}even where they profess to believe the Bible is the word of God. There is something so terribly humiliating about taking the place of "blind Bartimaeus" that the average man will simply not take it. Our text does not say, "When I see that you live in the right end of town and pay a real estate tax of \$5,000.00 a year, I will pass over." It does not say, "When I see that you are living a clean life and are a respected citizen of the community in which you live, I will pass over." The verse does not say, "When I see that you joined the church which Christ founded and are faithfully partaking of the sacraments, I will pass over." The verse does not say, "When I see that you have repented, confessed, []{#c1BJ.xhtml_page_249}believed, and have been baptized (like Alexander Campbell), I will pass over." Furthermore, the verse does not say, "When I see your proficiency in the original languages and your desire to make the Bible easier to understand, I will pass over." And it certainly did not say, "When I see that you have the initial evidence of the baptism of the Holy Ghost and believe the 'whole Bible,' I will pass over."

What the text says (in the "original"!) is: ["when I see the blood, I will pass over you" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 13). [Any other reading, writing, spelling, interpretation, translation, version, or text is a fatuity.]{.class_s4N4-0}

"Have you been to Jesus for the cleansing power? Are you washed in THE BLOOD of the Lamb?" "What can wash away my sins? Nothing but the BLOOD of Jesus!"

How does a "spiritual Conservative" skip a text like that one?

Simon Peter understood the text (1 Pet. 1:19). He called the blood ["precious"]{.class_s4N2-0} because it [redeems ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Col. 1:14), it [cleanses ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1 John 1:7), it [purges ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Heb. 9: 14), it [atones ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Rom. 5:11), it [justifies ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Rom. 5:9), and it [saves ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Rom. 5:10). Death cannot come to the child of God in the normal sense (John 11:25), for death has already visited (Rom. 6:4--8). The firstborn in Egypt, under the sheltering blood, could not die, for death had [already visited the home]{.class_s4N4-0} when the helpless lamb bled its life out into a bucket (vss. 21--22).

["When I see the blood" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 13). Never mind your pedigree. Never mind your sex, age, color, station in life, degrees, background, upbringing, talents, education, religion, sacraments, traditions, beliefs, motives, intentions, or ideals! ["When I see the blood"]{.class_s4N2-0}! ["The life of all flesh is the blood" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(Lev. 17:14), and God []{#c1BJ.xhtml_page_250}will no more accept your "religion" or your Scriptural knowledge than He accepted Cain's fruit stand (see Gen. 4:1--7 in the [Commentary on Genesis]{.class_s4N4-0}).

"Tickets, please!"

"I don't have a ticket."

"Then you cannot board the DC-7."

"But I'm wellknown here! My credit rating is A-1, and I've served on the city council a half dozen times."

"Do you have a[ ticket?"]{.class_s4N4-0}

"No, but I'm a member of the First Presbyterian Church, and I was director for the United Drive last year. I'm a 32nd degree Mason and\...."

"When I see your ticket, you can board."

"But my good man, I'm J. P. Smith of Smith, Smith, Smith, and Smith. My grandfather found--ed the National Association for the Advancement of Poor White Trash, and I've donated more than \$500,000 to the charitable cause of the\...."

["No ticket,]{.class_s4N4-0} no trip."

"Why, I'm better than three-fourths of these passengers you're letting on board. That man right there is a dirty lowdown crook, and that woman there is unfit to raise children, and that sailor is smoking, and that Jew is selling suits downtown that wouldn't make good cheesecloth, and\...."

"When I see your [ticket, ]{.class_s4N4-0}you can pass over."

"What do you mean? Do you realize to whom you are talking? 'J. P. Smith,' I said! My wife is president of the Garden Club, and my son is vice-president of the yacht club, and according to Dunn and Bradstreet, I'm worth more than\...."

"When I see your TICKET, you get on board, and until you get a ticket, stand aside and let these passengers board. We're taking off in eight minutes!"

Now tell me something, friend. How is it that you understand [that ]{.class_s4N4-0}conversation perfectly, and yet when it comes to [salvation, ]{.class_s4N4-0}you think your own opinion is a[ sane one?]{.class_s4N4-0} No ticket, no trip. [No blood, no salvation. ]{.class_s4N4-0}The conductor or ticket-taker (or stewardess or flight agent) is no more interested in your character, sex, color, background, motives, intentions, ideals, traditions, beliefs, sacraments, and education than Cassius Clay would be interested in a field manual on [The Tactics of the Rifle Platoon in the Attack.]{.class_s4N4-0}

Imagine any Christian writing a commentary on Exodus and failing to [comment ]{.class_s4N4-0}on Exodus 12:13.

Observe, further, that no one eats the blood; it is placed over the door. They eat BREAD (vss. 15, 17), while the blood-drinking Egyptians (cf. Rev. 11:6, 16:6) are smitten down in their houses (vss. 29--30). (See comments under Matt. 26:26--29 in the [Commentary on Matthew]{.class_s4N4-0}.)

["Ye shall keep it a feast by an ordinance for ever"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 14). This is the death knell for Israel as well as her deliverance, for the commandment forces Israel (fifteen centuries later) either to accept the Lamb of God (Heb. 13:10--14) or else to violate their own commandments. There are not a dozen cases on record in the history of Israel where individual Jewish families slew a lamb on the Passover and shared it in their houses with a neighbor; at least not [after ]{.class_s4N4-0}the time of Christ. The Passover becomes a Temple function before the time of Christ, and with no Temple in which to worship (Dan. 1--10), there is no chance to observe the regulations given []{#c1BJ.xhtml_page_252}above (see Hos. 3:4). With the desecration of the Holy of Holies (by the Lord first---Matt. 27:51) by Titus (A.D. 70), the sprinkled blood of lambs ceases to have significance, and Israel is compelled to accept the Lamb of Isaiah chapter 53 or abandon their own ordinances given to them from Heaven (Rom. 3:1--4). Check any synagogue in America within 200 miles of where you live and see if the Passover is kept after the manner of Exodus chapter 12. And when the Rabbi at that synagogue starts to spin the web of Talmudic tradition, ask him what these inspired words mean in his own Bible:[ "ye shall keep it a feast by an ordinance for ever"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 14).

["Seven days shall ye eat unleavened bread\...whosoever eateth leavened bread\...that soul shall be cut off from Israel" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 15). Leaven plainly stands for moral or doctrinal corruption (1 Cor. 5:6--7; Matt. 16:12). The feast begins on the fifteenth of Abib (or Nisan) and runs seven days. The first day is here instituted as a holy day BEFORE the Sabbath. The reader will observe that in spite of the anti-Christian literature published by the Seventh-day Adventists, the FIRST day of the week is instituted as a day of "rest" (see vs. 16) [before ]{.class_s4N4-0}the Sabbath is revealed at Sinai (Neh. 9:14; Ezek. 20:12). The soul that is ["cut off"]{.class_s4N2-0} is excommunicated from the assembly (Matt. 18:17). The expression is used this way 154 times in the Old Testament. It refers to physical death most of the time, but "souls" being ["cut off"]{.class_s4N2-0} are rare outside of the Pentateuch (see Gen. 17:14; Exod. 12:15; Lev. 7:25; Num. 9:13, for example.

["And in the seventh day there shall be an holy convocation" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 16). This is the first mention of [an assembly]{.class_s4N4-0} gathered together for purposes of worship. Up until here, worship has been a [family affair ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Gen. 8:20--[]{#c1BJ.xhtml_page_253}21) or an [individual affair ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Gen. 4:3--6) or, at the most, a[ household affair]{.class_s4N4-0} (Gen. 18:19). Now, over 1,500,000 people are called to observe [a special day for a special purpose ]{.class_s4N4-0}(see Col. 2:16 and comments in the [Commentary on Galatians---Colossians]{.class_s4N4-0}), and this is the first time this has happened to the descendants of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. (The week period of seven days was first used by the Babylonians and seems to have been unknown to the Egyptians. It was known to Jacob \[see Gen. 29:27--28\], and of course, the use of seven is the standard unit of measurement used in all of God's dealings with men; see the extensive treatment in [The Seven Sevens).]{.class_s4N4-0}

All of the Catholic Popes, Cardinals, Priests, Nuncios, Monks, Nuns, and laymen (from A.D. 325 to the present) stumble at the text. Failing to notice that the day following the Passover was a "Sabbath" (cf. Lev. 23:39; Num. 28:17--18; Exod. 29:35; Acts 20:7), all of the Catholics from Augustine to Teddy Kennedy misread the ["sabbath"]{.class_s4N2-0} of John 19:31 and Luke 23:56 to be the weekly Sabbath that came on the seventh day of the week. This gross error in primary reading is responsible for the almost universally held opinion that Christ was crucified on "Good Friday." But these gross errors are deliberate and intentional on the part of Rome, and are never to be treated as oversights or "accidents." Students of [Rome---the Great Private Interpreter ]{.class_s4N4-0}will learn quickly and thoroughly that Roman private interpretations are never [accidental;]{.class_s4N4-0} they are carefully planned and carried out with the utmost disregard for truth of any kind---Biblical or secular.

Christ was crucified [Wednesday morning ]{.class_s4N4-0}at 9 o'clock (Roman time), and no amount of appealing to Rabbinic tradition, Josephus, Edersheim, or Catholic tradition will []{#c1BJ.xhtml_page_254}ever alter the salient truth that Jesus was ["in the heart of the earth"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Matt. 12:40) all day Thursday, Friday, and Saturday. The proof of this does not lie in juggling texts to prove that "half a day can sometimes be counted as a whole, etc." The proof lies in the fact that the Gospel accounts---especially Mark---give the exact chronology of the last week as Sunday, Monday, Tuesday, GETHSEMANE. There is no "Wednesday" or "Thursday" activity found anywhere in Matthew, Mark, Luke, or John; Tuesday begins the story of the betrayal, trial, and execution of Jesus Christ. The Sabbath mentioned here (vs. 16) is called ["an high day"]{.class_s4N2-0} in John 19:31.

When in doubt, throw all religious teaching out.

["For in this selfsame day have I brought your armies out of the land of Egypt" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 17). The naive commentators in the [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_s4N4-0}suppose that these words could not have been spoken BEFORE the Exodus since they read[ "have I brought\...."]{.class_s4N2-0} They suggest that we change it to "I WILL bring you out," if we desire to "place it where it is found." (What do you suppose the commentators would think of Isa. 53? [Half the chapter is written in the past tense,]{.class_s4N4-0} though it deals with a future event.)

12:18 "In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month at even, ye shall eat unleavened bread, until the one and twentieth day of the month at even.

19 Seven days shall there be no leaven found in your houses: for whosoever eateth that which is leavened, even that soul shall be cut off from the congregation of Israel, whether he be a stranger, or born in the land.

20 Ye shall eat nothing leavened; in all your habitations shall ye eat unleavened bread.

21 Then Moses called for all the elders of Israel, and said unto them, Draw out and take you a lamb according to your families, and kill the passover.

22 And ye shall take a bunch of hyssop, and dip it in the blood that is in the bason, and strike the lintel and the two side posts with the blood that is in the bason; and none of you shall go out at the door of his house until the morning.

23 For the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}will pass through to smite the Egyptians; and when he seeth the blood upon the lintel, and on the two side posts, the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}will pass over the door, and will not suffer the destroyer to come in unto your houses to smite you.

24 And ye shall observe this thing for an ordinance to thee and to thy sons for ever.

25 And it shall come to pass, when ye be come to the land which the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}will give you, according as he hath promised, that ye shall keep this service.

26 And it shall come to pass, when your children shall say unto you, What mean ye by this service?

27 That ye shall say, It is the sacrifice of the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}'S passover, who passed over the houses of the children of Israel in Egypt, when he smote the Egyptians, and delivered our houses. And the people bowed the head and worshipped.

28 And the children of Israel went away, and did as the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}had commanded Moses and Aaron, so did they."

We are struck again with the foolishness of the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} in talking about "past tense" in regards to Israel's deliverance, for here, staring us in the face, is a text which says,[ "born in the land"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 19). How did []{#c1BJ.xhtml_page_256}God give this verse to Moses "later" [when Moses never entered the land ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Deut. 34:1--6)?\![ ]{.class_s4N4-0}No, the instructions are clearly [prophetic]{.class_s4N4-0}, and the Lord is anticipating the future exactly as He did when He gave instructions for entering the land in Numbers chapter 15. Did you notice that in [Numbers chapter 14 ]{.class_s4N4-0}the Lord had just [stopped ]{.class_s4N4-0}the children of Israel from [going in?]{.class_s4N4-0} (You will do well to ignore "tenses" where they contradict the preserved text of the Reformation. See the extensive comments on A. T. Robertson's "eight balls" in the [Commentary on Acts]{.class_s4N4-0}.)

Three verses (vss. 18, 19, 20) are devoted to discussing ["leaven." ]{.class_s4N2-0}The words are taken so seriously by the Jews---in their literal sense (while condemning ["the Lord of glory," ]{.class_s4N2-0}1 Cor. 2:8)---that they sweep their houses to make sure that no bread crumbs are on the floor; in some homes (A.D. 1500--1900), the house was considered to be unclean if anyone spotted a mouse running across the floor with ordinary bread in its mouth (Clarke, Comment., pg. 354).

The reader will observe that the fourteenth of Abib ends at 6 p.m., and thus, ["the feast of unleavened bread"]{.class_s4N2-0} [begins on the fifteenth. ]{.class_s4N4-0}This explains the stumbling block placed in Mark 14:1 over which the Catholic and Reformed theologians stumbled. ["TWO DAYS"]{.class_s4N2-0} would have been true Tuesday afternoon (see the remarks under vss. 16 and 17)! The first day of Mark 14:1 would take the believer to near 5 p.m. Wednesday night, and at 6 p.m. Wednesday night the second day would have started. Now here is a true case of "part of a day being counted as a whole." Part of Tuesday has been counted as one day, and then [all of Wednesday]{.class_s4N4-0} has been counted as another: this makes the Feast of the Passover TWO DAYS away. Notice how Mark 14:1 does not give the []{#c1BJ.xhtml_page_257}exact time element in relation to WHEN verses 1 and 2 fit into the narrative.

The unleavened bread is eaten at 6 p.m. (which, in the New Testament case, would be 6 p.m. Wednesday evening). This is during the fourteenth day of the month (vs. 16); hence, the bodies could not remain on the cross after 6 p.m. Wednesday night. That is, the [King James]{.class_s4N4-0} has it exactly right, and Dr. A. T. Robertson's inability to find this Scriptural truth is only one case in a score which bears testimony to the monumental Bible ignorance of the greatest Greek scholar in the United States. [Forty years of godly living and thirty years of formal education never equipped any man to correct the AV text.]{.class_s4N4-0}

["Ye shall eat nothing leavened"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 20). The Christian life is to be a separated life, and leaven in [doctrine ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Matt. 16:12), as well as leaven in [life ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1 Cor. 5:6--7), is not permitted. A little girl was asked one time where her mother was. She replied, "She's gone to a [Christian-and-Devil]{.class_s4N4-0} meeting." (This was intended to be ["a Christian Endeavor ]{.class_s4N4-0}meeting," but like many Christian "endeavors"---A.D. 325, Nicea, for example---they can often turn into Christian-and-Devil meetings, like the ecumenical council in 1970.)

[Leaven ]{.class_s4N4-0}is best illustrated by a daily newspaper. Nothing under God's heaven could be more mixed-up than the Associated Press unless it is the subscriber. Did you ever look closely at [any ]{.class_s4N4-0}big daily newspaper? Here, for God and angels to behold, is a movie ad about a [flying nun;]{.class_s4N4-0} below it is a movie on a blood-sucking teenage [werewolf;]{.class_s4N4-0} and across from these there is an ad for a [revival meeting. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Above this is a Seagram's "Seven Crown" ad, next to a Schlitz ad, sandwiched between a []{#c1BJ.xhtml_page_258}crossword puzzle and the astrological forecast for today. Up the page is [Peanuts]{.class_s4N4-0} and [Lil' Abner]{.class_s4N4-0} next to a Jim Beam ad, and below is a picture of two half-dressed women necking each other in the "Mountain Beyond the River, Beyond the Valley of the Valley of the Dolls." Scattered throughout the pages, on either side, will be found football scores, the weather report, an article on Women's Lib, a snarling gangster aiming a gun at a policeman, [Pogo,]{.class_s4N4-0} the [Wizard of Id]{.class_s4N4-0}, a news item on Billy Graham, scientific proof that Adam ate an [apricot,]{.class_s4N4-0} a picture of Pope Paul VI blessing Jack Kennedy before he got assassinated, a church ad promoting attendance on Easter, and a teenage girl smoking pot. You're a blank idiot if you think you can learn any truth about anything from such a magpie's nest. ["In all your habitations shall ye eat unleavened bread"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 20).

If people are what they eat---and [words ]{.class_s4N4-0}are [food ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Luke 4:4)---most Christians in America should look like a "giant-sized" economy loaf of "refined" white bread. How could any man ["grow in grace"]{.class_s4N2-0} (2 Pet. 3:18) or have the power of God in his life (or ministry) if he spent even thirty minutes with a newspaper every day of his life?

["Then Moses called for all the elders of Israel"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 21). Now the instructions follow, and we shall treat the instructions as a single unit as they continue down to verse 28. To begin with, the post-apostolic [LXX ]{.class_s4N4-0}blasphemes the blood of Christ (in type) by substituting the word ["threshold" ]{.class_s4N4-0}for ["bason"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 22). That is, Origen, Eusebius, Pamphilus, et al. decided that this prototype of Christ's blood should be placed [where you could STEP on it. They all had Hebrews 10:29 on their writing tables when they made this alteration ]{.class_s4N4-0}of the inspired text, and we have commented on this matter []{#c1BJ.xhtml_page_259}many times before (see especially the comments under Acts 7 in the Commentary on Acts). If your school or church is still carrying on with this inane nonsense about the Christians "quoting the [Septuagint]{.class_s4N4-0}," either pray for them or give them a CARE package for Christmas; [they need help. ]{.class_s4N4-0}The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} insists, now, that God alone was the destructive agent in verse 29, even though the clear statement of verse 23 is that the Lord would ["not suffer the destroyer to come in unto\...you."]{.class_s4N2-0}

The ["hyssop" ]{.class_s4N2-0}of verse 22 is a famous plant mentioned many times in the Scripture, and always with [purification of sin]{.class_s4N4-0} in mind (Lev. 14:4, 6, 51; Num. 19:6; Psa. 51:7; etc.), with the possible exception of John 19:29. That last verse indicates that the stalk of the hyssop plant was of various lengths. The plant is accepted to be "[Origanum Aegyptiacum"]{.class_s4N4-0}---a species of marjoram; descriptions can be found in any commentary.

The slain lamb (vs. 21) is plainly a type of Christ according to the Apostle Paul (1 Cor. 5:7). The word[ "houses"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 27) is used, again, in the sense of "households." We have commented on the Death Angel already under verse 23. The word ["strike"]{.class_s4N2-0} has been inserted into the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}text at verse 22, giving an ominous overtone to the death of ["the lamb"]{.class_s4N2-0} (see Mark 14:65). Again, grace causes the sinner to worship: ["And the people bowed the head and worshipped"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 27; cf. remarks under 4:31).

["And the children of Israel went away, and did as the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord ]{.class_s4N9-0}[had commanded Moses and Aaron, so did they"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 28). They went back to their houses and drew a lamb out of the flock. As the operations began, one little boy asks, "Daddy, what are we doin'? What are we doin\'?"

"Well, son," replies the Hebrew father, "It's a long story. You know we haven't had a family altar around here for a long time, but we're gonna start tonight."

"What's a 'family altar,' Daddy? Why do we have to have a[ family altar?"]{.class_s4N4-0}

"Well, boy, as I said, it's a long story. You see, years ago we used to be a great people, son. The God of our fathers told Abraham that He'd bless those that blessed us and curse those that cursed us; and ole' Pharaoh has cursed us, son, so God has cursed him!"

"But what's the lamb for, Daddy? Why are you killin' it? Momma said she already had planned somethin' else for supper. "

"Well now, son, this here lamb is going to save you from the Death Angel."

["The Death Angel? ]{.class_s4N4-0}What's the Death Angel, Daddy? Is that God?"

"Oh no, son. 'Course they teach that down at the world's most unusual University, but they got that from Kittel's text---not the [Receptus]{.class_s4N4-0}. You see, boy, God is going to pay us a visit tonight, and if we don't have blood over the door, the [firstborn ]{.class_s4N4-0}in each house is going to die."

"But Daddy! I'm the [firstborn!"]{.class_s4N4-0}

"I know it, son; I know it. That's what the blood is for. Here, hold the bucket still!"

Out comes that red fluid that first stained the grass in Genesis 3:21. The boy gazes at it as the lamb kicks a few times, closes its eyes, and expires.

"W-w-what does God l-l-look like?" asks the boy.

"Why, no one has ever really seen Him, son. He appeared as an angel to Abraham and Noah and Jacob and Moses, but nobody knows what He really looks like."

"Well, they will in awhile," the boy says firmly.

"Why do you say that, son?"

"Cause," says the boy, as he puts the bucket down and wipes his hands off, "I'm going inside and draw a picture of Him!"

All afternoon the bloody business goes on. The Egyptians go sightseeing (they used to call it "slumming" before the Lord wiped out Pithom and Ramses). The Egyptians heckle the "fanatics."

"Hey, you religious nut! What are you doin' there? Whatcha gonna do with that lamb? You better hadn't kill it or we'll getcha!"

The Hebrew father looks up from his work, and holding the knife in his hand, he says, "Then how come you didn't get Jehovah last year? He sure killed enough of your sheep to stock Kansas City. If you don't like it, run on and look at something else!"

And by now, the Egyptians know better than to mess with the children of Israel. All day the slaughter goes on. The air is filled with the aroma of barbecued lamb and fresh bread. Over in Tanis and Heliopolis, the royal family is having a hard time swallowing their saliva as they smell the savor drifting that way.

"What are those blankety-blank Jews up to now?" growls Pharaoh.

Evening comes. Shortly before darkness envelops the land of Egypt, the Queen of the Orient arises over the Sinaitic Peninsula and spangles the Nile with flecks of silver and gold. A dry wind sweeps leaves up and down the streets with a soft rustling sound, and silence descends upon the land of the Pharaohs.

Around 10 o'clock, a bunch of drunken Egyptians come staggering down the street---three sheets in the wind and two blown away---and they stop their drunken []{#c1BJ.xhtml_page_262}singing of ["Sweet Cleopatra"]{.class_s4N4-0} long enough to remark: "Aagh, look at those blankety-blank Jews! Blood all over the door! Blood all over the house! What a blankety-blank bloody mess! Whazza matter with them people? Don't they know it's 1571 B.C.? Blood!"

And one of the up-to-date, progressive, scientific, broad-minded, tolerant Egyptians (majoring in Social Studies at the University of Alexandria) pitches a half-filled bottle of "Kickapoo joy juice" up against the doorway and the liquor runs down---[mixed with the blood. ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Some folks never could get [blood ]{.class_s4N4-0}separated from [booze ]{.class_s4N4-0}and shinny. See Matt. 26:26--29 and comments in the [Commentary on Matthew]{.class_s4N4-0}.)

Shall we "window-peek" before Death comes[ "up into the windows" (Jer. 9:21)?]{.class_s4N3-0}

Home number one:

[A family is sitting relaxed in the living room. ]{.class_s4N3-0}Father is reading [The Daily Egyptian, ]{.class_s4N6}and mother[ is making last minute preparations for leaving at midnight. At 10 p.m., the oldest boy says, "Daddy, I want to borrow the camel. I gotta date down by the pyramid tonight, and\...."]{.class_s4N3-0}

"No go," says the old man.

"But\...." the boy objects.

"Sorry, it's out," says the father. "The Lord told us to stay in until midnight, and if any of us go out of that door before twenty-four hundred for anything but putting blood on the door, we'll be just as good as dead."

"But\...."

"But [nothing!"]{.class_s4N4-0} says the father.

"Get ready to go, or lie down and rest in the bedroom till I call you!"

"But\...."

"THAT'S IT!"

(And blessed is the young man who has a father who can "communicate" across the "generation gap"!)

Home number two:

All the family is resting and ready to go except the head of the household. He is pacing up and down the living room like a caged tiger, and as he churns through his own wake, he leaves a vapor trail after him like a steam locomotive (Egyptian cigars!).

His wife is "sitting and knitting." And after thirty minutes of it, she says, "For goodness sake, sit down; you're getting on my nerves!"

"Can't! [Can't!"]{.class_s4N4-0}

"Well, why not? You did what the Lord told you to do. Worrying about it isn't going to help a bit. You did put the blood on the door, didn't you?"

"Yes, but I just don't feel like that is enough\...I think you gotta 'live it'!"

"Well, Moses said if we would put the blood on the door, that would do it, and if Moses is lying, that is the first time he's lied since we've known him."

"Yeah, but how do I know he got the [right interpretation? ]{.class_s4N4-0}Did he have the original [God-breathed manuscripts?]{.class_s4N4-0} What about the heathen that never heard? Where did Cain get his wife? Why did God let sin come into the world? How come the elect have\...."

"Oh, for heavens sake! You're acting like a two year old; sit down and rest!"

"But what if you have to be baptized? Didn't you hear what those Egyptians were singing out there?"

"What Egyptians? I haven't heard anything."

"A bunch of drunks went by about an hour ago, and they were singing ['How DRY I Am'!"]{.class_s4N4-0}

"Oh, Obadiah!! You ought to be ashamed of yourself!"

"Well, maybe, but what if\...[what if]{.class_s4N4-0}\...?"

(Now, gentle reader, let me ask you something. What is the difference between home number one and home number two as far as SAFETY is concerned? You see, there isn't any. One family was [enjoying ]{.class_s4N4-0}their salvation, and the other one [wasn't.]{.class_s4N4-0})

Home number three:

A widow and her twenty-year-old son are sitting in the kitchen. They have turned out the lights and are gazing out on the houses, streets, fields, and trees all bathed, now, in the pastel blue-green of the full moon.

"It's been a long time," says the boy wearily.

"Well, son, it's been longer for me than it has for you. Your father died under the whip more than sixteen years ago."

"I know it; I know it," says the boy, "And if Moses doesn't get us out this time\...."

"He WILL; he will," assures the mother.

"What if he [doesn't?"]{.class_s4N4-0} asks the boy while he clenches his teeth.

"Now son, God's ways are slow but sure, and He promised to come through at [midnight, ]{.class_s4N4-0}and He'll fulfill His word."

"Humph! It's 11:30 already; I don't see any action."

"Twelve o'clock, son---[midnight."]{.class_s4N4-0}

"Oh mother, you always take it too literally. If He came over at 12:05 you would have to give up your belief in the Bible!"

"That's right, son; I would! But God is not going to be five minutes late or even one minute late; when He says midnight, He means [midnight!"]{.class_s4N4-0}

"Well, I heard Dr. Amenhamen say that that isn't in the 'original,' and you can't count on a parable for a doctrine."

"Ah hush, boy; never mind the great men. I've translated a few of them promises myself in the last sixteen years."

11:45---nothing happens.

11:50---nothing happens.

11:55---Pharaoh tosses in his sleep. Down in Goshen, some are still sitting up and watching, while many others who were too tired to stay up and wait are lying on sofas, divans, cots, beds, and pads---fully clothed and ready to travel.

11:56---nothing happens.

11:57---the widow's son gets up from the kitchen table and goes to his bedroom. "See you in the morning, Mom," he says, "Nothing's going to happen tonight anyway."

And at 11:58 nothing [had ]{.class_s4N4-0}happened.

11:59---The full moon floods Egypt with its pale light. The pacing tiger in home number two has collapsed an hour ago and is snoring on the living room rug. In several homes, only the father is awake, staring out the window into the silent night. Now the wind has stopped; only the sound of frogs and crickets can be heard in the distance, drifting across the sands.

Thirty seconds till midnight---nothing happens.

Twenty seconds till midnight---nothing happens.

Ten. Nine. Eight. Seven. Six. Five. Four. Three. Two. [One---!]{.class_s4N4-0}

["Slam, slam, slam!"]{.class_s4N4-0} Someone is knocking at the palace door! Pharaoh arises in a cold sweat, throws on his bathrobe, and rushes through the marble and terraced corridors of his palace. On the way, he notices that all []{#c1BJ.xhtml_page_266}of his guards, attendants, doorkeepers, watchmen, and chamberlains are in a dead sleep. They are scattered all over the hallways like broken glass on a turnpike. ["BOOM, BOOM, BOOM!"]{.class_s4N4-0} (Whoever is knocking must be some kin to Masoyama!)

Pharaoh opens the door. Then his heart nearly stops beating. Towering over him, nearly [thirteen ]{.class_s4N4-0}feet high, is a huge [black ]{.class_s4N4-0}shadow. The raised fist (that had nearly knocked the door down) is as big as a basketball, and it holds a drawn sword. Some nauseating and hideous aroma surrounds this ghastly apparition, and the radiations from it nearly knock Pharaoh off his feet.

["My God!"]{.class_s4N4-0} gasps Pharaoh, ["W-w-w-who, who, who are YOU!!?"]{.class_s4N4-0}

A voice like two giant gears grinding together answers: "I am the servant of Jehovah! [Stand aside!"]{.class_s4N4-0}

"No, no, no!" screams Pharaoh, "You can't come in here!"

"I am the servant of Jehovah, and I have the power of death! Stand aside, bud, [I'm coming in!"]{.class_s4N4-0}

The figure stoops and splits the palace doors as it forces itself into the throne room. And then, with footsteps that shake the palace from the capstone to the foundation, the apparition pounds its way to the royal nursery. Pharaoh, screaming, races ahead of it and throws his body across the door.

"NO! My God, no!" he cries,[ "You can't do this!]{.class_s4N4-0} I'm Pharaoh! I'm King of Egypt! This palace cost \$15,000,000!!"

"Looky here, bud!" growls Abaddon, "I don't care if this dump is a swalhi hut or the Taj Mahal! I don't care if you're a slave girl or Napoleon. I don't give a flip if your door posts are bamboo or marble. I'm looking for BLOOD []{#c1BJ.xhtml_page_267}on the door---not [dirt]{.class_s4N4-0}---and where I don't find BLOOD, I PRODUCE IT!"

A massive hand sweeps Pharaoh aside like he was a feather in a hurricane. [CRASH!]{.class_s4N4-0} The door to the nursery is torn off its hinges. [SWISH!]{.class_s4N4-0} Down comes the six-foot-long two-edged sword. There is a scream in the nursery, and the firstborn of Pharaoh lies dead in his crib.

12:29 "And it came to pass, that at mid--night the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}smote all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, from the firstborn of Pharaoh that sat on his throne unto the firstborn of the captive that was in the dungeon; and all the firstborn of cattle.

30 And Pharaoh rose up in the night, he, and all his servants, and all the Egyptians; and there was a great cry in Egypt; for there was not a house where there was not one dead.

31 And he called for Moses and Aaron by night, and said, Rise up, and get you forth from among my people, both ye and the children of Israel; and go, serve the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}, as ye have said.

32 Also take your flocks and your herds, as ye have said, and be gone; and bless me also.

33 And the Egyptians were urgent upon the people, that they might send them out of the land in haste; for they said, We be all dead men.

34 And the people took their dough before it was leavened, their kneadingtroughs being bound up in their clothes upon their shoulders.

35 And the children of Israel did according to the word of Moses; and they borrowed of the Egyptians jewels of silver, and jewels of gold, and raiment:

36 And the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}gave the people favour in the sight of the Egyptians, so that they lent unto them such things as they required. And they spoiled the Egyptians."

The moral of the lesson is clear: [when God says He is going to do something, He does it. ]{.class_s4N4-0}When did He ever say anything that didn't come to pass? It's real simple: under the blood you are [safe]{.class_s4N4-0}, and without the blood you are[ lost.]{.class_s4N4-0}

And oh, the weeping and wailing and gnashing of teeth! And oh, the empty cribs and the empty beds and the empty chairs at the table! And oh, the sorrow and the tears that came upon mankind (Egypt is a type of this world) for following the leadership of an educated agnostic (Exod. 5:2) instead of the prophets of God.

["And there was a great cry in Egypt; for there was not a house where there was not one dead"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 30). Every household with a firstborn male felt the "touch of the Master's hand" when it fell in judgment. Only those sheltered by the blood could thank God for what took place that night, and by 12:05, there wasn't a skeptical Israelite left in Egypt. This time, Pharaoh manifests the true fruits of repentance (vss. 31--32), and although this repentance is quite similar to Catholic and Campbellite "PENANCE," it lasts long enough to accomplish God's purpose---[liberation of His people.]{.class_s4N4-0}

Throughout this bloody scene, the major commentators (all inclusive) have failed to notice the use of Gentile time, exactly as Rome (and the United States) uses the clock today (see vs. 22 and comments). Notice the peculiar expression in verse 22: ["and none of you []{#c1BJ.xhtml_page_269}shall go out at the door of his house until the morning."]{.class_s4N2-0} Yet it is perfectly apparent from verse 31 that the children of Israel leave their doors ["by night"]{.class_s4N2-0} (see also vs. 42). This means that until Israel left Egypt, they went by Roman time, still used throughout Europe and America. Hebrew mornings do not begin until 6 a.m., and Hebrew evenings begin at 6 p.m. (see Gen. 1:3--31 and comments in the [Commentary on Genesis]{.class_s4N4-0}).

"Papasan" Clarke presents the bamboozle of the ages, here, by taking Habakkuk 3:13 and postdating it back to 1500 B.C.; [whereas, everything in that entire chapter is a reference to the Second Advent of Jesus Christ]{.class_s4N4-0} and the literal fulfillment of Genesis 3:15. But again, this is typical, not exceptional. After presenting 35 pages of chronology (see Appendix to Clarke's Commentary) showing us every possible day of every possible week in every possible lunar and solar year for centuries, the good doctor cannot now read, explain, understand, exposit, or apply a passage with which a sixth grader would have no trouble at all. [Habakkuk 3:10--13 has no more to do with past history than Revelation chapter 20. ]{.class_s4N4-0}A man who would put Habakkuk 3:13 back to Moses would believe that "Madam Butterfly" was a caterpillar before she hatched.

["And the Egyptians were urgent upon the people\...for they said, We be all dead men" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 33). Israel, as the true church, is a ["savour of death unto death"]{.class_s4N2-0} to those that perish (2 Cor. 2:15--16). The Israelites go next door to borrow utensils, weapons, and clothes; and lo and behold, they meet with: "Help yo'self, honey! Here is the TV; take the radio. You can have this heirloom if you want it! Can we get you anything else? You're [leaving?]{.class_s4N4-0} Excellent! Excellent! I mean, ah, [so sorry []{#c1BJ.xhtml_page_270}you have to leave in such a rush!]{.class_s4N4-0} What's the hurry? Here, take this too; you might need it! See anything else you want? [Raincoat?]{.class_s4N4-0} Help yourself, and here, take two of them---one for the little lady! [Gold? ]{.class_s4N4-0}Help yourself! We went off the gold standard two years ago after all the cattle died. Take as much as you need\![ The 12-gauge shotgun?]{.class_s4N4-0} Comin' right up! And here's two cases of shells to go with it: number 6, good for quail. Anything else?"

["And they borrowed of the Egyptians jewels of silver, and jewels of gold, and raiment\...And they spoiled the Egyptians" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vss. 35--36). For the error of the revisers, translators, commentators, and expositors in changing ["borrowed"]{.class_s4N2-0} to "asked," see the remarks under 3:22. The men responsible for the mass deviation from the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}text are Hengstenberg and Kurtz. Typical of the type of men who accept their theories as authoritative are the writers of the [Pulpit Commentary,]{.class_s4N4-0} who have suggested that Moses and Aaron could not have had a personal audience with Pharaoh (vs. 31) after the statements of 10:28--29. Clarke also takes this view. Evidently, neither Clarke nor the[ Pulpit]{.class_s4N4-0} commentator had anything happen in their own lives that caused them to break their word. This is the only charitable way to look at it, for if we take any other view than this, we cease to attribute their error to [ignorance ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [lack of experience,]{.class_s4N4-0} and then we must attribute it to deliberate DISHONESTY. (We always try to give Conservatives the benefit of a doubt and accuse them of stupidity or fear before an accusation of CROOKEDNESS.)

12:37 "And the children of Israel journeyed from Rameses to Succoth, about six hundred thousand on foot that were men, beside children.

38 And a mixed multitude went up also with them; and flocks, and herds, even very much cattle.

39 And they baked unleavened cakes of the dough which they brought forth out of Egypt, for it was not leavened; because they were thrust out of Egypt, and could not tarry, neither had they prepared for themselves any victual.

40 Now the sojourning of the children of Israel, who dwelt in Egypt, was four hundred and thirty years.

41 And it came to pass at the end of the four hundred and thirty years, even the self-same day it came to pass, that all the hosts of the LORD went out from the land of Egypt.

42 It is a night to be much observed unto the LORD for bringing them out from the land of Egypt: this is that night of the LORD to be observed of all the children of Israel in their generations."

We have spent a good deal of time (and typewriter ribbon) discussing the problem of the date [of the Exodus]{.class_s4N4-0} under 1:8, which see. The only thing we need to observe here is the manner in which the phony [Septuagint ]{.class_s4N4-0}(written around A.D. 300--500) has sought to replace the Masoretic text. The corruption here, cited from [Alexandrinus ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Fifth Century), says: ["They and their fathers\...in the land of Canaan"]{.class_s4N4-0} (this has been inserted into vs. 40). This, again, is "God's little helpers" protecting the Holy Bible from a "contradiction." Here they have attempted to take the 430 years and run them back to Abraham, making [two periods of 215 years each]{.class_s4N4-0} (see comments under 1:8).

As we have mentioned many times before, God needs no man's help in defending His word, if that "defence" []{#c1BJ.xhtml_page_272}consists of lying alterations of the text or a lying church history which distorts facts or a lying commentator who corrects the Hebrew Old Testament with the depraved ravings of the faculty at the Alexandrian University. There is not [one ]{.class_s4N4-0}Greek translation of Exodus 12---[even in the apostolic age]{.class_s4N4-0}---which contains the corruption of the Alexandrian manuscript. When Clarke passes on the age-old "party line" about the Alexandrian manuscript being a "COPY of the Septuagint," all we can do is lean over the rail of the ship and help feed the fish.

["And the children of Israel journeyed from Rameses to Succoth, about six hundred thousand on foot that were men, beside children. And a mixed multitude went up also with them"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 37--38). Many of the commentators go to work immediately to prove that it could not have been 600,000 men ["beside children."]{.class_s4N2-0} This is done to maintain their scholastic rating with infidels and atheists. If we are to take the numbers literally, a conservative estimate of the nation would be somewhere near 1,000,000 people. Whoever lowers this number very much will have to amend Numbers chapter 1, for there the number of males over twenty years old (!) is well over 600,000 (625,550 to be exact).

Dr. Johnson ([Wycliffe Commentary,]{.class_s4N4-0} pg. 62) jumps the gun, so to speak, and is so anxious to revive the theology of Astruc, Porphry, Celsus, Voltaire, and Tom Paine, that he guides Israel into the "marshy lakes" near the present "Suez Canal" to make sure they cross the[ "Suph Sea"]{.class_s4N4-0} instead of the Red Sea. (We shall comment on this more under 13:17.) This heresy (hearsay) is an ancient one, and it has been passed down from one campus campfire to another to form the present day []{#c1BJ.xhtml_page_273}mythological legend that Moses and his church did NOT go through the "Red Sea," but rather, they waded through some sloppy mud during a "low tide." There are as many [Conservatives]{.class_s4N4-0} in favor of this fairy tale as there are Liberals. As Herod and Pilate got together on one issue (Luke 23:12), so Liberals and Conservatives often agree when engaged in attacking the [Authorized Version ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1611), where they could not get together on the "Resurrection" or the "Virgin Birth."

Out of Egypt goes the thundering train---men, women, children, cattle, horses, carts, tents, camels, boxes, barrels, wagons, and staffs. Hundreds of Egyptians melt into the column and pick up the step. They had seen a demonstration of real power and felt drawn to it; they had seen the impotency of their gods and idols; they had come to reverence Moses and Aaron more than their own Pharaoh; and most of them were leaving farms and houses that looked like the Kansas dust bowl in 1933. For many of them, Moses' ministry had been a "wipe-out," and they had nothing to lose by leaving and everything to gain by going. The Scofield note (OLD [Scofield Reference Bible]{.class_s4N4-0}) is excellent, and it sheds light on the problems which arise in a local church because of an unregenerate membership. The mixed multitude can never be satisfied with "bread from heaven" (Num. 21:5); they continually long for ["the flesh pots"]{.class_s4N2-0} (16:3) of [Life ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [Look ]{.class_s4N4-0}magazine. They would rather die amongst frogs, lice, flies, and boils, watching the TV entertainment, than live in the tents of Jacob under the grace of God (Num. 24:5).

Typical of the attitude of the so-called "Conservative" is the [Wycliffe Commentary,]{.class_s4N4-0} which tells us that even though we [cannot ]{.class_s4N4-0}know the number of the children of Israel who came out, that this does not diminish from []{#c1BJ.xhtml_page_274}the "miracle" involved. This is the "agnostic" mentioned under verse 9. You were given the number. God preserved the number. [Anyone can find the number. ]{.class_s4N4-0}The number was clearly written in third-grade English where anyone can see it, BUT (!) ["we cannot tell what he saith"]{.class_s4N2-0} (John 16:18). The [Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} goes so far (towards Hell) that it even suggests that the number [might have been]{.class_s4N4-0} fifty or sixty thousand. Well, could it not also have been 400,000,000? If the text is not correct, who is to say WHOSE alteration is correct? If Sammy Davis or Archie Bunker said the number was 2,651, what would [that ]{.class_s4N4-0}mean?

Again (at the risk of antagonizing the reader!), we must pause to remark that these attacks on the Reformation text are quite characteristic of all modern Christian education in America. The apostasy since 1901 has not been within the ranks of the Liberals; they apostatized before 1900. The apostasy of the Twentieth Century---in the period preceding the Advent of Christ---is among [the faculty members of the Conservative Colleges who make a living by replacing the Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}text [with their own authority.]{.class_s4N4-0}

The very idea of telling a Bible believer that he does not know the [exact number]{.class_s4N4-0} of verse 37, while suggesting (in the context) that the number may have been "60,000"! Then imagine tying [that type of exposition]{.class_s4N4-0} with the name of[ John Wycliffe!]{.class_s4N4-0} That's a "gasser" for any man who knows church history or the Bible. John Wycliffe would have cut off his right arm before he would have changed the reading of verse 37 to any number other than 600,000, and if you don't know that, you don't have the blood running in your veins that ran in Wycliffe's veins.

If there had been only four sons per generation through a period of 215 years, there would have been [835,000 ]{.class_s4N4-0}men at the time of the Exodus, and this overlooks the fact that 1:7 speaks of an[ abnormal rate of increase. ]{.class_s4N4-0}What we have here is 600,000 young men who are able to walk, 100,000 older men who rode on wagons or cattle, 30,000 Hamites going along for a ride, and a minimum of 160,000 women and 100,000 children. You cannot get a figure much under 1,000,000 if you believe 1:7, and [we believe it. ]{.class_s4N4-0}If [your ]{.class_s4N4-0}preacher, teacher, reviser, Bishop, commentator, Priest, mother, father, expositor, pastor, or etymologist doesn't believe it, his (or her) infidelity will have no more effect on the truth than the spot on a menhaden will affect the tides.

["Now the sojourning\...was four hundred and thirty years"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 40). The ["epitrechon"]{.class_s4N4-0} (a parenthetical running commentary) has been omitted so the reader can see how the "[Septuagint]{.class_s4N4-0}" got the 215--215 year bracket for the sojourning either in Genesis 12:1--3 or, at the latest, in Genesis chapter 17. The additions made by Alexandria, many, many years AFTER the New Testament was in circulation, cannot in any way be connected with any B.C. flop supposedly manufactured under Ptolemy Philadelphus (see [The Christian's Handbook of Manuscript Evidence]{.class_s4N4-0}).

["Even the selfsame day\...it is a night to be much observed unto the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord]{.class_s4N9-0}["]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 41--42; see comments under vs. 30). This ["day"]{.class_s4N2-0} (and ["night"]{.class_s4N2-0}) should be near 1300, according to the [Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} (pg. 63). The reason for this selection is apparent to the student who has examined the theories listed under 1:8. Dr. Johnson (as many other Conservatives) wishes to maintain his scholastic standing []{#c1BJ.xhtml_page_276}with Bullinger, Ussher, and the Conservatives, while maintaining his standing with Peale, Weigle, and the Liberals. If the date of 1300 B.C. is used, we get into the same pickle that the Liberals got into when they chose 1220 B.C. for the date of the Exodus. That is, we make the Scripture contradict to match our opinion about them. Now this does not bother the conscience of a Liberal, but when you go throwing John Wycliffe's name around, you should be more careful. Thirteen Hundred B.C. will put you at [1260 ]{.class_s4N4-0}for Joshua, [1240 B.C. ]{.class_s4N4-0}for Joshua chapter 24, [810 B.C. ]{.class_s4N4-0}for 1 Samuel chapters 1--4, and [730 B.C. ]{.class_s4N4-0}for 1 Kings 6:1. This would give you[ less than 150 years ]{.class_s4N4-0}for the reign of every King between 1 Kings chapter 12 and 2 Kings chapter 25. To do this, Dr. Johnson does what every Bible-denying infidel has done since Genesis 3:1---[he simply suggests that God did not SAY 1 Kings 6:1 ]{.class_s4N4-0}and that the date there should be changed to match whatever theory is invented.

["It is a night to be much observed unto the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord]{.class_s4N9-0}[" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 42). Every Christian should know his birthday. Paul never forgot his, and nearly every time you pick up the book of Acts (Acts 22, 24, 26), Paul is talking about the day God took him out of "Egypt." This sojourning could have begun in Genesis 12:1--3, where the sojourner was not allowed to conquer the land or possess it (Heb. 11:9). From this lone pilgrim, there now proceeds an [army ]{.class_s4N4-0}(17:8), harnessed with weapons of war (13:18), that is destined to fight, conquer, possess, hold, and defend the very land wherein its fathers were "strangers and sojourners" (Gen. 23:4). There is no doubt about the importance of this night, for ["this is that night of the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord ]{.class_s4N9-0}[to be observed of all the children of Israel in their generations" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 42). []{#c1BJ.xhtml_page_277}To obscure the meaning of this last verse as much as possible, the[ Cambridge Bible,]{.class_s4N4-0} the [RSV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1952), and Keil and Delitzsch ball up the text till nobody but Houdini could unravel it; these fabrications are not worth reproducing.

12:43 "And the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}said unto Moses and Aaron, This is the ordinance of the passover: There shall no stranger eat thereof:

44 But every man's servant that is bought for money, when thou hast circumcised him, then shall he eat thereof.

45 A foreigner and an hired servant shall not eat thereof.

46 In one house shall it be eaten; thou shalt not carry forth ought of the flesh abroad out of the house; neither shall ye break a bone thereof.

47 All the congregation of Israel shall keep it.

48 And when a stranger shall sojourn with thee, and will keep the passover to the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}, let all his males be circumcised, and then let him come near and keep it; and he shall be as one that is born in the land: for no uncircumcised person shall eat thereof.

49 One law shall be to him that is homeborn, and unto the stranger that sojourneth among you.

50 Thus did all the children of Israel; as the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}commanded Moses and Aaron, so did they.

51 And it came to pass the selfsame day, that the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}did bring the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt by their armies."

Additional instructions are now given to supplement those of verses 1--13.

["There shall no stranger eat thereof: But every man's servant that is bought for money, when thou has circumcised him\...a foreigner and an hired servant shall not eat thereof"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 43--45). The Lord seems never to grow weary of [discrimination ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [segregation]{.class_s4N4-0}---the two chief sources of war and civil unrest, if we are to believe the College graduates of our day. The gist of the passage is that no man can partake of the Passover unless he is circumcised (vs. 44). Any man who refuses circumcision is to be considered a[ "stranger,"]{.class_s4N2-0} whether he be someone outside the land (a foreigner) or a servant working in a Hebrew home (vs. 45) or an "auslander" (see Neh. 13:26) sojourning with Israel. For the significance of circumcision, see the lengthy remarks on Genesis chapter 17 in [the Commentary on Genesis]{.class_s4N4-0}.

["Neither shall ye break a bone thereof" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 46) \[cf. John 19:36 and Num. 9:12\] .

["And he shall be as one that is born in the land" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 48) plainly is [prophetic]{.class_s4N4-0} and has nothing to do with those born in the land of Egypt. Bullinger falls into the trap here, as he is quite accustomed to changing the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}1611 where it displeases him. He now suggests that Egyptians should have circumcised their children in the dark (margin, [Companion Bible,]{.class_s4N4-0} pg. 90). (He could not have been referring to Israelites, for there was no darkness in Goshen during the three days and nights. See 10:23.)

All that is found in the passage hints of the great memorial of the Lord's Supper (1 Cor. 11:24), where only those born of the "holy seed" (1 Pet. 1:23) should partake (1 Cor. 11:27). Many Baptists push the typology all the way and insist that immersion in water replaces physical []{#c1BJ.xhtml_page_279}circumcision, and therefore, a born-again Christian who has not been immersed in water should not partake of the memorial (see vss. 48--49).

Here, then, under GRACE, we find the first ceremonial law mentioned in connection with a ceremonial feast (vs. 43), called an ["ordinance."]{.class_s4N2-0} The Law has not yet been given by Moses (John 1:17), and ["the handwriting of ordinances"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Col. 2:14) has not yet showed up. But already (since we are now dealing with a corporate body), there is a hint of the great Levitical laws which are to follow. Salvation has been[ by grace and through faith in the shed blood of the lamb. ]{.class_s4N4-0}No one has been told what or what not to eat (Lev. 11), how to wear their hair (Lev. 21:5), what sacrifices to bring (Lev. 1--5), or what washing or purifying they are to use (Num. 19, 31).

In connection with the Passover, we notice several ceremonial regulations (vss. 5, 8, 10, 16, 20, 46, 48). Of course, all of these are veiled pointers to Calvary, which not even Moses could have interpreted. However, the Lord insists that a man be true in observance of TYPES (Num. 20:8), and the Lord's feelings about this matter are so clearly demonstrated (Num. 20:12) that His greatest servant in the Old Testament (Heb. 3:5) dies a premature death for not observing the [correct typology. ]{.class_s4N4-0}A positive interpretation of the typology of TWO FINGERS RAISED (see [Mark of the Beast]{.class_s4N4-0}), instead of a negative interpretation, can destroy a nation of young people.

A [positive interpretation ]{.class_s4N4-0}of a CLENCHED FIST (see photos of U.S. Communist party in 1930--1933) can put a country under Socialism. A [positive interpretation ]{.class_s4N4-0}of the "crux anasta" (female or male sex symbol used by []{#c1BJ.xhtml_page_280}the Pope) instead of a negative one can fool 40,000,000 people a year into thinking they belong to a "Christian church." Unregenerate religious leaders always place a [POSITIVE ]{.class_s4N4-0}interpretation where the Bible places a NEGATIVE one. That is a law of life and nature, and it is as consistent and universal as any law known to science (see comments in [the Commentary on Proverbs, ]{.class_s4N4-0}Prov. 14:5, 15; 15:12, 21, and companion verses).

["The ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord ]{.class_s4N9-0}[did bring the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt by their armies"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 51). The [King James]{.class_s4NG} text preserves the truth of the matter. This nation is armed with weapons (see 17:13), and their Captain (Josh. 5:13--15) is ["a man of war" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(15:3). Although they have never had to fight as a nation and are not battle-hardened troops who have been "blooded" (13:17), they leave Egypt with battle harness, and the proper terminology for them is ["armies."]{.class_s4N2-0}

[]{#c1KD.xhtml}

# Chapter 13 {.heading_s4G-2}

13:1 "And the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}spake unto Moses, saying.

2 Sanctify unto me all the firstborn, whatsoever openeth the womb among the children of Israel, both of man and of beast: it is mine.

3 And Moses said unto the people, Remember this day, in which ye came out from Egypt, out of the house of bondage; for by strength of hand the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}brought you out from this place: there shall no leavened bread be eaten.

4 This day came ye out in the month Abib.

5 And it shall be when the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}shall bring thee into the land of the Canaanites, and the Hittites, and the Amorites, and the Hivites, and the Jebusites, which he sware unto thy fathers to give thee, a land flowing with milk and honey, that thou shalt keep this service in this month.

6 Seven days thou shalt eat unleavened bread, and in the seventh day shall be a feast to the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}.

7 Unleavened bread shall be eaten seven days; and there shall no leavened bread be seen with thee, neither shall there be leaven seen with thee in all thy quarters.

8 And thou shalt shew thy son in that day, saying, This is done because of that which the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}did unto me when I came forth out of Egypt.

9 And it shall be for a sign unto thee upon thine hand, and for a memorial between thine eyes, that the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}'S law may be in thy mouth: for with a strong hand hath the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}brought thee out of Egypt.

10 Thou shalt therefore keep this ordinance in his season from year to year."

For the first time in the Scriptures, the word ["sanctify"]{.class_s4N2-0} appears (although it appeared in its past tense in Gen. 2:3), fixing ever-afterward the correct meaning of the word, no matter how Greek and Hebrew scholars may misuse it to suit their individual idiosyncrasies. Observe how the "King's English" serves as its own dictionary and interpreter (vs. 12), without asking the advice of Keil, Hengstenberg, Delitzsch, Kalisch, Harkavey, Gesenius, or the A.D. 150--500 "[Septuagint]{.class_s4N4-0}." The word [sanctify]{.class_s4N4-0} means "to SET APART," and although other definitions can be further expansions of that meaning, that meaning is the[ root meaning]{.class_s4N4-0} (and if your church does not teach that, don't take much else they say too seriously).

["Both of man and of beast: it is mine" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 2). This is clearly a cross-reference to 12:12, which means that God never allows the destroyer to do his work (Job 2:3--8) without compensation. The firstborn of Israel must now take the place of the firstborn of Egypt, and the price of their salvation is total dedication and consecration to God. Paul says, ["Ye are not your OWN\...ye are bought with a price"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 Cor. 6:19--20). The similitude is striking. The verse also tells us that it was the firstborn MALES who were killed in Egypt.

["This day came ye out in the month Abib"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 4; cf. 12:2 and comments). We have already commented on verses 3, 5, 6, and 7 under 3:17 and 12:15, 17, 20.

["And thou shalt shew thy son in that day"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 8). This is a reference to verse 14 in the same chapter, and both verses point to the time of verse 11.

["And it shall be for a sign unto thee upon thine hand, and for a memorial between thine eyes, that the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord]{.class_s4N9-0}['S law may be in thy mouth"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. []{#c1KD.xhtml_page_283}9). The instructions remind the saved Hebrew what to DO (["thine hand"]{.class_s4N2-0}), what to LOOK AT (["thine eyes"]{.class_s4N2-0}), and what to SAY (["thy mouth"]{.class_s4N2-0}). Most of the commentators take the passage literally (for a change), but only do so because there is nothing near the passage that would indicate what was to be taken literally. All of them assume that the ["tephillin"]{.class_s4N4-0} (or ["phylacteries"]{.class_s4N2-0} mentioned in Matt. 23:5) are referred to here, but exactly where this reference is turns out to be a great mystery. [DRESS and WEAR are not the subjects of anything in Exodus chapter 13. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Some of the commentators seem to have the same frame of mind as the Pharisees.

However, it is quite common for carnal people to take passages [spiritually ]{.class_s4N4-0}that are [literal ]{.class_s4N4-0}(cf. Matt. 15:15, 25:41) and take spiritual [passages literally]{.class_s4N4-0} (cf. Matt. 26:28, 16:6, 12). It would be possible for a man to interpret Deuteronomy 6:9 and 11:20 literally, but there the word ["write"]{.class_s4N2-0} has been placed into the text in no uncertain terms. Not one word is said about "writing" anything in [Deuteronomy 6:8 ]{.class_s4N4-0}or [Deuteronomy 11:18.]{.class_s4N4-0}

To illustrate the muddled mess that a man can get into trying to take a spiritual passage literally (cf.
