John 10:7, for example), the Jews were unable to bind a phylactery over their HEART (Deut. 6:6), so the phylactery was bound (so help me Caiphas) [on the inside of the left arm above the elbow, ]{.class_s4N4-0}where it would be "[near]{.class_s4N4-0} the heart." But if you are going to be a literalist in HALF of the verse (Matt. 26:27), why not go "whole hog" and say that the CUP was Christ's blood, instead of what was IN the cup (Matt. 26:27). Is "[near]{.class_s4N4-0} the heart" the same as "IN the heart" (Deut. 6:6)? No, it is not, nor has it ever been, nor will it ever be. "NEAR" (Luke 18:24) is not "in," for you can be [near ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Mark 12:34) and be OUT.

The phylactery (or ["tephillin"]{.class_s4N4-0}) consisted of thirty verses of Scripture from Exodus chapter 13, Deuteronomy chapter 6, and Deuteronomy chapter 11, written on small pieces of paper. These slips were covered with leather and then tied to the forehead, hand, or arm. They were worn when the Jews read the Law or prayed, but were not worn on the Sabbath or during feast days. Jews of a later date (500 B.C.--A.D. 1900) used them for charms and amulets. Although they were supposed to arouse fear and respect from the ["Goyim"]{.class_s4N4-0} (Europeans, Asiatics, Africans, and Americans), there is not a case on record where any Gentile responded to them in any other way than ridicule, indifference, or disgust.

Regardless of how the phylacteries came into being, there is one thing that is certain: [there is no reference to what or how anyone should WEAR anything in Exodus chapter 13. ]{.class_s4N4-0}As a matter of fact, the "SIGN" referred to in verse 9 is the [unleavened bread]{.class_s4N4-0} of the Passover (see vss. 6--7). There is nothing in a near or remote context that would point to anyone "writing," "wrapping," "binding," or "wearing" anything. Unleavened bread is to be a sign for the hand---[pure works. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Unleavened bread is to be a sign for the eyes---[pure sight]{.class_s4N4-0} (Matt. 6:22; Job 31:1). Unleavened bread is to be a sign for the mouth---[purity of utterance]{.class_s4N4-0} (Matt. 12:34--36).

Now! If you read a commentary that doesn't tell you THAT, write your own, or write the author and straighten him out. If you meet a Rabbi who didn't get that "nugget," rebuke him and exhort him to leave the Hebrew text and return to the "faith." That is the meaning of the text as it stands in its context, and any "textbook" that ignores that meaning is a [pretext.]{.class_s4N4-0}

13:11 "And it shall be when the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}shall bring thee into the land of the Canaanites, as he sware unto thee and to thy fathers, and shall give it thee,

12 That thou shalt set apart unto the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}all that openeth the matrix, and every firstling that cometh of a beast which thou hast; the males shall be the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}'S.

13 And every firstling of an ass thou shalt redeem with a lamb; and if thou wilt not redeem it, then thou shalt break his neck: and all the firstborn of man among thy children shalt thou redeem.

14 And it shall be when thy son asketh thee in time to come, saying, What is this? that thou shalt say unto him, By strength of hand the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}brought us out from Egypt, from the house of bondage:

15 And it came to pass, when Pharaoh would hardly let us go, that the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}slew all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, both the firstborn of man, and the firstborn of beast: therefore I sacrifice to the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}all that openeth the matrix, being males; but all the firstborn of my children I redeem.

16 And it shall be for a token upon thine hand, and for frontlets between thine eyes: for by strength of hand the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}brought us forth out of Egypt."

The reader will observe an identical occurrence of the ["frontlets"]{.class_s4N7-0} and "signs" in verse 16 which match verse 9. Again, there is no mention of anyone writing anything, reading anything, binding anything, or typing anything, anywhere on anybody. This time, however, the sign is "the dedication of the firstborn" instead of the unleavened bread .

Again, all of the "spiritual" commentators who look to "the Lord Jesus as the final authority" (while tearing up His Bible) miss the spiritual nugget. [Here in verse []{#c1KD.xhtml_page_286}13 is the unsaved man---hair, ears, and all]{.class_s4N4-0} (see Job 11:12 and Prov. 26:3)! The unridden colt of Mark 11:2--3 is one of the greatest pictures of an [unsaved man]{.class_s4N4-0} found anywhere in the Bible, and since he is ["cursed"]{.class_s4N2-0} that ["hangeth"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Gal. 3:13), the Lamb HANGS instead of the ASS (Gal. 3:13). Observe: ["If thou wilt not redeem it, then thou shalt break his neck."]{.class_s4N2-0} Before our Conservative commentators the Holy Spirit has placed the only four-footed animal in the whole Bible whose [neck ]{.class_s4N4-0}is to be broken; all the rest of them are [decapitated ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Lev. 1--5). With the lamb ready to redeem the ["firstling"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Col. 1:14, [AV]{.class_s4N4-0} 1611), and with the Passover lamb appearing in the context (12:46), Williams, Dummelow, Clarke, Lange, Henry, Ellicott, Keil, Delitzsch, Johnson, Schaff, Weigle, Whitelaw, Gesenius, Hengstenberg, Bullinger, and the Scofield Board of Editors missed the cue as neatly as Casey Jones (A.D. 1900) missed John Newberry's flares and lanterns. (Williams hints at the matter, but likens the ass to a dead ass whose neck has already been broken; he fails even to find Job 11:12, which is a doctrinal statement on the first birth by nature.)

The male child must be redeemed by a lamb (vs. 13), and no provision is made for "breaking his neck," as in the case of the ass. Human sacrifices are forbidden under the Law (Mic. 6:7); Jephthah's defection does not alter the "rule" in the least. ["All the firstborn" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 13) points to grace---unmerited and undeserved. It clearly indicates God's desire to save ["whosoever will"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rev. 22:17), instead of a limited group of pre-chosen "elect." ["All"]{.class_s4N2-0} of the firstborn does not single out any [elect group]{.class_s4N4-0} of firstborn from another group, and those born later are not even mentioned. Moreover, the[ "firstborn"]{.class_s4N2-0} []{#c1KD.xhtml_page_287}is sometimes a Hell-bound sinner (1 Sam. 2:12); being ["firstborn"]{.class_s4N2-0} guarantees [no elective rights]{.class_s4N4-0} (1 Sam. 1:3) whatsoever.

Students of the word who desire greater insight into reality and truth than the nearsighted British Israelites (Garner Ted Armstrong, Herbert. W. Armstrong, et al.), Seventh-day Adventists (Mary Ellen White, et al.), Jehovah's Witnesses (Russell, Rutherford, et al.), Bullingerites (Stam, Baker, et al.), and Fundamentalists (Scofield, Darby, English, McClain, Larkin, et al.) should observe that the word ["MATRIX"]{.class_s4N2-0} is found in verse 15 (see [The Bible Babel]{.class_s4N4-0}). Words that end in "X" had significance long before dirty movies had "X" pasted on them. Long, long before the demoniacal Constantine had his hallucination at the Battle of Saxa Rubra (A.D. 312), the letter [X]{.class_s4N4-0} was connected with Mr. "X" of Revelation chapter 13. The symbols [O ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [X, ]{.class_s4N4-0}with variations, are the universal marks of [Satan]{.class_s4N4-0}, interpreted in a POSITIVE manner by Protestants, Christians, atheists, Catholics, Nazis, Communists, Jews, Rosicrucians, Spiritists, Satanists, Hindus, and medical doctors. (See lengthy dissertation on [X ]{.class_s4N4-0}in[ Mark of the Beast]{.class_s4N4-0}.)

["And it shall be when thy son asketh thee in time to come" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 14). Again, the Lord reviews the matters of verses 8--9, but as it has been said before, this time the inspiration for proper conduct (vs. 16) and proper view (vs. 16) is the ceremony of consecrating the firstborn (vs. 15). In verse 15, it is made clear that the firstborn of Israel, who have already been redeemed by the blood of the lamb, must be (in a sense) redeemed again; this is done to show that they take the place of those who were smitten by the Death Angel: ["The ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord]{.class_s4N9-0}[ ]{.class_s4NB}[slew all the firstborn in the land of Egypt" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 15). []{#c1KD.xhtml_page_288}Later (Num. 3:12--13, 41, 43, 47--51), the Levites fill up this number, and at that time, the firstborn of all the tribes are required to pay five shekels to the Levites for "taking their place" (Num. 8:16, 18:15--16).

The [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1901) has the [RSV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1952) reading for this passage in the [margin]{.class_s4N4-0}. This is the normal procedure, as the [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}translators had exactly the same attitude towards the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1611) Bible that the [RSV ]{.class_s4N4-0}translators did, except they did not have enough nerve to put their marginal readings into the text. This archaic reading is a substitution for[ "when Pharaoh would hardly let us go"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 15). It is apparent that the entire board of revisers on both translating committees were out of contact with Twentieth-Century American speech when they altered this text, for the common man on the street (1700--1980) uses the [King James]{.class_s4N4-0} expression as it stands in 1611.

a\. "I [hardly ]{.class_s4N4-0}got outta there 'fore the joint fell apart."

b\. "You can [hardly ]{.class_s4N4-0}do it unless you roll up the twine first."

c\. "I can [hardly ]{.class_s4N4-0}imagine anything much worse."

d\. "I can't [hardly ]{.class_s4N4-0}see tonight with the smog the way it is."

One must remember that the highly accurate and scientific [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1611) is a MODERN translation in the language of the people, and it is never to be set aside for such archaic and outmoded translations as the [NASV]{.class_s4N4-0} (1963) and the [NRSV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1973).

13:17 "And it came to pass, when Pharaoh had let the people go, that God led them not through the way of the land of the Philistines, although that was near; for God []{#c1KD.xhtml_page_289}said, Lest peradventure the people repent when they see war, and they return to Egypt:

18 But God led the people about, through the way of the wilderness of the Red sea: and the children of Israel went up harnessed out of the land of Egypt.

19 And Moses took the bones of Joseph with him: for he had straitly sworn the children of Israel, saying, God will surely visit you; and ye shall carry up my bones away hence with you.

20 And they took their journey from Succoth, and encamped in Etham, in the edge of the wilderness.

21 And the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}went before them by day in a pillar of a cloud, to lead them the way; and by night in a pillar of fire, to give them light; to go by day and night:

22 He took not away the pillar of the cloud by day, nor the pillar of fire by night, from before the people.

None of the commentators are able to face verse 17 squarely, as it affords a difficulty so great that the commentators will not even trust their lexicons and "authorities on the original languages" to get them out of the mess. The school at Alexandria, here, is faced with a REAL discrepancy; and after flinching, dodging, fainting, and ducking at verses like 9:14 and 12:37, the whole school just "lets out" for the semester and joins the students in a "love-in" with "grass and pot." Neither Adam Clarke nor J. P. Lange are able to help the reader any, and Thomas Whitelaw ([Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0}) doesn't even attempt to handle the verse. Dr. Johnson ([Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0}) only states the obvious truth already given in the text:[ "Lest peradventure the people repent when they see war"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 17). The other commentators rest with the spiritual explanation: God is tender with the []{#c1KD.xhtml_page_290}new believer coming up out of "Egypt" and does not put him into the thick of the fight (Eph. 6:10--13) until he has had time to be disciplined and grow (Heb. 5:14). (These expositions make good sermon material, but none of them attempt even to define the problem in the passage.)

["That God led them not through the way of the land of the Philistines, although that was near"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 17). But they were NOT going into Canaan immediately anyway (3:12). They had to go SOUTH before going anywhere (19:2). What is worse, they couldn't have gone up the ["Via Maris"]{.class_s4N4-0} (the sea coast road to Gaza) even if there hadn't been a soldier there,[ for they were to meet God on Mt. Sinai more than 150 miles south of there.]{.class_s4N4-0}

Furthermore, we cannot take the verse in retrospect from Moab (Num. 22--25) and say that God did not lead the children of Israel up the sea coast AFTER their visit to Sinai. This would be improper because:

1\. They had just as many wars on the east side of Jordan as they would have had on the west (Num. 21--31).

2\. When they started into the Promised Land (Num. 13--14), they were right next to the land of the Philistines, and they later hit the "giants" who survived in the person of Goliath THE PHILISTINE (cf. Num. 13:33; Josh. 13:12).

Here, then, is a genuine problem. It is not quite clear why the "godly Conservative scholars" refused to face the problem after taking the trouble to alter 3:22, 4:26, 3:14, and 9:14. However, it gets terribly clear when we read verse 18. Now the old "scholastic cat" jumps out of the bag, and it is an ATHEISTIC CAT with Liberal claws.

["Through the way of the wilderness of the Red sea"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 18). Now THIS is the reason why the "majority of Conservative anarchists" have given the students a midterm vacation at verse 17.

The old ["Red Sea-Suph Sea-Sea of Reeds"]{.class_s4N4-0} argument is just about as old as Moses himself. Every decade the Bible believer has to be subjected to some silly Ph.D. or Th.D., who just found out that the word for ["Red"]{.class_s4N2-0} was[ "Suph"]{.class_s4N4-0} (Heb.). Like the "apricot" of Genesis chapter 3 that often appears as an "apple" (some writers) or an "orange" (other writers), the "Sea of Reeds" never fails to tickle the ears of the egotistical Athenians (Acts 17:21) who keep thinking they are able to improve on the living ["words of the living God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Jer. 23:36).

The attempt to force Israel into crossing the Red Sea at Lake Timsah or Lake Menzaleh (near Rameses) is as old as Genesis 3:1, and has less documentation than Jephthah's rooster that lost its virginity (Judg. 11:31). (What if a [rooster ]{.class_s4N4-0}had come out first?! [Think of that!]{.class_s4N4-0}) [Unbelief ]{.class_s4N4-0}is the same sin whether it appears in the writings of [Dr. A. T. Robertson]{.class_s4N4-0} (as he tries to prove that Christ was crucified on Friday) or the writings of [Benjamin Warfield]{.class_s4N4-0} (as he attacks the [King James ]{.class_s4N4-0}Bible) or the speeches of [Tom Paine]{.class_s4N4-0} (as he tried to save himself from Hell by pretending there wasn't any Hell around anywhere) or the books of [Einstein ]{.class_s4N4-0}(who tried to rid himself of absolute truth so his own opinions would be as valid as Jesus Christ's). All of this infidelity is just like a publication of [John R. Rice]{.class_s4N4-0} which recommends that you adopt the corrupt Roman Catholic [NASV,]{.class_s4N4-0} or the sermons of Norman Vincent Peale who refused to preach on Hell or the Second Coming since he didn't believe in either one.

The fact that 2,000 "Conservative" scholars are stupid enough to believe that Pharaoh got drowned during a [high tide]{.class_s4N4-0} in one of [his own marshes]{.class_s4N4-0} means absolutely NOTHING at all to any man seeking the truth or loving the truth. Only the briefest study of the Bible indicates the route that Israel took in leaving Egypt, and one cannot help but notice how devoid of [Scripture ]{.class_s4N4-0}are the lengthy dissertations which try to prove that Israel crossed the Red Sea in Goshen, ten miles from Rameses, in what is now the Suez Canal. I wonder how much need there would have been for ["a pillar of a cloud" ]{.class_s4N2-0}and ["a pillar of fire"]{.class_s4N2-0} (by day and night---vs. 21) to lead someone from Goshen to Goshen on a trip any army could make in twelve hours or less! Goshen is just west of the region of the Suez Canal. The children of Israel LEFT Goshen, and verse 17 states that they did not leave it going west to east. Our "cat's paws" have appeared over the edge of the bag.

Now before showing the Bible reader the tremendous power and authority of the infallible [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}text as it corrects all the errors of [all the Conservative scholars]{.class_s4N4-0} through 200 years, let us return to verse 17 and arrive at a sensible exposition.

1\. The children of Israel had to come to Sinai [before entering Canaan;]{.class_s4N4-0} that much is clear.

2\. The question, then, is: "Which route to Sinai" shall be taken?"

3\. There is clearly a route going down the western shore of the Sinaitic Peninsula.

4\. There is clearly a route (the [Via Maris]{.class_s4N4-0}) going up into Palestine through Migdol---see note on that city under 14:2---by the "way of the Philistines."

5\. The only sensible conclusion at which one can arrive is that the Israelites could have gone up the [Via Maris]{.class_s4N4-0} 50--75 miles, and then they would have [turned southward]{.class_s4N4-0} and gone down to Sinai.

In view of the danger mentioned in verse 17, we must conclude that the Philistines had garrisons down ["the way"]{.class_s4N2-0} to within 75 miles of Egypt. The careful student of the Scripture (who wisely ignores the comments of Clarke, Schaff, Weigle, Keil, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, Gesenius, Johnson, Whitelaw, Bullinger, Brown, and Bantzsch where they cross the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}text) has noted that the Philistines came from Casluhim (Gen. 10:14 and comments in the [Commentary on Genesis]{.class_s4N4-0}) who was the [son ]{.class_s4N4-0}of the founder of Egypt (cf. Gen. 10:13; Amos 9:7.)

Now, if the "Red Sea" of Liberal and Conservative scholarship is the area of the Suez Canal or the "marshy lake area" north of Rameses and Pithom, how can one explain the statement that God did NOT lead the children of Israel through this area? He ["led them not through the way of the land of the Philistines" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 17). Any move from Goshen EASTWARD would have taken them right into this way. Obviously they must ["TURN"]{.class_s4N2-0} (14:2) and go south, not east. This ["TURN"]{.class_s4N2-0} (14:2) is made 24 hours before any crossing of any "sea" is made, for observe 14:19--20. The evidence that the crossing was made fifty to seventy miles south of Goshen (not five to fifteen miles east of Goshen) is so preponderous, when we search the Scripture, that we can only reach the conclusion that a man who would have Israel cross marshy ground near Suez is a Bible-rejecting infidel; his [profession of faith]{.class_s4N4-0} has nothing to do with his [heart attitude.]{.class_s4N4-0}

A. The sea which the children of Israel crossed has [EZION-GEBER]{.class_s4N4-0} on its shore (1 Kings 9:26). That is some "marshy ground" you have there, sonny! Ezion--Geber is ["Elath"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Deut. 2:8) on the Gulf of Aquaba,[ more than 200 nautical miles from the Suez Canal. ]{.class_s4N4-0}(That is some "Suph Sea" you poured out there, kids!)

B. The sea which the children of Israel cross laps the shores of Edom (Deut. 1:40, 2:1). [Edom's territory isn't within fifty miles of the Suez area in any direction.]{.class_s4N4-0}

C. The sea which the children of Israel crossed was a campsite AFTER they left Egypt (Num. 14:25, 21:4, 33:11). Are you trying to tell us, Doctor, that Israel [returned ]{.class_s4N4-0}to the marshy lakes of Goshen to encamp with 2,000,000 people,[ forty years after they LEFT the place? ]{.class_s4N4-0}Exactly what are you trying to tell us, Doctor? Aren't you really trying to tell us that you would lose your accredited rating in the Scholars' Union if you began to believe the Bible? That is, in order to keep your name where it is, you have to talk like the rest of the "brethren"? That is, like a [babbling idiot?]{.class_s4N4-0}

D. The sea which the children of Israel crossed dried up in the same manner as the river Jordan dried up (Josh. 4:23). (You see, Doctor, your problem lies much deeper than merely rejecting miracles; you have a problem in [remedial reading]{.class_s4N4-0} which you should have corrected at least twenty years before you undertook to express your destructive mind on paper, as it writhed under the lash of the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1611) Bible. Are you trying to tell us, Professor, that a [high tide]{.class_s4N4-0} affected the [Jordan River]{.class_s4N4-0} (Josh. []{#c1KD.xhtml_page_295}3--4) so that it got "marshy" for awhile before the water came back? No? Then what in blazes does this mean: ["For the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord]{.class_s4N9-0}[ ]{.class_s4NB}[your God dried up the waters of Jordan\...as the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord]{.class_s4N9-0}[ ]{.class_s4NB}[your God did to the Red sea, which he dried up from before us "]{.class_s4N2-0} (Josh. 4:23)??

E. The sea which the children of Israel crossed drowned troops (15:4) in ["depths"]{.class_s4N2-0} (15:5), as ["they sank"]{.class_s4N2-0}[ ]{.class_s4N4-0}(15:5) into ["floods"]{.class_s4N2-0} (15:8) that were standing ["]{.class_s4N4-0}[UPRIGHT"]{.class_s4N2-0}[ ]{.class_s4N4-0}(15:8), exactly as the Holy Spirit recorded it in Joshua 3:16--17 in a[ King James]{.class_s4N4-0} (1611) [Authorized Version.]{.class_s4N4-0}

Moral: [where all scholars]{.class_s4N4-0} (Conservative or Liberal) [correct the English text with any Hebrew text]{.class_s4N4-0}---in this case "Suph,' for "reeds''---[correct the Hebrew with the English.]{.class_s4N4-0}

The problems in applying the "Sea of Reeds" (Heb. ["suph"]{.class_s4N4-0}) to the Red Sea amounts to little or nothing at all. Driver says that ["suph"]{.class_s4N4-0} was SUPPOSED to have been "some kind" of a reed, but what it is for sure "we cannot know." In Isaiah 19:1--6 and Isaiah 18:2 (using the highly accurate and scientific [King James]{.class_s4N4-0} text), we learn why the Red Sea was called the "Sea of Reeds." It was so named for the [reed boats]{.class_s4N4-0} that sailed on it. (When in doubt, throw all the commentaries out.)

Not even the great ultra-dispensationalist Bullinger escapes the plague of infidelity that sweeps across the commentaries like a black scourge, for in his [Companion Bible,]{.class_s4N4-0} he has taken the time and the space to alibi the northmost crossing by remarking about how that area is still gradually "drying up." (And Bullinger a Pre-millennialist! Tsk, tsk, tsk!) Furthermore, Brother Bullinger tips his hat to the faculty at Alexandria by []{#c1KD.xhtml_page_296}passing on the famous campfire legend that the word ["Red"]{.class_s4N2-0} came from the "[Septuagint]{.class_s4N4-0}." [Of course, it did nothing of the kind,]{.class_s4N4-0} and the Masoretic text of 1917, put out by the Jewish Publication Society of America, reads[ "Red Sea" ]{.class_s4N4-0}in all passages, [never ]{.class_s4N4-0}"Sea of Reeds."

Deuteronomy 1:40 places the infamous "Sea of Reeds"[ along the whole length of the Sinaitic Peninsula, ]{.class_s4N4-0}on either side. The waters around the Sinaitic Peninsula, on either side, are the waters of the Red Sea, and the mythological "Sea of Reeds" is no more fitting to an exposition of the context than a dissertation on "the Illuminati" would be at a Jewish synagogue.

So---in spite of Conservative scholarship---out of Egypt they go! Down they go, southeast from Goshen, and encamp at the north extremity of the Red Sea (vs. 20). The Conservative scholar J. A. Thompson refuses to locate the city of ["Etham"]{.class_s4N2-0} when he draws his map of "The Path of the Exodus" ([The Bible and Archeology,]{.class_s4N4-0} Eerdman's Publishing Co., 1957, 1958, 1959, 1960, and 1962, pg. 61.), and here at Etham, Dummelow shoves the hosts of Egypt across the "Bitter Lakes" by giving them the benefit of an ebb tide! (Dummelow, Commentary, pg. 61)! If Dummelow had checked with the [International Standard Bible Encyclopedia,]{.class_s4N4-0} Volume IV, he would have found that tides do not affect the "Sea of Reeds" and the wind only causes variations in depth of three feet near Suez. (Ah, so!)

When one gives the matter serious thought, one must confess that the Liberal "miracle" is perhaps better than the Bible miracle, for how the King of Egypt, [with a trained army of horsemen,]{.class_s4N4-0} could drown in three feet of water is certainly a "sign" worthy of commemoration (14:31). Don't you love that word ["sea shore"]{.class_s4N2-0} (14:30) []{#c1KD.xhtml_page_297}in the Bible text, where it should have been "lakeside" according to the most authoritative, conservative [monkeys ]{.class_s4N4-0}of our day?

Down they roar to Etham; the caravan thunders out over the midnight plains of Egypt, guided by ["a pillar of fire"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 22). This pillar stays with them (Neh. 9:19) until the encampment at Beth--jesimoth (Num. 33:49). For the child of God, this presence is a "light to his pathway" (1 Thess. 5:1--6), but to the unsaved it is a black light (John 9:39--41), for ["they know not at what they stumble"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Prov. 4:19). The caravan halts at Succoth on the way and then resumes its march.

The Conservative scholar Dr. Shaw (see appendix to ["Exodus"]{.class_s4N4-0} in Clarke's Commentary) goes into considerable details speaking of these "journeyings." Yet the reader will be safer sticking with the text, for even Dr. Shaw has caught the virus of "it couldn't possibly mean what it says." Time and time again, we catch him trying to "shepherd the flock" (Isa. 63:11) [as far north as he can]{.class_s4N4-0} so he will not be ridiculed by the atheists, agnostics, infidels, and other graduates of Harvard, Columbia, Princeton, Yale, Berkeley, and Chicago University. Fortunately, we people who believe the Book are never under any compulsion to maintain our "standing" with [anybody ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Gal. 1:10), so we have an advantage in expositing the Bible which Dr. Shaw and his associates can never gain.

At Etham, the children of Israel can still move northeast and get back on the "way of the Philistines"; furthermore, if they go EAST they can make a clean getaway into Sinai without having to cross the Red Sea. But the ways of God are sometimes inscrutable to those who must follow them, and as we shall see, in the next []{#c1KD.xhtml_page_298}chapter the Lord has no intention whatsoever of letting Israel get out of Egypt until TWO things have been accomplished:

1\. The destruction of Pharaoh and his army.

2\. A violent demonstration of God's power to deliver, even if He has to overthrow the laws of nature.

In closing the chapter, we cannot help but notice the tremendous [spiritual truth]{.class_s4N4-0} found in verse 19, which is completely overlooked by the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0}, the [Wycliffe Commentary,]{.class_s4N4-0} Dummelow, Williams, Clarke, etc., etc. Joseph is a type of Christ (see the 152 similitudes mentioned in Pink's work on[ Gleanings from Genesis]{.class_s4N4-0}). None of Christ's bones are on this earth; Christ's bones were RAPTURED (Luke 24:39, 51; Acts 1:9--10). Since Egypt is a type of this world ([The Commentary on Genesis,]{.class_s4N4-0} 1970, Gen. 50), [Joseph's bones cannot remain in the royal cemetery at Tanis. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Furthermore, the New Testament Christian is ["in Christ" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(Rom. 12:5) and is part of his flesh and BONES (Eph. 5:30). In such a position (Eph. 5:31), [the Christian's bones cannot stay on this earth]{.class_s4N4-0}---buried or alive. My bones are going to be raptured (1 Cor. 15:41--52). Exactly as the bones of Jesus Christ were carried out of "Egypt" (Acts 1:9--10)[ before the destruction of Jerusalem by Rome]{.class_s4N4-0} (typifying Rev. 11:1--2), so my bones are going to leave this earth before the wrath comes down (1 Thess. 5:1--9).

Verse 19 is not only a fulfillment of Genesis 50:25 by faith (Heb. 11:22)---to follow the "customs of the ancient Hebrews" ([Pulpit Commentary, ]{.class_s4N4-0}Vol. 1, pg. 306)---it is ["the voice of the archangel"]{.class_s4N2-0} and ["the trump of God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 Thess. 4:16), where the Christian is concerned. It clearly shows that [Exodus chapters 14 and 15 are a []{#c1KD.xhtml_page_299}type of the Rapture where the living and the dead]{.class_s4N4-0} (John 11:25--26) will pass up through the "Great Deeps" (Gen. 1:2; Hab. 3:10, 15; Judg. 5:19--20; Exod. 14:26--29; etc., etc.), followed by the principalities and powers of darkness (Eph. 6:12), and will live to see the door slammed shut (John 10:2, 6) on their adversaries, who will then "sink like a stone" into the depths (15:8--10).

(Don't look in the commentaries for it; [it isn't there. ]{.class_s4N4-0}It is found only in the infallible word of the living God, and no commentator from Origen to Williams could even find the cross-references, as all major commentators---except those who limited themselves to [devotional matter]{.class_s4N4-0} exclusively---from Origen to Williams were engaged in altering the word of God to suit their opinions.)
