# Chapter 14 {.heading_s4G-0}

14:1 "And the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}spake unto Moses, saying,

2 Speak unto the children of Israel, that they turn and encamp before Pi-hahiroth, between Migdol and the sea, over against Baal-zephon: before it shall ye encamp by the sea.

3 For Pharaoh will say of the children of Israel, They are entangled in the land, the wilderness hath shut them in.

4 And I will harden Pharaoh's heart, that he shall follow after them; and I will be honoured upon Pharaoh, and upon all his host; that the Egyptians may know that I am the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}. And they did so.

5 And it was told the king of Egypt that the people fled: and the heart of Pharaoh and of his servants was turned against the people, and they said, Why have we done this, that we have let Israel go from serving us?

6 And he made ready his chariot, and took his people with him:

7 And he took six hundred chosen chariots, and all the chariots of Egypt, and captains over every one of them.

8 And the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}hardened the heart of Pharaoh king of Egypt, and he pursued after the children of Israel: and the children of Israel went out with an high hand.

9 But the Egyptians pursued after them, all the horses and chariots of Pharaoh, and his horsemen, and his army, and overtook them encamping by the sea, beside Pi-hahiroth, before Baal-zephon."

For an orthodox Jew---a true child of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob---this is one of the greatest chapters, if not the greatest []{#c1P3.xhtml_page_301}chapter, in the whole Bible. The drama builds to a climax so terrific that to this day Hollywood, Chicago, and New York must continue to reproduce it fifty times a week. The trained eye sees immediately the "race against time" where it looks like "all is lost," and whether this race be pictured as the "Fifth Calvary" dispatched from Fort Laramie to rescue the wagon train from the Indians, or Elliott Ness being pursued by two Rolls Royces filled with "gorillas," or a squadron of Messerschmidts chasing the British refugees, or the Spanish Armada sailing up the channel to attack England, or the "Dragons" from the East Side closing in on Little Orphan Annie, the scene is the same. The [heroine ]{.class_s4N6}is there (Israel, Rev. 12:1--6); the [hero ]{.class_s4N6}is there (Moses, Exod. 12--14); the threatened wealth (or estate) is there (Exod. 15:17); the [villain ]{.class_s4N6}is present (Ezek. 32:2); and unless there is Divine intervention---a twist in the narrative, a turn to the plot, an unexpected deliverance, an unforeseen helper ---all is over! [The Perils of Pauline]{.class_s4N6} never followed any other plot than the one laid down in the [AV ]{.class_s4N6}1611 text; it is never "out of date."

The caravan rolls on. In spite of Divine segregation and discrimination, it is leavened and integrated again, for many in the column have made a change of residence and leadership without a change of heart. They had only observed that money and power accompanied Moses, and they thirsted for both. Up above, looking out over the battlements of Heaven, Michael (Dan. 12:1) and the Lord surveyed the scene:

"I don't like the looks of all that mixed multitude," says Michael.

"Well," says the Lord, "Lucky Thirteen has a few more years to mess up the details, but I've got a Sunday punch coming later that will finish him off good. Let 'em go. Besides that, some of these Jews need a little more trial and tribulation. Notice how cocky some of them are acting?"

"Yes, Lord," agrees Michael, "They're ridin' mighty high now, but wait till they get out in that desert and run out of water!"

"You're getting to know those people, aren't you, Michael?"

"Yes, Lord, and the more I study your word, the more I understand human nature. And that old Pharaoh back there, asking for prayer and all that bit, do you reckon he REALLY is going to stay back there and let Israel go?"

"Him?! Stay back there? Not by a sight! Why, that old rascal's heart is harder than a piece of millstone (Job 41:24); when they get like that, I cut them off---["and that without remedy"]{.class_s4N7-0}[ ]{.class_s4N6}(Prov. 29:1)! He isn't about to let them go. He wouldn't let them go if his life depended on it. He doesn't know it, but that is just what his life [depends on!"]{.class_s4N6}

"Lord, how come you ended that last sentence with a [preposition?"]{.class_s4N6}

"That's for cultured Christians to stumble over, Michael. When a man gets proud of his grammar, he's not fit for service. Did you ever find any Christian in Acts who was proud of his grammar?"

"Lord, I never even heard of any book called ['Acts'!"]{.class_s4N6}

"Well, you WlLL."

From the account, no one has to guess about the ["possible]{.class_s4N6} route of the Exodus." The children of Israel are heading out east-south-east from Goshen, and at Etham, near the north end of the Red Sea, they are told to turn southward.

["That they turn and encamp\...by the sea" ]{.class_s4N7-0}(vs. 2). The[ "sea,"]{.class_s4N7-0} here (as it appears in vss. 16, 21, 22, and 26), is never a reference to the Mediterranean Sea or the "Sea of Reeds" or the ["Suph Sea"]{.class_s4N6} or the Bitter Lakes or any other frantic invention of the unfaithful expositor as he seeks to "tone down" the miracle of the Red Sea crossing. If this MIGDOL (vs. 2) were at the north end of the Red Sea, or any other body of water, Pharaoh could []{#c1P3.xhtml_page_303}have spared himself the trouble of going [through ]{.class_s4N6}water to reach the Israelites. Common sense---a quality sadly missing in the dissertations by Shaw, Kennicott, Thompson, Keil, Delitzsch, Gesenius, and Johnson---would tell anyone (let alone a military commander!) that you don't have to take a chance on wet ground with chariots if [you can go around it]{.class_s4N6} and accomplish the same purpose. If the "Red Sea" were the fictitious "Suez area," Pharaoh only had to skirt it to the north (or the south), and he wouldn't have rusted a horseshoe of his "regimental combat team."

It is perfectly apparent from the text (vs. 3) that Israel is entangled IN THE LAND OF EGYPT, not "on the border of Egypt." It is even clearer that Pharaoh feels compelled to go down into a dry seabed in order to stop the escape (vs. 23). With all the hot air about locations of places, identification of names, directions of flight, and archaeological "discoveries," it is astounding that the Conservative commentators who reject the [AV ]{.class_s4N6}text have never considered the results of making the Red Sea any other sea, or even the results of making the "crossing" an oblique crossing at the north end of the Red Sea. The crossing, according to Pharaoh's actions and the actions of his army, is a west to east crossing that is impossible to flank or "contain." Those of differing opinion may be disqualified as not knowing about what they are talking, since:

1\. They weren't there.

2\. They never attacked anyone in a chariot.

3\. They never lived in Egypt, as lord over a slave race.

4\. They never chased anyone out of Goshen before 1000 B.C.

Whatever Pharaoh's madness was (see comments under vs. 5), he certainly was not batty enough to jeopardize 2,000 chariots unnecessarily (see vs. 7) to stop a people which he planned only to RECAPTURE (vs. 5). Pharaoh did not set out to annihilate the column. He set out to recapture prisoners of []{#c1P3.xhtml_page_304}war, and there is no necessity whatsoever to commit his army to a pitched battle, let alone commit them to dangerous terrain where they could get bogged down in mud (see comments under 13:18). If Israel crossed the "Suez" region, all Pharaoh had to do was skirt the region southward; if 2,000 trained charioteers with Arabian horses couldn't outflank a column of 1,000,000 refugees, a greyhound can't outflank a bulldog.

["Entangled in the land, the wilderness hath shut them in"]{.class_s4N7-0} (vs. 3). To fulfill the requirements of the text, Israel must still be IN Egypt and must be penned up in a wilderness area where it is impossible for them to get out. [No location north of the present town of Suez can meet BOTH of the requirements of the text. ]{.class_s4N6}The ["Migdol"]{.class_s4N7-0} of verse 2 is said to be ["IN the land of Egypt"]{.class_s4N7-0} (Jer. 44:1), and Thompson is very much in error in identifying it as some "Migdol" on the [Via Maris,]{.class_s4N6} twenty miles east of Goshen. The Conservative scholar Thompson (as the other writers) is as anxious to lower the depth of the "Red Sea" as a Jesuit priest is to obtain Protestant tax money.

When the Jews get to Etham (13:20), Dr. Brugsch takes command of the column and steers it northward again, toward the Mediterranean, in spite of the instructions given by Jehovah[ ]{.class_s4NH}God in the text two verses before the mention of Etham. The turn is clearly [southward]{.class_s4N6}. If it were northward, no one would be in any ["wilderness,"]{.class_s4N7-0} nor could any ["wilderness\...shut them in."]{.class_s4N7-0} Dr. Brugsch's fantastic invention is the basis of Thompson's placing ["Migdol"]{.class_s4N7-0} on the [Via Maris]{.class_s4N6}---[which goes directly into "]{.class_s4N6}[the land of the Philistines"]{.class_s4N7-0}[!]{.class_s4N6} (This placement is based on Herodotus' guesswork that ["Magdolus"]{.class_s4N6} seems to represent ["Megiddo." "Magdolus,"]{.class_s4N6} in Hecataeus' Fr. 282 "Itin. Antonin," pg. 76, is located in this spot, but what this has to do with the MIGDOL of the text is a little obscure. To tell the truth about it, MIG and MAG are antonyms, not synonyms; they []{#c1P3.xhtml_page_305}match MINI and MAJU or MICRO and MAGNA---that is, [they are nothing similar.]{.class_s4N6})

Now there is a place in the land of Egypt where there is a ["wilderness,"]{.class_s4N7-0} and there is a place where this wilderness would box an army in and there would be no possible escape route but straight south for 500 miles. That place is the flat area on the west bank of the Red Sea where it runs along the eastern Egyptian border for nearly 700 miles. ["The edge of the wilderness"]{.class_s4N7-0} (13:20) is clearly the north boundary of this strip, and there is at least a night's and a day's journey (13:21) south of Etham (["in the edge of the wilderness"]{.class_s4N7-0}) before ["Pi-hahiroth\...over against Baal-zephon."]{.class_s4N7-0} On a forced march of 24 hours, this would be sixty miles, but that is a military figure. With a column of old men, women, and children, plus Hamites (!), one could reduce the rate of march to fifteen miles down the west bank of the Red Sea, and we have only figured the minimum. If two days and two nights elapsed (which is highly probable \[13:21--22\]), [Israel is fifty miles down the coast ]{.class_s4N6}with mountains and desert on their right and nothing but water on their left. Thus, they are clearly ["entangled in the land," ]{.class_s4N7-0}and the attempts of the Conservative and Liberal scholars to alter this truth are quite similar to the attempts of the Pharisees (Matt. 28:15) and the [NASV]{.class_s4N6} of 1963 (Luke 24:51--52) and the [RSV ]{.class_s4N6}of 1952 (Acts 1:3) to get rid of the risen body of Jesus Christ.

When the Israelites start through the waters of the Red Sea (not the soppy ground of the Suez marshes), Pharoah sees immediately what Dummelow, Clarke, Dummelow, Johnson, Thompson, Bullinger, Baentz, Schaff, Weigle, and Brugsch cannot see. If he (Pharaoh) is to recapture the column, he must drive the horses [straight ahead ]{.class_s4N6}(vss. 23--24). In order to outflank Israel in the situation where the [AV ]{.class_s4N6}1611 places them, Pharaoh must make a 140 mile detour. When this detour is made, he will be fifty miles behind the column, seventy miles []{#c1P3.xhtml_page_306}outside his own land, exposed to the Amalekites (17:8), and then he will find himself with 2,000--3,000 horses which have to be pastured where there is little or no water (see Exod. 15:24 and Num. 20:2). Pharaoh has more sense than the Conservative commentators and archaeologists, even if he did go to Hell when he got through. There is not much doubt about the approximate location of the crossing; it is between forty to sixty miles south of the northern extremity of the Red Sea, long before the Red Sea touches the locks of the Suez Canal.

["For Pharaoh will say"]{.class_s4N7-0} (vs. 3). That is, when he is told that the Jews have fled with no thought of returning---don't forget Exodus 5:1!---he will say verse 3. It is apparent, from verses 5 and 6 that this is just what Pharaoh says. As a matter of fact, everything from here on points to the perfect "setup" for Exodus 9:15. Pharaoh knew exactly how far away the Jews said they were going---["three day's journey"]{.class_s4N7-0} (5:3). They have been gone at least two days (see notes above). He can overtake them in 24 hours (vs. 9).

If the Jews had gone up to Bullinger's "Migdol" ([Companion Bible,]{.class_s4N6} pg. 91), they would have been gone ONE day at the time of verse 5, and they would have been ["entangled"]{.class_s4N7-0} nowhere. To help Bullinger out, the [American Translation ]{.class_s4N6}has changed ["entangled"]{.class_s4N7-0} to ["wandering aimlessly,"]{.class_s4N6} but we would expect this of ANY translation since 1885. [The new "Bible" translations are all designed to replace the Holy Bible with the private interpretations of destructive critics who worship "Christian education."]{.class_s4N6}

While on the subject of destructive criticism, one should observe the revival of the ancient cliché "in the original," occurring this time in the [Pulpit Commentary,]{.class_s4N6} Volume I. pg. 321, as touching verse 9. According to the writer, there is no ["and"]{.class_s4N7-0} in the "original," and therefore, we are to abandon the [AV ]{.class_s4N6}1611 for "all the chariot horses." Why the Masoretic text []{#c1P3.xhtml_page_307}refused to make this substitution and went ahead and followed the [AV ]{.class_s4N6}1611 is apparent when one considers that ALL OF THE FUNDAMENTAL EXPOSITORS' desire to cut down the size of Pharaoh's pursing force: if you can't lower the water level to degrade the miracle, at least cut down the roll-call of troops---[anything but believe the text!]{.class_s4N6}

The text "in the original" (to coin an ancient lie) says ["horses," "chariots,"]{.class_s4N7-0} and ["horsemen,"]{.class_s4N7-0} indicating cavalry as well as chariots. This is what the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N6} is trying hard to cover up: observe the second attack on the Holy Spirit by the writer as he insists that ["horsemen"]{.class_s4N7-0} should have been "mounted men; i.e., those who rode IN THE CHARIOTS."

Perhaps the greatest hindrance to Bible study, outside of common sense, is the lack of a [sense of humor. ]{.class_s4N6}If the average reader would approach the Conservative commentators with the same anticipation that he awaits a TV comedy serial, he would get a much clearer picture of the truth. For example, what you are to imagine in the fictitious Exodus chapters 13--14 fabricated by the Conservative and Liberal commentators is:

1\. A King of Egypt so stupid that he cannot get [around ]{.class_s4N6}a column of refugees.

2\. A dry seabed, which was a wet lakebed where the ebb tide leaves it dry enough to be called ["dry ground"]{.class_s4N7-0} (vs. 22), and then it got wet enough again, [in an hour,]{.class_s4N6} to drown grown men in three feet of water.

3\. A column ["entangled"]{.class_s4N7-0} in a ["wilderness,"]{.class_s4N7-0} which was located on [a main trade route]{.class_s4N6} through the most fertile part of the Delta.

4\. And this column is pursued by an armored unit that has stuffed horses, horsemen, riders, drivers, and an ARMY (vs. 9) into chariot boxes without any supporting cavalry.

(That's some "Bible" you wrote there, honey!)

How do these things happen? Well, one has to be familiar with the long history of Biblical criticism to flush all the rats out of the nest. The real trouble with the Conservatives who are trying to whittle down Pharaoh's forces to a couple of horse-drawn buggies is that they have gotten their noses bloodied before in trying to explain 2 Samuel 8:4; 2 Samuel 10:18; 1 Kings 10:26; 1 Chronicles 18:4; and 1 Chronicles 19:6. Unable to discern ["horsemen"]{.class_s4N7-0} who ride individual horses into combat from horsemen who take care of chariot horses, and soldiers who ride in chariots from soldiers who march, the Conservatives have, at last, given in to the demands of the Devil to alter the Reformation text; this is all done with the pious explanation that "the words are not in the ORIGINAL." The truth of the matter is ["the brains are not in the]{.class_s4N6} commentators."

["And it was told the king of Egypt that the people fled" ]{.class_s4N7-0}(vs. 5). Well, he knew they were leaving two days ago (12:31--33). The answer, then, to Pharaoh's change of heart does not lie in supposing that he felt like he had been deceived. The situation is, Pharaoh has spies in the column, and the mixed multitude is sending back reports on longitude, latitude, azimuth, and terrain. Pharaoh has just received news of the last encampment (vs. 2); he knows the location and the surrounding area. He knows that from this place they can only go in one of two directions: either straight on south until they are in Ethiopia, or they must return back to Etham and Goshen. They cannot go east, for they don't have any boats. If they go west, they go to a wilderness[ "in the land."]{.class_s4N7-0} They specifically told Pharaoh that the wilderness for which they were heading was OUTSIDE the land (5:1, 7:16).

Two factors, then, influence Pharaoh's decision (vs. 4).

1\. [They lied to me. T]{.class_s4N6}hey are not going into the Sinaitic wilderness to worship "Jehovah," or they would have gone east of Etham and turned down the Peninsula.

2\. Where they are,[ they are trapped,]{.class_s4N6} and I can catch them in 24 hours with a forced march.

["And he made ready his chariot\...and he took six hundred chosen chariots and all the chariots of Egypt\...and his horsemen" ]{.class_s4N7-0}(vss. 6--9). To correct the bamboozles of the Conservative critics, the Holy Spirit has preserved for us, in the original [Authorized ]{.class_s4N6}text[,]{.class_s4N6} ["the horse and his rider"]{.class_s4N7-0} (15:21). This is "very interesting" if none of the horses had "riders."

No, it is no Santa Anita Handicap that comes out after Moses and Aaron; it is 600 of the best chariots from divisional headquarters, plus 100-150 mounted cavalrymen. There are probably 900-1000 National Guard chariots accompanying the unit (see Josephus' comment under vs. 23).

Question! How in the world could Pharaoh have forgotten his dead son so quickly? Before a week is gone, the King of Egypt has forgotten the frogs, the lice, the flies, the hail, the boils, and the death of the firstborn. He is right back in the same old rut: ["Why have we\...let Israel go from serving us?"]{.class_s4N7-0} (vs. 5). [Sin is madness.]{.class_s4N6}

Whether or not Moses had lied to Pharaoh about WHERE the worship would be and whether or not the Jews were "absquattulating" or intending to return is beside the point. The point is, it was DEATH to have the Jews in the land (12:13), [and it was a blessing to get rid of them. ]{.class_s4N6}But Pharaoh was not the first man or the last who ever lost his life (and his soul) trying to prove a point (2 Kings 14:11); this temporary madness that descends and seizes men, at such times, is as common a thing as dewberries in south Alabama. There is no logic at all to Pharaoh's thought processes in verse 5. The only possible regret anyone could have would be the thought that he would have to work for a living now that the slaves were gone, but what man in []{#c1P3.xhtml_page_310}his right mind would want slave labor at the wage scale of 6:1 to 12:13? [Sin is madness.]{.class_s4N6}

["And he pursued after the children of Israel"]{.class_s4N7-0} (vs. 8). Satan does not retire to his palace to meditate after the blood-bought sinner has been redeemed by the Lamb; to the contrary, he mounts the attack and pursues immediately to bring the Christian back into bondage (Gal. 3--4). Some of the greatest religious systems in the world are nothing but Pharaoh's army disguised as "Christian" organizations. The Seventh-day Adventists, the Jehovah's Witnesses, the Mormons, the Roman Catholics, and the so-called "Church of Christ" (Campbellites) are nothing more than Satanic attempts to bring the new Christian back into BONDAGE. One will observe that most of these groups have a membership composed of professing Christians from other churches who were brought back to Egypt [after ]{.class_s4N6}they were saved. The Catholics have a more effective system: each generation is born in bondage by virtue of its family ties with the Vatican State.

Moses' rear guard reports. They come sailing into the encampment with their horses foam-flecked and shaking, and before they are out of their saddles they are crying, ["Moses! Moses!]{.class_s4N6} Do you know what is happening?"

Moses shakes his head and says, "Naw, man, I've been out of it for seven years. How in the world would I know what's happening? I've seen so many frogs, flies, lice, locusts, and dead cows I could\...."

"Pharaoh's coming!"

"Pharaoh's coming?"

"Yes, Moses, we put out a recon patrol last night, and this morning, we made contact with his lead scouts about 0700, and man, he's coming like the north wind on a nasty day!"

"Oh good night nurse! He'll butcher us!"

"You ain't kiddin', brother! Them chariots are souped up with four-barrel carburetors, power packs, and one-inch cams; and they're coming like a tomcat spittin' tobacco juice!"

["Pharaoh, and his horsemen, and his army\...overtook them encamping by the sea\...before Baal-zephon"]{.class_s4N7-0} (vs. 9). In closing the account, one should observe that the Jewish tradition places the crossing of the Red Sea on the twenty-first of Abib; this is SEVEN DAYS after the Passover (cf. 12:6), which would make SIX DAYS of traveling. If this is true, much of the preceding account becomes even clearer. In the first place, Pharaoh sees that they are not going "three days" into the wilderness, but further than that. This has a bearing on his decision. It certainly would have taken Pharaoh a day or so to muster his troops and overtake the refugees in pursuit. In this case, the children of Israel travel [two more days before Pharaoh catches them]{.class_s4N6} (this would give the pillar of cloud and fire plenty of time to be used in guiding the column---13:21).

But, alas! this would mean that the crossing of the Red Sea was such a tremendous miracle that all hope of placing it in the "Suez region" would be gone forever, for SEVEN DAYS of travel at a snail's pace---say ten miles a day and five miles per night---would place the Hebrew column more than [100 miles down the coast. ]{.class_s4N6}At that point, the Red Sea is more than twenty miles wide.

If you believe the Bible at all, you are left with the distinct impression that Israel crosses the Red Sea more than fifty miles south of the Suez area. The problem of getting across a stretch of land ten to twenty miles wide has been exaggerated beyond the wildest imagination (by Conservatives and Liberals alike) by assuming that 1,000,000 people had to march single file. With an encampment of 1,000,000 people beside a seashore (opposite Marah and Elim), the width of the camp would be less than five miles if it were ["by the sea."]{.class_s4N7-0} This means the encampment (north to south) is probably ten miles long. The []{#c1P3.xhtml_page_312}Israelites cross the Sea in ranks of 200 at a minimum. With each rank (crosswise) numbering 5,000 men, a column of 200 ranks will do nicely.

The march starts in the evening, and it is a twelve-hour march (15:23, 27). Any column of 2,000 yards---that is giving ten-yards room for each rank---can march ten to twenty miles in twelve hours when they are being pursued by an armed enemy. A conservative picture of the whole event would make the Exodus sixty miles south of Suez with a whole camp moving sidewise across the sea. The same movement is found in the miliary command, "By the right flank---march!" On such a command, a column of men four-men [wide ]{.class_s4N6}and twelve-men [long ]{.class_s4N6}converts to a column four-men [long ]{.class_s4N6}and twelve-men [wide]{.class_s4N6}. The column of the Exodus moves 5,000 abreast and runs 200 deep (or 2,000 abreast and 500 deep) and completes a twelve-mile stretch in less than ten hours.

14:10 "And when Pharaoh drew nigh, the children of Israel lifted up their eyes, and, behold, the Egyptians marched after them; and they were sore afraid: and the children of Israel cried out unto the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}.

11 And they said unto Moses, Because there were no graves in Egypt, hast thou taken us away to die in the wilderness? wherefore hast thou dealt thus with us, to carry us forth out of Egypt?

12 Is not this the word that we did tell thee in Egypt, saying, Let us alone, that we may serve the Egyptians? For it had been better for us to serve the Egyptians, than that we should die in the wilderness.

13 And Moses said unto the people, Fear ye not, stand still, and see the salvation of the LORD, which he will shew to you to day: for the Egyptians whom ye have seen to day, ye shall see them again no more for ever.

14 The[ ]{.class_s4ND}[Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}shall fight for you, and ye shall hold your peace.

15 And the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}said unto Moses, Wherefore criest thou unto me? speak unto the children of Israel, that they go forward:

16 But lift thou up thy rod, and stretch out thine hand over the sea, and divide it: and the children of Israel shall go on dry ground through the midst of the sea.

17 And I, behold, I will harden the hearts of the Egyptians, and they shall follow them: and I will get me honour upon Pharaoh, and upon all his host, upon his chariots, and upon his horsemen.

18 And the Egyptians shall know that I am the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}, when I have gotten me honour upon Pharaoh, upon his chariots, and upon his horsemen.

19 And the angel of God, which went before the camp of Israel, removed and went behind them; and the pillar of the cloud went from before their face, and stood behind them:

20 And it came between the camp of the Egyptians and the camp of Israel; and it was a cloud and darkness to them, but it gave light by night to these: so that the one came not near the other all the night.

21 And Moses stretched out his hand over the sea; and the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}caused the sea to go back by a strong east wind all that night, and made the sea dry land, and the waters were divided.

22 And the children of Israel went into the midst of the sea upon the dry ground: and the waters were a wall unto them on their right hand, and on their left."

We have already commented at length on verses 10, 17--18. ["The children of Israel lifted up their eyes" ]{.class_s4N7-0}(vs. 10). Now this is exactly the thing to do in an emergency (Luke 21:28; Num. 21:9), but this time, they are looking away from the Author and Finisher of their faith (Heb. 12:1--2) and are observing the opposition (Eccl. 11:4--6).

["And they were sore afraid: and\...cried out unto the ]{.class_s4N7-0}[Lord ]{.class_s4N8-1}["]{.class_s4N7-0} (vs. 10). Simon Peter goes through the same thing in Matthew 14:30. Now it is Israel's turn to "repent" (vs. 11). The murmuring of 1 Corinthians 10:10 starts here and never ceases until Joshua gets through with his complaining (Josh. 7:7--9).

["Wherefore hast thou dealt thus with us\...?" ]{.class_s4N7-0}(vs. 11). This is just the echo of 5:22, so now the Lord is giving Israel a taste of what He gave their leader Moses. Moses' deliverance appears to be (in the eyes of the refugees) a total flop, and it would seem from the text that none of them remembered 5:21, where they had accused him of being a flop when he started. Both of the "flops" were interludes in which God placed Pharaoh in positions where He would be justified in dealing with him as He did (Psa. 51:4; Rom. 3:4). Now God is "setting up the pins" in the other alley for a strike. ["I will get me honour upon Pharaoh, and upon all his host"]{.class_s4N7-0} (vs. 17).

["For it had been better for us to serve the Egyptians"]{.class_s4N7-0} (vs. 12). Isn't it amazing how "time cures all wounds"? Isn't it remarkable how [time ]{.class_s4N6}has a way of obliterating the terrors of the past and paints them, instead, in rosy colors that smell of aloes, cassia, and "all pleasant spices"?

To a man over seventy, "the good old days" were always the "GOOD old days," until he tries to teach a generation gap-TV brainwashed idiot the lessons of discipline, patience, hard work, frugality, and reliability; then all is changed. Then it becomes: "Why, back in my day, my boy, we didn't think nothin' of a fifty-hour work week!"

And the kid says, "I don't think much of it either."

Then it becomes: "Why, son, we walked to school Fall, Spring, and Winter when I was a boy; and that was three miles each way! What do you think God gave you two feet for, anyway?"

"One for the gas pedal and one for the brake," says the kid.

Along with memories of "suppertime" and "the old homestead," there is little memory of back-breaking jobs, heart-breaking deaths, body-breaking sicknesses, chilly nights, broken pumps, cuts and bruises, cold shaving water, lack of fresh fruit and sweets, and no cash. All that is left is the smell of mown hay or the whistling of robins and nightingales or the bottled-up ecstasy of a lark uncorking itself in early April. The smell of the fireplace with its smouldering embers, the sound of the wagon wheels creaking on the "old ox road," the glorious splash of white on the dogwood trees, and the feel of heavy patchwork quilts still linger decades after the "old timer" has forgotten making his own soap, boiling water for the laundry, splitting rails for the fences, repairing the traces, burying the baby, sweeping the dirt floor, weeping over a childhood sweetheart, and the weekly visits to the woodshed for a "parental confrontation and meaningful dialogue, etc."

["For it had been better for us to serve the Egyptians"]{.class_s4N7-0} (vs. 12). [Sin is madness. ]{.class_s4N6}Unbelief is sin no matter where it appears (see comments under vss. 6--9). Would you really rather live as a slave laborer under the Devil than die in fellowship with God [under grace?]{.class_s4N6}

Would you rather be "red" than "dead"? Wasn't it Patrick Henry who said: "Give me liberty or give me death"? But a servitude [far away]{.class_s4N6} seems preferable to a [death ]{.class_s4N6}at hand (Num. 14:2). And besides, this generation went from the original to "Give me liberty---[period"]{.class_s4N6} more than twenty years ago; in the last ten years it has been quoted as "GIVE ME"!

["Fear ye not, stand still, and see the salvation of the ]{.class_s4N7-0}[Lord]{.class_s4N8-1}[" ]{.class_s4N7-0}(vs. 13). This ["stand still"]{.class_s4N7-0} (as the "stand still" of Josh. 10:12) is not an admonition to sit down and do nothing; it is an admonition to quit running around tearing your hair and rest in the Lord (Rom. 4:5). Moses speaks these words by the same authority found in the words of verse 4. Although God hasn't told Moses WHAT he is going to do to Pharaoh and his hosts, He has promised to deal with Pharaoh, and Moses believes it.

["The ]{.class_s4N7-0}[Lord ]{.class_s4N8-1}[shall fight for you, and ye shall hold your peace" ]{.class_s4N7-0}(vs. 14). What Clarke mistakes for prophecy (["the Egyptians whom ye have seen to day, ye shall see them again no more for ever,"]{.class_s4N7-0} vs. 13) turns out to be Moses' own guesswork. They do see them again: once in pursuit and again dead all over the seashore (vs. 30).

The problem is that, according to verse 15, Moses is also howling for help. Now, since there is no [reference ]{.class_s4N6}in the preceding verses to Moses doing any praying, and since it [appears ]{.class_s4N6}from verse 13 to verse 14 that Moses is not worried about anything, Bullinger calls verse 15 an "Erotesis" or "Hysteresis." Unable to find these words in a dictionary, the Syriac version has helped God out by inserting ["Moses then cried to Jehovah"]{.class_s4N6} BEFORE ["wherefore."]{.class_s4N6}

The text is clear---as usual---and it is perfectly apparent that Moses has gone to the Lord in prayer, following verses 13--14, exactly as he does on numerous occasions (e.g., 5:22, 6:12; Num. 16:15, etc.).

["Lord?!]{.class_s4N6} Lord, what on earth are we gonna do? When Pharaoh hits the rear end of this column, it's going to look like Custer's last stand!"

"Well, why don't you all just cross over the ocean?"

"Oh, Lord, you must be joking! You don't mean that!"

"Well, I don't know, now. Those folks in your camp think they're real smart, and they think I made a mistake in taking []{#c1P3.xhtml_page_317}them out of Egypt. If they're so all-fired smart, let 'em figure a way to cross it."

"Oh, Lord God, this is no time for levity! Why, we don't have any boats or rafts, not even a canoe! We don't have a ferryboat, and nobody knows how to ski! We don't have any paddle boards, and none of us have been surfing for over 400 years! Now, Lord, you know we can't do that!"

"Oh, you might work it out. Why not just step on top of the water and [walk ]{.class_s4N6}across?"

["Oh, Lord! Oh, God!]{.class_s4N6} Oh\...!!" \[For a similar situation see Gen. 32:24 and comments in the [Commentary on Genesis]{.class_s4N6}.\]

In the meantime, to the tune of Rossini's "William Tell Overture," the hosts of Pharaoh are thundering out past Etham, down the coastal plain to Pi-hahiroth. The galloping of the horses raises a cloud of sand and dust as high as Mount Vesuvius. Up in the lead chariot, Pharaoh is yelling: ["Drag it, man! ]{.class_s4N6}Put it on the floor board! Is that all you can get out of this wreck? [Step on it!"]{.class_s4N6}

The chief vizier is standing beside him so pale that one would mistake him for a Shemite. "But Pharaoh, yo' majesty! We uns is already gwine so fast dem telephone poles looks like a picket fence!"

"Never mind!" screams Pharaoh, "Step on the gas! [Giddap!]{.class_s4N6} Hyaah theyah! Move! [Move!"]{.class_s4N6}

Back at the camp, Moses is still trying to ring the right number. Finally, the Lord answers: "Well, don't talk to [me ]{.class_s4N6}about it. Talk to THEM about it" (vs. 15: ["speak unto the children of Israel"]{.class_s4N7-0}).

"What'll I say, Lord?"

"Tell them to pull stakes, pack up their goods, load the women and kids on the wagons, and then follow you."

"W-w-where am I going, Lord?"

["You're going to a mass baptism, son" ]{.class_s4N6}(1 Cor. 10:1--2).

"But the ocean, Lord---[the ocean!!"]{.class_s4N6}

"What about the ocean? What have you got in your hand, Moses?" (4:2)

Moses slaps his forehead, snaps his fingers, and his eyes light up like someone pulled the switch. ["The rod!]{.class_s4N6} I still have the rod! Why good grief, great goodness\...!"

["Careful, ]{.class_s4N6}careful there, Moses. You almost said it. No cussin'!"

"Oh, pardon me, Lord. The rod! Why, I had forgotten all about it!"

"You see there, Moses, I even have to do your thinking for you. You spent so much time trying to locate Baal-zephon in your lexicon, you forgot about the rod. Now, I'll tell you what to do. You go out there and tell those folks to get ready to travel. Pharaoh will camp tonight five miles north of your column, and I'll keep the pillar between you and him so he won't pick off any of your stragglers (vs. 20). Give your people two hours to be ready to travel; then you step out to the high-water mark and raise that rod, and I'll do the rest."

["And Moses stretched out his hand over the sea; and the ]{.class_s4N7-0}[Lord ]{.class_s4N8-1}[caused the sea to go back by a strong east wind all that night, and made the sea dry land"]{.class_s4N7-0} (vs. 21). The reader will observe that the ground was dry, not "marshy," and that the wind performed the work, not a "tide." There is about as much similarity between Dummelow's account of the crossing and the Bible account as there is between the Roman Mass and the Biblical Lord's Supper (see the comments given under 13:18 and 14:2).

From the top of the mountains (about five miles inland), the children of Israel had seen the moving column of dust and troops at a distance of about twenty miles. Now as the shadows lengthen, the pillar of fire appears between the north end of Israel's column and the lead elements of Pharaoh's []{#c1P3.xhtml_page_319}Headquarter's Company. What appears as light to [the believer]{.class_s4N6} (Isa. 28:9) appears as a low-flying thunderhead to the Egyptians (Isa. 28:13), and they suddenly find a smoke screen across their path which makes it impossible for the patrols to "maintain contact" with the enemy. Pharaoh, much to his dislike, pitches camp and vows by Seth, Isis, Osiris, Thoth, Ptah, Seb, Mary, Joseph, and Blessed John the Baptist that he will land on his prey by the time the sun is up.

At sundown, or shortly before, Moses steps out in front of a rank of people who stretch nearly five miles to his right and four miles to his left. Moses and Aaron walk out on a promitory of sand. Moses stops with his feet touching the water, and---[up goes that rod!]{.class_s4N6}

There is a hissing and a boiling and a sucking sound, like a huge bath tub emptying into a drain. The assembled hosts of Israel stand trembling, and the forward ranks gaze ahead of them like men in a state of shock. Along the whole length of the front rank the waters are receding. The men there (standing on the extreme north and south end of the camp) nearly faint, for they perceive that as the waters recede [directly in front of them,]{.class_s4N6} the waters remain lapping on the beach one foot on [either side of them!]{.class_s4N6} No one has to be told what to do. Moses and Aaron are fifty yards out on a sand bar that was formerly six feet under water. As the stunned Israelites stumble forward into the abyss, they notice that the clam shells and stones on the bed of the ocean are as dry as the cactus of the desert.

As the waters move out, the column moves in. As the east wind blows over the passage, the water recedes before them. With shouts of victory and prayers of repentance---[for their lack of faith!]{.class_s4N6}---the redeemed horde walks, then runs, then jumps up and down, as they go down into a canyon of water. Those on the flanks run with their hearts in their mouths and the adrenaline  pumping through their bodies like water going through the city []{#c1P3.xhtml_page_320}main. When they jostle against one another, occasionally one of them bumps into the ["wall"]{.class_s4N7-0} (vs. 22) and gets [wet!]{.class_s4N6} Two miles across the ocean bed they find themselves gazing up at waves standing 100 feet high, waving their fingers of foam and spray in the wind; Israel was baptised by [aspersion ]{.class_s4N6}(1 Cor. 10:1--2).

Those looking straight up from the center of the column learn that they are buried in water, for directly over the opening there hangs a [cloud ]{.class_s4N6}(1 Cor. 10:1--2). Isaiah says the children of Israel were ["brought up out of the sea" ]{.class_s4N7-0}(Isa. 63:11) exactly as Jesus came ["up straightway out of the water"]{.class_s4N7-0} (Matt. 3:16). This clearly shows four things that most of the commentators missed.

1\. Water baptism is by IMMERSION, and if the walls of water to the right and left of Israel were not high enough to cover them a minimum of seven feet, then 1 Corinthians 10:1--2 is wrong.

2\. Any baptism that does not put you [down into something]{.class_s4N6} and bring you back [up from it]{.class_s4N6} is not water baptism.

3\. Water baptism begins under GRACE, not the Law, and it is not a [ceremonial commandment. ]{.class_s4N6}Nor is it a ["carnal ordinance"]{.class_s4N6} or a ["washing"]{.class_s4N6} (Heb. 6:2), no matter how hard Stam and Baker try to get rid of it.

4\. Water baptism (immersion underneath water) is plainly ["from heaven"]{.class_s4N7-0} (Matt. 21:25), for when John the Baptist wishes to identify the "prophet like unto Moses" (Deut. 18:15), he baptizes in WATER.

The baptism is ["unto Moses"]{.class_s4N7-0} (1 Cor. 10:1--2); it occurs BEFORE the giving of the Law, exactly as the [tithing ]{.class_s4N6}of Abraham begins before the Law. This baptism is a perfect anti-type for the baptism of the Philippian jailer. In either case, the man was saved BEFORE he went into the water; in either case, he believed on the Lamb BEFORE he was baptized; and in either case, the baptism pictured the death and burial of a sinner under []{#c1P3.xhtml_page_321}the wrath of God (1 Pet. 3:21). Whereas the blood-bought sinner emerges alive from the wrath of God (vs. 29), the sinner who serves Pharaoh and ["the god of this world" ]{.class_s4N7-0}(2 Cor. 4:4) stays under the wrath until he is dead (vs. 30; Rom. 6:23). We go "under" at birth and can only "come up" if we are baptized into that One (Rom. 6:3--4) who went under (Psa. 42:7) and lived to tell about it (Eph. 4:9--10 cf. Luke 24:45). Water baptism, then, is a picture of death, burial, and resurrection. The Jew who was misled into thinking that it was ["purification"]{.class_s4N6} was misled by men like Bullinger who kept insisting that "the washing of pots and pans" qualifies for "baptism." The Levitical "sprinklings" for [purification of sins ]{.class_s4N6}(see John 2:6) had no connection with Exodus chapter 14 at all.

Over they go! At midnight, the huge army of Israelites with its ["mixed multitude"]{.class_s4N7-0} is ten miles across the ocean (at a rate of less than three miles per hour), and it was, undoubtedly, the greatest walking prayer meeting held in the history of the nation. (Even at the rate of one mile per hour, the column could have crossed a twelve mile stretch of ocean bed before Pharaoh could get across. Pharaoh does not plunge into the abyss until around five in the morning; by sunrise \[vs. 27\], he is a drowned rat. We realize that in the movie "The Ten Commandments," Pharaoh got back to his palace, but when could Hollywood ever get ANYTHING straight?)

Only a Liberal (or Conservative!) scholar could fail to understand the passage. An out-and-out miracle is being presented, without apologies to anyone, and any man who would apologize for it must alter the account to fit the apology.

The crossing is in ["the midst of the sea"]{.class_s4N7-0} (vs. 22); the sea returns to its[ "STRENGTH" ]{.class_s4N7-0}(vs. 27); in the middle of the sea it is ["DRY"]{.class_s4N7-0} (vs. 22); and the water has [two sides ]{.class_s4N6}(vs. 22), showing the childish stupidity of Conservative scholarship in prating about "tides." [Show me one tide on any lake in the world that []{#c1P3.xhtml_page_322}parts in the MIDDLE, leaving water on either side of where the tide "dropped."]{.class_s4N6} Nonsense.

That isn't all; the waters were DIVIDED (vs. 21) They did not "ebb" or "flow," and no water did anything until one man raised a stick. It worked the opposite way the [second time]{.class_s4N6} the man raised the stick (vss. 16, 27). [Now there is a real "tide" for you!]{.class_s4N6}

Now, if you want to remain respectable and go on passing for a distinctive scholar in the "Union," whose "considered opinion" is valuable in "re-evaluating the trustworthiness of the account," then go to work and alter those verses and dream up some cock-and-bull story like ["otogeny recapitulates phylogeny,"]{.class_s4N6} and perhaps you will get a higher salary teaching at the next "Christian" University. However, if you've got enough manhood to follow the instructions of Hebrews 13:13 and Galatians 1:10, and you are more concerned with what the Lord Jesus thinks about it (John 5:39, 44;, Luke 24:25, 45) than where your next pay check is coming from, put the account down as a [genuine miracle]{.class_s4N6} which took place by a direct intervention of God Almighty. And if you can't make that grade, then go back to teaching Einstein, Pogo, Huxley, Lil' Abner, Darwin, Mother Goose, Marx, the Green Hornet, Freud, Spider Man, and other assorted fairy tales for "adults." But don't teach us any Bible. [Please!]{.class_s4N6}

We don't have nearly the trouble in believing the Biblical account that we do in getting 1,000,000 people hung up in a swamp, after taking [seven ]{.class_s4N6}days to travel [twenty ]{.class_s4N6}miles, and then being attacked from the rear by chariots, where horses and riders have been stuffed into the carts and where the captain doesn't have enough sense to wait [for a change in the tide!]{.class_s4N6} Why do it the hard way, brother?

14:23 "And the Egyptians pursued, and went in after them to the midst of the sea, even all Pharaoh's horses, his chariots, and his horsemen.

24 And it came to pass, that in the morning watch the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}looked unto the host of the Egyptians through the pillar of fire and  of the cloud, and troubled the host of the Egyptians,

25 And took off their chariot wheels, that they drave them heavily: so that the Egyptians said, Let us flee from the face of Israel; for the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}fighteth for them against the Egyptians.

26 And the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}said unto Moses, Stretch out thine hand over the sea, that the waters may come again upon the Egyptians, upon their chariots, and upon their horsemen.

27 And Moses stretched forth his hand over the sea, and the sea returned to his strength when the morning appeared; and the Egyptians fled against it; and the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}overthrew the Egyptians in the midst of the sea.

28 And the waters returned, and covered the chariots, and the horsemen, and all the host of Pharaoh that came into the sea after them; there remained not so much as one of them.

29 But the children of Israel walked upon dry land in the midst of the sea; and the waters were a wall unto them on their right hand, and on their left.

30 Thus the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}saved Israel that day out of the hand of the Egyptians; And Israel saw the Egyptians dead upon the sea shore.

31 And Israel saw that great work which the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}did upon the Egyptians: and the people feared the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}, and believed the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}, and his servant Moses."

Now, the drama which began in Exodus chapters 3--6 breaks out into the final scene of "Act Three." What began as a veiled threat against the life of the ["firstborn,"]{.class_s4N7-0} ends in the total annihilation of an army which Josephus lists as being 50,000 men on horses (including chariots) and 200,000 footmen.

["The morning watch"]{.class_s4N7-0} of verse 24 would be 2--6 a.m. in the Old Testament (Judg. 7:19), and 3--6 a.m. in the New Testament (Mark 13:35). (There was a switch from[ three watches]{.class_s4N6} of four hours each in the Old Testament to [four watches]{.class_s4N6} of three hours each in the New Testament.)

At 3 a.m., Pharaoh's army, with his cavalry and chariots, is on the move; by 4:30 a.m., his cavalry has passed through the debris of the previous night's encampment. By the looks of things, the children of Israel have gotten out in a hurry. It is still fairly dark. As Pharaoh's forward units scour the area, sending patrols back into the hills, Pharaoh rants, raves, and storms up and down in front of his troops.

"They couldn't have gotten out! If they broke camp, they're not ten miles down the beach. All right, if you can't find them, back in the saddle! Assemble! [Assemble!"]{.class_s4N6}

About that time, a platoon that has been searching the shoreline comes up to Pharaoh. A pale and breathless staff sergeant says, "Your Majesty! [There is no ocean on our left flank!"]{.class_s4N6}

"Courtmartial him!" roars Pharaoh, "Drunk on duty!!"

Before the M.P.s can seize the sergeant, there are 500 voices affirming the truth of the matter. It is now 5:10 a.m., and Pharaoh is beginning to see the beach. For a moment he thinks a fog or low cloud bank is playing tricks on his vision. Now he realizes that he is not only facing a bare stretch of beach running hundreds of yards out to the east, but in the early morning hours, he has unwittingly bypassed nearly a mile of beach that had neither tide nor waves touching it.

Cautiously, Pharaoh leads his army out across the inner bar and then the outer bar. The sand is as dry as a dune in Florida on the Fourth of July. The reports begin to come in from the flanking units. It is apparent that the Red Sea has not disappeared overnight, for on the left flank, the cavalry is reporting a wall of water getting higher and higher. It is about 5:25 a.m. The sun has not yet risen, but it is broad daylight for all practical purposes. Now the situation is clear. At 5:40 a.m., number one and number two scouts come galloping back from running more than two miles ahead of the main body of troops. They have spotted the rear end of a vast column of people moving eastward.

Pharaoh claps his hands and hollers, "All right, good! We got 'em! We got 'em! Let's go!"

"But y-y-y-yo' M-m-majesty ! " says General Mohammed Ali Elijah Allah King, "We, we, we ain't gwine down into DAT, is we?" He points at the opening ahead. On either side, going into the vanishing point of perspective on the horizon, are [walls ]{.class_s4N6}of water. At Pharaoh's feet they are six feet high, but ten miles further out they tower hundreds of feet into the air.

"Sure we are," snarls Pharaoh, "We're going in after them just like a submarine!"

"B-b-but yo' Majesty, we doesn't want to get on no ship dat sinks on purpose!"

"Why, you bunch of chicken-livered, yellow-bellied blankety-blanks! [Watchee here!"]{.class_s4N6} Pharaoh dismounts from the chariot (15:19, 21), leaps into the saddle of a thoroughbred Arabian steed (Rev. 6:2), and cries, "Giddyap!" He rockets down into the ocean bed going down a long slope for about 400 yards; he then reins up short, turns around in the saddle, and swinging one arm over his head he screams: "Cooomoon! Follooow Meee!" [And in they go]{.class_s4N6} (vs. 23)!

Thirty minutes have passed since Pharaoh's entrance into the trough (cf. Psa. 136:15), and as the non-coms goad the chariots forward at double time, Pharaoh leads his cavalry over the ocean bed like a steeple chase. Things are going fine, if you look at it as an Egyptian, until---POW!

Pharaoh almost goes over the mane of his charger. He pulls the horses up and asks first one and then another, "What was that? What was that?"

Pow! Bloop! Blap!

"Blowouts, your Majesty!"

"Blowouts?!"

"Yes, your Majesty. Look behind you. One hundred chariots are out of commission, and they are blocking the passage of the others. We'll have to jack up the chariots and change tires."

"Change tires?"

"Yes, your Majesty. You see the oyster and clam shells have punctured the\...."

And then the crack of doom sounds in the ears of Pharaoh and his troops. There is a deep gurgling that sounds like something between the hiss of a steam valve and sucking of water down a drain. Pharaoh's face turns upward, and he screams: "THE WAVES!"

There were still a dozen ranks of Israelites coming up out of the ocean bed when the first wall of water collapsed; it collapsed a mile behind the rear of the Israelites' column. Instead of rushing forward on either side and filling up the opening, this wall of water closed the opening between Pharaoh and Israel, and the next wall of water that collapsed fell in [Pharaoh's compartment.]{.class_s4N6}

As the sun came over the horizon (vs. 27), the huge cliffs of water on either side of Pharaoh's army suddenly rose like gigantic hands reaching out for them. The fingers of foam and spray blew out over the heads of the column, and then the []{#c1P3.xhtml_page_327}whole ocean engulfed them. The mountains of water---many of them made of waves 200 feet high---collapsed, bashing out brains, mashing horses, splintering chariots, breaking bones, and beating minds into unconsciousness. Those in the rear of the column survived the longest, some of them being able to swim to the top, but armor drowned 70% of them as the first wave of twenty feet went over them.

With ear drums ringing and lungs bursting, Pharaoh's horsemen drowned in the Red Sea (15:4). A few temporary survivors, in shallow water, held their breath and tried to get to the top, but they were met with two bodies of water meeting each other at about seventy miles an hour. In the whirlpools, eddies, and currents of the catastrophe, they panicked, swallowed water, crashed into floating pieces of wood, and perished. By 7:00 in the morning, the last Israelite had gained the far bank of the Red Sea, and by ten in the morning, all they could see of Pharaoh and his glory was a score of corpses which washed up on the beach. As the beasts of the earth and the fowls of the air will devour the glory of ["the god of this world"]{.class_s4N7-0} (2 Cor. 4:4) at the last battle (Rev. 19:17--21), so the fishes and the crabs of the ocean breakfasted on Pharaoh and his army.

(You say, "Now, Brother Ruckman, that is stretching things a little, isn't it?" Well, not as much as Clarke, Ellicott, Lange, Dummelow, Brown, Williams, and the [New Bible Commentary]{.class_s4N6} stretch things; but it might be a "little"!)

Psalm 136:15 says that Pharaoh was destroyed with his troops. Exodus 15:1, 21 states that someone rode horses into the sea and did not "ride in chariots." Notwithstanding, the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N6} goes right on with the nonsense and insists that verse 28 should read "and the [chariot men"]{.class_s4N6} instead of ["horsemen."]{.class_s4N7-0} Who is stretching what?

The corrupt [Septuagint ]{.class_s4N6}has written "clogged the axles of their chariots" for ["took off their chariot wheels"]{.class_s4N7-0} (vs. 25). []{#c1P3.xhtml_page_328}Is this stretching things any further than "puncturing their inner tubes"? What is the difference? Either way you alter what God said. Why is the Alexandrian alteration [respectable ]{.class_s4N6}and the [plain American]{.class_s4N6} revolting?

The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N6} has placed the whole scene in the "Bitter Lakes" north of Suez (about eighty miles from where the Bible scene took place). Why is it that cultured infidelity (that doesn't blush to call God a liar) is considered to be "scholarly," while a crude, updated picture of reality is considered to be "unscholarly"?

Aaron walks quietly up to Moses' side, and the two survey the beach for four or five minutes. Here and there, Israelites are picking up weapons and pieces of armor from the corpses. The waters have rolled back together as neatly as a woman folds a tablecloth and puts it back in the cupboard.

"Beats anything I ever saw in my life!" says Aaron. "We saw some things back in Egypt, but this takes the cake! God help us if we ever forget this one!"

"Make sure you don't," says Moses. "You'd better put it down in your little black book. You do have a [little black book, ]{.class_s4N6}don't you?"

"Sure, sure; everybody does, don't they?"

"Well, just make sure it's a [black ]{.class_s4N6}one; lotta imitations runnin' around these days."

"And the people feared the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}, and believed the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}, and his servant Moses"[ (vs. 31). The last verse is the most important one. The Jews had seen a genuine miracle, so their reaction matches their first reaction on seeing the one in 4:30--31. No amount of "ebb tides" sweeping across "marshy ground" and "Bitter Lakes" could have possibly produced this reaction.]{.class_s4N5-1}

They believed Moses, for Moses had said that the LORD would do the fighting (vs. 14), and they had seen the Man of []{#c1P3.xhtml_page_329}War (15:3) in action. No one  attributed the victory to tides, winds, lakes, marshes, clogged axles, or any other fool thing that some dumb, stupid, backslidden, carnal, God-defying, Bible-rejecting "dedicated Conservative" scholar invented to make the Israelites look like a bunch of superstitious heathen. Of this group of egotistical asses---they all profess to believe in the "God-breathed" originals!---Jesus said: ["If ye believe not his writings, how shall ye believe my words?" ]{.class_s4N7-0}(John 5:47). The ["his"]{.class_s4N7-0} in the passage just cited is [Moses. ]{.class_s4N6}The way that Conservative pigheads answer this accusation is the ancient Roman Catholic answer of A.D. 325: "We don't believe your WORDS; we believe the 'fundamentals of the faith' extracted from the Bible." There are very few places in the Bible (see Col. 2:8 and comments in the [Commentary on Galatians--Colossians]{.class_s4N6}) where the apostasy of Conservative scholars is demonstrated any better than in this chapter we have just finished.

You see, all egotistical scholars (saved or lost) desire to be recognized as having vast learning and keen minds. This truth is applicable to nearly any scholar of any persuasion, and the New Birth rarely affects it one way or another. Not even the best of the premillennial brains which were consecrated to God---Lewis Sperry Chafer, Harry Rimmer, Wilbur Smith, Kenneth Wuest, R. B. Thieme, J. P. Lange, C. I. Scofield, R. A. Torrey, and E. Schuyler English---could resist the temptation to replace the authority of the [Authorized Version]{.class_s4N6} with their own humanistic studies in the Greek grammars, written originally by unregenerate Greek grammarians.

Now to be recognized for having a "keen mind" and "vast learning," or having "intellectual insight," one is required to acquaint himself with the thoughts and findings of unregenerate historians, musicians, artists, authors, philosophers, poets, scientists, linguists, archaeologists, textual critics, Bible revisers, []{#c1P3.xhtml_page_330}and scholars. But! all unregenerate people are controlled by ["the prince of the power of the air" ]{.class_s4N7-0}(Eph. 2:1--3); they are part of a system that lies in the wicked one (1 John 5:19). By nature they instinctively tend to [magnify ]{.class_s4N6}what God [ignores ]{.class_s4N6}(Luke 16:15) and ignore what [God ]{.class_s4N6}magnifies (Psa. 138:2). Therefore, exposure to this learning will eventually mold and shape the Conservative Christian scholar to the place where he will, instinctively, put too much emphasis on what God [ignored]{.class_s4N6} and too little emphasis on what God [magnified]{.class_s4N6}.

There is only one way that a Conservative Christian scholar can expose himself to this deadly plague without becoming infected: he must judge [all ]{.class_s4N6}writings, findings, comments, revisions, alterations, additions, subtractions, discoveries, inventions, findings, and publications by [one fixed, infallible standard. ]{.class_s4N6}However, there is no evidence that any Conservative Christian scholar since 1880 followed this procedure, with the possible exception of three or four men who have been given no publicity in any Christian school since that date. Nearly all (98%) of the saved scholars since 1880 judged the infallible standard by the information they dug up from [somewhere else.]{.class_s4N6}

Nowhere in the Bible are these truths demonstrated any better than in Exodus chapters 13--14 at the Red Sea crossing, for this is the greatest event that took place on this earth since the Flood; with the exception of "Joshua's long day" (Josh. 10:13), it was the most momentous occasion in history up to the resurrection of Jesus Christ. The attempts, therefore, to reduce the miracle to the class of "migrant farm workers' escape from sheriff's possee near the Okefenokee Swamp" is to openly confess that one is a frightened child trying to conform to the world system for fear of being labeled a "literalist," "Bibliolater," or "pre-scientific." (Note the remarks in the first two chapters of [Satan's Masterpiece,]{.class_s4N6} 1972, concerning inferiority complexes and stage fright.)

The fact that there are no secular documents of any authority bearing record to this shattering event means nothing at all. The world system has never kept close tabs on ANYTHING God emphasized, and the scholars' writings themselves bear witness to this everlasting truth.

Now, haven't you noticed something about this monkey business? Haven't you noticed how all contemporary sources---bar none---play down anything in which God the Holy Spirit had a hand and magnify what God purposely [ignored?]{.class_s4N6} What would lead you to think that the world handled these matters any differently in 1500 B.C. or 1000 B.C. or 500 B.C. or A.D. 500 than they do in A.D. 1900 or A.D. 2010?

Haven't you ever thought it rather odd that anyone can find written historical material on [Hammurabi ]{.class_s4N6}but can't find any on []{#c1P3.xhtml_page_332}SARAH? All right. [which one of the two]{.class_s4N6} did God the Holy Spirit emphasize? (There, we touched the nerve ending!)

How do you suppose it is that we can find all the Egyptian dynasties for Early, Middle, and Late Kingdoms, but we can't find JOSEPH's name anywhere? Did God emphasize Joseph or [Tutankhamun]{.class_s4N6}?

Again, why do you suppose it is that we can get literally reams of information on the lives of the Caesars, and yet, when we want to get one secular historical record on the life of the Apostle John, we find there is nothing there. Did God write the wrong way or what?

Now the answer of the Bible-rejecting Liberal to all of this is that the historians have naturally emphasized the [outstanding characters]{.class_s4N6} of their day and age. All right, granted; now! [why didn't God think they were "outstanding"?]{.class_s4N6} Evidently, God and the writers look at things differently.

The answer of the Bible-rejecting CONSERVATIVE to all of this is that the Holy Spirit has selected His material for a [purpose;]{.class_s4N6} so far, that is the truth. But when our "Conservative" infidel is pressed on the Holy Spirit's CHOICE OF WORDS in telling the truth---13:18, 14:2, 14:3, 14:9, 14:21, 14:22, 14:23, 14:27, 15:1, 15:4, 15:8, 15:10; and 15:19---the "Conservative" suddenly decides that God's selectivity must match the information furnished to him by books with which God had nothing to do.

This is all that is involved in the writings of Smith, Origen, Rimmer, Machen, Warfield, Robertson, Hort, Lachmann, Tregelles, Ellicott, Tischendorf, Albright, Free, Garstang, Ramsay, Zodhiates, Schaff, Wuest, Thieme, Hengstenberg, Keil, Delitzsch, Smith, Williams, Glueck, Bruce, Robertson, Diesmann, Trench, Vincent, [and any other 30,000 where they alter the King James' text. ]{.class_s4N6}Every alteration was made to match some piece of information they picked up from the [secular []{#c1P3.xhtml_page_333}world ]{.class_s4N6}of [secular history,]{.class_s4N6} where secular authors have borrowed [secular material ]{.class_s4N6}from each other since the day that the ["Book of the Dead"]{.class_s4N6} was compiled.

You will discover (and you should not be shocked to discover) that[ there is not one secular history on Egypt available that even mentions the Red Sea crossing. ]{.class_s4N6}God the Holy Spirit devoted two chapters to it and mentioned it again, later, on more than fifteen occasions. Was it [history?]{.class_s4N6} Was the RESURRECTION [history?]{.class_s4N6}

You see, the problem is quite simple. Unregenerate men hate the Bible and treat it as folklore, myth, and legend: anything but [historical fact. ]{.class_s4N6}Therefore, every lost man, from Adam to H. G. Wells, underemphasizes the historical facts recorded in the Bible and magnifies historical facts found [outside ]{.class_s4N6}the Bible. This is to create the impression that [Biblical accounts]{.class_s4N6} of [historical events]{.class_s4N6} (like the one we have just read) are in the "folk lore" category: they are "tribal legends" passed down from one campfire to another.

When the Conservative scholars "tone down" or "low-rate" the miracles in the Bible---the Red Sea crossing, the Jordan crossing, the "long day," the burning bush, resurrections, etc.---they simply hire out (temporarily) to the interests of Satan. They are paid for this work handsomely by better teaching positions, more publicity, raises in salary, and prestige as a "scholar." What they have actually done is, they have played into the hands of the world system (Luke 4:5--6) and have used their Christian profession as a cover for sabotaging the Reformation text.

Do you see the picture clearly yet? If not, we'll come through again on another runway. Can you read a morning newspaper? Do you watch the "ten o'clock telecast"? Do you take [Life ]{.class_s4N6}or [Time ]{.class_s4N6}magazine? Every name given is the name of a Bible-believing, soulwinning preacher or a Bible-believing, Christ-exalting scholar who lived in the Twentieth Century.

Does God put the emphasis on His Son and His word, or does He not?! You cannot answer [no]{.class_s4N6} unless you are a lost man who resents the Bible.

Again, my dear brother, where did[ Life, Look, Time, Newsweek,]{.class_s4N6} and the Associated Press put the [emphasis]{.class_s4N6}? (I said "emphasis," not ["mention"!)]{.class_s4N6} They put it where Pliny, Plutarch, Virgil, Horace, Herodotus, Florenz Ziegfeld, Cecil B. DeMille, Aristotle, Ovid, Plato, Charlie Darwin, Socrates, Charlie Chaplin, Philo, Origen, Albert Einstein, Bloody Mary, John Dewey, George Hegel, Baruch Spinoza, Guez Balzac, Paul Gauguin, Madame Bovary, Ludwig von Beethoven, Rita Hayworth, Karl Marx, Gottfried Leibnitz, Immanuel Kant, "Shipwreck" Kelly, Bruno Haupmann, Benny Goodman, Louis Armstrong, Ted Kennedy, Ralph Bunche, Alger Hiss, and the last 3,000 you picked up put it: [anywhere except on what God emphasized in that Book.]{.class_s4N6}

In the Bible, isn't Noah the one given the publicity in the place of ["Menes"]{.class_s4N6} and ["Cheops"]{.class_s4N6}? In the Bible, it is Abraham, not ["Hammurabi"]{.class_s4N6} and ["Seostris I."]{.class_s4N6} In the Bible, it is Moses, not ["Ahmose."]{.class_s4N6} In the Bible, it is Aaron, not ["Thutmose I."]{.class_s4N6} In the Bible, it is the Lord Jesus Christ, not [Mohammed ]{.class_s4N6}or [Buddha ]{.class_s4N6}(that's a good one!). And in the Bible, the historical facts point to God more than 3,500 times.

In the eyes of unregenerate mankind, this disqualifies the Bible as being a HISTORY BOOK; therefore, the [historical facts]{.class_s4N6} in it---see Exodus chapter 14!---are to be altered so the Book can []{#c1P3.xhtml_page_335}stay in its "class." This "class," for unregenerate historians, is ["religious beliefs."]{.class_s4N6} A "Conservative" who helps create or sustain this classification by altering the historical facts of the text should keep his seat when infidel attacks are made upon the [historicity ]{.class_s4N6}of the physical resurrection of Jesus Christ if the detailed account of the facts in Exodus chapter 14 is no good (cf. John 5:47) [.]{.class_s4N6}

There it is; [choke on it. ]{.class_s4N6}You only place an "order of importance" on statements in the creed so you can use the Bible to promote [yourself. ]{.class_s4N6}["Every word of God is pure"]{.class_s4N7-0} (Prov. 30:5), and man is to live ["by "every word of God"]{.class_s4N7-0} (Matt. 4:4), even if John R. Rice and Bob Jones University recommend a "Bible" that omitted that statement from Luke 4:4. If the faculty members at Lynchburg, Hyles-Anderson, Tennessee Temple, BIOLA, Southwestern, Mideastern, Midnorthern, Eastern, Western, Southeastern, Northeastern, Northwestern, and North-North-East by East-North-East don't like the "words" of Scripture, [they can take a flying jump at Venus.]{.class_s4N6}

[]{#c1X6.xhtml}

# Chapter 15 {.heading_s4G-1}

15:1 "Then sang Moses and the children of Israel this song unto the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}, and spake, saying, I will sing unto the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}, for he hath triumphed gloriously: the horse and his rider hath he thrown into the sea.

2 The [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}is my strength and song, and he is become my salvation: he is my God, and I will prepare him an habitation; my father's God, and I will exalt him.

3 The [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}is a man of war: the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}is his name.

4 Pharaoh's chariots and his host hath he cast into the sea: his chosen captains also are drowned in the Red sea.

5 The depths have covered them: they sank into the bottom as a stone.

6 Thy right hand, O [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}, is become glorious in power: thy right hand, O [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}hath dashed in pieces the enemy.

7 And in the greatness of thine excellency thou hast overthrown them that rose up against thee: thou sentest forth thy wrath, which consumed them as stubble.

8 And with the blast of thy nostrils the waters were gathered together, the floods stood upright as an heap, and the depths were congealed in the heart of the sea.

9 The enemy said, I will pursue, I will overtake, I will divide the spoil; my lust shall be satisfied upon them; I will draw my sword, my hand shall destroy them."

This is the first mention of someone actually [singing ]{.class_s4N4-0}or dancing in the order of books in the Bible, although musicians have appeared already (see Gen. 4:21 and []{#c1X6.xhtml_page_337}comments in the [Commentary on Genesis]{.class_s4N4-0}). Dancing was going on before the Flood, according to Job 21:11--12. This all takes place 800 years before a Greek "muse" or "poet" shows up, so the [secular ]{.class_s4N4-0}histories on music (see notes above) all purposely ignore the passage, You see, it states an [historical fact]{.class_s4N4-0} that is very disagreeable to unsaved musicians who like to trace their ancestry back to Greek civilizations---where they can claim some "class." This is respectable because it gets around any "religious issue"; no musician likes to be reminded that David had a highly developed form of chorale symphony functioning [more than 500 years before Socrates heard a flute.]{.class_s4N4-0}

["Then sang Moses and the children of Israel this song" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 1). There are many songs in the Bible (Gen. 31:27): [the midnight song]{.class_s4N4-0} (sung in Job 35:10; Acts 16:25) and the [song of drunkards]{.class_s4N4-0} (Psa. 69:12, which is an old and expensive song); then we have the [song of Moses]{.class_s4N4-0} (Deut. 31:19--22, 30, 32:44) which the Lamb picked up (Rev. 15:3), and the 1,005 [songs of Solomon]{.class_s4N4-0} (1 Kings 4:32), etc. But there is no recorded song until there is deliverance and redemption, so the first recorded song in the divine history bursts forth as the redeemed see their enemies buried in a watery grave that separates them (the redeemed) from Egypt forever! Psalm 126:2 is the fulfillment of this song, which begins here on the banks of the Red Sea.

["I will sing unto the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord]{.class_s4N9-0}[" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 1), not the [congregation]{.class_s4N4-0} or the "audience." It is ["comely"]{.class_s4N2-0} to sing praises unto God (Psa. 33:1). Renati Tebaldi, Victoria De Los Angeles, or Maria Callas never had a greater audience to which to sing than the tone-deaf country Christians []{#c1X6.xhtml_page_338}who sang "The Wayfaring Stranger," if they sang it ["to the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord]{.class_s4N9-0}["]{.class_s4N2-0} (Col. 3:16) while hoeing the cotton.

Moses began his ministry in the wilderness with this song, and he ends it with the song recorded in Deuteronomy chapter 32. The song of Exodus chapter 15 is the oldest hymn of praise in existence which praises the God of HISTORY and the God of CREATION. Other songs of praise to this true God are found in Numbers 21; Judges 5; 1 Samuel 2; 2 Samuel 22; Luke 1; Luke 2; Revelation 14, etc. The reader should notice that the ["song," ]{.class_s4N2-0}here, is[ "the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord]{.class_s4N9-0}[" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 2): He is the theme of the entire song (vss. 2, 3, 6, 11, 12, 13, 16, 17, and 18).

The Holy Spirit never wastes sheet music praising women's hair (Jeannie with the Light Brown Clairol) or women's eyes ("Green Eyes," "Irish Eyes," "Dark Eyes," etc.) or a woman's body ("It Must be Jelly 'Cause Jam Don't Shake Like That," etc.). Nor does He waste time on the moonlight ("Blue Moon," "Moon over Miami," "Harvest Moon," "Carolina Moon," "Moonglow," "Moon love," "Moon Ray," etc.) or anything else outside of a [doctrinal context]{.class_s4N4-0} which glorifies [Jesus Christ. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Note in particular the ["song of songs, which is Solomon's" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(Song of Sol. 1:1). Lamech's "Country and Western music" comes from the lips of a killer (see Gen. 4:23 and comments in the[ Commentary on Genesis]{.class_s4N4-0}).

Moses is the first SINGER mentioned in sacred history, exactly as Miriam is the first DANCER mentioned by the Lord (vs. 20). This means that God the Holy Spirit has emphasized the true worship of the true God ["in spirit and in truth"]{.class_s4N2-0} as being the proper function of [dancing ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [singing]{.class_s4N4-0}. All secular historians, therefore, must now immediately [underemphasize this truth.]{.class_s4N4-0}

We shall have no trouble finding tap dancers, ballet dancers, belly dancers, "whirling dervishes," and choreographers; but we shall have a little trouble finding newspapers and magazines that describe David dancing in 2 Samuel 6:16. We will have no trouble finding minnesingers, meistersingers, troubadours, muses, opera singers, concert singers, folk singers, and jazz singers; but we will not find any books on music discussing the songs of Zion (Psa. 1--150).

Here is Roland and here is Blondino. There stands Jan Peerce, Enrico Caruso, Renati Tebaldi, Robert Merrill, Beniamino Gigli, Jussi Bjoerling, Victoria De Los Angeles, Mario Lanza, and Maria Callas glorifying love, murder, passion, loneliness, treachery, suicide, and humor as they sing the arias from ["Il Trovatore," "Pagliacci," "Cavalleria Rusticana," "Carmen," "Madame Butterfly," "Tannhauser,"]{.class_s4N4-0} and ["Figarro."]{.class_s4N4-0}

Here are John McCormack, Nelson Eddy, Lily Pons, Jenny Lind, and Jeanette McDonald entertaining millions with "The Road to Mandalay," "Indian Love Call," "The Bluebird of Happiness," and "Ah, Sweet Mystery of Life." There stands Bing Crosby, Perry Como, Russ Columbo, Rudy Vallee, Frank Sinatra, and Dean Martin moaning about "The Last Roundup," "Blue Hawaii," "Sweet Lelani," "The Whiffenpoof Song," "The Prisoner of Love," "Thine Alone," "I'll Never Smile Again," and "These Foolish Things." Here whines Hank Williams, Tex Ritter, Eddy Arnold, Roy Acuff, Bob Wills, Johnny Cash, Cowboy Copas, Spade Cooley, Charlie Pride, and Red Foley with "Fireball Mail," "Rose of Old San Antone," "A Boy Named Sue," "Wabash Cannonball," "Boll Weevil," "Smoke on the Water," and []{#c1X6.xhtml_page_340}God knows what. The French sing ["Alouette"]{.class_s4N4-0}; the Irish sing "Where the River Shannon Flows"; the Scotch sing "Annie Laurie"; the Germans sing ["Du, Du liegt Mir am Hertzen";]{.class_s4N4-0} the Japanese sing ["Naniwabushi"; ]{.class_s4N4-0}and Ham sings of sex orgies, animal lusts, black magic, and "the blues."

God's children have ["a new song"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Psa. 40:3) in three stanzas---psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs (Col. 3:16); not "spirituals." No man or woman is glorified in "Jesus Lover of My Soul" but ["the man Christ Jesus"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 Tim. 2:5); no country is glorified but New Jerusalem in "There's a Land That is Fairer Than Day"; no emotion is glorified but joy in "Arise my Soul, Arise!"; no God is magnified but the God of Israel in "A Mighty Fortress is our God"; and no love can stand when compared to "No One Ever Cared for Me Like Jesus."

["I will sing unto the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord]{.class_s4N9-0}["]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 1). No sooner has Moses started to sing than the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} censors the lyrics and reminds Moses that he must not mention anyone [riding horses]{.class_s4N4-0} (see comments under 14:7, 9). There is a "no-no," as all of the cavalry must be stuffed into the chariots (14:9, 17, 23, 26). Hence, we are to correct Moses' composition to read "the horse and his DRIVER." The word [driver]{.class_s4N4-0}, incidentally, [does not occur in one single Hebrew manuscript in existence. ]{.class_s4N4-0}All Masoretic texts read as the [King James Authorized Version.]{.class_s4N4-0}

["He is become my salvation\...my God\...my father's God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 2). This is the new song of the Christian, as well as Moses. My strength is Christ (Eph. 6:10), my salvation is Christ (1 Cor. 1:30), my God is Christ (John 1:1--2), and He is the god of my "fathers" (Gal 3:29). As the song writer has said: "Some []{#c1X6.xhtml_page_341}may sing to pass the weary hours along, and some may sing to entertain a worldly throng; but when I sing, I worship God in song! It's in my heart! It's in my heart!"[ "I will prepare him an habitation"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 2).

The last verse mentioned (vs. 2) is plainly [prophecy ]{.class_s4N4-0}(cf. 12:25), so the majority of commentators bitterly resent it. Most of them defect to the corrupt [Septuagint ]{.class_s4N4-0}from which Jerome's Roman Catholic [Vulgate ]{.class_s4N4-0}came. The Alexandrian corruption (preserved in North African "Bibles") reads: ["I will glorify him."]{.class_s4N4-0} The [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1901) uses this ancient corruption as it, and the [NASV]{.class_s4N4-0} (any edition) is a Roman Catholic translation which Premillennial scholars are currently promoting in order to rid themselves of the authority of the hated [Authorized Version.]{.class_s4N4-0}

Moses DOES prepare God a ["habitation"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Exod. 24--40), and although this habitation is destroyed (1 Sam. 2:32) and [rebuilt ]{.class_s4N4-0}(2 Chron. 6:2), destroyed again (Jer. 25:30) and [rebuilt ]{.class_s4N4-0}again (John 2:20), destroyed again (Luke 21:24---Titus, A.D. 70) and [rebuilt ]{.class_s4N4-0}again (Rev. 11:1--2), and destroyed again (Matt. 24:15) and [rebuilt ]{.class_s4N4-0}again (Ezek. 40--46), Moses is still telling the truth. The [ASV's]{.class_s4N4-0} conjecture is blank and void, even though Dummelow, Knobel, Cook, and Kalisch subscribe to the same nonsense.

["My father's God" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 2) is to be altered to "My God is my father" according to Clarke and Houbigant, but of course, this is the reading of Galatians and Romans which was written back into Exodus many years after the New Testament was complete. There are no individual human "sons of God" in the Old Testament, so Adam Clarke's reading is what is known in theology as HERESY []{#c1X6.xhtml_page_342}(see comments under 4:22 and under Job 38:4--7 in the [Bible Believer's Commentary on Job]{.class_s4N4-0}).

["The LORD is a man of war: the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[LORD ]{.class_s4NE-0}[is his name"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 3). This is the first time anything like this has showed up since the war was declared in Genesis 3:15. From here on, God manifests Himself as Commander-in-Chief of an army (6:26, 7:4) that is engaged in spreading a [literal]{.class_s4N4-0}, visible, [political ]{.class_s4N4-0}kingdom (Num. 21--31) by armed force (1 Sam. 15:3). As a matter of fact, when the war in Palestine starts (Josh. 5--6), the Lord appears as an armed [Captain ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Josh. 5:13--14), and He is called a ["captain"]{.class_s4N2-0} in the New Testament (Heb. 2:10).

As Commander-in-Chief, He is my ["strength"]{.class_s4N2-0} as well as my ["song" ]{.class_s4N2-0}in the Old Testament (vs. 2).
