He is ["my salvation"]{.class_s4N2-0} in the Church Age; He is ["my God"]{.class_s4N2-0} at the resurrection (John 20:28). He is ["my father's God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 2) to Israel in the Tribulation (Rev. 7:1--8). "His people" (vs. 13) are: ["redeemed"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 13), ["led forth" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 13), ["purchased"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 16---[glory!]{.class_s4N4-0}), ["guided"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 13), brought in (vs. 17), and planted (vs. 17)!

The passage which follows has been commented on before in our exposition of chapter 14. To rid themselves of the obvious truths of the passage, the major commentators have insisted that the passage is "figurative and highly poetical." But the observant reader who has learned to take the Bible critic with a ton of salt can see that:

1\. Pharaoh's chariots were ["cast into the sea"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 4).

2\. Among the chariots were ["chosen captains"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 4 cf. 14:7).

3\. They ["SANK"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 5).

4\. The waters were ["gathered together"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 8).

5\. ["The floods stood upright"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 8).

6\. The enemy said exactly what verse 9 says he would say (14:3, 9).

7\. The wind DID blow (vs. 10 cf. 14:21).

8\. The sea DID "cover them" (vs. 10).

9\. The people were ["led forth"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 13).

10\. The children of Israel DID go ["on dry land" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 19).

But how much more proof does a real seeker of truth need before he gets bored to death? Mark any man who talks about the language being "figurative and highly [poetic"]{.class_s4N4-0} as a man whose attitude towards the Bible is "skeptical and highly prejudiced." There are a few poetic expressions found in the passage---verses 7, 8, and 12---but any fool can see that the word ["AS"]{.class_s4N2-0} has been inserted in every description that is not [literal:]{.class_s4N4-0} verse 5---["as a stone,"]{.class_s4N2-0} verse 7---["consumed them as stubble,"]{.class_s4N2-0} verse 10---["sank as lead in the mighty waters,"]{.class_s4N2-0} and verse 8---["stood upright as an heap."]{.class_s4N2-0} These are not "poetical figures"; they are statements of fact using illustrations or similitudes to portray the truth vividly. When the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} says "are we justified in taking literally the strong expressions of a highly wrought poetical description," the answer is: "We are always justified in believing what God said, as He said it, in the context in which it is found; and if it is NOT literal, the Lord will have enough sense to indicate it."

The corrupt [LXX]{.class_s4N4-0}, written more than 200 years after John died on Patmos, has altered verse 9 to read[ "acquire the lordship over them."]{.class_s4N4-0} This is an attempt to reconcile 14:5 with the text; Mamma's little helpers at Alexandria are "up to snuff" as usual.

Two new revelations appear in the[ "song" ]{.class_s4N2-0}which are not recorded in chapter 14. The first of these is the fact that the ground split open under Pharaoh's troops when the waves came down (vs. 12). The second is that the wall of water was ["congealed"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 8); that is, it was [stiffened ]{.class_s4N4-0}to a solid mass in some way.

In spite of this massive evidence for the crossing of the Red Sea fifty to seventy miles south of Suez, the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} stubbornly insists (at vs. 10) that a northwest wind blew the water of the "Bitter Lakes" (see 13:18) back over Pharaoh. Well then (ho hum!), there are [two Pharaohs]{.class_s4N4-0} and [two columns]{.class_s4N4-0} of Israelites and [two seas]{.class_s4N4-0} and [two winds]{.class_s4N4-0} and [two crossings]{.class_s4N4-0} and [two miracles. ]{.class_s4N4-0}One is the Biblical set recorded by the Holy Spirit and preserved intact without error in the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1611), and the other is the fictitious myth of Conservative scholarship borrowed from Paine, Voltaire, Celsus, Origen, Porphyry, Graf, Wellhausen, Semler, Astruc, Strauss, Renan, and Weigle, preserved in the [Wycliffe Commentary,]{.class_s4N4-0} Dummelow, and the [Pulpit Commentary,]{.class_s4N4-0} and espoused by the translators of the Lockman Foundation.

The[ "habitation"]{.class_s4N2-0} of verse 2 is clearly indicated in verse 17, where it is called ["the Sanctuary"]{.class_s4N2-0} which God's[ "hands have established."]{.class_s4N2-0} The Millennial reign of Christ is mentioned in verse 18, showing that chapters 3--14 will take place again in the Tribulation (see Rev. 11:1--6 and comments in the [Commentary on Revelation]{.class_s4N4-0}). The[ "for ever"]{.class_s4N2-0} of that verse confounds Amillennial scholarship, but the believer, of course, knows that the seventh millennium is a sabbath of rest (Isa. 14:7). It has no ["evening and morning"]{.class_s4N2-0} (cf. Gen. 2:1--3), and it goes out into eternity with the reign of Christ and His saints (Dan. 7:27).

Once again, the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} (vs. 19) grasps the opportunity to enforce its teaching over the Holy Spirit's teaching: according to Whitelaw, we are to read "and with his chariot MEN" instead of ["with his horsemen."]{.class_s4N2-0} (These poor cavalrymen have been stuffed into chariots so many times by now that they have forgotten how to ride a horse.)

15:10 "Thou didst blow with thy wind, the sea covered them: they sank as lead in the mighty waters.

11 Who is like unto thee, O [Lord]{.class_s4N9-1}, among the gods? who is like thee, glorious in holiness, fearful in praises, doing wonders?

12 Thou stretchedst out thy right hand, the earth swallowed them.

13 Thou in thy mercy hast led forth the people which thou hast redeemed: thou hast guided them in thy strength unto thy holy habitation.

14 The people shall hear, and be afraid: sorrow shall take hold on the inhabitants of Palestina.

15 Then the dukes of Edom shall be amazed; the mighty men of Moab, trembling shall take hold upon them; all the inhabitants of Canaan shall melt away.

16 Fear and dread shall fall upon them; by the greatness of thine arm they shall be as still as a stone; till thy people pass over, O [Lord]{.class_s4N9-1}, till the people pass over, which thou hast purchased.

17 Thou shalt bring them in, and plant them in the mountain of thine inheritance, in the place, O [Lord]{.class_s4N9-1}, which thou hast made for thee to dwell in, []{#c1X6.xhtml_page_346}in the Sanctuary, O [Lord]{.class_s4N9-1}, which thy hands have established.

18 The [Lord ]{.class_s4N9-1}shall reign for ever and ever.

19 For the horse of Pharaoh went in with his chariots and with his horsemen into the sea, and the [Lord ]{.class_s4N9-1}brought again the waters of the sea upon them; but the children of Israel went on dry land in the midst of the sea."

["Who is like unto thee, O LORD, among the gods?"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 11). The Lord asks Israel the same question in Isaiah 40:18, 25 and then answers the question in Isaiah 45:5, 14, 18, and 21. ["The gods"]{.class_s4N2-0} are present (Gen. 3:5; Psa. 82:1, 6; 1 Cor 8:5; Psa. 86:8), but the Lord God (called [Jehovah]{.class_s4N4-0} in the Old Testament \[6:3\] and [Jesus]{.class_s4N4-0} in the New Testament \[1 John 5:20\] is ["the GOD of gods"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Psa. 136:2). No "god" can compete with Him long (see Jer. 10:10--11). "Gods" currently running for office have been putting their hats "in the ring" ever since Eve nominated herself (Gen. 3:5). Among them are:

1\. GREEK GODS AND GODDESSES: Adonis, Aland, Apollos, Metzger, Artemis, Trench, Heracles, Vincent, the Cyclops, Thayer, Philo, Origen, A. T. Robertson, the Minator, Zodhiates, Westcott, Hort, Dionysius, [Sinaiticus]{.class_s4N4-0}, Phi Beta Kappa, Kronos, [Vaticanus]{.class_s4N4-0}, Thieme, Griesbach, Tischendorf, Zeus, Nestle, Poseidon, Rendall, Hestia, Aphrodite, Eros, and Lightfoot.

2\. ROMAN GODS AND GODDESSES): Ceres, Pope Paul VI, Venus, Diana, Pope John XXIII, Victor Mature, Sargent Shriver, Vesta, Lares, Deverra, Premier Diem, Bing Crosby, Castro, Hitler, Sophia Loren, Fortuna, Juno, Jupiter, DeGaulle, Pope []{#c1X6.xhtml_page_347}Leo I, Cardinal Spellman, Tellus, Promitor, Fulton Sheen, Mercury, Mars, Pluto, and the Kennedy family.

3\. AMERICAN GODS: money, sex, education, African music, sports, Socialism, science, and television.

Since no "gods" can be likened to the one true God (Isa. 46:9), the second commandment forbids placing any god even with the God of Israel.

["The inhabitants of Palestina\...the dukes of Edom\...the mighty men of Moab\...the inhabitants of Canaan shall melt away"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 14--15). Again, we are dealing with [prophecy]{.class_s4N4-0}, exactly as the song of Deborah deals with the Second Advent of the Lord Jesus Christ (Judg. 5). Verses illuminating the passage are found in Genesis 36:15--19; Numbers 20:14; Numbers chapters 22--24; and Joshua 5:1.

["By the greatness of thine arm they shall be as still as stone; till thy people pass over"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 16). This time the "passing over" is a reference to Joshua 4:23, and the same "high hand" (3:20, 7:4, 9:15) mentioned in versus 6 and 12 is attached to ["thine arm."]{.class_s4N2-0} This right hand on God's "right arm" is identified as the Lord Jesus Himself in Psalm 17:7, 20:6, 44:3, 45:4, 77:10, etc.

15:20 "And Miriam the prophetess, the sister of Aaron, took a timbrel in her hand; and all the women went out after her with timbrels and with dances.

21 And Miriam answered them, Sing ye to the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}, for he hath triumphed gloriously; the horse and his rider hath he thrown into the sea.

22 So Moses brought Israel from the Red sea, and they went out into the wilderness of Shur; and []{#c1X6.xhtml_page_348}they went three days in the wilderness, and found no water."

Three other words occur now for the first time in the Scriptures: ["prophetess," "timbrels,"]{.class_s4N2-0} and ["dances"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 20). Women prophesy in the New Testament (1 Cor. 11:5; Acts 21:9) and the Old Testament (Judg. 4:4; 2 Chron. 34:22); as the male prophets, they come in two sizes---true and false. Jezebel (Rev. 2:20) and Noadiah (Neh. 6:14) are prophetesses who match such prophets as those described in Jeremiah chapter 23, while Anna (Luke 2:36) and Deborah (Judg. 4:4) are fit company for Isaiah, Jeremiah, Ezekiel, Daniel, and the rest of the Nabhim. Noadiah is usually found at business meetings in Baptist churches, egging Sanballat and Tobiah on to victory and telling them what the order of business should be over which she has already "prayed."

The dance is [unisexual:]{.class_s4N4-0} no males in the lineup. ["All the women went out after her with timbrels and with dances"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 20). A Bible dance (Luke 15:25) is held at a time (Eccl. 3:4) when God needs to be praised and "sex" needs to be ignored. Since sex (bodily contact to arouse the passions) is the life of all of the modern dances, you will find okra grown in British Columbia before you will find a "prom" where the women dance with the women and quote Bible verses while they do it. You might find the "Marges" dancing with women at Greenwich Village (the "Nut Club") or in Pensacola (the "Yum Yum Tree") or in Los Angeles and Washington, D.C., but if you think the "butches" are [quoting Scripture, ]{.class_s4N4-0}there is something wrong with your hearing.

Take [sex ]{.class_s4N4-0}out of the modern American dance, and no dance will be held. The slow music is for the girls, []{#c1X6.xhtml_page_349}the fast music is for the boys, and there hasn't been a dance in America like Judges 11:34 since the Declaration of Independence refused to acknowledge the Lord Jesus Christ. No [red-blooded]{.class_s4N4-0} American young man would walk around the block to dance with another young man, and if you find it going on, you will find the bloodline is PINK---or a deep YELLOW.

We shall reserve most of our comments for the passage in 32:6, 19, where a real American dance is going on. That is, a Twentieth-Century American dance has nothing to do with praising God. Since modern dance music is African, coming up from Jamaica, Puerto Rico, Cuba, and New Orleans, one must attend a ["mixed multitude"]{.class_s4N2-0} dance (12:38) where the ["mixed multitude" ]{.class_s4N2-0}is AFRICAN (Lev. 24:10).

The original dance of this kind began in Egypt anyway and was originally a religious function for the worship of the great whore Isis, who was her son's mother and lover at the same time (see [The Two Babylons]{.class_s4N4-0} by Alexander Hislop), exactly as Tammuz committed fornication with his own mother ([Semiramis]{.class_s4N4-0}). The Phoenician sex dances conducted as "fertility rites" come from this Egyptian dance, so again we are faced with the phenomenon mentioned under 14:31.

Everywhere it is taken for granted that the dances of Exodus 15:20; Jeremiah 31:13; and Psalm 149:3 were simply variations of a respectable function. This overlooks the [historical fact]{.class_s4N4-0} that the Holy Spirit has selected incidents from history which emphasize the FACT that the dances of Exodus 15; Jeremiah 31; and Psalm 149 are [proper]{.class_s4N4-0} dances, whereas the dances of Phoenicia, Phyrgia, Thrace, and Rome were [improper]{.class_s4N4-0} dances. (If you are hung up on dancing, just put the []{#c1X6.xhtml_page_350}commentary down, for here is the same old moral issue injected again in all its discriminatory, divisive, schismatic, absolute, infallible glory.) Dances performed for the "gods" (see comments under vs. 11) are worship services for devils according to the greatest Christian who ever lived (1 Cor. 10:20).

Modern Western dances are "animal dances" whose origination was New Orleans, Louisiana (1910); their progenitor was the ["Tango,"]{.class_s4N4-0} introduced into Parisian society by Roman Catholics in 1912. This is the first "body-rubbing" dance to be approved by high school teachers and College professors. Christians, in 1893, considered the waltzes of Johann Strauss to be sensuous exercises in pre-seduction maneuvers; many of them rioted at the first performance of such tunes as "The Merry Widow" and "The Blue Danube."

Now, to the unsaved intellectual (who swears by evolutionary materialism), such a response would be called "blue-nosed" or "mid-Victorian prudery" or "intolerance" or something else. The unregenerate American of the 1970s would thank his god---several are available (see vs. 11)---that "modern man" has outgrown this sense of "false modesty" and has become "broad-minded" enough to tolerate what he formerly condemned. But this is the growth cycle of all sin.

The "evolution" (I think that is the Nineteenth-Century cliché) of the dance is about like the evolution of Bible translating between the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1611) and the [NRSV]{.class_s4N4-0} (1989). All of this "evolution" is sort of like art going from Rembrandt to Miro, and music going from Beethoven to the Beatles. What you do is this: [you tolerate LAWBREAKING more with the passing of the years till you treat TRANSGRESSIONS as "normal []{#c1X6.xhtml_page_351}growth processes."]{.class_s4N4-0} This is the evolution of education from Zinzendorf to John Dewey, and it is the standard operating procedure in psychotherapy for talking a guilty sinner out of his conviction and into an "adjusted state of harmony" with his sins.

The "dance" begins with bodies apart and the vertical and horizontal movements of the polka, hornpipe, mazurka, reel, square dance, and waltz. This turns into dances where the bodies are apart with snakelike movements or sex movements, such as the Samba, Mambo, Big Apple, Charleston, Limbo, Frug, Lambeth Walk, etc. Accompanying this "evolution" will be found slow and fast movements where the bodies rub: the Fox Trot, Bunny Hug, Grizzly Bear, the Dog, Turkey Trot, Tango, and nearly any dance after 1976.

Dancing is the only universal, socially acceptable sin for young ladies. It is approved of by teachers, parents, and educators; and it affords the young lady the best chance to arouse a young man's animal nature without the logical completion of the arousal. It affords the young lady a chance to "bag a mate" or "go steady," which normal activities do not carry with them. Above all, it is a gradual admission to the less acceptable sins---smoking, drinking, drugs, fornication, adultery, Satanism, and murder. The dance hall is the training ground for the prostitute's profession; and the dance is the incubator of fornication, perversion, adultery, drunkenness, and murder.

If you caught a man in your living room holding your wife the way you would hold a dancing partner at a Country Club dance---[and there was no music heard anywhere in the room!]{.class_s4N4-0}---what would you do? They are just rocking in each other's arms, embracing each other []{#c1X6.xhtml_page_352}around the neck and waist! How does that strike you? All right, then how does MUSIC suddenly change the whole environment? And if the music is African SEX music, how do you figure this [improves ]{.class_s4N4-0}the picture? (As one has so aptly said, "I don't think much of any dance where it looks like a man is carrying a woman out of a burning building!")

There are teenage girls in America who would rather lose their right arm than give up dancing (1 Cor. 8:12--13). I have talked to scores of young ladies who refused to accept Christ for only one reason: they felt if they received Him (John 1:12), they would have to give up the Dance of Death (1 Cor. 15:33).

["And Miriam answered them, Sing ye to the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord\...]{.class_s4N9-0}[the horse and his rider hath he thrown into the sea" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 21). This time---finally!---the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} throws in the towel and refuses to revive the struggle about "chariot men," "horsemen," "horse drivers," and "charioteers." The text still reads ["THE HORSE AND HIS RIDER"]{.class_s4N2-0} (exactly as vs. 1), and since the song begins with the men on this note and ends with the women on this note, we should accept the Bible's suggestion that someone rode on horseback in chapter 14. The ["timbrel"]{.class_s4N2-0} of verse 20 is the modern tambourine, and Miriam and her maidens answer the men as they sing the song of verses 1--19.

["THE HORSE AND HIS RIDER"]{.class_s4N2-0} is certainly a picture of the horse and rider of Revelation 6:2, although none of the major commentators are able to find the riches of the revelation. This "horse and rider" is found again in Revelation 19:18, and the "plural of class" has no application to the expression. It is ONE rider on ONE horse in John's Revelation, as we have noted before, []{#c1X6.xhtml_page_353 .class-1}[Pharaoh is one of the greatest types of Antichrist found anywhere in the Bible. ]{.class_s4N4-0}This sheds much light on the obscure and "highly figurative"[ Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0}, for it means that far from being "senseless remarks," their notes on "the horse and rider" (see 15:1 and 14:28) were inspired by [Satan,]{.class_s4N4-0} who covers himself with their scholarship as neatly as a squid ever covered himself with a cloud of "ink."

["So Moses brought Israel from the Red sea\...and found no water"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 22). This would place the southern limit of[ "the wilderness of Shur"]{.class_s4N2-0} about seventy miles down the western coast of the Sinaitic Peninsula, and no archaeological discovery of the last 200 years has ever proved that the area ended north of this location. One can allow[ "the wilderness of Shur"]{.class_s4N2-0} to begin in the area of Etham, but it must extend to a minimum of fifty miles down to the coast to meet the demands of the text. To confirm this opinion, one will notice how the Holy Spirit has called the northern end of this same wilderness ["the wilderness of ETHAM"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Num. 33:8).

The reader who believes the Bible has a tremendous advantage over its critics (Dummelow, Lange, Clarke, Bullinger, Weigle, Schaff, Toy, Frankenberg, Keil, Delitzsch, Bantzsch, et al.), for he is able to discern the real cause for the complaining which follows in verse 24. The truth of the matter is that Israel has [obeyed ]{.class_s4N4-0}Moses' previous injunction (8:27 and 5:3), and has a right to expect their journey to be over! They have gone the ["three days"]{.class_s4N2-0} required by the Lord and Moses (8:27), and they are "nowhere," to use the colloquial expression. Somehow, all the commentators were so busy trying to push Marah and Shur back up near Etham (see comments []{#c1X6.xhtml_page_354}under 13:18) that they forgot to compare "things spiritual with things spiritual" (1 Cor. 2:13).

["They went THREE DAYS IN THE WILDERNESS" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 22). This is exactly what Moses wanted them to do. Why then should they not murmur against HIM (vs. 24) when nothing happens?

15:23 "And when they came to Marah, they could not drink of the waters of Marah, for they were bitter: therefore the name of it was called Marah.

24 And the people murmured against Moses, saying, What shall we drink?

25 And he cried unto the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}; and the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}shewed him a tree, which when he had cast into the waters, the waters were made sweet: there he made for them a statute and an ordinance, and there he proved them.

26 And said, If thou wilt diligently hearken to the voice of the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}thy God, and wilt do that which is right in his sight, and wilt give ear to his commandments, and keep all his statutes, I will put none of these diseases upon thee, which I have brought upon Egyptians: for I am the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}that healeth thee.

27 And they came to Elim, where were twelve wells of water, and threescore and ten palm trees: and they encamped there by the waters."

The column moves on. All that day and the next night the fiery pillar and the cloud guide the column. The second morning they get up, no one knows where they are or where they are going (Heb. 11:8). The Lord is leading them EAST, now, which is perfectly apparent from 16:1. What would normally be a three-day journey for mounted men (Etham, on the Egyptian border, to Sinai---140 []{#c1X6.xhtml_page_355}miles) turns into around 49 days (see comments under 19:1). One must allow for the great multitude, and one must allow for encampments that may have lasted seven to fifteen days, plus the events mentioned in 16:4--36 and 17:8--14.

At the end of the second day, the caravan runs out of water. Mothers and fathers give what they have left to their children; the last drop goes to the horses and cattle; and when they "bed down" that night, there is some grumbling in the camp. The ["mixed multitude"]{.class_s4N2-0} doesn't help things a bit. A Hamite pulls alongside a Hebrew and says: "Hey, man, you notice dat de drink done gib out?"

"Well, the Lord's probably testing us to see how we'll take it."

"Yeah, but man, like what if we doan find no water! How's we gonna rap wid de worship when we's dryer den dust in a paper sack?"

"Well, I don't know, but 'my Lord knows the way through the wilderness. All I have to do is follow; strength for today is mine all the way, and all that I need for tomorrow.'"

"But man, like isn't you gettin' thirsty? I took one of dem dehydrated watah pills back yondah, and it didn't do nothin' fo' me! [I'm thirsty!"]{.class_s4N4-0}

On the third day, the Israelites were griping. Obadiah pulls alongside Shammah and says: "Man, I'm as dry as a piece of sandpaper in the bottom of a cracker barrel. You reckon we're going to die of thirst out here?"

"Beats me, man. I'm just claiming Philippians 4:19."

On they tramp. A mile later Obadiah says: "Lookee here, you reckon them Egyptians was right? Suppose this Moses is a freak out and this thing's a dead end, what then?"

"Well, we're outta Egypt."

"Yeah, but we ain't outta [trouble!"]{.class_s4N4-0}

"Well, something will show up in awhile, but man, I am getting [thirsty!"]{.class_s4N4-0}

Second mile. "Do you suppose he brought us out of Egypt to let us die here instead of back yonder?"

"Oh, no man, don't be ridick. You know God's with that character. You saw what the Lord did down in Egypt. He knows what he's doin'."

"Well, if God's with him, how come he can't find no water?"

A third voice chimes in: "He better find water! See those droves? Tomorrow they won't be able to walk if they don't get watered!"

A fourth voice joins the gripers' chorus:

"Well, if we had kicked off down in Egypt, we coulda died in style! I haven't seen a fig or a cashew nut since we left."

"Quit talking about nuts, man; you make me [thirsty!"]{.class_s4N4-0}

The desert sun beats down. The sweat evaporates on the bodies. A hot, dry wind blows across the plains (April--May). The children begin to cry: "Mama,[ I'm thirsty!"]{.class_s4N4-0} "Mama, give me a drink!" "Daddy, is there any more water in the canteen?" "Hasn't anybody got any water?" "I'm thirsty! [I'm thirsty!"]{.class_s4N4-0}

About that time they arrive at an oasis. ["Marah"]{.class_s4N2-0} has never been identified for certain by the scholars, but if you take any of their findings and move their location eastward ten to thirty miles, you will be fifty miles closer to the truth than they will be. ["Ain Hawarah"]{.class_s4N4-0} is the traditional legend passed down from one Bible-rejecting Conservative to another (see the [New Bible Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} or [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} or [Wycliffe []{#c1X6.xhtml_page_357}Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0}). The word itself is defined in the [King James]{.class_s4N4-0} text---"bitterness" (as in Ruth 1:20).

The Lord has been in charge of the column (13:21--22), so one must seek Divine instruction in the incident. Moses has not accidently stumbled upon an oasis after wandering about "aimlessly" (see corrupt reading for Exod. 14:3, "[American Translation]{.class_s4N4-0}"). No, the Lord has deliberately tested Israel: ["and there HE PROVED THEM"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 25). So must it ever be: bitter before sweet, Law before grace, curse before blessing, earthly body before heavenly body, probation before liberty, testing before fruitfulness,[ "Marah"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 24) before ["Elim"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 27).

The wagon train circles the oasis, and there is a mad rush to the waters before anyone can even test them. People who have been twelve to twenty hours without water are not very "pickaunish." (This last word is an unused Aramaic root derived from an original Sanskrit monosyllabic sibilant found on the Walt Disney Stele in [Ras Shamra]{.class_s4N4-0} by Dr. Smell Fungus, 1859 3/4.)

Clarke's comment on verse 24 is as rare as the one we reproduced under 12:30. It reproduces the "original" about as well as [Look ]{.class_s4N4-0}magazine reports the truth. The "original" runs like this:

"Water! Water!!"

"Get the lister bag out! Grab this pan! Here, fill this too!"

["Outta my way, man!]{.class_s4N4-0} Quit shoving!"

"Gangway, let me at it! Here, son, fill this up too!"

"Who you shovin' anyway?"

Men and women and children are stepping all over each other to get to the waters. Tempers explode, as the nerves of many of the refugees have been frayed to the []{#c1X6.xhtml_page_358}breaking point, and the general movement toward the oasis can best be described as "last one in is a dirty so-and-so!"

The major commentators fail entirely to appreciate the scene. Their lack of contact with TRUTH and REALITY makes it almost impossible for them to conjure up the picture. Ask the survivors on the sailboat who were picked up south of New Orleans (Bay of Pigs, 1961) what a man does when he gets thirsty. Ask the men who survived the Death March (Bataan, 1942) how it was when they passed by the Caribou mud holes.

When Clarke attributes the murmuring to "no firmness of character," he is talking like a fool in an air-conditioned office, drinking Coca-Cola, commenting on the problems of highway construction west of El Paso in August. I'd like to see Clarke's "firmness of character" after an eight-hour march in eighty degrees where the last water he sipped was 24 hours back!

The column runs "pell mell," "lickety-split," "like a bat outta hell," "like a blue streak," "like mad," "double-time," "hell bent for election," etc.) to the water hole; faces and heads disappear beneath its surface. It doesn't take five minutes for the word to go five miles down the column: [bad water!]{.class_s4N4-0} The first men up from the drink look like Jannes and Jambres after their "snort" from the canteen cup (7:20), and those who go ahead and drink anyway are soon vomiting.

["And the people murmured against MOSES"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 24). The rumor (rumble, scuttlebutt, etc.) passes on to Division Headquarters, and Moses is back on his knees again before the Commander-in-Chief!

"Lord?"

"Yeees?"

"Are you still there, Lord?"

"Ah, yeees."

"Well, Lord, I just can't understand why you do things like you do them. Can't you pull off something just ONE time without a hitch in it somewhere? Now understand, I'm not complaining\...."

"Oh no, not YOU. You wouldn't [complain."]{.class_s4N4-0}

"But really, Lord, why is it that every time I mix a drink like you tell me to, there's always a lemon in it somewhere. Every rose we got has a thorn in it. Isn't there just [one thing ]{.class_s4N4-0}that\...."

"Now, Moses, every thoroughbred collie needs just enough fleas to remind him that he's a [dog]{.class_s4N4-0}. If I didn't send you a few frustrating problems, you wouldn't come to Me for help. I send you those things to make you turn to Me, Moses."

"But Lord, I would turn to you anyway without them."

["You would?]{.class_s4N4-0} Well tell me something then, Moses. Where were you that forty years you were on the backside of the desert? I didn't see you rushing off to prayer meeting every Wednesday night out there."

"Ah, yes, Lord. You're right."

"Now, do you really want to know where some good drinking water is?"

"Yes, Lord, please give us some water."

"All right, there's a fine oasis up ahead just about a mile and a half: enough water there to water the whole caboodle---cattle and all. And if you want a refresher here, just cut down that small tree growing about ten yards in front of your tent and pitch it into the water. There's enough there to give everybody a quart to take with them."

["A tree, which when he had cast into the waters, the waters were made sweet"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 25). In typology, Christ is the ["tree"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Song of Sol. 2:3), and the Scofield note is as good as any for the verse.

["Statute"]{.class_s4N2-0} and ["ordinance"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 25) are words used like the ["commandments" ]{.class_s4N2-0}of Genesis 26:5; nothing yet has been said about that vast body of "ordinances" which accompany the Levitical priesthood (see comments under 12:16--17). The reader should observe that immediately following [obedience ]{.class_s4N4-0}(vs. 25), the water shows up (vs. 27), and instead of one well, there are [twelve ]{.class_s4N4-0}wells---with shade.

["The righteous"]{.class_s4N2-0} are as ["the palm tree" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(Psa. 92:12), and the ["threescore and ten"]{.class_s4N2-0} is connected with ["the days of our years"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Psa. 90:10).

Verse 26 introduces the great issue of healing into the Bible. We have seen how Moses' leprous hand indicated the physical condition of Israel in Egypt (4:6); now, healing is promised to a [nation]{.class_s4N4-0}. But the seeker of truth should observe three things about the passage.

A. [It is given to Israel. ]{.class_s4N4-0}["I am the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord ]{.class_s4N9-0}[that healeth THEE."]{.class_s4N2-0}

B. It is connected with the [signs ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [wonders ]{.class_s4N4-0}of the first deliverer, Moses (Deut. 34:11).

C. [It is conditional upon conduct:]{.class_s4N4-0} no healing "by grace" is found in the context (see Phil. 2:26--27 and comments in the [Commentary on Galatians-Colossians]{.class_s4N4-0}).

FOUR conditions for "divine healing" are laid down in the text.

1\. If you will ["diligently hearken" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs.26).

2\. If you will ["do that which is right in HIS sight" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 26).

3\. If you will ["give ear to HIS commandments" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 26).

4\. If you will ["keep ALL of his statutes"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 26).

One can see immediately that a lot of "healing" carried on by the Assemblies of God, the Churches of God, and the Pentecostal groups (plus Allen, Coe, Ewing, Branham, Robinson, Roberts, Stewart, Reverend Ike, Bishop Johnson, Daddy Grace, Father Divine, et al.) is a ridiculous sideshow.

You were commanded to WITNESS. [Do you witness?]{.class_s4N4-0}

You are commanded to ["do that which is right"]{.class_s4N2-0} in God's sight, not your own; is that slop you watch on television "pleasing to God" (1 John 3:22)? Then what is the "prayer line" for? Do you ["hearken" ]{.class_s4N2-0}as[ "diligently"]{.class_s4N2-0} to 2 Corinthians 12:1--8; Colossians 3:23; and 2 Timothy 4:20 as you do to Hebrews 13:8; Matthew 8:17; and 3 John 2? Then what is the "prayer cloth" for?

[Do you ]{.class_s4N4-0}["keep ALL of his statutes"?]{.class_s4N2-0} Do you even know what the word means? Look at Joshua 24:25; Ezra 7:10; Psalm 81:4; Ezekiel 20:24--25; Numbers 18:23; and Ezekiel 11:20. Then why are you singing "Only Believe"? The context of ["I am the LORD that healeth thee"]{.class_s4N2-0} is the context of an absolute obedience given wholeheartedly [at all times]{.class_s4N4-0} (see the comments under Matt. 8:17 in the [Commentary on Matthew]{.class_s4N4-0}).

Clarke has altered verse 25 to read that God proved Moses instead of the children of Israel, and other commentators have made various changes in the text which mean nothing one way or another. It is spiritually significant to note that it is [twelve ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [seventy ]{.class_s4N4-0}disciples who bring spiritual refreshment to thirsty Israel in Matthew chapter 10 and Luke chapter 10; the twelve wells []{#c1X6.xhtml_page_362}of verse 27 are apparently prepared to slake the thirst of twelve tribes---one well per tribe.

The murmuring of verse 24 is the first gripe recorded east of the Red Sea (see 14:11--12 and comments). It is the first in a long series of complaints (the Army has a word for it, if the Greeks don't!) that harass and dog Moses' footsteps until he loses his temper and loses his inheritance with it (Num. 20:10). "Murmuring" is the first step in rebellion (Deut. 31:27; Neh. 9:17), and rebellion (Ezek. 2:3; Num. 20:24, 27:14) is as witchcraft and idolatry (1 Sam. 15:23), according to the Third Person of the Godhead.

The order is:

1\. A noncooperative attitude.

2\. A silent resentment, [unexpressed.]{.class_s4N4-0}

3\. An irritable disposition.

4\. A quiet, low complaint.

5\. A secret underground muttering.

6\. A resentment, [sounded ]{.class_s4N4-0}forth.

7\. A complaint sounded forth [loudly.]{.class_s4N4-0}

8\. An angry complaint voiced [publicly.]{.class_s4N4-0}

9\. An angry exhortation to act on the complaint.

10\. [ARMED SEDITION.]{.class_s4N4-0}

The Korean and Vietnam Wars are beautiful illustrations of this sequence. [Korea ]{.class_s4N4-0}will take you through the first five steps. [Vietnam ]{.class_s4N4-0}begins with a resentment sounded forth and [freely expressed. ]{.class_s4N4-0}By 1965, this is "an angry complaint voiced publicly." The campus "crusades" of 1967--1970 were ARMED SEDITIONS, and no amount of baloney served up by [Life ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [Time ]{.class_s4N4-0}magazines (and the Democratic Party) will ever produce sirloin steaks. What these sources, in their naiveté ([and money-making tradition]{.class_s4N4-0}) called "dialogues," []{#c1X6.xhtml_page_363}"confrontations," "minority opinions," "expressing their viewpoint," etc., was, and shall be, ARMED SEDITION to overthrow a form of government. In 1920--1930, these people were drawn in cartoons with bombs in their hands, and they were called ["Bolsheviks."]{.class_s4N4-0} In 1970, they planted the bombs and threw them, and they were called ["Civil Rights' Workers"]{.class_s4N4-0} or "minority extremists." (Why not rename a rattlesnake with a moniker like ["a repine circumfinest"]{.class_s4N4-0}?).

The murmuring of verse 24 is a universal trait in human nature. We never get enough (Eccl. 6:7). The great military commander Joshua had occasional lapses in his thanksgiving and can be found, at least one time, bellyaching right along with his troops (Josh. 7:7). The mighty [Elijah]{.class_s4N4-0}, who decapitated 400 false prophets in one afternoon, can be found moaning and groaning under the juniper tree like the world had come to an end. [Jonah ]{.class_s4N4-0}growls because he had the greatest revival in the history of evangelism, and [Moses ]{.class_s4N4-0}whines because he doesn't have any revival. [Peter ]{.class_s4N4-0}objects to having his feet washed; [David ]{.class_s4N4-0}complains, at times, until one would think nothing in the world was right (Psa. 25, 27, 36, 38, 40, etc.). The chosen [apostles ]{.class_s4N4-0}murmur (Luke 19:7) exactly as the most vicious enemies that Christ ever had (John 6:41, 43, 61). Only Paul seems to have learned to be ["content" ]{.class_s4N2-0}in whatever[ "state"]{.class_s4N2-0} he found himself (Phil. 4:11), and we cannot find [one record]{.class_s4N4-0} in the Pauline Epistles of a [personal complaint]{.class_s4N4-0} lodged against God because of adverse circumstances or "hard times." [Paul "glories" in his infirmities]{.class_s4N4-0} (2 Cor. 11:30)!

[]{#c22K.xhtml}

# Chapter 16 {.heading_s4G-0}

16:1 "And they took their journey from Elim, and all the congregation of the children of Israel came unto the wilderness of Sin, which is between Elim and Sinai, on the fifteenth day of the second month after their departing out of the land of Egypt.

2 And the whole congregation of the children of Israel murmured against Moses and Aaron in the wilderness:

3 And the children of Israel said unto them, Would to God we had died by the hand of the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}[ ]{.class_s4ND}in the land of Egypt, when we sat by the flesh pots, and when we did eat bread to the full; for ye have brought us forth into this wilderness, to kill this whole assembly with hunger.

4 Then said the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}[ ]{.class_s4ND}unto Moses, Behold, I will rain bread from heaven for you; and the people shall go out and gather a certain rate every day, that I may prove them, whether they will walk in my law, or no.

5 And it shall come to pass, that on the sixth day they shall prepare that which they bring in; and it shall be twice as much as they gather daily."

Now the vast assembly is on its way. They are going down the west coast of Sinai, but they are not going anywhere near in a straight line. The trip takes them back and forth into the wilderness area as they move. They have spent fifteen to twenty days encamped in the wilderness of Etham. "[The]{.class_s4N2-0} [wilderness of Sin"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 1) is the last half of the peninsula, stretching out approximately 80--90 miles down the coast. (If we assume the Israelites crossed the Red Sea []{#c22K.xhtml_page_365}after seven days\' journey down the coast---see chapter 12 and comments---they have used up [ten days]{.class_s4N4-0} of the thirty before they get to Marah.) They arrive at ["the wilderness of Sin" ]{.class_s4N2-0}thirty days after the Passover of chapter 12.

Five camping places are mentioned in Numbers 33:3--9 in traveling from Rameses to Elim; from Elim to Mt. Sinai,[ six ]{.class_s4N4-0}stations are mentioned (Num. 33:10--15). The reader should observe that the three-days trip of 15:22 [was NOT in a straight line,]{.class_s4N4-0} as you will find it on [all the maps. ]{.class_s4N4-0}The journeys from Etham to Marah to Elim are inland, and the children of Israel return to the Red Sea after leaving Elim.

This explains why all of the critics of chapters 13--14 erred in "finding the stations." All of them placed the crossing [too far north,]{.class_s4N4-0} and then they stretched the stations out in a straight line along the Red Sea to get to Sinai in the allotted time. (This Bible perversion is based on rejection of the text through unbelief. The words [scholarship, scientific research,]{.class_s4N4-0} and [investigations]{.class_s4N4-0} should never be attached to such irrational doggerel. An encampment ["by the Red sea"]{.class_s4N2-0} \[Num. 33:10, 21:4\] is noted as such. The efforts of the entire body of "godly Conservatives" to nullify the account of chapters 13--14 is the cause of the displacement of Marah and Elim on the maps.)

["Which is between Elim and Sinai"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 1). The wilderness of Sin, then, lies to the north and northwest of the wilderness of Sinai (Num. 1:1, 19, 3:1, 9:1--5, etc.). The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} is completely out of place in making the irrelevant comment that ["there is no wilderness]{.class_s4N4-0} between Wady Ghurundel and Sinai" down the coast "as the crow flies." Israel is traveling southwest []{#c22K.xhtml_page_366}from the desert area a long way [inland]{.class_s4N4-0} from the coast. They are not flying like crows either; they are flying like EAGLES (Deut. 32:11) if you want to really be Scriptural about it.

To say that ["there is no wilderness"]{.class_s4N4-0} between Elim and Sinai is only to say that the writer has misplaced Elim. If the children of Israel had gone down the coast from the Red Sea encampment (Num. 33:7--10), the area called "[the wilderness of Sin]{.class_s4N2-0}" would still be a wilderness. The [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_s4N4-0}excludes this area as being a wilderness because "all is mountain and valley." Exactly how this makes the place "civilized" is a little hard to say. The writer (Dr. Whitelaw) must have had some preconceived idea running through his mind about "wildernesses" being "rocky deserts." But a check with Matthew 15:33 and Mark 8:4 should have cleared that up. The crowds of Matthew 14:19 ["sit down"]{.class_s4N2-0} on ["GRASS"]{.class_s4N2-0} in a ["DESERT"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Matt. 14:13, 15). (Many times, the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}\[1611\] text will clear up the obscurities of archaeologists, Egyptologists, etymologists, and Arabic "wadys.")

["And the whole congregation of the children of Israel murmured against Moses and Aaron"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 2). First it was WATER (15:23--24); now it is BREAD (vs. 3). Again, Clarke abuses the caravan for its lack of character until one would think they were the lowest scum on the face of the earth for complaining. But has not Clarke himself complained about the greatest book in the world on numerous occasions, while eating and drinking in fine style? Isn't human nature peculiar? Here are Conservative scholars talking about the "degradation of Israel" and their stupid carnality and "depravity" in complaining because they are hungry, [while the same scholars have griped, complained, and bellyached about []{#c22K.xhtml_page_367}the living bread of the living God for centuries on end!]{.class_s4N4-0} Bullinger---a Premillennial Conservative---has altered the words of God more than 5,000 times in 1,700 pages, while the [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(any edition) has made better than [10,000 changes]{.class_s4N4-0}. What's the trouble with these "godly, dedicated Conservatives"? [HUNGRY FOR THE FLESH POTS?]{.class_s4N4-0}

["Would to God we had died by the hand of the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord]{.class_s4N9-0}[\...when we sat by the flesh pots\...when we did eat bread to the full\..." ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 3). Having made one error, the majority of the commentators make the second error. It is assumed, by nearly all, that the children of Israel were well fed in Egypt, since they state that they ate ["bread to the full."]{.class_s4N2-0} Again, our commentators have misread the dodges of human nature. If they HAD eaten FLESH to the full, what could have stopped them from eating flesh to the full [in the wilderness?]{.class_s4N4-0} Did you ever read Deuteronomy 32:14? Do you reckon the Egyptians fed them any better than that? What is better than fresh barbecued steak, leg-of-lamb gravy, and goat fixed like DEER meat (Gen. 27:9)? You see, the problem arose when all the commentators rejected the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}reading ["bread"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 3). They were so anxious to read it as ["food in general" ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Exod. 23:25; Deut. 8:3; 2 Sam. 9:7), they forgot that the most common use of the word in the Bible is a literal loaf (or piece) of literal BREAD (Gen. 21:14, 45:23; Lev. 7:13, 26:26; Deut. 16:3; Judg. 8:5; 1 Sam. 10:3--4; etc.).

Often God, in His infinite mercy, places the key to interpretation in a verse right in the same passage; this is done to stop the crackpot critics of the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1611) Bible. Notwithstanding the clear warning in this place, the major commentators go right ahead in their jaded imaginations with expositions that mean nothing at all. []{#c22K.xhtml_page_368}In verses 4--8, you are told that the BREAD they are hollering for is BREAD, and that this bread is something different from "[flesh]{.class_s4N2-0}" (vs. 8). The Jews could not grow wheat (Exod. 29:2; Deut. 32:14) until [later encampments]{.class_s4N4-0} which lasted from eight to sixteen months at a time. The complaint is ["when we did eat bread to the full"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 3). The ["flesh"]{.class_s4N2-0} for the "[flesh pots]{.class_s4N2-0}" is provided in 16:13, and it has no connection at all with ["the bread"]{.class_s4N2-0} found in verses 12 and 15; Bullinger's note here ([Companion Bible,]{.class_s4N4-0} pg. 95) is a nullity.

The Christian observes how God provides necessities first (1 Tim. 6:8), according to His promise (Phil. 4:19). First they were delivered from a [violent death]{.class_s4N4-0} (chap. 14), then they were delivered from dying by [thirst ]{.class_s4N4-0}(chap. 15), and now they are delivered from dying by [hunger ]{.class_s4N4-0}(16:3).

The gripe of verses 2--3, as most gripes, is an exaggeration, and no commentator with sense to breathe would assume that they were "well fed" in Egypt. As most complaints, about 30% of it is an outrageous lie. Moses had NOT brought them out to kill them with hunger (vs. 3). They did NOT choose death in Egypt, or they would have left their cattle in the fields (9:21) and ignored the Passover regulations (12:13). Therefore it follows, as three follows two, that they did NOT eat as well as they said they did (14:13), and although they may have been allowed to fill up on BREAD, they filled up on bread in a queue of slaves who had to stand in line and wait their turn. Time and circumstances again (see remarks under 14:13) have mellowed the past, and they only imagine that they were glutted on "dainties" in Egypt. If Pharaoh was killing all the male children for forty years (Exod. 1:8--16 cf. Acts 7:30), do you reckon he was fattening the ones who survived? Not hardly.

The children of Israel haven't gotten out of the ball and chain for a month, and we find: "How much better it was back in the [good old days]{.class_s4N4-0} when we got up at four in the morning and worked an eighty hour week on two bowls of soup, a cup of coffee, and three pieces of black bread, under a whip-wielding overseer!" [My, my, how time does change things!]{.class_s4N4-0}

Had they forgotten the "black militants," supported by the Supreme Court, rushing into their homes and killing babies in front of them just like Herod had it done in Matthew chapter 2 and just like the priests in Columbia did it in 1950--1955? Had they forgotten the government taking their land from them by allowing illiterate non-landowners to vote taxes on their property till the tax equalled the price of the property, and then they confiscated the land and called it "public property" so "the people" could urinate on it, leave beer cans on it, hold sex orgies on it, and eventually burn it? Had they forgotten the "consolidated government" which made Goshen a part of Tanis---a sort of extended "city limit"? This was done so Pharaoh could integrate any swimming pool they built or any ball diamond they laid out. Had they forgotten the forced bussing which made their children go to schools where they were taught [African ]{.class_s4N4-0}morals instead of Hebrew morals, and [African ]{.class_s4N4-0}music for Hebrew music, and [African ]{.class_s4N4-0}gods for the God of Abraham, Isaac and Jacob? Had they forgotten how [Time, Life, Newsweek, Look, Ebony, Esquire,]{.class_s4N4-0} and the [National Lampoon]{.class_s4N4-0} had used them for whipping posts and scapegoats for 215 years by:

1\. Accusing them of "alarmism" every time they uncovered one of the government plots to destroy their children in public schools by acquitting []{#c22K.xhtml_page_370}anyone who slashed anyone with a knife or beat anyone up with a chain?

2\. Accusing their leaders of "demagoguery" every time they spoke their convictions which didn't match those of the editors of the daily newspapers?

3\. Slanting news stories and setting up propaganda photos to give Egypt the impression that anyone who believed Genesis was a "reactionary"?

4\. Putting pressure on the politicians to keep moving LEFT or lose their popularity at the polls?

Had they forgotten all of this in a little over one month? Evidently!

["I will rain bread from heaven for you\...that I may prove them\..."]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 4). One cannot help but notice the difference in God's provision before the Law (vss. 4--8) and His provision AFTER the Law (Num. 11:33). When the glory of the Lord appears here before the Law (vs. 10), no one panics, but when the glory appears in Numbers 14:10, only Moses' intercession saves the nation from being massacred. The ["certain rate"]{.class_s4N2-0} of verse 4 is found in verse 16. (So much material follows concerning the manna that we shall not print the whole passage in a block, as usual, but we will finish chapter 16 one verse at a time.)

["I will rain bread from heaven"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 4). This ["bread"]{.class_s4N2-0} is called ["angel's food"]{.class_s4N2-0} in Psalm 78:25, and from this we derive the term [angel food cake. ]{.class_s4N4-0}It is called ["bread from heaven,"]{.class_s4N2-0} but Jesus is careful to tell Israel that the source of this bread was not [directly ]{.class_s4N4-0}from God, while the then ["true bread from heaven"]{.class_s4N2-0} (John 6:32) [was ]{.class_s4N4-0}directly from God.

The ["manna"]{.class_s4N2-0} in this chapter is connected with Satan. (Try that one on the next Hebrew or Greek scholar []{#c22K.xhtml_page_371}you meet, who is always talking about "finding things in the originals"!) The manna is corruptible (vs. 21), it breeds worms (vs. 20), and it is connected with the SCALES of Job 41:15 (a scale-like flake \[vs. 14\]), which has the characteristics of a snowflake and a hailstone (Job 38:22). It will rain on Israel (Lam. 5:9) during the Great Tribulation (Rev. 12:6; Mic. 7:14) [when one of ]{.class_s4N4-0}["the heads of leviathan"]{.class_s4NC} (Job 41 cf. Psa. 74:14) [is wounded]{.class_s4N4-0} (Rev. 13:3). But who is sufficient for these things?! Certainly not Baker, Stam, Bullinger, Ballinger, O'Hair, Johnson, Clarke, Lange, Thieme, Smith, Dummelow, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, Robertson, Grotius, Larkin, Pember, the Lockman Foundation, Scofield, Gaebelin, Tremmellius, Henry, Lightfoot, Gill, Coke, Scott, Calvin, Louis De Dieu, Frankenberg, Toy, Whitelaw, Ellicott, Green, Schaff, or the other 30,000 critics of the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}Bible[.]{.class_s4N4-0}

Now, one can make the manna a type of Christ or a type of the word of God and preach some great spiritual truths from it; see Williams' commentary, for example, or the devotional comments of Matthew Henry. But DOCTRINALLY, we are dealing with a fleshy expedient that is only a temporary provision until ["the true bread"]{.class_s4N2-0} (John 6:32) shows up. This is the third reference to God\'s ["law" ]{.class_s4N2-0}in the book of Exodus (see 12:49, 13:9): see the comments under 15:26, where the laws of God have not yet been collated into official, [written statutes.]{.class_s4N4-0}

["On the sixth day they shall prepare that which they bring in; and it shall be twice as much as they gather daily"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 5). The sixth day is either the sixth day of a seven day week, or it is the sixth day after the manna begins to fall. Either interpretation will work because the first manna falls on the sixteenth day of the []{#c22K.xhtml_page_372}month, which would make it one day after the sabbath established in chapter 12. That sabbath was the first day following the fourteenth day of the first month. However, in a month of thirty days (see comments under Gen. 7:11 in the [Commentary on Genesis]{.class_s4N4-0}), the fifteenth day of the second month would not match the Passover date at all; it would be three days later. For the student who has mastered the dispensations (see [The Seven Sevens,]{.class_s4N4-0}), the sixth day is significant in that it is the period of time in which the student is living---A.D. 1000--2000.

16:6 "And Moses and Aaron said unto all the children of Israel, At even, then ye shall know that the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}[ ]{.class_s4ND}hath brought you out from the land of Egypt."

Dummelow is in error in commenting that God was known to be the Lord (vs. 6) by the coming of the quail (vs. 13). This is an easy mistake to make since the quail came in "the evening" (vs. 13). However, a careful reading of the text which honors the word of God sees quickly that the ["KNOW"]{.class_s4N2-0} of verse 6 is because "[the glory of the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord]{.class_s4N9-0}[ ]{.class_s4NE-0}[appeared in the cloud"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 10) that very evening BEFORE any "quail" showed up. The real knowledge that He was ["the LORD ]{.class_s4N2-0}[your ]{.class_s4NC}[God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 12) does not come until the morning (see vss. 8, 12). In typology---see the comments on application under verse 4---it is clear that no man can know the Lord God or God's glory (Heb. 1:3) through the flesh (vs. 8). Only the bread of life can bring assurance that ["the LORD"]{.class_s4N2-0} is ["your God" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 12 cf. Luke 4:4.)

16:7 "And in the morning, then ye shall see the glory of the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}; for that he heareth your murmurings []{#c22K.xhtml_page_373}against the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}: and what are we, that ye murmur against us?"

It is in "[the morning"]{.class_s4N2-0} that ["the glory of the LORD" ]{.class_s4N2-0}is seen (vs. 7), and the glory revealed in verse 10 is revealed to Aaron and Moses---not the congregation. Furthermore, the man who "rightly divides the word of truth" knows that God's glory always appears ["in the morning"]{.class_s4N2-0} (19:16). Since the glory of God is Jesus Christ (2 Cor. 4:6), He rises as the [sun ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Mal. 4:2) in the [morning ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Matt. 13:43) to end the nighttime dispensation (1 Thess. 5:1--6) of the Church (Song of Sol. 3:1, 8). (All of this seems to be obscure to Bullinger, Clarke, Lange, Williams, and the [New Bible Commentary.]{.class_s4N4-0})

["And what are we, that ye murmur against us?"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 7). Notice how Paul uses this refrain in rebuking the Corinthians (1 Cor. 1:12--13). The man bearing reproach for the Lord's sake (Psa. 69:7) is always a target for criticism (Num. 14:10; Amos 7:12--13), and with no visible object to attack (John 1:18), the "stand in" (2 Cor. 5:20) will do (Gal. 6:17).

16:8 "And Moses said, This shall be, when the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}[ ]{.class_s4ND}shall give you in the evening flesh to eat, and in the morning bread to the full; for that the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}[ ]{.class_s4ND}heareth your murmurings which ye murmur against him: and what are we? your murmurings are not against us, but against the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}[.]{.class_s4ND}"

Here the Lord answers the complaint as it came verbatim from the grumbling mouths of the gripers: ["the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord]{.class_s4N9-0}[ ]{.class_s4NE-0}[shall give you in the evening flesh to eat\...bread to the full."]{.class_s4N2-0} Again, Moses says, ["what are we? your murmurings are not against us, but []{#c22K.xhtml_page_374}against the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord.]{.class_s4N9-0}["]{.class_s4N2-0} This should be compared with 1 Samuel 15:26 and Matthew 10:40. Paul also warns the Christian about this sin when he writes Romans 14:3; 1 Corinthians 16:10--11; and 1 Timothy 6:2. Every Christian is a vessel in which the Holy Spirit dwells; hence, griping and complaining about elders "[who]{.class_s4N2-0} [labour in the word and doctrine"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 Tim. 5:17) is classified with such sins as adultery, fornication, and drunkenness (see 1 Cor. 6:9--10; Gal. 5:19--21). From this truth, the Catholic Church has erected the great blasphemous monstrosity of "Papal Infallibility," which includes absolute obedience to the "Vicar of Christ" or the local Bishop (see [Rome, the Great Private Interpreter,]{.class_s4N4-0} 1970). Cyprian (A.D. 195--258) was one of the first to propagate this teaching: i.e. that the Bishop is the direct representative of God---later "vicar of Christ"---and that he is immune from criticism. The first man to call a local pastor "PRIEST" was the great apostate "fundamentalist" Origen (A.D. 185--254), who constructed the Greek text for the [New American Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} (1963) and the [New Revised Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} (1989).

Neither Moses nor Aaron are infallible (see Num. 12; Exod. 32), but both are protected by God (Num. 12:6--8; Acts 7:37), as they were called by God (Heb. 3:1--5, 5:4) for special places of leadership. However, no Catholic "Priest" was ever "called" to anything, as it is apparent from the New Testament that there is no special priesthood (1 Pet. 2:5, 9), no literal sacrifice (Heb. 13:15), no continued "Mass" (Heb. 10:8--12), and no "Holy Father" but God the Father (John 17:11). Murmuring against such a class of hypocrites is not merely permissible, it is ADVISABLE (Tit. 1:13) and to be encouraged. No parallel can be drawn between 16:8 and []{#c22K.xhtml_page_375}the works of Avro Manhattan or Paul Blanshard, unless the "draw-er" is Picasso or Miro. People who can draw a straight line know a "crook" when they see it. Observe: murmur (vs. 2), murmur (vs. 9), murmur, murmur (vs. 7), murmur, murmur, murmur (vs. 8), and MURMUR (vs. 12).

16:9 "And Moses spake unto Aaron, Say unto all the congregation of the children of Israel, Come near before the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}: for he hath heard your murmurings.

10 And it came to pass, as Aaron spake unto the whole congregation of the children of Israel, that they looked toward the wilderness, and, behold, the glory of the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}[ ]{.class_s4ND}appeared in the cloud."

We have commented on the verses under verses 4 and 6. The expression ["come near before the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord]{.class_s4N9-0}["]{.class_s4N2-0} clearly indicates the tabernacle of 33:7, which was in existence before the Tabernacle of chapters 34--40 was built.

16:11 "And the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}[ ]{.class_s4ND}spake unto Moses, saying,

12 I have heard the murmurings of the children of Israel: speak unto them, saying, At even ye shall eat flesh, and in the morning ye shall be filled with bread; and ye shall know that I am the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}[ ]{.class_s4ND}your God."

We have already commented on these matters under verse 6.

16:13 "And it came to pass, that at even the quails came up, and covered the camp: and in the morning the dew lay round about the host."

Flesh and bread are provided, and it's a shame that they couldn't have asked for this (vs. 2) instead of griped about it. (I realize that is no way to end a sentence, but that is the "original [American koine,]{.class_s4N4-0}" and you can't improve on it!)

The quails were not really ["quails"]{.class_s4N2-0} according to Ehrenberg; they were ["flying fish."]{.class_s4N4-0} (That's some [flying fish]{.class_s4N4-0} you found there, Doc---with FEATHERS?! You don't read the Bible very carefully, do you, Doc? Did you see Psa. 78:27?) The quails are not really flying fish according to Ludolf, Patrick, and Scheuchzer; they were [locusts! ]{.class_s4N4-0}(You don't have to "stand up"---Num. 11:32---to catch locusts, silly! You wade into them---[see 10:4--6!]{.class_s4N4-0})

The quails around this area fly low, and 160,000 have been seen at one time during one season on the island of Capri. Quails from Syria and Arabia "winter" in North Africa, and their migratory flights go right across the Sinaitic Peninsula.

Clarke devotes more than 1,000 words to "dress up" the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1611) text where it will be respectable, but he doesn't do anything more than just superimpose his own authority over the words of the Holy Spirit. Adam Clarke's motive (as the motives of Origen, Eusebius, Pamphilus, Westcott, Hort, Thieme, Wuest, Alford, et al.) was fine: he wanted to defend the Bible from the apparent contradiction of Numbers 11:31--35. If the passage is read one way, a skeptic can assume that 16,000,000,000 quail are lying three feet high on the ground for an area of 225 square miles: about 8,000 quail per person! But this ancient "boo-boo" is as old as Celsus (c. A.D. 180) and Porphyry (A. D. 232--303). It is quite typical of that type of Bible exegesis which assumes the whale couldn't have swallowed Jonah, the bush could not []{#c22K.xhtml_page_377}have really burned (3:2), the Red Sea was the Bitter Lake called the "Sea" etc., etc.

The text in Numbers 11 states that ["the people STOOD UP"]{.class_s4N2-0} to gather live quail (Num. 11:32); they did not [stoop down]{.class_s4N4-0} to pick up [dead ones. ]{.class_s4N4-0}The Lord ["let them fall"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Num. 11:31); He did not slaughter them in the air. And none of them were said to "fall to the ground." (The [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}Bible is always a good bit clearer than any of its correctors.)

16:14 "And when the dew that lay was gone up, behold, upon the face of the wilderness there lay a small round thing, as small as the hoar frost on the ground."

The manna is from above and has nothing at all to do with any "tamarisk" or anything like a "tamarisk." It is supernaturally provided from a supernatural source; to emphasize this, the Lord Jesus supernaturally provides ["bread in the wilderness" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(Matt. 15:33--38, 14:15--21), exactly as He will supernaturally provide it again in Revelation 12:6, 14. Those who get this bread in the Tribulation do so at the "peril of their lives" (Lam. 5:9); and it will fall in the areas of Carmel, Gilead, Bashan and Sela--Petra in Edom (see comments under Rev. 12:6 in the [Commentary on Revelation)]{.class_s4N4-0}.

The ["coriander]{.class_s4N2-0} [seed" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(Num. 11:7) is the Hebrew word for the Babylonian god of fortune---J[upiter]{.class_s4N4-0}! The manna looks like frost or snowflakes, and it melts like frost or snowflakes (vs. 21). It is plainly the "treasures" mentioned in Job 38:22 which God reserves for ["the time of Jacob's]{.class_s4N2-0} [trouble" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(Jer. 30:7).

16:15 "And when the children of Israel saw it, they said one to another, It is manna: for they wist not what it was. And Moses said unto them, This is the bread which the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}[ ]{.class_s4ND}hath given you to eat."

This is what Clarke calls "a most unfortunate translation" (see similar lament on 3:22); the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} feels exactly the same way about it. They both correct the Holy Bible here to read "this is a [gift]{.class_s4N4-0}" instead of ["it is manna."]{.class_s4N2-0} Clarke thinks it should have been "what is this?" But of course, Clarke really thought nothing of the kind; he simply read one of the corrupt [Septuagint ]{.class_s4N4-0}manuscripts written 300 years after the death of Paul. This manuscript was copied in [Vaticanus]{.class_s4N4-0} and [Sinaiticus]{.class_s4N4-0} and others, and it was inserted in verse 15 in place of the Hebrew text. The Hebrew text ["man hu"]{.class_s4N4-0} cannot really be translated "[what ]{.class_s4N4-0}is this," for as anyone knows, the Hebrew word for "what" is ["mah."]{.class_s4N4-0}

The highly accurate and scientific text of 1611 corrects both of these erroneous readings. Israel gave it a proper name (["manna"]{.class_s4NC}) since they did not know what it was. (Captain Cook reported an animal in Australia as being a "kangaroo." Someone discovered later that [kangaroo]{.class_s4N4-0} was the native word for "what did you say?") Deuteronomy 8:3, 16 makes it clear that they had never encountered the substance before, so a local weed (tree or bush) is out of the question. The manna is a flake-like substance (Job 41:15) that feeds the people in the wilderness (Psa. 74:14). and it is the "bleeding" of a "wound" which no man on this earth has ever seen.

"This is the bread which the [Lord]{.class_s4N9-1}[ ]{.class_s4NE-1}hath given you to eat"[ (vs. 15). As we have noted, beautiful []{#c22K.xhtml_page_379}spiritual similitudes can be found in making the manna a type of Christ, or even better, a type of the Bible.]{.class_s4N3-2}

1\. [All men]{.class_s4N4-0} need bread; it is a universal food (John 6:48).

2\. It suits [all ages]{.class_s4N4-0} from babies to the senile (Luke 4:4).

3\. It can be fixed in a [number of forms]{.class_s4N4-0} (Heb. 5:12)---cakes, pancakes, pies, biscuits, rolls, loaves, cookies, etc.

4\. It is white (purity---Psa. 12:6).

5\. It is loathed by [carnal believers]{.class_s4N4-0} (Num. 21:5).

6\. It is from Heaven (Psa. 105:40).

7\. It can suffice [any ]{.class_s4N4-0}appetite (vs. 18).

8\. IT MUST BE USED, OBEYED, AND PASSED ON TO OTHERS (vs. 19).

9\. It will not be needed on the ["seventh day"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rev. 20:1--6 cf. Jer. 31:33--34; Zech. 13:3--4), for the incarnate Word will be present on the earth at that time.

16:16 "This is the thing which the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}hath commanded, Gather of it every man according to his eating, an omer for every man, according to the number of your persons; take ye every man for them which are in his tents."

The ["omer" ]{.class_s4N2-0}of the text is nearly three quarts and a pint; the ephah (vs. 36) is about seven gallons and two quarts. This means that in order to feed 2,000,000 people daily, over 1,000,000 gallons of manna had to fall every day! If that isn't ["bread to the full"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 8), what is?

16:17 "And the children of Israel did so, and gathered, some more, some less.

18 And when they did mete it with an omer, he that gathered much had nothing over, and he that gathered little had no lack; they gathered every man according to his eating."

It is apparent that some could not eat the full quota of an omer, while others went beyond it. The thing to notice about the text, spiritually, is that no matter how much of the Bible you are able to learn, you won't get more than you need, and no matter how little you are able to grasp, it will meet the demands of your spiritual life.

16:19 "And Moses said, Let no man leave of it till the morning.

20 Notwithstanding they hearkened not unto Moses; but some of them left of it until the morning, and it bred worms, and stank: and Moses was wroth with them.

21 And they gathered it every morning, every man according to his eating: and when the sun waxed hot, it melted."

The commandment of verse 19 is to ["prove them"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 4), exactly as the commandment of verse 27. The children of Israel (as all men everywhere) miss the mark and ["come short of the glory of God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rom. 3:23).

["They hearkened not unto Moses"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 20). And so today, many despise the word of God as ["light bread" ]{.class_s4N2-0}unfit for a gourmet's stomach (Num. 121:5); they no more "hearken to Moses" than they do to Peter, James, John, Matthew, Mark, Jude, Luke, and Paul (John 5:46). A []{#c22K.xhtml_page_381}modern Jewish Rabbi pays no more attention to Moses (where Moses contradicts the Babylonian [Talmud]{.class_s4N4-0}) than a Reformed Presbyterian pays attention to dispensational truth where it contradicts Calvin and Gill.

["It bred worms, and stank\...and when the sun waxed hot, it melted" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vss. 20--21). Plainly, then, it was CORRUPTIBLE and quite unlike the word of God in that respect (1 Pet. 1:23). It is the wicked who "melt" (Psa. 58:8); the hills will melt (Psa. 97:5); and even this earth will melt (2 Pet. 3:10--12). Long after the sun, moon, and stars have vanished in a universal conflagration, accompanied by the blast of the archangel and the summons to the Judgment (Rev. 20:11--14), the Bible will stand as authoritative and as eternal as God Almighty Himself (Matt. 24:35).

16:22 "And it came to pass, that on the sixth day they gathered twice as much bread, two omers for one man: and all the rulers of the congregation came and told Moses.

23 And he said unto them, This is that which the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}hath said, To morrow is the rest of the holy sabbath unto the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}: bake that which ye will bake to day, and seethe that ye will seethe; and that which remaineth over lay up for you to be kept until the morning.

24 And they laid it up till the morning, as Moses bade: and it did not stink, neither was there any worm therein.

25 And Moses said, Eat that to day; for to day is a sabbath unto the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}: to day ye shall not find it in the field.

26 Six days ye shall gather it; but on the seventh day, which is the sabbath, in it there shall be none.

27 And it came to pass, that there went out some of the people on the seventh day for to gather, and they found none.

28 And the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}said unto Moses, How long refuse ye to keep my commandments and my laws?

29 See, for that the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}hath given you the sabbath, therefore he giveth you on the sixth day the bread of two days; abide ye every man in his place, let no man go out of his place on the seventh day.

30 So the people rested on the seventh day.

31 And the house of Israel called the name thereof Manna: and it was like coriander seed, white; and the taste of it was like wafers made with honey."

This section reveals that the fourth commandment is a CEREMONIAL LAW, absolutely, and it is an [extra-Decalogue law]{.class_s4N4-0} given before the moral laws of the "Ten Commandments." Observe how the Sabbath is never connected with Genesis 2:1--3 until Moses receives the revelation from Mount Sinai (see detailed comments under Gen. 2:1--3 in the [Commentary on Genesis]{.class_s4N4-0}). The ceremony of "resting on the seventh day" every week is first instituted here---not on Mount Sinai!

Mary Ellen White and the Seventh-day Adventists show a woeful amount of irreverence and ignorance in treating the Scriptures about the Sabbath as they do. The "commandments and laws" (vs. 28) are tied in with a [ceremonial Sabbath,]{.class_s4N4-0} given to man (Mark 2:27) by God (vs. 29) BEFORE any tables of stone show up anywhere and long AFTER everyone in the book of Genesis was buried along with their grandchildren (Gen. []{#c22K.xhtml_page_383}50:23). The Pharaoh-like compromise of the Seventh-day Adventists (see remarks under 10:10--11) is successfully pulled off on carnal Christians by refusing to exposit 2 Corinthians chapter 3 or any part of it, refusing to accept the New Testament revelation on the Sabbath given in Romans chapter 13, insisting on basing doctrine on pre-Crucifixion passages given to Jews under the Law (Matt. 5--7), and altering the [commandments of Christ]{.class_s4N4-0} (John 15:10, 14, 17) to the [Mosaic ]{.class_s4N4-0}commandments when reading l John 2:4.

Real Bible believers, of course, are not fooled by this juggling act for a minute. The entire system of Seventh-day Adventism is no more Scriptural than the ["Charismatic Glossolalia"]{.class_s4N4-0} of the "Christian Businessmen Association" (1960--1970) or the Immaculate Conception or the Assumption of Mary or Baptismal Regeneration or Unconditional Election or any other dozen pagan superstitions taught by Bible-perverting "Christians."

["To morrow is the rest of the holy sabbath unto the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord]{.class_s4N9-0}["]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 23). They are to obey the New Testament commandment ["give us this day our daily bread"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Matt. 6:11), and where the daily ration is missing (vs. 26), the Lord has provided [two days\']{.class_s4N4-0} rations the day before (vs. 29). All attempts to make the manna a natural food end in dismal failure, for what "natural food" shows up every day but [one]{.class_s4N4-0}---and the [same one day ]{.class_s4N4-0}each time!---and then has the forethought to show up in [twice ]{.class_s4N4-0}the quantity on the day preceding the lack? And further, what natural product remembers to breed worms every time it is left overnight EXCEPT ONE NIGHT, which is the [same night]{.class_s4N4-0} each time? On that ONE night, it decides to stay pure! (What were you saying, Doctor, []{#c22K.xhtml_page_384}about the "tamarisk"? Are you sure you aren't getting a little psychotic?)

From this passage, the Rabbis developed the doctrine of the "Sabbath-day's journey" (see comments under Acts 1:12 in the [Commentary on Acts]{.class_s4N4-0}). It was to the effect that any trip over around 1,000 cubits (or near an English mile) was a violation of the Sabbath, as you didn't have to go any further than that to pick up the manna because it ["lay round about the host"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 13). The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} gives the distance at "six stadia" (Roman measurement).

["To day ye shall not find it in the field\...and they found none" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vss. 25, 27). If they had believed Moses, they would have stayed in their tents and would not have gone out to look for it (note Num. 14:41--42 for a similar action). ["For to gather"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 27) is archaic English for "in order to gather" (see "Swing low, sweet chariot, comin' FOR TO CARRY ME HOME," etc.).

No sensible person has trouble with ["mete" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 18), and if they do, they can look it up in any modern dictionary. No one should be bothered about ["wist"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 15) from the old Germanic ["wis," "weiss," "wissenschaft," "wissen,"]{.class_s4N4-0} etc. The price for getting these words "updated" is amply demonstrated in the [New Scofield Reference Bible, ]{.class_s4N4-0}where the reader (who is stupid enough to desire a "clearer translation") is confronted with the word "SACRAMENT" [for water baptism]{.class_s4N4-0} (see footnote on pg. 1174)! This wolf in sheep's clothing has also inserted the [ASV]{.class_s4N4-0} readings of 1901 into the book of Proverbs with the blatant profession that it is the [KING JAMES ]{.class_s4N4-0}TEXT! See this amazing bamboozle by comparing the frontispiece ["Authorized King James Version"]{.class_s4N4-0} with the text printed in Proverbs 1:3, 3:34, 5:20, 6:26, 7:27, 13:23, 15:11, 17:3, []{#c22K.xhtml_page_385}8, 23--24, 19:24, 20:16, 21:3, 23:11, 33, 30:16, 26; and other places. (Note: this is true of editions printed before Oxford made changes in the 1990s.)

["How long refuse ye to keep my commandments and my laws?" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 28). Observe that the blast is aimed at Moses as well as the children of Israel. Moses has certainly not disobeyed the injunction, but God classifies Moses with Israel even where Moses segregates himself. Notice that Moses does not fully learn the lesson of "sharing the afflictions of God's people" [as one of them]{.class_s4N4-0} until after his mountain-top experience in 34:9; observe: ["pardon OUR iniquity and OUR sin, and take US\...."]{.class_s4N2-0}! How different this all sounds from: ["YE have sinned\...atonement for YOUR sin"]{.class_s4N2-0}! (32:30). Daniel goes through this same thing, and when he gets to his prayer in Daniel chapter 9, it is[ "our," "we,"]{.class_s4N2-0} and[ "us"]{.class_s4N2-0} throughout. Peter learns the lesson and graduates from ["though all men\...yet will I never"]{.class_s4N2-0} to ["look on us"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Acts 3:4)!

["And the house of Israel called the name thereof Manna"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 31). The reader will observe that now the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} "banks the furnace," and in the face of the proper name with a capital letter, it decides that the word used here was that of verse 15 and that it ["probably"]{.class_s4N4-0} meant "a gift." But this is a long way from "it should have been [gift]{.class_s4N4-0}"! Clarke got too far in, and now he has to overlook the verse or change his comment on verse 15. To avoid the embarrassment of being caught redhanded, the worthy exegete refers us to verse 15 without comment. This leaves the reader staring crosseyed at an exposition which insists the first manna was a[ question answered by Moses,]{.class_s4N4-0} but the question []{#c22K.xhtml_page_386}was [retained ]{.class_s4N4-0}as the proper name even [after ]{.class_s4N4-0}Moses had answered the question.

But this is exactly what the [King James]{.class_s4N4-0} text stated to start with. They called it[ "manna," ]{.class_s4N2-0}for they "knew not what it was"! They never did accept Moses' explanation for it, and the surest proof that this interpretation is infallible and without contradiction is the fact that the Holy Spirit Himself recorded the word as ["manna"]{.class_s4N2-0} in the New Testament (John 6:31, 49, 58); never once did He suggest that it was a "gift" or "what is it"? The Greek for ["what is it"]{.class_s4N4-0} is certainly not [mu, alpha, nu, nu, alpha]{.class_s4N4-0} (Gk. for ["manna"]{.class_s4N2-0}). It should therefore be reinforced in the heart of the Christian that no commentator, living or dead, is able to correct the [King James]{.class_s4N4-0} text as it now stands. Any and all corrections are departures from revealed truth, and neither the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} with its discussion of ["mah,"]{.class_s4N4-0} nor Clarke with his discussion of ["muh,"]{.class_s4N4-0} can do anything with the text that a baby or calf couldn't do with the same words.

["And the taste of it was like wafers with honey"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 31). It is therefore counterfeited in the Episcopal and Catholic Churches by giving the communicant a small, [round]{.class_s4N4-0} piece of sweet-tasting wafer. This [round ]{.class_s4N4-0}object, patterned after the sun (see 10:21 and remarks), is reduplicated in the circular tonsure, the clerical collar, the large gold plate over the Roman altar, the circles over the heads of the "saints," and the circle around the Egyptian obelisk at St. Peters in Rome. The symbol is common among the depraved religions of ancient Greece, Rome, Syria, Phoe--nicia, China, Egypt, India and Asia Minor (see the detailed work on the matter by Hislop, [The Two Babylons]{.class_s4N4-0}).

16:32 "And Moses said, This is the thing which the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}commandeth, Fill an omer of it to be kept for your generations; that they may see the bread wherewith I have fed you in the wilderness, when I brought you forth from the land of Egypt.

33 And Moses said unto Aaron, Take a pot, and put an omer full of manna therein, and lay it up before the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}to be kept for your generations.

34 As the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}commanded Moses, so Aaron laid it up before the Testimony, to be kept.

35 And the children of Israel did eat manna forty years, until they came to a land in--habited; they did eat manna, until they came unto the borders of the land of Canaan.

36 Now an omer is the tenth part of an ephah."
