The ["omer"]{.class_s4N2-0} that is filled maintains its purity for forty years, and the writer of Hebrews tells us that it was laid up in the Holy Place with Aaron's rod and placed in a ["golden pot"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Heb. 9:4). This pot (vs. 33) is undoubtedly one of those "borrowed" from Egypt. It is missing entirely in 1 Kings 8:9, indicating that it was either stolen or destroyed during the time of the book of Judges (possibly in 1 Sam. 4--6), or the Lord raptured it exactly as he did the Ark of the Covenant (Jer. 52; 2 Chron. 36 cf. Rev. 11:19). Israel's enemies never did get ahold of the ark that Moses had made.

Verses 34--36 could have been written after the construction of the Tabernacle because verse 35 is apparently written on the plains of Moab (Num. 23--25). However, 33:7 clearly indicates that a tabernacle of some kind (for a ["Testimony"]{.class_s4N2-0}) was [already in existence]{.class_s4N4-0} before the main Tabernacle was built, and it is to this []{#c22K.xhtml_page_388}tabernacle that the children of Israel assemble in verse 9. Clarke's conjecture that Ezra may have written the words is quite reminiscent of the ancient Graf-Wellhausen-Astruc combobulation (there's one for you!) which was disproved several hundred times before 1920. (It would seem, if one reads the commentators attentively, that [Hezekiah, Josiah,]{.class_s4N4-0} and [Ezra ]{.class_s4N4-0}wrote or collected three-fourths of the Old Testament; when a Conservative leans over this way, it simply means that he has lost his balance. See comments under Prov. 31:1--4 in the [Commentary on Proverbs]{.class_s4N4-0}.)

Still, there is beautiful spiritual application in the chapter. God's people have left the world and are on a journey to the Promised Land, and John Bunyan never improved on the allegory. When we look back at Egypt, we murmur and complain, but when we walk by faith and face the wilderness, we see the "glory of the Lord." Paul wrote his revelations on the "heavenlies" (Eph. 1--3) while rotting in a [Roman dungeon. ]{.class_s4N4-0}In the wilderness, God provides for one day at a time (Matt. 6:34), and He supplies ["food and raiment"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 Tim. 6:8). He also gives an infallible leadership (40:36) which has no more need for Ra--guel's eyes (see comments under 18:17) than an avalanche needs a push.

Rest follows redemption, and so the Sabbath is only revealed now---2,500 years after Adam's hoe broke God's rest (Gen. 2:1--3). A trip which should have taken twelve to twenty days took forty years (Deut. 1:2) because the people of God lacked faith to overcome "giants."

This is the sad tale of the life of many a Christian where Canaan stands for [spiritual victory]{.class_s4N4-0} instead of Heaven itself. Not even Moses, who stayed in communion with God while his church apostatized (see chaps. []{#c22K.xhtml_page_389}32--34), was able to avoid the snares of the "old nature" (Num. 20:11). Like Peter, James, John, and Paul, he goes the way of all flesh (Deut. 34:5), for ["the wages of sin is DEATH"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rom. 6:23).

[]{#c264.xhtml}

# Chapter 17 {.heading_s4G-0}

17:1 "And all the congregation of the children of Israel journeyed from the wilder--ness of Sin, after their journeys, according to the commandment of the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}, and pitched in Rephidim: and there was no water for the people to drink.

2 Wherefore the people did chide with Moses, and said, Give us water that we may drink. And Moses said unto them, Why chide ye with me? wherefore do ye tempt the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}?

3 And the people thirsted there for water; and the people murmured against Moses, and said, Wherefore is this that thou hast brought us up out of Egypt, to kill us and our children and our cattle with thirst?

4 And Moses cried unto the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}, saying, What shall I do unto this people? they be almost ready to stone me.

5 And the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}said unto Moses, Go on before the people, and take with thee of the elders of Israel; and thy rod, wherewith thou smotest the river, take in thine hand, and go.

6 Behold, I will stand before thee there upon the rock in Horeb; and thou shalt smite the rock, and there shall come water out of it, that the people may drink. And Moses did so in the sight of the elders of Israel.

7 And he called the name of the place Massah, and Meribah, because of the chiding of the children of Israel, and because they tempted the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}, saying, Is the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}among us, or not?"

By now, the reader of the Bible should be fairly well acquainted with the style of the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}text of 1611.

a\. It is always clear, concise, plain, and pointed in its statements.

b\. Wherever a verse seems to be "obscure," the reader is failing to investigate some other information relevant to it.

c\. It consistently corrects the errors of the commentators and revisers, and occasionally it presents a reading superior to the Hebrew and Greek text of the [Receptus.]{.class_s4N4-0}

d\. It is quite able to hold its own against the flaccid and insipid texts of the [New ASV,]{.class_s4N4-0} Old [ASV]{.class_s4N4-0}, [Amplified Version, Living Bible, New English Bible, RV, RSV, NRSV,]{.class_s4N4-0} the [Vulgate, ]{.class_s4N4-0}and Origen's [Hexapla.]{.class_s4N4-0}

The Christian will always do well to use the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1611) as a scientific textbook for higher and lower criticism to correct any other "Bible" printed, before or after it.

["And all the congregation of the children of Israel\...pitched in Rephidim" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 1). Now the column moves slowly down the coast, and they turn inland to camp at two more stations  ([Dophkah and Alush]{.class_s4N4-0}---Num. 33:12--14) before reaching [Rephidim]{.class_s4N4-0}. On the way, they are collecting ["badger's skins"]{.class_s4N2-0} (36:19). The trip from the coast to Sinai runs thirty days with four encampments; this would be around eight to ten days apiece. Rephidim is within five miles of Mount Sinai (vs. 6).

["And there was no water for the people to drink"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 1). Having proved to the children of Israel that He was able to supply their needs (Phil. 4:19), the Lord now tests them again and tests them on the same grounds He tested them less than 25 days before! As usual, they flunk it (see "the Law of Human Collapse," Judg. 1--13).

["Give us water that we may drink\...and the people thirsted there for water; and the people murmured against Moses"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 2--3). Same song, same key (15:24). This is the fourth murmuring (16:2), but now there is less excuse for griping than when thirst first struck the column (15:24). Two things are apparent, now, that were not then: First, the Lord could produce water out of nowhere if He so desired, for this was demonstrated in the case of the manna and the quail. Secondly, anyone who really got thirsty could try DIGGING (Num. 21:18), and he probably would strike water. They have now traveled more than 160 miles (see comments under 15:22), and approximately 2,000,000 people and thousands of sheep, goats, cows, horses, mules, and camels have been kept in good health. Obviously there is water somewhere near them, but, no! no one gets out his "entrenching shovel." It is easier to---(and I forbear to use an old army expression), so they do!

["Wherefore is this that thou hast brought us up out of Egypt, to kill us and our children and our cattle with thirst?"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 3). God's people again are pained and discouraged, tested and tempted. The wilderness journey becomes long, hot, dusty, dull, and tedious (Psa. 42:1); and the flesh asserts itself again and again (1 Cor. 15:47) with its everlasting and infernal "I want," "I need," "me first," "What about ME," etc.

The modern "civil rights worker," radical revolutionary, hippy, zippy, yippy, student demonstrator, "rebel against the establishment," etc., would be terribly humiliated if he could really see his picture "like it is." You see, his clan, from the hedonists to the New Morality, can be described in two words; no more than TWO are needed to describe their complete character, values, []{#c264.xhtml_page_393}goals, ambitions, ethics, worth, intellect, principles, motives, plans, and philosophy: ["ME FIRST."]{.class_s4N4-0}

["Why chide ye with me? Wherefore do ye tempt the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord]{.class_s4N9-0}[?"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 2). Moses reminds them of this sin (16:8) again in Deuteronomy 6:16. The Lord speaks about it in no uncertain terms in Numbers 14:22. The sin is so cardinal that Paul resurrects the rebuke in the New Testament (1 Cor. 10:9) to warn the Christian believer.

They are "tempting the Lord" because they are saying, ["Is the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord ]{.class_s4N9-0}[among us, or not"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 7); this implies that they are demanding proof that God is "among them." Since they have had proof that God is "among them" sixteen times in a row since Moses and Aaron confronted Pharaoh, the complaint is a dangerous and ominous "murmuring." (The Lord finally took them at their word, or rather their heart attitude, and He refused to go "among them"---see Num. 14:42 and Deut. 1:42.) But when you're thirsty, [you're thirsty!]{.class_s4N4-0}

["And Moses cried unto the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord]{.class_s4N9-0}[\...they be almost ready to stone me" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 4). By now, the ministerial student should be getting a real picture of the pastorate. The ordained elder (1 Pet. 5:1; 1 Tim. 3:1) is being forced to his knees every time he turns around (15:25, 32:30), and it is a miracle that he is able to walk on his feet any distance at all.

As others have said: "The reason why most successful churches have a [dictator ]{.class_s4N4-0}for a pastor is because he has to be able to put down all the [dictators ]{.class_s4N4-0}in the church." Korah, Dathan, and Abiram (Num. 16) are not exactly a pastor's dream of ideal Sunday School teachers, and Aaron and Miriam certainly couldn't be trusted with a bus ministry or a Daily Vacation Bible School (Exod. 32; Num. 12).

Someone is always whining about something, and the easiest and most popular ministry the average "church leader" has is the organization and cultivation of gripers (Heb. 12:15). In the South, it is a universal pastime. The gripers are in fulltime service, wholly dedicated to the proposition that THEY ONLY are qualified to make decisions for the flock, and only SOMEONE ELSE should be held responsible for their decisions.

More than one Bible-believing preacher has been "stoned" (vs. 4) without getting it from a bottle. Observe that Stephen (Acts 7:58--60), David (1 Sam. 30:6), Paul (Acts 14:19), Naboth (1 Kings 21:14), and the Lord (John 8:59) all go through the experience that Moses mentions. If you think men would not stone God if given the chance, read John 8:59, 10:31, and 11:8.

["And the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord ]{.class_s4N9-0}[said\...take\...thy rod, wherewith thou smotest the river\...I will stand before thee there upon the rock in Horeb\...and there shall come water out of it, that the people may drink"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 5--6). This is plainly the rod of GRACE, for it was used in the Egyptian deliverance before the Law. The first time it was used, it was both the serpent of Numbers 21:9 and the POLE ( 7:10). John 3:14--16 is the context of 17:6, then, and the rush of water which follows is plainly a forecast of the Holy Spirit (John 7:38) who was made available (John 7:39) through the Rock (1 Cor. 10:4) that was smitten for sinners (Isa. 53:3--8)!

This ["rock"]{.class_s4N2-0} is the[ "Rock"]{.class_s4N2-0} of Deuteronomy 32:15, 18 and 30--31, and from it we can get ["oil"]{.class_s4N2-0}[ ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Deut. 32:13), ["fire"]{.class_s4N2-0}[ ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Judg. 6:21), ["honey"]{.class_s4N2-0}[ ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Psa. 81:16), and[ "water" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(text). This ["Rock"]{.class_s4N2-0} is not the rock of Romanism, ["for their rock is not as our Rock"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Deut. 32:31), even when they (["our enemies"]{.class_s4N2-0}---Deut. 32:31) []{#c264.xhtml_page_395}bear witness. ["Their rock"]{.class_s4N2-0} is the one Jesus called ["Satan" ]{.class_s4N2-0}in the context of the passage which every Pope since Leo I used as a proof text for his religious monstrosity (Matt 16:18--19, 23). ["Their ]{.class_s4NC}[rock"]{.class_s4N2-0} is a cursing (Matt. 26:74), grace-rejecting (Gal. 2:14), arguing (Acts 10:14) commercial fisherman (Matt 4:18); ["our Rock" ]{.class_s4N2-0}is the holy, spotless, sinless, infallible ["Son of the living God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Matt. 16:16), who would no more approve of the Apocrypha or the Vatican See than the trash put out by the Lockman Foundation or the ecumenical council of His day (Matt. 23:3--33).

["The rock in Horeb" ]{.class_s4N2-0}plainly tells us that Rephidim is near Mt. Sinai, because in the next move (19:2), they camp at the base of ["Jebel Musa"]{.class_s4N4-0} and Moses goes up (19:3). Horeb, then, stands for the area of Mt. Sinai as well as the mount itself (vs. 1).

There are two ["Meribahs"]{.class_s4N4-0} (vs. 7) in the Scripture, and Moses has named the disaster of Numbers 20:7--13 after this first Meribah. As Napoleon, Moses has trouble with places connected with [water]{.class_s4N4-0}; Moses' "Waterloo" was at the second Meribah where the old temper (see 2:12, 32:19) asserted itself again, in spite of God's commandment (Num. 20:11--12). The Rock "once smitten" (Isa. 53:3--6) is not to be smitten again every Sunday morning at 11:00 a.m. in a Baalite Black Mass of Babylonian black magic. Once smitten (Heb. 10:8--12), its work is permanent, and the real Christian has no more need for the "sacrament of the Mass" than he has need of a six-legged skunk. Roman "rocks" have never been the Christian Rock, and when the Lord Jesus Christ was "smitten," it is instructive to notice that a ROMAN did it (John 19:34).

["Massah, and Meribah"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 7) are from ["nasah"]{.class_s4N4-0} (Heb.) and ["rub"]{.class_s4N4-0} (Heb.), and the words mean "to try or tempt," or "to quarrel and strive." The archaic English ["chiding"]{.class_s4N2-0} is better than the [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1901) reading since the word [chide]{.class_s4N4-0} is the word applied to children's struggles, and ["the children of Israel" ]{.class_s4N2-0}are plainly children at this stage if we are to believe Galatians chapters 3--4. (Never make the mistake of thinking the [ASV]{.class_s4N4-0}---[any edition,]{.class_s4N4-0} old or new---is an improvement over the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}text.)

17:8 "Then came Amalek, and fought with Israel in Rephidim.

9 And Moses said unto Joshua, Choose us out men, and go out, fight with Amalek: to morrow I will stand on the top of the hill with the rod of God in mine hand.

10 So Joshua did as Moses had said to him, and fought with Amalek: and Moses, Aaron, and Hur went up to the top of the hill.

11 And it came to pass, when Moses held up his hand, that Israel prevailed: and when he let down his hand, Amalek prevailed.

12 But Moses' hands were heavy; and they took a stone, and put it under him, and he sat thereon; and Aaron and Hur stayed up his hands, the one on the one side, and the other on the other side; and his hands were steady until the going down of the sun.

13 And Joshua discomfited Amalek and his people with the edge of the sword.

14 And the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}said unto Moses, Write this for a memorial in a book, and rehearse it in the ears of Joshua: for I will utterly put out the remembrance of Amalek from under heaven.

15 And Moses built an altar, and called the name of it Jehovah-nissi:

16 For he said, Because the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}hath sworn that the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}will have war with Amalek from generation to generation."

No sooner has Jesus Christ (in type) showed up, and no sooner has the Holy Spirit (in type) appeared than---along comes the FLESH and asserts itself! (The Hebrew roots for "Amalekite" are [ayin, mem, lamed,]{.class_s4N4-0} and [mem, lamed, and koph,]{.class_s4N4-0} which mean "sin" or "mischief.") Amalek is descended from Jacob's brother Esau (Gen. 36:15--16), and there is not much doubt about Esau's typology in the Old Testament (see remarks under Gen. 25:25 in the [Commentary on Genesis]{.class_s4N4-0}).

Paul describes Amalek's attack in Galatians 5:17 and Romans 7. It is a perfect picture of the spiritual warfare that goes on between the two natures of the believer. It is edifying to note that the flesh lies dormant in the sinner until the Holy Spirit shows up, and then, my, what a revelation (cf. Rom. 7).

The flesh thirsts for the[ physical water]{.class_s4N4-0} of Rephidim, while the Spirit thirsts for the [spiritual water]{.class_s4N4-0} of Ephesians 5:26. As soon as the [blessing ]{.class_s4N4-0}comes (Acts 6:7--8), here comes Amalek (Acts 6:9--11). As soon as [revival ]{.class_s4N4-0}shows up (Acts 8:7--8), here comes Amalek (Acts 8:19). As soon as sinners start getting under [conviction ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Acts 13:44), here comes Amalek (Acts 13:45). (The reader is referred to the detailed exposition of these verses in the [Commentary on Acts.]{.class_s4N4-0}) Every time the ["rock"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Matt. 16:18) gave forth its healing streams (John 11:41--44), the descendants of Amalek (John 11:47--57) show up---[armed to the teeth]{.class_s4N4-0} (Matt. 26:47).

["Then came Amalek, and fought with Israel" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 8). Up until here, in mercy, the Lord did the fighting for them (see chaps. 14--15) and guided them around armed combat (13:17), but by now, they should have tasted enough victory to fully trust in God for everything, and they can not go on ["by sight"]{.class_s4N2-0} forever (2 Cor. 5:7). Somewhere, they must exercise faith (Heb. 5:14); following the "fullness of the Spirit," the "exercise" starts! The Amalekites pounce on the tail end of the column (Deut. 25:17--18) and undoubtedly carry off men, women, children, cattle, and goods (see 1 Sam. 30:1--2).

["And Moses said unto Joshua"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 9). The statement is remarkable. This is the first mention of the name, and it is the Old Testament form of ["Jesus."]{.class_s4N4-0} Notice the unusual handling of this truth in Acts 7:45 and Hebrews 4:8 by the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}text of 1611. "Jesus" and the "Rock" are synonymous, so "Jesus" does not show up until [the "Rock" is smitten for sinners. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Furthermore, it is "Jesus" who is now going to do the [fighting ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Gal. 2:20). Observe: ["So Joshua\...fought with Amalek"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 10). Joshua was the son of Nun (Num. 13:16, 14:6) and a descendant of Ephraim (Num. 13:8). He is 53 years old, or 27 years younger than Moses, and his name is mentioned 250 times in the Bible. (The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} here goes back to the old Graf-Wellhausen kick and suggests that perhaps "Ezra" wrote down the word ["Joshua"]{.class_s4N2-0} here instead of ["Hoshea."]{.class_s4N4-0} But all of this means nothing; see the comments on [Jethro]{.class_s4N4-0} under 4:18.)

["Moses, Aaron, and Hur"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 10). This is probably the Hur of 1 Chronicles 2:19, and the Rabbis insist that he was Moses' [brother-in-law, ]{.class_s4N4-0}having married Miriam. His grandson helped construct the Tabernacle (31:2).

["When Moses held up his hand, that Israel prevailed: and when he let down his hand, Amalek prevailed"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 11). Moses is praying (9:33), and the "lifting up of hands" (1 Tim. 2:8) shows that prayer must accompany any battle which the Lord is fighting for the believer (Eph. 6:10--13). The flesh has attacked first (vs. 8), and since "surprise" is one of the decisive elements in a good attack, the believer cannot resort to ["confidence in the flesh"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Phil. 3:3); he must take the resource of prayer and use it as a weapon. Joshua chooses his men (vs. 9) as Christ chose His (Matt. 10:1--4), but ["all flesh is grass"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Isa. 40:6), and it is ["not by might, nor by power, but by my spirit, saith the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord ]{.class_s4N9-0}[of hosts" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(Zech. 4:6).

The deeper student of the Scripture will observe the [Prophet ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Moses) and the [Priest ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Aaron) and the [King ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Hur is from the tribe of Judah---Gen. 49:10) are in charge of this battle (vs. 10). Clarke refuses to accept verse 11 as a true statement and insists that ["when Moses held up his hand"]{.class_s4N2-0} and ["let down his hand"]{.class_s4N2-0} that he was not actually [holding up his hand and letting down his hand]{.class_s4N4-0} ([Commentary,]{.class_s4N4-0} pg. 391). However, this only succeeds in destroying the mine where the jewels are (see comments under 6:1--8), for here is a jewel if you ever saw one! If both of Moses' hands are literally raised---as the text states (["hands,"]{.class_s4N2-0} vs. 12)---then we have a fourth picture of Christ in the passage; [our victory lies in the Crucifixion]{.class_s4N4-0} (Rom. 6:1--8), where ["both hands" are held up by nails!]{.class_s4N4-0} (Comments like the above, by men like Adam Clarke, are never to be countenanced by the believer, and he should avoid all such comments, by any commentator of any hue, like the Black Death. Clarke made the passage figurative on the basis of the [Jerusalem []{#c264.xhtml_page_400}Targum. ]{.class_s4N4-0}My, what a "reliable authority for correcting the Holy Bible.)

1\. Christ is smitten as the Rock (vs. 6).

2\. Christ is smitten for sinners by the grace of God (vs. 6).

3\. Jesus (Joshua) can then help the believer after he has received the Holy Spirit (vss. 6, 10).

4\. The Holy Spirit, in the person of Christ, ["maketh intercession for us"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 12 cf. Rom. 8:26) when we are too weak to pray. The [ground ]{.class_s4N4-0}of our petitions (and every prayer we ever had answered) was the finished [blood atonement]{.class_s4N4-0} of a crucified Prophet (vs. 12).

When in doubt, throw Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, Lange, Dummelow, Clarke, and the [New Bible Commentary ]{.class_s4N4-0}OUT!

["And his hands were steady until the going down of the sun" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 12). The Lord Jesus does not waver until His work is finished (Heb. 12:1--3). His hands remain up, and His head remains up, until the atonement is over (John 19:30). The prayer warrior should be ["faithful unto death"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rev. 2:10), and the real Christian soldier fights until his hand cleaves to the sword (2 Sam. 23:10).

["And Joshua discomfited Amalek\...with the edge of the sword"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 13). Then it is the Lord Himself who does the fighting, although the troops must [handle the weapon]{.class_s4N4-0} (Jer. 48:10; Psa. 149:6). ["The sword of the Spirit" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(Eph. 6:17) is the weapon for handling the flesh (2 Cor. 10:4); the "butter-knives of the spirit," the "hairpins of the spirit," and the "toothpicks of the spirit" ([RV, ASV, NASV, RSV, NRSV,]{.class_s4N4-0} etc.) are no more capable of putting down the flesh than a shad minnow []{#c264.xhtml_page_401}can "put down" a bluefish. It is ["the sword of the Spirit" ]{.class_s4N2-0}that brings "discomfort" (see archaic English above!) to the flesh; if you don't believe it, watch Felix and Agrippa (Acts 24, 26) writhing under the slashes of the greatest swordsman of their day.

["Write this for a memorial in a book, and rehearse it in the ears of Joshua"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 14). This is the first time that "writing" has been mentioned in the Bible directly. The superstitious scholars of the [RV ]{.class_s4N4-0}committee (1881--1885), who followed the ridiculous advice of Ellicott, Lightfoot, Hort, and Westcott, altered the passage in Judges 5:14 so the ["ready writer"]{.class_s4N2-0} (cf. Psa. 45:1) would be eliminated. This was done to satisfy the infidelic scholarship of German "higher criticism," which insisted that no one could possibly have handled a "pen" [before ]{.class_s4N4-0}1200 B.C. (Observe how the ignorant translators of the [RV, ASV,]{.class_s4N4-0} and [RSV ]{.class_s4N4-0}all affix the late date to Exodus \[1200--1300\].) It was then discovered---too late to do the [RV ]{.class_s4N4-0}translators any good---that writing had been going on as far back as 1500 B.C. And yet, in spite of this archaeological "light," the truth-rejecting translators of the [American Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} (1901) [went right on with the damnable mess by preserving the RV reading of Judges 5:14. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Such is the folly and stupidity of "godly Conservatives." There was no reason for the alteration of the text in the [RV]{.class_s4N4-0}, [and after this had been proved,]{.class_s4N4-0} the [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1901) continued with the alteration as though nothing had happened. This is very typical of the kind of men who compose the Lockman Foundation and other Bible rejecting Conservatives who believe in "the verbally inspired ORIGINALS"!

["Write this for a memorial in a book"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 14). Dummelow would never let any of his readers think for []{#c264.xhtml_page_402}a minute that the ["book"]{.class_s4N2-0} referred to is the BIBLE; therefore, he tells us that the record is to be classified with any "general record" written describing any general event that may have been "preserved for many generations." Williams is absolutely incorrect in stating that this book was the book of 1 Kings 2:1--4; 2 Chronicles 23:11; and Malachi 4:4. It is ["the book" ]{.class_s4N2-0}of ["the wars of the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord]{.class_s4N9-0}["]{.class_s4N2-0} (Num. 21:14), and one section of this book is called ["the book of Jasher" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(Josh. 10:13; 2 Sam. 1:18), meaning ["The Book of the Upright."]{.class_s4N4-0} Joshua is plainly the writer of some passages in the Law (Josh. 24:26), and there is no more sense in hunting "lost books" to disprove the canon of Scripture---study the famous Dead Sea Scroll hoax about some ["Book of Enoch"]{.class_s4N4-0}---than there is in taking trips to the moon to prove Darwin's theory.

Verse 14 matches Deuteronomy 25:17--19, and the reader should also study Numbers 24:20 in connection with the battle, for God's wrath against Amalek seems to be of a peculiar ferocity that He rarely places against a nation. Students who wish to identify Amalek in the Twentieth Century should make a prayerful study of the material in Genesis chapters 29--39 in the [Commentary on Genesis]{.class_s4N4-0}. No one has yet determined the peculiar place of Amalek in the Twentieth Century. The slighting of the verses which are given above in Numbers 24, Deuteronomy 25, Joshua 10, and 2 Samuel 1 cannot be justified.

The serious student of the Scripture who ignores such trivial translations as the[ "New" ASV]{.class_s4N4-0} (1963) can see that Amalek is not merely a reference to some desert tribe that died out in 1 Samuel chapter 30. Amalek is the downfall of Saul (1 Sam. 15), and David carries out the orders of Exodus 17:14 when he has the Amalekite executed (2 []{#c264.xhtml_page_403}Sam. 1:13--16) more than 400 years after Israel came out of Egypt. Furthermore, Haman (one of the greatest types of the Antichrist in the Bible) is a descendant from the king whom Saul refused to kill (1 Sam. 15:8 cf. Esth. 3:1).

["I will utterly put out the remembrance of Amalek from under heaven"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 14). As a type of the old nature or "flesh" in the believer,[ "the]{.class_s4N2-0} [Lord ]{.class_s4N9-0}[hath sworn that the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord ]{.class_s4N9-0}[will have war with Amalek from generation to generation"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 16). From Adam to Eternity (Rev. 20), there is no peace declared between God and the "flesh" (Rom. 8:1--13).

["And Moses built an altar, and called the name of it Jehovah-nissi"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 15). The proper name means "Jehovah is my banner." Until A.D. 1900, wars were fought around a ["standard bearer,"]{.class_s4N4-0} and the illustrations of valor connected with this standard bearer are staggering in number. The "royal banner given for display to the soldiers of the cross" signifies the warfare of 2300 B.C. to A.D. 1900, which is now obsolete. Occasionally (as in the terrible defensive warfare of the Korean hills, 1950--1952), the banner was still the rallying point, but now it is only a vicious matter of "saving face," and the banner is kept by battles of attrition which deplete both sides to no purpose. The invention of the [automatic weapon]{.class_s4N4-0} (machine guns, automatic rifles, etc.) has put the "standard bearer" out of business.

["Rally 'round the flag, boys!"]{.class_s4N4-0} might have been a fair sentiment for 1700--1880, but even in the Civil War men quickly discovered that the Napoleonic and Washingtonian methods of infantry battle were getting out of hand. The advance point of any army, at that time, was the "standard" or the "colors." The Romans carried []{#c264.xhtml_page_404}one (200 B.C.--A.D. 300), the armies of Charlemagne carried one (A.D. 742--814), the Crusaders carried one (A.D. 1000--1200), and many a soldier lost his life picking up the colors or trying to prevent the colors from falling into the hands of the enemy.

["Oh say, does that Star-Spangled Banner yet wave\...?"]{.class_s4N4-0} is the American version, but this too is meaningless. Long ago, the government allowed its citizens to make shirts, hats, and dresses out of the "banner." Americans have also spit on the "banner," burned it, cut it up, and hung it upside down. Long before these actions took place, Roman Catholic priests were dumping "holy water" on the flag when it draped a casket.

Any man stupid enough to carry a "banner" into battle today will receive no sympathy from the grave-digging detail when they start looking for his dogtags. Modern warfare is a mobile, treacherous, hide-and-seek, death-dealing marathon, where "to be seen" is [to be "shot."]{.class_s4N4-0} The troops of 17:15 can fight under "the [Lord ]{.class_s4NB}their banner," for they cannot get shot until they are within [bow range]{.class_s4N4-0} (which is about one-tenth the range of an "88"), and the battle standard is a rallying point to show the advance of the troops through the enemy lines.

["The ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord ]{.class_s4N9-0}[hath sworn that the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord]{.class_s4N9-0}["]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 16). The verse causes considerable confusion among the major commentators, and the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}reading is a vast improvement over any and all of the improvisations.

A. "Because the hand of Amalek was against the throne of the Lord" ([Pulpit Commentary,]{.class_s4N4-0} pg. 72).

B. "We pledge loyalty to the Eternal's banner" ([Moffatt]{.class_s4N4-0}).

C. "Surely the hand lifted up on the banner of Jah" ([Bullinger]{.class_s4N4-0}).

D. "Because his hand is against the throne" ([Dummelow]{.class_s4N4-0}).

E. "For the hands shall be upon the ensigns of war" ([Houbigant]{.class_s4N4-0}).

Since none of these readings have any connection with anything anyone is talking about, the "scholarship" of the commentators may be dispensed with without further comment. There is no "throne of Jehovah" in the passage, near the passage, [or near the book;]{.class_s4N4-0} there is no "ensign" present at this time [or before this time;]{.class_s4N4-0} no one has pledged loyalty to [anything ]{.class_s4N4-0}before or after the passage. Amalek's hand was not against a THRONE or an ENSIGN. The commentators have simply lost their senses temporarily, and as this is so recurrent (see vss. 12--14 and comments!) among the enemies of the [Authorized Version]{.class_s4N4-0} (1611), we should not think too much about it. The reading of the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1611) is correct as usual, and the alterations by Liberals or Conservatives are not to be taken seriously by anyone looking for Biblical truth.

[]{#c28H.xhtml}

# Chapter 18 {.heading_s4G-0}

18:1 "When Jethro, the priest of Midian, Moses' father in law, heard of all that God had done for Moses, and for Israel his people, and that the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}had brought Israel out of Egypt;

2 Then Jethro, Moses' father in law, took Zipporah, Moses' wife, after he had sent her back,

3 And her two sons; of which the name of the one was Gershom; for he said, I have been an alien in a strange land:

4 And the name of the other was Eliezer; for the God of my father, said he, was mine help, and delivered me from the sword of Pharaoh:

5 And Jethro, Moses' father in law, came with his sons and his wife unto Moses into the wilderness, where he encamped at the mount of God:

6 And he said unto Moses, I thy father in law Jethro am come unto thee, and thy wife, and her two sons with her."

For the identification of ["Jethro,"]{.class_s4N2-0} see the comments under 2:18 and 4:18. The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} changes ["father in law"]{.class_s4N2-0} to "brother in law" in verses 1 and 6, but they flinch at verses 7 and 8 and leave ["father in law"]{.class_s4N2-0} as it stands. (Why not do it as the [King James']{.class_s4N4-0} translators did it and save yourself the contradiction?)

The ["burnt offering and sacrifices"]{.class_s4N2-0} which Jethro offers, indicates some knowledge of the Law; however, "burnt offerings and sacrifices" are certainly offered by Abel, Noah, and Abraham [before ]{.class_s4N4-0}the giving of the Law. ["The statutes of God, and his laws"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 16) has been taken to mean the statutes and laws of chapters 20--28; []{#c28H.xhtml_page_407}however, caution is necessary in the light of 15:26, which see.

Jethro now brings Zipporah, Gershom, and Eliezer from their "home" to Mount Horeb; "home," for [Jethro,]{.class_s4N4-0} is in the vicinity. He has kept Moses' family for him until the frogs, lice, lightning, blood, hail, and tidal waves are over.

["Gershom"]{.class_s4N2-0} is true of any Christian (see Moses as a type of Christ and Zipporah as a type of the Church in 2:21) whose conversation is in Heaven (Phil. 3:20--21) and who can truthfully sing ["This world is not my home, I'm just a passin' thru!"]{.class_s4N4-0} ["Eliezer"]{.class_s4N2-0} is especially true of the Christian in the wilderness way, for he has been delivered from "Pharaoh's sword" (2 Tim. 4:17), and God has been his help (Acts 26:22).

At this point, the Conservative scholar Adam Clarke becomes greatly confused at the chronology, and nearly all of the commentators follow his lead into nowhere. Bullinger, Clarke, Lange, and Williams all insist that the chapter is a [parenthesis ]{.class_s4N4-0}to be inserted between verses 10 and 11 in Numbers chapters 10. None of them consider that [18:27 ]{.class_s4N4-0}is a parenthesis occurring before. The arguments used to prove that chapter 18 takes place AFTER the giving of the Law and after the building of the Tabernacle are:

1\. The ["burnt offering and sacrifices"]{.class_s4N2-0} of verse 12. This overlooks the Biblical truth that burnt offerings and sacrifices were given by Abel, Noah and Abraham long before Moses was born.

2\. ["The statutes of God, and his laws"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 16). This overlooks the Biblical statements of Exodus 15:26 and Genesis 26:5, which clearly show that ["statutes"]{.class_s4N2-0} and[ "laws"]{.class_s4N2-0} were in existence before []{#c28H.xhtml_page_408}the giving of the Ten Commandments or the Levitical regulations that attended the Mosaic Law.

3\. The fact that Jethro ate bread ["before God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 12), implying "before the Tabernacle." However, God's presence had been fixed to certain localities long before here; for example, see Genesis 10:9, 18:22, 19:27, 20:15.

Furthermore, the student should observe that the ["laws"]{.class_s4N2-0} and ["statutes"]{.class_s4N2-0} discussed here deal with [individual ]{.class_s4N4-0}matters and [private ]{.class_s4N4-0}matters arising between individuals (vs. 16); the vast body of Mosaic legislation is not discussed. There is no problem with the passage if one remembers that Jethro stayed with Moses until chapter 19, [and the statement of 18:27 ]{.class_s4N4-0}is a real "parenthesis" as it records something that took place BEFORE then.

Williams (who is a Hyperdispensationalist like Bullinger and Stam) insists that Zipporah cannot be a type of the Church, but of course, this insistence is always on the basis of that ancient misapprehension that a thing cannot be PRESENT until it is REVEALED (see comments under Eph. 3:1--5 in the [Commentary on Galatians--Colossians]{.class_s4N4-0}). [Zipporah, Asenath,]{.class_s4N4-0} Eve and [Rebekah ]{.class_s4N4-0}are types of the Church; and if Ephesians chapter 3 had never been written, no one would have been any wiser.

Moses' deliverance from Pharaoh (vs. 4) is a reference to 2:15, not 5:12, which is apparent by the fact that the boys were both born [before they were "sent back"]{.class_s4N4-0} (vs. 2). (See the comments under 4:24.)

The corrupt [Septuagint]{.class_s4N4-0}, written many years after the death of Paul, has altered verse 6 to read ["and THEY said\...."]{.class_s4N4-0} This, as usual, is an effort to help God out of []{#c28H.xhtml_page_409}a jam so verse 7 would not contradict verse 6. But we cannot appreciate the "thoughtfulness" of Origen's little helpers. "Mamma's little helpers" have corrupted enough Scripture to fill a fair-sized garbage can, and we have learned long ago that the Platonic-Origenistic method of Bible revision is about as reliable as a linen gun barrel. Canon Cook, Geddes, Kennicott, Bullinger, with the Syriac, follow the Alexandrian alteration; but Keil, Kalisch, Rosenmuller, and Patrick (for a change!) side with the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}text.

18:7 "And Moses went out to meet his father in law, and did obeisance, and kissed him; and they asked each other of their welfare; and they came into the tent.

8 And Moses told his father in law all that the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}had done unto Pharaoh and to the Egyptians for Israel's sake, and all the travail that had come upon them by the way, and how the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}delivered them.

9 And Jethro rejoiced for all the goodness which the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}had done to Israel, whom he had delivered out of the hand of the Egyptians.

10 And Jethro said, Blessed be the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}, who hath delivered you out of the hand of the Egyptians, and out of the hand of Pharaoh, who hath delivered the people from under the hand of the Egyptians.

11 Now I know that the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}is greater than all gods: for in the thing wherein they dealt proudly he was above them.

12 And Jethro, Moses' father in law, took a burnt offering and sacrifices for God: and Aaron came, and all the elders of Israel, to eat bread with Moses' father in law before God."

Most of the passage is self-explanatory and needs little comment. The word ["obeisance"]{.class_s4N2-0} is plainly the word for bowing (2 Sam. 1:2; Gen. 17:3), while the "kissing" is the standard Oriental salutation between friends or loved ones (Luke 7:45; 1 Cor. 16:20; Ruth 1:9; 1 Kings 19:20; Gen. 31:55; etc.).

["And they came into the tent"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 7) is a reference to [Moses' tent,]{.class_s4N4-0} for not even the elders of Israel were allowed in the Tabernacle. They appear AT the Tabernacle or "before" the Tabernacle, [but not IN it]{.class_s4N4-0} (Num. 11:16 cf. 7:8--9).

The [LXX ]{.class_s4N4-0}has added twelve words to verse 8 to match verse 9, but that is to be expected for the entire "[LXX]{.class_s4N4-0}" (written between A.D. 150--550) breathes of Platonic logic and Neo-Platonic rationalism. (Nowhere is this stink and stench of corrupt heathen philosophy more manifest than in the [New American Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} \[recommended by Bob Jones University\] and the "brand new, spanking new, revised, new, NEW, NEW American Standard TRIPE." \[You have to study [Madison Avenue soap ads]{.class_s4N4-0} to get the proper picture of Bible revision these days.\])

There is a difference among the commentators about Jethro's supposed conversion (vs. 11). On the face of the account, it appears that Jethro's experience is the experience of Naaman (2 Kings 5:17) and the experience of Nebuchadnezzar (Dan. 3:29). (Note the ["New" Scofield Reference Bible's]{.class_s4N4-0} alteration of the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}text of Daniel 3:25 while professing to be the[ Authorized Version. They have advertised a fake text.]{.class_s4N4-0})

18:13 "And it came to pass on the morrow, that Moses sat to judge the people: and the people stood by Moses from the morning unto the evening.

14 And when Moses' father in law saw all that he did to the people, he said, What is this thing that thou doest to the people? why sittest thou thyself alone, and all the people stand by thee from morning unto even?

15 And Moses said unto his father in law, Because the people come unto me to inquire of God:

16 When they have a matter, they come unto me; and I judge between one and another, and I do make them know the statutes of God, and his laws.

17 And Moses' father in law said unto him, The thing that thou doest is not good.

18 Thou wilt surely wear away, both thou, and this people that is with thee: for this thing is too heavy for thee; thou art not able to perform it thyself alone.

19 Hearken now unto my voice, I will give thee counsel, and God shall be with thee: Be thou for the people to Godward, that thou mayest bring the causes unto God:

20 And thou shalt teach them ordinances and laws, and shalt shew them the way wherein they must walk, and the work that they must do.

21 Moreover thou shalt provide out of all the people able men, such as fear God, men of truth, hating covetousness; and place such over them, to be rulers of thousands, and rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens:

22 And let them judge the people at all seasons: and it shall be, that every great matter they shall bring unto thee, but every small matter they shall judge: so shall []{#c28H.xhtml_page_412}it be easier for thyself, and they shall bear the burden with thee.

23 If thou shalt do this thing, and God command thee so, then thou shalt be able to endure, and all this people shall also go to their place in peace.

24 So Moses hearkened to the voice of his father in law, and did all that he had said.

25 And Moses chose able men out of all Israel, and made them heads over the people, rulers of thousands, rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens.

26 And they judged the people at all seasons: the hard causes they brought unto Moses, but every small matter they judged themselves.

27 And Moses let his father in law depart; and he went his way into his own land."

The lengthy passage is all the speech of one man. This in itself should have been a warning to the commentators, but all of them, without exception, ignore the warning. (I have never read a commentary yet that did not brag about Jethro's great advice and his "spiritual discernment" etc.; even Clarke suggests that Jethro was inspired when he "conned" Moses in the passage.) [The International Sunday School Lessons]{.class_s4N4-0} have presented this portion of Scripture for its readers every other year for twenty years, and every time with the same comment: Jethro was smart; Jethro was prudent; Jethro gave excellent advice; etc., etc. Bullinger drops six verses of the passage with no comment, and the [Wycliffe ]{.class_s4N4-0}commentator assumes that Moses DID ["inquire of God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 15, 24), although it is mentioned nowhere in the text. Dummelow, Ellicott, the [New Bible Commentary, ]{.class_s4N4-0}Lange, Matthew Henry, and all of the []{#c28H.xhtml_page_413}Conservative commentators give Jethro every benefit of the doubt; and their handling of his character and advice sounds like [Look ]{.class_s4N4-0}magazine writing up Angela Davis or Abbie Hoffman. The universal departure at this point from Biblical truth is one of the most amazing phenomena to take place among "GODLY Conservatives"; to hear them comment on Jethro, one has the uneasy feeling that he is listening to the Associated Press reporting on the [Irish Republican Army]{.class_s4N4-0} (1970--1972).

For violent contrast with the opinions of these apostates, let us examine the Holy Bible and see what it has to say about Jethro.

1\. Jethro objects to Moses SITTING, while everyone else STANDS (see vs. 14---Matt 23:1--2).

2\. Jethro's criticism (vs. 17) is a perfect statement of modern hedonism or personal expediency ("situation ethics"). [Not one word is said about the thing being "right" or "wrong";]{.class_s4N4-0} observe: ["the thing\...is not good."]{.class_s4N2-0}

3\. Why isn't it ["GOOD,"]{.class_s4N2-0} Jethro? ["Thou wilt surely wear away"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 18). [Did Moses wear away]{.class_s4N4-0} (Deut. 34:7)? Wasn't he in better health at 120 than any of your kinfolk were at 90? Would he have "worn away" with God sustaining him (Deut. 34:7)?

4\. ["Thou art not able to perform it thyself alone"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 18). Didn't God know that before Jethro found it out (Num. 11:16, 24)?

5\. ["I will give thee counsel"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 19). What in blazes would a man need with the counsel of a "father-in-law" (see comments under vs. 6) when God talked directly to the man whenever He wanted to communicate (Deut. 34:10).

6\. How does Moses determine who "hates covetousness" (vs. 21) and who doesn't? [How did it work out]{.class_s4N4-0} (look at chap. 32.)

7\. ["So shall it be easier for thyself"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 22). How did all of the major commentators miss that one?! There is old Epicurus, and his hedonism is aiming right in your face as plain as a shotgun barrel. ["Easier for thyself"]{.class_s4N2-0}? Is this the motive for service (Matt. 16:24)? Is this the language of ["and God shall be with thee"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 19)?

What kind of "Christian education" produces commentators who would think that Jethro was even being "helpful"? [He is plainly led by Satan,]{.class_s4N4-0} and ["plainly"]{.class_s4N4-0} is the terrible truth. Jethro's advice is so clearly the language of expediency and unregenerate counsel that it would be a miracle if ONE commentator was fooled by it; yet ALL of the major commentators were deceived by it. What should the Bible believer make of this phenomenon?

8\. ["But every small matter they shall judge"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 22). Is this the language of Zechariah 4:10; Luke 14:27, 16:10; Matthew 13:32?

9\. ["And God command thee so"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 23). When did God start commanding according to Jethro's dictation? Is this a case of Matthew 16:22, with Jethro substituted for Peter?

10\. ["So Moses hearkened to the voice of his father in law"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 24), as Adam hearkened to his wife, no doubt (Gen. 3:17)! Since when did "inlaws" take God's side in a controversy where the supernatural was to be obeyed before the rational? [Did any of the commentators read Luke 9:59--62?]{.class_s4N4-0}

11\. ["And Moses chose able men out of all Israel"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 25). [How able did they turn out?]{.class_s4N4-0} Couldn't Clarke and Lange find Numbers 16:1--12?

12 . The men that God chose after Jethro left (!) were [Spirit-filled]{.class_s4N4-0} men, anointed for a job (Num. 11:16--17, 24--25). How did Jethro miss the [prime qualification]{.class_s4N4-0} for an "able man"? (See Acts 6:3, 8 and comments in the [Commentary on Acts]{.class_s4N4-0}.)

You see, in chapter 18 (as in chaps. 13--14), we are dealing with a preposterous situation among the commentators, and it is so confounding that it staggers the mind of a real Christian. How does one explain a [100% agreement]{.class_s4N4-0} between all of the scholars in the interpretation of a passage where every word in the passage breathes of the opposite interpretation? Here is a [unanimous defection]{.class_s4N4-0} from the truth when the entire testimony of either Testament is to the effect that Jethro was led by the Devil!

[Explain it. ]{.class_s4N4-0}There is no way on earth to explain it unless one assumes that every one of the commentators who believed in "our God-breathed Book" approached the passage with [a positive view of unregenerate philosophy]{.class_s4N4-0} and a disregard for the English text as it stands. (See this brought out again in the comments under Col. 2:8 in the [Commentary on Galatians--Colossians.]{.class_s4N4-0}) We may say, without overstatement, that every Conservative commentator from Origen to Dummelow was infected with the virus of Alexandrian philosophy, in spite of the potent inoculation given by Dr. Paul (Col. 2:8).

13\. But the greatest damage done by Jethro's "pastoral counselling" was done by ["autosuggestion,"]{.class_s4N4-0} for one will observe that ever since Jethro placed his wisdom on Moses that Moses felt dependent on Jethro instead of []{#c28H.xhtml_page_416}the Lord! This fatal transference of trust is so manifest in Numbers 10:29--32 that a sober student of the word would almost laugh out loud when he read the passage. Tell me, Doctor, what need did Moses have of Jethro's ["eyes"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Num. 10:31) [when the ark and the cloud]{.class_s4N4-0} (Num. 10:33--36) [scouted out the route three days ahead of time]{.class_s4N4-0} (Num. 10:33)?!

14\. Finally, observe that Ruth the Moabitess (Ruth 1:16--17) had more spirituality than Jethro (Num. 10:30), for no matter how much "goodness" God had spoken concerning Israel (Num. 10:29), Jethro didn't want any part of it! [He couldn't even follow his own advice!]{.class_s4N4-0}

Jethro's advice is about as sound as the advice given by ["Hushai the Archite"]{.class_s4N2-0} in 2 Samuel 17:7--14; it stands---contrary to the opinion of every great "Bible scholar" who ever lived---in the same rank with Stalin's advice to Roosevelt at Yalta and Tehran, Dewey's advice to the faculty members of Columbia University, Huxley's advice to students of history, Hitler's advice to attack Russia, and someone's advice to Abe Lincoln to see a good play on April 14, 1865. The Bible believer should take note again that where there is a unanimous agreement of "all the Conservative scholars" in the interpretation of a passage ([which runs afoul of the King James text]{.class_s4N4-0}), the reader may dump his entire library in the incinerator and chalk it off to "office expenses."

Moses would have done just as well to consult a good astrologist or a good palmist or a good necromancer. [Saul got better advice from a witch at Endor.]{.class_s4N4-0}

[]{#c2AR.xhtml}

# Chapter 19 {.heading_s4G-0}

19:1 "In the third month, when the children of Israel were gone forth out of the land of Egypt, the same day came they into the wilderness of Sinai.

2 For they were departed from Rephidim, and were come to the desert of Sinai, and had pitched in the wilderness; and there Israel camped before the mount.

3 And Moses went up unto God, and the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}called unto him out of the mountain, saying, Thus shalt thou say to the house of Jacob, and tell the children of Israel;

4 Ye have seen what I did unto the Egyptians, and how I bare you on eagles' wings, and brought you unto myself.

5 Now therefore, if ye will obey my voice indeed, and keep my covenant, then ye shall be a peculiar treasure unto me above all people: for all the earth is mine:

6 And ye shall be unto me a kingdom of priests, and an holy nation. These are the words which thou shalt speak unto the children of Israel."

We now view one of the greatest mountain climbers in history, for according to the clear text, Moses goes up into Mt. Sinai SEVEN TIMES in the next few weeks. (Williams can only locate six out of seven, but Bullinger finds all of them.)

["The same day came they into the wilderness of Sinai"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 1). The ["desert"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 2) shows us that when Jethro visited the encampment (18:5), they were already camping ["before the mount"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 2), for the words say ["and had pitched in the wilderness,"]{.class_s4N2-0} showing the past tense.

["The same day"]{.class_s4N2-0} is thought to have been either the [first day]{.class_s4N4-0} of ["the third month" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(most expositors) or the [third day]{.class_s4N4-0} of ["the third month"]{.class_s4N2-0} or the same day of the month they left Egypt (the fifteenth day of the month). The conventional tradition states that ["the third day"]{.class_s4N2-0} of verse 11 was the fiftieth day following the Exodus; hence, Pentecost marks the giving of the Law. If one applies the chronology to the New Testament, fifty days is the interval between the Resurrection and the coming of the Holy Ghost. The Jews allow 45 days for the trip from Egypt to Sinai, making ["the]{.class_s4N2-0} [same day"]{.class_s4N2-0} of our text the first day of the third month.

["And Moses went up unto God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 3). Moses goes up this mountain again in 19:9, 20, 20:1, 24:13, 32:31, and 34:4. He comes down it in 19:7, 14, 25, 20:19, 32:15, 33:7, and 34:29.

From here on in the book of Exodus, we will be dealing with the calling out of a nation; the order of events is:

1\. The national calling (chap. 19).

2\. The national response (chap. 19).

3\. The holiness of God (chap. 19).

4\. The moral commandments of God (chaps. 20--23).

5\. The blood covenant with God (chap. 24).

6\. The order and form of worship (chaps. 24--31).

7\. The disobedience of the nation and their punishment (chaps. 32--33).

8\. The grace of God in dealing with the nation (chap. 34).

9\. The carrying out of the commandments (chaps. 35--40).

["Thus shalt thou say to the house of Jacob"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 3). This is of the utmost importance, for nearly every heresy taught by "Christians" in the last nineteen centuries comes from taking passages aimed at ["the house of Jacob"]{.class_s4N2-0} and placing them heedlessly on the Christian who is in the Body of Christ. To this day, 90% of the "conservative scholars" believe that the covenant of Hebrews 8:8--11 is the covenant made with Christians in the Church Age, although the passage is aimed directly at ["the house of Israel"]{.class_s4N2-0} and ["the house of Judah."]{.class_s4N2-0} This is stated in words that a six year old could not misunderstand. Other examples are the twisted private interpretations of Rome in handling Ezekiel 36:25; Acts 2:38; Malachi 3:10; and Hebrews 10:12. Many of the Fundamentalists, such as Dr. DeHaan, Theodore Epp, and Charles Fuller, handle the passages with the same irreverent carelessness; but this is due to their lack of faith in the English text, not their desire to establish the United Nation's "church."

["Ye have seen\...how I bare you on eagles' wings"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 4). The verse is to be compared with Deuteronomy 32:11 and Isaiah 40:31. It will take place in a more literal fashion in Matthew 24:20 when [the woman]{.class_s4N4-0} of Revelation 12:14 boards planes to fly to Sela---Petra. According to the [Wycliffe ]{.class_s4N4-0}commentator, the Lord should have said "buzzards'" instead of[ "eagles',"]{.class_s4N2-0} for we are informed (Commentary, pg. 68) that the eagle of our text degenerated into a "griffin-vulture." Fortunately, we have the Holy Spirit's interpretation of the word in Revelation 12:6, where he corrects Dr. Phillip Johnson's blundering by reproducing ["eagle."]{.class_s4N2-0} (The [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}\[1611\] is always a much better commentator than any Hebrew or Greek scholar, living or dead.)

["Now therefore, if ye will obey my voice indeed, and keep my covenant"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 5). This begins a period of time afterwards referred to as "from Moses" or "from the Law" or "from the giving of the Law." At this juncture, the nation voluntarily places itself under ["the curse of the law"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Gal. 3:13) in order to get the privileges and blessings promised in the text (vss. 5--6). [With these privileges]{.class_s4N4-0} (Deut. 30:5--10) come judgment and wrath (Lev. 26:14--39) if the covenant is broken or the ["voice"]{.class_s4N2-0} disobeyed. The history of Israel, from this point on, is the history of a nation who broke the covenant made at Mt. Sinai (Judg. 1--10) and never was able to keep it perfectly (Jer. 1--23; Isa. 1--10; Dan. 9; Rom. 9, 11).

["Peculiar treasure\...kingdom of priests, and an holy nation"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 5--6). The three titles indicate:

1\. [A valuable possession]{.class_s4N4-0} (Matt. 13:44) that is different from ordinary possessions and is literally ["peculiar"]{.class_s4N2-0} in that its laws, customs, and manner of life will be looked upon as PECULIAR by the Gentile nations. To change the meaning of the word ["peculiar"]{.class_s4N2-0} (see any [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}in any edition) in order to prove the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1611) is "obsolete" is the height of stupidity. The Jews are ["peculiar" ]{.class_s4N2-0}to this day and always have been, and no change in the word is needed whatsoever. The man who changes it is simply transferring your awe in the word to [himself ]{.class_s4N4-0}so that you will look upon [him ]{.class_s4N4-0}as the final authority instead of the Book. (Yes, that means [you ]{.class_s4N4-0}or any of your friends, even if the man you have in mind is not named JETHRO!)

2\. A[ national entity]{.class_s4N4-0} whose function is [religious ]{.class_s4N4-0}as well as political (Matt. 6:13). This is the enviable possession coveted by the Popes and Cardinals; it []{#c2AR.xhtml_page_421}explains why the last half of Matthew 6:13 has been omitted in all Catholic publications such as the [New American Standard Version,]{.class_s4N4-0} recommended by John R. Rice, and the Jesuit[ Rheims]{.class_s4N4-0} edition of 1582. ["A kingdom of priests"]{.class_s4N2-0} indicates that Israel will serve as a sort of "mediator" between God and the Gentile nations (Isa. 66:12, 21) for a period of time.

3\. ["An holy nation"]{.class_s4N2-0} is something new for a change. There are no "holy nations" on the earth today; the sprawling mass of unregenerate mankind is ["less than nothing"]{.class_s4N2-0} and ["a drop of a bucket"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Isa. 40:17, 15). To international Socialists who are making a living off "making the world a better place to live in," this revealed truth is a monstrous horror. However, when one examines the conduct of international Socialists---Dean Rusk, Sergeant Shriver, Henry Kissinger, Averill Harriman, Alger Hiss, Whittaker Chambers, George McGovern, Henry Luce, Dwight Eisenhower, Friedrich Engels, Joseph Stalin, Robert McNamara, Ralph McGill, Franklin Delano Roosevelt, Leon Trotsky, Ted Kennedy, David Rockefeller, Karl Marx, J. William Fulbright, Drew Pearson, and the Fabian Society---one realizes that they toy with the masses with less reverence than someone who considers them to be ["a drop of a bucket."]{.class_s4N2-0} People have been known to conserve [a drop in the bucket]{.class_s4N4-0} for some thirsty soul; but the conduct of people like Lyndon Johnson, Fidel Castro, Nicolai Lenin, Pope Paul VI, Ho Chi Minh, Che Guevara, Mao Tse-tung, and the demagogues of the Health, Education and Welfare Department is not the conduct of anyone concerned about anyone's "welfare" but [their own.]{.class_s4N4-0}

Russia is no more a ["holy nation"]{.class_s4N2-0} than Italy, and Italy (Rome included) is about as ["holy" ]{.class_s4N2-0}as a garbage []{#c2AR.xhtml_page_422}dump somewhere east of Suez. The United States today is as far from being a ["holy]{.class_s4N2-0} [nation" ]{.class_s4N2-0}as Spain was in the Fourteenth Century; no one but a demented imbecile would think of calling America "a Christian nation." America has NEVER been a "Christian" nation, and she is further from it today than she was in 1900, when she was further from it than she was in 1800, when she had moved 10,000 miles from it since 1700.

God only called ONE nation a ["holy nation,"]{.class_s4N2-0} and when a thing is ["holy"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 Cor. 3:17), [it is for God's use]{.class_s4N4-0} (Num. 16:38). Consequently, when that nation became an "unholy" nation (Judg. 1--10), the [negative prophecies]{.class_s4N4-0} were fulfilled with the same exactness that the [positive prophecies]{.class_s4N4-0} had been fulfilled (Deut. 32--33).

In a "Christian nation," the national leaders of the nation would have to publicly confess Jesus Christ as Lord and Saviour, and they would have to be engaged in missionary activity constantly to win other peoples to Jesus Christ---[not His "teaching."]{.class_s4N4-0} (Jumped too quick, didn't you?!) No national leader in America has ever publicly testified of his "New Birth" before a TV camera or on a national radio network. No national leader in America has ever expressed ANY concern over the fact that 90% of his nation, [in any age,]{.class_s4N4-0} was unregenerate and headed for Hell.

The word [Christian]{.class_s4N4-0}, as used by the press in America, is never a reference to anything that the word originally meant (Acts 11:26). The word has come to mean an international Socialist who is working to attain "peace on earth, good will to men." (Of course, this is like saying the word [Catholic]{.class_s4N4-0} means "Christian." It will when [NASV]{.class_s4N4-0} means "Holy Bible.")

In spite of the fact that the text says ["These are the words which thou shalt speak unto the children of Israel"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 6), Dummelow steps in and filches them and applies them to the Church. The reason for doing this is the reason which all postmillennialists give when stealing: i.e., the sacred function of Israel passed to the Church when Israel rejected the Messiah, and therefore, 1 Peter 2:9 is proof that the Church has [permanently replaced Israel. ]{.class_s4N4-0}This is the "conceited ignorance" spoken of by Paul in Romans 11:25. (For a beautiful display, the reader is referred to Matthew Henry's infamous "commentary" still recommended by every "Conservative" school in America. It would seem that [conceit, ignorance, and thievery]{.class_s4N4-0} are highly desirable qualities for a ministerial student who is "studying the Bible." Why would anyone recommend an ignorant, conceited crook simply because he was a "godly, dedicated Conservative"? [You did read Romans 11:25, did you not?]{.class_s4N4-0})

The peculiar people are so ["peculiar"]{.class_s4N2-0} (in the sense of [peculiar]{.class_s4N4-0}, as Englishmen understand the word [peculiar]{.class_s4N4-0}) that Dr. Batmiriam and Dr. Sachs (Israel Inst. of Biol. Research) say that the patterns of whirls and loops on the fingers of the remnant now returning to Israel (1919--1960) are totally unlike the country from which they migrated.

19:7 "And Moses came and called for the elders of the people, and laid before their faces all these words which the LORD commanded him.

8 And all the people answered together, and said, All that the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}hath spoken we will do. And Moses returned the words of the people unto the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}.

9 And the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}said unto Moses, Lo, I come unto thee in a thick cloud, that the people may hear when I speak with thee, and believe thee for ever. And Moses told the words of the people unto the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}.

10 And the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}said unto Moses, Go unto the people, and sanctify them to day and to morrow, and let them wash their clothes.

11 And be ready against the third day: for the third day the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}will come down in the sight of all the people upon mount Sinai.

12 And thou shalt set bounds unto the people round about, saying, Take heed to yourselves, that ye go not up into the mount, or touch the border of it: whosoever toucheth the mount shall be surely put to death:

13 There shall not an hand touch it, but he shall surely be stoned, or shot through; whether it be beast or man, it shall not live: when the trumpet soundeth long, they shall come up to the mount."

When Moses calls the elders in (see note under 4:29), he lays ["before their faces"]{.class_s4N2-0} the ["WORDS which the LORD commanded him"]{.class_s4N2-0}---not the thoughts, message, principles, teaching, fundamentals, or instructions. The modern tendency of Fundamentalists and Conservatives---who will go into total apostasy in the next twenty years---is to adopt the Neo-orthodox position that NOW the Bible merely ["contains]{.class_s4N4-0} the word of God." The greatest proponents of "Neo-orthodoxy" in America, between 1930 and 1970, were men who [rejected ]{.class_s4N4-0}(or altered) [more than 5,000 WORDS in the AV (1611) text and then would not even profess to believe that the alterations were infallible. ]{.class_s4N4-0}The modern "Conservative" is usually a man who believes that somewhere God wrote a book []{#c2AR.xhtml_page_425}which no one ever found. If it could be found, it would be the "Word of God," but since no one [has ]{.class_s4N4-0}it, we should be content to reconstruct something [like ]{.class_s4N4-0}it which is "holy" as long as it preserves the "message of the Bible." The prophets of God were much more "idolatrous" in their approach to the Bible than the modern "Conservative" (see Jer. 1:9, 6:19, 13:10, 15:16; Dan. 9:12, etc.). One would think that John (Rev. 22:18--19; John 3:34, 5:47, 8:47, 14:23, etc.) was a "Bibliolater," and Paul (Rom. 9:17; Gal. 3:8) must have been a blasphemer, if we are to believe the publication in 1970 by John R. Rice on "OUR" Bible (whatever in the world that is).

As we have remarked many times before in this series of commentaries, the only difference between Wilbur Smith, Harry Rimmer, Adolph von Harnack, A. T. Robertson, Toyohiko Kagawa, John R. Rice, and Theodore Epp, Frederick Kenyon, B. F. Westcott, F. J. A. Hort, Johann Griesbach, Harry Emerson Fosdick, Kenneth Wuest, Sloan Coffin, Bishop Pike, Voltaire, Eberhard and Erwin Nestle, Karl Barth, and Emil Brunner is that some of them take out [one ]{.class_s4N4-0}verse, some take out [ten, ]{.class_s4N4-0}some take out a [hundred, ]{.class_s4N4-0}and some [two thousand. THEY ALL IGNORE PROVERBS 30:5.]{.class_s4N4-0}

When a Fundamental Greek teacher---at any Conservative University---says he believes the Bible is the inspired, infallible authority of God, he lies. He means "some Bible WAS" (not "is"), for he cannot produce anything to back up his belief. His belief is a [past-tense]{.class_s4N4-0} faith in something that is no longer available.

["All that the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord ]{.class_s4N9-0}[hath spoken we will do"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 8). Observe the careful preservation of ["words"]{.class_s4N2-0} throughout the passage (vss. 8--9, 20:1), never "the message." If the people had gotten a preview of chapters []{#c2AR.xhtml_page_426}20--23, they would have prayed before saying what they say in verse 8. When a man says "All that the [Lord ]{.class_s4NB}hath spoken I will do," he has not "said a mouthful"; he has said a [truck full.]{.class_s4N4-0}

["Lo, I come unto thee\...that the people may hear when I speak with thee"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 9). But what they hear is what the attendants of Paul heard in Acts chapter 9. This is what the crowd heard in John 12:29; Job describes it in Job 37:1--5 (cf. vs. 16 and 20:18).

["Sanctify them to day and to morrow, and let them wash their clothes"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 10). The first time we found the word ["sanctify"]{.class_s4N2-0} was in 13:2. Again, we are impressed with the meaning of the word; it means "to set something apart for God's use." From this association has come the ancient saying: "Cleanliness is NEXT TO godliness." (Many Germans \[who overdo nearly anything in which they really believe\] have interpreted it to mean: "Cleanliness is godliness." The only variation of the reading to the average German would be: ["Performance of duty]{.class_s4N4-0} is godliness.")

["And be ready against the third day: for the third day the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord ]{.class_s4N9-0}[will come down in the sight of all the people" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 11). It is ["the morning"]{.class_s4N2-0} of ["the third day"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 16) on which God appears to Israel at Sinai, and all of the major commentators, without one exception, miss the import of the passage.

Here again we have demonstrated for us the superiority of the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1611) over any and all revisers, commentators, teachers, scholars, and critics of any persuasion, using any text. One hundred percent (unanimous agreement! See comments under 18:2, 18:17--26) of the commentators skip the passage like the ["young hart upon the mountains of spices"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Song []{#c2AR.xhtml_page_427}of Sol. 8:14), and they don't get enough spice on the whole trip to season a ham.

Let us overlook the "inspired originals" and put the Greek and the Hebrew in the waste basket for a minute so we can find the truth of the matter.

1\. The Lord will return to this earth in ["the morning" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(cf. Mal. 4:1--3); hence, it is referred to throughout as ["the DAY of the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord]{.class_s4N9-0}["]{.class_s4N2-0} (Isa. 13:6, 9; Jer. 46:10; Ezek. 30:3; Joel 1:15, 29, 11, 31, 3:14; Amos 5:18, etc.).

2\. When the SUN comes up (Psa. 19:4--5), ["the]{.class_s4N2-0} [righteous shine"]{.class_s4N2-0} with Him (Matt. 13:43) ["in the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[fourth watch"]{.class_s4NC} (Matt. 14:25), which is the [morning watch ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Mark 13:35).

3\. ["The night"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Isa. 21:11--12) ends with the morning (2 Pet. 1:19), and ["the children of the DAY"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 Thess. 5:1--7) know the time and season of His coming (Song of Sol. 2:11--12), for He comes ["in the fourth watch"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Matt. 14:25; Mark 13:35).

4\. Since ["one day is with the Lord as a thousand years"]{.class_s4N2-0} (2 Pet. 3:8), there are FOUR days before Christ (see Gen. 1:20; note the word ["life" ]{.class_s4N2-0}on the fifth day) and THREE days after Christ before the "wind up." (See detailed analysis in [The Seven Sevens,]{.class_s4N4-0} 1970.)

5\. This breakdown of FOUR and THREE is found throughout the Scripture in either Testament (see John 2:1; Luke 13:32; Luke 9:28; Matt. 17:1; Rev. 2--3; etc.).

6\. The text of chapter 19, then, is plainly telling you that when the Lord Jesus shows up beyond A.D. 2000 (don't panic just because of your superstitious upbringing!), He will return to Mt. Sinai (Judg. 5:5; Deut. 33:2; Hab. 3:3) long before He lands on the Mount of Olives (Zech. 14:3--4), and the ["words"]{.class_s4N2-0} of chapter 19 []{#c2AR.xhtml_page_428}in the [King James 1611 Authorized Version]{.class_s4N4-0} mean exactly what they say as they are found. They reveal a truth which no commentator---including Bullinger and Darby---could find, and a truth which would make the "body mystery" (see [Commentary on Galatians--Colossians]{.class_s4N4-0}, Eph. 3:1--6) a small matter indeed for Christians living in the last half of the Twentieth Century and first part of the Twenty-first Century.

Chapter 19 dates the ADVENT (at the beginning of the Millennium) within one year, [if our calendar is correct. ]{.class_s4N4-0}(For those tender-hearted and tender-footed souls, who flinch when Judge Rutherford is mentioned, may we say as tactfully as we know how: "You can't reject the truth all your life simply because somebody misinterpreted it 100 years ago.")

["And thou shalt set bounds unto the people\...whosoever toucheth the mount shall be surely put to death: There shall not an hand touch it, but he shall surely be stoned"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 12--13). According to the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} (pg. 117), the [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1901), Clarke (Commentary, pg. 399), Bullinger ([Companion Bible, ]{.class_s4N4-0}pg. 100), and Dr. Johnson (unanimity if you ever saw it!), the text---verse 13---should have read: "There shall not an hand touch HIM" (i.e., the man who touches the mount, vs. 12).

This alteration of text is arrived at by insisting that the MASCULINE (Heb. [bo]{.class_s4N4-0}) must be a reference to some [man ]{.class_s4N4-0}and cannot refer to ["it" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(["the mount"]{.class_s4N2-0}). Since there is "unanimity of opinion" against the Reformation text, we can safely assume that all 100% are mistaken again.

1\. One of the main arguments used by "higher criticism," when trying to prove that a book is written LATER than it was written, is to call our attention to []{#c2AR.xhtml_page_429}the usage of certain words. To counter this, Robert Dick Wilson (Princeton) and others have often referred to the fact that the use of the masculine [him ]{.class_s4N4-0}for [neuter objects]{.class_s4N4-0} is characteristic of Hebrew writing [before the time of David]{.class_s4N4-0} (see 40:10, 35:13--15). The book of Job has this characteristic, and the Pentateuch is filled with masculine "hims" that refer to[ ]{.class_s4N2-0}[things.]{.class_s4N4-0}

2\. Even in the altered text---[RV, ASV, RSV,]{.class_s4N4-0} etc.---the [he ]{.class_s4N4-0}would have to refer to an animal, for the verse goes right on and says ["whether it be BEAST or man"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 13). Notice that this ["he" ]{.class_s4N4-0}has been altered to ["IT"]{.class_s4N2-0} in the parallel text (Heb. 12:20) even in the [RV, ASV,]{.class_s4N4-0} and [RSV. ]{.class_s4N4-0}The translators of these, like the commentators who preceded them, were not able to out-think the Holy Spirit when they changed the [King James']{.class_s4N4-0} text.

3\. Observe, further, that the ["shot through"]{.class_s4N2-0} of verse 13 was not a supernatural blast from the lightning, but rather the thrusting ["through with a dart"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Heb. 12:20), and the "stoning" of the passage can easily be done without "touching" anyone (cf. Lev. 20:2, 27; Josh. 7:25). The Bible believer will do well to accept the verse as it stands and ignore the men who think they are smart enough to tamper with the God-honored text.

Verse 12 states a basic truth which all Liberals reject. If a man were to reduce to one verse the stating of this basic error of all modern theology, he could point to verse 12 and say that the rejection of that verse is the root, ground, source, and foundation of all "Modernism." The Liberal's trouble is not merely that he rejects the Deity of Christ and the Virgin Birth. His trouble goes far deeper than a mere rejection of the Johannine writings and the Pauline Epistles. Basically, all Modernists refuse to believe that God is UNAPPROACHABLE without []{#c2AR.xhtml_page_430}accepting His revealed will in regards [as to HOW He is to be approached.]{.class_s4N4-0}

To put it plainly:

1\. God is holy, so a man must ["take heed"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 12) in approaching God.

2\. God is holy, so ["DEATH" ]{.class_s4N2-0}is the penalty for approaching Him the wrong way (vs. 12; cf. 2 Sam. 6:7).

3\. No man can ["go\...into the mount"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 12) where God dwells (Heb. 12:18--29) unless He accepts God's conditions for going (vss. 23--24).

Now these three revealed truths found in the passage are flatly rejected by every Liberal or "Modernist" who ever lived. His theme song is the theme song of Genesis 3:4 (["Ye shall not surely die"]{.class_s4N2-0}), so the Liberal's approach to God is made:

1\. [Without a virgin birth,]{.class_s4N4-0} since there is no necessity for a sinless man to be born because Adam did not fall and, therefore, [no supernatural birth]{.class_s4N4-0} is necessary to recover Adam's race.

2\. [Without a bodily resurrection, ]{.class_s4N4-0}since Christ was like any of Adam's race and none of Adam's race could come up from their graves.

3\. [Without a blood atonement,]{.class_s4N4-0} since God demands no such thing from man because men are good enough to go ["up into the mount" ]{.class_s4N2-0}without the shedding of [blood ]{.class_s4N4-0}(cf. 24:6--8).

You see, the basic trouble is the matter of [approach. ]{.class_s4N4-0}The born-again child of God believes (and teaches) that "approach" can only be made through a sinless man who offers a perfect sacrifice (Matt. 11:27; 1 Tim. 2:5; John 14:1--6) in the place of a sinful man who cannot approach in his own righteousness (Isa. 64:6). The "Christian" []{#c2AR.xhtml_page_431}in the NCCC and ecumenical council believes that "approach" can be made by any and all, any way they see fit, without any regard whatsoever to any revealed truth on man's [inability to approach]{.class_s4N4-0} (Psa. 50:16--23).

One of the first lessons we learn in Exodus is that there is no such thing as the "Brotherhood of Man" (cf. 11:7). The next thing we learn is that death is the penalty for rejecting blood atonement (12:13), and the next thing we learn is that death is the penalty for treating the Godhead and the revealed WORDS of God as they are treated by Timothy O'Leary, Theodore Epp, Fulton Sheen, Mandy Rice Davis, Fulton Oursler, Malcolm Boyd, Kenneth Wuest, A. A. Allen, Billy Graham, Elvis Presley, Bob Jones III, the Beatles, and Eberhard Nestle. And you don't go charging up into the mount ["to gaze"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 21) on the majesty of John 17:24 unless you are covered with the blood (Heb. 9:11--14) of a man who was ["shot]{.class_s4N2-0} [through"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 13) in your place (Isa. 53:3--9).

That is, you don't unless you are a "Liberal!" What no "Liberal" ever considered---since no "Liberal" is a dualist---is that the god they contact (after charging through the bounds---vs. 12) is the god who[ removed the bounds]{.class_s4N4-0} (Acts 17:26--27) which the Lord God set up (Deut. 32:8). This "god" is the "father of all men" (John 8:44; 2 Cor. 4:4); but he was no more the God of Moses, Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, David, and the Christian than Errol Flynn was the god of Walt Disney.

["When the trumpet soundeth long"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 13). The apparent contradiction is left in a contradictory form by the [Pulpit Commentary. ]{.class_s4N4-0}When the trumpet sounds long, the people are NOT allowed to come up into the mount (vs. 19); only Moses and Aaron go up (vs. 24) when the trumpet "sounds long." Clarke drops the whole mess and []{#c2AR.xhtml_page_432}does not attempt to explain it, and Dummelow backs out with some mumbling about the "representatives" of the people being the ["they"]{.class_s4N2-0} in verse 13. ["They shall come up to the mount."]{.class_s4N2-0} However, the ["they"]{.class_s4N2-0} never show up because only [Moses ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [Aaron ]{.class_s4N4-0}ascend.

Again, we are face to face with one of those problems which the geniuses in the "original languages" cannot solve due to their disdain and disrespect for the [King James]{.class_s4N4-0} text. Right before their noses God has written: ["that ye go not up into the mount,"]{.class_s4N2-0} and ["or touch the border of it"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 12), which is not the wording of verse 13. The people ARE to ["come up TO the mount,"]{.class_s4N2-0} exactly as verse 13 states. Why Keil, Delitzsch, Kalisch, Knobel, Rosenmuller, and Whitelaw could not see verse 17 is past finding out, unless one recognizes the truth which we have stated so many times already: the "best brains," of any age, are helpless when they attempt to alter the Reformation Text of the English Bible.

The people[ "come up TO the mount"]{.class_s4N2-0} in verse 17, according to the commandment of verse 13, but none of them can [touch ]{.class_s4N4-0}it or go up [INTO ]{.class_s4N4-0}it (vs. 12). There is no difficulty in the text whatsoever, and the 167 words by Conservative scholars in the [Pulpit 'Commentary ]{.class_s4N4-0}discussing the problem are wasted paper and ink.

19:14 "And Moses went down from the mount unto the people, and sanctified the people; and they washed their clothes.
