Astruc and others used the verse to prove that anywhere from two to six different men wrote the Pentateuch, but as we have said before, a knowledge of Astruc's theory is about as essential to understanding the Pentateuch as a bag of golf clubs is for understanding hieroglyphics. One will be surprised to find in the recent publication by Garrison ([Strange Facts About the Bible, ]{.class_s4N4-0}1968, pg. 276) an article to the effect that Astruc had something to do with scientific methods of "understanding the Bible better." The French physician Astruc did not know as much about Genesis as the Juniors in the Canton Baptist Temple in Canton, Ohio. I have taught students in the Pensacola Bible Institute who never finished high school, and [any one of them ]{.class_s4N4-0}could critique Astruc until he didn't have enough truth left with which to sharpen a pencil. The average mature Christian in the hills of North Carolina []{#c2DW.xhtml_page_479}could teach Astruc more about the Bible in one day than anyone could learn from reading Astruc for ten years.

["Ye have seen that I have talked with you from heaven"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 22). This is like the ["sure word of prophecy"]{.class_s4N2-0} (2 Pet. 1:19) that speaks from Heaven in Matthew 17:1--5; there are no flying saucers, P.A. systems, or hallucinations in the text. (Some of the commentators have unidentified flying objects in their heads, perhaps, and hallucinations are quite common to critics of the Bible. But what happens here is "for real," and the only people fooled by the miracle are the faculty members of Harvard, Heidelberg, Yale, Chicago, Tubingen, Union Theological Seminary, and Colgate-Rochester Divinity School.)

["Ye shall not make with me gods of silver\...gods of gold"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 23). ["The times"]{.class_s4N2-0} that ["God winked at" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(Acts 17:30) are over, at least for Israel; and from here on, Israel cannot walk in "her own ways" (Acts 14:16), even though the Lord puts up with the other descendants of Shem, Ham, and Japheth for awhile (Acts 17:30).

The "gods of gold and silver" are found from Rachel's teraphim (Gen. 31:19) to the fall of Rome (Rev. 18:9--12), and there is no indication that Jackson (\$20.00), Hamilton (\$10.00), and Lincoln (\$5.00) have reduced the number of idolaters from those who worshipped the pictures of Ashtoreth, Ishtar, Semiramis, Venus, Diana, Juno, Baal, El, Bel, Osiris, Isis, Horus, Seb, and Mary.

The molten and graven ["gods"]{.class_s4N2-0} of the heathen (34:15--17) caused the destruction of Israel before they ever got into the Promised Land (Num. 25:2--5), and they hadn't arrived in Palestine before the ["wedge of gold"]{.class_s4N2-0} appears (Josh. 7:21). In the book of Judges, the []{#c2DW.xhtml_page_480}whole nation goes after the ["gods"]{.class_s4N2-0} of the heathen (Judg. 2:3, 12, 19), and at the end of that book, the Roman Catholic priest, called ["father,"]{.class_s4N2-0} is well entrenched with his "aids to worship" (Judg. 18:19--20). ["The gods which your fathers served on the other side of the flood" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(Josh. 24:14) are obviously the images of the "gods" who appeared in Genesis 6 (see the[ Commentary on Genesis,]{.class_s4N4-0} Gen. 6:1--4), and these gods are the ones who will "perish from under the heavens" (Jer. 10:11). Neither David nor Josiah are able to keep the nation on top forever, and by the time of Jeremiah, all twelve tribes are worshipping idols (Jer. 2:11, 28, 5:7, 19; 7:6, 9, 18, 11:10, 12, 13, 16:11, 13, 20, 19:4, 13, 22:9, 25:6, 44:3, 5, 8, 15, etc., etc.).

Aaron's calf is plainly a violation of the commandment, both here and in 20:4. The gods that came down ["in the likeness of men"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Acts 14:11) leave their imprint (Acts 19:35) in the shops of history; and not one time in 2,000 years of history has art, science, education, religion, or philosophy ever eradicated the [idol business. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Materialism is idolatry, plain and simple, and ["covetousness\...is idolatry"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Col. 3:5) according to Paul. Furthermore, ["stubbornness is as\...idolatry" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(1 Sam. 15:23) according to Samuel.

Aaron's calf survived chapter 32 in excellent condition. It rears its head on ["the plain of Dura"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Dan. 3:1); it thrusts its snoot through the palace walls of Belshazzar (Dan. 5:4); it kicks up its hoofs in the theology of the scribes and Pharisees (Matt. 23:16); and then it dons robes and crowns to speak "ex cathedra" from the seat of the Great Whore of Revelation 17:4. (When you call [any man]{.class_s4N4-0} "Holy Father" \[cf. John 17:11\]), you are an idolator (Matt. 23:9).

The "gods of gold and silver" control the Illuminati, the international bankers, Wall Street, the Vatican, the National Broadcasting Company, the Columbia Broadcasting System, the news stands, the Associated Press, the National Education Association, the heroin traffic, the American Medical Association, the cocaine traffic, the United Nations, the Communist Party, and the "revolutionaries." What revolutionary did not desire to obtain money or property which he coveted (see remarks under vs. 17)? The only difference between "the Establishment" and the hippies at Berkeley (1967--1971) is that the hippies at Berkeley can't get their hands on the money the Establishment has! Why "overthrow" something unless you want what it HAS? If you don't covet what it has, why not ignore it? (You see, you don't get all the answers in school; as a matter of fact, you seldom are given the proper questions.)

An image lover is a God hater.

["An altar of earth thou shalt make unto me\...And if thou wilt make me an altar of stone, thou shalt not build it of hewn stone\...Neither shalt thou go up by steps unto mine altar"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 24--26). The altar is to be "God-made," and that is the reason for the instructions about natural stones. "The stone made without hands" (Dan. 2:34, 45) is the counterpart, and the violation of the instruction is clearly given in Genesis 11:2--6 where we are told that BRICKS were used instead of STONES. The altar of stone and earth was erected in the time of Elijah (1 Kings 18:30--31); it appears when the Tabernacle has been forsaken (Judg. 2:13) and before the Temple has been built (1 Kings 3:1--4). Noah and Abel are the first to build altars, and Abraham follows their practice (Gen. 8:20, 12:8). ["The []{#c2DW.xhtml_page_482}high places"]{.class_s4N2-0} become notorious as places for "altars" (2 Kings 12:3, 14:4, 15:4, 35, 17:9, 11, 29, 23:5, 8, 9, 13, 15, etc., etc.). Since the original desire of Satan was to ASCEND (Isa. 14:12--14), his children build phallic symbols called "obelisks"; they send up rockets named after the Death Angel (Apollyon, Rev. 9:11); they erect Baal poles (Jer. 10:1--5) to commemorate Baal's birthday; they dance around May poles (fifth month) to honor Mr. Five-by-Five (!---[pull that one on your professor]{.class_s4N4-0}!); they build Empire State Buildings and Chrysler buildings; they erect pyramids; and they point their church steeples where they cannot go.

["The high places"]{.class_s4N2-0} are worshipped today exactly as they were 3000 B.C., and there is no more difference between the activity in Genesis chapter 11 and Cape Canaveral than there is between a [New Scofield Reference Bible]{.class_s4N4-0} and a corrupt [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}of 1901 or 1971.

The ["nakedness"]{.class_s4N2-0} of the priest is obviously displayed if he walks up a long flight or open stairway (or ladder) to the place of offering. The effect is exactly that of a woman in a short skirt climbing up a stepladder (although nowadays the same effect is displayed when a woman sits down, stands up, walks, crouches, kneels, runs, reaches, or reclines. A dress so short that it makes a pair of "shorts" look like a robe is hardly designed for anything but "discovering nakedness").

The "ascents" to the altar [at the time of Solomon ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1 Kings 10:5) are not like this, for those "steps" were to a brazen altar, not a stone altar. Only the [priests ]{.class_s4N4-0}are near the altar (chap. 29), and the steps do not rise more than three feet off the ground. The "steps" forbidden in the text (vs. 26) are the "steps" found in the Mayan and Aztec and Incan memorials and the "ziggurats" of Chaldea.

["In all places where I record my name I will come unto thee, and I will bless thee"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 24). God comes to the worshipper, and it is not the other way around. Whether we come by "tools" or "steps," we mess things up. It is enough that God initiates the approach (see comments under 25:10), and without His "coming," all is useless.

["Where I record my name"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 24). Well, that name is recorded from Genesis chapter 1 to Revelation chapter 22, so if the reader desires a blessing (see the end of the verse), he should spend more time between Genesis chapter 1 and Revelation chapter 22 than he does between [Life ]{.class_s4N4-0}Magazine and Channel 7. Historically, God records His name first in [Shiloh ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Jer. 7:12), then in [Bethel ]{.class_s4N4-0}(l Sam. 10:3), then in [Zion ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Psa. 78:68). The last time His name is recorded on this earth, it is recorded on the [Mount of Olives]{.class_s4N4-0} (Acts 1:11-12), and it will be recorded there on that spot again in the coming years (Zech. 14:3-4).

The greatest place of blessing where God ever recorded His name was outside the "camp" at Jerusalem (Heb. 13:12-13), on a hill called [Calvary]{.class_s4N4-0}. The record read: "[THIS IS JESUS OF NAZARETH THE KING OF THE JEWS.]{.class_s4NB}" (Matt. 27:37 cf. John 19:19). It was recorded in the languages of Shem, Ham, and Japheth for all humanity to read (Luke 23:38), and God chose that altar to meet the sinner (Heb. 13:10--13). No other altar in this age (Hos. 10:2, 8) will do. This altar, as the one erected in 24:6, was sprinkled with blood (Isa. 53:3--8), and as the altar of 24:6, it was erected outside the Tabernacle and outside the Temple (Heb. 7:13).

[]{#c2N5.xhtml}

# Chapter 21 {.heading_s4G-0}

21:1 "Now these are the judgments which thou shalt set before them.

2 If thou buy an Hebrew servant, six years he shall serve: and in the seventh he shall go out free for nothing.

3 If he came in by himself, he shall go out by himself: if he were married, then his wife shall go out with him.

4 If his master have given him a wife, and she have born him sons or daughters; the wife and her children shall be her master's, and he shall go out by himself.

5 And if the servant shall plainly say, I love my master, my wife, and my children; I will not go out free:

6 Then his master shall bring him unto the judges; he shall also bring him to the door, or unto the door post; and his master shall bore his ear through with an aul; and he shall serve him for ever."

We shall use the convenient paragraph markings of the [King James Bible ]{.class_s4N4-0}for the section even though regulations for the slaves continue through verse 11. Serious students of the Bible who have studied these paragraphic markings know they are far superior to the ones invented in any of the reference Bibles of the Nineteenth and Twentieth Centuries.

Note Joel chapter 2 in the [Scofield Reference Bible,]{.class_s4N4-0} for example, where the classic case occurs; there a Premillennial, evangelistic, orthodox, Bible-believing Conservative made a paragraph out of [half of a sentence]{.class_s4N4-0} because he could not [believe ]{.class_s4N4-0}that verses 1--10 were a reference to a verse that followed verse 11. This abortion was done in the face of the fact that verse 11 is part []{#c2N5.xhtml_page_485}of a sentence that began in verse 10. Profession in the "fundamentals of the faith" and in the "verbal inspiration of the originals" is evidently no safeguard against calling [the Lord's army]{.class_s4N4-0} the DEVIL'S ARMY!

The slave, here, is a Hebrew slave who may sell himself to get out of debt, or he may be sold into slavery to pay back for something he stole (e.g., 22:3--5), or he may have been taken as a prisoner of war. An insolvent debtor could be sold (2 Kings 4:1), and a man might sell himself to better his financial position if he were in a condition of extreme poverty (Lev. 25:39). This slavery was not lifelong, and regardless of the seven year term, it ended if the Jubilee occurred anytime during the seven years (Lev. 25:39--41). Not only that, but the slave was well-stocked by his master when he left a seven-year servitude (Deut. 15:12--14) .

The "wife and children" of the passage (vs. 4) are lifelong slaves, but these are the "servants of servants" (cf. Gen. 9:25) mentioned in Leviticus 25:45--46 and Joshua 9:23. The implication is that they are [Canaanites]{.class_s4N4-0}. Servitude for the Hebrew (vs. 2) is only temporary unless he [volunteers ]{.class_s4N4-0}to be a bond-servant forever (vs. 6). There is some typology in the passage (Psa. 40:6 cf. Heb 10:5), for Jesus Christ became ["a servant" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(Phil. 2:6--7) and ["a servant of servants"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Matt. 10:24) to win his WIFE (Eph. 5:23--32) and children (Rom. 8:1--19).

The reader should observe the expression ["free for nothing"]{.class_s4N2-0} found in verse 2. This is a part of the "King's English" which still survives in America, notwithstanding "up-to-date translations" in the "living language of the\...etc., etc." Sailors ["take liberty"]{.class_s4N4-0} because Paul took it in Acts 27:3, and on shore it is called ["liberty"]{.class_s4N4-0} (Acts 27:3) []{#c2N5.xhtml_page_486}because that is what the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1611) text called it. You call a judge ["your Honor"]{.class_s4N4-0} (John 5:22--23) because the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1611) lays down the terms and conditions of your speech, and you will follow it whether you believe it or not. You ["wind up"]{.class_s4N4-0} newscasts when you finish broadcasting them because Ananias and Sapphira were ["wound up"]{.class_s4N4-0} when they died (Acts 5:6), and every man that ["set"]{.class_s4N4-0} his watch had to follow the last verse in Matthew chapter 27. The "half-baked" man is in Hosea 7:8, and the ["horse laugh"]{.class_s4N4-0} is in Job 39:19--25. There are more than 200 of these expressions in a [King James ]{.class_s4N4-0}Bible (see [Our Roving Bible,]{.class_s4N4-0} Nelson, Apex Books), and no amount of work by the Lockman Foundation or the [Biblical Viewpoint ]{.class_s4N4-0}will ever be able to take them out of the mouths of the English-speaking people. ["Good for nothing"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Matt. 5:13) and ["clean gone"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Psa. 77:8) are just as contemporary as ["you said it"]{.class_s4N4-0} (Matt. 26:64) and ["whadaya know?"]{.class_s4N4-0} (Job 15:9).

Verse 6 is a very interesting study in a translator's ability to rightly divide the words of truth. The Hebrew word here is the word for God ("[Haelohim]{.class_s4N4-0}"; literally "the gods"). How should the word be translated? It is translated as a [singular ]{.class_s4N4-0}in Genesis 1:1 by all but the most pagan Liberals. If it is ["gods,"]{.class_s4N4-0} then are not the ["judges"]{.class_s4N2-0} gods? (Psa. 82)? If this is so, then there are no real "gods" other than men. But this is where Eve messed up. Not only that, but the word[ "gods,"]{.class_s4N2-0} meaning ["gods,"]{.class_s4N4-0} is found in 22:20 and 28, (The last passage has been reduced to ["God"]{.class_s4N4-0} in the Masoretic publication of 1917 by the Jewish Publishing Society.)

Here the translators have three possibilities open to them:

A. He may translate "GOD," making a singular out of a plural.

B. He may translate "gods" retaining the plural.

C. He may translate "judges," which may lead an apostate to believe that the ["gods"]{.class_s4N2-0} will ["die like men"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Psa. 82:6--7).

Now notice how the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1611) straightens all of this out without detracting from the words of truth and without destroying the "number" of the noun. Moses was said to act in God's place for Pharaoh (4:16)---["as a god"]{.class_s4N2-0} (7:1). In this capacity, Moses was a ["JUDGE" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(see Acts 7:35); and not only a judge, but a [supreme]{.class_s4N4-0} judge, for he was the Lawgiver to a nation (24:12). The people who then took over this function were supposed to represent Moses (Num. 11:16--17), and they sat ["in Moses' seat"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Matt. 23:2) representing God (note ["all things" ]{.class_s4N2-0}in Matt. 23:21).

Now, only in THIS SENSE could a judge of Israel ever be called a "god." There is certainly no similarity at all between this "god" and the "gods" mentioned in Exodus 12:12, 20:3, 20:23, 32:4, 34:15; Deuteronomy 5:7, 6:14, 10:17, 13:2, 28:36, 32:16--17, etc., etc.

The ["gods"]{.class_s4N2-0} of Psalm 82 are plainly the ["gods"]{.class_s4N2-0} of 1 Corinthians 8:5 and Joshua 22:22 and 1 Samuel 28:13 The human judges of Israel were only human substitutes for the Angel of the Lord (Gal. 4:14), for one must never forget that the ANGELS were called[ "gods"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Acts 14:11; Psa. 82:1--6) when they set up the pre-Deluge civilization. Marks of this civilization remain today, and Erich Von Daniken ([Chariot of the Gods]{.class_s4N4-0}) is not the first man or the last to confound the Biblical account with the historical account.

As we have said in other places, the outstanding difference between the Bible believer and the "Christian" is that the Bible believer takes the NEGATIVE VIEW of any historical revelation dealing with "humanoids" or superhumans, apart from one man who was ["God\.... manifest in the flesh"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 Tim. 3:16). "Visitors from outer space" have nothing to do with [moral ]{.class_s4N4-0}or [spiritual progress]{.class_s4N4-0} to a Bible believer; they might to a Darwinian or "Evangelical Christian," but not to a Bible believer. We know the "chariots of the gods" have been here, and furthermore, we know what they did when they were here and what they will do [when they come back. ]{.class_s4N4-0}(That is a great deal more than any historian or scientist can find out in the Library of Congress.)

Again, we find beautiful typology in verse 6 in regards to the "pierced ear lobe." In Hebrews 10:5, the writer quotes Psalm 40:6 and gives the free interpretation of the Holy Spirit to show the reader that Christ's submission to God's will as ["a servant of servants"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Heb. 10:5--9) was the voluntary submission of a slave who bore marks of "piercing" (Psa. 22:16) in His flesh.

At this point, the mythological [Septuagint ]{.class_s4N4-0}again demonstrates its corruptness for all to behold. As we have said in [Manuscript Evidence,]{.class_s4N4-0} the writers of the [LXX]{.class_s4N4-0} lived many years after the resurrection and ascension of Christ. The factual proofs of the matter are clearly shown in the [Septuagint Concordance]{.class_s4N4-0} (Samuel Bagsters, London, 1895), for there one will find every single quotation---without an exception---is taken from manuscripts written more than 200 years after the New Testament was completed (A.D. 330--550).

Philo, Pantaenus, Clement, Origen, Aquila, Symmachus, Theodotian, et al., who manufactured this []{#c2N5.xhtml_page_489}trivial publication, had a hobbyhorse they could never unsaddle. They insisted that when they found a quotation in the New Testament which they were unable to match in the Old Testament, it was proof the Old Testament was written WRONG. Hence, [wherever they were able]{.class_s4N4-0} to spot a place where the New Testament writer failed to quote the Hebrew text verbatim, they [altered the Hebrew Old Testament to match the New Testament quotation. ]{.class_s4N4-0}(["This is a faithful saying and worthy of all acceptation"]{.class_s4N2-0} \[cf. 1 Tim. 4:9\], and if the men that taught your professors don't believe it, tell them to get their chaplain to punch their card.)

Since the habitual practice of these dead-orthodox apostates was to alter the Bible, we are not surprised to find ALL of the [Septuagint ]{.class_s4N4-0}manuscripts altering Psalm 40:6--8 to make it match Hebrews 10:5--7. The reader may put this down as the consistent practice of the first "Christian University" which inaugurated "Christian education," and it is typical (not exceptional) of the manner in which publications like [The Biblical Viewpoint]{.class_s4N4-0} and organizations like the Lockman Foundation handle the living words of the living God TODAY.

For example, shortly after [Manuscript Evidence]{.class_s4N4-0} hit the market, there was a stir among some of the faculty members at Bob Jones University. In an attempt to justify the mythological "[LXX]{.class_s4N4-0}," a couple of professors there actually presented the[ Dead Sea Scrolls]{.class_s4N4-0} as modern proof that the [LXX]{.class_s4N4-0} was a [pre-Christian]{.class_s4N4-0} publication. This was done with all the seriousness of an ass and an owl investigating a fence rail. In their publication ([The Biblical Viewpoint]{.class_s4N4-0}), the evidence of a Greek copy of Habakkuk was presented as though this proved something. Readers of [The Biblical Viewpoint]{.class_s4N4-0} were []{#c2N5.xhtml_page_490}probably stupid enough to think that some great "touché" had been delivered in favor of the "[LXX]{.class_s4N4-0}." However, those of us who examined the contents of the Dead Sea Scrolls more than eight years ago knew about the matter long before writing anything about it. The article referred to is[ a HEBREW COMMENTARY on Habakkuk---not GREEK. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Furthermore, the "commentary" matches the Masoretic HEBREW text, not the phony Greek of Origen's [Hexapla ]{.class_s4N4-0}and the "[Septuagint ]{.class_s4N4-0}manuscripts." All the [Septuagint ]{.class_s4N4-0}manuscripts cited for the text we are now reading were written [more than 100 years after John died on Patmos.]{.class_s4N4-0}

[The Biblical Viewpoint]{.class_s4N4-0} is about as scholarly and as "Biblical" as [Alice in Wonderland. ]{.class_s4N4-0}It is, rather, the standard ["scholastic viewpoint" ]{.class_s4N4-0}of every dead-orthodox apostate from Origen to the Lockman Foundation. When Gleason Archer tells us that a Dead Sea scroll "inclines" to an [LXX]{.class_s4N4-0} reading or is "like" an[ LXX]{.class_s4N4-0} reading or "favors" an LXX reading ([A Survey of Old Testament Introduction,]{.class_s4N4-0} Moody Press, pp. 31--36), he is merely repeating the perennial and interminable nonsense that Swete, Lange, Clarke, Keil, Delitzsch, Hengstenberg, et al., have given us for three centuries. He is judging the Holy Bible by [Vaticanus, Sinaiticus, Sarravianus, Marchalianus, Venetus, ]{.class_s4N4-0}or[ Alexandrinus]{.class_s4N4-0}; [every manuscript listed was written more than 200 years after John finished writing the book of Revelation. ]{.class_s4N4-0}(See the prefatory remarks in this Commentary on the Old Latin "borrowing" from the [Septuagint ]{.class_s4N4-0}and compare it with Archer's remarks on page 43 of the work cited!)

21:7 "And if a man sell his daughter to be a maidservant, she shall not go out as the menservants do.

8 If she please not her master, who hath betrothed her to himself, then shall he let her be redeemed: to sell her unto a strange nation he shall have no power, seeing he hath dealt deceitfully with her.

9 And if he have betrothed her unto his son, he shall deal with her after the manner of daughters.

10 If he take him another wife; her food, her raiment, and her duty of marriage, shall he not diminish.

11 And if he do not these three unto her, then shall she go out free without money."

Further instructions are given, and these deal with a woman instead of a man, so the paragraph placement of the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1611) is a little more accurate than the divisions you will find suggested in the commentaries.

The slave-wife of the text is not released at the end of the seven years (vs. 2) but remains as the wife of the master, with a [real wife's privileges]{.class_s4N4-0} (vs. 10), regardless of the number of wives the master may have. If he does not marry her, she shall be given to one of his sons (vs. 9). If he will not give her to his son, then he cannot "resell" her to a foreigner; she is free to go wherever she wants (vs. 11). The only other alternative is for another Hebrew (not a foreigner---vs. 8) to take her off his hands and assume the obligations of a husband to her. Throughout, polygamy is allowed, but only temporarily and for the reasons mentioned in Matthew 19:8. ["Without money"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 11) means that he cannot sell her again if he refused the alternatives listed; if the girl's father sold her to him, he is not entitled to a "money back guarantee" from the original seller.

21:12 "He that smiteth a man, so that he die, shall be surely put to death.

13 And if a man lie not in wait, but God deliver him into his hand; then I will appoint thee a place whither he shall flee.

14 But if a man come presumptuously upon his neighbour, to slay him with guile; thou shalt take him from mine altar, that he may die.

15 And he that smiteth his father, or his mother, shall be surely put to death.

16 And he that stealeth a man, and selleth him, or if he be found in his hand, he shall surely be put to death.

17 And he that curseth his father, or his mother, shall surely be put to death.

18 And if men strive together, and one smite another with a stone, or with his fist, and he die not, but keepeth his bed:

19 If he rise again, and walk abroad upon his staff, then shall he that smote him be quit; only he shall pay for the loss of his time, and shall cause him to be thoroughly healed.

20 And if a man smite his servant, or his maid, with a rod, and he die under his hand; he shall be surely punished.

21 Notwithstanding, if he continue a day or two, he shall not be punished: for he is his money.

22 If men strive, and hurt a woman with child, so that her fruit depart from her, and yet no mischief follow: he shall be surely punished, according as the woman's husband will lay upon him; and he shall pay as the judges determine.

23 And if any mischief follow, then thou shalt give life for life,

24 Eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot,

25 Burning for burning, wound for wound, stripe for stripe.

26 And if a man smite the eye of his servant, or the eye of his maid, that it perish; he shall let him go free for his eye's sake.

27 And if he smite out his manservant's tooth, or his maidservant's tooth; he shall let him go free for his tooth's sake."

The passage deals with domestic laws, and most of it (vss. 12, 14, 15, 18, 20, 22, 26) deals with brawls and arguments. Three offences meriting capital punishment are listed---murder, manstealing, and cursing (or smiting) parents (vss. 12, 16, 17). Verses 23--25 are the famous ["Lex Talionis"]{.class_s4N4-0} (like for like) to which Jesus refers in Matthew 5:38. The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} calls verse 23 an "excessive penalty" and leads the reader to believe that it must have been an "old tribal law" and NOT the divinely inspired commandment of God. But one would expect this after reading through Exodus chapters 1--21 in the "Conservative" [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} (where all the writers professed to believe in the "divinely inspired ORIGINALS").

The modern counterpart is found in America in the condemning of prison guards (Attica, 1971) for shooting rioting prisoners, condemning Lt. Calley (Vietnam) for killing Viet Cong "civilians," and howling about "police brutality" against armed thugs (on dope) who break into stores and rob their owners. The Kent State fiasco is often referred to in the press as "the Kent State MASSACRE," meaning "the Establishment massacred [innocent ]{.class_s4N4-0}young []{#c2N5.xhtml_page_494}people." The "[innocent ]{.class_s4N4-0}young people" were throwing potatoes with razor blades in them and bags of urine, and they were cursing and threatening to kill people after hearing a "pep talk" from some idiot (Hoffman) who recommended that they kill their mothers and fathers (1 Tim. 1:9) before trying the police. "A life for a life" is the standard of either Testament, under Law or Grace (Rom. 13:1--6; Num. 35:16--21), in any dispensation (Gen. 9:5--6; Acts 25:11).

The utility of the ["Lex Talionis"]{.class_s4N4-0} is apparent when one studies the prison system of Israel between 1500 and 600 B.C. Dungeons and prisons were occupied by a few political prisoners or captives of war; not a dozen common criminals are spoken of as languishing in jail until Matthew 27:16 and Luke 23:19.

Verses 12 and 13 are explained thoroughly in Numbers 35. The expression ["take him from mine altar" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 14) may find a literal fulfillment in 1 Kings 1:50 and 2:28, but we don't find many men running to Jerusalem to lay hold of an altar (Exod. 35--2 Samuel), and there is no case recorded in Exodus or Numbers while Israel is in the wilderness. We may assume that the expression originally meant that the murderer could find no sacrifice to atone for his sins, and he would be cast out of the congregation before being put to death (see Lev. 24:14). [The cities of refuge]{.class_s4N4-0} (Num. 35) become the appointed places for the manslayer, to save him from ["the revenger of blood"]{.class_s4N2-0} who could be any near of kin to the slain man.

["God deliver him into his hand"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 13) shows that "accidents" are more than "premeditated carelessness." God's hand is behind the "accident" in His permissive power, and He allows the "accident" []{#c2N5.xhtml_page_495}to take place. (As someone has said: "Our ['hap']{.class_s4N2-0} (Ruth 2:3) is God's MAP" (Ruth 4:13). Verses 14--15 are self-explanatory.

If verse 16 is to be taken literally, the Yankee and Dutch merchantmen of 1600--1800 are in a pretty pickle, as well as the Arabs and many African tribes. Slavery is an AFRICAN institution, and although the stealing and selling of men is forbidden, the [buying ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [keeping ]{.class_s4N4-0}of slaves is NOT forbidden (Gen. 47:23; Exod. 21:2; Lev. 25:44; 2 Chron. 28:10; etc.). "Bondmen" are found in Revelation 6:15 and 18:13, and this period is still in the future ahead of us.

Cursing parents (vs. 17) comes under the same heading as slapping or hitting parents (vs. 15); [capital punishment]{.class_s4N4-0} is meted out for both offences. By such standards, about 40,000 teenagers in America would go to the electric chair yearly. No one today would think of reinstalling such a law, and yet, is it not amazing how few ORIENTALS are found behind prison bars in San Francisco, where the Chinatown section has more inhabitants than Anaheim, California? The price for believing what Horne, Dewey, Russell, Gentile, and Mann said about a "philosophy of education" is the highest crime rate among young people of any nation in the world. I wonder what American youth would be like today if verses 15--17 had been enforced for 200 years? No matter who speculates about the outcome of such legislation, the fact remains that juvenile delinquency has never been a major problem in China, Japan, Turkey, India, Persia, or Israel [in 22 centuries.]{.class_s4N4-0}

Verses 18--19 are handled in courts of law today exactly as they appear here. The aggressor in a case of "assault with a deadly weapon" is sued and pays []{#c2N5.xhtml_page_496}the hospital bill, although in the Biblical case, he must pay for lost time which is now paid by the insurance companies. Verse 21 states that where a servant has been "beaten up" but not killed, the master cannot be held accountable for his death; it is assumed that a servant could die of some [other cause]{.class_s4N4-0} and the people blame it on the master. It is also assumed that the loss of the servant himself is a punishment to a master, for he loses his investment plus the work the slave could have done. Genesis 9:5--6 make it clear that the killing of a slave is murder exactly like the killing of any other individual where the murderer "lies in wait" (vs. 13). If permanent injury results from a beating (vss. 26--27), the slave gains his freedom immediately. Thus TWO loopholes are found in the law. If a slave can aggravate a master to an assault which takes a tooth or an eye---and one good punch in the mouth will take the tooth---he can get free. This is a great incentive for a master to treat his servants properly (Eph. 6:9).

["If men strive, and hurt a woman with child\...he shall pay as the judges determine. And if any mischief follow"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 22--23). Advocates of birth control will notice that the destruction of a child in the fetal or embryonic stage is NOT counted as [murder, ]{.class_s4N4-0}in spite of the literature published between 1970 and 1990 by overzealous kingdom builders who habitually ignore the authority of Scriptures when dealing with personal and emotional issues.

["Life for life, Eye for eye, tooth for tooth\...stripe for stripe" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vss. 23--25). This is not the law of [personal ]{.class_s4N4-0}revenge but that of [judicial procedure]{.class_s4N4-0} (see Lev. 24:17; Deut. 19:15--21), and the Lord Jesus points this truth out to His audience in Matthew 5:38. []{#c2N5.xhtml_page_497}(The reference found in Heb. 2:2 also deals with the passage.)

We have commented on verses 26--27 before under verses 20--21.

21:28 "If an ox gore a man or a woman, that they die: then the ox shall be surely stoned, and his flesh shall not be eaten; but the owner of the ox shall be quit.

29 But if the ox were wont to push with his horn in time past, and it hath been testified to his owner, and he hath not kept him in, but that he hath killed a man or a woman; the ox shall be stoned, and his owner also shall be put to death.

30 If there be laid on him a sum of money, then he shall give for the ransom of his life whatsoever is laid upon him.

31 Whether he have gored a son, or have gored a daughter, according to this judgment shall it be done unto him.

32 If the ox shall push a manservant or a maidservant; he shall give unto their master thirty shekels of silver, and the ox shall be stoned.

33 And if a man shall open a pit, or if a man shall dig a pit, and not cover it, and an ox or an ass fall therein;

34 The owner of the pit shall make it good, and give money unto the owner of them; and the dead beast shall be his.

35 And if one man's ox hurt another's, that he die; then they shall sell the live ox, and divide the money of it; and the dead ox also they shall divide.

36 Or if it be known that the ox hath used to push in time past, and his owner hath not kept him in; he shall surely pay ox for ox; and the dead shall be his own."

The entire passage deals with unruly oxen. The reader should not fail to notice the similitudes between the rules laid down and the modern system of jurisprudence where it deals with rabid dogs and dogs that have broken out of chain link fences or gotten loose from leashes.

Many of the commentators have attached a religious "taboo" to the eating of the ox that killed a man, but the explanation is much simpler. Since the ox was ["surely stoned"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 28), [the blood was not let out of it in the manner prescribed:]{.class_s4N4-0} cutting the throat and letting the heart pump the blood out. "Eating the blood" is strictly forbidden before and during the Law (Gen. 9:4; Lev. 17:14), so the reason for not eating the ox (vs. 28) is quite simple: it violated the Law.

Verse 29 brings up the case of a "repeated offender," and although the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} assures us that capital punishment would never be administered under these circumstances, the plain text says: ["and his owner also shall be put to death."]{.class_s4N2-0} If the injured party desires to commute the sentence to a fine, he may do so, but if the injured party is a Shylock, he can have his "pound of flesh" according to the Law. One of the commentators suggests a fine "worthy of the life of the dead man," but it is a little hard to imagine how much this fine would be. People in America have been known to sue someone for \$15,000,000 for the crippling of a loved one. ["Whatsoever is laid upon him"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 30) is quite a "ransom"!

The devout student will see the Lord Jesus Christ clearly typified again as the ["servant of servants" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 32). The ["bulls of Bashan"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Psa. 22:12) are doing the goring, and the reimbursement for His death is ["thirty []{#c2N5.xhtml_page_499}pieces of silver"]{.class_s4N2-0} (cf. Matt. 26:15); in American money this is the equivalent of about \$18.00.

Verses 33--34 match the laws regarding liability on someone else's property. A cistern or well has been left uncovered (see Gen. 29:3), and a man's animal gets killed falling into it. Dead or injured, the [well owner]{.class_s4N4-0} must take the beast and pay the owner for it whether he wanted to buy it or not. Verses 35--36 conclude the laws on irascible oxen, and they state that the owners split money from the death of an ox by an ox, except where the attacking ox has been in the habit of goring other animals. In such a case, the owner of the attacking ox must pay for the dead ox and keep the carcass for his own.

[]{#c2RP.xhtml}

# Chapter 22 {.heading_s4G-0}

22:1 "If a man shall steal an ox, or a sheep, and kill it, or sell it; he shall restore five oxen for an ox, and four sheep for a sheep.

2 If a thief be found breaking up, and be smitten that he die, there shall no blood be shed for him.

3 If the sun be risen upon him, there shall be blood shed for him; for he should make full restitution; if he have nothing, then he shall be sold for his theft.

4 If the theft be certainly found in his hand alive, whether it be ox, or ass, or sheep; he shall restore double."

The section deals with thieves. Second Samuel 12:6 illustrates verse 1. The believer should observe that David still knows half of one verse in the middle of the Pentateuch even when he is in a backslidden condition.

Verse 2 is a reference to [night burglary]{.class_s4N4-0} where the "second story man" is shot or clubbed or stabbed in the act of stealing. ["Breaking up"]{.class_s4N2-0} is the ancient equivalent of "breaking and entering." If the thief gets killed [in the dark,]{.class_s4N4-0} it is "tough apples" as far as the Law is concerned; there is no retribution made for him. In Job 24:14--16, we have these matters mentioned, and the ["breaking up" ]{.class_s4N2-0}turns out to be a literal breaking through a house by digging under it or through it. However, if the thief is killed [in the daytime,]{.class_s4N4-0} it is taken for granted that the home owner could have used less strenuous measures.

Again, loopholes are evident on both sides, but there are more loopholes in the American laws than the Bible laws. American laws since 1950 favor the criminal 80% of the time. In the 1970s, a homeowner was fined \$30,000 []{#c2RP.xhtml_page_501}and lost his own home for shooting the legs off a thief who broke into one of his [locked ]{.class_s4N4-0}buildings---where a [warning ]{.class_s4N4-0}was posted outside. If he had killed the rascal, he could have saved his own home.

Again, an armed thief could "burgle" in daylight hours only and never get shot because he could shoot the homeowner or threaten him. Again, a [disguised ]{.class_s4N4-0}burglar could not be recognized in the daytime and so could not be caught to ["make full restitution."]{.class_s4N2-0} But against these factors (which favor the thief) may be counterbalanced a great truth: the truth that a Hebrew married woman, who obeyed the Law, spent most of her time in the tent or home with her family. Daylight burglary would usually find the house filled with women and children.

In reading between the lines, we see that a thief who breaks and enters at [night ]{.class_s4N4-0}is taking his life in his hands, and if he is shot, that is just too bad. If Ballew (1971) had fired quicker at the hippy-looking Treasury men who broke into his home and entered it at night, he would probably not be now lying in a bed crippled for life; however, if he had killed one of [them]{.class_s4N4-0}, American law would have probably put him in prison as a murderer. Jewish law is a good bit better than American law, for American laws, since 1950, are made to protect the criminal from arrest and prosecution. If the thief enters in the [daytime, ]{.class_s4N4-0}he is taking a chance on being caught or identified. Either way, he must either restore double (vs. 4), or he is sold as a slave to restore what he took (vs. 3).

Again, we can see that the laws of Moses were a great deterrent to evil, and they kept crime at a minimum even in the worst periods of Israel's history. The reader may notice that throughout the instructions, none of the laws are for the purpose of "rehabilitating" or "reforming" []{#c2RP.xhtml_page_502}the criminal. They are not designed to make him into a "useful member of society"; they are designed to [scare the living H-E-L-L out of him. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Modern educators and philosophers will not tolerate this kind of philosophy for a minute, so modern America (at least in its large cities) has become an asphalt jungle of rape, mugging, assault, murder, stealing, dope peddling, prostitution, and terrifying sex perversion.

If we are to judge a tree by its fruits, we will have to say that the [Lex Talionis]{.class_s4N4-0} of chapters 21--23 is a much better legal code than the one used as a guideline by the "nine old men."

22:5 "If a man shall cause a field or vineyard to be eaten, and shall put in his beast, and shall feed in another man's field; of the best of his own field, and of the best of his own vineyard, shall he make restitution.

6 If fire break out, and catch in thorns, so that the stacks of corn, or the standing corn, or the field, be consumed therewith; he that kindled the fire shall surely make restitution."

Verse 5 comes under the heading of "trespassing," and whether this is intentional or unintentional, the guilty party must restore from ["the]{.class_s4N2-0} [best of his own field."]{.class_s4N2-0} The fire of verse 6 is accidental, as in the case of a brush fire that gets out of hand, but the man who kindled it is still held responsible (as he would be in a Twentieth-Century court) and shall ["surely make restitution."]{.class_s4N2-0} This is what is involved in Samson's crime (Judg. 15:5), and it is the cause of Joab's complaint in 2 Samuel 14:31.

We cite these two examples, for one will find in them a beautiful illustration of how Conservative scholars will look anywhere except in the Bible for their material. The []{#c2RP.xhtml_page_503 .class-1}[Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} runs to "customs" for its authority, and Clarke wastes nearly half a page telling us about ["Harmers's Observations."]{.class_s4N4-0} Neither commentary located Joab or Samson, so there is little wonder they failed to notice [Nebuchadnezzar making restitution]{.class_s4N4-0} (Jer. 50--51) [for burning the Lord's field (s]{.class_s4N4-0}ee Jer. 52).

22:7 "If a man shall deliver unto his neighbour money or stuff to keep, and it be stolen out of the man's house; if the thief be found, let him pay double.

8 If the thief be not found, then the master of the house shall be brought unto the judges, to see whether he have put his hand unto his neighbour's goods.

9 For all manner of trespass, whether it be for ox, for ass, for sheep, for raiment, or for any manner of lost thing, which another challengeth to be his, the cause of both parties shall come before the judges; and whom the judges shall condemn, he shall pay double unto his neighbour.

10 If a man deliver unto his neighbour an ass, or an ox, or a sheep, or any beast, to keep; and it die, or be hurt, or driven away, no man seeing it:

11 Then shall an oath of the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}be between them both, that he hath not put his hand unto his neighbour's goods; and the owner of it shall accept thereof, and he shall not make it good.

12 And if it be stolen from him, he shall make restitution unto the owner thereof.

13 If it be torn in pieces, then let him bring it for witness, and he shall not make good that which was torn.

14 And if a man borrow ought of his neighbour, and it be hurt, or die, the owner thereof being not with it, he shall surely make it good.

15 But if the owner thereof be with it, he shall not make it good: if it be an hired thing, it came for his hire."

The entire section deals with loaning, borrowing, and stolen goods.

Briefly, the rules are:

1\. If any goods are deposited, loaned, or transferred to a neighbor for safekeeping, [he ]{.class_s4N4-0}is responsible for those goods.

2\. If the goods disappear, it must be determined if the man entrusted with them STOLE them or whether they were stolen by an [outsider.]{.class_s4N4-0}

3\. The thief pays double (vss. 4, 9) whether he is a thief who broke into the home or whether he is the home owner who filched the goods entrusted to him.

4\. The judges try all of these cases to determine who the real thief is.

5\. In case the goods are hurt or die (in the case of cattle), the man entrusted with them is to swear by God that he did not kill the animal or hurt it or drive it away (["driven away,"]{.class_s4N2-0} vs. 10); this oath has to be accepted at face value by the man who loaned him the goods.

6\. If the animal has been attacked by another animal and torn to pieces, the pieces are to be brought before the judge as evidence (vs. 13).

7\. However, if the goods are STOLEN, the man to whom they were entrusted must restore them, as it is taken for granted that he should be careful over anything loaned to him and guard it as well as [his own property.]{.class_s4N4-0}

8\. When a man borrows something from a neighbor, he is responsible for it if the owner is not around when he has it or uses it (vs. 14). He must make it good if it is lost or destroyed.

9\. If a man rents something from a neighbor and the neighbor is with him "on the job" when the thing is broken, lost, or destroyed, the thing does not need to be replaced; the owner must settle for the money he received when he rented the thing out.

22:16 "And if a man entice a maid that is not betrothed, and lie with her, he shall surely endow her to be his wife.

17 If her father utterly refuse to give her unto him, he shall pay money according to the dowry of virgins.

18 Thou shalt not suffer a witch to live.

19 Whosoever lieth with a beast shall surely be put to death.

20 He that sacrificeth unto any god, save unto the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}only, he shall be utterly destroyed.

21 Thou shalt neither vex a stranger, nor oppress him: for ye were strangers in the land of Egypt.

22 Ye shall not afflict any widow, or fatherless child.

23 If thou afflict them in any wise, and they cry at all unto me, I will surely hear their cry;

24 And my wrath shall wax hot, and I will kill you with the sword; and your wives shall be widows, and your children fatherless."

Various civil laws follow. If a young man has "his way" with a maid (Prov. 30:19), he must [marry ]{.class_s4N4-0}her. If the girl's father refuses to give her to him as a wife, he must pay ["the dowry of virgins."]{.class_s4N2-0} That is, he must pay a dowry just as if he had taken her for a wife. (See the remarks []{#c2RP.xhtml_page_506}on this "flesh joining flesh" relationship under Matthew 19:1--12 in the [Commentary on Matthew]{.class_s4N4-0}.)

Again we see a powerful obstacle raised against "situation ethics" and "the new morality." If a young man knew he was going to have to marry the first girl he seduced or pay "cash on the valve-head" to her father (Deut. 22:29), do you suppose that "making out" would be as popular as it is in America today? Again, Clarke and the [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_s4N4-0}are so wrapped up in the ["Targumist"]{.class_s4N4-0} (Clarke, pg. 415) and ["Eastern customs"]{.class_s4N4-0} that they fail to alert the serious student of the Scripture to 2 Samuel 13:13--18. (Dead orthodox apostates always prefer "light from the original languages" or "light from the ancient East" or "light from the Bodmer papyrii" or "light from archaeological excavations" to the Light that comes from the word of God.)

["Thou shalt not suffer a witch to live"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 18). The verse was used by Calvinists and Catholics---[both Church-State religions]{.class_s4N4-0}---to burn and torture a variety of people on both sides of the Atlantic. The [King James]{.class_s4N4-0} translators had a good bit more sense about these religious phonies than the Lockman Foundation of 1970. The [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}translators saw the "catholicity" of the Puritans, and they recognized the persecuting spirit in their religion. All religions or beliefs which resort to politics, law enforcement, litigations, arrests, imprisonments, financial pressure, or social ostracizing to obtain their objectives are SATANIC. John Calvin's Reformed groups carried this Church-State set up with them exactly as they inherited it from their papal forebearers; for this reason, no one should be surprised to find "Salem Witchcraft" hangings going on in "New" England to replace the fires of Smithfield in [Old ]{.class_s4N4-0}England.

No alert student could have failed to observe that the regulations given here (chaps. 21--23) are given to a political body called out as a national entity. [There is no direct reference to the born-again Christian in the Body of Christ anywhere within 1,500 verses of the chapter in either direction. ]{.class_s4N4-0}How then did John Calvin and the Popes manage to appropriate the verse for "Christian" practice? No man who believed the Bible would ever burn anyone at the stake in this dispensation, nor would he sympathize with anyone who had it done or [condoned it.]{.class_s4N4-0}

Deuteronomy 18:10 and Leviticus 19:26, 31 make it clear that witches are real people who must be dealt with under the Jewish theocracy (1 Sam. 28). God dealt roughly with Manasseh for consorting with these people and others (2 Chron. 33:6). Encyclopedias on witchcraft (and "histories of witchcraft") bear witness to seven salient truths.

1\. There [have ]{.class_s4N4-0}been people who were in contact with demoniac spirits and the Devil.

2\. These people had unusual or "supernatural powers," [at times.]{.class_s4N4-0}

3\. Many of these people used [drugs ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [hypnotism ]{.class_s4N4-0}as part of their communicative operations.

4\. Many used WOODEN objects for a basis of communicating.

5\. Many of them were fakirs who did NOT have real supernatural power.

6\. Many of those tortured into confession, or burnt at the stake, were Bible-believing Christians who rejected the Roman Mass and Roman sacraments.

7\. But there are still many real witches on earth today (male and female), some of whom are genuinely []{#c2RP.xhtml_page_508}demon-possessed, psychic mediums, while others are merely play-actors seeking "accreditation."

Symbols are universal: the [he-goat]{.class_s4N4-0} of Daniel 8:5--8, the [Tarot cards,]{.class_s4N4-0} the crystal [ball]{.class_s4N4-0} (cf. Rev. 4:6), the [frog ]{.class_s4N4-0}of Revelation 16:13, the [black cat ]{.class_s4N4-0}of Revelation 13:1--2, naked women and sex orgies (Rev. 17:4), [bulls]{.class_s4N4-0} (Psa. 22:12), and [wands ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Exod. 7:9--12).

The witches in the New Testament (Acts 16:16--18) are [rebuked]{.class_s4N4-0}; they are not burned at the stake. This proves again that the Roman Catholic Church (as the Calvinistic "Reformed Churches" of the Puritans) was basically led by Bible-rejecting "Christians." They may have believed in the "fundamentals" of the "Apostles' Creed," but where the New Testament crossed their [political setups,]{.class_s4N4-0} they simply ignored it (cf. remarks under 32:6, 19) .

["Whosoever lieth with a beast shall surely be put to death"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 19). The home of this practice, known as "bestiality," is the same place as the home of Origen, hard rock, [Vaticanus]{.class_s4N4-0}, acid rock, [Sinaiticus]{.class_s4N4-0}, Pharaoh, Nestle's Greek text, Rap Brown, Martin Luther King Jr., the Bodmer Papyrus, Westcott and Hort's text, the Latin church, jazz music, the Jesuit Bible of 1582, Cassius Clay, Symmachus, Theodotian, the Arian Controversy, the NAACP, Ralph Abernathy, and the [New American Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} (1963) recommended at Bob Jones University: [Africa.]{.class_s4N4-0}

Bestiality was part of the worship services in Egypt according to every Egyptologist (see [Pulpit Commentary,]{.class_s4N4-0} pg. 191), and it will be reincorporated into American "worship services" at a later date (see [Mark of the Beast,]{.class_s4N4-0} 1961, 1970). It is still practiced in Egypt, and until World War I, it was practiced in night clubs in New Orleans as part of the "tourist attractions." In the Bible, the act []{#c2RP.xhtml_page_509}merits capital punishment. It is called ["confusion"]{.class_s4N2-0} in Leviticus 18:23, as the counterpart to Babel ("confusion") and Babylonian worship (see Rev. 17:1--8).

["He that sacrificeth unto any god\...utterly destroyed"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 20). Here we see the word ["god"]{.class_s4N2-0} plainly used as a reference to "gods"---[not ]{.class_s4N4-0}"judges" (see 21:6 and comments). This crime is committed without any letup from Rehoboam to Zedekiah (1 Kings 13; 2 Kings 25), and it was the cause of the captivity of the ten northern tribes (2 Kings 17). Defining "other gods" as anything or anybody placed before God (Dan. 5:4, 23 for example), we can readily understand why America is in the pitiful condition in which it has been since World War II. In America, there are three "gods" to which people make sacrifices: these gods are MONEY, SEX, and EDUCATION, in about that order.

The "supreme sacrifice" has often been made for College educations and higher incomes, and it has cost the devotee his health, sanity, peace of mind, and soul. Where the god of "Christian education" rears its head, the authority of [the Bible]{.class_s4N4-0} is replaced with the authority of the [faculty members. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Where the god of sex rears its head, venereal diseases, divorces, and illegitimate births triple and quadruple until it takes a Welfare State to handle the populace. Where the god of mammon appears (Luke 16:13), ethics and moral principles are abandoned, and even a "Christian businessman" will go after the "sucker" like a Wall Street broker.

Naturally, the major commentators fail to make any up-to-date application of the verse. We should expect them to fail, for the people who talk the longest and loudest about the "archaic" and "obsolete words" in the [Authorized Version]{.class_s4N4-0} are never able to bring anything up []{#c2RP.xhtml_page_510}to date when [preaching ]{.class_s4N4-0}or [teaching ]{.class_s4N4-0}the Bible. As dead-orthodox apostates, they deal with a dead book that has no spiritual application to the world in which they live.

["Thou shalt neither vex\...nor oppress\...for ye were strangers"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 21). NO precedent is given in either Testament for the "Simon Legree" way of handling servants (see Col. 4:1). The appeal to past experience is identical to the appeal made to the Christian when it comes to forgiving others (see Eph. 4:32). See, for example, Leviticus 19:33--34, and Exodus 23:9.

["Ye shall not afflict any widow\...If thou afflict\...my wrath shall wax hot" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vss. 22--24). As verse 21 and verse 25 state, these are humanitarian laws; the God of the universe has an eye for the widow (Luke 21:2) and the fatherless child (Deut. 24:17--21, 26:12--13), as well ["as the lilies of the field"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Matt. 6:28--30) and ["the fowls of the air"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Matt. 6:26). An abstract "Universal Mind" or "First Cause" or "Unmoved Mover" or "Prime Mover" is unknown to any of the Biblical writers. These "gods" are the product of the depraved mind of the unregenerate philosopher who doesn't want God digging into his conscious and subconscious life (Psa. 139:1--4) . The God who controls the political destinies of nations is also concerned about [bird's nests]{.class_s4N4-0} (Deut. 22:6). This is the God whom Liberal theologians desire to get rid of by stating that either He has "died" or that He never lived.

Punishment is meted out to those who oppress widows and orphans by God Himself---not the judges (vs. 24). James defines ["pure religion"]{.class_s4N2-0} as tending to widows and orphans (James 2:27); the Lord Jesus accuses the religious council of His day of violating the commandment (Matt. 23:14).

God borrows a sword when He executes judgment for this crime (vs. 24). It is not a case of a judge wielding the sword as a ["minister"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rom. 13:1--5), but God Himself using the sword (Jer. 14:12--18; Ezek. 22:7--15; etc.).

22:25 "If thou lend money to any of my people that is poor by thee, thou shalt not be to him as an usurer, neither shalt thou lay upon him usury.

26 If thou at all take thy neighbour's raiment to pledge, thou shalt deliver it unto him by that the sun goeth down:

27 For that is his covering only, it is his raiment for his skin: wherein shall he sleep? and it shall come to pass, when he crieth unto me, that I will hear; for I am gracious.

28 Thou shalt not revile the gods, nor curse the ruler of thy people.

29 Thou shalt not delay to offer the first of thy ripe fruits, and of thy liquors: the firstborn of thy sons shalt thou give unto me.

30 Likewise shalt thou do with thine oxen, and with thy sheep: seven days it shall be with his dam; on the eighth day thou shalt give it me.

31 And ye shall be holy men unto me: neither shall ye eat any flesh that is torn of beasts in the field; ye shall cast it to the dogs."

In business matters, the Jew could not loan money to another Jew and charge him [interest]{.class_s4N4-0}. He was allowed to charge interest to other nations (Deut. 23:20) however, and this has given the anti-Semitic researchers enough fuel to kindle a fire as hot as a Bessemer blast furnace. The discrimination, however, is divinely ordered and []{#c2RP.xhtml_page_512}divinely inspired (2 Pet. 1:21), and Gentile objections to it only come under the heading of Jeremiah 33:24.

"Compound interest compounded semiannually, etc.," is the great black magic act of Capitalism. In a nut shell it means: "the figures don't mean what they say." A house bought on a 7% or 8% loan amounts to a payment of 90% or more. By "double compounding the collateral of the initial investment with a semiannual accumulation of escrow and dividend holdings, deductible from the surplus of the interest, subtracted from the total net gain of the principle plus the interest, etc.," one can get \$50,000 for a \$2,000 deal and make a man pay ten years at \$80.00 a month to pay off \$7,000.

A man who thinks that America survives because of its [economic system]{.class_s4N4-0} is bananas. We have survived so far because---until World War II---we honored [God's word]{.class_s4N4-0} to some extent, and Christian mothers and fathers spent some time in [prayer]{.class_s4N4-0}. National Socialism (1970--1980) is the only logical end for a system where 6% interest means 80% interest. Marx knew that, and so did Engels, Trotsky, and Lenin. So far, they were right, but as all international Socialists, they reckoned without God or the Bible; the Lord God Almighty deals the last hand (Rev. 19--22) when any (and all) economic systems will go out of business in less than four hours (Rev. 19:21) .

The[ "pledge" ]{.class_s4N2-0}of verse 26 is the pledge of Proverbs 20:16 (see the [Commentary on Proverbs]{.class_s4N4-0}). It is the pawnbroker's "unredeemed" item found throughout America today. In this case, the ["raiment"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 26) is the outer garment, which was a large woolen shawl like a blanket; it is still worn by the Arabs today in Palestine. Americans, spoiled on luxuriant food, clothing, and shelters, find it hard to imagine that a man could be []{#c2RP.xhtml_page_513}so poor (Deut. 24:12--13) that his sole possession would be a [blanket]{.class_s4N4-0}, but travelers in the East (India, Pakistan, etc.) find the sight quite common.

The universal testimony of history and nations is that where the Bible is slighted, ignored, or destroyed, the material prosperity collapses with it. Japan and Germany were momentary exceptions to the rule, but time and history also caught up with them. [Communism ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [Roman Catholicism]{.class_s4N4-0} are the temporary expedients to be used when the prosperity collapses. That is all they ever have been or ever will be. They are [man-made substitutes]{.class_s4N4-0} for getting right with God and getting back to God's word, so they can only [temporarily ]{.class_s4N4-0}alleviate the misery and suffering of sinful men. Material prosperity is not a great deal different in China than in Mexico, and most of the people in Spain and Italy live about as they do in Russia and Albania when it comes to personal wealth and material goods. England never lived like Spain until she rejected the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}text of the Bible (1904) and adopted the  heretical "Nestle's text" for her missionary societies.

The poor man, "out in the cold," will cry to God at night, and God will judge the man who refuses to lend him the raiment. The idea is that the holder can keep the pledge during the daytime but not at [night]{.class_s4N4-0} if the man is so poor that the pledge is all that he has to keep him from catching pneumonia.

["Thou shalt not revile the gods"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 28 cf. 23:13 and comments). The commentators are divided on the[ "gods"]{.class_s4N2-0}; some say "God." The word has no article on it (as 21:6) and therefore cannot refer to human judges. It can be translated "God" or "gods," but since the former "God" is [already covered]{.class_s4N4-0} in Leviticus 24:15--16, []{#c2RP.xhtml_page_514}the [Authorized Version]{.class_s4N4-0} has the best of the argument. Naturally, the apostate corruptions of the Twentieth Century, [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1901) and [NASV]{.class_s4N4-0} (1971), choose the reading of the [Revised Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} of 1952 put out by the National Council of "Christian" Churches. Dummelow will allow any reading but the reading of the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1611); this is the attitude of the revisers of the [American Standard Version ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1901).

The reader will observe, again, the peculiar obsession which dead-orthodox apostates have to get rid of ["the gods"]{.class_s4N2-0} and force them to be a reference to Israelish judges every time the word appears. All of these revisers---Lockman Foundation, Scofield Board of Editors, etc.---blithely overlook the statement in Psalm 82:5 that ["all the foundations of the earth are out of course"]{.class_s4N2-0} at the time that these "gods" die, and when they die they[ "die LIKE men"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Psa. 82:7). They also fail to notice that the context is the SECOND ADVENT (Psa. 82:8), [not the Old Testament theocracy. ]{.class_s4N4-0}But all of this, of course, comes from the refusal of the dead-orthodox apostate (Schaff, Roberson, Warfield, Machen, Bruce, Neal, Custer, Souter, Aland, Nestle, Bathgen, Davies, et al.) to reckon on "visitors from outer space" (Gen. 6:1--4) who will show up again (Rev. 12:3--9). (See extensive comment in the[ Commentary on Genesis,]{.class_s4N4-0} Gen. 6:1--4.)

There is to be no delay in giving the "firstfruits" to God (vss. 29--30), whether it be sons, crops, oxen, or sheep (vs. 30). Later (Num. 3:12, 41), the Levites assume the place of the firstborn, but here they are still replacements for the slaughter in Egypt.

The serious student of Scripture should observe how the word ["liquors"]{.class_s4N2-0} is used here: it is a reference to ANY juice squeezed out of ANY fruit. This truth, which []{#c2RP.xhtml_page_515}ties in with Isaiah 65:8; Genesis 40:9--11; and Matthew 26:29, is relative to one of the greatest controversies in Christianity---the "Lord's Supper" (or the "Mass" as it is blasphemously called in papal dominion). [The omission of the word]{.class_s4N4-0} in the [New American Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} (and other dead-orthodox corruptions) is very typical of modern "Conservative scholarship" and the Lockman Foundation. With no daily experience in practical Christian warfare, and with no knowledge of Satan's devices, the modern "Conservative" scholar erases revelations like this without giving them a second thought. The Bible believer, as always, will do well to take the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}text of 1611 over any (and all) the "newer translations."

Leviticus 22:27 is a comment on verse 30. The reader should notice that ["the eighth day"]{.class_s4N2-0} has the same significance for the oxen and sheep that it does for the circumcised[ "man child"]{.class_s4N2-0} of Genesis 17:12.

Verse 31 follows the same thought. ["Holy]{.class_s4N2-0} [men" ]{.class_s4N2-0}are men given to God as the oxen, sheep, and firstborn man child were given to God (vss. 29--30). Exodus 19:5--6, 10 defines the new nation as being a[ "holy nation."]{.class_s4N2-0}

The [Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} (as Clarke and Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown) again forgets to guide the reader to the living words of the living God and blithely overlooks a New Testament passage which no real Bible believer could possibly have overlooked if he were engaged in COMMENTING instead of CRITICIZING: the [Syrophenician woman!]{.class_s4N4-0} The woman of Matthew 15:21--28 knew the passage and took her place as a Gentile [dog]{.class_s4N4-0}, with an understanding of Scripture (see Deut. 14:21) that would astound the faculty members of Wheaton College. Isn't it odd how a Canaanite woman could tangle Jesus []{#c2RP.xhtml_page_516}Christ up in His talk by using two truths of the Law, when the greatest legal brains of His day tried to do the same thing for three years (Matt. 22:15) and never made it?! (Talk about "nuggets in the original language" and "light from the original Hebrew," how did all of the major commentators miss that one?)

["Ye shall cast it to the dogs"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 31). The Syrophenician woman took her place as a female dog (I forbear to use the term in the modern vernacular), and instead of hollering about racial discrimination, civil rights, and defamation of character, she humbled herself to one place [lower ]{.class_s4N4-0}than ["a servant of servants"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Phil. 2:5--11) AND GOT THE BLESSING!

[]{#c2UM.xhtml}

# Chapter 23 {.heading_s4G-0}

23:1 "Thou shalt not raise a false report: put not thine hand with the wicked to be an unrighteous witness.

2 Thou shalt not follow a multitude to do evi1; neither shalt thou speak in a cause to decline after many to wrest judgment:

3 Neither shalt thou countenance a poor man in his cause.

4 If thou meet thine enemy's ox or his ass going astray, thou shalt surely bring it back to him again.

5 If thou see the ass of him that hateth thee lying under his burden, and wouldest forbear to help him, thou shalt surely help with him.

6 Thou shalt not wrest the judgment of thy poor in his cause.

7 Keep thee far from a false matter; and the innocent and righteous slay thou not: for I will not justify the wicked.

8 And thou shalt take no gift: for the gift blindeth the wise, and perverteth the words of the righteous."

The section deals mainly with [court matters]{.class_s4N4-0}---witnessing, bribery, judgments, and reporting misdemeanors.

["Thou shalt not raise a false report"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 1). This is substantially the same as the ninth commandment, and the comments on the verse by the Holy Spirit in Proverbs (Prov. 12:17, 22, 14:25, 15:28, 16:27--30) are far superior to any of the comments made by Lange, Clarke, Dummelow, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, and the [Wycliffe]{.class_s4N4-0} commentator (Dr. Phillip Johnson).

As we remarked earlier, the most notorious "false reporters" are the men who handle the wires of the Associated Press, the International News Service, and the United Press; and beside their reporting, the message of Zedekiah (1 Kings 22:11) sounds like the gospel truth. The failure to report bills slipped through the House of Representatives which affected the families and properties of citizens, the failure to report the racism practiced by the NAACP continually, the failure to report the correct number of casualties in Vietnam, the failure to report the killing of Bible believers in Columbia (1950--1960), the failure to tell the people that the members of the Irish Republic Army all belong to one church (and that church is a minority in Ulster), the failure to inform the people of the agreements made between Mussolini, Franco, Hitler, and the Popes, the failure to tell the truth about the religious issue in Vietnam, the failure to report both sides of the Warren report, the failure to tell the public that Johnson was sworn into office on a Roman Catholic Missal instead of a Bible, the failure to publish statistics on rape, assault, and robbery in the high schools from a racial standpoint, the refusal to print articles AGAINST integration, the refusal to publish pro-Bible articles while printing anti-Bible articles, the refusal to publish ads stating race, religion, and sex---[when ALL THREE ITEMS are required in a federal census,]{.class_s4N4-0} and the refusal to fight the liquor traffic while fighting tobacco, all brand the news media as the cheapest opportunists known to man. You could not find enough religious or political truth in a year's subscription to [Life, Newsweek, Time,]{.class_s4N4-0} and [Look ]{.class_s4N4-0}to fill the left eye of a blind mosquito.

The "false report" in America is usually voiced as follows: "Police Brutality!" "We can't get out of the ghetto!" "Children need sex education by [accredited ]{.class_s4N4-0}single women!" "The [government ]{.class_s4N4-0}must get federal control over four year olds!" "Pollution!" "We need the [government ]{.class_s4N4-0}to take over our factories!!" "Poverty!" "We need the [government ]{.class_s4N4-0}to double taxes on the wage earners!" "Crime! We need the federal [government ]{.class_s4N4-0}to let all the prisoners out of the State penitentiaries!" etc.

The[ "unrighteous witness" ]{.class_s4N2-0}of verse 1 is found in 1 Kings 21:13 and Matthew 26:59--61.

["Thou shalt not follow a multitude"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 2), as in any modern "demonstration" where the hypocrites pretend to be assembling "peaceably" with switchblades, Molotov cocktails, [razor blades,]{.class_s4N4-0} acid bottles, billiard cue sticks, bicycle chains, cleated boots, and rotten eggs. The Memphis and Montgomery riots (often miscalled "[peace ]{.class_s4N4-0}marches") are quite typical of the hell-raising that goes on under the name of a "Southern CHRISTIAN Leadership Conference." (We have not yet been able to find anywhere in the New Testament a record of such "peaceable" demonstrations which get TV sets, FM radios, vacuum cleaners, and jewelry out of broken store windows. This is definitely a new kind of "Christian!")

When the Communists marched from Selma to Montgomery, the [Washington Post]{.class_s4N4-0} called it a ["crusade for victory"]{.class_s4N4-0} and a ["crusade for righteousness,"]{.class_s4N4-0} but when the same cigarette-butt-dumping, public-urinating, prophylactic-slinging, dope-peddling "civil rights workers" encamped in Washington, D.C., the [Washington Post]{.class_s4N4-0} had a perfect spasm and moaned about how the "poor people's camp" would hurt the tourist trade! (Evidently, Washington is not going to be able []{#c2UM.xhtml_page_520}to tell anybody in Selma or Montgomery how to handle anything; at least, not honestly.)

The last half of verse 2 is the old gimmick of the city councilman voting with the majority (["to decline after many to wrest judgment"]{.class_s4N2-0}) so he will not stick out like a sore thumb. Job was always careful to maintain his integrity in these matters (Job 31:34), and even if he was a "staggering minority" (like Enoch and Noah), he still voted his conscience and not the "party ticket." (I saw this matter demonstrated remarkably in a church, one time, where I asked for a standing vote of confidence from the congregation for another year as pastor. Three hundred people stood, but among them were four deacons and their families [who had just asked me to resign]{.class_s4N4-0} and were, at that moment, meeting in a private home and collecting a separate treasury by proselyting other members of the church to give to them. Nice people.)

["Neither shalt thou countenance a poor man in his cause"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 3; see Lev. 19:15, 32). The idea here is that you are not to give the poor man the "edge" in a case just because he is [poor]{.class_s4N4-0}. This is just as much "discrimination" as giving the rich man the "edge." For a perfect example one sees the tendency of Southern policemen to take it for granted---from years of firsthand experience---that the Colored man is the culprit in an accident or a brawl. On the other hand, we find the peculiar prejudice of the federal government in assuming that if a man is [Black, ]{.class_s4N4-0}he is [innocent. ]{.class_s4N4-0}This conflict between local and federal authorities has gone on now for four decades with the news media taking the FEDERAL position; as a consequence, the crime rate had tripled by the decade and will double yearly from now on. []{#c2UM.xhtml_page_521}(Many ignorant people are so anxious to prove they are not "prejudiced" that they abandon experience, common sense, and fact.)

If the poor man is at fault, his poverty is not to be considered according to verse 3, even though this is completely contrary to the modern method. The modern method is to blame society or "the environment" for the criminal activity of the [poor ]{.class_s4N4-0}man. Strangely enough, the environment is never blamed for the criminal activity of the rich man! If ghettos and rats produce one class of criminal (and are therefore to be gotten rid of), can one not say, conversely, that [liquor ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [leisure ]{.class_s4N4-0}time produce another class of criminals and they should be eliminated also? (On these points, the Communist is more consistent than the Associated Press and the psychologists. \[See also vs. 6 which gives the reverse position.\])

Verses 4 and 5 cut to the heart of domestic and social problems, and again, our commentators miss a beautiful cross-reference in Luke 10:30 and Mark 3:4. If you are to help lift up an ass that is fallen, what are you to do with a wounded [man?]{.class_s4N4-0} Here, in the middle of legalistic instructions, we find an instruction that sounds more like something from the Sermon on the Mount (see Matt. 5:43). This has to do with "heart attitude"; it is no legal ordinance dealing with [court actions]{.class_s4N4-0} as the previous three verses have been. How many Americans would help jack up the car of one of their enemies if they saw him on the "shoulder" trying to change tires? How many would return a coon dog or a rabbit dog if they found it out in the swamp (cf. Rom. 12:20; 1 Thess. 5:15)?

Serious students of the Bible will naturally reject the corrupt [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1901) here which has befuddled the verse beyond recognition:[ "thou shalt surely release it with []{#c2UM.xhtml_page_522}him," ]{.class_s4N4-0}which, of course, means nothing. The common American expression is "give me some help WITH this," not merely "give me some help!" The [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1611) is correct; the [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [NASV]{.class_s4N4-0} are, quite naturally, WRONG.

We have already commented on verse 6 under verse 3.

["Keep thee far from a false matter\...I will not justify the wicked"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 7). Two things appear immediately to the Bible believer which are necessarily obscured from the Hebrew and Oriental scholars. Clarke, Lange, Dummelow, John--son, Payne, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown go sailing by the verse like it was not even in the Bible; and when one considers the matter, he is struck again with that salient truth which is so often demonstrated by "Conservative scholars": they are blind as a bat backing into a barroom when it comes to finding real[ Bible truth. ]{.class_s4N4-0}We have seen this demonstrated on so many occasions (see the [Commentary on Galatians---Colossians,]{.class_s4N4-0} Eph. 3:18 and Col. 2:23) that it is no longer surprising, but it may come as a surprise to people who think that profession in the "verbally inspired originals" guarantees a "godly" Conservative scholar insight into the Bible.

1\. In the first place, [God DOES justify the wicked]{.class_s4N4-0} (Rom. 4:5--6). If David wasn't "wicked" (2 Sam. 12:13), who WAS? The passage, therefore, is plainly a reference to [judicial dealings]{.class_s4N4-0} within [Israel]{.class_s4N4-0} in the Old Testament. It is not a doctrinal statement of God's actions, because God is ["the justifier of him which believeth in Jesus"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rom. 3:26). Among those believers are [murderers ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Paul), [blasphemers]{.class_s4N4-0} (B. H. Carroll), [whoremongers ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Cyclone Mac), [drunkards ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Mel Trotter), [thieves ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Jim Vaus), [cussers ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Simon Peter), and [dope addicts]{.class_s4N4-0} (Jimenez). How does one explain nearly forty []{#c2UM.xhtml_page_523}major commentators skipping over the passage like it was a busted axe handle? Even the mythological [LXX ]{.class_s4N4-0}saw the problem and altered the text to avoid a contradiction---very typical of Alexandria.

2\. Christ WAS ["the innocent"]{.class_s4N2-0} and the ["righteous"]{.class_s4N2-0} (see text), and He was "slain" in ["a false matter"]{.class_s4N2-0} where the judgment was wrested (vs. 6) and the witnesses were bribed (vs. 8). If we are to ["search the scriptures"]{.class_s4N2-0} (John 5:39) because they testify of Christ, and if the final authority---according to Conservatives who reject the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1611) as an infallible authority---is the "Word" (not the "word"), then how do these old lying rascals keep right on missing reference after reference [to the One whom they claim is the final authority?]{.class_s4N4-0} Doesn't this strike you as rather "inconsistent?" As we have said before in many, many other places, the outstanding characteristic of these men who talk about "the Lord Jesus" being the final authority, and the infallible accuracy of the "verbally, plenary inspired ORIGINALS," is their [deliberate, constant, and habitual rejection of revealed truth.]{.class_s4N4-0}

Verse 8 is self-explanatory. Abram, Elisha, Peter, and Daniel all reject the "gift" though it came to them from Kings (Gen. 14:21--24), Captains (2 Kings 5:1, 15--16), sorcerers (Acts 8:9, 18--20) and world rulers (Dan. 5:16--17). See also Isaiah 33:15 and 1 Samuel 8:3. (We have made considerable comment on bribery under Prov. 17:23, in the [Commentary on Proverbs]{.class_s4N4-0}.)

23:9 "Also thou shalt not oppress a stranger: for ye know the heart of a stranger, seeing ye were strangers in the land of Egypt.

10 And six years thou shalt sow thy land, and shalt gather in the fruits thereof:

11 But the seventh year thou shalt let it rest and lie still; that the poor of thy people may eat: and what they leave the beasts of the field shall eat. In like manner thou shalt deal with thy vineyard, and with thy oliveyard.
