12 Six days thou shalt do thy work, and on the seventh day thou shalt rest: that thine ox and thine ass may rest, and the son of thy handmaid, and the stranger, may be refreshed.

13 And in all things that I have said unto you be circumspect: and make no mention of the name of other gods, neither let it be heard out of thy mouth.

14 Three times thou shalt keep a feast unto me in the year.

15 Thou shalt keep the feast of unleavened bread: (thou shalt eat unleavened bread seven days, as I commanded thee, in the time appointed of the month Abib; for in it thou camest out from Egypt: and none shall appear before me empty:)

16 And the feast of harvest, the firstfruits of thy labours, which thou hast sown in the field: and the feast of ingathering, which is in the end of the year, when thou hast gathered in thy labours out of the field.

17 Three times in the year all thy males shall appear before the Lord [God]{.class_s4N8-0}.

18 Thou shalt not offer the blood of my sacrifice with leavened bread; neither shall the fat of my sacrifice remain until the morning.

19 The first of the firstfruits of thy land thou shalt bring into the house of the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}thy God. Thou shalt not seethe a kid in his mother's milk."

The passage deals, in the main, with the land and crops. For the third time we run into the "Seven System," which is the universal basis for either Testament where God is dealing with a [finished work. ]{.class_s4N4-0}We found this once in Genesis 2:1--3, and it was revealed to the Jews in the wilderness while they were gathering manna (16:22--23). It crops up again in discussing the matter of servitude (21:2), and now again, it appears in regard to sowing and harvesting. Readers of Clarence Larkin's [Dispensational Truth]{.class_s4N4-0} know the significance of this [Seven System,]{.class_s4N4-0} although (again) it is hidden from the majority of the commentators (see 22:21 for comment on vs. 9).

The sabbatical year (vs. 11) was one of the greatest miracles ever shown to a nation. When one considers what took place every seven years, one is amazed to think that a single infidel could grow up in the land of Palestine, [for every family in the nation had to trust God for two years to supply food that would not be sown or reaped]{.class_s4N4-0} (you would not reap the crop planted the eighth year until the beginning of the ninth year). Furthermore, the servants and beasts had to count on this miracle of God exactly as the land owner or tenant farmer (vs. 11). That this law was violated continually is apparent by the statements found in Jeremiah 34:8--11. It took real faith to obey this law (2 Chron. 36:21), so it was transgressed time and time again (cf. Neh. 13:15--19), exactly as the law was transgressed in regards to the manna (16:27).

The modern Postmillennialist or Amillennialist---like the translators on the Lockman Foundation who kicked the Jew out of his inheritance (see John 18:36 in the [NASV]{.class_s4N4-0} and 1 Thess. 2:16 in the [Amplified Version]{.class_s4N4-0})---has never given the passage serious consideration, for it shows clearly that the God of nature and the universe, []{#c2UM.xhtml_page_526}who controls the weather (Psa. 147), thought nothing of giving special favors to [one nation]{.class_s4N4-0} all out of proportion to His dealings with any other nation at any other time in history. Observe how this vicious discrimination will be practiced by the Almighty AGAIN in the future (Zech. 14:18 cf. Amos 9:13).

People who believe that "there is no respect of RACES" with God, or people who believe that God would not favor Israel above every other nation, are not very careful readers of either Testament. [There isn't a farmer in Europe or America who could let his land rest every seventh year and trust God for the increase. ]{.class_s4N4-0}The "Govmint" takes care of these matters, and any Bible believer knows that when any nation quits trusting God for its living, it comes under the domination of a federal bureaucracy (see 1 Sam. 8:10--18 and especially Prov. 28:2). The only way an American farmer could survive three years of barren crops would be by leaning on the Black capital of the world (Washington, D.C.) for financial support (see also Lev. 25:1--7; Deut. 15:12--18, 31:10--13).

Verse 12 is another reminder of the weekly Sabbath of 20:8 with the additional note that the hired help is resting to be[ "refreshed."]{.class_s4N2-0}

["And in all things that I have said unto you be circumspect" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 13). The reader will notice how the [King James ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1611) has preserved the correct cross-reference to Ephesians 5:15, since the passage just given demonstrated the life of faith (vss. 10--11) and the grace of God (vs. 9) in deliverance.

The [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}of 1901, as the vile corruption of 1963 (the [NASV,]{.class_s4N4-0} recommended by Bob Jones University), naturally fails to see the connection and mistranslates the word so []{#c2UM.xhtml_page_527}the Bible student cannot find the cross-reference. As we have said on more than a dozen occasions, this is typical and to be expected of such blasphemous corruptions as the [New American Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} (1963) and its apostate sister, the [American Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} of 1901. (While "Conservative" scholars recommend these as "devotional studies" for the "devout student," they fail to tell you that they [themselves ]{.class_s4N4-0}cannot find the devotional material, that they [change ]{.class_s4N4-0}it if they do find it, and then they destroy the cross-references so [you ]{.class_s4N4-0}can't find it: some more nice people.)

["And make no mention of the name of other gods"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 13). The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} (pg. 201) is in error in assuming that the silence was to show the "contempt" of the Israelite for the ["gods." ]{.class_s4N2-0}To the contrary, any manifestation of contempt (see 22:28 and comments) for the ["gods"]{.class_s4N2-0} would get a man in plenty of trouble (Jude 9). You will observe that in the reforms of Josiah and Hezekiah, neither reformer blasts the "gods" [by name.]{.class_s4N4-0}

The writer (speaking under the inspiration of the Holy Spirit) records the names, but not even Elijah, [while mocking the prophets of Baal,]{.class_s4N4-0} mentions the NAME (1 Kings 18:27). The prophets raised up by God to point out ISRAEL'S sins (1 Kings 13:1--6; Jer. 11:17) do ["mention"]{.class_s4N2-0} the names of the gods (Jer. 9:14; 1 Kings 18:21), but the prophets were handpicked spiritual stormtroopers entrusted with [particular ministeries]{.class_s4N4-0} (Isa. 20:3) and [peculiar messages]{.class_s4N4-0} (Hos. 1--2) which could hardly be recommended to any populace (Jon. 1:1--2). (See extensive comments on the prophets in Gen. 20:7 in the[ Commentary on Genesis]{.class_s4N4-0}.)

The reason for not mentioning the gods is related to the old saying: "Speak of the Devil and he'll appear" (see 1 Sam. 28:13). Hearing the names reminds one of the reality, [constant hearing]{.class_s4N4-0} of the names accustoms one to the reality, discussion of the names brings interest, and interest leads to investigation, and [investigation ]{.class_s4N4-0}leads to 2 Kings 16:3--4, 21:3. (Clarke, Dummelow, and the [Wycliffe commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} just pretend the verse is not in the Bible and go hop, skip, and jump to verse 14 without any "comment.")

["Three times thou shalt keep a feast unto me in the year"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 14). Bible students, who have learned to trust the English text of the Reformation, are quick to see three dates given which match the three raptures of the First Resurrection. The first feast ([Unleavened Bread]{.class_s4N4-0}) is the Passover which represents forgiveness. The second feast ([First Fruits or Pentecost]{.class_s4N4-0}) represents the work of the Holy Spirit. The third feast ([Tabernacles]{.class_s4N4-0}) is called ["the feast of ingathering" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 16) which pictures the Second Advent in the Fall. The first feast (Passover, vs. 15) pictures the grace of God in the crucifixion of Jesus Christ, the second feast (Harvest, vs. 16) pictures the power of Christ's resurrection and the coming of the Holy Spirit, while the third feast (Ingathering, vs. 16) pictures the crowning of the King at the completion of this age. Note Deuteronomy 16:16.

Again, readers of Clarence Larkin's [Dispensational Truth]{.class_s4N4-0} will gain an insight into the prophetic significance of the feasts which is completely denied to Gesenius, Olhausen, Van Impe, Delitzsch, Keil, Johnson ([Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0}), Archer, Dummelow, Lange, Pentecost, Clarke, Ellicott, Luzatto, Geiger, Cheyne, Dill--man, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, and nearly any other fifty []{#c2UM.xhtml_page_529}critics at random. (If Williams suspected any connection with the first resurrection, he was afraid to mention it, in spite of the fact that the resurrection is spoken of distinctly as a HARVEST OF A CROP in the Pauline Epistles: \[1 Cor. 15:35--38\].)

Our best "bet," at this point, will be to abandon every Conservative scholar of the last three centuries who made a living correcting the [King James ]{.class_s4N4-0}Bible with "the originals" (to quote the lying cliché of the average "Conservative"). By depositing the works of these destructive Bible critics in the wastebasket and going to the Holy Bible for our material, we can learn that:

1\. [The Passover]{.class_s4N4-0} in the Spring (March--April) touched off the resurrection of Old Testament saints (Matt. 27:52--53); these were called the ["first fruits"]{.class_s4N2-0} of another harvest in 1 Corinthians 15:23.

2\. [The day of Pentecost,]{.class_s4N4-0} fifty days after the Passover (or Feast of Unleavened Bread), touched off the coming of the Holy Spirit to indwell a body of believers who will be Christ's ["at his coming"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 Cor. 15:23). This is the "Rapture" referred to in 1 Thessalonians 4:13--18 and 1 Corinthians 15:49--53. All the indications of Scripture point to a day somewhere in [May ]{.class_s4N4-0}or [June ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Song of Sol. 2:10--13). Since this is called "heresy" by most Conservatives, we will not teach it as an [absolute ]{.class_s4N4-0}truth. However, the serious student of Scripture, who has learned to trust the Bible instead of its critics, cannot fail to observe that the Holy Spirit COMES DOWN to start this dispensation in the SPRING. What would lead anyone to think that He will GO UP without regard for the typology which He Himself established? [Did you see what God did to Moses for not following typology ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Num. 20:12 cf. 1 Cor. 10:2--4)?

3\. [The Feast of Tabernacles]{.class_s4N4-0} (Sept.--Oct., [Yom Kippur]{.class_s4N4-0}) is plainly the DATE of the Second Advent (Matt. 17:4), when Jesus Christ will land on this earth as King of the Jews (Lev. 23:34, 42 cf. Zech. 14) and usher in the Millennial kingdom; it marks a "gleaning" of Tribulation saints at a post-Tribulation rapture.

Now all of this sounds too much like Judge Rutherford and Pastor Russell to the ears of Bible-rejecting Conservatives like Neal, Thieme, Wuest, Custer, Payne, English, Weniger, et al., so we need not expect any sympathy from them. However, if the reader is really balanced and objective in his approach to the Bible, he will think twice before he abandons the text of the Bible for the private opinions of any Conservative simply because someone says he is "godly" and "qualified." The truly objective student has noticed that the Bible which these men (and others) recommend flatly denies the [Virgin Birth]{.class_s4N4-0} of Jesus Christ in Luke 2:33 and the [Deity of Christ]{.class_s4N4-0} in Luke 24:51--52, and teaches, instead, the official teaching of Judge Rutherford and Pastor Russell in John 1:18. John 1:18, in the [New American Standard Version,]{.class_s4N4-0} promoted by Wilbur Smith and John R. Rice, is the official statement of Arianism from the writings of Origen and Arius, preserved in the Roman Catholic Vatican manuscript. (If you don't believe it, look it up; don't just holler and yell and carry on like a two year old simply because you are such a bigot that you can't tell a "crackpot" from a Greek scholar. Look it up!)

Further instructions and comments are found on the feasts in Leviticus 23:5--44; Deuteronomy 16:9--11, 31:10; John 7:2; and Nehemiah 8:17. Feasts of this nature tended to unify Israel, and they set the pace for the practice of the Gentiles later to "gather" at Thanksgiving []{#c2UM.xhtml_page_531}and Christmas. (Religious gatherings were common among all heathen tribes, but not on a nationwide scale.) Among other feasts observed by the Israelites were the Feast of the Sabbatical Year (Lev. 25:1--7), the Feast of Trumpets (Lev. 23:24), the Feast of the New Moon (Isa. 66:23), the Feast of Expiation (Day of Atonement---Lev. 23:27--28), the Feast of Purim (Esth. 9:26--32), the Feast of Dedication (Hanukkah, see John 10:22---man-made), "the feast of the discovery of the sacred fire" (man-made), the Feast of Xylophoria (man-made), as well as several others (see Clarke's comments on the passage in his Commentary). To counterfeit the real Kingdom of Heaven (see [The Sure Word of Prophecy]{.class_s4N4-0}), the Roman Catholics have invented Christmas, Easter, Shrove Tuesday, Ash Wednesday, etc. (see comments under 12:14, 16).

["All thy males"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 17) would exclude males under 13, plus sick men and men over sixty (Lev. 27:7), according to the Rabbis and Jewish commentators. In the case of the Rapture at Pentecost (see notes above), all of those who appear will be [male ]{.class_s4N4-0}anyway (see 1 John 3:1--3; Phil. 3:21; and Rom. 8:29)! (Again, this Biblical truth will be called "heresy" by the Bible-rejecting Conservatives who could not find it, but "the majority of accredited scholars" could not find it because God would not show it to them. He would not show it to them because He knew their HEARTS. At heart, they hated and resented the authority of the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}English text.)

Another phenomenon which is interesting is that most Jewish exegetes identify the time of the giving of the Law with the time of Pentecost or the "Feast of Firstfruits" (see 19:1). Is not this strange when one []{#c2UM.xhtml_page_532}considers that the Law will [return ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Rev. 14:12, 12:17) [after the Rapture of the Church]{.class_s4N4-0} (Dan. 12:1--4, 9:27)?

(Again, the modern Bible-rejecting Conservative must reject the truth, for he has been taught to alter Hebrews 3:14, 6:6, 10:26; Matthew 24:13; Revelation 22:14 in line with his own bias against the Reformation text. This is done by appealing to the "original Greek" or "THE Greek text" \[to quote the faculty at BJU\].)

Dr. Johnson ([Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0}) informs us that [unleavened bread]{.class_s4N4-0} was to be used in "[all ]{.class_s4N4-0}the feasts." Again, this is typical of the modern Conservative who believes in "the verbally inspired originals." Leviticus 23:17 tells us, in no uncertain terms, that the feast of Pentecost is to be celebrated with ["two wave loaves of two tenth deals: they shall be of fine flour; they shall be baken with leaven."]{.class_s4N2-0} Dr. Johnson (as 300 more like him) has simply trifled with the word of God. Verse 18 (in the text) said: ["Thou shalt not offer the blood of my sacrifice with leavened bread."]{.class_s4N2-0} There was nothing said about the "Feast of Firstfruits" one way or another. The reference is to the [Passover ]{.class_s4N4-0}(34:25), and that can be seen at one glance if that "glance" is the glance of a Bible believer who is submissive to the authority of the Bible (see 18:12).

["Neither shall the fat of my sacrifice remain until the morning"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 18) has been commented on under 12:10.

["Into the house of the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord ]{.class_s4N9-0}[thy God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 19) is the first mention of the Tabernacle as a ["house,"]{.class_s4N2-0} and of course, up till this time, no instructions have been given for the Tabernacle of chapters 25--30.

["Thou shalt not seethe a kid in his mother's milk"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 19). Two explanations are given. The first []{#c2UM.xhtml_page_533}is that the Egyptians used this method (Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown) or that the Canaanites did ([Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0}) in their pagan ceremonies. The second is that it is a prohibition to notify the Jew that God is interested in natural decency and is against unnatural perversion of those laws. Rationally---from the "scientific point of view," I believe is the modern cliché---there could be nothing wrong with cooking a piece of goat meat in the milk of its mother; however, Paul tells us that there is nothing wrong (rationally) with eating something offered to an idol (1 Cor. 8:7).

The Christian's testimony is at stake on the "steak" (1 Cor. 8:13) when he eats with an unbeliever, and the Jew's testimony is at stake on the veal when he gives the impression that he used a mother to kill her own offspring. Boiling a dead goat in the substance which God furnished for the purpose of giving it life is a violation of "good taste" (i.e., "discrimination"), which is not one of the strong points of the modern American, especially the religious leaders of the ecumenical council and their sponsors---the TV networks and newspapers. Anyone can look at a daily newspaper or twelve hours of TV and then get the point. "Good taste" is as foreign to the news media as tact was to Friedrich Nietzsche.

The modern Liberal preacher in America (who is an international Socialist at heart) will abandon Bible preaching, altar calls, public prayer, personal work, and tract distribution before he will abandon seances, Watusi performances, jazz masses, hippy weddings, collections for Black Muslims, pornographic advertisements, "adult" movies, strip teasers, and "social involvement." What these men call "bad taste" is [straight Bible preaching in plain language. ]{.class_s4N4-0}What they call "good taste" is two hours []{#c2UM.xhtml_page_534}of color cameras following the corpse of a dead Catholic to his grave, with all the morbid water-sprinkling, Tammuz-crossing, black-robed, mourning Baalites and widow attending. Liberal preachers have been known to criticize Bob Jones Sr. and Billy Sunday for their "bad taste," but they have never been known to criticize ["Jesus Christ, Superstar"]{.class_s4N4-0} for being in bad taste. (Something is wrong with somebody\'s "tastebuds.")

23:20 "Behold, I send an Angel before thee, to keep thee in the way, and to bring thee into the place which I have prepared.

21 Beware of him, and obey his voice, provoke him not; for he will not pardon your transgressions: for my name is in him.

22 But if thou shalt indeed obey his voice, and do all that I speak; then I will be an enemy unto thine enemies, and an adversary unto thine adversaries.

23 For mine Angel shall go before thee, and bring thee in unto the Amorites, and the Hittites, and the Perizzites, and the Canaanites, and the Hivites, and the Jebusites: and I will cut them off.

24 Thou shalt not bow down to their gods, nor serve them, nor do after their works: but thou shalt utterly overthrow them, and quite break down their images.

25 And ye shall serve the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}your God, and he shall bless thy bread, and thy water; and I will take sickness away from the midst of thee."

The passage deals with the "Angel of the Lord," who is identified in the New Testament as the Lord Jesus Christ (Gal. 4:14; Acts 27:23) in His preincarnate manifestations. The reader should observe that an "angel" may or may NOT be synonymous with "THE []{#c2UM.xhtml_page_535}Angel of the [Lord]{.class_s4NB}" (Matt. 2:19). (This is important, because the Conservative critics of the Bible who yap so long and loud about "God-breathed originals" and "the original Greek text" often make a big "to-do" about the presence or absence of an article in the [Authorized Version. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Strangely enough, these are the same people who say "THE Greek text says\..." \[see any publication by Dr. Custer or Dr. Neal\] when they know perfectly well that there are dozens of Greek texts.)

Dummelow makes one three-word reference to the Angel and then sits out the rest of the game. Dr. Johnson is careful to avoid any reference to Jesus Christ; instead, he follows the lead of Judge Rutherford and tells us that the Angel was a manifestation of Jehovah, not Jesus ([Wycliffe Commentary, ]{.class_s4N4-0}pg. 73). Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown follow this lead. The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} says "the BEST expositors" identify the Angel with Christ, but then they alter the word "Angel" to "messenger." This is more of the same old mess, since every angel in the Bible IS NOT a "messenger," yet every angel in the Bible is an APPEARANCE. (See comments on Rev. 2:1 in the [Commentary on Revelation]{.class_s4N4-0}. It is traditional among dead-orthodox scholars to call angels "messengers.")

The most unique interpretation of the passage is the one suggested by Bullinger, who tells us that the "angel" can be none other than Michael. One of the cross-references Bullinger gives for this conclusion is Isaiah 63:9. It probably never occurred to Bullinger that everything in [that ]{.class_s4N4-0}context is a reference to Jesus Christ.

Applying the passage to Christ's dealing with people---and all dead-orthodox Conservatives accept "Christ" as the final authority instead of the Bible---we find:

1\. Jesus Christ is the AUTHOR of our race (Heb. 12:1--2), and He [goes before us]{.class_s4N4-0} (vs. 20).

2\. He will keep us ["in the way" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 20), according to Hebrews 13:5--6.

3\. He will bring us into the [prepared place]{.class_s4N4-0} (vs. 20) which He Himself has prepared (John 14:1--3).

4\. We should not [provoke ]{.class_s4N4-0}Him (1 Cor. 10:5--10), and we should obey Him (John 14:15).

5\. He will pardon no one's transgressions in the Old Testament, even where they are forgiven (Exod. 34:7; Rom. 3:25; Heb. 9:22), and it is not until His blood is shed---ANGELS HAVE NO BLOOD---that there is any full pardon and "redemption of transgressions" (Heb. 9:15).

6\. His name is "Jesus" (vs. 21), and this is God's New Testament name. Corrupt translations like the [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(either edition) try their best to destroy the connection between Jehovah and Jesus (Psa. 68:4), and this is done by refusing to print "LORD" in the Old Testament. Only "Jehovah" appears, thus breaking hundreds of cross-references to the "Lord" of both Testaments. (If this were the only damage done in the [American Standard Version,]{.class_s4N4-0} it would be enough to consign the unholy mess to the incinerator, but this is a small sample of their gnostic distortions of the truth.)

7\. The Angel will do the fighting (vs. 23; see 17:13 and comments). Observe, once more, the vicious racial discrimination, the source of which is the Lord God Almighty, Creator of heaven and earth (vs. 23). Blessing (vs. 25) is conditioned upon genocide (vs. 23). That is, [no material blessings]{.class_s4N4-0} could come to the race of people who came from Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob until they took [an aggressive military]{.class_s4N4-0} action against the descendants []{#c2UM.xhtml_page_537}of Ham. (You talk about "Southern WHITE supremacy"! [What do you make of vs. 23?]{.class_s4N4-0})

Furthermore, the destruction of Hamitic culture---art, music, religion, literature, and commerce---was to be complete; it was to be [annihilated ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Deut. 12:2--3, 7:23--24, 2:12, 21--22; Num. 33:52). The first two warnings against racial integration are [religious ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [artistic ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Num. 33:52). Note that ["their works"]{.class_s4N2-0} are connected with their religious convictions. There has never been any way to separate religion from politics, for a man's works are the outcome of what he believes. Eventually, all Western politics get [religious, ]{.class_s4N4-0}and all Western religions get involved in [politics]{.class_s4N4-0}. Communism is a religion exactly like Fascism, Catholicism, evolution, or Buddhism. Every one of those groups submit to a supreme authority of some kind. Communism, Fascism, and Catholicism are political groups with [political designs]{.class_s4N4-0} on the races of mankind, and an evolutionist---a genuine one, not a "professor"---believes in "progress" just as religiously as a papist believes in an infallible church.

The supreme god of Communism is "peace on earth" through a just economic distribution. The supreme god of evolution is "growth and change" till men become "humanoids" controlling the universe. The supreme god of Fascism is an authoritative STATE or man. The supreme god of Roman Catholicism is an authoritative religious leader; where the Bible and his "tradition" contradict (Matt. 23; 1 Tim. 2; Heb. 10; etc.), the church is the deciding factor: it is "god." The only "god" who can fill all the requirements listed above would be ["the beast" ]{.class_s4N2-0}of Revelation 13:1--8 (see [Mark of the Beast]{.class_s4N4-0}).

You cannot have a "religious conviction" or philosophical conviction without it affecting your choice []{#c2UM.xhtml_page_538}of candidates, your party platforms, your method of dealing with social and civic issues, your choice of residences, and your cooperation [with ]{.class_s4N4-0}(or revolt [against]{.class_s4N4-0}) the established authorities. You are a mad man if you think any Roman Catholic can be President of ANY country and perform the functions of his office without being subject to the orders and pressure of the Vatican State .

["Thou shalt not\...do after their works"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 24). ["Their works"]{.class_s4N2-0} were:

1\. [Temple prostitution,]{.class_s4N4-0} where the gathering public committed fornication (for a fee) with the ladies in charge of the worship services (Rev. 2:20--23 and comments in the [Commentary on Revelation]{.class_s4N4-0}). These "deaconesses" (see the [Amplified Version,]{.class_s4N4-0} Rom. 16:1) were called "vestal virgins" in pagan Rome and Greece (see Num. 25:1--6).

2\. [Sodomy,]{.class_s4N4-0} incorporated as part of a program of "reevaluation of spiritual realities" in "ethnic dialogues" with the "meaningful relevant" phases of "communicating understanding" in a "changing environmental culture" where we "rethink the values of blah, blah, blah, etc." Perversion was for the "rough trades" and the "twinks" who came to the worship services. Since all those listed in verse 23 were descended from the first sodomite in the Bible (see Gen. 9:21, 24 in the [Commentary on Genesis]{.class_s4N4-0}), we should not be surprised to find that the Glide Memorial Church (San Francisco) and the ["First Gay Church of Los Angeles"]{.class_s4N4-0} were instituted in the African jungle long before Hammurabai cut his baby teeth.

3\. Jungle music (32:18), used to stir the worshippers up into a state where they could strip off their clothes in "an act of God" and perform a "meaningful dance" etc.

4\. The eventual extremism in "community involvement" as it sought to present an "indepth" worship relative to "ethnic values": that is, [fornication with goats, dogs, horses, and bulls]{.class_s4N4-0} (Lev. 18:23).

The ["images"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 24) were used in these services (Ezek. 16:17). One may sum up the whole "ethnic dialogue" in "cultural exchanges" by saying that it was [devil worship,]{.class_s4N4-0} pure and simple (1 Cor. 10:20; Deut. 32:17); the reason why Africa has never been (or ever will be) a leading world power is because of the emphasis its inhabitants place on these matters (Lev. 18:24--28). That is what all of the texts say in any Bible from 1400 B.C. to A.D. 1900, and the only way you can avoid those truths is to reject the writings of Moses (John 5:45--47) and, thereby, attack the Lord Jesus Christ.

Hislop's [The Two Babylons]{.class_s4N4-0} is a detailed historical analysis of the things mentioned in verse 23 and verse 24 of our text, and no one but a crook or an ignoramus could deny the truth of the matters. Ham has a sex problem, and his descendants show the same tendencies in ANY civilization, on ANY continent, in any age. (The exceptions prove the rule: see Genesis 9--10 in the [Commentary on Genesis]{.class_s4N4-0}.)

Verse 25 has been commented on under 15:26, which see. God's little helpers at Alexandria have added ["Girgashite"]{.class_s4N4-0} to the list of nations in the post-apostolic "[LXX]{.class_s4N4-0}," written more than 200 years after the death of Christ. The reader may ignore these corrections which run riot in the mythological ["Septuagint."]{.class_s4N4-0}

23:26 "There shall nothing cast their young, nor be barren, in thy land: the number of thy days I will fulfil,

27 I will send my fear before thee, and will destroy all the people to whom thou shalt come, and I will make all thine enemies turn their backs unto thee.

28 And I will send hornets before thee, which shall drive out the Hivite, the Canaanite, and the Hittite, from before thee.

29 I will not drive them out from before thee in one year; lest the land become desolate, and the beast of the field multiply against thee.

30 By little and little I will drive them out from before thee, until thou be increased, and inherit the land.

31 And I will set thy bounds from the Red sea even unto the sea of the Philistines, and from the desert unto the river: for I will deliver the inhabitants of the land into your hand; and thou shalt drive them out before thee.

32 Thou shalt make no covenant with them, nor with their gods.

33 They shall not dwell in thy land, lest they make thee sin against me: for if thou serve their gods, it will surely be a snare unto thee."

This is an expansion of verses 24--25, which enforces what we have said under those verses: namely, that there is to be no compromise of any sort with the descendants of Ham who dwell in Canaan. They are to be [driven out ]{.class_s4N4-0}(vs. 31); they are [not ]{.class_s4N4-0}to[ "dwell in thy land" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 33); they are to be [destroyed ]{.class_s4N4-0}(vs. 27); and [no covenants]{.class_s4N4-0} are to be made with them. (The modern meaning of ["covenant" ]{.class_s4N2-0}is "agreement" or "dialogue leading to []{#c2UM.xhtml_page_541}mutual understanding" or "deeper understanding from mutual sharing," etc. One must remember that the modern educated American is always first \[above all things\] a first-class hypocrite, so he never "tells it like it is." If he did, it would cost him his income and his social image, so sin is always glossed, veneered, varnished, masked, cloaked, and perfumed with the terminology of Dewey, Russell, Gentile, Freud, and the holistic and Gestalt psychologists.)

Verse 26 is self-explanatory (see Deut. 5:16; Psa. 55:23). Verse 27 is self-explanatory and should be compared with Joshua 2:9 and Deuteronomy 7:23.

["And I will send hornets before thee"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 28). Most expositors make the ["hornets"]{.class_s4N2-0} a figurative expression. However, in Joshua 24:12, the ["hornets"]{.class_s4N2-0} are distinguished from military weapons. Furthermore, where flying pests are mentioned in Deuteronomy 1:44 and Psalm 118:12, the Holy Spirit is careful to use[ "AS" ]{.class_s4N2-0}and ["LIKE" ]{.class_s4N2-0}to show that the flying stingers are used figuratively. Furthermore, we should notice that ["the hornet"]{.class_s4N2-0} of Deuteronomy 7:20 is ["THE hornet"]{.class_s4N2-0}---not [hornets]{.class_s4N4-0}.

Dummelow messes around with an apocryphal book in trying to explain things and runs back to our old buddy Rameses III (see 1:8 and comments). The [Wycliffe Commentary---]{.class_s4N4-0}on the authority of Keil and Delitzsch---assures us that it is a reference to "the sting of fear" ([whatever that is]{.class_s4N4-0}). The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} insists that the hornets are "plagues" and "troubles." (It would seem that some of the commentators have never been stung by a wasp. It would further appear that some of them have never considered what it would be like to fight hand to hand with clubs, axes, staves, knives, and spears []{#c2UM.xhtml_page_542}while 10,000 to 20,000 "yellow jackets" worked on your bare face, legs, hands, and arms.)

To those commentators who are still skeptical of a literal interpretation, the Holy Spirit has recorded the swarms of 8:24 and 10:14. How did the commentators miss those passages? God can draft and muster an army of 50,000,000 insects any time of the day or night. ["The hornet"]{.class_s4N2-0} is undoubtedly [the hornet,]{.class_s4N4-0} and ["hornets"]{.class_s4N2-0} are probably [hornets ]{.class_s4N4-0}(that is, according to the Hebrew text).

Observe how the Holy Spirit has only written THREE Hamitic tribes in verse 28. This is important, for the mythological [LXX ]{.class_s4N4-0}has assumed in verse 23 that the Holy Spirit has made a mistake in listing SIX. Therefore, Alexandria added one to match the seven of Deuteronomy 7:1. Now what should be done here (vs. 28) to help God out? Well, by all rights, [four tribes]{.class_s4N4-0} should have been added! But lo and behold, when we pick up the corrupt [Septuagint ]{.class_s4N4-0}manuscripts upon which the [NASV]{.class_s4N4-0} is based, we find only [one tribe]{.class_s4N4-0} added to the list---the "[Amorites]{.class_s4N4-0}." This time, [all of the commentaries cover up]{.class_s4N4-0} for the corrupt [Septuagint ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [do not even mention the addition. ]{.class_s4N4-0}This is the old traditional conspiracy passed down from one "unusual university" to another, based on the doctrine of the infallibility of the "Scholars' Union." This tradition protects the dead-orthodox apostates in the Origen-English line from detection, and it is based on the perennial dictum that ["the MAJORITY of Conservative scholars are able to correct the Bible at will"]{.class_s4N4-0} (see the notes on the mythological "[LXX]{.class_s4N4-0}" under 20:23). The real Bible believer may disregard the entire line through nineteen centuries of destructive criticism of the [Received Text.]{.class_s4N4-0}

Verses 29--30 are self-explanatory and should be compared with Deuteronomy 7:22 and 2 Kings 17:25. Judges 2:21--22 give a third reason why the complete destruction of the Hamites was not accomplished in a few months. The reader should also note that ["the river"]{.class_s4N2-0} of verse 31 is the Euphrates River of Genesis 15:18. We have around 2,000,000 people about to enter the land, but the land grant could "eat up" that many people. Two million people would be a small population in a state the size of New York State, but this land grant is 600 miles long and 200 miles wide (see Ezek. 40--48). The spiritual lesson in all of this is apparent. The Christian must be exercised by fighting; a victory that is won too easily and too quickly will make him underestimate the power of the adversary (Eph. 6:10--13).

["Inherit the land"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 30). This verse ties up the doctrine of the literal, visible, political advent of Jesus Christ, for wherever the "inheritance" is found, [thereafter]{.class_s4N4-0} (except in 1 Pet. 1:4) there is a reference to the land of Palestine or the manifestation of the Kingdom of God. Every time the word [inherit]{.class_s4N4-0} or [inheritance]{.class_s4N4-0} appears in a Pauline epistle, it is connected with an earned reward (Col. 3:24), except in Ephesians 1:18; and even then, it is connected with the [completed salvation]{.class_s4N4-0} of the Christian (Eph. 1:14), where his new body has been received (see comments under Gen. 12:1--3, 13:15--16 in the [Commentary on Genesis]{.class_s4N4-0}).

We have commented on verses 31--33 under verses 24--25.

["Lest they make thee sin against me: for if thou serve their gods, it will surely be a snare unto thee"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 33 cf. Josh. 23:13; Judg. 2:3). The prophecy is infallible (as all Bible prophecies), and it is fulfilled []{#c2UM.xhtml_page_544}to the letter in 2 Kings chapter 17, where we read the descending order of degeneration which is generally applicable to any Western nation at any period of history:

1\. [They ]{.class_s4N4-0}["feared other gods"]{.class_s4NC} (vs. 7): they feared Communists, Catholics, loss of money, educators, philosophers, freedom of expression, the federal government, etc., etc.

2\. [They ]{.class_s4N4-0}["walked in the statutes of the heathen" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 8): African clothes, African music, African morals, French cosmetics, Italian foods, the New Morality, Indian religions, etc.

3\. [They ]{.class_s4N4-0}["did secretly those things that were not right" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 9): discotheques, night clubs, "sensitivity experiments," "love-ins," "make-outs," TV programs, seances, drive-ins, group therapy, theaters, etc., etc.

4\. [They set up images on high places]{.class_s4N4-0} (vss 9--10): Washington monument, Bunker Hill, Gettysburg, St. Peter's, St. Mary's, St. Francis, Smith--sonian Institute, etc.

5\. [They ]{.class_s4N4-0}["wrought wicked things" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 11): pornography, freedom of sex, premarital intercourse, SIECUS, bestiality, black magic, white magic, sex deviation, "gay" liberation, sex perversion, ritual murders, Manson, etc.

6\. ["They served idols"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 12): ouija boards, tarot cards, palmistry, tea leaves, necromancy, phrenology, astrological readings, science, education, sex, commerce, money, sociology, etc.

7\. They wound up as an integrated Phoenician-African-Baal-worshipping population [worse than the Hamites]{.class_s4N4-0} who were ejected before them (vss 13--20).

8\. Israel was witnessed to by the Lord (vs. 13), and they rejected His Book (vs. 15); so ["the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord ]{.class_s4N9-0}[]{#c2UM.xhtml_page_545 .class-1}[removed Israel out of his sight, as he had said by ALL HIS SERVANTS the prophets"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 23). Not one major commentator on the market, from 1600 to 1980, made any application of the text we have just read. (The peculiar scholastic exclusiveness which isolates most Conservative scholars from the main streams of time and history \[Prov. 18:1--2\] prevents them from ever accomplishing anything of an effective nature when it comes to the teaching or preaching of the word of God. Where there is no "understanding of the reading" \[Neh. 8:8\], there is no conviction, obedience, or response; the majority of Conservative scholars who are "contending for the faith" have been duped by Satan into believing that ["understanding the reading"]{.class_s4N4-0} means "going to the original languages." But the worst of the matter is, having done this, they then think that ["expositing the truth"]{.class_s4N4-0} is kidding a student into thinking that [Vaticanus ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [Sinaiticus ]{.class_s4N4-0}are the ["originals"]{.class_s4N4-0}.)

[]{#c2YF.xhtml}

# Chapter 24 {.heading_s4G-1}

24:1 "And he said unto Moses, Come up unto the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}, thou, and Aaron, Nadab, and Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel; and worship ye afar off.

2 And Moses alone shall come near the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}: but they shall not come nigh; neither shall the people go up with him."

Five men are connected with the events that now take place. The two sons of Aaron (Nadab and Abihu) and Aaron go up with Moses the first time, and Joshua goes up with him the second time (vs. 13). The "seventy elders" (vs. 1) are from the elders of 4:29, and they are not allowed to go clear up to the top of the mount with Moses. They did ["eat and drink"]{.class_s4N2-0} with Moses and Aaron and Nadab and Abihu (vs. 11), but this eating place is obviously further down the slopes of Sinai than the place where verse 18 is fulfilled.

24:3 "And Moses came and told the people all the words of the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}, and all the judgments: and all the people answered with one voice, and said, All the words which the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}hath said will we do.

4 And Moses wrote all the words of the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}, and rose up early in the morning, and builded an altar under the hill, and twelve pillars, according to the twelve tribes of Israel.

5 And he sent young men of the children of Israel, which offered burnt offerings, and sacrificed peace offerings of oxen unto the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}.

6 And Moses took half of the blood, and put it in basins; and half of the blood he sprinkled on the altar.

7 And he took the book of the covenant, and read in the audience of the people: and they said, All that the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}hath said will we do, and be obedient.

8 And Moses took the blood, and sprinkled it on the people, and said, Behold the blood of the covenant, which the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}hath made with you concerning all these words."

Verse 3 takes us back to 23:33 showing again that the chronological order of the Bible is sometimes "non-chronological." ["All the words which the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord ]{.class_s4N9-0}[hath said will we do"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 3). (See the comments under 19:8 and the [Lord's]{.class_s4N4-0} response in Deut. 5:28--29.) The response shows willingness, but alas, "the spirit is willing" while ["the flesh is weak"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Matt. 26:41).

["The words"]{.class_s4N2-0} are in chapter 20, and they were written in a book. ["The]{.class_s4N2-0} [judgments"]{.class_s4N2-0} were in chapters 21--23, and they were written in a book (vs. 7). This book was sprinkled with blood according to Hebrews 9:19, and this explains why all of the old [King James]{.class_s4N4-0} Bibles had either [gold ]{.class_s4N4-0}or [red ]{.class_s4N4-0}edges to the pages. You see, the Holy Bible is a [bloody ]{.class_s4N4-0}book, and it is the book of [Deity]{.class_s4N4-0}. (You will not find that information furnished by any reviser of any version since 1800, although these men will sometimes gild the edges of their apostate corruptions to pass them off as "Bibles.")

Notice that the ["law, and commandments"]{.class_s4N2-0} of verse 12 are not the ["words"]{.class_s4N2-0} and ["judgments" ]{.class_s4N2-0}of verse 3. Further, the word "is settled forever in Heaven" (see Psa. 119:89), for verse 12 says, ["which I have written."]{.class_s4N2-0}

["And builded an altar\...and twelve pillars"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 4; see comments under 18:12 and 19:3). The ritual preparation, then, for the giving of the Law has almost []{#c2YF.xhtml_page_548}the same elements in it that are found in the New Testament. The only exception is that here God is making a covenant with a NATION---["the twelve tribes of Israel"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 4); in the New Testament God is confirming a covenant with an [individual believer. ]{.class_s4N4-0}However, note the elements:

A. AN ALTAR (vs. 4). [The altar]{.class_s4N4-0} (Heb. 13:10--12) represents the sacrificial work of Jesus Christ (our blood atonement) who died in our place to reconcile us to God (Heb. 10:8--12).

B. A BLOOD-SPRINKLED BOOK (vss. 7--8). [The book]{.class_s4N4-0} (1 Thess. 2:13) is a signed contract (John 17:17), sworn under oath (Heb. 7:20--21) by the Author of the transaction, that the covenant will be kept (John 5:24).

C. THE TWELVE PILLARS (vs. 4) represent a nation which stands as a "firstborn son" (4:22). In the Christian's case (Rom. 8:14--18), [his body is the temple]{.class_s4N4-0} and the "pillars of the church" (Gal. 2:9) are living Christians!

Ah, the boundless riches of the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}text. How superior is its revelation to "recent scientific discoveries," modern critical editions, and "the great uncial codices."

[Three times]{.class_s4N4-0} the children of Israel (as a nation) subscribe to the conditions of the covenant (vss. 3, 7, and 19:8). The sprinkled blood (vss. 6, 8) is mentioned frequently in the book of Hebrews (Heb. 8--10); it accounts for the non-scriptural practice of Catholics, Methodists, Lutherans, and Episcopalians in substituting water [sprinkling ]{.class_s4N4-0}for immersion in water when performing the ordinance of baptism. These groups, which maintain the traditions of Irenaeus, Cyprian, Justin Martyr, and other Conservatives, have []{#c2YF.xhtml_page_549}confounded PURIFICATION (John 2:6) with death, burial and [resurrection ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Rom. 6:3--5, Col. 2:12--13). They are trying to regenerate their babies by throwing water at them, as a type of Christ's blood (Isa. 52:15), not realizing that the Old Testament purifying rites (Heb. 9:18, 23) could not ["take away sins"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Heb. 10:4) even when they were performed [daily ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Heb. 10:11). Sins are not "taken away" unless Christ [dies ]{.class_s4N4-0}for them with sin ON Him. He is [buried ]{.class_s4N4-0}with the sins ON Him (Heb. 9:28), and He takes them down into the heart of the earth (Matt. 12:40) and leaves them there (Heb. 9:28). When He [comes up]{.class_s4N4-0} (Acts 2:27, 31), He comes up sinless and bearing no sins on Him. Water baptism, therefore, has to be a [burial ]{.class_s4N4-0}by immersion. This is too heavy a diet of protein and calories for 90% of the "Scholars' Union," so it is not surprising that nearly 70% of professing Christendom is counting on baby sprinkling to ["take away" ]{.class_s4N2-0}their sins.

We should note, further, that it is the blood of an OX that is sprinkled on the book and the people, [not the blood of the lamb. ]{.class_s4N4-0}We have here the [application ]{.class_s4N4-0}of blood to a living nation and a living book, and not merely the judicial recognition of blood as a ransom for a nation (see 12:1--6). A careful reader will discern that in the Passover, the "blood of the LAMB" is never put on any person in the Old Testament. The blood of [calves ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Lev. 9), [bullocks ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Lev. 4), rams (Lev. 8), and even other lambs (see Lev. 14:13--14, 25, 4:32--35) is, but never "the lamb." The blood of the Passover lamb does not contact human flesh at all, for it is one with the Holy Spirit (1 John 5:6) as sinless blood (Acts 20:28). It can only be applied to a soul (John 3:3, 5) that has been cut loose from ["the body of the sins of the flesh" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(see extended comments []{#c2YF.xhtml_page_550}under Col. 2:11--12 in the [Commentary on Galatians---Col]{.class_s4N4-0}ossians)

24:9 "Then went up Moses, and Aaron, Nadab, and Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel:

10 And they saw God of Israel: and there was under his feet as it were a paved work of a sapphire stone, and as it were the body of heaven in his clearness.

11 And upon the nobles of the children of Israel he laid not his hand: also they saw God, and did eat and drink."

The passage affords the serious student of the Scripture with another opportunity to study the intentional mistakes of the first Fundamentalist Christian school at Alexandria, which followed the apostate leadership of Philo, Pantaenus, Clement, Origen, and other Bible-rejecting "Christians."

Here, the mythological [LXX ]{.class_s4N4-0}reveals its true nature again in no uncertain manner, as it dumps another bucket of tar on the living words of the living God. Unable to resist the old ["gnostic- asceticaeons"]{.class_s4N4-0} temptation (see the writings of Valentinus, Marcion, Philo, et al.), the corrupt [Septuagint ]{.class_s4N4-0}gives us ["they saw THE PLACE where God stood,"]{.class_s4N4-0} for ["And they saw the God of Israel"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 10). (See the same thing in Isa. 6:5; Judg. 13:21--22.)

Bible believers who are acquainted with the facts of church history know the monkey paws when they see them. This is that old gnostic bit about "God is ONLY a spirit and has no bodily shape," "everything [physical ]{.class_s4N4-0}is [evil; ]{.class_s4N4-0}therefore, Christ was not God incarnate in the FLESH," etc. (see [Believing Bible Study,]{.class_s4N4-0} Edward []{#c2YF.xhtml_page_551}Hills, 1969). The Gnostic faculty members at Alexandria, who believed that Christian education was the supreme authority in all matters of faith and practice instead of the Holy Bible, are back at their old tricks again: helping God out by correcting His Scriptures so they will not contradict (see comments under Gen. 46 in the[ Commentary on Gen]{.class_s4N4-0}esis)!

The Conservative commentator Adam Clarke is very sympathetic with this view, for he, as the depraved faculty at Alexandria, could not reconcile Deuteronomy 4:15 and John 1:18 with the passage. He therefore tells us an amazing thing. Clarke says that the "feet'' of verse 10 were not really[ "feet,"]{.class_s4N4-0} but only "the PLACE where His feet stood." [This is the Gnostic position exactly. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Keil and Delitzsch take the [agnostic ]{.class_s4N4-0}position on the passage, and their comments are cited in the [Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} (pg. 75). Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown also stumble at the verse in spite of the fact that the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} warns them that the word ["people"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Deut. 4:10 cf. 4:15) and ["elders"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 9--10) are not the same words. But again, the [King James ]{.class_s4N4-0}Bible of 1611 has all the answers which the "brethren" cannot find, so we have no trouble at all with the passage as long as we approach the Bible as believers instead of "critics."

Jacob SAW God (Gen. 32:30), Manoah SAW God (Judg. 13:22), Gideon SAW God (Judg. 6:22), and the "Angel of His presence" just mentioned (23:23) was clearly identified in BOTH Testaments (Gal. 4:14). [What was the problem?]{.class_s4N4-0}

When the Lord "appeared unto Abraham" (Acts 7:2) and Moses (Acts 7:30), He appeared as an Angel (Acts 7:38). [What is the problem?]{.class_s4N4-0}

The statement in John 1:18 is in regards to God the Father (the Soul of God, not His Body or Spirit). The fact that the [New American Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} has used the Jehovah's Witness translation for this verse (from the Roman Catholic [Vaticanus]{.class_s4N4-0}) means nothing except that the Lockman Foundation ([NASV]{.class_s4N4-0}) is as [Arian ]{.class_s4N4-0}as Arius (A.D. 325), who was blinded by ["the god of this world" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(2 Cor. 4:4)

It is the [Angel of the Lord]{.class_s4N4-0} whom the elders see, and it is He with whom they have communion and fellowship (vs. 10). He does not kill them since He did not kill Manoah or his wife (Judg. 13), and this is perfectly clear in either context. Therefore, the alteration made by the mythological [LXX]{.class_s4N4-0}---found in the Greek texts used as authoritative at Bob Jones University---is a nullity. And the alteration of John 1:18 in the [NASV]{.class_s4N4-0} is another nullity. No "devout" Christian or "godly Conservative" would condone [either reading]{.class_s4N4-0} in either Old or New Testament texts. You have to idolize "scholarship" to regard such rot.

Verse 10 should be compared with Isaiah 6:1 and Ezekiel 1:26. The [sapphire ]{.class_s4N4-0}may be transparent or clear blue. Dummelow's note on the passage (Commentary, pg. 73) is nonsense. Clarke tells us that some sapphires are GREEN, and this would match the passage on the emerald in Revelation 4:3.

24:12 "And the [Lord ]{.class_s4N9-1}said unto Moses, Come up to me into the mount, and be there: and I will give thee tables of stone, and a law, and commandments which I have written; that thou mayest teach them.

13 And Moses rose up, and his minister Joshua: and Moses went up into the mount of God.

14 And he said unto the elders, Tarry ye here for us, until we come again unto you: and, behold, Aaron and Hur are with you: if any man have any matters to do, let him come unto them.

15 And Moses went up into the mount, and a cloud covered the mount.

16 And the glory of the [Lord ]{.class_s4N9-1}abode upon mount Sinai, and the cloud covered it six days: and the seventh day he called unto Moses out of the midst of the cloud.

17 And the sight of the glory of the [Lord ]{.class_s4N9-1}was like devouring fire on the top of the mount in the eyes of the children of Israel.

18 And Moses went into the midst of the cloud, and gat him up into the mount: and Moses was in the mount forty days and forty nights."

All is self-explanatory. Joshua goes half way up with Moses and Aaron and Hur while the elders go back down, which is apparent by chapters 32 and 33. This is the second mention of Joshua, who now appears as a MINISTER; whereas his first appearance (see 17:13) was as a [soldier.]{.class_s4N4-0}

Students acquainted with the ["Seven System"]{.class_s4N4-0} (see 19:16 and notes) see the Advent again clearly pictured in verses 16 and 17.

We have commented on ["which I have written"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 12) under verses 3 and 4. Dummelow is unable to believe what he has read and insists that ["which I have written"]{.class_s4N2-0} should follow the expression ["tables of stone"]{.class_s4N2-0} because Dummelow (as other dead-[]{#c2YF.xhtml_page_554}orthodox apostates) cannot believe that the ["law, and commandments"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 12) were pre-written. The seventy elders return to the camp with Aaron and Hur, and Moses goes up to disappear for forty days and nights. At this time, he is without food (Deut. 9:9), and his ascension matches the incidents connected with Jesus Christ (Luke 4:1--3) and Elijah (1 Kings 19:3). (See full treatment in the [Commentary on Revelation,]{.class_s4N4-0} Rev. 11:1--5, 1970.)

The reader should observe, again, the peculiar junction of "7 and 40" in regards to the Second Advent. All the elements of the Advent are there: the re-establishment of Israel, the re-establishment of the [Law]{.class_s4N4-0}, the [cloud,]{.class_s4N4-0} ["the glory of the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord]{.class_s4NS}[,"]{.class_s4N2-0} the two witnesses (in this case Joshua for [Elijah]{.class_s4N4-0}), the seven-day period, and the ["devouring fire"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 17, cf. 2 Thess. 1:8). But "7" and "40" crop up in the wildest places (Num. 13:22, 25; 2 Kings 8:2, 9, etc.). This phenomenon has not been noticed by Olson, Pember, Keil, Delitzsch, Scofield, Hengstenberg, Larkin, Bullinger, Cheyne, Davidson, Payne, Kuenen, Hitzig, Thenius, Wellhausen, Kurtz, Zockler, Kalisch, Kennicott, Cimchi, Weniger, Knobel, Reinke, Rashi, Gleason, Shrader, Havernick, Schultz, Schlottman, Bleek, Frankenberg, Geiger, Bertheau, Bahr, or Dillmann (and the other 300); consequently, exposition awaits the Bible-believing mind which will dig into the problem. The "7" and "40" are clearly connected with a period immediately preceding or immediately [following ]{.class_s4N4-0}the Tribulation, but more cannot be said, for no more than that has been discovered.

Those destructive Bible critics of the Twentieth Century (many of the faculty members at Bob Jones, Tennessee Temple, Moody, Fuller, Dallas, []{#c2YF.xhtml_page_555}Columbia, Hyles-Anderson, Pensacola Christian, Trinity, Lynchburg, Clearwater, etc.) will furnish no Christian with any light on the matter, for they refer to this type of Bible study as "hobbyhorse riding." (They do this[ while they devote ten to fifty years of their ministry trying to replace the Authorized text of the Reformation with the corrupt Greek text of Nestle!]{.class_s4N4-0})

In closing, the reader should notice some great spiritual truths.

1\. There is no fellowship with God (vs. 11) till [the blood]{.class_s4N4-0} has been applied (vs. 7).

2\. There is no revelation of God (vs. 10) until God has [revealed Himself]{.class_s4N4-0} in a book (vs. 4, 7).

3\. Fellowship with God implies obedience to God's laws (vs. 12).

4\. Moses may give the Law, but Jesus ([Joshua]{.class_s4N4-0}) has to "minister it" (vs. 13)!

5\. The meal with God was by His grace. God supplied the food; no one brought their "picnic lunch" up with them.

6\. While Israel stood at the foot of the mount ([first class]{.class_s4N4-0} of Christians---["called"]{.class_s4N2-0} and under the blood, Rev. 12:11 and 17:14), Joshua and the elders were half-way up ([second class ]{.class_s4N4-0}of Christians[---"chosen"]{.class_s4N2-0} and testifying, Rev. 12:11 and 17:14). Moses was at the top of the mount ([third class ]{.class_s4N4-0}of Christians[---"faithful"]{.class_s4N2-0} and faithful to death, Rev. 12:11 and 17:14). The three classes indicate the outer court, the Holy Place, and the Holy of Holies in the Tabernacle.

[]{#c307.xhtml}

# Chapter 25 {.heading_s4G-1}

25:1 "And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

2 Speak unto the children of Israel, that they bring me an offering: of every man that giveth it willingly with his heart ye shall take my offering.

3 And this is the offering which ye shall take of them; gold, and silver, and brass,

4 And blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine linen, and goats' hair,

5 And rams' skins dyed red, and badgers' skins, and shittim wood,

6 Oil for the light, spices for anointing oil, and for sweet incense,

7 Onyx stones, and stones to be set in the ephod, and in the breastplate.

8 And let them make me a sanctuary; that I may dwell among them.

9 According to all that I shew thee, after the pattern of the tabernacle, and the pattern of all the instruments thereof, even so shall ye make it."

Here follows a detailed description of worship for the nation of Israel. A [Tabernacle ]{.class_s4N4-0}is diagrammed, [material ]{.class_s4N4-0}for it is described, the [ministers ]{.class_s4N4-0}who enter it are noted, [their garments]{.class_s4N4-0} are mentioned, and the [furniture ]{.class_s4N4-0}and the [utensils ]{.class_s4N4-0}found in the Tabernacle are described, with the [consecration of the ministers]{.class_s4N4-0} laid out in a ritual form.

There have been reams written about the spiritual significance of all these things, and great detailed treatises have been published regarding the "typology" of the Tabernacle. Among these treatises---which number several hundred---there is not ONE which assumes that the writer of Hebrews knew what he was talking about. []{#c307.xhtml_page_557 .class-1}[The writer of Hebrews reminds us over and over again that the Tabernacle was made according to the pattern ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Heb. 8:1)[ of the things in the universe ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Heb. 9:24).

Now all of this has been ignored in the sincere desire of the "godly Conservative" writer to "find Christ" in the Tabernacle, and though it is perfectly true that we can "find Christ" in the various pieces of furniture and in the functions of the Aaronic priesthood, it is also true that the Tabernacle ITSELF cannot be [a figure of Christ]{.class_s4N4-0} unless one believes in [the eternity of matter]{.class_s4N4-0} or identifies the universe [as a type of God]{.class_s4N4-0} (Heb. 1:11), or at least His "dwelling place" (Heb. 1:11--12). The Tabernacle, therefore, is primarily a picture of the [first, second, and third heavens]{.class_s4N4-0} (Heb. 8--10), and the data missing in Genesis chapter 1 and Job chapter 38 will be found in Exodus chapters 25--30 and Exodus chapters 35--39.

Now, we shall walk humbly and circumspectly on this ground for two reasons:

1\. The author is not a [physicist,]{.class_s4N4-0} nor does he pretend to be. It would take a [Bible-believing]{.class_s4N4-0} mathematical genius to work out the material found in chapters 25--30 in the proper fashion. The author is a Bible believer, but (to quote Billy Sunday) he knows no more about mathematics "than a jack rabbit knows about ping pong."

2\. It would take a solid five to [ten years work]{.class_s4N4-0} in Exodus ALONE to work out the doctrinal content which Hebrews chapters 8 and 9 indicates, and the author does not have that time, even without a book ministry, tape ministry, Bible Institute, pastorate, and evangelistic meetings.

The work, therefore, awaits the time, talent, and energy of a Bible believer whom God can trust with Proverbs 22:17--21; this will take a New Testament []{#c307.xhtml_page_558}"Samuel" who will ["let none of his ]{.class_s4N2-0}\[i.e., God's\][ WORDS fall to the ground"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 Sam. 3:19). We cannot look to any major Conservative Hebrew scholar alive on the earth today for this information, for every last one of them is a Bible-rejecting critic who delights in altering the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}text ten times per page and, many times, [fifty times a page. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Such men can produce nothing but rehashes of previous generations. They have no "new light," for they have turned out the only light God gave them. The reader may abandon all hope (immediately) of receiving the proper information from any Greek or Hebrew scholar on any faculty of any "Fundamental" Bible College or University in Europe or America. These men make their living by CORRECTING the Bible, and God will show them absolutely nothing: N-O-T-H-I-N-G, as in NOTHING!

["Speak unto the children of Israel"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 2). The offering is to be a [freewill offering]{.class_s4N4-0} (Ezra 3:5, 7:16) given ["willingly"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 2). There is no law for it, so all is done under grace (vs. 2), from the heart (vs. 2).

All of the scholars have trouble with the ["brass"]{.class_s4N2-0} of verse 3. It would seem that the "ancients" did not know how to make brass since brass is a combination (i.e., an alloy) of copper and zinc. We are told that the word was actually [copper, ]{.class_s4N4-0}but those who have checked the Holy Spirit's use of the word in Judges 16:21 and 2 Kings 25:7 know that copper is hardly the right material out of which to make manacles and handcuffs, It is certainly not very good material for PILLARS to hold up a roof.

The substitution [bronze ]{.class_s4N4-0}is better, but since the word [bronze]{.class_s4N4-0} comes from "brazen" and "brassy" and ["brass,"]{.class_s4N4-0} and modern ["brass"]{.class_s4N2-0} instruments (1 Cor. 13:1) are not COPPER, what is the point? ["Sounding brass"]{.class_s4N2-0} never []{#c307.xhtml_page_559}has been "sounding [copper,"]{.class_s4N4-0} before or after Noah, so what is the point? The astounding Dr. Johnson ([Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0}) has confounded the time of the "Iron Age" (pg. 74) and would have you think that iron was a later improvement on brass. But this is quite typical of the dead-orthodox apostate who goes to archaeology for his information instead of the Holy Spirit. Observe that IRON was in use [before ]{.class_s4N4-0}the Flood (Gen. 4:22), and the Flood occurred [more than 800 years before the passage you are now reading. ]{.class_s4N4-0}The giant of Deuteronomy 3:11 had ["a bedstead of iron,"]{.class_s4N2-0} and his civilization was flourishing in the days of Lot's grandchildren. The Canaanites had ["]{.class_s4N4-0}[chariots of iron"]{.class_s4NC} (Josh. 17:16) before the Tabernacle was erected. The note, therefore, in the [Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} is to be classified with Dummelow's note on 24:10.

(The Lord did not want any IRON in His "works," in His ark \[Gen. 6:14\], or in His Tabernacle. The blacksmith of Genesis 4:22 \[see comments under Gen. 4 in the [Commentary on Genesis]{.class_s4N4-0}\] is too close to Goliath's spear \[1 Sam. 17:7\] and behemoth's bones \[Job 40:18\] for a Bible believer to fool with. The iron men of Daniel 2:43 cannot rust till they get blood in them; [tell that to the next psychiatrist you see and watch his eyes.]{.class_s4N4-0}\])

[Gold:]{.class_s4N4-0} for the crowns, rings, cherubim, mercy seat, spoons, dishes, snuffers, candlesticks, bowls, hooks, taches, tongs, pillars, and staves.

[Silver:]{.class_s4N4-0} for sockets, vessels, utensils, covering for the altar, and the pins of the court.

(Real brass---plainly NOT "copper"---is mentioned in Job 28:2, and the reference in Gen. 4:22 is probably the same thing. The superstition that the ancients did not know how to make brass is quite similar to "camels and []{#c307.xhtml_page_560}grapes not being in Egypt," etc.: same song, different key.)

The typology of the three metals is fairly well fixed. The gold would represent [Deity]{.class_s4N4-0}, since it stands for the highest values on this earth; the silver is plainly [the price of redemption]{.class_s4N4-0} (1 Pet. 1:18; Zech. 11:13); and the brass is connected with[ the judgment of sin]{.class_s4N4-0} (Rev. 6) on the brazen altar.

["And blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine linen, and goats' hair"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 4). Three animals are listed with three colors. The animals are the [goat]{.class_s4N4-0}, the [ram]{.class_s4N4-0}, and the [badger ]{.class_s4N4-0}(vss. 4--5); and the colors are blue, purple, and scarlet. The blue and the scarlet mixed produce [purple]{.class_s4N4-0}. The blue is plainly the color of heaven (see Num. 15:38), and the scarlet is plainly a reference to the blood of Jesus Christ, in typology. (This is manifest by the reference to the "red worm" in the Hebrew word---see Psa. 22:6 cf. John 3:14. None of the major commentators get the full significance of those verses, but again, we are dealing with Bible critics \[not commentators\], and we do not expect any "nuggets" from their mines. They live in the ghost town of "once upon a time God wrote a book which [was]{.class_s4N4-0} accurate.")

Purple is the color of [royalty ]{.class_s4N4-0}wherever it occurs; it came originally from a shellfish ([murex]{.class_s4N4-0}). It was so highly valued that most Oriental monarchs would not allow any of their subjects to wear it except by special permission. Blue, purple, and scarlet are pictures of a God who dwells in Heaven, who reigns as supreme sovereign over heaven and earth (Dan 4:3,34), and who has BLOOD (see Acts 20:28 in the [Authorized text]{.class_s4N4-0}).

["And fine linen, and goats' hair,"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 4). ["Fine linen"]{.class_s4NC} is defined in Revelation 19:8. The ["]{.class_s4N4-0}[goats' hair"]{.class_s4NC}[ ]{.class_s4N2-0}[]{#c307.xhtml_page_561 .class-1}was often as fine as silk, and it was used in weaving garments (Clarke, pg. 428). In typology, we find the goat as a sin offering (Lev. 9:15), a sin bearer (Lev. 16:9--10), and a type of the Antichrist (Dan. 8:5--12). The [goat ]{.class_s4N4-0}is the universal symbol for Satan, and it is found in most fertility rites and modern sex rites carried on by high school and College students, under the direction of a "warlock" or "witch" (1950--2010). "Goat glands" for revitalized sex life were part of Dr. Brinkley's fabulous ministry in Kansas and New Mexico in the 1920s and 30s.

["And rams' skins dyed red, and badgers' skins"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 5). The ram represents Christ as a sacrifice (Gen. 22:13) connected with the ministry of a priest (chap. 29); red leather is obtained from the ram in the verse mentioned. The ram is connected with the burnt offering, the peace offering, the trespass offering, and the atonement in the books of Leviticus and Numbers.

Now the horrible ["badger" ]{.class_s4N4-0}appears, and he badgers the whole line of Conservative scholarship right out of their inheritance (Heb. 12:17) and into the arms of Graf, Wellhausen, Boyd, Astruc, Renan, Ewald, Strauss, Rubin, DeWette, and Pharaoh. We are told by most of the major commentators that "badgers" were unknown on the Sinaitic Peninsula, which is proved by the fact that there were none there [in the Eighteenth and Nineteenth Centuries A.D. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Once more we are face to face with the old Angel of Light (Gen. 3:1), and he is still giving us the same old "PROOFS": i.e., "it is generally agreed among\...etc." (It kind of reminds one of the quorum of ["presidents and princes"]{.class_s4N2-0} in Dan. 6:6--7, doesn't it? "The majority of Conservative scholars say, 'blah, blah, blah, and blah'?")

Strangely enough, the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} admits that there are badgers in Palestine! Furthermore, Bullinger tells us that the feet of people of Palestine in his day were shod with badgers' skin (see Ezek. 16:10), and the word for [badger ]{.class_s4N4-0}in both places (here and in Ezekiel) is the same word. (You see, in order to understand the Bible, one of the first prime essentials is to understand that every man who corrected the [King James ]{.class_s4N4-0}Bible[ ]{.class_s4N4-0}did it on the authority of [some other man]{.class_s4N4-0} who did it on the authority of [some other man]{.class_s4N4-0} who did it on the authority of [some other man, ]{.class_s4N4-0}etc. God never entered the transaction once. These men are simply aping each other through three centuries of destructive criticism while passing off as "Bible believers" because they profess to believe in some "original" that no one ever found.)

The corrupt [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}of 1901, recommended by Dallas Theological Seminary from 1940--1970, says ["seal skins"]{.class_s4N4-0} and then recommends ["porpoise skins"]{.class_s4N4-0} in the margin. Why not ["alligator skins"]{.class_s4N4-0}? How about ["catfish skins"]{.class_s4N4-0}? How were the porpoises caught, Doctor? Did you ever try to catch a porpoise without a [boat?]{.class_s4N4-0} WHERE WERE THE BOATS? I thought you said they crossed the "Reed Sea" (see remarks under chaps. 14--15)? Are there [inland ]{.class_s4N4-0}porpoises landlocked in the [marshy ground]{.class_s4N4-0} of Goshen? Seal skins?

How do you know 10,000,000 badgers couldn't have bred on the Peninsula, with [three trade routes going through it]{.class_s4N4-0} and people traveling from Egypt to Palestine regularly? How do you know Jacob and his family didn't bring some with them in Genesis chapter 46?

You see, infidelity is always irrational, and it is always subjective in its approach to Biblical truth. If Jacob and his family had introduced badgers into Egypt, []{#c307.xhtml_page_563}they would have had 215--400 years to multiply. Did you ever try [rabbits]{.class_s4N4-0} for that long a time? Didn't one of those old dead-orthodox apostates say that the ["hare"]{.class_s4N2-0} of Leviticus 11:6 was a "rock [badger]{.class_s4N4-0}"? It seems to me I remember something of this kind of talk when Harry Rimmer was sued by a plaintiff on "contradictions in the Bible." (James Bennett of New York was representing the defendant at that time, and the plaintiff's case was thrown out of court.)

Shall we do the same with the ["porpoise"]{.class_s4N4-0} and the ["seal"?]{.class_s4N4-0} Let's. The "badger" is a [badger]{.class_s4N4-0}. The alteration made on the text by the [New American Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} (I am taking the liberty of correcting it sight unseen, as we know how apostates operate in any "revision") means nothing: as in NOTHING.

["And shittim wood"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 5). The wood is hard, insect proof, and durable. It is found everywhere on the Sinaitic Peninsula. It would represent the humanity of Christ, in typology, since men are plainly likened to trees (Ezek. 31; Psa. 1:3; Mark 8:24).

["Oil for the light, spices for anointing oil, and for sweet incense"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 6). The oil is [olive oil]{.class_s4N4-0} (27:20) which was brought from Egypt. Chapter 30 gives the prescription for the anointing oil and the incense (see notes).

["Onyx stones, and stones\...the ephod\...the breastplate" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 7). The onyx stone is black and white; most of the commentators throw it out for the beryl, the emerald, the "Egyptian pebble," and God only knows what all. Anything but believe the text! Josephus and Aquila say "the sardonyx," [so we know that isn't right!]{.class_s4N4-0} (Leave it as it is, and you'll probably make out better at the Judgment Seat of Christ anyway). The other "stones" []{#c307.xhtml_page_564}are all listed in 28:17--20. They represent the New Jerusalem (see Rev. 21--22) and are part of the ["glory"]{.class_s4N2-0} which God gave Christ ["before the foundation of the world"]{.class_s4N2-0} (John 17:24); hence, the high priest wears them on his breast over his heart (28:21--28).

["That I may dwell among them" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 8). The Tabernacle foreshadows two things even greater than Solomon's Temple (Acts 7:49), for it prophesies of Revelation 21:22 where there will be no more need for "making a sanctuary." It also warns the reader that the Holy Ghost CANNOT dwell (John 14:16) in a body of flesh (Col. 2:11) unless the soul inside that body of flesh has been cut loose from the flesh itself (Col. 2:12--13). The [souls ]{.class_s4N4-0}that are stuck to the flesh in the Old Testament (see Gen. 2:7; Ezek. 18:4; 1 Sam. 26:21) are defiled [when the flesh is defiled]{.class_s4N4-0} (Lev. 22:6), for they are [joined to the flesh]{.class_s4N4-0} as man and wife are joined together (Rom. 7:1--4). (But of course, all of this is obscure to the Conservative Greek and Hebrew scholars of the Twentieth Century.) God will not DWELL in an unclean place (see 33:3, and comments), so when the camp is defiled, the Tabernacle is moved outside the camp (33:7--8), exactly as JESUS CHRIST removes Himself from Jerusalem (Matt. 24:1) in the New Testament.

From this passage and the Hebrew word "[mishkan]{.class_s4N4-0}" (vs. 9), the Jewish Rabbis invented a word called "[Shekinah,]{.class_s4N4-0}" which appears quite frequently in the literature of the dead-orthodox Conservatives. No such word, however, appears anywhere in either Testament. The word [Shekinah]{.class_s4N4-0} is one of those Madison Avenue clichés like "lanolin," "K-15," "polyunsaturated fats," etc. Like the ["asherah"]{.class_s4N4-0} and the ["wadis" ]{.class_s4N4-0}of the [RSV ]{.class_s4N4-0}and the [ASV]{.class_s4N4-0}, it is a sort of "uptown" word which sounds []{#c307.xhtml_page_565}impressive and is designed to make the student think he is in the presence of a smart man. [There is no "Shekinah" in either Testament in Greek or Hebrew, so you may as well get back in your Bible and learn something.]{.class_s4N4-0}

["After the pattern\...and the pattern\...so shall ye make it"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 9). [Keil ]{.class_s4N4-0}misses it and says that God showed Moses the tabernacle of Revelation 15:5 and told him to copy it. [Clarke ]{.class_s4N4-0}misses it and says it is Christ and the Church. [Dummelow ]{.class_s4N4-0}misses the whole thing and alters the word ["pattern"]{.class_s4N2-0} to a generalized revelation of a generalized way to worship God, in general. [Williams, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown,]{.class_s4N4-0} and [Johnson]{.class_s4N4-0} say there must have been a "model," but they cannot find the model.

["After the pattern of the tabernacle"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 9). From here on it goes by many names: ["the tent" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(Psa. 78:60), ["the tent of the congregation"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Exod. 40), ["the tent of the testimony" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(Num. 9:15) ["the tabernacle of witness" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(Num. 17:7--8), the ["sanctuary" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(Exod. 25:8), ["the temple" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(1 Sam. 1:9), the ["habitation" ]{.class_s4N2-0}of the Lord (1 Sam. 2:29) etc. As we have said before, the Holy Spirit has told us very plainly that the pattern Moses was to use was to match the ["greater and more perfect tabernacle, not made with hands"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Heb. 9:11).

The matching up of the Holy of Holies with the throne in the third heaven (Rev. 4:2--6; Heb. 9:24) is so definite in the New Testament that one can only marvel at the refusal of the commentators to believe what they read. But we have seen this kind of business before. Readers of[ the Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis]{.class_s4N4-0} (1970) saw the Scofield Board of Editors (comprising some of the best Conservative scholarship in the world) knocked clean out of the ring before they had traveled six verses []{#c307.xhtml_page_566}in a[ King James ]{.class_s4N4-0}Bible. ["Science falsely socalled"]{.class_s4N2-0} did the work. And that is why the word ["science"]{.class_s4N2-0} is missing from the [New American Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} (1 Tim. 6:20) and similar apostate corruptions.

The ["pattern" ]{.class_s4N2-0}for the Christian is Paul (1 Tim. 1:16).

25:10 "And they shall make an ark of shittim wood: two cubits and a half shall be the length thereof, and a cubit and a half the breadth thereof, and a cubit and a half the height thereof.

11 And thou shalt overlay it with pure gold, within and without shalt thou overlay it, and shalt make upon it a crown of gold round about.

12 And thou shalt cast four rings of gold for it, and put them in the four corners thereof; and two rings shall be in the one side of it, and two rings in the other side of it.

13 And thou shalt make staves of shittim wood, and overlay them with gold.

14 And thou shalt put the staves into the rings by the sides of the ark, that the ark may be borne with them.

15 The staves shall be in the rings of the ark: they shall not be taken from it.

16 And thou shalt put into the ark the testimony which I shall give thee.

17 And thou shalt make a mercy seat of pure gold: two cubits and a half shall be the length thereof, and a cubit and a half the breadth thereof.

18 And thou shalt make two cherubims of gold, of beaten work shalt thou make them, in the two ends of the mercy seat.

19 And make one cherub on the one end, and the other cherub on the other end: even of the mercy seat shall ye make the cherubims on the two ends thereof.

20 And the cherubims shall stretch forth their wings on high, covering the mercy seat with their wings, and their faces shall look one to another; toward the mercy seat shall the faces of the cherubims be.

21 And thou shalt put the mercy seat above upon the ark; and in the ark thou shalt put the testimony that I shall give thee.

22 And there I will meet with thee, and I will commune with thee from above the mercy seat, from between the two cherubims which are upon the ark of the testimony, of all things which I will give thee in commandment unto the children of Israel."

The first thing mentioned when the Lord begins to go into detail about the Tabernacle is the FURNITURE. The furniture is considered to be of more importance than the structure itself or the court around it. And the first item that shows up clearly is a reference to the place where God will "meet" with the children of Israel; it matches the throne of Revelation 4:2--6---cherubims and all.

The moral lesson learned in this "order of importance" is one of the greatest in the entire Bible, for it shows us:

1\. That unless God [Himself ]{.class_s4N4-0}initiates the action, we cannot approach unto Him.

2\. Salvation begins with God Himself as its ["author and finisher"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Heb. 12:2),[ ]{.class_s4N4-0}and all work of []{#c307.xhtml_page_568}reconciliation must proceed out from God to man, not in the reverse order.

3\. As an ark saved the human race (Gen. 6--9), so now an ark is used as a meeting place of communion for the nation of Israel (vs. 22). In both cases, the Lord Himself had to give the instructions for the building of the Ark; it was a [supernatural revelation]{.class_s4N4-0} both times.

Therefore, "Babel building" (Gen. 11:1--4) is not the way to build a structure ["whose top may reach unto heaven."]{.class_s4N2-0} The plumb bob must hang DOWN to get a straight wall or chimney. No carpenter or mason in the world is going to stand all day at the base of a structure, throwing a plumb bob up in the air trying to build STRAIGHT. [Liberal preachers]{.class_s4N4-0} and [international Socialists]{.class_s4N4-0} are the only people who go about it in that fashion.

Since the "abode" for God in the passage is given to a nation, one will notice the plural used (["they shall make an ark"]{.class_s4N2-0}).
