We would expect Keil, Delitzsch, Dummelow, Williams, Bullinger, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, and "the majority of Conservative scholars" to miss this nugget, for as all dead-orthodox Bible critics, they devoted their lives to correcting the Reformation text. It is quite natural, therefore, that none of them noticed the ark [which Moses himself made]{.class_s4N4-0} (by himself) in Deuteronomy 10:1--3. We are told, in Deuteronomy 10:5, that Moses made this ark by himself (["which I had made"]{.class_s4N2-0}), and this was done [before ]{.class_s4N4-0}Bezaleel and Aholiab (36:1--2) "wrought" anything for the Tabernacle.

The nation of Israel has no Ark until AFTER [they have apostatized and gone back into Egyptian idolatry]{.class_s4N4-0} (chap. 32) to the tune of "acid rock." The Lord gives them time []{#c307.xhtml_page_569}to break all Ten Commandments (chap. 32) before He lets them build a "[mercy seat.]{.class_s4N2-0}" You can be certain that when they built it, they knew what it was for! (This great spiritual truth, naturally, is hidden from the eyes of many "godly, qualified" Conservatives who talk about "the Lord Jesus Christ" being the only infallible authority, while they spend their lives destroying His Book.)

This Ark remained in [Shiloh ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Josh. 18:1) after the conquest of the land. It was captured, temporarily, by the [Philistines ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1 Sam. 4--6) and was brought up from the house of [Obed-edom]{.class_s4N4-0} by David in 1 Chronicles chapter 13 (cf. 2 Sam. 6). Solomon placed it in the [Temple ]{.class_s4N4-0}in 1 Kings chapter 8, but there is no record of Shishak or Nebuchadnezzar getting their hands on it. In Revelation 11:19, we can assume that it was RAPTURED before the destruction of Jerusalem under Nebuchadnezzar. (This will raise the hellish cry of "Brother Ruckman is doing a lot of harm and confusion with his hobbyhorse" from the dead-orthodox apostates who are building monuments instead of Bible believers; but since all of them---without exception---failed to notice [the mobility of the ark]{.class_s4N4-0} as it FLEW across the desert \[see Num. 10:33--36\], we should not be surprised to find the advanced revelation of the [Authorized text]{.class_s4N4-0} rejected for the "traditions of men"---Mark 7:9, 13.)

The Ark stands for the essence of God in His dwelling place in the third heaven, beyond Alpha Draconis (see the [Commentary on Genesis,]{.class_s4N4-0} Gen. 1--2). Taking the cubit to be eighteen inches, we have a rectangular object overlaid with pure gold about three feet nine inches long, two feet two inches wide, and two feet three inches high. (Clarke takes the cubit to be 21 inches, which is probably closer []{#c307.xhtml_page_570}to the truth, and gets an ark four feet five inches long and two feet six inches wide and two feet six inches high.)

The careful reader of the English text will notice that the top of this ["ark,"]{.class_s4N2-0} which is covered by the ["mercy seat"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 17--19), is on an [exact even level]{.class_s4N4-0} with the [brazen grate]{.class_s4N4-0} in the sacrificial altar. The brazen altar had this "barbecue grill" hanging down inside it to the exact height (27:1), with the notation that it hung ["even to the midst of the altar"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 27:5). This means that [God's presence]{.class_s4N4-0} was pictured as being on the same level with the [sacrificial fire]{.class_s4N4-0} of the burnt offering. No sacrifice, no presence. No shedding of blood, no remission (Lev. 17). No shedding of blood, no [redemption ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Col. 1:14 in the [AV]{.class_s4N4-0}). (This last great Protestant truth has been removed from the following Roman Catholic Bibles: the Jesuit [Rheims ]{.class_s4N4-0}of 1582, the [New American Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} \[1963\] promoted by Bob Jones University, the old [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}of Philip Schaff \[1901\], the [Revised Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} of the National Council of Churches \[1952\], and Jerome's Latin [Vulgate]{.class_s4N4-0}.)

In verse 11, we see that the Ark is not like the ["whited sepulchres"]{.class_s4N2-0} of the Pharisees; the Ark is the same thing INSIDE that it is OUTSIDE (cf. Matt. [23:27). ]{.class_s4N2-0}It is,[ "within and without," "pure gold."]{.class_s4N2-0} The ["crown of gold"]{.class_s4N2-0} suggests that we are dealing not only with a Redeemer and Saviour (the Kingdom of God), but an earthly ruler (the Kingdom of Heaven). The ["rings"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 12) and the ["staves"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 13) were for carrying the Ark (all of the furniture inside the Tabernacle was "moveable"). The staves were left in the rings (vs. 15) even after the Ark was deposited in the Holy of Holies. It is not until 1 Kings 8:8 that these staves are removed from the Ark. This passage clearly shows us []{#c307.xhtml_page_571}that Solomon's reign was a type of the Millennial reign of Christ, and when Christ returns to this earth, His reign goes right on out into Eternity (see details in the [Commentary on Genesis, ]{.class_s4N4-0}Gen. 2:1--3).

All of the major commentators missed the cross-reference, but this was to be expected from any man who is foolish enough to follow the lead of dead-orthodox apostates like Philip Schaff and A. T. Robertson. [Both of these men]{.class_s4N4-0} were Amillennialists who rejected the literal interpretation of three-fourths of the Old Testament. Neither Johnson, Bleek, Jamieson, Eichorn, Fausset, Brown, Dummelow, Bullinger, nor the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} noted the Holy Spirit's exception to His own rule (1 Kings 8:8). This is "par for the course" in dealing with destructive critics who profess to believe in "the authority of the verbally inspired originals, etc." (["The]{.class_s4N2-0} [staves"]{.class_s4N2-0} of Num. 4:6 are an additional pair.)

["And thou shalt put into the ark the testimony which I shall give thee"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 16). ["The testimony"]{.class_s4N2-0} came in three installments: (Heb 9:4): the first was the [pot of manna, ]{.class_s4N4-0}the second was the [Ten Commandments,]{.class_s4N4-0} and the third was [Aaron's rod]{.class_s4N4-0} that budded (Num. 17:8--10). Deuteronomy 31:26 indicates that the Decalogue on the tables of stone was the main "testimony," but no man who compares the verse with Numbers 17:8--10 would fail to see that God "testified" in more ways than the giving of the Law.

Observe the beautiful typology of the three "testimonies," which all of the major commentators missed (Liberal or Conservative):

1\. God graciously feeds unsaved people and gives them a foretaste of ["the powers of the world to []{#c307.xhtml_page_572}come" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(Heb. 6:5) to get their attention. Paul says this is a ["witness"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Acts 14:17).

2\. He then [convicts of sin]{.class_s4N4-0} (John 16:8--11) by revealing His holiness (19:21), and brings the sinner to the end of himself (34:9).

3\. At this point, [the resurrected high priest]{.class_s4N4-0} (Num. 17:8--10) is revealed (Heb. 3:1) to prove that there is ["one mediator between God and men"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 Tim. 2:5).

If this typology seems stretched, let the reader never forget that [unsaved people who had no faith]{.class_s4N4-0} (Heb. 3:18--19) were in the Israelite camp from the time it left Egypt (12:38) until it finished its forty-year pilgrimage in the desert.

["And thou shalt make a mercy seat of pure gold"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 17). This was a solid slab of gold fitting the length and width of the ark, and by such proportions, it would have been worth about \$40,000 in American money, depending upon the thickness of it. The two cherubims were to be made out of the same piece of gold (["even of the mercy seat shall ye make the cherubims,"]{.class_s4N2-0} vs. 19).

["And the cherubims shall stretch forth their wings" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 20). We suddenly run into an epidemic of agnosticism at this point, since none of the Conservative scholars like to stick their necks out when it comes to celestial beings that "science" has not yet located with telescopes. [Dummelow ]{.class_s4N4-0}has written that the "exact form" of the cherubim is doubtful. [Bullinger ]{.class_s4N4-0}confesses that "we know nothing" about them. [Johnson ]{.class_s4N4-0}([Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0}) does not describe them and limps off with some mumbo jumbo about "symbolizing the presence of the Lord." [Clarke ]{.class_s4N4-0}tells us that "what these are we []{#c307.xhtml_page_573}cannot distinctly say." Our commentators are much like the apostles in John 16:18 who ["cannot tell what he saith."]{.class_s4N2-0}

Now all of this "unanimity among the recognized scholars" comes from the unconverted Jewish historian [Josephus ]{.class_s4N4-0}([Jewish Antiquities,]{.class_s4N4-0} viii 3, pg. 3), who says: "[No one]{.class_s4N4-0} is able to state or [conjecture ]{.class_s4N4-0}of what form the cherubim were." Such majestic dogmatism calls for investigation!

[How about God? ]{.class_s4N4-0}Do you suppose that God the Holy Spirit could "conjecture" on what form they were? Well, if you grant that, would you grant that God once wrote a book where He made some "conjectures" on what cherubim look like?

1\. There [were ]{.class_s4N4-0}five of them (Ezek. 28:14--16 cf. 10:1--15).

2\. There are now [four ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Rev. 4:6--9).

3\. The fifth one represented the [amphibian-reptile class,]{.class_s4N4-0} which is now missing (cf. Ezek. 1:10).

4\. They represent the creation: the wild beasts, the flying beasts, the domesticated beasts, and man (Ezek. 10:14)---not "the Lord's presence."

5\. They are connected with the throne of God (Rev. 4:6--7).

6\. They are connected with an unidentified flying object that will appear at the Second Advent (Ezek. 1:4--5).

7\. They use four and six wings alternately (Ezek. 1:6 cf. Rev. 1:8).

8\. When stationery, they have six wings (Rev. 4:8); when moving four (Ezek. 1:6--12).

9\. [There is more information on their APPEARANCE than there is on the appearance of Paul, Peter, James, John, and Judas Iscariot combined.]{.class_s4N4-0}

Why the epidemic of agnosticism unless you are ashamed of the revelation of God? [A little infidelity in there again, eh, Doctor?]{.class_s4N4-0}

Furthermore, all of the commentators seem to have forgotten that the Creator of Adam and the physical body of Christ (Heb. 10:5) was the Creator of the birds and the animals. These creatures need redemption as much as man, at least in regards to their animal natures derived from the curse (Gen. 9:1--17; Rom. 8:15--27; Isa. 11:6--10). Furthermore, our Lord was born in a STABLE (not a Roman Catholic "cave"---[Papias]{.class_s4N4-0}) and took His place as a LAMB (John 1:36). The cherubim cannot receive the benefits of the atonement, for they (as the seraphim and angels) [have no BLOOD. ]{.class_s4N4-0}However, even the animals need MERCY, and so we find their representatives kneeling over the ["mercy seat,"]{.class_s4N2-0} where blood is sprinkled for sinful men. Without the mercy of God, no cherubim would survive anymore than a seraphim (Isa. 6:2) or an archangel.

The serious student of Scripture, who studies the [Authorized Version]{.class_s4N4-0} of 1611 instead of the degraded ["New American Standard Version,"]{.class_s4N4-0} sees that the cherubims of 1 Kings 6:23 are made out of OLIVE TREES, not gold, and they are not [part of the mercy seat]{.class_s4N4-0} as we find them here. The moral lesson is clear to the devout mind which believes in an absolute, infallible authority as a present reality, instead of a past transaction: Christ will no longer be in the third heaven during the Millennium (typified by Solomon's reign), for He has [detached Himself from the "throne"]{.class_s4N4-0} (Rev. 3:21). (What is often []{#c307.xhtml_page_575}obscure in the Hebrew, American, Coptic, Syriac, Arabic, Mumbo-Jumbo, [LXX, Hexapla, ]{.class_s4N4-0}Greek, and Pig Latin is often very vivid and thrilling in the "King's English.")

The cherubim, here, are kneeling facing each other, and their heads are bowed so that they are ["toward the mercy seat"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 20). From the other passages (Ezek. 1:23), we know that the other two wings were stretched back to the sides of the Holy of Holies (1 Kings 6:27).

Unlike the pagan deities who were paraded on the shoulders of their worshippers at religious festivals, this Ark remained hidden except when it was moving ahead as a "scout" for the camp of Israel (Num. 10:33). Furthermore, there was one great difference between this Ark and the pagan palanquins that are carried about by Mexicans, Spaniards, Italians, and South Americans. All of these pagan "arks" have a [visible figure]{.class_s4N4-0} of the object of worship that is being carried about.

When the Jew looked at the Ark, he saw only the cherubim, and he had just been told NOT to bow down to them (20:4--5). The only thing, therefore, to which he could bow down was an invisible presence (Rom. 1:20), which he was told was somewhere ["between the two cherubims"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 22). Worshipping God ["in spirit and in truth"]{.class_s4N2-0} (John 4:24) is clearly taught in the very construction of the Ark and the[ "mercy seat."]{.class_s4N2-0} The Greek chimeras, English griffins, Egyptians sphinxes, and Assyrian winged bulls WERE objects of adoration to the heathen, exactly as Aaron's golden calf (see the ["cherub"]{.class_s4N2-0} of Ezek. 10:14 cf. 1:10) was an object of worship. Confounding [the closest thing to the throne]{.class_s4N4-0} with the One who sits [on the throne]{.class_s4N4-0} is quite typical of unregenerate worship; and we see this amply demonstrated in the writings of Samuel Colderidge, []{#c307.xhtml_page_576}Martin Luther King Jr., Friedrich Engels, Karl Marx, Ted Kennedy, and other international Socialists in the substitution of the second commandment for the [first one]{.class_s4N4-0} (see the [Commentary on Matthew,]{.class_s4N4-0} Matt. 22:37--39).

For comments on verse 22, see 23:14--17, 29:42--46, and 34:22--24.

25:23 "Thou shalt also make a table of shittim wood: two cubits shall be the length thereof, and a cubit the breadth thereof, and a cubit and a half the height thereof.

24 And thou shalt overlay it with pure gold, and make thereto a crown of gold round about.

25 And thou shalt make unto it a border of an hand breadth round about, and thou shalt make a golden crown to the border thereof round about.

26 And thou shalt make for it four rings of gold, and put the rings in the four corners that are on the four feet thereof.

27 Over against the border shall the rings be for places of the staves to bear the table.

28 And thou shalt make the staves of shittim wood, and overlay them with gold, that the table may be borne with them.

29 And thou shalt make the dishes thereof, and spoons thereof, and covers thereof, and bowls thereof, to cover withal: of pure gold shalt thou make them.

30 And thou shalt set upon the table shewbread before me alway."

This table would be about three feet long, two feet three inches high, and one foot six inches broad; it was made of wood overlaid with gold (as the Ark). It had the embellished ornamental ["crown"]{.class_s4N2-0} around the top (as the []{#c307.xhtml_page_577}Ark) and was equipped with rings and staves for mobility (as the Ark). The top of the table was even with the mercy seat and the altar of burnt offering (["a cubit and a half the height thereof," ]{.class_s4N2-0}vs. 23).

Instructions for the bread to be laid upon it are found in Numbers 4:7 and Leviticus 24:5--6. There were twelve loaves arranged in two rows of six in a row. The bread on this table is properly called, in the Masoretic version (Jewish Pub. Soc., 1917, pg. 105), "SHOWBREAD," exactly as it appears in the [Authorized Version]{.class_s4N4-0} of 1611. The altering of this word to ["bread of the presence"]{.class_s4N4-0} or ["face bread"]{.class_s4N4-0} or ["presence bread"]{.class_s4N4-0} is just more of the old flapdoodle that we got when we tried to find out about the "cherubim." (Dead-orthodox Conservatives are very consistent in their habits, and among their depraved dodges and twists, none are more perennial than saying that a figure represents a general number showing "completion" or "the figure symbolizes the presence of the Lord." These two \[with the never-ending phrase "the [Authorized Version]{.class_s4N4-0} is to be commended for its stately language, rhythmic cadences, and flowing Elizabethan, etc."\] are part of the stinking inheritance which every dead-orthodox Conservative passes on to the next generation.)

(Readers not familiar with the terms "dead-orthodox [apostate]{.class_s4N4-0}" and "dead-orthodox [Conservative]{.class_s4N4-0}" should note that apostates and Conservatives are often in agreement and often stand side by side when it comes to questioning the power and authority of the English text of the Reformation. For example, the [New American Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} \[New Testament, 1963\] is the same Greek text which was used by the [Revised Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} of 1952. When we say "dead-orthodox apostate," we mean []{#c307.xhtml_page_578}an [Amillennial]{.class_s4N4-0} or [Postmillennial]{.class_s4N4-0} scholar who professes to believe in the [Nicene Creed:]{.class_s4N4-0} for example, Dummelow, Sanday, Schaff, Clarke, Machen, Warfield, Lange, Robertson, Westcott, Hort, Ellicott, Lightfoot, and Berkhof. When we say "dead-orthodox Conservative," we mean a [Premillennialist, ]{.class_s4N4-0}like Thieme, Wemp, Wuest, Custer, Walvoord, Weniger, Free, Archer, Zodhiates, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, Chafer, et al., who professes to believe in all of the "fundamentals of the faith" and "the verbally inspired et ceteras," [while they devote their lives to destroying the authority of the English Bible. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Apostates are men like Pike, Sheen, Inge, Kagawa, Ptoeat, Holscher, Niebuhr, Barth, Gedder, Brunner, Tillich, Balke, et al. But "dead-[orthodox ]{.class_s4N4-0}apostates" are [saved men]{.class_s4N4-0} who set themselves up as the final authority in matters of faith and practice and deny three-fourths of the Bible by using the Amillennial or Postmillennial systems of private interpretation.)

"The bread of the presence" (for the ["shewbread"]{.class_s4N2-0}) is about as edifying as calling the candlestick ["the wick of the nearness."]{.class_s4N4-0} In the first place, the Table of Showbread was NOT in the Holy of Holies where God's presence was; it was outside in the "Holy Place." In the second place, by making this bread "the bread of the presence," you stumbled right back into the lap of the old whore (Rev. 17:1--6) whose business was making bread (Matt. 13:33), and she has taught "from her youth up" that [Christ's presence]{.class_s4N4-0} is [present ]{.class_s4N4-0}when the papists eat the BREAD of the Mass. (We recognize the fingerprints, we have seen the tracks before, and we smell the stench of "Holy Mother Church" before she gets through the vestibule into the committee of 1880--1885, via Westcott and Hort.)

["Shewbread"]{.class_s4N2-0} will do just fine, even if Dr. Johnson ([Wycliffe Commentary,]{.class_s4N4-0} pg. 75) resents the English martyr Tyndale borrowing it from the Bible of the German Reformation (see Heb. 9:2). (When it comes to [Tyndale ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [Luther ]{.class_s4N4-0}versus [Philip Schaff]{.class_s4N4-0} and the [Lockman Foundation,]{.class_s4N4-0} no Bible believer really has to guess very long about the choice of any reading.)

The table has ["dishes\...spoons\...covers\...and bowls"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 29) that go with it; and these are obviously for mixing batter, stirring the batter, and protecting the bread from getting stale in the seven days it is displayed.

The peculiar twist of logic which makes the commentators (any of any persuasion) talk about the bread being "before the face of Jehovah" and "in the presence of Jehovah" comes from trying to trying to translate the Hebrew literally ("[lechem panim]{.class_s4N4-0}") after [refusing to translate many Hebrew words literally]{.class_s4N4-0} for 25 chapters, and then refusing to [believe ]{.class_s4N4-0}the Bible literally after they have translated some of it literally (see the lengthy discussion of the "swarms," "flies," and "locusts" in chaps. 7--9, for example). ["Bread of the face,"]{.class_s4N4-0} according to a Hebrew lexicon, can be translated:

1\. Angry bread.

2\. Edge bread.

3\. Favorable bread.

4\. Form bread.

5\. Old-time bread.

6\. Bread for the mouth.

7\. Open bread.

8\. Bread of heaviness.

9\. Prospective bread.

10\. Bread of the forepart or forefront.

["Shewbread"]{.class_s4N2-0} plainly indicates bread that was set out as a "show" to demonstrate something. The variety of interpretations inherent in this word would cover every possibility listed above, while "bread of the face" or "presence bread" not only restricts the meaning, but worse, it teaches something that was not so.

Now what does the showbread show?

1\. It shows that [God will provide all twelve tribes]{.class_s4N4-0} with their daily bread.

2\. It shows that [God does things by sevens]{.class_s4N4-0} (the loaves were baked [weekly]{.class_s4N4-0}), and that the eighth thing has to be new.

3\. It shows the priests that [they are dependent for their living on the twelve tribes;]{.class_s4N4-0} this is a constant reminder that their main calling in life is to MINISTER, ["not to be ministered unto"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Matt. 20:28).

4\. It typifies the "Sixty-Six." The bread which man must live by (Luke 4:4---omitted in the mangled [New American Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0}) TURNS out to be [66 books,]{.class_s4N4-0} which can only be understood [in the light]{.class_s4N4-0} of the next object we are about to study---[the Golden Candlestick. ]{.class_s4N4-0}In the darkness of the Holy Place---there were no windows anywhere in the Tabernacle---the only light was the Holy Spirit (1 Cor. 2:10--14), and He is the only One [who can "open our understanding"]{.class_s4N4-0} (Luke 24:45) [that we might appreciate the "showbread"]{.class_s4N4-0} (2 Cor. 3:1--12).

The [King James]{.class_s4N4-0} 1611 has the word right, and the other trash put out by the Lockman Foundation is just as wrong as if it had never been put out in the alley in the first place.

The "bread" comes on a GOLD service, which symbolizes its [divine origin,]{.class_s4N4-0} even though it was baked by [men ]{.class_s4N4-0}(showing its human origin). Like []{#c307.xhtml_page_581}Jesus Christ Himself---the eternal Word---the Bible has [two natures]{.class_s4N4-0}. (Believers should observe how all dead-orthodox apostates used the "Word" for the "word" when trying to fool the student into thinking that they are Bible believers. When a modern, Bible-denying "Fundamentalist" like John R. Rice \[[Sword of the Lord,]{.class_s4N4-0} Feb. 23, 1973\] says "I believe God has preserved His Word," he means it exactly as the expression is used by Barth and Brunner. He is making no reference to God preserving the [words ]{.class_s4N4-0}of a [written ]{.class_s4N4-0}book. Rather, he is referring to the preservation of several [truths ]{.class_s4N4-0}\["fundamentals"\] through [several ]{.class_s4N4-0}"reliable" or "credible" translations. This leaves the dead-orthodox apostate in the position of the final authority to decide which is "credible" and which is not. That is, it makes [saved sinners]{.class_s4N4-0} the [final authority]{.class_s4N4-0} and cancels the Holy Spirit. The men who take this position---98% of all Conservative Greek and Hebrew scholars in any generation since 1700---do the most harm to the Body of Christ that it is possible to do, for they take the individual Christian's authority away from him \[[as Rome]{.class_s4N4-0}\] and leave him with an authoritative hierarchy \[[as Rome]{.class_s4N4-0}\] instead of an authoritative Bible \[[as Rome]{.class_s4N4-0}\]. The confusion and harm wrought by these "blameless Conservative scholars" in the Twentieth Century is incalculable. We may say, without overstatement, that the greatest hindrance to real revival and real consecration in America stems from the same place that Communism uses for a breeding bed---[the Universities. ]{.class_s4N4-0}The fact that these Conservatives profess to be "contending for the faith" means nothing if they are [destroying the Christian's authority]{.class_s4N4-0} while they are doing it. This is merely seeking to substitute scholarship for the Bible, and in essence, it is no different []{#c307.xhtml_page_582}than the lifelong ministries of papists and international Socialists.)

Observe the[ "crown"]{.class_s4N2-0} showing up again (vs. 24). This makes three crowns for the ministries of the Father, Son, and Spirit. The [Father's]{.class_s4N4-0} crown (on the Ark) stands over the Decalogue given to Israel. The [Son's]{.class_s4N4-0} crown (on the Table) encircles ["the bread of life"]{.class_s4N2-0} which[ "came down from heaven"]{.class_s4N2-0} (John 6:26--35). And the [Holy Spirit's ]{.class_s4N4-0}crown (on the Incense Altar) leads us in prayer (Psa. 141:2) ["with groanings which cannot be uttered"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rom. 8:26). Ah, the infallible power of the [Authorized Version. ]{.class_s4N4-0}How excruciating are its incidentals and its simplicity past finding out!

Now observe how this order---Father, Son, and Spirit---was carried out in the first instructions given to Moses. It is the [Ark]{.class_s4N4-0}, the [Table]{.class_s4N4-0}, and the [Candlestick]{.class_s4N4-0}; no other order would be possible, under ANY condition, in giving the revelation. The conjectures of the "majority of Conservative scholars" as to the [reasons ]{.class_s4N4-0}for the order given in chapter 25 are wasted paper and wasted ink. Williams' remark that the three crowns represent Jesus as Saviour, King, and Priest are a BLANK. Christ has ["MANY crowns"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rev. 19:12), not just three; and His offices are Prophet, Priest, and King (in that order, cf. Rev. 19:16), not "Saviour, King, and Priest." Williams---an ardent Bullingerite---stands on these verses in the same position as an Amillennial or Postmillennial dead-orthodox critic, and we would expect him to do so; for he, as Bullinger, O'Hair, and Baker, corrects the Holy Bible liberally where it crosses his dispensational systems.

A good example of this truth is found in the corrupt [New American Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} (and the old one---1901 ), promoted by Bob Jones University and other []{#c307.xhtml_page_583}Bible-rejecting "Fundamentalist institutions." Here, at verse 29, we find the [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(either edition of any printing) saying that the ["bowls thereof"]{.class_s4N2-0} were not for [covering the loaves of showbread,]{.class_s4N4-0} but were for the "pouring out of libations" ([ASV]{.class_s4N4-0}, "[to pour out]{.class_s4N4-0}"). Where did this unique reading come from? Well, where do 90% of any "corrections" come from? Need we ask? Haven't we already discovered the root of the "final authority for all matters of faith and practice" at Dallas, Fuller, Moody, Wheaton, Hyles-Anderson, BIOLA, Fort Worth, Southeastern, Southwestern, Lynchburg, Columbia, Trinity, etc.? Why, of course.

The [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}reading is from the Roman Catholic [Vulgate ]{.class_s4N4-0}of Jerome, via [Africa]{.class_s4N4-0}. It is found in the mythological "[LXX]{.class_s4N4-0}" (written by Origen, Aquila, et al., long after the death of the Apostle John), and it is preserved in the [New American Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} exactly as Westcott and Hort (and Dr. Green and Dr. Schaff) preserved it in the corrupt [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}of 1901, recommended by "the majority of Conservative et ceteras." The serious student of Scripture will notice that ["cups"]{.class_s4N4-0} has been added to Numbers 4:7 to reenforce this ancient corruption. It is in the corrupt [American Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} (any edition) to make you think that "pouring" is the correct reference. Again, this is quite typical of the faculty members of Conservative schools whose hobbyhorse is the promotion of [Vaticanus ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [Sinaiticus ]{.class_s4N4-0}as "[reliable ]{.class_s4N4-0}authorities." One should expect such conduct from Bible-rejecting "Christians" who desire to replace the authority of the word with the traditional superstitions of Alexandria, Egypt. At all these places the reader will do well to accept the highly accurate and scientific text of the [King James]{.class_s4N4-0} 1611 [Version ]{.class_s4N4-0}and discard the incredible (and wholly []{#c307.xhtml_page_584}unreliable) [New American Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} (see [Satan's Masterpiece,]{.class_s4N4-0} Bible Baptist Bookstore, 1972).

25:31 "And thou shalt make a candlestick of pure gold: of beaten work shall the candlestick be made: his shaft, and his branches, his bowls, his knops, and his flowers, shall be of the same.

32 And six branches shall come out of the sides of it; three branches of the candlestick out of the one side, and three branches of the candlestick out of the other side:

33 Three bowls made like unto almonds, with a knop and a flower in one branch; and three bowls made like almonds in the other branch, with a knop and a flower: so in the six branches that come out of the candlestick.

34 And in the candlestick shall be four bowls made like unto almonds, with their knops and their flowers.

35 And there shall be a knop under two branches of the same, and a knop under two branches of the same, and a knop under two branches of the same, according to the six branches that proceed out of the candlestick.

36 Their knops and their branches shall be of the same: all it shall be one beaten work of pure gold.

37 And thou shalt make the seven lamps thereof: and they shall light the lamps thereof, that they may give light over against it.

38 And the tongs thereof, and the snuffdishes thereof, shall be of pure gold.

39 Of a talent of pure gold shall he make it, with all these vessels.

40 And look that thou make them after their pattern, which was shewed thee in the mount."

There is no doubt whatsoever about the typology of the Golden Candlestick. The seven branches match the ["seven Spirits"]{.class_s4N2-0} of Revelation 4:5 and Isaiah 11:1--2. The olive oil plainly comes from the "tree of life" (Rom. 11:17), and this candlestick furnishes the [only light]{.class_s4N4-0} inside the sanctuary (1 Cor. 2:14--16), as it illuminates the ["]{.class_s4N4-0}[shewbread" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(the Bible) and the Altar of Incense (prayer). It shines evening and morning (27:21), and when it goes [out ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1 Sam. 3:3), there is no revelation about ["the word of the LORD"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 Sam. 3:1, 7, 21). It is made of ["one beaten work of pure gold"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 36) since there is ["one Spirit"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Eph. 4:4), and although the dimensions are not given (Psa. 139:7--8), if we are to believe the figures of Bishop Cumberland ([Clarke's Commentary,]{.class_s4N4-0} pg. 434), taken from ["a talent of pure gold"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 39), it was worth well over \$826,000.

The [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_s4N4-0}naturally confounds the instructions of 27:20 with 1 Samuel 3:3. The reasoning is that [since Eli let the lamp go out,]{.class_s4N4-0} contrary to the instructions given (27:20), that it was a [regular practice]{.class_s4N4-0} to let it go out. Again, knowing our commentators like we know them, we would guess that they would stumble at the passage, for the typology given above clearly shows that [when the Holy Spirit is not around to interpret the word, the commentator cannot interpret it. ]{.class_s4N4-0}So---! The [Pulpit Commentary's]{.class_s4N4-0} "Golden Candlestick" is kind of like the "[eternal ]{.class_s4N4-0}flame" on JFK's monument that was extinguished with some kid's water pistol.

We are supposed to be greatly distressed by the term ["candlestick"]{.class_s4N2-0} instead of "lampstand," according to the critics; but since we are plainly dealing with seven candles, and since the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}called them ["lamps"]{.class_s4N2-0} anyway (vs. 37), there is no particular need for the consternation. The ["shaft"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 31) is plainly the upright centerpiece upon which the candlestick rests. The altering of [this ]{.class_s4N4-0}word to ["base"]{.class_s4N4-0} ([ASV, NASV, RSV,]{.class_s4N4-0} and other corruptions) confounds the truth, for verse 32 says ["six branches shall come out of the sides of it."]{.class_s4N2-0}

The picture, therefore, presented by the corrupt [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [NASV]{.class_s4N4-0} (recommended by Reuben Olson of the Lockman Foundation, Wilbur Smith, and John R. Rice) is that we have six branches coming out of the BASE of a lamp. [This is exactly what the text does NOT mean. ]{.class_s4N4-0}The six branches come out of a SHAFT, exactly as the [King James]{.class_s4N4-0} states it, and the suggested alteration of ["shaft"]{.class_s4N2-0} to ["base"]{.class_s4N4-0} by 99% of the Conservative scholars since 1885 only shows that Conservative scholars are as glued to their traditional hobbyhorse (altering the Reformation Bible) as any Trappist monk who ever lived. This alteration is one of the 30,000 made in 1885 and preserved in the apostate [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1901) and[ NASV]{.class_s4N4-0} (1963), and these depraved pieces of trash are no more "credible" or "reliable" than Jeane Dixon's horoscope readings or Edgar Cayce's dream book. The fact that these two hoaxes "contain the fundamental [message ]{.class_s4N4-0}of the Word" (see comments under vss. 23--27) has nothing to do with their "credibility" or "reliability." When a dead-orthodox Conservative (Bob Jones III or Stewart Custer, for example) uses these two clichés, he really means that [some readings]{.class_s4N4-0} in [some verses]{.class_s4N4-0} are "credible." To say that a translation is "reliable" or "thoroughly reliable" []{#c307.xhtml_page_587}simply because you can find ten fundamentals IN it, is to confess that you are a criminal. [No law court in America or Europe thinks that way, acts that way, speaks that way, or judges that way. ]{.class_s4N4-0}That is, the lawlessness of 2 Thessalonians 2:1--3 appears in this century among the faculty members of "Conservative" schools.

The almond tree cannot be hid (vs. 33), for it is the sign of the resurrected High Priest (Num. 17:8); and even though most of the commentators missed the cross-reference, a real believer could not have missed it blindfolded, flying upside down. Christ was ["declared to be the Son of God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rom. 1:3--4) by the power of the Holy Spirit who raised him from the dead (Rom. 8:11). The reference to the ["flower"]{.class_s4N2-0} and the "fruit" (vs. 33) is unmistakable; it matches the ["bell"]{.class_s4N2-0} and the ["pomegranate" ]{.class_s4N2-0}of 28:34---profession and fruit, profession and fruit, etc. The ["knop"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 31, 33--35) was like a [knob]{.class_s4N4-0}. It was shaped like the pomegranate, and it is thought that the flower represented was the lily. The candlestick contained 22 almond-shaped ["bowls"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 33--34), [matching the Jewish canon of the Old Testament]{.class_s4N4-0} and the number of letters in the Hebrew alphabet! For Israel, there is no fruit (the "almond") unless it comes from the [oracles ]{.class_s4N4-0}given to it by God (Rom. 3:1--2).

We are not through with application yet. (The Bible believer can always find a more spiritual truth in the Bible from the English text than the greatest Bible-denying linguist can find in the "original, plenary, verbally inspired et ceteras. ")

["I am the vine, ye are the branches"]{.class_s4N2-0} (John 15:5). We can only bear fruit through Him, and [He]{.class_s4N4-0} sustains and maintains us; not vice versa. The fruit is born by the Holy Spirit (Gal. 5:22--23) and we are not to ["quench"]{.class_s4N2-0} []{#c307.xhtml_page_588}this Spirit (1 Thess. 5:19). ["The snuffdishes"]{.class_s4N2-0} and ["the tongs"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 38) are for [trimming ]{.class_s4N4-0}the lamps one at a time; [at no time are all seven lamps to be extinguished]{.class_s4N4-0} (27:20). (There is a Roman picture of this candlestick on the Arch of Triumph \[Titus\], and it is pictured as being about three feet high, although it may have been larger.)

When the priest entered the Holy Place, the first thing he saw was the seven-pronged candlestick. Coming from broad daylight---the natural revelation of Solomon ["under the sun"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Eccl. 1:3)---he was at first blinded by the darkness, and if he did not believe in "God shining in a dark place" (2 Cor. 4:6), he would have turned around and walked out of the Holy Place. You see, he could not see the bread---[let alone EAT it]{.class_s4N4-0} (Luke 4:4)---until his eyes grew accustomed to the [supernatural light]{.class_s4N4-0} from "the tree of life." (Ah yes, there is more in the Bible than "meets the eye." What the "majority of Conservative scholars" call "riches and nuggets" are often nothing but burnt-out meteorites and clinkers.)

The candlestick stood on the priest's left (the south side) as he entered the Tabernacle. It was the ONLY light in the sanctuary, so once the flap of the tent closed behind him, the priest was abandoned to [three resources:]{.class_s4N4-0} the word of God (the Table), prayer (the Incense Altar), and the Holy Spirit (the Candlestick). These turn out to be the three spiritual resources for the spiritual man; growth in grace (2 Pet. 3:18) is determined by the use of these three means. God does not want the [natural light]{.class_s4N4-0} of the natural man[ "under the sun"]{.class_s4N2-0} giving "light" by "shedding new light" on the "ancient manuscripts" through archaeological findings "brought to light" by the "light of modern Conservative scholarship," etc. He wants Joseph to give God the glory (Gen. 41:16) and []{#c307.xhtml_page_589}the Christian to start [believing the Bible]{.class_s4N4-0} instead of the conceited asses who make a living off Christian education by substituting [scholarship ]{.class_s4N4-0}for [spiritual understanding.]{.class_s4N4-0}

(Before you cry "heresy" or "Thiemism," you'd better get you a mirror and take a long look. [James 1:23--24 is for you. ]{.class_s4N4-0}The "hobbyhorse" riders in America are not the people who defend the Bible as the word of God; the hobbyhorse riders are the ones who devote their ministries to [altering the God-honored text to meet the demands of the Roman Catholic Bible of 1582,]{.class_s4N4-0} via Souter, Metzger, Aland, Robertson, Nestle, Tregelles, Tischendorf, Griesbach, Lachmann, Milligan, Jerome, Aquila, Moulton, Thayer, Vincent, Davis, and the Lockman Foundation. These are the man-following menpleasers who accuse Bible believers of ["following a man."]{.class_s4N4-0} THEY ARE LIARS.)

["And look that thou make them"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 40). No substitutes or "facsimiles" or "reasonably accurate translations" will do. It is to be ["after their pattern, which was shewed thee," ]{.class_s4N2-0}and when God shows an unsaved man the right Book and [saves him]{.class_s4N4-0} with that Book and then [calls him into the ministry]{.class_s4N4-0} with that Book, He does not intend for the man to alter that pattern and invent a "better one." Any candlestick other than the one prescribed by Moses was ["another spirit" ]{.class_s4N2-0}and ["another gospel" ]{.class_s4N2-0}and ["another Jesus"]{.class_s4N2-0} (2 Cor. 11:4); the Lord would have never used it to "give light" on anything. The moral of the story is clear: if the Christian is to shine like a light in a dark world (2 Pet. 1:19), he must consult the light fed with "olive oil" (1 John 2:27), and where that light sheds light on the open Book (2 Cor. 3:14--17), the fanatical ravings of "recognized Greek and []{#c307.xhtml_page_590}Hebrew scholars" are to be set aside exactly as one would set aside the remains of a dead cat.

[]{#c356.xhtml}

# Chapter 26 {.heading_s2W-0}

26:1 "Moreover thou shalt make the tabernacle with ten curtains of fine twined linen, and blue, and purple, and scarlet: with cherubims of cunning work shalt thou make them.

2 The length of one curtain shall be eight and twenty cubits, and the breadth of one curtain four cubits: and every one of the curtains shall have one measure.

3 The five curtains shall be coupled together one to another; and other five curtains shall be coupled one to another.

4 And thou shalt make loops of blue upon the edge of the one curtain from the selvedge in the coupling; and likewise shalt thou make in the uttermost edge of another curtain, in the coupling of the second.

5 Fifty loops shalt thou make in the one curtain, and fifty loops shalt thou make in the edge of the curtain that is in the coupling of the second; that the loops may take hold one of another.

6 And thou shalt make fifty taches of gold, and couple the curtains together with the taches: and it shall be one tabernacle."

The reader will observe that the Altar of Incense---representing prayer---was skipped in chapter 25. The reason for this is quite obvious to the devout student who has learned to ignore inferior "Bibles" like the [NASV]{.class_s4N4-0} and the [NRSV. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Chapter 25 is dealing with the Trinity. The Altar of Incense (though it stood for [the ministry of the Spirit in intercession]{.class_s4N4-0}) is dealing with [man's approach to God;]{.class_s4N4-0} this comes under the heading of the [Priesthood ]{.class_s4N4-0}and its mediating place in Israel's approach to God. Thus, []{#c356.xhtml_page_592}the Altar of Incense shows up in chapter 30, [after ]{.class_s4N4-0}a description of Aaron and his sons (chaps. 28--29).

The deluded scribes of 1880--1901 ([RV ]{.class_s4N4-0}under Wescott and Hort and [ASV]{.class_s4N4-0} under Philip Schaff) missed this fundamental Old Testament truth, so we find them saying that "no sufficient reason has been shown for the existing order, which hence appears ACCIDENTAL" ([Pulpit Commentary,]{.class_s4N4-0} pg. 303). Dummelow calls the absence of the Altar of Incense from chapters 25--26 "somewhat strange" and informs us that the "whole of chapters 30--31 is believed by some to be a later addition" (Commentary, pg. 79). The Samaritan text also "helps God out." It takes out the first ten verses of the chapter and [reinserts ]{.class_s4N4-0}them back at verse 32, where "they should have been" all along, etc.

We know all about this family of "helpers." We have traced their bare feet, moonshine, shotguns, whiskey stills, revenue stamps, copper tubes, bags of sugar, malt mash, hound dogs, and "tailor mades" through Genesis, Acts, Proverbs, Matthew, Revelation, Galatians, Ephesians, Philippians, and Colossians; and we know a bootlegger when we see one. The real "hillbillies" in America are not the country folk from the high country of North Carolina and Tennessee; the real hicks are the most highly educated "Christians" in the country who rest in the highest places of "Christian" education.

The placement of the Altar of Incense (chap. 30) [is perfectly placed by God Himself,]{.class_s4N4-0} and any alteration only reveals the carnality and ignorance of the scribe who has assumed (temporarily) the third place in the Trinity for the purpose of perverting the truth. Did you fail to notice that even though the Ark was mentioned (chap. 25), not one word was said about its use as an [approach to God?]{.class_s4N4-0} []{#c356.xhtml_page_593}That material came later---chapters 28--29. The Altar of Incense (30:1--10) is talking about the [application of blood to an object in the Tabernacle]{.class_s4N4-0} (30:10). Not one word was said about this in regards to the Ark (or the Mercy Seat) in chapter 25. The [King James ]{.class_s4N4-0}Bible has the correct order, and any alteration of this order is disorder.

Henry W. Soltau has published a lengthy work on [The Tabernacle]{.class_s4N4-0} (Kregel Pub., Grand Rapids, MI) which is as good as any in describing the spiritual lessons found in the tent of testimony. As we have said before (under 25:1--9), the Tabernacle is a picture of the [universe]{.class_s4N4-0}, not a picture of the Lord Jesus Christ; although spiritual similitudes can be found, the typology cannot be pressed very far. Neuman, Friedrich, and Gotha (1861, 1841, and 1861) wrote much of the source material used by later writers (1870--1970) on the typology of the Tabernacle. Any standard work in that period will give you the general idea of the "types of Christ" found in the construction of the Tabernacle proper.

The number "five," which shows up in multiples ([fifty ]{.class_s4N4-0}boards, fifteen bars, [fifty ]{.class_s4N4-0}loops, one hundred sockets, [fifty ]{.class_s4N4-0}taches, roof coverings of [fifteen ]{.class_s4N4-0}yards in length, etc.), are said to picture "GRACE" ([Williams Commentary,]{.class_s4N4-0} pg. 57), but any Bible believer knows that the whole operation from the altar of burnt offering ([five by five]{.class_s4N4-0}) to the Mercy Seat represents DEATH (see the detailed analysis under Gen. 5:5 in the [Commentary on Genesis]{.class_s4N4-0}). This is one of these matters upon which there is no doubt, although every Conservative Premillennialist from 1800--1970 said that "five" meant "grace." They are wrong (100%), and that can be proved by (or without) the Scripture with more ease than one can prove a pre-Tribulation Rapture.

Since this commentary is not illustrated, every believer should try to obtain a copy of Clarence Larkin's [Dispensational Truth. ]{.class_s4N4-0}There he will find drawings of the outer court, the altar, the laver, the Holy Place, the coverings, the boards, and the Holy of Holies (with the furniture); Larkin's drawings are as good as any that have been made on the matters. We cannot prove that the Tabernacle had a peaked roof with a [ridgepole ]{.class_s4N4-0}as a regular tent, although it would appear that way. Furthermore, we cannot prove that it was staked out exactly like a regular tent, but the nature of its covering and the ["pins" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(27:19) would indicate some kind of "pinning to the ground" would have been necessary. The Tabernacle itself was oblong or rectangular, but it may have had a gable running the length of it, over which a tent was spread. There are eight tabernacles in the Scripture, which match the temple of Revelation 11:19.

1\. [The Mosaic Tabernacle]{.class_s4N4-0} (1500--1000 B.C.).

2\. [Solomon's Temple]{.class_s4N4-0} (1000--586 B .C .) .

3\. [Zerubbabel's Temple]{.class_s4N4-0} (516--40 B.C.).

4\. [Herod's Temple]{.class_s4N4-0} (40 B.C.--A.D. 70).

5\. [Christ's Body]{.class_s4N4-0} (4 B.C.--A.D. 33).

6\. [The Christian's body]{.class_s4N4-0} (A.D. 33--Rapture).

7\. [The Tribulation temple]{.class_s4N4-0} (2 Thess. 2:3--4).

8\. [The NEW]{.class_s4N4-0} (eight is new!) [temple ]{.class_s4N4-0}in the Millennium (Ezek. 40--46).

This oblong tent is 30x10 cubits, and it stood within ["the court of the tabernacle"]{.class_s4N2-0} (27:9--14). Four layers of coverings went over this tent, and they are given as ["fine linen," "goats' hair," "rams' skins,"]{.class_s4N2-0} and[ "badgers' skins"]{.class_s4N2-0} (see 25:4--5, and comments). Williams tells us that all of this represents Christ as a [Servant ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Mark)---the badger skin, Christ as [King ]{.class_s4N4-0}[]{#c356.xhtml_page_595 .class-1}(Matthew)---the ram skin dyed red, Christ as [Priest ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Luke)---the goat hair, and Christ as [God ]{.class_s4N4-0}(John)---fine linen. All of this is "very interesting" (to quote "Laugh In") but not particularly edifying nor particularly true. In the first place, Christ is [not ]{.class_s4N4-0}a Priest in Luke; His genealogy is traced to Adam as a MAN. In the second place, rams' skins are about as fitting for a King as sackcloth and ashes for Liberace. (We know when to quit; we'll quit here.)

["Thou shalt make the tabernacle with ten curtains of fine twined linen"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 1). There were ten curtains, each 42 feet long and six feet wide (vs. 2). When fastened together, they made a sheet sixty feet by forty feet (vs. 3) which was adorned with blue, purple, and scarlet needlework (vs. 1), made by weavers (35:35). The ["taches of gold"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 6) coupled these curtains together through ["loops"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 5), and they probably appeared as clasps fashioned like hooks that fit into eyelets. If the Tabernacle was 45\'x15\'x15\' (vss. 15--30), we have a covering that covers the roof and then extends down each side twelve-and one-half feet; that is, within two-and-one-half feet of the ground. (We are assuming here that the first covering is a flat roof.) The covering (being sixty feet long) would completely cover the length of the Tabernacle and then extend halfway down the front and back of the building. However, a separate hanging (vs. 36) is used for the front, clearly telling us that the sixty-foot linen covering dropped over the back the full fifteen feet to the ground.

Typology is apparent. ["Fine linen]{.class_s4NC}[ is the righteousness of saints"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rev. 19:8) and it matches Christ's personal righteousness where it has been imputed (Rom. 3:24--25). It is associated with the []{#c356.xhtml_page_596}cherubim (vs. 1), for it is connected with the essence of God on the Mercy Seat (see remarks under 25:18--20). The ["cunning work"]{.class_s4N2-0} of verse 1 is clearly defined in 2 Chronicles 2:7--15 and Exodus 35:35. There is no need to "update" the word simply because Christians are too lazy to read the Bible through. The righteousness is INSIDE the Tabernacle (cf. Matt. 23:27).

26:7 "And thou shalt make curtains of goats' hair to be a covering upon the tabernacle: eleven curtains shalt thou make.

8 The length of one curtain shall be thirty cubits, and the breadth of one curtain four cubits: and the eleven curtains shall be all of one measure.

9 And thou shalt couple five curtains by themselves, and six curtains by themselves, and shalt double the sixth curtain in the forefront of the tabernacle.

10 And thou shalt make fifty loops on the edge of the one curtain that is outmost in the coupling, and fifty loops in the edge of the curtain which coupleth the second.

11 And thou shalt make fifty taches of brass, and put the taches into the loops, and couple the tent together, that it may be one.

12 And the remnant that remaineth of the curtains of the tent, the half curtain that remaineth, shall hang over the backside of the tabernacle.

13 And a cubit on the one side, and a cubit on the other side of that which remaineth in the length of the curtains of the tent, it shall hang []{#c356.xhtml_page_597}over the sides of the tabernacle on this side and on that side, to cover it."

The following instructions all apply to the next three layers since the ["badgers' skins,"]{.class_s4N2-0} the ["rams' skins,"]{.class_s4N2-0} and the ["goats' hair"]{.class_s4N2-0} are all mentioned in verses 7 and 14. Hair symbolizes [separation from the world,]{.class_s4N4-0} and it marked Israel as bearing a shame and reproach, for the Gentiles cut their hair (1 Cor. 11:14). The ["man of sorrows"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Isa. 53:3) wore long hair and hung on a tree, but so did [Absalom ]{.class_s4N4-0}(2 Sam. 18:9), and Absalom---as any modern "revolutionary"---was dedicated to overthrowing the establishment (2 Sam. 15:1--12). He seems also to have enjoyed kissing [men ]{.class_s4N4-0}(2 Sam. 15:5), as did Judas (Matt. 26:48). (Ah, yes, there is more in the English text than Keil, Gunkel, Delitzsch, Thieme, Wuest, DeWette, Bewer, Rendall, Salmon, Alford, Davidson, Ellicott, Lange, Pfeiffer, Clarke, Bullinger, Dummelow, Sanday, Schaff, Schnedermann, Holtzmann, Lipsius, Luthard, Luken, Vincent, and Wordsworth \[and Satan\] would care for you to find out.)

["Goats' hair"]{.class_s4N2-0} plainly cuts the ["fine linen"]{.class_s4N2-0} off from that which is ["under the sun"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Num. 6:7; Lev. 13:30, 42). If the ["goats' hair"]{.class_s4N2-0} is the same as that used for ["the tents of Kedar"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Song of Sol. 1:5), it is as ["black as a raven"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Song of Sol. 5:11). The curtain of ["goats' hair"]{.class_s4N2-0} was three feet wider than the linen, being 66 feet by 45 feet. It was fastened by brass taches (vs. 11), instead of gold taches, and it would have covered both sides of the Tabernacle completely, since the Tabernacle had fifteen foot sides and a fifteen foot breadth to the roof. Sixty-six feet in length would leave 21 feet to completely cover the back end of the Tabernacle westward and []{#c356.xhtml_page_598}yet leave another six feet to hang over down the front eastward.

The same measurements stand for the ["rams' skins"]{.class_s4N2-0} and the badger covering (vs. 14), and by putting all three covers on, we have a waterproof tent, gold on the inside and brass on the outside. With a ridgepole (or gable), the hangings would not have reached quite so far down, and in this case, they would have been staked out with tent pins (see Isa. 54:2, for example).

Now, we cannot locate all of this typology for certain. Soltau says that the goats were offered on the Day of Atonement (Num. 29:11) as a sin offering for Israel (Num. 7) and on the consecration of the priesthood (Lev. 9). In the former case, a prince representing each tribe made the offering, thus connecting each tribe with the Levitical priesthood. We may say that the ["goats' hair"]{.class_s4N2-0} is a reminder that we are connected with our High Priest, that He offered Himself for us as a sin offering to atone for our sins. But of course, this is all typified more clearly in the priesthood of Aaron and his sons, and in the altar of burnt offering, so the sermon is a little "over-exegeted."

26:15 "And thou shalt make boards for the tabernacle of shittim wood standing up.

16 Ten cubits shall be the length of a board, and a cubit and a half shall be the breadth of one board.

17 Two tenons shall there be in one board, set in order one against another: thus shalt thou make for all the boards of the tabernacle.

18 And thou shalt make the boards for the tabernacle, twenty boards on the south side southward.

19 And thou shalt make forty sockets of silver under the twenty boards; two sockets under one board for his two tenons, and two sockets under another board for his two tenons.

20 And for the second side of the tabernacle on the north side there shall be twenty boards:

21 And their forty sockets of silver; two sockets under one board, and two sockets under another board.

22 And for the sides of the tabernacle westward thou shalt make six boards.

23 And two boards shalt thou make for the corners of the tabernacle in the two sides.

24 And they shall be coupled together beneath, and they shall be coupled together above the head of it unto one ring: thus shall it be for them both; they shall be for the two corners.

25 And they shall be eight boards, and their sockets of silver, sixteen sockets; two sockets under one board, and two sockets under another board."

The construction of the Tabernacle proper would resemble "pine paneling" in a modern home, or one of the "redwood fences" one sees around ranch-style homes. The sheets of shittim wood are set against each other vertically so that the wall of the Tabernacle is fifteen feet high (figuring eighteen inches to the cubit; Clarke figures a little higher). We are not told how thick the panels were, but like pine-paneling, they were probably []{#c356.xhtml_page_600}less than one-half inch thick. If they were thicker than this, they may have been a "lattice" work (Prof. Kennedy) rather than solid boards. We are told that each board was overlaid with [gold ]{.class_s4N4-0}(vs. 29). If the frames were solid, it meant that \$1,250,000 worth of gold was used up in the construction of the Tabernacle and its furniture.

Further, it would mean that the Tabernacle was a load to carry, for sheets of wood fifteen feet by 27 inches overlaid with gold would have loaded down Gershon and Merari (Num. 10:17) pretty well. However, we read of ["covered wagons"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Num. 7:3) for handling these matters, so they are not outside the bounds of reason. One can assume they were solid, thin sheets, thinly covered with gold or a lattice work heavily gold-plated. Song of Solomon 2:9 would indicate "lattice work" like a window, but again, we are caught in the meshes of a Bible metaphysics and astronomy which are considerably in advance of anything that has been going on at Houston or Canaveral in the last twenty years.

["Two tenons"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 17). As the curtains were joined together, so the boards are all held together by bars running through the "tenons" which coupled them horizontally (vs. 26). The bottoms of the boards (running fifteen feet high) are mounted on silver sockets stuck into the ground (vs. 21). According to Moorehead ([The Tabernacle,]{.class_s4N4-0} Kregel Publications, 1957, pg. 37), the silver came to about \$200,000. The middle bar runs the length of the Tabernacle through tenons in the centers of the "paneling" (vs. 28), and the other four bars (two above it and two below) couple pairs of panels together (vss. 26--29).

26:26 "And thou shalt make bars of shittim wood; five for the boards of the one side of the tabernacle,

27 And five bars for the boards of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the boards of the side of the tabernacle, for the two sides westward.

28 And the middle bar in the midst of the boards shall reach from end to end.

29 And thou shalt overlay the boards with gold, and make their rings of gold for places for the bars: and thou shalt overlay the bars with gold.

30 And thou shalt rear up the tabernacle according to the fashion thereof which was shewed thee in the mount."

The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} (and Dummelow) flounder around on verses 23--24 and "help God out" by altering the translation to suit their fancy. Naturally, the [New American Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} and the Communist [Revised Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} of 1952 try their hand at "straightening the Holy Spirit out." According to the history of textual corruption, the first "Bible" to incorporate these "helps to the reader" was the one produced by Philip Schaff, Westcott and Hort, and Bishop Lightfoot---the Roman Catholic [RV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1881--1885), along with its counterpart the [ASV]{.class_s4N4-0} (1901).

In the confused mess that follows (notice 36:29 in the [ASV, RSV, RV,]{.class_s4N4-0} and [NASV]{.class_s4N4-0} recommended by Bob Jones University), we come out with the wild thought that double boards are stuck [under the framework of the Tabernacle,]{.class_s4N4-0} and then two up the side of the Tabernacle till they [squeeze into a ring]{.class_s4N4-0} at the top. Nothing could []{#c356.xhtml_page_602}be more balled up unless it was planned by the United Nations Assembly.

What the text says, in plain English for any English-speaking man to grasp, is that the EAST end of the Tabernacle [had no corner]{.class_s4N4-0} unless two boards were placed there. In their haste to correct the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}Text used by the Holy Spirit, the commentators have invented every "cotton picking" thing from "buttresses" (Dummelow) to "some way of strengthening" ([Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0}) so they could alter one of the greatest truths in the Bible. You see, ["the head of the corner"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Matt. 21:42) is a great truth, so when we find ["together above the head\...they shall be for the two corners"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 24), we know where we are even if Lange, Sanday, Clarke, Bullinger, Dummelow, Kennedy, Prideaux, Shaw, Keil, Delitzsch, Payne, Archer, and Soltau do not. (Believing the English text always gives the Christian a head start on the Hebrew and Oriental scholars whose hobbyhorse is trying to get rid of it.)

You see, the ["it"]{.class_s4N2-0} of verse 24 (["above the head of it"]{.class_s4N2-0}) was plainly a reference to a completed Tabernacle (vs. 23, ["for the corners of the tabernacle in the two sides"]{.class_s4N2-0}). Well, there are only [two ]{.class_s4N4-0}sides. No front has been allowed for; the only directions given were south (vs. 18), north (vs. 20) and west (vs. 22). The two boards, then, are obviously to turn the [open sides]{.class_s4N4-0} on the east end, and they are joined by one bar that runs horizontally across the front of the Tabernacle through rings in the tops and bottoms of the boards. You will never get it that clear if you spend a lifetime in the Hebrew Old Testament with the Samaritan Pentateuch and the Targums right smack in front of your schnazolla. What would the ["hanging"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 36) do at the corners of the eastern end []{#c356.xhtml_page_603}of the Tabernacle with nothing to keep it from blowing back into the Holy Place?

When in doubt, throw all of the commentators out. O-U-T! OUT!

Jesus Christ is ["the head of the corner"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 Pet. 2:7); and it is remarkable how all of our "devotional," "spiritual" exegetes, who major in looking for "the deeper things in the Scripture" (while transferring the authority of Hermes and Mercury to themselves instead of the Holy Spirit), took time out to mess up a comparison that should have been obvious to them. After all, He said ["I AM THE DOOR"]{.class_s4N2-0} (John 10:9); why would He not be found [where one entered the Tabernacle?]{.class_s4N4-0}

Eight additional boards furnish the "buttresses" about which the commentators had so much to say. There are plainly [two extra boards for each corner]{.class_s4N4-0} of four corners (vs. 25): not the back TWO corners, as given by all of the major commentators. [What would be the point in reinforcing two strong corners and leaving the two weakest corners open?]{.class_s4N4-0} But that shows what a "major in Oriental languages" can do for you if you let it.

We have already commented on verses 26--30.

26:31 "And thou shalt make a vail of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen of cunning work: with cherubims shall it be made:

32 And thou shalt hang it upon four pillars of shittim wood overlaid with gold: their hooks shall be of gold, upon the four sockets of silver.

33 And thou shalt hang up the vail under the taches, that thou mayest bring in thither within the vail the ark of the testimony: and the vail shall []{#c356.xhtml_page_604}divide unto you between the holy place and the most holy.

34 And thou shalt put the mercy seat upon the ark of the testimony in the most holy place.

35 And thou shalt set the table without the vail, and the candlestick over against the table on the side of the tabernacle toward the south: and thou shalt put the table on the north side.

36 And thou shalt make an hanging for the door of the tent, of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen, wrought with needlework.

37 And thou shalt make for the hanging five pillars of shittim wood, and overlay them with gold, and their hooks shall be of gold: and thou shalt cast five sockets of brass for them."

All is self-explanatory. Four pillars of shittim wood divide the Tabernacle into two compartments: one is the Holy Place (twenty by ten cubits), and the other is the Holy of Holies (ten by ten cubits). The four pillars are ["overlaid with gold"]{.class_s4N2-0}[ ]{.class_s4N4-0}(vs. 32), and they have hooks of [gold ]{.class_s4N4-0}(as the curtains of linen) and ["sockets of silver" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 32), as the sockets of the Tabernacle proper. The hooks are to hang the ["vail of blue, and purple, and scarlet"]{.class_s4N2-0} on, and this veil is hung up directly beneath the golden ["taches"]{.class_s4N2-0} of 26:6 where they cross the roof over the division of the two compartments.

The ["vail"]{.class_s4N2-0} matches the curtain which covers the Tabernacle proper (vs. 31), and we have already remarked on its typology. (The veil of Herod's Temple was supposed to have been sixty feet high, four inches thick, and rewoven yearly.)

The ["hanging for the door of the tent"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 36) also matches the partition between the Holy and the Most Holy places. Three differences are noted. The front ["hanging"]{.class_s4N2-0} is ["needlework" ]{.class_s4N2-0}only; it is mounted on [five ]{.class_s4N4-0}pillars of wood on brass sockets (vss. 36--37), while the inner veil has ["cunning\...cherubim"]{.class_s4N2-0} and is mounted on [four ]{.class_s4N4-0}pillars on silver sockets (vss. 31--32).

On entering the Holy Place, we would find the Table of the Showbread on our right (the north), the Candlestick on our left (the south) and directly ahead, before the inner veil, the Altar of Incense (west). Behind this veil we would find the Ark of the Covenant covered with the Mercy Seat and the cherubims (vs. 34).

(Until here, the Altar of Incense has not been mentioned, for it clearly is connected with the sinner's APPROACH to God. The Candlestick, Table of Showbread, and the Ark have nothing to do with the actual approach of the people to God; they are in the Tabernacle, representing the Godhead \[see comments under 26:1 and 30:1--10\]. We shall say more about these objects when we begin to study the actual approach of the sinner to God through the Tabernacle.)

Naturally, one can "find Christ in the Tabernacle" if types are searched out. In the first place, the Tabernacle was "without form or comeliness," and there was nothing beautiful about it ["that we should desire"]{.class_s4N2-0} it (Isa. 53:1--2). It appeared in a ["waste howling wilderness"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Deut. 32:10) as a large [grey coffin;]{.class_s4N4-0} at best, a large grey tent (assuming the center pillar of the front five pillars extended higher than fifteen feet). To the natural eye, Jesus is undesirable (Isa. 53:1--2), but to the eye of faith, He is ["the lily of the valleys\...the chiefest among ten []{#c356.xhtml_page_606}thousand\...altogether lovely"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Song of Sol. 2:1, 5:10, 16). But you have to get IN HIM to APPRECIATE HIM (Col. 2:10--12). Once inside the Tabernacle, a man was illuminated by the Holy Spirit who "indwelt the temple" (1 Cor. 6:19) by the seven-pronged Candlestick.

Looking overhead and on either side, a man would find [pure white walls]{.class_s4N4-0} and a ceiling adorned with a beautiful ["needlework"]{.class_s4N2-0} of blue, purple, scarlet, and figures of cherubims. Any light slipping in under the bottom of the "paneling" had to reflect on SILVER, for the whole structure rested on a silver foundation. Silver is plainly the price of [redemption ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Zech. 11:13). If the white wall had been lifted, it would have revealed solid gold underneath, exactly as the [outer garments]{.class_s4N4-0} of the Christian (Rev. 19:8) are connected with his own works, while [his inner garments]{.class_s4N4-0} next to his skin (don't forget Psa. 45:13) are the IMPUTED RIGHTEOUSNESS OF JESUS CHRIST.

Ah, the unlimited treasures of the Authorized English Version! How excruciating are its judgments and its absolute authority past finding out.

Made out of incorruptible wood, the Tabernacle pictured Christ's [humanity]{.class_s4N4-0}. ["Overlaid with gold,"]{.class_s4N2-0} it pictured His [deity]{.class_s4N4-0}. You entered it east to west, exactly as history moves east to west. The Son and the sun (Mal. 4:1--2) move contrary to the world---east to west.

In closing the account, notice the obtuse theorizing of the "Conservative commentators" in approaching the problem of directions. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown tell us the purpose of the priests entering the Tabernacle from the east was to turn their back on the sun, since the sun was worshipped by heathen nations; the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} tells us that the south was [more honorable]{.class_s4N4-0} []{#c356.xhtml_page_607}than the north since Orientals were told to face east; others say that east to west was "natural" since the sun moved that way. Bible believers who are acquainted with Psalm 48:2; Ezekiel 1:4, 8:3; Genesis 3:24, 4:16, 12:1--4; and the verses that deal with the Second Advent (Acts 1:9--12; Zech. 14:1--4) know that EAST to WEST is the only possible direction [on the ground]{.class_s4N4-0} and SOUTH to NORTH is the only possible direction "up" [in the universe. ]{.class_s4N4-0}(But of course, all of this is obscured to the faculty members of Conservative schools whose hobbyhorse is messing up the words of God.)

[Five]{.class_s4N4-0} pillars face the sinner coming into the court of the Tabernacle. It spells death for him, so there is no way of "getting into Christ" without death (Rom. 6:3--8). Grace has nothing to do with the number [five]{.class_s4N4-0}, nor will it ever till heaven and earth have vanished. Five pillars face a sacrificial altar that is 5x5 (see chap. 27 and comments), and as sure as there are five wounds in the body of Jesus Christ, and as sure as He left five pieces of clothes to His enemies at Calvary, five equals DEATH. In only one case in 9,000,000,000 did it ever mean "grace," and "the exception [proves ]{.class_s4N4-0}the rule" to any man except an evolutionist or an international race-mixer. (Race-mixers, Communists, Socialists, pragmatists, and evolutionists say: "the exception OVERTHROWS the rule.")

In the next four chapters we take up the means and ways whereby the Old Testament sinner in ["the commonwealth of Israel"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Eph. 2:12) could approach the Holy of Holies and ["plead the blood" ]{.class_s4N2-0}for his transgressions.

[]{#c37X.xhtml}

# [Chapter 27]{.class_s4NN} {.heading_s2E2-1}

27:1 "And thou shalt make an altar of shittim wood, five cubits long, and five cubits broad; the altar shall be foursquare: and the height thereof shall be three cubits.

2 And thou shalt make the horns of it upon the four corners thereof: his horns shall be of the same: and thou shalt overlay it with brass.

3 And thou shalt make his pans to receive his ashes, and his shovels, and his basins, and his fleshhooks, and his firepans: all the vessels thereof thou shalt make of brass.

4 And thou shalt make for it a grate of network of brass; and upon the net shalt thou make four brasen rings in the four corners thereof.

5 And thou shalt put it under the compass of the altar beneath, that the net may be even to the midst of the altar.

6 And thou shalt make staves for the altar, staves of shittim wood, and overlay them with brass.

7 And the staves shall be put into the rings, and the staves shall be upon the two sides of the altar, to bear it.

8 Hollow with boards shalt thou make it: as it was shewed thee in the mount, so shall they make it."

We now behold the first thing that any Israelite would see if he came to the Tabernacle bringing an offering. (The sweet offerings included burnt offerings, meat offerings, peace offerings, sin offerings, and trespass offerings \[Lev. 1--8\].) The [sin offerings]{.class_s4N4-0} came first (chap. 29), while the sweet offerings were listed later in Leviticus. They had to be brought voluntarily (Lev. 1:3) []{#c37X.xhtml_page_609}and had to be free from malformations and disease (Mal. 1:8, 13), whether they were goats, beeves, sheep, turtle doves, or pigeons (Lev. 5:7, 9, Lev. 12:6--8).

There was ["a continual burnt offering" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(29:42) morning and evening on the altar of the three grades of sacrifices described in Leviticus 1:3, 10, 14. Instructions for the disposal of the sacrificial victim are found in Leviticus 7:8 and Leviticus chapter 1. With the exception of the skin---a type of Adamic flesh---the burnt offering was given entirely to God. Ephesians 5:2 points out this burnt offering as a type of Christ's sacrifice, and the alteration of Leviticus 1:9 by Origen, Aquila, Symmachus, and others---[many years after the completion of the New Testament]{.class_s4N4-0}---to match Ephesians 5:2 reinforces what we have said many times about the so-called "[Septuagint]{.class_s4N4-0}."

The [meat offering]{.class_s4N4-0} (see Gen. 40:17 for definition of "meat"; note the [RV, ASV, NASV,]{.class_s4N4-0} or similar corruptions) consisted of unbaked flour (Lev. 2:1), baked loaves or cakes (Lev. 2:4--10), and green ears of corn, parched or roasted (Lev. 2:14). Leaven and honey were forbidden in these offerings because the offerings picture the Lord Jesus and remind us of a spotless life (Matt. 16:6; Luke 12:1; 1 Cor. 5:6--8) and a life of sorrow and grief (Isa. 53:1--6).

The [Brazen Altar]{.class_s4N4-0} of shittim wood (not the "copper altar" of "acacia" wood) was a hollow frame, brass-plated altar, about seven-and-one-half feet square and four-and-one-half feet high. The grating which hung down [inside ]{.class_s4N4-0}it is so significant that the [Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} and Bullinger immediately remove it so that it will not be connected with the Mercy Seat. Instead, Dr. Johnson (a "leading Conservative") tells us that the grating was OUTSIDE the altar and the priests stood on it. That is, []{#c37X.xhtml_page_610}when God clearly shows us a great devotional truth of a spiritual nature, the "recognized scholar" gets rid of it; so why would God bother to show some of [you ]{.class_s4N4-0}anything to start with? (You would undoubtedly cast the pearl to pigs and swine, so God just reserves His deepest truths for those who believe His Book.)

Dummelow divides the difference between the obvious truth and the obvious lie, and ducks out of the tent of testimony, under one flap, by saying: "the position and purpose of this grating are not [clear]{.class_s4N4-0}." Clarke gives us some truth from "Bishop Patrick," but then adds quickly---to save face with the Lockman Foundation, the Scofield Board of Editors, the Revision Committees of 1885 and 1901, and Satan---that "even on this there is a great [variety of opinion."]{.class_s4N4-0} In spite of the "great variety of opinion," Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown got it right this time (for a change), while the [Pulpit Commentary,]{.class_s4N4-0} as usual, subscribes to the guesswork of some traditional critic---this time it is Knobel---and says that the grating was just "an ornament."

Intelligent readers who have learned to trust the Bible instead of "Christian education" have observed that:

1\. The grating is an object with rings mounted on it (vs. 4) for hanging on something.

2\. It is placed ["UNDER the compass of the altar"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 5)---["beneath"]{.class_s4N2-0}; not ["around about" ]{.class_s4N4-0}or on the ["sides of "]{.class_s4N4-0} the altar.

3\. It is a ["net"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 4) as well as a grating, and it hangs ["even to the midst of the altar"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 5).

4\. Four horns on the corners of the altar are made to support the four rings which are joined to this network of brass grating.

Now, where are you at this point if you believe the Bible text? Well, you certainly are not staggering around in a jungle or swamp of "various opinions." The only way a "variety of opinions" can crop up (see Rev. 11:2--6 in the [Commentary on Revelation]{.class_s4N4-0}) is by altering the God-honored text and trying to prove that you are smarter than God. (These are two things we will never do in this commentary---or any commentary---and that is why this set of books bears the truthful title: [The Bible BELIEVER'S Commentary. ]{.class_s4N4-0}This may sound like conceit to the dead-orthodox apostates whose hobbyhorse is getting rid of the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}text[,]{.class_s4N4-0} but the Judgment Seat of Christ will straighten a lot of these gentry out in no uncertain terms.)

["And thou shalt make the horns of it"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 2). Disregarding Dummelow's comments (he says that the only passage in relation to binding things to them---Psa. 118:27---is corrupt), we find that the horns were for:

1\. [Holding up the brazen grating]{.class_s4N4-0} which sank down into the altar.

2\. [Tying sacrifices]{.class_s4N4-0} to them when they were "wholly" for God (Psa. 118:27).

3\. Clearly associating (in the sinner's mind) the connection between the HORNED CHERUB who fell (see the [Commentary on Genesis,]{.class_s4N4-0} Gen. 3:1) and the fires of Hell which will go up "continually" ([the smoke of their torment ascendeth up for ever and ever; and they have no rest day nor night,"]{.class_s4N2-0} Rev. 14:11).

Somehow or another, all of the major Conservative "Fundamentalists" forgot their "orthodoxy" when they got near the altar. That is, they "absquattulated" on the first object they encountered upon entering the court. Some of them have gone into great detail about "sin and []{#c37X.xhtml_page_612}trespass offerings." Moorhead has a little to say about God's wrath in connection with the sacrifice of Christ, but where are the references on the [Lake of Fire?]{.class_s4N4-0}

Where are the references that tell you that Jesus Christ suffered the agonies of eternal punishment in FLAMES for your sins? In view of Genesis 22:6; Exodus 12:9--10; Leviticus 9:24; Numbers 16:35; Deuteronomy 32:22; Psalm 140:10; Matthew 25:41; Isaiah 66:24; etc., how does one explain the flippant dismissal of Bible truth on HELL where it is plainly represented at the Brazen Altar? [Dummelow?]{.class_s4N4-0} Silence. [Jamieson?]{.class_s4N4-0} Silence. [Fausset?]{.class_s4N4-0} Silence. [Brown?]{.class_s4N4-0} Silence. [Bullinger?]{.class_s4N4-0} Silence. [Clarke?]{.class_s4N4-0} Silence. L[ange?]{.class_s4N4-0} Silence. [Henry?]{.class_s4N4-0} Silence. [Johnson?]{.class_s4N4-0} Silence. [Williams?]{.class_s4N4-0} Silence. [Delitzsch?]{.class_s4N4-0} Silence. [Keil?]{.class_s4N4-0} Silence.

Now this is what we mean when we talk about "dead-orthodox Conservatives." There are no soulwinners in the list given above, even though the Scriptures state that the soulwinner is the wise man (Prov. 11:30), not the College professor. Why all this silence on an object that pictures the greatest issue for the sinner this side of the grave (see Rom. 9:3; 1 Cor. 9:22)? How did all of our "godly, dedicated, devoted Conservatives" who "searched long and hard in the original languages" possibly err in their expositions and exegesis at this point? The answer is simple: when a "godly, dedicated, consecrated, holy, separated, blameless Conservative" sets out to destroy [the authority of the AV (1611),]{.class_s4N4-0} he can err in [every verse]{.class_s4N4-0} he tackles.

We have a common superstition among Fundamentalists which is quite similar to the "apostolic succession of the vicars of Christ" among papists. It is to the effect that if a "[majority ]{.class_s4N4-0}of godly dedicated []{#c37X.xhtml_page_613}Conservatives" agree on altering the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}text[,]{.class_s4N4-0} that THEY are right and the man who[ believes the text]{.class_s4N4-0} as it stands is wrong. Vast experience with this type of "Fundamentalist" will prove to the honest investigator that all these alterations in text were made because of pride, jealousy, ignorance, or the fear of man; there has not been one major, legitimate alteration made on this text since 1800.

"Mister five-by-five" (vs. 1) is clearly the ["Death, and Hell"]{.class_s4N2-0} of Revelation 6:8, at least typically. No sinner who ever looked into the coals of a barbecue pit or the burning embers of a beach fire failed to be "turned on" by its hypnotic power.
