There is something about the deep white heat of burning coals (Psa. 140:10), where something has burned (Ezek. 20:47), that forces the [Conservative ]{.class_s4N4-0}Greek exegete to say that Luke 16:23 is a ["parable. "]{.class_s4N4-0}

["Pans"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 3) were for carrying ashes around, ["shovels"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 3) were for the same thing, ["basons"]{.class_s4N2-0} were for collecting blood before it was sprinkled, ["fleshhooks" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 3 cf. 1 Sam. 2:13) were for handling the meat while it was roasting, and ["firepans"]{.class_s4N2-0} were for transportation of the burning coals and burning embers. We have already commented on verses 4 and 5. The Altar (as the Table and the Ark) was portable, being carried about on staves overlaid with brass (vs. 6).

This [Brazen Altar]{.class_s4N4-0} could be fitted over the top of an [earthen altar]{.class_s4N4-0} (20:24) since it was ["hollow"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 8). It moved---to symbolize that the perpetual cleansing of blood was associated with God's presence in ANY location, under any condition. It saw the slaughter of well over 5,000 animals a year; hence, the Old Testament religion was called a "slaughter house religion" (Rom. []{#c37X.xhtml_page_614}8:36) or a "religion of blood" (Lev. 17:4--14) by the [jaded ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [depraved ]{.class_s4N4-0}evolutionists of the Nineteenth and Twentieth Centuries. We say jaded and depraved because a real evolutionist must believe in a far bloodier religion---"the survival of the fittest" and the "law of the jungle." Furthermore, this can be proved from history without going into a long thing.

The bloodiest murderers who ever lived---bar none---were [Roman Catholics ]{.class_s4N4-0}(A.D. 800--1800) and [Communists ]{.class_s4N4-0}(A.D. 1850--1990). Both of these groups had a "run-in" with the Holy Spirit, and when they locked horns with the Bible, they left the fracas determined to invent a substitute for the blood of the Lamb. The Catholics substituted a [jug of fermented liquor,]{.class_s4N4-0} and the Communists substituted the [Dialetical Materialism]{.class_s4N4-0} of Hegel and Marx. Communists and Catholics murder whole populations while trusting in wine and "social class leveling."

The real Christian will never murder anyone, for the blood he is trusting is not the blood shed by Socialists as they try to rid the world of "enemies of the people," nor is it a bottle of hootch elevated by a "hood." The real Christian is trusting the shed blood of the Lamb (12:1--13). People who know they have eternal life through the righteousness of another are never concerned about KILLING those who don't agree with them; [killing, jailing, arresting, threatening, and swindling are the fruits of trusting Rome or Russia]{.class_s4N4-0} (or Arabia)[.]{.class_s4N4-0}

The [Brazen Altar]{.class_s4N4-0} represents more than the death of Christ. It is a picture of the Lake of Fire, it is a picture of Hell in ["the midst"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 5) of the earth, it is a picture of God's judgment on the sinner, it is a presentation of []{#c37X.xhtml_page_615}God's attitude towards sin, and it is a picture of God's provision for the sinner.

No sinner can go anywhere "seeking God" until he faces the matter of sin, which separates him from God. No sinner "seeks God" (Rom. 3:11) who is not dealing directly with the matter of his own [personal accountability to God for wrongdoing. ]{.class_s4N4-0}The chance of an unsaved man rushing past the Brazen Altar and into the Holy Place alive---[without a blood atonement]{.class_s4N4-0}---would be less than the chances that Nadab and Abihu took when God dropped them dead.

You talk about "significance." Laying aside the mealy-mouth "comments" of 90% of the Conservative and Liberal commentators (the Liberals are found in [The Interpreter's Bible]{.class_s4N4-0}), let us apply what we have learned from the Brazen Altar.

1\. No Roman Catholic trusting WINE instead of [blood]{.class_s4N4-0} has ever gotten into God's presence, even when he had the presumption to pretend that one turned into the other.

2\. No Greek philosopher (Socrates, Anaximander, Plato, Aristotle, Zeno, Epicurus, et al.) or modern scholar (Dewey, Spinoza, Hegel, Kant, Descarte, Leibnitz, Russell, Pierce, Gentile, Marx, et al.) ever got to the starting line as far as "God knowledge" (theology) was concerned. You cannot even enter this race till you face the Brazen Altar.

3\. Far from being a "history of man's search for God" (Liberal teaching on the content of the Bible), the Holy Bible is a history of [man's attempts to avoid the main issue. ]{.class_s4N4-0}The issue is SIN (see the comments under Gen. 4:7 in the [Commentary on Genesis]{.class_s4N4-0}). No amount of casuistry, "inductive methods," sophistry, []{#c37X.xhtml_page_616}"existential happenings," provable phenomena, working hypotheses, "relative truths," "verifiable data," "inner light," pragmatic solutions, sensitivity therapy, or "cosmic consciousness" will keep you out of Hell long enough to light a cigarette. No atonement---no access. No shedding of blood---no [redemption]{.class_s4N4-0}. No fire consuming sin---no entrance to God's presence. No burning---no [blessing]{.class_s4N4-0}. Opinions to the contrary come under "freedom of speech," not [fact ]{.class_s4N4-0}or [truth]{.class_s4N4-0}. And the silence of the Conservative commentators at this point is only an evidence of their face-saving, opportunistic techniques.

27:9 "And thou shalt make the court of the tabernacle: for the south side southward there shall be hangings for the court of fine twined linen of an hundred cubits long for one side:

10 And the twenty pillars thereof and their twenty sockets shall be of brass; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets shall be of silver.

11 And likewise for the north side in length there shall be hangings of an hundred cubits long, and his twenty pillars and their twenty sockets of brass; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets of silver."

[Here begins a discussion of the court which surrounds the Tabernacle. It is 75 feet by 100 feet (approximately), or 100 cubits by fifty cubits. (Clarke's measurements would make it about 178 feet by 84 feet.) There is an opening in the east end of the court, right in front of the Brazen Altar, and this opening is about five cubits wide (vss. 13--15) or about seven feet six inches. Unlike the Tabernacle proper, the court poles are set on sockets of brass (instead of silver), but they are still covered with ]{.class_s4N3-0}[]{#c37X.xhtml_page_617}["fine twined linen" ]{.class_s4N2-1}[(vs. 9) and have fillets of silver to hang on the silver hooks which are on each pillar (vs. 10).]{.class_s4N3-0}

27:12 "And for the breadth of the court on the west side shall be hangings of fifty cubits: their pillars ten, and their sockets ten.

13 And the breadth of the court on the east side eastward shall be fifty cubits.

14 The hangings of one side of the gate shall be fifteen cubits: their pillars three, and their sockets three.

15 And on the other side shall be hangings fifteen cubits: their pillars three, and their sockets three."

The pillars run ten to the back end (west), six to the front end (east), and twenty on each side (vss. 11--12). The front entrance to the court is always open to the Israelite, although the entrance to the Tabernacle proper is closed to him. Thus, any sinner can get access to the first fundamental truth of his condition without any priesthood or any intermediary. He can see at a glance that [he is a sinner]{.class_s4N4-0} who deserves to [burn ]{.class_s4N4-0}for his sins. All he has to do is LOOK (Num. 21:8--9), and if he "just can't see it" (like they say down South), it is for the same reason found in John 1:5, 10 and John 3:20.

27:16 "And for the gate of the court shall be an hanging of twenty cubits, of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen, wrought with needlework: and their pillars shall be four, and their sockets four.

17 All the pillars round about the court shall be filleted with silver; their hooks shall be of silver, and their sockets of brass.

18 The length of the court shall be an hundred cubits, and the breadth fifty every where, and the height five cubits of fine twined linen, and their sockets of brass.

19 All the vessels of the tabernacle in all the service thereof, and all the pins thereof, and all the pins of the court, shall be of brass."

["The pins"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 19) were tent pins for holding the court walls upright, and the ["filleted with silver"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 17) refers to the[ "chapiters"]{.class_s4N2-0} (or capitals) on the top of each pillar (see 38:17). There is no reference to the expression "connected with rods" (Bullinger), which popped up in Westcott's and Hort's [RV ]{.class_s4N4-0}committee (1885) and passed on down to the [RSV ]{.class_s4N4-0}via Dummelow and the Christian Universities; [Harvard, Yale, Princeton, Columbia, and the University of Chicago were ALL "Christian Universities" before they abandoned the Authorized text.]{.class_s4N4-0}

Junction rods (Heb. ["chashoq"]{.class_s4N4-0}) may have been used to join the pillars at the top, but they are not translated like this from the Masoretic text. What is vastly more important is the fact that no rods are mentioned in connection with the court. The [linen hangings]{.class_s4N4-0} hang from the top of the chapiters of the pillars. This is the reference given in verse 17, and the [horizontal rods]{.class_s4N4-0} are mentioned nowhere except in the imagination of the [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}committee and the Lockman Foundation. Along these lines, the serious student of the Scripture should heed the constant warnings against doing exactly what these []{#c37X.xhtml_page_619}"Conservatives" have done and are doing continually (25:9), for 25:40 and 26:30 are double warnings NOT to put rods in where there are no rods, and not to take out any where any appear. In spite of these obvious warnings, we find the meddling critics of the Bible (A.D. 1800--2000) slapping "rods" around the court where there are none.

The court pictures [righteousness ]{.class_s4N4-0}from start to finish (Rev. 19:8), and although it rests on the [judgments of God]{.class_s4N4-0} (brass), it is held up by redemption (silver). The gate to the court has a hanging which matches the hanging over the front of the Holy Place (26:36), but unlike the latter, the gate to the court was not closed perpetually to the offerer. The hanging for the Holy Place could only be entered by a Levite. The hanging of the "Holy of Holies" could only be entered by the High Priest. Therefore, we have a clear [succession ]{.class_s4N4-0}of Holiness as we approach the third heaven and the throne of God (Rev. 4:1--5). It is only when ["the veil\...was rent"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Matt. 27:51) that the saved sinner could go through all three hangings without paying attention to any of them (Heb. 4:16). His great ["High Priest"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Heb. 3:1) is the ["one mediator"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 Tim. 2:5), and he has no more need of some black-robed clown in a Halloween costume than he has need of a dislocated shoulder. (You can find a lot of articles making fun of the robed KKK in the news media, but haven't you ever noticed the strange absence of articles on the robes of [Nuns ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [Priests]{.class_s4N4-0}? Odd, isn't it?)

27:20 "And thou shalt command the children of Israel, that they bring thee pure oil olive beaten for the light, to cause the lamp to burn always.

21 In the tabernacle of the congregation without the vail, which is before the testimony, Aaron and his sons shall order it from evening to morning before the [Lord]{.class_s4N9-1}: it shall be a statute for ever unto their generations on the behalf of the children of Israel."

Before going into a discussion of the priesthood---its regalia and rites---one more mention is made of the Golden Candlestick. Although some of the commentators make remarks about the verses, none of them give us the reason for the sudden insertion of ONE article of furniture in the Tabernacle proper, coming as it does after a description of the outer court. Clarke has things screwed up (as usual) and cites 30:8 to prove that the lamps [went out]{.class_s4N4-0} every morning and were [relighted ]{.class_s4N4-0}every night (see 25:31 and comments). Again, if we are to find the blessed truths in the typology and the order, we must turn to the [King James]{.class_s4N4-0} text of 1611 and abandon the jealous fanatics who resent it so bitterly.

Now the Golden Candlestick is the only item that [could ]{.class_s4N4-0}be mentioned between a discussion of the[ outer court]{.class_s4N4-0} and the [priesthood]{.class_s4N4-0}. You see, the Ark and the Mercy Seat were [hidden from view]{.class_s4N4-0} by two veils (26:31, 36) because they pictured God's presence in the third heaven, away from the solar system, the earth, and Israel. The Table of Showbread pictured only God's revelation to the earth (and Israel) and the daily provision for the nation as a redeemed people. But the Candlestick (!), which represented the Holy Spirit (Phil. 2:15--16; Rev. 1:11--12, 20), is at work on this earth dealing with sinners [inside and outside the Tabernacle]{.class_s4N4-0} (Zech. 4:6; John 16:8--11). Without a placement of this item between the Altar []{#c37X.xhtml_page_621}and the outer court, as it is related to the priests and the priesthood (chaps. 27--29), there is not one point where man could meet God even with the blood already shed. ["It is the spirit that quickeneth"]{.class_s4N2-0} (John 6:36), and when He sheds light on the word (2 Cor. 3:14--17), it strikes to the heart (Acts 2:37, 7:54). The placement of verses 20--21 [before ]{.class_s4N4-0}instructions on the Aaronic priesthood is as inspired as the placement of John 2:25 before John 3:1.

Without the lamp that burns from "the tree of life" (!), there is no life in any religion. The "divine spark," which Liberals assume is in every man (making both Charles Manson and Clyde Barrow "children of God"), is no more present without the Holy Spirit than live rabbits are present in a tank full of gasoline. Without ["the seven Spirits of God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rev. 4:5) and the anointing of the Holy Spirit (Heb. 1:9), the Tabernacle, the court, and the priesthood are merely sacramental, pagan rites on a level with the worst animism, totemism, and sacerdotalism of Mexico, Spain, Italy, Africa, and New Guinea.

Aaron and his sons are to ["order it from evening to morning"]{.class_s4N2-0}: not "light it" or "[relight ]{.class_s4N4-0}it" ["from evening to morning."]{.class_s4N2-0} Clarke's note, therefore (as those found in the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0}), is a blank. Both of the writers have not only proved that they are "has beens," but "never-wuzzers." The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} tells the naive reader that "we cannot assign any meaning to these words" unless we suppose that ["order it"]{.class_s4N2-0} meant to put it out and relight it. But then again, doesn't this sound like the Lockman Foundation ([NASV]{.class_s4N4-0}) assigning the meaning ["powers of death"]{.class_s4N4-0} to ["the gates of hell"]{.class_s4N2-0} ([AV]{.class_s4N4-0}, Matt. 16:18)? Yes it does; very definitely [it does.]{.class_s4N4-0}

(We realize that the Conservative scholarship behind the [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_s4N4-0}includes the writings of Von Bolhen, Hengstenberg, Kirzel, Keil, Delitzsch, Torrey, Knobel, DeWette, Lange, Seferus, Raschi, Von Lengerke, Stahelin, Gotha, Neuman, Millington, Byrant, and Friedrich; but we have learned long ago to put 100% of the commentators in the waste basket where they go around messing with a Book that is quite able to judge any of them---[or their teachers. ]{.class_s4N4-0}The Bible believer never has to worry about an "inferiority complex" when discarding the works of these men---or any like them \[Psa. 138:4\]---for, after all, the final authority is the Book; not what some fanatic thinks the Book should "teach.")

["On the behalf of the children of Israel"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 21). These last words are "most significant" (to quote A. W. Pink), for they show that ["Aaron]{.class_s4N2-0} (a type of [Christ]{.class_s4N4-0}, ["the light of the world,"]{.class_s4N2-0} John 8:12) ["and his sons"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 21---a type of the [believer, ]{.class_s4N4-0}["the light of the world,"]{.class_s4N2-0} Matt. 5:14) are to act as intermediate "luminaries" ["in a dark place" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(2 Pet. 1:19). They are to keep the lights turned on (Phil. 2:15) and to shine ["more and more"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Prov. 4:18) until ["the Sun"]{.class_s4N2-0} comes up (Mal. 4:1--2). At that time, they are not "snuffed," as the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} would have it (with Adam Clarke's approval); to the contrary, they ["shine forth as the sun in the kingdom of their Father"]{.class_s4N2-0} (see Matt. 13:43 and comments in the [Commentary on Matthew]{.class_s4N4-0}). Aaron and his sons must keep the light on for Israel, and the Lord Jesus Christ and His "sons" (Rom. 8:14--17) must keep it burning for the Church.

[Are your lights on? ]{.class_s4N4-0}They should be on thirty minutes before sundown!

[]{#c3A2.xhtml}

# [Chapter 28]{.class_s4NN} {.heading_s2E2-0}

28:1 "And take thou unto thee Aaron thy brother, and his sons with him, from among the children of Israel, that he may minister unto me in the priest's office, even Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar, Aaron's sons.

2 And thou shalt make holy garments for Aaron thy brother for glory and for beauty.

3 And thou shalt speak unto all that are wise hearted, whom I have filled with the spirit of wisdom, that they may make Aaron's garments to consecrate him, that he may minister unto me in the priest's office.

4 And these are the garments which they shall make; a breastplate, and an ephod, and a robe, and a broidered coat, a mitre, and a girdle: and they shall make holy garments for Aaron thy brother, and his sons, that he may minister unto me in the priest's office.

5 And they shall take gold, and blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine linen.

6 And they shall make the ephod of gold, of blue, and of purple, of scarlet, and fine twined linen, with cunning work.

7 It shall have the two shoulderpieces thereof joined at the two edges thereof; and so it shall be joined together.

8 And the curious girdle of the ephod, which is upon it, shall be of the same, according to the work thereof; even of gold, of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen.

9 And thou shalt take two onyx stones, and grave on them the names of the children of Israel:

10 Six of their names on one stone, and the other six names of the rest on the other stone, according to their birth.

11 With the work of an engraver in stone, like the engravings of a signet, shalt thou engrave the two stones with the names of the children of Israel: thou shalt make them to be set in ouches of gold.

12 And thou shalt put the two stones upon the shoulders of the ephod for stones of memorial unto the children of Israel: and Aaron shall bear their names before the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}upon his two shoulders for a memorial.

13 And thou shalt make ouches of gold;

14 And two chains of pure gold at the ends; of wreathen work shalt thou make them, and fasten the wreathen chains to the ouches.

15 And thou shalt make the breastplate of judgment with cunning work; after the work of the ephod thou shalt make it; of gold, of blue, and of purple, and of scarlet, and of fine twined linen, shalt thou make it.

16 Foursquare it shall be being doubled; a span shall be the length thereof, and a span shall be the breadth thereof.

17 And thou shalt set in it settings of stones, even four rows of stones: the first row shall be a sardius, a topaz, and a carbuncle: this shall be the first row.

18 And the second row shall be an emerald, a sapphire, and a diamond.

19 And the third row a ligure, an agate, and an amethyst.

20 And the fourth row a beryl, and an onyx, and a jasper: they shall be set in gold in their enclosings.

21 And the stones shall be with the names of the children of Israel, twelve, according to their names, like the engravings of a signet; every one with his name shall they be according to the twelve tribes.

22 And thou shalt make upon the breastplate chains at the ends of wreathen work of pure gold.

23 And thou shalt make upon the breastplate two rings of gold, and shalt put the two rings on the two ends of the breastplate.

24 And thou shalt put the two wreathen chains of gold in the two rings which are on the ends of the breastplate.

25 And the other two ends of the two wreathen chains thou shalt fasten in the two ouches, and put them on the shoulderpieces of the ephod before it.

26 And thou shalt make two rings of gold, and thou shalt put them upon the two ends of the breastplate in the border thereof, which is in the side of the ephod inward.

27 And two other rings of gold thou shalt make, and shalt put them on the two sides of the ephod underneath, toward the forepart thereof, over against the other coupling thereof, above the curious girdle of the ephod.

28 And they shall bind the breastplate by the rings thereof unto the rings of the ephod with a lace of blue, that it may be above the curious girdle of the ephod, and that the breastplate be not loosed from the ephod.

29 And Aaron shall bear the names of the children of Israel in the breastplate of judgment upon his heart, when he goeth in unto the holy place, for a memorial before the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}continually.

30 And thou shalt put in the breastplate of judgment the Urim and the Thummim; and they shall be upon Aaron's heart, when he goeth in before the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}: and Aaron shall bear the judgment of the children of Israel upon his heart before the[ ]{.class_s4ND}[Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}continually.

31 And thou shalt make the robe of the ephod all of blue.

32 And there shall be an hole in the top of it, in the midst thereof: it shall have a binding of woven work round about the hole of it, as it were the hole of an habergeon, that it be not rent.

33 And beneath upon the hem of it thou shalt make pomegranates of blue, and of purple, and of scarlet, round about the hem thereof; and bells of gold between them round about:

34 A golden bell and a pomegranate, a golden bell and a pomegranate, upon the hem of the robe round about.

35 And it shall be upon Aaron to minister: and his sound shall be heard when he goeth in unto the holy place before the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}, and when he cometh out, that he die not.

36 And thou shalt make a plate of pure gold, and grave upon it, like the engravings of a signet, HOLINESS TO THE LORD.

37 And thou shalt put it on a blue lace, that it may be upon the mitre; upon the forefront of the mitre it shall be.

38 And it shall be upon Aaron's forehead, that Aaron may bear the iniquity of the holy things, which the children of Israel shall hallow in all their holy gifts; and it shall be always upon his forehead, that they may be accepted before the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}.

39 And thou shalt embroider the coat of fine linen, and thou shalt make the mitre of fine linen, and thou shalt make the girdle of needlework.

40 And for Aaron's sons thou shalt make coats, and thou shalt make for them girdles, and bonnets shalt thou make for them, for glory and for beauty.

41 And thou shalt put them upon Aaron thy brother, and his sons with him; and shalt anoint them, and consecrate them, and sanctify them, that they may minister unto me in the priest's office.

42 And thou shalt make them linen breeches to cover their nakedness; from the loins even unto the thighs they shall reach:

43 And they shall be upon Aaron, and upon his sons, when they come in unto the tabernacle of the congregation, or when they come near unto the altar to minister in the holy place; that they bear not iniquity, and die: it shall be a statute for ever unto him and his seed after him."

We come now to one of the most beautiful parts of the Scripture, and the typology here is much clearer than that of chapters 25--27, at least where it regards the person of Christ. (As we have said before, the original typology of the Tabernacle was dealing with the [universe]{.class_s4N4-0}; hence, its revelations of Christ are only [indirect]{.class_s4N4-0}. Here, however, we have some [direct ]{.class_s4N4-0}references which no devout mind could miss.) For the sake of unity, we will take up each one of the garments ["for glory and for beauty"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 2), and print the appropriate references that deal with them. Seven items are mentioned.

1\. ["The breastplate" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vss. 15--20), which would include the Urim and Thummim (vs. 30).

2\. ["The ephod" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vss. 6--7, 25).

3\. ["The robe" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vss. 31--35).

4\. The ["linen breeches"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 42--43).

5\. The ["broidered coat"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 4, 39).

6\. ["The mitre" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vss. 36--38).

7\. The ["girdle" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vss. 8, 40).

There are two great priests in the Old Testament. One ministered to Gentiles (Melchizedek), and one ministered to Israel (Aaron). Both were called of God. Aaron was head of an order which continued by virtue of sons being born into the priesthood, while Melchizedek was head of an order that ended with him (Heb. 7:1--3). The Levites were given to Aaron and his sons to minister to them (Num. 3:9), so Aaron's sons are spoken of as one unit with their father. Note in verse 1: ["take thou unto thee Aaron\...and his sons with him\...that HE may minister unto me\...."]{.class_s4N2-0} A list of his sons follows, plainly linking them with the high priest himself. (This is the first in a long series of types which demonstrate the union of the believer with the great High Priest of the New Testament \[Heb. 3:1--3\], the Lord Jesus Christ \[Heb. 2:11--12\].)

["Minister unto me in the priest's office"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 1). This is the office that was coveted and stolen by the black-robed Baal worshippers of the "Queen of Heaven" in this dispensation. The famous "father confessors" of Babylonian origin are not in the least like the Biblical priests of Exodus. Later (Mal. 2:1--3), the Jewish priesthood gets into the same corrupt condition which is so characteristic of the Roman priesthood; but the instructions given by God for the priesthood are a far cry from the instructions given at Xavier, Fordham, Notre Dame, and Loyola for the depraved and hellish political []{#c3A2.xhtml_page_629}operations of the papal hierarchy at Rome. Originally, the priest had to be physically fit (Lev. 21:17--21), and he had to be properly [married ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Lev. 21:14--15), not single. The high priest could not mourn for the dead (Lev. 21:10--11), for he represented LIFE. Therefore, the vulgar obscenities of the JFK (or Bobbie Kennedy) funeral would be unthinkable for a Biblical priesthood. The great High Priest Himself said ["weep not for me"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Luke 23:28). His "mourning" was limited to weeping (John 11:35) when a loved one died; there was no tearing of clothes, drooping of the head, sprinkling of holy water, or mumbling of rosaries and Latin incantations. This paraphernalia belongs to the [Saturnalia ]{.class_s4N4-0}and the drunken orgies of [Baachus, ]{.class_s4N4-0}not the Aaronic or Melchizedekan priesthood of the Scriptures.

Aaron, as the Lord Jesus, was chosen of God (Heb. 5:1, 4), not the papal hierarchy. Aaron was anointed (as the Lord Jesus---Luke 4:18) to be a representative of the people (Heb. 2:17, 5:1). Jesus is our perfect representative, for He sorrowed with us (Isa. 53:3--4) and He died with us (Rom. 6:3--8). He rose with us (Eph. 2:5--6), and He intercedes for us (1 Tim. 2:5), so we have no more need of some dunce in a black robe than we have need of a hole in the head.

The priest must have something to offer (29:35--38), so the great High Priest of the Twentieth Century (Heb. 8:3) offered HIMSELF (Heb. 7:27; 10:10, 9:12). He is sinless (Heb. 7:26), His offering is perfect (Heb. 9:25--28), His intercession is prevailing (Heb. 7:27), and His priesthood is unchangeable (Heb. 7:23--24).

The typology goes even beyond this, for the careful reader of the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}text (who has the help of the Comforter in discerning the truth) sees that although []{#c3A2.xhtml_page_630}there are [seven garments given]{.class_s4N4-0} (see above), only FIVE pieces of clothing for the body are listed in verse 4 (the mitre is for the head). John 19:23 clearly shows the great High Priest's [five pieces of clothing]{.class_s4N4-0} which were stripped from His body just as He offered the one, perfect, effectual, and ETERNAL sacrifice (Heb. 10:8--12).

["Holy garments\...for glory and beauty"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 2). Thus, the Lord Jesus is made beautiful to the Christian, and in Psalm 45, this glorious array is described, for He is the One who clothes Himself ["with light as with a garment"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Psa. 104:2). The [first item]{.class_s4N4-0} in the ["garments"]{.class_s4N2-0} is the breastplate with the Urim and Thummim (vs. 30), which is used to inquire of God (1 Sam. 28:6). Is it not remarkable how this matches the [seventh item]{.class_s4N4-0} in the ["whole armour of God"]{.class_s4N2-0} listed in Ephesians chapter 6? Observe that the [seventh item]{.class_s4N4-0} in this armor is [not named. ]{.class_s4N4-0}But by noticing the Bible armament (1 Sam. 17:6), we know what the seventh piece of armor was: it was the leg greaves given to cover the shins. Since the soldier of Ephesians chapter 6 is KNEELING in his spiritual warfare, [the shins are covered, so the item is omitted.]{.class_s4N4-0}

Ah, the unfathomable depths of the [Authorized Version. ]{.class_s4N4-0}How superior are its revelations compared to the unsteady and flickering light of the "original text"! (We speak with tongue in cheek, of course. Any Bible believer believes in the inspiration of the [real ]{.class_s4N4-0}"original text," but when the Lockman Foundation or the [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}committee says "the original text," they are usually referring to Kittle's or Nestle's text and have no more reference to the real "original" than an Eighteenth Century racing form.)

1\. [The BREASTPLATE]{.class_s4N4-0} (25:7, 28:15, 22--24, 26, 29; Lev. 8:8).

The breastplate was not a piece of metal or leather as the breastplate of a soldier; rather, it was a pouch made out of fine linen (as the ephod of vss. 6, 8 and the girdle of vs. 8) about nine inches square. It was doubled before it was suspended on the chest of the high priest (vs. 16), and it held the "Urim and Thummim" (see above). The breastplate was adorned with [four rows]{.class_s4N4-0} of precious stones, [three stones in a row,]{.class_s4N4-0} set in golden settings. The pouch was held up by two golden chains which were fastened to the onyx stones on the shoulders of the high priest (vss. 22--25). Two golden rings were placed at the bottom of the breastplate (vss. 26--29), and these were for the "laces of blue" (vs. 28) which were tied around the body of the high priest under his arms. They joined two golden rings (vs. 27) on the ephod itself (see below), thus fastening the breastplate to the sides of the ephod permanently.

The stones of the breastplate are listed in verses 17--20 (and the Christian should use the list given in the [AV]{.class_s4N4-0} 1611 Bible). The list in the [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(any edition), promoted by zealous fanatics who wish to usurp the authority of the Holy Spirit---any Greek or Hebrew teacher on any Christian faculty at nearly any Conservative school---is not to be taken seriously for a minute. (The list given there was taken from the outdated [Pulpit Commentary,]{.class_s4N4-0} which material was furnished nearly a century back \[1880\] by the dead-orthodox apostates of the English Revision Committee. Just give it the old "heave ho" and forget it.)

The order of tribes (reading from right to left, as in the Hebrew language) would be the same as the [order of []{#c3A2.xhtml_page_632}birth]{.class_s4N4-0} (see Gen. 29--30 in the [Commentary on Genesis]{.class_s4N4-0}): Reuben, Simeon, Levi, Judah, Dan, Naphtali, Gad, Asher, Issachar, Zebulun, Joseph, and Benjamin. However, the listing on the shoulder stones is Reuben, Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, Zebulun, Dan, Naphtali, Gad, Asher, Joseph, and Benjamin. However, the careful reader of Scripture will notice that there are several orders given (see App. 45, [Companion Bible,]{.class_s4N4-0} Bullinger), and consequently, the key to Biblical astrology (see comments Gen. 29:34 in the [Commentary on Genesis]{.class_s4N4-0}) has probably been lost.

Whatever the order, we are told that ["Aaron]{.class_s4N2-0} [shall bear the names of the children of Israel in the breastplate of judgment upon his heart"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 29). Companion verses which explain the spiritual truth of this statement are found in Jeremiah 17:1, Jeremiah 31:33, and Malachi 2:2. The elect nation was taken to God's heart and loved as a wife (Hos. 1--3). Israel lay where John the apostle lay (John 13:25) and where the Church now resides (Eph. 5:24--33). However, the saints are never sinless, and exactly as God loved them, so He hated their sins and bore them (1 Pet. 2:24) in ["judgment upon his heart."]{.class_s4N2-0} One can see immediately (vs. 38) that Aaron himself is set forth as a "sin bearer" in addition to the sacrifices which his sons offered.

Using Soltau's order from Numbers 7, we find:

1\. [Judah, ]{.class_s4N4-0}represented by the SARDIUS.

2\. [Issachar,]{.class_s4N4-0} represented by the TOPAZ.

3\. [Zebulun,]{.class_s4N4-0} represented by the CARBUNCLE.

4\. [Reuben, ]{.class_s4N4-0}represented by the EMERALD.

5\. [Simeon, ]{.class_s4N4-0}represented by the SAPPHIRE.

6\. [Gad,]{.class_s4N4-0} represented by the DIAMOND.

7\. [Ephraim,]{.class_s4N4-0} represented by the LIGURE.

8\. [Manasseh,]{.class_s4N4-0} represented by the AGATE.

9\. [Benjamin,]{.class_s4N4-0} represented by theAMETHYST.

10\. [Dan,]{.class_s4N4-0} represented by the BERYL.

11\. [Asher, ]{.class_s4N4-0}represented by the ONYX.

12\. [Naphtali, ]{.class_s4N4-0}represented by the JASPER.

(This is not like the engraving on the onyx stones set on the shoulder of the high priest, for the two shoulder stones have the order given according to Gen. 29--30; that is, according to BIRTH. The order here is that of Num. 2, 7.)

As we have said before, the student will do well to go by the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}text of the Reformation and ignore the changes made in the corrupt [NASV]{.class_s4N4-0} and similar cheap imitations. The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0}---following the apostate leadership of Baer, Sanday, Delitzsch, Keil, Green, Schaff, Ellicott, Hort, Lightfoot, and other "Conservatives"---tells us that "here ALL THE NAMES must be wrong" ([PuIpit Commentary,]{.class_s4N4-0} pg. 286). This is God's way of showing the Christian that NONE of the names are wrong, and where the "majority of godly commentators" agree as to corrections, simply ignore them.

The placement of the names on the shoulders and the heart shows that the great High Priest of Hebrews 3:1 is to bear the government "upon His shoulders" (Isa. 9:6) as well as "in His heart." The reader will observe the peculiar order of GRACE as found on the onyx stones (shoulder epaulets): Reuben, though ["unstable as water"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Gen. 49:4) is given first place (!), while Benjamin and Joseph, the two favorite sons of Israel (Gen. 37:3, 42:38), are on the end of the list. Judah, who would bring forth ["the sceptre" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(Gen. 49:10), is stuck clear down in the middle. The ["onyx stones"]{.class_s4N2-0} are set ["in []{#c3A2.xhtml_page_634}ouches of gold"]{.class_s4N2-0} attached to ["the shoulders of the ephod"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 9--12) so that the tribes are an inseparable part of ["the ephod,"]{.class_s4N2-0} whether they are engraved on the shoulders or on the breastplate. The shoulder stones are twice said to be a ["memorial"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 12) and are therefore clearly connected with that time where Jesus Christ will reign at Jerusalem and the sacrifices will be a MEMORIAL (Ezek. 44--46), looking BACK to the finished work of Calvary, instead of looking [forward ]{.class_s4N4-0}to it (observe, in particular, Ezek. 45:17).

There have been many conjectures about these stones and their symbology, but Soltau (pp. 209--250) has as good an exposition as anyone, at least as far as spiritual truth is concerned. Gazing on the breastplate, one would behold a dazzling sight: four rows of stones, three stones to a row. The glittering colors which would strike the eye would be: a deep red, a yellow-green, a deep bluish red, a dark green, a pure white, a yellow-brown, a mixture of light brown and yellow, a deep purple, a bluish green, a bluish white, and green with red specks. Revelation 21:19--20 describes the New Jerusalem in similar fashion, except in that case, we are dealing with stones well over ninety feet thick. The God of [nature ]{.class_s4N4-0}is the God of [revelation,]{.class_s4N4-0} the God of [minerals ]{.class_s4N4-0}is the God of the [Bible,]{.class_s4N4-0} and the God who planted the ore deposits is quite capable of producing pearls ninety feet in diameter or diamonds twice that size.

Paul had his eyes "knocked out" when he saw the holy city (2 Cor. 12:1--6), but the Lord told him that he could not speak about it in particular until John showed up (Rev. 4:1--6). The "lights and perfections" (see below) within the pouch probably shown through these stones, and the appearance would have been quite similar []{#c3A2.xhtml_page_635}to a modern Christmas tree. This "Christmas tree," however, is ["according to the pattern"]{.class_s4N2-0} shown on the mount (Heb. 8:5)---not the Babylonian maypole of Roman Catholic mythology (Jer. 10:1--5).

Before we can discuss the second garment ["for glory and for beauty"]{.class_s4N2-0} ([the ephod]{.class_s4N4-0}), we must note the curious item listed which has caused the commentators grief ["from the foundation of the world"]{.class_s4N2-0}: [the Urim and Thummim.]{.class_s4N4-0}

The [Urim and Thummim]{.class_s4N4-0} (vs. 30) are about as easy to identify as Melchizedek and the Pharaoh of the Exodus (see comments under 1:8). Whatever they were, they were not made by man, although everything else was man-made. The Urim and Thummim were simply "placed" (vs. 30) into the pouch of the breastplate. These two strange articles are mentioned in Leviticus 8:8; Deuteronomy 33:8; Numbers 27:21; 1 Samuel 28:6; Ezra 2:63; and Nehemiah 7:65. They are evidently connected with DIVINATION, exactly as we have implied that the breastplate and "birthstones" were connected with [Biblical astrology]{.class_s4N4-0} (see Gen. 29:34 and comments in the[ Commentary on Genesis]{.class_s4N4-0}).

Bullinger, as many commentators, connects the Urim and the Thummim with [two stones]{.class_s4N4-0} (perhaps a black one and a white one) which were drawn out of the pouch "by lot" to determine issues (see Josh. 18:11, 19:1, 17; Num. 26:55, 34:13, etc.). However, this is a little "oversimplified," for when David "inquires" at the ephod (1 Sam. 23:12), it comes to a great deal more than the maneuvers described in 1 Samuel 14:41. [God answers David with a sentence. ]{.class_s4N4-0}(See also 1 Sam. 30:8, which Bullinger \[and others\] completely overlooked in talking about "casting lots.") The Urim and Thummim could do []{#c3A2.xhtml_page_636}more than answer "yes" or "no" (like a Ouija board); they could speak out complete sentences (like a Ouija board). They plainly are connected with a God-honored [Biblical divination.]{.class_s4N4-0}

However, this God-honored "Biblical divination" is connected inseparably and continually (vss. 28--30) with the great High Priest who offered one, effectual, eternal blood atonement for sinners (Heb. 10:8--12). [Any divination apart from the finished work and revelation of this living Intercessor and Mediator is SATANIC]{.class_s4N4-0} (see Acts 16:16--17). If you want to shut up a Ouija (French---"oui," German---"[ja"]{.class_s4N4-0}) board, just start it talking about the blood atonement of the Lord Jesus Christ or the application of that blood to the heart and mind of the regenerated sinner (Heb. 9:14). That'll bring the butter. If you want to "rap with the spirits" in "rapport" (as the table "RAPS" with Rap Brown), rap it one time with Hebrews 9:14 and watch it sign off so quick you'll think all the fuses in the box blew at one time.

The Vatican [LXX ]{.class_s4N4-0}and the Latin [Vulgate ]{.class_s4N4-0}(from which the [NASV]{.class_s4N4-0} and the [NRSV]{.class_s4N4-0} were translated) completely confound the words with ["manifestation and truth"]{.class_s4N4-0} or[ "doctrine and truth."]{.class_s4N4-0} But this is an attempt by the dead-orthodox apostate (who is "in a bastion of orthodoxy contending for the faith, etc.") to line up the Bible with rabbinical tradition; many of the Alexandrian Rabbis taught that the Urim and Thummim were not real objects (Clarke Comment., pg. 448).

(Origen and his old allegorizing buddies never conk out, do they? They are still with us, telling us that ["the gates of hell"]{.class_s4N2-0} \[Matt. 16:18\] are ["the powers of death"]{.class_s4N4-0} in the [New Standard American]{.class_s4N4-0} [Trash]{.class_s4N4-0} \[1963\], just like []{#c3A2.xhtml_page_637}they told it to you in the [Old Standard American Sewage]{.class_s4N4-0} \[1901\].)

The conjecture to which the Jews are held, in regards to the manner of "inquiring at the breastplate," sounds much more Scriptural than the "stones for casting lots" suggested by Bullinger and others. The ordinary Jews held that the "lights and perfections" inside the breastplate LIT UP the proper letters on the names of the tribes to spell out the answer which God gave to the inquirer. The twelve names of the tribes contain the following letters: [Yod, He, Daleth, Vau, Sin, Shin, Caph, Resh, Beth, Lamed, Nun, Aleph, Mem, Ayin, Pe, Gimmel,]{.class_s4N4-0} and [Tau]{.class_s4N4-0}. These constitute seventeen letters (or sixteen) out of 23---[Cheth, Zayin, Teth, Samech, Tzaddi,]{.class_s4N4-0} and [Koph ]{.class_s4N4-0}being omitted. ([Sin ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [Shin ]{.class_s4N4-0}are the same radical in Hebrew, making actually [22 letters.]{.class_s4N4-0}) If this position is adopted, we must assume that seventeen letters (or sixteen) were enough for the Lord to use in answering any question that the inquirer might bring. ["Teraphim"]{.class_s4N4-0} are connected with the Urim and Thummim, in the minds of some (Judg. 17:5, 18:14; Hos. 3:4), which would point to two images laid within the breastplate, for the teraphim were images (see 1 Sam. 19:13). But this seems a little farfetched in view of the orders given in 20:4.

The first item on our list has taken up most of the material of Exodus chapter 28. It presents the picture of a priest wearing a "plate" across his chest in which sparkle twelve precious stones. This plate is fastened to his garments under the arms and up to the shoulders; within it are the Urim and Thummim. [Two sets]{.class_s4N4-0} of the names of the children of Israel can be seen on the high priest if he is approached: one set is found on his shoulders---six tribes on one shoulder and six on the []{#c3A2.xhtml_page_638}other; the other set is found across his chest in four rows with three tribes to a row. The front of the priest sparkles with ["glory"]{.class_s4N2-0} and ["beauty"]{.class_s4N2-0} as it flashes forth the gold (the settings), black stones (the onyx stones), and a blazing variety of white, blue, blue-green, gold, brown, red, purple, and deep green.

The high priest is a perfect duplicate of SATAN (Ezek. 28:13). You see, you forgot that exactly as Satan counterfeits light (2 Cor. 11:14), God (2 Cor. 4:4), and the perfection of God (Ezek. 28:12), he also counterfeits [the great High Priest]{.class_s4N4-0} without whom no man may approach to God (Matt. 11:27).

(The young people in America who are involved in Satanism and black magic---that's what their educators told them: "total INVOLVEMENT!"---are inquiring for knowledge at the [wrong breastplate ]{.class_s4N4-0}\[Gen. 3:1--5\]. The One they need is not worn by the ["anointed cherub that covereth"]{.class_s4N2-0} \[Ezek. 28:14\], but is worn by ["the author and finisher"]{.class_s4N2-0} of saving faith (Heb. 12:2), the Creator and Sustainer of the universe, the Architect and Builder of New Jerusalem, who is ["the Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rev. 22:13). The Scriptures call Him the Fairest of Ten Thousand \[cf. Song of Sol. 5:10\], the Lord God Almighty, the ["great shepherd of the sheep"]{.class_s4N2-0} \[Heb. 13:20\], and the Lord Jesus Christ. He is my Saviour, my Beloved, my Friend, and He is ["God blessed\...who is over all"]{.class_s4N2-0}---amen and amen \[Rom. 9:5\].)

In closing, we record another sample from the thousands of errors that have crept into the text of the Twentieth-Century translations from the source of corruption (Alexandria, Egypt). This monstrous accumulation of errors, which Dr. Neal and Dr. Custer []{#c3A2.xhtml_page_639}refer to (in the New Testament) as "THE Greek text," is still preserved in such standard wipeouts as the [NASV]{.class_s4N4-0} and the [NRSV,]{.class_s4N4-0} which are promoted by dead-orthodox Conservatives and dead-orthodox apostates.

At verse 1, we find the amazing statement (in the mythological [LXX]{.class_s4N4-0}) that priests can be chosen from any tribe among the children of Israel, not just [Aaron's]{.class_s4N4-0} sons. This remarkable addition was made by Philo, Origen, Clement, Symmachus, Aquila, Theodotian, and other writers of the post-Christian fiasco. It states that priests are to come from Aaron ["AND FROM among the children of Israel." ]{.class_s4N4-0}This is exactly the error that the book of Hebrews was written to correct, and no one but a [Roman Catholic Priest]{.class_s4N4-0} could condone the reading of the [LXX ]{.class_s4N4-0}at this point. The reading could be used as a prooftext that ANY "Jew" could be a priest. When this blatant falsehood is coupled with Romans 2:29---and all Jehovah's Witnesses, Roman Catholics, Sev[enth-day Adventists, Armstrongites, and Church]{.class_s4N5-0} of Christ "elders" make this connection---["the synagogue of Satan"]{.class_s4N2-0} who ["say they are Jews and are NOT"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rev. 2:9) can usurp the Old Testament priesthood. The reading, therefore, of the Alexandrian [LXX]{.class_s4N4-0}---the source of the[ NASV]{.class_s4N4-0} in "Nestle's"---is a remarkable public display of the motives and intentions (Heb. 4:12) of the kind of men who are now promoting the [NASV]{.class_s4N4-0} and the [NRSV]{.class_s4N4-0} and the ["Living Bible."]{.class_s4N4-0}

You see, it was ORIGEN who first applied the term [priest]{.class_s4N4-0} to a New Testament pastor, and it was ORIGEN who first stated that an ordinary human sinner could be called a ["HIGH PRIEST."]{.class_s4N4-0} Every man, therefore, who recommends this vile text is (at heart) a [Roman ruler. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Their profession of faith in "the Bible as the sole []{#c3A2.xhtml_page_640}authority in all matters of faith and practice" is the lying profession of a two-faced hypocrite. They are men who wished to be looked upon as the "final authority," and their profession of "Fundamentalism" is no more significant than the profession of Karl Barth or Emil Brunner.

The same corrupt writers of the "[LXX]{.class_s4N4-0}" have altered 1 Samuel 14:41 to suit their fancy along these lines, since we are discussing ["the Urim and the Thummim." ]{.class_s4N2-0}This was done to force the reader to accept the theory that the [Urim ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [Thummim ]{.class_s4N4-0}were only "yes" and "no" articles (see above). Kennicott and Smith subscribe to this corruption of text. Dummelow informs us that "our Hebrew text\...is evidently mutilated"; whereupon he suggests that we adopt the Roman Catholic reading of Jerome (found in [Vaticanus ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [Sinaiticus]{.class_s4N4-0}) which reads: ["If the iniquity be in me or Jonathan my son give Urim; and if the iniquity be in the people give Thummim."]{.class_s4N4-0} This nicely ties the matter up and forces the Holy Spirit to accept the conjecture of "white and black stones" as the gospel truth. But we have been around the faculty at Alexandria (and some of these "Fundamental" schools) long enough to know a jackass when we hear one. (You see, on one occasion a lion and a jackass were hungry, so they decided to cooperate in scaring up some game. The lion stationed himself at the south end of the forest, and the jackass imitated a lion's roar on the north side. The game then ran southward back into the mouth of the real lion. After the lion had finished his repast, he led the donkey to some nice pasture where he could eat. "That was a pretty good roar," said the lion. "If I hadn't known you were a [jackass, ]{.class_s4N4-0}I would have been scared to death.")

When these theological jackasses in "Fundamental" schools begin to bray and wave their lexicons and grammars around like a Black demonstrator, and holler and roar about "it should be translated as" and "a better reading is" or "the word in the original was" or "a more accurate rendering would be," just go find them some pasture. They are much better adapted to grazing in a tax-exempt field than "fighting with wild beasts" (1 Cor. 15:32) in the arena of faith.

2\. [The EPHOD]{.class_s4N4-0} (vss. 6--7, 25) .

The ephod was a sort of vest or "waistcoat" which was worn over the robe (see below). The ephod was made out of fine linen, and its description matches the material found in the sanctuary of the Tabernacle (vs. 6). The vest had no sides, but rather, it hung over the front and back of the high priest, joined at the shoulders by the two onyx stones (vss. 12, 25). From other references (Judg. 18:18, 20; Hos. 3:4 cf. 1 Sam. 2:18, 28, 14:23, 23:6, 9; 2 Sam. 6:14), we notice that the ephod could become an object of worship since it was connected with the Biblical divination of the breastplate (see above). It was characteristic of the PRIESTHOOD (1 Sam. 22:18), but it was not only worn by Aaron as a [priest,]{.class_s4N4-0} but by David as a KING (2 Sam. 6:14) and Samuel as a PROPHET (1 Sam. 2:18). This clearly shows us that our Saviour was the perfect representative of all three offices. A careful student of the truth will not fail to notice that if an "ephod" were suspended from a pole by the shoulder joints (where the onyx stones were), it would be a FLAG, which men salute and to which they "swear allegiance."

3\. [The ROBE]{.class_s4N4-0} (vss. 31--35). This is the item about which Lloyd Douglas and Hollywood (and Mrs. Constantine and Sir Galahad) raised such a stink. They []{#c3A2.xhtml_page_642}could have saved themselves the trouble, for Christ had no "robe." The Holy Spirit is careful to tell us in the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}text that Christ's clothes were a ["vesture"]{.class_s4N2-0} (John 19:24),[ "garments" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(Matt. 27:35), ["clothes" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(Mark 15:20),[ "raiment"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Matt. 27:31), etc. [ANYTHING BUT A "ROBE."]{.class_s4N4-0}

Apart from the ritualistic priesthood of the Old Testament theocracy, "robes" (in the New Testament) are treated with very little respect by the Holy Spirit; observe, for example, Luke 20:46 and John 19:2, 5. We have a good reference in Luke 15:22 which matches Isaiah 22:21 and Isaiah 61:10, but the "robe" of Hollywood productions was taken [off ]{.class_s4N4-0}Jesus Christ before He got to Calvary (Luke 23:11), and it was anything but a "Christian robe" (Matt. 27:31). It was a [Roman robe,]{.class_s4N4-0} and it was [Romans ]{.class_s4N4-0}who nailed Him and pierced Him. (You had better forget Ian Paisley, John Huss, Alex Dunlap, William Tyndale, and Savonarola before you forget that.)

["The robe of the ephod"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 31) is [blue]{.class_s4N4-0}. The only similarity between it and the ["vesture"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Psa. 22:18) over which the soldiers shot craps was the fact that it was ONE piece of cloth with a hole in the top like a "poncho" (vs. 32). But even if the hack writers of productions like ["Quo Vadis," "The Sign of the Cross,"]{.class_s4N4-0} and ["The Robe"]{.class_s4N4-0} had discerned the difference between the High Priest who died and the high priest who killed Him (John 11:51), they would still have missed the typology; for as we have said at the beginning, the ephod matches the Tabernacle, and BOTH are [types of the universe]{.class_s4N4-0} (Heb. 1:12, 8:2, 9:24). Those of us who know about the "height, length, depth, and breadth" of the great "vesture" (not ["the love of Christ"]{.class_s4N2-0} as in all the commentaries---see Eph. 3:18 and comments in the [Commentary on Galatians---[]{#c3A2.xhtml_page_643}Colossians]{.class_s4N4-0}), know where the hole is, and what is better, [we know the HEAD that protrudes through that hole.]{.class_s4N4-0}

Blue would be the only proper color for the ephod (["all of blue,"]{.class_s4N2-0} vs. 31). If you want to see the "ephod," look up, and if you want to see the High Priest, [He has gone through the hole]{.class_s4N4-0} at the top (Heb. 6:19). They are still shooting dice over His "raiment" in 2000 (Houston Space Center and Cape Canaveral). (Ah, the fabulous scope of the Elizabethean English. How mighty are its similitudes and its scientific data past finding out.)

Why waste time with a dead-orthodox linguist, whose motive for teaching is to replace the authority of the Book with his own authority, when you have at your disposal the greatest textbook on absolute scientific truth the world has ever seen or ever will see?! (The word ["habergeon"]{.class_s4N2-0} \[Heb. ["tachara"]{.class_s4N4-0}\] indicates that the hole is like the hole in the top of a coat of mail armor which is donned like a sweater over the head of the wearer.)

4\. [The LINEN BREECHES]{.class_s4N4-0} (vss. 42--43; Lev. 6:10, 16:4).

These form a sort of underwear worn under the ephod (and the robe), and they are to "cover the nakedness" of the priest clear down to his knees, or at least shortly above them ["from the loins\...unto the thighs"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 42).

Again, the devout student cannot help but recall how the Lord Jesus Christ was stripped of His clothes when He died on Calvary (John 19:23) and thus was placed in the position of the guilty sinner (Gen. 3:10--11) who stands naked before the judgment bar of God (Matt. 22:11). The linen breeches had to be worn ["when they come in unto the tabernacle of the congregation, or when they come near unto the altar to []{#c3A2.xhtml_page_644}minister in the holy place"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 43). One entrance into the Tabernacle to "minister," without the prescribed underwear, would have given the undertaker another grave to fill.

The believer may thank God that his ["nakedness"]{.class_s4N2-0} is covered with the righteousness of the High Priest (Heb. 3:1), and that he can now enter the Holy of Holies (Heb. 9:24) in the same naked body with which he was born (1 Tim. 6:7) without being blasted into perdition (Job 1:21). As we have stated earlier, ["fine linen is the righteousness of saints"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rev. 19:8), but the reader will observe that the linen of the ephod (the outer garment) has [gold wires]{.class_s4N4-0} worked through it (see the Song of Sol. 1:11; Psa. 45:13; and especially Exod. 39:2). Thus, the believer's righteousness is inseparable from the righteousness of Jesus Christ which was imputed to him, at least as far as his OUTER clothing at the judgment is concerned. His [standing ]{.class_s4N4-0}is ["gold"]{.class_s4N2-0}; his [state ]{.class_s4N4-0}is[ "fine linen."]{.class_s4N2-0}

On the hem of the robe, the colors of the Tabernacle are repeated; this time they are connected with an ornamentation of [bells ]{.class_s4N4-0}(gold) and [pomegranates]{.class_s4N4-0}, alternately, around the bottom of the robe (vss. 33--34). The bells are real bells which make a sound (vs. 35), and this furnishes the believer with the only possible reference to "Ring the Bells of Heaven" or "Can't You Hear Those Bells a' Ringing?" or the traditional "Bells of St. Mary's" and "the clear ringing bell" of the "little brown church." We find ["bells"]{.class_s4N2-0} on the bridles of horses in the Millennium (Zech. 14:20), but aside from that, the Scriptures are silent. If Hell has any "bells" (Hell's bells, etc.), they are not mentioned directly in Scripture.

The typology is striking. The bells represent [profession of fruit]{.class_s4N4-0} (1 Cor. 13:13, 14:1), and the fruit should follow (cf. Mark 11:13--14, 20--21).

The pomegranate is connected with fresh grapes in the Scripture (Song of Sol. 6:11, 7:12), and it represents the "firstfruits" from the land of Canaan (Num. 13:23). There is some confusion over the statement ["his sound shall be heard\...that he die not"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 35). Certainly [the Lord]{.class_s4N4-0} did not need to be warned of Aaron's approach, and the peculiar idea that if the bells stopped ringing, the [congregation]{.class_s4N4-0} would know that Aaron was dead and would have to be dragged out of the [Holy of Holies]{.class_s4N4-0} with a rope is also rather stupid.

To tell the truth, when the high priest entered into the Holy of Holies (not[ "the holy place"]{.class_s4N2-0} as here ), [he did NOT wear this robe]{.class_s4N4-0} (Lev. 16:4). In Leviticus, we are told that he had on only the LINEN COAT, the LINEN BREECHES, and the LINEN GIRDLE (Lev. 16:4); notice the specific instruction: ["the holy place within the vail before the mercy seat"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Lev. 16:2). The garments for "glory and beauty" (vs. 2) become only the ["holy garments"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Lev. 16:4). The breastplate, the ephod, and the robe for the ephod are left [outside ]{.class_s4N4-0}in the Holy Place which contained the Table of Showbread, the Candlestick, and the Altar of Incense. No one would have heard any bells if Aaron had come back OUT of the Holy of Holies, for he did not have any on. Furthermore, ANY Levite serving the Tabernacle could [go into the Holy Place to SEE if Aaron were dead or alive. ]{.class_s4N4-0}If he didn't see him in there, obviously his corpse was in front of the Ark.

The ["sound"]{.class_s4N2-0} is for the benefit of the Israelites in the camp who are observing Aaron's movements. He must [profess ]{.class_s4N4-0}and he must [possess]{.class_s4N4-0}. If he does not bear fruit, []{#c3A2.xhtml_page_646}according to John 15:1--6, he dies. The "golden sound" which comes from the swish of the robe as Aaron marches to and from the Tabernacle is a reminder to the people that the high priest is about "his Father's business," that he claims to be connected with [Deity ]{.class_s4N4-0}(gold symbolizes Deity), that he is anointed to bear [fruit ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Gal. 5:22), and that even in his earthly pilgrimage through the camp, he should never lose the "heavenly ring" of his testimony (Col. 3:1--3).

Ah, the unsearchable riches of the "original English." How boundless are its revelations and how critical its judgments on "recognized" scholarship.

5\. [The BROIDERED COAT]{.class_s4N4-0} (vss. 39--40) .

The ["broidered coat"]{.class_s4N2-0} has a hard time getting on Aaron because the dead-orthodox apostates who translated the [RV, ASV, RSV, NASV, NRSV]{.class_s4N4-0}---and the New ARCV, New VRSA, New ASVR, New VRSA, New VASR (same line)---resent the wording of the text so bitterly that one gets the impression they would throw the verse clear out if they thought they could get away with it.
