In the corrupt [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(old or new), recommended by the faculty members of Wheaton, Moody, Fuller, Dallas, BJU, BIOLA, Midwestern, Mid-South, etc., we find: ["thou shalt weave the coat in checker work"]{.class_s4N4-0} (whatever in blazes that is). The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} (pg. 292) assures us that the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}text is "certainly NOT the meaning of the Hebrew." (This is why we often are quite dogmatic in correcting the errors of men like "the majority of Conservative scholars." They are quite bigoted themselves on occasion, and their contempt for the [King []{#c3A2.xhtml_page_647}James ]{.class_s4N4-0}Bible can only be matched by their contempt for the Holy Spirit who preserved it.) We also find that the ["bonnets"]{.class_s4N2-0} of verses 40 are "certainly NOT bonnets." To help us out with the ["bonnets,"]{.class_s4N2-0} the [RV ]{.class_s4N4-0}of 1885 has written ["HEAD-TIRES."]{.class_s4N4-0} [(My, how much clearer.)]{.class_s4N4-0}

Not content with changing the middle of the verse, the corrupt [NASV]{.class_s4N4-0} has changed ["make the girdle of needlework"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 39) to[ "the work of a weaver." ]{.class_s4N4-0}But we are not through yet. Having altered the [middle ]{.class_s4N4-0}of the verse and the [end ]{.class_s4N4-0}of the verse, Dr. George Rawlinson ([Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0}) tells us that the expression ["thou shalt make the mitre of fine linen"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 39) is "a little out of place." (Well, if you are God the Holy Spirit, I suppose you might make the correction and put it in the right place.)

You see, the putrid stink of Alexandria did not evaporate in A.D. 400. It is still wafted on the hot air from one College professor to another, right up to the Lockman Foundation ([Amplified Version]{.class_s4N4-0} and [New American Standard]{.class_s4N4-0}), so that fifteen centuries of "Conservatives" can smell for themselves the sickening stench of ["Christian]{.class_s4N4-0} education." Isn't [Malachi ]{.class_s4N4-0}a little "out of place"? Isn't [Genesis 5:1--3 ]{.class_s4N4-0}"a little out of place"? Isn't the genealogy of Genesis chapter 46 "a little out of place"? How about Judges chapters 1--2? Shouldn't they be back in Joshua? [YEA, HATH GOD SAID?]{.class_s4N4-0}

Bypassing the ["New" American Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} (and similar trash), we may now examine the text of the Holy Bible.

["The coat"]{.class_s4N2-0} is the innermost garment worn by the high priest, and it was placed on him as soon as he was washed (Lev. 8:7) . The word is the same one used in Genesis 3:21, where God clothed Adam and Eve in[ "coats []{#c3A2.xhtml_page_648}of skin."]{.class_s4N2-0} Josephus states that it had long sleeves which showed out through the ephod (which, of course, had no sleeves). All of the priests wore one of these "coats."

The coat represents inner righteousness, exactly as we find it represented in the ["linen breeches"]{.class_s4N2-0} (see comments on vs. 42). We may make it to be a type of Christ's spotless righteousness as man, but we must never forget that pure GOLD represents His righteousness as God-incarnate in the flesh.

6\. [The MITER ]{.class_s4N4-0}(vss. 37, 39, and 39:28).

The miter is connected with the high priest, both in the English text of Zechariah 3:5 and the Hebrew text of Ezekiel 21:26. In the latter reference, the phony "high priest" of Zechariah 11:15--17 is reigning on earth, but he is overthrown (Rev. 19:19--20) and has to pass the miter on to the "Son of David."

The miter is a sort of "wrap-around turban," and this explains why the Holy Spirit led the translators of the [King James ]{.class_s4N4-0}Bible to substitute ["diadem"]{.class_s4N2-0} for the same word in Ezekiel: the Antichrist has usurped not only the office of Priest ([as Saul]{.class_s4N4-0}), but of KING ([as Saul]{.class_s4N4-0}). (This should teach any novice a great lesson in understanding the Bible. The lesson is learned many times over by those who read the series of [Bible Believer's Commentaries. ]{.class_s4N4-0}It is simply that there is a supernatural hand on the "inconsistencies" of the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}text which cannot even be found in the [consistencies ]{.class_s4N4-0}of the Twentieth Century "Bibles"---any and all of them, by any group of translators of any profession.)

Aaron's sons do not have the miter (for the head) or the breastplate (for the heart) or the onyx stones (for the shoulders of Aaron). Even though their identification with him is tremendous (note ["Aaron and his sons"]{.class_s4N2-0}---[]{#c3A2.xhtml_page_649}29:4, 9, 10, 15, 19, 28, 32), it is never complete, for the Lord Jesus Christ was the ["only begotten"]{.class_s4N2-0} Son of God. Other sons are ["begotten"]{.class_s4N2-0} after they have been born once (1 Cor. 4:15; Phile. 10; 1 John 5:1, 18). Jesus Christ was ["begotten" ]{.class_s4N2-0}the first time He was born. Observe further how there is no "anointing" (vs. 41) until the priests have "put on Christ," in typology!

On the front of the miter was a plate of pure gold, and engraven on it was ["HOLINESS TO THE LORD"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 36). It was tied to the turban by means of a blue lace, exactly as the breastplate was tied around the chest of the priest in verse 28. The ["bonnets"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 40) are not the same as the miter, and the placement of the making of the miter (vs. 39) after its mention (vs. 37) is no more "out of place" than a bowl of rice in front of a Chinaman.

The reader will observe that the expression ["the coat"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 39) shows up out of nowhere with no previous mention between verse 4 and verse 39. Why should one be surprised to find ["the mitre"]{.class_s4N2-0} popping up in verse 39? After all, the main subject of verses 36--38 is the inscription ["HOLINESS UNTO THE LORD"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 36) and its connection with ["the iniquity of the holy things"]{.class_s4N2-0} which ["shall be upon Aaron's forehead"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 38). The golden plate (39:30--31) was connected with ["Aaron's forehead,"]{.class_s4N2-0} thus reminding the serious student of the Scripture that Uzziah---who attempted to usurp the priest's office (2 Chron. 26:16--17)---was smitten in the forehead with leprosy; it also reminds him of the Antichrist who places a mark in the forehead of his worshippers (Rev. 13:15--17). The hand is raised to the forehead in a salute, clearly connecting the "thought" (the forehead) with the "deed" (the hand). []{#c3A2.xhtml_page_650}Hence, the Beast of Revelation 13:16--18 places the mark in either spot.

This would also seem to indicate that ["the]{.class_s4N2-0} [name of blasphemy,"]{.class_s4N2-0} which is on the heads of Leviathan (Rev. 13:1; Job 41:1; Psa. 74:14), is the inscription of verse 36 transferred to the "Son of PERDITION," who will minister in the temple and profess to be ["God\...manifest in the flesh" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(2 Thess. 2:3--9 cf. 1 Tim. 3:16).

The gifts from the Israelites cannot be accepted by the Lord if Aaron takes this golden plate off his miter (vs. 38). That is, holiness is the [first thing]{.class_s4N4-0} due to the Lord (Mal. 1:8), and this means ["WHOLLY"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 Sam. 7:9) as well as ["HOLY"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Lev. 2, 5--8) or ["hallowed"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Matt. 6:9). Conversely, holiness is to be ascribed to God Almighty at all times (Psa. 105:3, 145:21, 89:18, 30:4; Isa. 30:15, 45:11, 47:4, etc., etc.). ["HOLINESS UNTO THE LORD"]{.class_s4N2-0} appears on the bells of the horses (Zech. 14:20--21) in the Millennium.

7\. [The CURIOUS GIRDLE]{.class_s4N4-0} (vs. 8 cf. 39:5).

Again, we are faced with the colors used in the Tabernacle and the ephod (vss. 6--8). The ["girdle"]{.class_s4N2-0} is like a belt, and it held the ephod tight to the priest's waist. It must be remembered that the ephod was only two loose pieces of material hanging over the front and the back of the priest and joined only at the shoulders by the two onyx stones. Soltau's half page on the ["girdle"]{.class_s4N2-0} is remarkable when we consider he gave nine pages to the ["linen breeches"]{.class_s4N2-0}; but one must remember that here Soltau fell into line with Origen, Jerome, Symmachus, Aquila, Keil, Custer, Neal, Thieme, Wuest, Nestle, Delitzsch, et al. when he found the word ["girdle"]{.class_s4N2-0} in verse 8.

This word has been altered to ["skillfully woven band"]{.class_s4N4-0} in the corrupt [New ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [old ASV]{.class_s4N4-0} ([medium old ASV, medium New ASV, and later and earlier ASV]{.class_s4N4-0}), as it is in the corrupt Communist version of 1952 (the [RSV]{.class_s4N4-0}). Soltau tells us that "no idea of[ girding"]{.class_s4N4-0} is connected with the ["girdle"]{.class_s4N2-0} at all. The translators of the [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [NASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}took this at face value, and [without even checking the Holy Spirit's opinion of the matter, they removed Aaron's girdle,]{.class_s4N4-0} since it was not connected with the "idea of girding." To straighten out these old reprobate "Fundamentalists," the Holy Spirit has preserved for us in the infallible [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}text[:]{.class_s4N4-0} ["and he girded him with the curious girdle"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Lev. 8:7). If this is not the idea of "girding," then lassoes are not connected with loops. (Again, the reader will do well to avoid the clumsy bungling of the Lockman Foundation and the "majority of Conservative dupes" and stick with the God-honored text.)

(You were told in the English text that it was a ["curious girdle,"]{.class_s4N2-0} exactly as you were told that the whale of Jonah 2:1, 10---and it was a ["whale" ]{.class_s4N2-0}according to Jesus Christ \[Matt. 12:40\]---was a ["great fish"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Jon. 1:17), clearly indicating a very unusual KIND of fish. We realize that the faculty members at Liberty University are trying to "help God out" \[like Origen at Alexandria\] by forcing the New Testament word to match Jonah 2 \[see the corrupt [New Scofield Reference Bible]{.class_s4N4-0}\], but since Genesis 1:21 clearly listed whales [as separate from the other fishes,]{.class_s4N4-0} we don't have to bring the Scripture in line with something some jackleg found out after the time of Christopher Columbus. Moses antedated him over 2,800 years.)

The ["curious girdle"]{.class_s4N2-0} is unusual in that it is part of the ephod exactly as many modern belts are made as [part of the pants,]{.class_s4N4-0} and the ignorance of the Lockman Foundation (1971) in regards to these matters is not to be emulated by any Christian---Liberal or Conservative. (Go shopping in a men's store this afternoon and get your theology straightened out.)

All of the sons of Aaron have [ordinary girdles]{.class_s4N4-0} (vs. 40), and these typify inner righteousness (Isa. 6:5). The girdles are for "glory and beauty" (vs. 40), exactly as the garments of Aaron (vs. 2). The typology goes further to the "Servant of servants" (John 13:1--5), who girded Himself with a towel to wait on commercial fishermen. (What could be much more smelly than the feet of a commercial fisherman? "Old fishermen never die; they just smell that way," etc.)

Christ's loins were [girded ]{.class_s4N4-0}with swaddling clothes when He lay in Bethehem's manger. They were [girded ]{.class_s4N4-0}with a working towel when He set the pattern for service (see above). They were [girded ]{.class_s4N4-0}with our sins when He died on the cross (1 Pet. 2:24). And they will be [girded for battle]{.class_s4N4-0} (Isa. 59:17) when He returns in blood, fire, and smoke (Rev. 19:11--21).

[]{#c3EG.xhtml}

# Chapter 29 {.heading_s4G-0}

29:1 "And this is the thing that thou shalt do unto them to hallow them, to minister unto me in the priest's office: Take one young bullock, and two rams without blemish.

2 And unleavened bread, and cakes unleavened tempered with oil, and wafers unleavened anointed with oil: of wheaten flour shalt thou make them.

3 And thou shalt put them into one basket, and bring them in the basket, with the bullock and the two rams.

4 And Aaron and his sons thou shalt bring unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, and shalt wash them with water.

5 And thou shalt take the garments, and put upon Aaron the coat, and the robe of the ephod, and the ephod, and the breastplate, and gird him with the curious girdle of the ephod:

6 And thou shalt put the mitre upon his head, and put the holy crown upon the mitre.

7 Then shalt thou take the anointing oil, and pour it upon his head, and anoint him.

8 And thou shalt bring his sons, and put coats upon them.

9 And thou shalt gird them with girdles, Aaron and his sons, and put the bonnets on them: and the priest's office shall be theirs for a perpetual statute: and thou shalt consecrate Aaron and his sons.

10 And thou shalt cause a bullock to be brought before the tabernacle of the congregation: and Aaron and his sons shall put their hands upon the head of the bullock.

11 And thou shalt kill the bullock before the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}, by the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.

12 And thou shalt take of the blood of the bullock, and put it upon the horns of the altar with thy finger, and pour all the blood beside the bottom of the altar.

13 And thou shalt take all the fat that covereth the inwards, and the caul that is above the liver, and the two kidneys, and the fat that is upon them, and burn them upon the altar.

14 But the flesh of the bullock, and his skin, and his dung, shalt thou burn with fire without the camp: it is a sin offering."

The instructions are for the consecration of ["Aaron and his sons"]{.class_s4N2-0} in the office of priests and high priest, and the expression ["Aaron and his sons"]{.class_s4N2-0} occurs in verses 4, 9, 10, 15, 19, 20, 28, 32, 35, and 44. Having established Aaron as a type of the Lord Jesus Christ (note Numbers chapter 17 in particular, in regards to the resurrection), it follows that ["his sons"]{.class_s4N2-0} are types of the spiritual priesthood of the believer (see 1 Pet. 2:5--9 cf. Heb. 13:15--16). It is absolutely necessary following this consecration for the ["altar of incense"]{.class_s4N2-0} to show up, as the priest must intercede for the people (Luke 1:9--10). The placement of this altar (chap. 30) is absolutely correct, and the befuddled thinking of the commentators about its "proper placement" is only a witness to their darkened hearts and blinded minds. Exodus 30:1 is the proper place for prayer (Rev. 8:3--4), for the priests have been set apart to serve in chapter 29.

The consecration consisted of WASHING (vs. 4), INVESTITURE (vss. 5--9), ANOINTING (vs. 7), and SACRIFICE (vss. 10--14). Three types of bread []{#c3EG.xhtml_page_655}accompanied the offering: ["unleavened bread" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 2), ["cakes"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 2), and ["wafers"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 2). These were all "waved" before the Lord (vss. 23--24) as an offering, along with the animal sacrifices. The ["young bullock" ]{.class_s4N2-0}of verse 1 was not offered until AFTER the erection of the Tabernacle (Lev. 8:10). The two rams are for: 1. a ["burnt offering,"]{.class_s4N2-0} 2. a ["ram of consecration"]{.class_s4N2-0} (see Lev. 8:18--19, 22). The order of investiture is found in Leviticus 8:7--9, which states that first of all the linen tunic went on, then the undergirdle, then the robe of the ephod, then the ephod, the curious girdle, the breastplate, then the Urim and Thummim, finally the miter, and then ["the golden plate"]{.class_s4N2-0} on the miter reading ["HOLINESS TO THE LORD."]{.class_s4N2-0} After this, Aaron was anointed (vs. 7 cf. Lev. 8:30).

A number of great and precious truths present themselves in the passage, and in spite of the bungling [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} (which states "the directions\...are [incomplete ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [not quite in the right order"]{.class_s4N4-0}) and other commentaries, we find:

1\. Aaron and his SONS were to be [taken ]{.class_s4N4-0}(vs. 1), [brought ]{.class_s4N4-0}(vs. 4), [washed ]{.class_s4N4-0}(vs. 4), [clothed ]{.class_s4N4-0}(vss. 5--6), and [anointed ]{.class_s4N4-0}(vss. 7, 21). The New Testament parallels are striking to any child of God. He was [taken ]{.class_s4N4-0}out of the world and [brought ]{.class_s4N4-0}to Calvary (John 6:44), he was [washed ]{.class_s4N4-0}by the blood and the Spirit (1 Cor. 6:11; 1 Pet. 1:2), he was [clothed ]{.class_s4N4-0}with God's righteousness (Isa. 61:10), and then he was [anointed ]{.class_s4N4-0}by the Holy Spirit (1 John 2:27). He is [girded ]{.class_s4N4-0}with the truth (vs. 9) and is a priest forever (vs. 9---["for a perpetual statute"]{.class_s4N2-0}), sharing the eternal priesthood of the great Melchizedek who ["abideth a priest continually" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(Heb. 7:1--3).

2\. Aaron is anointed before his sons (vs. 7), and the oil is "poured out," while his sons have it "sprinkled" on them (vs. 21). The anointing of 40:15 is still not the "pouring" of verse 7, clearly showing us that Christ was ["anointed with the oil of gladness above thy fellows"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Heb. 1:9), and God gave ["not the Spirit by measure unto him" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(John 3:34). (It is possible that 40:15 refers to future high priests who succeed Aaron and not to all of the sons contemporaneous with Aaron at that time.)

3\. Aaron was anointed on the [head ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Lev. 21:10--12) as a King was (1 Sam. 10:1), and the anointing oil ran down over his garments as well (Psa. 133:2). This anointing oil is described in 30:22--33.

Adam Clarke is greatly mistaken in telling you that "no man was ever dignified by holding the three offices" (Prophet, Priest, and King) before Christ (Comment. pg. 452). David was anointed three times (1 Sam. 16:3, 13; 2 Sam. 2:4, 7, 5:3, 17). David was a PROPHET according to Simon Peter (Acts 2:30); he was a PRIEST according to 2 Samuel 24:25; and he was a KING by all accounts, anywhere (Matt. 1:6).

4\. The Tabernacle and its furniture were all anointed (Lev. 8:10--11; Exod. 30:26--29), so the typology is clear. The Holy Spirit Himself anointed the Son of Man (Matt. 3:16). This Spirit is ["one"]{.class_s4N2-0} with the water and the blood (1 John 5:7--8) and was poured out at Pentecost on some "temples" (1 Cor. 6:19) instead of the Temple. The approach to God (the Altar, Laver, Candlestick, etc.) was sanctified by the Holy Ghost, and the Christian is ONE with the Lord by ONE Spirit in ONE body (Eph. 4:4--6).

The blasphemous [New American Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} (as the old one of 1901) takes the "anointing" off []{#c3EG.xhtml_page_657}the Lord Jesus when He dies by removing the word ["Lord"]{.class_s4N2-0} from Luke 23:42, thus enforcing the ancient Monarchist teaching that "the Christ" came on Jesus at His baptism and [left Him]{.class_s4N4-0} at his crucifixion. This blatant and blasphemous denial of the Deity of Christ is recommended [by the faculty at Bob Jones University]{.class_s4N4-0} and other "face-saving" organizations who fear the wrath of dead-orthodox "scholarship." The real Bible believer is not to countenance such infidelity for a moment, no matter [who recommends what.]{.class_s4N4-0}

The reader will observe, again, the order of regeneration and daily cleansing (Tit. 3:5; John 13:10). Aaron and his sons are washed ALL OVER (John 13:10 cf. Exod. 29:4) once, but still must wash their feet (John 13:10) daily at the Laver.

There can be no [washing ]{.class_s4N4-0}until the [sacrifice ]{.class_s4N4-0}is brought (vs. 1). There can be no "putting on of Christ" till there has been the washing (vs. 4), and no "anointing" (vs. 7) until Christ has been ["put on"]{.class_s4N4-0} (vs. 5). What the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} calls "not quite the right order" is the divinely inspired and perfectly preserved, infallible truth, in the exact order of doctrinal revelation. ("Mistakes" in the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}\[1611\] are usually [advanced truth]{.class_s4N4-0} which linguists cannot find "in the [originals."]{.class_s4N4-0})

In regards to the bullock (vss. 1, 10, 11--14), the reader should notice that MINISTERS are likened to [oxen ]{.class_s4N4-0}(1 Cor. 9:9--11); hence, although a LAMB does the work of atoning for every sinner (chap. 12), a [bullock ]{.class_s4N4-0}is needed for the consecration of the minister. The bullock is ["a sin offering"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 14) for the minister, and although the blood of a ram (vs. 20) is applied to him, ["the blood of the bullock"]{.class_s4N2-0} is applied directly to the altar (vs. 12) to consecrate the minister's attendance on that part of []{#c3EG.xhtml_page_658}God's service. Another remarkable similitude shows up, since the "laying on of hands" for the "ministry" (1 Tim. 4:14; Acts 13:3) is shown by laying hands on the [bullock ]{.class_s4N4-0}(vs. 10). Although the former is a transference of an authority and the latter is a transference of sins (in type), the similitude is remarkable.

The [bullock ]{.class_s4N4-0}was the first offering. Without it, none of the offerings of Israel would have been accepted, for the bullock sanctified the altar upon which the rest of the sacrifices were offered (vs. 12). Since the blood was poured out at the bottom of the altar (vs. 12), the altar literally stood on bloody ground (Mark 14:24; Heb. 9:21; Lev. 4:7, 18, 25, 30). The best parts of the bullock were given wholly to God (vs. 13), and the smelly part (hair, hide, dung) was burned outside the camp (vs. 14), where it certainly sent up a stench (2 Cor. 2:16). ["WITHOUT THE CAMP"]{.class_s4N2-0} shows us Hebrews 13:11--13 in no uncertain terms. Jesus was not only crucified as a perfect Lamb without spot or blemish, He was cast outside of His own city by the Levitical priesthood. As far as they were concerned, His testimony was a dead ox that "stank" (see 2 Cor. 2:16)! (The ["caul"]{.class_s4N2-0} of verse 13 is the membrane over or upon the liver.)

29:15 "Thou shalt also take one ram; and Aaron and his sons shall put their hands upon the head of the ram.

16 And thou shalt slay the ram, and thou shalt take his blood, and sprinkle it round about upon the altar.

17 And thou shalt cut the ram in pieces, and wash the inwards of him, and his legs, and put them unto his pieces, and unto his head.

18 And thou shalt burn the whole ram upon the altar: it is a burnt offering unto the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}: it is a sweet savour, an offering made by fire unto the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}.

19 And thou shalt take the other ram; and Aaron and his sons shall put their hands upon the head of the ram.

20 Then shalt thou kill the ram, and take of his blood, and put it upon the tip of the right ear of Aaron, and upon the tip of the right ear of his sons, and upon the thumb of their right hand, and upon the great toe of their right foot, and sprinkle the blood upon the altar round about.

21 And thou shalt take of the blood that is upon the altar, and of the anointing oil, and sprinkle it upon Aaron, and upon his garments, and upon his sons, and upon the garments of his sons with him: and he shall be hallowed, and his garments, and his sons, and his sons' garments with him.

22 Also thou shalt take of the ram the fat and the rump, and the fat that covereth the inwards, and the caul above the liver, and the two kidneys, and the fat that is upon them, and the right shoulder; for it is a ram of consecration:

23 And one loaf of bread, and one cake of oiled bread, and one wafer out of the basket of the unleavened bread that is before the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}:

24 And thou shalt put all in the hands of Aaron, and in the hands of his sons; and shalt wave them for a wave offering before the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}.

25 And thou shalt receive them of their hands, and burn them upon the altar for a burnt offering, for a sweet savour before the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}: it is an offering made by fire unto the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}.

26 And thou shalt take the breast of the ram of Aaron's consecration, and wave it for a wave offering before the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}: and it shall be thy part.

27 And thou shalt sanctify the breast of the wave offering, and the shoulder of the heave offering, which is waved, and which is heaved up, of the ram of the consecration, even of that which is for Aaron, and of that which is for his sons:

28 And it shall be Aaron's and his sons' by a statute for ever from the children of Israel: for it is an heave offering: and it shall be an heave offering from the children of Israel of the sacrifice of their peace offerings, even their heave offering unto the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}."

The ram is ["a burnt offering"]{.class_s4N2-0} instead of a sin offering. The distinctions are as follows:

1\. BURNT OFFERINGS were daily offerings (vss. 41--42) and could be taken from the herd, flock, or fowls (Lev. 1:3, 10, 14). The offerer presented himself at the door of the tabernacle ["before the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord]{.class_s4N9-0}["]{.class_s4N2-0} (Lev. 1:3) and laid his hands on the victim. It was then killed and the blood was sprinkled. (This much of the ceremony matches the "sin offering" and "peace offering.") [Burnt offerings,]{.class_s4N4-0} except for the skin (Lev. 7:8), were burned completely, for they were given wholly to the Lord (Lev. 1:9, 13, 17) as ["a sweet savour"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Lev. 1:9). (The corrupt [Septuagint ]{.class_s4N4-0}---written many, many years after the New Testament was completed---has transferred ["sweet SMELLING savour" ]{.class_s4N4-0}\[Eph. 5:2\], but this is SOP for the depraved scribes at Alexandria \[A.D. 150--400\] who wanted you to think that the apostles were stupid enough to reverence a GREEK Old Testament.)

2\. SIN OFFERINGS were connected with a guilty sinner coming and seeking forgiveness, not a [saint ]{.class_s4N4-0}in fellowship offering something of his own voluntary free will (as the burnt offerings were offered). [The sin offerings]{.class_s4N4-0} of Leviticus chapter 4 and 6:25--30 cover four classes: the priest (Lev. 4:3), the congregation (Lev. 4:13), the ruler (Lev. 4:22), and an individual member of the congregation (Lev. 4:27). The victim is to be ["without blemish"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Lev. 4:3, 23, 28, 32), and in the case of a ruler, the blood was not carried into the holy place (Lev. 4:25). (In the case of the whole congregation, or the priest, the blood was sprinkled seven times before the veil and put on the horns of the Altar of Incense, Lev. 4:17--18.) Furthermore, the flesh of the sin offering could be "eaten" (Lev. 6:26--29), provided no part of it had been connected with blood sprinkled INSIDE the sanctuary (see Lev. 6:30). The "eating" would symbolize the restored communion of the sinner after offering the required sacrifice for sins.

Unlike the [bullock]{.class_s4N4-0}, the whole ram is burned on the altar---hide, dung, skin, and all (vs. 18). Note further that the offering has been decapitated (vs. 17, ["and unto his head"]{.class_s4N2-0}), as human beings will be decapitated (Rev. 20:4) at the altar (Rev. 6:9) in the Tribulation, before they are eaten by the "priests of Baal" ([COHEN-BAAL,]{.class_s4N4-0} "cannibal") .

In all of the sacrifices, the sacrifice of Jesus Christ is evident. It takes blood before our offerings are accepted, it takes [blood ]{.class_s4N4-0}when they are accepted, and even our "consecration" is "through His blood" (Heb. 9:14). Leviticus 14:14, 17 is quite similar to the cleansing process described here in verse 20. The ["ram of consecration"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 22) is different than the ram for []{#c3EG.xhtml_page_662}the[ burnt offering,]{.class_s4N4-0} and it is the blood of the former that is applied to the ears, thumbs, and toes of the sons of Aaron (vs. 20). This application shows us clearly that "consecration" leads us to [listen ]{.class_s4N4-0}to the right kind of music and preaching, to [walk ]{.class_s4N4-0}in the paths of righteousness for His name's sake, and to do only ["those things that are pleasing in his sight"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 John 3:22). The hand, the foot, and the ear are involved. The same blood that was used in anointing them is sprinkled ["upon the altar]{.class_s4N2-0} [round about"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 20). That is, consecration (as salvation) is connected inseparably with the shed blood of the atonement at Calvary.

There is no ["consecration"]{.class_s4N2-0} till the whole ram is burned (Rom. 12:1--2). The reader will observe that in the case of the first ram (and the bullock), the inner and outer flesh is burned (["flesh"]{.class_s4N2-0} and ["skin,"]{.class_s4N2-0} vs. 14 cf. vs. 18). The flesh cannot please God, so the fire of His wrath is the only cure for its infirmities. God requires a NEW CREATURE in the New Testament: first a spiritual new creature (John 3:3--5) and then a physical new creature (1 Cor. 15:49--53). No substitute will do. The old creature is only fit for the fire (Ezek. 15:4--7), exactly as the old "creation" (2 Pet. 3:4--7) is fit for the fire (2 Pet. 3:10--13).

The Christian is not his own; he is ["bought with a price"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 Cor. 6:19--20). Therefore, his hands and feet and ears (and eyes---see Num. 15:39) are the Lord's (1 Cor. 12:12--27).

The parts that are to be offered are:

a\. ["The fat and the rump"]{.class_s4N2-0}[:]{.class_s4N4-0} the heavy fleshy tail of the ram, which in some cases weighed as much as thirty pounds.

b\. ["The fat that covereth the inwards"]{.class_s4N2-0}: all the fleshy portions around the internal organs.

c\. ["The caul above the liver"]{.class_s4N2-0}: the membrane surrounding the liver.

d\. ["The two kidneys."]{.class_s4N2-0}

e\. The fat that is on the kidneys.

f\. ["The right shoulder."]{.class_s4N2-0}

g\. ["The breast of the ram"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 26). (Aaron and his sons are allowed to eat this part with the shoulder after the objects have been "heaved" and "waved" before the Lord \[vss. 24--28\].)

All of the offering bespeaks of the "best of the ram." The [inner part]{.class_s4N4-0} includes the parts which control the purity of the blood, and ["the life of the flesh is in the blood"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Lev. 17:11). ["The right shoulder"]{.class_s4N2-0}[ ]{.class_s4N4-0}is the burden-bearing part of a man (as in Isa. 9:6), and the [breast ]{.class_s4N4-0}is plainly a reference to the heart (Psa. 16:9; John 13:25). (Christ our sacrifice offered to God a pure, spotless, sinless sacrifice, cleansed within and without.) The second ram comes under the heading of a PEACE OFFERING (cf. remarks above on sin offerings and burnt offerings).

3\. [The PEACE OFFERING]{.class_s4N4-0} (Lev. 3:6, 7:11--21, 28--34).

As a ["thank offering,"]{.class_s4N4-0} unleavened bread mingled with oil accompanied it (vs. 23); it was a [communion ]{.class_s4N4-0}feast rather than an [atonement]{.class_s4N4-0}. Leavened and unleavened bread accompanied most of the peace offerings (Lev. 7:12--13), and the priests got to eat the breast and right shoulder of the offering (Lev. 7:33--34). The offerer was entitled to the rest of the offering (Lev. 7:15, 22:29--30). It symbolizes our ["peace WITH God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rom. 5:1), our ["peace FROM God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 Cor. 1:3), and ["the peace OF God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Phil. 4:7). No [unclean person]{.class_s4N4-0} could partake of []{#c3EG.xhtml_page_664}the feast of this [peace offering]{.class_s4N4-0} (Lev. 7:20--21), and no [stranger ]{.class_s4N4-0}could eat of it (Lev. 22:10, 25). Furthermore, it could not be eaten if it were defiled before it could be partaken of (Lev. 7:19). These lessons show us that God's peace is not as the world's peace (John 14:27), and that no one is entitled to it (John 3:36) save those who have already been cleansed at the Brazen Altar by the blood atonement of a sinless sacrifice. The offerings of verses 19--28 are said to be WAVED and HEAVED, and this points out another remarkable truth: in the act of WAVING the bread (vss. 23--24) back and forth and HEAVING the meat (vss. 27--28) up and down, a CROSS is clearly drawn in the air.

The ["meat offering"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Lev. 2:1, 4, 6:14--18) resembles the instructions from verse 23. In these items we have a picture of Christ as the bread of life, without leaven in character or deportment (Matt. 16:6--12), His life perfumed with the frankincense of Song of Solomon 3:5--7 and ["salted with salt"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Mark 9:49; Lev. 2:13).

Consecration then, [in type,]{.class_s4N4-0} consists of:

a\. Being saved: no ["ram of consecration" ]{.class_s4N2-0}[before ]{.class_s4N4-0}the burnt offering.

b\. The blood of Christ applied by the Holy Spirit (vs. 21).

c\. The members of the body given as a living sacrifice to God (vs. 20).

d\. The best things of life offered up to God (vss. 22--24) .

e\. These things offered up because of the CROSS (vss. 24--28).

f\. Real consecration is as deep an experience as salvation; note the matching of verse 18 (the ram of burnt offering) with verse 25 (the ["ram of []{#c3EG.xhtml_page_665}consecration"]{.class_s4N2-0}): ["a burnt offering\...made by fire unto the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord]{.class_s4N9-0}[."]{.class_s4N2-0}

g\. Earned rewards, which accompany any real service for God (vs. 27).

Observe, also, one item which is often overlooked. In the context, [Moses]{.class_s4N4-0}---not Aaron---is offering the sacrifice (Lev. 8:15). In verses 25 and 26, we read: ["And thou shalt receive them\...And thou shalt take\...and it shall be thy part."]{.class_s4N2-0} Here, plainly, is a transference of priestly power from Moses to [Aaron]{.class_s4N4-0}, for until now, Moses has been making the offerings (24:6). Moses, therefore, gets ["the breast of the ram"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 26) on the day that he installs the Aaronic priesthood.

29:29 "And the holy garments of Aaron shall be his sons' after him, to be anointed therein, and to be consecrated in them.

30 And that son that is priest in his stead shall put them on seven days, when he cometh into the tabernacle of the congregation to minister in the holy place."

The ["seven days"]{.class_s4N2-0} of verse 30 are found again in verses 35 and 37. They indicate a special time of installation for the priests and the complete sanctification and atonement for the ALTAR. Although Soltau has much to say about the "eighth day service" ([The Tabernacle,]{.class_s4N4-0} pp. 404--413), the real typology escapes him entirely. Again, the Bible believer who is acquainted with the ["Seven System"]{.class_s4N4-0} has a tremendous advantage over the Hebrew and Chaldean scholars whose meager knowledge is limited to 25 years of formal education in the "original languages."

The Feast of Tabernacles had an "eighth day service" (Lev. 23:36, 39; Num. 29:35) to picture the great ["Eighth Day"]{.class_s4N4-0} of Revelation chapters 21--22, where ["the tabernacle of God"]{.class_s4N2-0} will be ["with men"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rev. 21:3--6).

Circumcision was on the eighth day (Gen. 17:12), to foreshadow the revelation of the Creator (Rev. 20:11), when the heaven and earth will be dropped as a garment from His person (Heb. 1:11--12).

The firstling of oxen, man, and sheep were God's (22:30) ["on the eighth day,"]{.class_s4N2-0} for that is the FINAL day on God's calendar for the ["restitution of all things"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Acts 3:21), when Eden will be restored---animals and all (Isa. 11:6--9)!

The Nazarite, the cleansed leper, and the priest each had an ["eighth day"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Num. 6:10; Lev. 14:10, 23, 15:14, 29) to picture the saved sinner (serving as priest, see 28:1--3 and comments) resurrected with Christ, who rose on the EIGHTH DAY.

Now Origen (as Jerome, Augustine, Soltau, Moorehead, and the Postmillennialists of the Nineteenth Century) spiritualized this "eighth day" bit and confined it to the "new man in Christ" only. This entirely overlooks the fact that the whole Bible is erected on a ["Seven System"]{.class_s4N4-0} where eight is an OCTAVE up from seven. (There is no time here to speak of these things in detail, but those interested should purchase the pamphlet [The Seven Sevens]{.class_s4N4-0} or the work by Clarence Larkin, [Dispensational Truth.]{.class_s4N4-0})

Before speaking of the "seven days" of our text, we shall reproduce the related material found in verses 31--37.

29:31 "And thou shalt take the ram of the consecration, and seethe his flesh in the holy place.

32 And Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram, and the bread that is in the basket, by the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.

33 And they shall eat those things wherewith the atonement was made, to consecrate and to sanctify them: but a stranger shall not eat thereof, because they are holy.

34 And if ought of the flesh of the consecrations, or of the bread, remain unto the morning, then thou shalt burn the remainder with fire: it shall not be eaten, because it is holy.

35 And thus shalt thou do unto Aaron, and to his sons, according to all things which I have commanded thee: seven days shalt thou consecrate them.

36 And thou shalt offer every day a bullock for a sin offering for atonement: and thou shalt cleanse the altar, when thou hast made an atonement for it, and thou shalt anoint it, to sanctify it.

37 Seven days thou shalt make an atonement for the altar, and sanctify it; and it shall be an altar most holy: whatsoever toucheth the altar shall be holy."

No arrangements have been made for "new clothes" for Aaron and his sons (vs. 29) after they wear out, although we assume that some new clothes had to be made between 1400 B.C. and 600 B.C. (see 1 Sam. 2:18--19). It is possible that God could preserve the original []{#c3EG.xhtml_page_668}clothing of Aaron the high priest to pass on to the next high priest, for we are told that one suit of clothes (and shoes) lasted the Israelites forty years (Deut. 29:5) when they were walking on rock, sand, gravel, and stones.

["And the son that is priest in his stead"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 30) reminds us of our position as representatives of Christ on earth (Luke 10:16), and Paul's intercession for the Gentiles as the official successor to the ["minister of the circumcision"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rom. 15:8). Every Christian stands ["in Christ's stead"]{.class_s4N2-0} in the sense of 2 Corinthians 5:20, and as we have said before, we have no more need of a Baalite "hood" to "represent Christ" than we have need of a migraine headache.

The length of time for consecration (vs. 35) matches the length of time given for the complete sanctification of the altar (vs. 37). The ATONEMENT then, either here or in actuality (Heb. 10:8--12), stretches from eternity to eternity, for observers of the ["Seven System"]{.class_s4N4-0} know that it covers [seven millennia]{.class_s4N4-0} from Genesis 1:1 to Revelation 20:1. The One ["slain from the foundation of the world"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rev. 13:8) offers an atonement which covers the entire time span from the creation to the new creation.

The word ["atonement"]{.class_s4N2-0} occurs for the first time in the Bible in verse 33. The Hebrew word [kaphar]{.class_s4N4-0} signifies "to make a covering," so the spiritual mind will not fail to run back through Exodus to Genesis and stop at Genesis 3:21, where the Lord God covers His disobedient "children" that they might not be cast from His presence eternally. The scholars' penchant for running to Genesis 6:14 (["pitch it"]{.class_s4N2-0}) to prove that the English word in the [AV]{.class_s4N4-0} has no connection with the Hebrew is merely more foolishness, and this foolishness (as all scholastic foolishness) is designed to derail the believer from the []{#c3EG.xhtml_page_669}main track and get him following scholarship instead of the Author of revelation. When a man's sins are covered, he can be "AT ONE" with God, and the word ["atonement"]{.class_s4N2-0} could not have been improved upon if you had the "verbally inspired, God-breathed, etceteras" in your hands. There is no "reconciliation" without a "covering," and if the word [covering ]{.class_s4N4-0}had been used in verse 33, it would have been the [wrong word entirely,]{.class_s4N4-0} for the sentence would then have read that [Aaron and his]{.class_s4N4-0} [sons made the covering. ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Note: ["they shall eat those things wherewith the atonement was made,"]{.class_s4N2-0} vs. 33, but it is GOD who makes the "covering.")

["The atonement"]{.class_s4N2-0} was made with the "covering" that God furnished. The English word, therefore, is absolutely correct, and the fact that it does not match any Hebrew text, as defined in any Hebrew lexicon, means [NOTHING AT ALL.]{.class_s4N4-0}

(This is merely one more case---among scores---where we see the hand of God in the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}\[1611\] committee manifested to CONTRADICT and OVERTHROW all rules of grammar, scholarship, translation, exegesis, and interpretation. When in doubt, ignore the "originals" and go by the infallible, absolute, perfect, and authoritative living word of the living God. ["Atonement"]{.class_s4N2-0} is correct, and if the Hebrew does not mean this, then God will certainly [straighten the Hebrew lexicon out so that it reads correctly in this instance. ]{.class_s4N4-0}The fact that no major scholar since 1611 would translate [kaphar]{.class_s4N4-0} as [atonement]{.class_s4N4-0} means only one thing---100% of the major scholars, since that time, [were destructive critics who wished to replace the supreme authority with their own education. ]{.class_s4N4-0}\[Live on that one and die on it; it's as good as anybody's opinion\].)

In regards to the ["seven days"]{.class_s4N2-0} of atonement and consecration:

1\. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown miss the whole point of the entire passage by saying that the ["seven days"]{.class_s4N2-0} are to "AFFORD A SUFFICIENT interval for calm and devout reflection" (Commentary, pg. 76).

2\. Dummelow skips the whole passage, and the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} makes no application of ["seven" ]{.class_s4N2-0}to anything in the Scriptures or out of the Scriptures.

3\. Dr. Johnson ([Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0}) leaps over verses 34--38 with no mention of anything involved.

4\. Clarke wastes half a page on the mystical symbology of SEVEN in pagan literature and in the Jewish [Kaballah.]{.class_s4N4-0}

(These are some of the men who dislike the word ["atonement"]{.class_s4N2-0} for [kaphar]{.class_s4N4-0}!)

The detailed fulfillment of verses 32--38 is found in Leviticus chapter 8, where we are told that Aaron and his sons must remain INSIDE THE TABERNACLE (Lev. 8:33) for the seven days of consecration. On the eighth day (Lev. 9:1), they come out, and ["the glory of the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord]{.class_s4N9-0}["]{.class_s4N2-0} (Lev. 9:6) appears to the whole congregation (Lev. 9:24). At this time, the sin offering, burnt offering, meat offering, and peace offering are all given [at one time ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Lev. 9:3--4); and fire consumes any part of them left over (vs. 34). This clearly shows the reader that he is dealing with the ["eighth day"]{.class_s4N2-0} of Revelation 20:11, when the heavens and earth are on fire and the elements melt with a fervent heat. Everywhere the typology matches, as the ["Seven System"]{.class_s4N4-0} confirms itself to be the absolute rule for interpreting any prophetic passage that deals with time limitations or time brackets. Soltau wrongly []{#c3EG.xhtml_page_671}places the passage into Revelation 20:1--4. (We have commented on vss. 32--33 under vss. 23--28).

After SEVEN bullocks have been offered, the altar is fit to ["sanctify"]{.class_s4N2-0} any gift placed on it (vss. 36--37)---see Matthew 23:19. When Joab touches the altar (1 Kings 2:28--32), he is ["holy,"]{.class_s4N2-0} and this explains the reticence on the part of Jehoiada to kill him. However, the law of capital punishment given for murderers can turn the word ["holy"]{.class_s4N2-0} into something "set apart" and "dedicated" to God for DESTRUCTION (note Num. 16:38). Joab is killed exactly as a bullock tied to the ["horns"]{.class_s4N2-0} was killed (see comments on 27:2).

29:38 "Now this is that which thou shalt offer upon the altar; two lambs of the first year day by day continually.

39 The one lamb thou shalt offer in the morning; and the other lamb thou shalt offer at even:

40 And with the one lamb a tenth deal of flour mingled with the fourth part of an hin of beaten oil; and the fourth part of an hin of wine for a drink offering.

41 And the other lamb thou shalt offer at even, and shalt do thereto according to the meat offering of the morning, and according to the drink offering thereof, for a sweet savour, an offering made by[ ]{.class_s4NF-1}fire unto the [Lord]{.class_s4N9-1}.

42 This shall be a continual burnt offering throughout your generations at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation before the [Lord]{.class_s4N9-1}: where I will meet you, to speak there unto thee.

43 And there I will meet with the children of Israel, and the tabernacle shall be sanctified by my glory.

44 And I will sanctify the tabernacle of the congregation, and the altar: I will sanctify also both Aaron and his sons, to minister to me in the priest's office.

45 And I will dwell among the children of Israel, and will be their God.

46 And they shall know that I am the LORD their God, that brought them forth out of the land of Egypt, that I may dwell among them: I am the LORD their God."

The reader cannot fail to see the similarity between the last four verses of the chapter and Revelation chapter 20--21. We have a foreshadowing of this final day (["the eighth day"]{.class_s4N2-0}) in the Millennial sabbath (Ezek. 40--48), but we find the sons of Aaron (vs. 44) are still serving in Eternity (Isa. 66:20--24)---at least until the ["thousand generations"]{.class_s4N2-0} are past (Psa. 105:8), which precede the subjection of the Son to the Father (1 Cor. 15:28). Most of this is obscure to the eyes of the Lockman Foundation, the faculty at Moody Bible Institute, the teachers at Hyles-Anderson, the Scofield Board of Editors, and the Greek and Hebrew professors at Piedmont, Fuller, Mid-South, Pillsbury, Columbia, etc. But one must never forget that where the Bible is subjected to monument builders, its great and precious truths appear "impractical" and "nonessential" to the saved sinner. His love for the Book is replaced by his love for "God's work": i.e., [the work that the saved sinner himself is doing.]{.class_s4NG}

No lamb shows up on the altar until the altar is hallowed (vs. 38). It is not enough for the Lamb of God to be perfectly consecrated to God. His DEATH must be God's death, in [God's way,]{.class_s4N4-0} by [God's means]{.class_s4N4-0}. The FIRE that falls (Lev. 9:24) is from [Heaven]{.class_s4N4-0}, not from a [man]{.class_s4N4-0} (cf. counterfeit in Rev. 13:13).

The lambs are offered at the start of the day and the close of the day (vs. 39) as ["a continual burnt offering"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 42). This is the time of ["the evening sacrifice"]{.class_s4N2-0} spoken of in 1 Kings 18:29 and Ezra 9:4. David (Psa. 141:2) prays at this time, and in Psalm 55:17, his prayer life matches the prayer life of Daniel, who is ["upon his knees three times a day"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Dan. 6:10), windows open ["toward Jerusalem"]{.class_s4N2-0} (see also 2 Chron. 31:3).

We [start ]{.class_s4N4-0}the day out with the Lamb, and we end the day with the Lamb (Rev. 21:23). The difference between us and the Old Testament saint is that he had to have it offered daily (Heb. 10:11), but we have had it offered ONCE forever (Heb. 10: 12). Our Roman Catholic friends still insist on the Old Testament method. They weekly "offer the Lamb" and then go on with a defiled conscience (Heb. 10:2; Tit. 1:16) that has been ["seared with a hot iron"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 Tim. 4:23), unable to claim the promise of John 6:54---even where they have used the verse to prove the validity of their "sacrament."

(The grossly corrupt Vatican manuscript \[[B]{.class_s4N4-0}---[Vaticanus]{.class_s4N4-0}\], recommended by the Greek faculty at Bob Jones University \[it is the basis---90%---of Nestle's Greek text, of any edition\], stops at Hebrews 9:14 for the obvious reason that the men who wrote it \[and corrupted it\] deeply resented Hebrews chapter 10. This blasphemous corruption---as the forged ["Donation []{#c3EG.xhtml_page_674}of Constantine"]{.class_s4N4-0} and the forged ["Isidorian Decretals"]{.class_s4N4-0}---is quite typical of Rome. Her "Irish Republican Army," composed of nothing but Catholics who are in communion with Rome and in fellowship with the Catholic Church, is quite similar to Castro's armies and Hitler's armies in that none of the leaders of these armies were publicly excommunicated from the church, no matter what they did. The issue between Adolf and Pope Pius XI \[1935--1940\] was not the rape of Europe and the genocide of the Jews; it was "who got to run the German youth---the Hitler Youth organizations or Catholic Action"? The issue with Castro was [not ]{.class_s4N4-0}the bloody overthrow of established order or pacts made with Russia: [it was church property. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Small wonder that informed men like Dean Burgon, Dr. Wilkinson, Dr. Hills, Prebendary Scrivener, David Otis Fuller, and J. J. Ray would be highly suspicious of any "Bible manuscript" to which the Vatican might lay claim as being authentic. The wretched [New American Standard Version,]{.class_s4N4-0} advertised in [The Sword of the Lord,]{.class_s4N4-0} is established on the authority of this depraved manuscript. The real Bible believer, in the closing years of this age, should classify this depravation with the [New English Bible]{.class_s4N4-0} and the [RSV ]{.class_s4N4-0}of the NCCC; none of them are even "fairly reliable"---let alone "thoroughly reliable.")

The Vatican manuscript, as the phony [Septuagint ]{.class_s4N4-0}(called "[LXX]{.class_s4N4-0}" in the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0}), again has attempted to "help God out." Having read 1 Peter 1:19 many years after the death of the Apostle Paul, the writer of the "[LXX]{.class_s4N4-0}" has written ["without blemish"]{.class_s4N4-0} into verse 38 (see vs. 18 for similar shennanigans). These glaring examples of a post-Christian date for the "[LXX]{.class_s4N4-0}" are so numerous---and so obvious---that it is a marvel []{#c3EG.xhtml_page_675}that the dead-orthodox Conservatives still insist on a pre-Christian "[LXX]{.class_s4N4-0}." (When Dr. Neal and Dr. Custer \[Bob Jones University\] tried to justify their superstitions about the Dead Sea Scrolls, they pointed to a [Hebrew ]{.class_s4N4-0}COMMENTARY \[!\] as a case in point. Any intelligent man would know that one [Hebrew ]{.class_s4N4-0}commentary on Habakkuk in 150 B.C. is no evidence that an official version of the Old Testament was completed in [GREEK ]{.class_s4N4-0}before A.D. 200. The logic that leads men to think that one commentary [in HEBREW,]{.class_s4N4-0} before 4 B.C. is proof that a [whole Old Testament in GREEK was being used in 200 B. C. ]{.class_s4N4-0}is the same logic that led Darwin and Lyell to assume that marine fossils on mountain tops proved that it took 400,000,000 years for the oceans to drop down into "beds." \[However, this "logic" is quite typical among Bible-rejecting "Fundamentalists" who are taken up with the hobbyhorse of altering the Reformation Text.\])

["And according to the drink offering thereof"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 41). This is the first mention under the Law of an article which can become part of a service which worships [devils]{.class_s4N4-0} (Psa. 106:37; Deut. 32:17; 1 Cor. 10:20). The Catholic Church fits neatly into the description by making the ["drink"]{.class_s4N2-0} of her Sunday[ "offering"]{.class_s4N2-0} literal BLOOD, in spite of the clear warnings in three dispensations: Genesis 9:4 before the Flood, Leviticus 17:12--14 under the Law, and Acts 15:20, 29 under grace. Genesis 35:14 defines the term, and there it was connected with a stone pillar anointed with oil. Regardless of the pagan overtones of Jacob's act and the hints at "phallic symbols," it is certain that the drink poured out (see 2 Sam. 23:16) is likened to life blood spilled on the ground (see Isa. 53:12; Psa. 22:14). This likeness is so striking in Matthew 26:28 that []{#c3EG.xhtml_page_676}Rome has simply utilized the typology for her own ends and has coupled it with John 6:53, thereby producing what "The Articles of Faith" (Anglican, Article 31) call "A DANGEROUS DELUSION AND BLASPHEMOUS DECEIT."

["The fourth part of an hin of wine for a drink offering"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 40). Since the ["hin"]{.class_s4N2-0} is about a gallon and a half, ["the fourth part"]{.class_s4N2-0} would be around one quart and a pint of liquid. Since some of the peace offerings (vs. 24--28 and comments) were shared by the priests, the ["hin of wine"]{.class_s4N2-0} was probably treated in the same fashion. Obedient to placing its members back under ["the curse of the law"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Gal. 3:13), the Roman communion---from time immemorial---has allowed its "priests" to drink the fermented liquor of the ["drink offering." ]{.class_s4N2-0}This should be expected, as the entire Roman Catholic priesthood from the first pastor (whom Origen called "a [priest]{.class_s4N4-0}") to the last apostate who exchanged the "Virgin Birth" for the ["Immaculate Conception,"]{.class_s4N4-0} is erected on the supposition that Christ's one, final, eternal, effectual sacrifice (Heb. 10:8--12) was NOT enough to save the sinner and redeem his sins. Observe how rigorously papists have stuck to the Old Testament ["curse of the law"]{.class_s4N2-0} by applying verse 43 to the Gentile population of Mexico, Italy, Spain, and South America. Verse 43 makes it clear that God will meet with no one anywhere, in the sense of "SMELLING" (vs. 41), till the holy sacrifice has been made on a holy altar (vss. 33--43).

The papist makes the "holy sacrifice" the offering up of fermented hootch (bigotry if you ever saw it!), and he makes the "holy altar" the center of the split chancel where the counterfeit "priest" professes to "mediate" between God and man. The essence of Romanism is []{#c3EG.xhtml_page_677}a [plagiarized Judaism ]{.class_s4N4-0}which bypasses the only sure sacrifice for sin by professing that a counterfeit sacrifice is the real one (see[ The Sure Word of Prophecy]{.class_s4N4-0}).

The spiritual application of verses 42--46 are obvious, and of course, they have no relation to any sacrament that the papal princes ever invented, taught, promoted, preserved, practiced, or preached.

1\. Christ's priesthood is continual and perpetual (vs. 42) in HIMSELF (Heb. 3:1), and still would be so if every Christian on earth died a violent death in 24 hours.

2\. His sacrifice is continual and perpetual (vs. 42) since He is the DOOR (vs. 42, ["at the door of the tabernacle"]{.class_s4N2-0}) and the sacrifice (Heb. 10:8--12).

3\. God will meet with the sinner when he is "IN Christ" (Eph. 1:4), and He will speak (vs. 42) through His only begotten Son (Matt. 3:17) or through those whom His Son chooses to speak for Him (Heb. 1:1--2, 2:3--4). His Son was careful to choose Jews to record ["the oracles of God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rom. 3:2), and no [Latin ]{.class_s4N4-0}was given that privilege. There is a Latin false god in Acts 14:12--13, and there is another one in Acts 19:35, and there a [Latin ]{.class_s4N4-0}"passover" in Acts 12:4 ([correctly translated]{.class_s4N4-0} as ["EASTER"]{.class_s4N2-0} [in spite of John R. Rice's lame comments on the passage]{.class_s4N4-0})[. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Aside from a few Latin words [adopted by the Greeks]{.class_s4N4-0} for the koine street language, the only Latin references in the New Testament deal with three Latin [murderers ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Herod the Great, Herod Antipas, and Pontius Pilate) and the Roman soldiers who whipped and nailed the only begotten Son of God. In view of this, a man has to be playing with a "short deck" to think that a Latin priest at a Latin altar will enable the sinner to MEET with God and SPEAK with Him (vs. 42).

4\. ["The tabernacle shall be sanctified by my]{.class_s4N2-0} [glory"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 43) would match the temple of the Holy Ghost (1 Cor. 3:16) in which the Christian is to ["glorify God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 Cor. 6:20).

5\. It is God Himself who sanctifies this ["tabernacle"]{.class_s4N2-0} (2 Cor. 5:1--5 cf. 1 Cor. 3:16--17, 6:19) as ["the tabernacle of the congregation"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 44), and it is inseparable from ["the altar"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 44) which we now serve (Heb. 13:10). This ["altar"]{.class_s4N2-0} is not found in Jerusalem or Rome, but is ["without the camp"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Heb. 13:13), and it is said to be for spiritual sacrifices (Heb. 13:15--16)---[not LITERAL sacrifices]{.class_s4N4-0} (1 Pet. 2:5--9). This again explains why the Vatican manuscript (elevated by Dr. Wilbur Smith) omits all of Hebrews after Hebrews 9:14. (The Vatican readings of the [NASV]{.class_s4N4-0} are called "THE Greek text" by the Dr. Neal and Dr. Custer. We call them ["The Bloopers' Bible."]{.class_s4N4-0})

6\. Aaron and his sons have to come in contact with the blood (vs. 44) exactly as it was sprinkled on the Tabernacle and the Altar (vss. 20--21). The serious student of the Bible should observe that no WINE could be used as a "transubstitutionary sacrament" for BLOOD. ["Without shedding of BLOOD"]{.class_s4N2-0} there is no remission of sins (Heb. 9:22), and the psychotic gyrations of Rome, that make papists move Matthew 26:28 up against John 6:54, are similar to the "sick logic" of Alexander Campbell, who pasted Acts 2:38 up against Romans 6:3--4. Blood is [not ]{.class_s4N4-0}wine and wine is [not ]{.class_s4N4-0}blood, and if Christ could turn either into either, [your priest couldn't do it with a turning machine.]{.class_s4N4-0}

7\. ["And they shall know"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 46) puts the capstone on orthodox Biblical Christianity and emphasizes again the contrast between the "hope-so," "think-so," "guess-[]{#c3EG.xhtml_page_679}so" salvation of Catholicism and the "know-so" salvation of Matthew, Mark, Luke, John, Peter, James, Paul, and Jude (cf. 1 John 5:13). The surest proof that John 6:54--56 is not related to the so-called "sacraments" of Rome is the fact that no Roman Catholic in your town knows for certain that he has eternal life as a [present ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [eternal ]{.class_s4N4-0}possession. John 6:54--56 states that he DOES know it. It therefore follows, as ["Hell"]{.class_s4N2-0} follows ["Death"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rev. 6:8), that if you do not KNOW (see Cain, Gen. 4:3--5), you have either made a liar out of God (1 John 5:10), or you have followed a deluded Italian Prince instead of the Author of salvation and revelation (John 6:68).

["I am the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord ]{.class_s4N9-0}[their God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 46). Having given the details of the dress, offerings, and manners of the priesthood, the next item that the Holy Spirit presents is the ["altar of incense."]{.class_s4N2-0} Coming where it does---in chapter 30---its placement is [exactly correct,]{.class_s4N4-0} as we have mentioned before, and it reinforces an idea which is an absolute essential in understanding "approach to God" (Isa. 59:1--3; John 9:31). There can be no real "prayer life" (Acts 9:11) until the sinner has been accepted by God through the High Priest (Heb. 3:1) and a blood atonement (Col. 1:14, [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}1611). The heathen SAY prayers and make ["vain repetitions"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Matt. 6:7), but when the incense goes up with ["the prayers of all saints"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rev. 8:3), it has been preceded---every time---by the acceptance of the finished work of Jesus Christ on Calvary (Rom. 8:34--39).

# Chapter 30 {.heading_s2W-1}

30:1 "And thou shalt make an altar to burn incense upon: of shittim wood shalt thou make it.

2 A cubit shall be the length thereof, and a cubit the breadth thereof; foursquare shall it be: and two cubits shall be the height thereof: the horns thereof shall be of the same.

3 And thou shalt overlay it with pure gold, the top thereof, and the sides thereof round about, and the horns thereof; and thou shalt make unto it a crown of gold round about.

4 And two golden rings shalt thou make to it under the crown of it, by the two corners thereof, upon the two sides of it shalt thou make it; and they shall be for places for the staves to bear it withal.

5 And thou shalt make the staves of shittim wood, and overlay them with gold.

6 And thou shalt put it before the vail that is by the ark of the testimony, before the mercy seat that is over the testimony, where I will meet with thee.

7 And Aaron shall burn thereon sweet incense every morning: when he dresseth the lamps, he shall burn incense upon it.

8 And when Aaron lighteth the lamps at even, he shall burn incense upon it, a perpetual incense before the [LORD ]{.class_s4ND}throughout your generations.

9 Ye shall offer no strange incense thereon, nor burnt sacrifice, nor meat offering; neither shall ye pour drink offering thereon.

10 And Aaron shall make an atonement upon the horns of it once in a year with the blood of the sin offering of atonements; once in the year shall he make []{#c3JN.xhtml_page_681}atonement upon it throughout your generations: it is most holy unto the [LORD]{.class_s4ND}.

The corrupt Samaritan text omits all ten verses and places them in chapter 26 "where they ought to be," etc. Dummelow tells us that "many" believe it is a "later addition." (The standard way to state your infidelity, without people being able to hang you for it, is say ["many]{.class_s4N4-0} believe" or ["there are those]{.class_s4N4-0} who believe" or ["some]{.class_s4N4-0} say." All Liberals use this method, and many Conservatives have adopted it to "save face" and safeguard the future from losing their income or financial support.)

The Altar of Incense is so clearly connected with prayer that no remarks need to be made; see Exodus 30:36; Psalm 141:2; Revelation 5:8; 1 Timothy 2:8; Luke 1:9--10; etc.

An excellent picture of ["the golden altar"]{.class_s4N2-0} (39:38) is found in Moorehead's work on [The Tabernacle]{.class_s4N4-0} (Kregel Publications, 1957, pg. 66). It has [staves ]{.class_s4N4-0}of wood overlaid with gold (vs. 5) and golden rings for the staves (vs. 4), exactly as the Ark of the Covenant and the Table of the Showbread (which see). It has [horns ]{.class_s4N4-0}to match the Brazen Altar of 27:1--2, and this clearly connects it with the atonement of the offerings made OUTSIDE the Tabernacle. This brings us to another reason why the Holy Spirit has chosen to mention ["the golden altar"]{.class_s4N2-0} in chapter 30 instead of chapters 25--28. You see, ["the golden altar"]{.class_s4N2-0} is not only connected with the burnt sacrifices [outside the Holy Place,]{.class_s4N4-0} but it is also connected with the sprinkled blood (Heb. 9:3--4) [inside THE HOLY OF HOLIES.]{.class_s4N4-0}

Observe how the gold matches the Ark---[not ]{.class_s4N4-0}the altar of burnt offering, which was covered with [brass]{.class_s4N4-0}---and, []{#c3JN.xhtml_page_682}again, observe how the Ark and the Mercy Seat (vs. 6) are both mentioned in connection with the [Brazen Altar]{.class_s4N4-0} of 27:1--4. But further, observe that just as blood was applied to the horns of the brazen altar, blood was applied to the horns of ["the golden altar"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 10), [except this only took place once a year]{.class_s4N4-0} (vs. 10) at the ["day of atonement"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Lev. 23:27--28). Then prayer must follow salvation, for the altar is one-half cubit higher than the grating of the brazen altar, and it is one-half inch higher than the blood-sprinkled ["mercy seat"]{.class_s4N2-0} (cf. vs. 2, 27:1, 5).

There are many rich spiritual lessons suggested by ["the golden altar."]{.class_s4N2-0}

1\. It is not merely[ "holy"]{.class_s4N2-0} (as the Brazen Altar and the garments of Aaron)---it is ["most holy unto the LORD"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 10) and, therefore, is connected with the "Holy of Holies"---the inner sanctum of the Tabernacle (Heb. 9:3--4). Real prayer (Matt. 26:36--44) from the heart of the saint (Rom. 8:26) is more than ["a memorial before God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (see the prayers of Cornelius before conversion---Acts 10:4); it is a means of healing (James 5:15), childbearing (Luke 1:13), revival (Hab. 3:1--2), enlightenment (Dan. 9:17, 21), salvation (Rom. 10:1), release (Phil. 1:19), resurrection (Heb. 5:7-- 8), help (Psa. 109:4), and deliverance (Matt. 26:41).

2\. The Lord used the same words for ["the golden altar"]{.class_s4N2-0} that He used in 29:42. Observe ["where I will meet with thee"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 6) and ["where I will meet you"]{.class_s4N2-0} (29:42). God meets us on our knees and "speaks" (vs. 29:42) with us exactly as He [met with Moses and spoke with him at the DOOR]{.class_s4N4-0} (cf. 29:42 and comments).

3\. Furthermore! The prayers go up ["morning\...even"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 7--8) as the Candlestick (type of the Holy Spirit) is "dressed," clearly identifying our prayers with the great Intercessor's work of Romans 8:26. Furthermore. David says he will pray ["morning"]{.class_s4N2-0} and ["evening"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Psa, 55:17) at the time of prayer (Luke 1:9--10) .

4\. Yet, further, no [sacrifice ]{.class_s4N4-0}can be accepted in place of the true petition that comes from a [broken heart]{.class_s4N4-0} (Psa. 51:17); Saul's "sacrifices'' were turned down because his "heart" went after covetousness. Observe in verse 9, that no ["burnt sacrifice, nor meat offering\...neither\...drink offering"]{.class_s4N2-0} is to be offered. ["Behold, to obey is better than sacrifice"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 Sam. 15:22). David's prayers (1 Sam. 23:2) are answered without a sacrifice, while Saul's sacrifices bring no answer from "between the cherubim" (1 Sam. 28:6).

5\. But again! [Even our prayers are unclean,]{.class_s4N4-0} so they must be sanctified ["by the blood of the sin offering"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 10). As a famous preacher once said: "When I preach, [I sin;]{.class_s4N4-0} when I pray, [I sin;]{.class_s4N4-0} my very repentance needs to be repented of; and my [tears ]{.class_s4N4-0}need washing in the blood of Christ."

6\. But we have not "gotten off to a running start" yet. Prayers that are not connected with Christ's prayers on the cross are ["strange fire'']{.class_s4N2-0} (see Lev. 10:1). The Altar of Incense could only be kindled by fire from the Brazen Altar (Lev. 16:12), clearly warning the sons of Aaron (Lev. 10:1--2) that only the fire of God's wrath, falling from Heaven on a LAMB (Lev. 9:3, 24), could kindle an acceptable petition to God. You see, the LAMB showed up in Leviticus chapter 9 [at the initial consummation of the sacrifice,]{.class_s4N4-0} although it was [not ]{.class_s4N4-0}mentioned in connection []{#c3JN.xhtml_page_684}with the consecration of the priests [in chapters 28--29. ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Ah, the unsearchable riches of the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}\[1611\]. How infallible are its placement of subjects and its wording past finding out.) [In Leviticus 9:3, 16, the LAMB showed up. ]{.class_s4N4-0}You knew [it ]{.class_s4N4-0}would have to before any priest could be "consecrated" with bullocks and rams.

7\. And this does not exhaust the spiritual truths found in the context of ["the golden altar."]{.class_s4N2-0} It is [gold ]{.class_s4N4-0}for Deity, it is made of the [same wood]{.class_s4N4-0} as the Tabernacle (vs. 1), and it is [crowned ]{.class_s4N4-0}along with the Table of Showbread and the Ark of the Covenant.

Clarke tells us to omit ["before the mercy seat that is over the testimony"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 6) on the grounds of Kennicott's corrupt manuscript and the Samaritan text. We know what to make of this: [eggnog.]{.class_s4N4-0}

Here, where the rich spiritual truths that appeal to the "devotional mind" are so manifest, the "majority of Conservative scholars" suddenly contract "intestinal flu" and exhibit, for all time, one of those great operations which we make so much of in the [Bible Believer's Series. ]{.class_s4N4-0}(We have said, on more than one occasion, that Fundamentalists who reject the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}of 1611 for such trash as Nestle's or Metzger's Greek text, or the publications of the Lockman Foundation, will not hesitate to abandon every Greek lexicon, their own laws of translating, their own principles of selective readings, their own science of manuscript evidence, and even basic meanings of Freshman Greek vocabulary if they can overthrow the [KING JAMES BIBLE]{.class_s4N4-0} in doing it.)

We now have a startling case presented by the [New American Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} and the old [American Standard Version,]{.class_s4N4-0} recommended by Bob Jones University, Pillsbury, Pensacola Christian College, []{#c3JN.xhtml_page_685}Southeastern, Moody, Fuller, Wheaton, Northeastern, Columbia, BIOLA, etc. The word ["censer"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Heb. 9:4) ("[thumiasterion]{.class_s4N4-0}") has been altered to "[altar]{.class_s4N4-0}" ("[thusiasterion]{.class_s4N4-0}") in both versions on the basis of \_\_\_\_\_? No basis. [There is not one single copy of any Greek manuscript]{.class_s4N4-0} (papyrus, vellum, uncial, or cursive), [in any family of manuscripts]{.class_s4N4-0} (Western, Syrian, Alexandrian, or Caesarian), [in any century,]{.class_s4N4-0} that reads as the American Standard Hangover and New American Standard Washout. The reading ["altar"]{.class_s4N4-0} (Heb. 9:4) is arbitrary conjecture based on the stupidity and unbelief of the "majority of Conservative scholars. "

As we have said on numerous occasions (see the [Bible Believer's Commentaries on Genesis, Proverbs, Matthew, and Revelation]{.class_s4N4-0}), the dead-orthodox Conservatives of the Twentieth Century HATE the word of God exactly as Origen, Symmachus, Philo, Clement, Theodotian, Valentinus, and Aquila hated it---while [professing ]{.class_s4N4-0}to cherish it. To manifest their hatred, they changed as much of it as they could get away with, and to prove their "love" for it, they "helped God out" every time they found Him in a "tight place." Hebrews 9:4 is a "tight place" (if you are a conceited, arrogant Christian billygoat).

The Lockman Foundation (as Origen) reasoned that since ["the golden censer"]{.class_s4N2-0} was NOT inside the "Holy of Holies," but outside the veil, that the word could not be ["censer."]{.class_s4N2-0} This is apparent by the confused comments of Dummelow on the matter, who tells us that "the verse" (Heb. 9:4) is "overloaded and apparently self-contradictory." To help out the confused Holy Spirit---who wrote the verse (!)---the mythological ["LXX"]{.class_s4N4-0} has []{#c3JN.xhtml_page_686}omitted ["before the mercy seat\...that is over the testimony"]{.class_s4N2-0} from verse 6.

Replacing the ["censer"]{.class_s4N2-0} with the ["altar"]{.class_s4N4-0} ([ASV, NASV,]{.class_s4N4-0} Archaic ASV, Modern ASV, and Space Age ASV) only adds to the confusion, for the censer was brought into the Holy of Holies once a year. The altar stayed outside. This confusion of the muddled Lockman Foundation ([NASV]{.class_s4N4-0}, 1963) and Dr. Schaff ([ASV]{.class_s4N4-0}, 1901) is only matched by the stupid bungling of the "godly Conservatives" who recommend such trash to ministerial students.

Now we have beheld this three-ring circus before (see the [Commentaries on Genesis, Revelation, Joel, Amos, Proverbs, Acts, Jonah, Ephesians, Zechariah, Philippians, Hosea, Colossians, Galatians, Matthew]{.class_s4N4-0}, etc.), and it is no novel surprise to the Bible believer when "the majority of Conservative scholars," who believe "in the verbally inspired [originals,"]{.class_s4N4-0} make such a mess of the Holy Bible in trying to "help bring out the meaning of the original, etc." After all, God knows the [motives ]{.class_s4N4-0}of the Conservative scholars of the Twentieth Century, and He who ["knoweth the thoughts of the wise"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 Cor. 3:20) has a way of jamming the works if things get out of hand. If we are to find the truth of God, we will often have to sidestep many of the faculty members of "Conservative" schools, since they are interested in maintaining a double standard of authority---like Rome. When you find TWO or THREE "Bibles" recommended, what the man is trying to tell you is that [HE HIMSELF is the final authority]{.class_s4N4-0} on what is right and what is not. (Don't kid us, buster; some of us were grown before we read your stuff.)

1\. [The altar]{.class_s4N4-0} is placed outside the "Holy of Holies," but at the time it is described (vs. 6), the phrase ["before the []{#c3JN.xhtml_page_687}vail"]{.class_s4N2-0} [could be inside or outside. ]{.class_s4N4-0}It is not until 40:26 that we are told that ["before the vail]{.class_s4N2-0}" meant just [outside]{.class_s4N4-0} it.

[2. ]{.class_s4N3-0}[The CENSER]{.class_s4N4-1}[ is moveable and can go ]{.class_s4N3-0}[in and out]{.class_s4N4-1}[ of the Holy of Holies (Heb. 9:4); and in the very context of Hebrews 9:3--4 we are shown why the writer said ]{.class_s4N3-0}["the Holiest of all; Which]{.class_s4N2-1}[ had the golden censer."]{.class_s4N7-0} [It DID HAVE ]{.class_s4N6}["the golden censer"]{.class_s4N7-0}[---once a year. ]{.class_s4N6}Notice how Hebrews 9:7---in the Reformation text of the [King James ]{.class_s4N6}Bible---gives the means for interpreting ["censer,"]{.class_s4N7-0} while the substitution of ["altar"]{.class_s4N6} ([ASV, NASV,]{.class_s4N6} Ancient ASV, Contemporary ASV, and Roman American ASV) for ["censer"]{.class_s4N7-0} destroys the entire meaning of the passage.

[At no time]{.class_s4N4-0} did the ["Holiest of all"]{.class_s4N2-0} "HAVE" the altar. The reading, therefore, of the [NASV]{.class_s4N4-0}, etc., is not merely "unreliable," it is an extravagant farce and is to be classified with the other absurd tomfoolery found in it---Luke 2:33; Luke 24:51--52; Luke 23:42; John 1:18, etc., etc. (One must remember that the [NASV]{.class_s4N4-0} is the product of minds that have been saturated with television for nearly a quarter of a century. [A make-believe world produces "goof-ball" bibles. ]{.class_s4N4-0})

The incense for the altar follows a prescription which appears in the following passage.

30:22 "Moreover the [LORD ]{.class_s4ND}spake unto Moses, saying,

23 Take thou also unto thee principal spices, of pure myrrh five hundred shekels, and of sweet cinnamon half so much, even two hundred and fifty shekels, and of sweet calamus two hundred and fifty shekels,

24 And of cassia five hundred shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary, and of oil olive an hin:

25 And thou shalt make it an oil of holy ointment, an ointment compound after the art of the apothecary: it shall be an holy anointing oil.

26 And thou shalt anoint the tabernacle of the congregation therewith, and the ark of the testimony,

27 And the table and all his vessels, and the candlestick and his vessels, and the altar of incense,

28 And the altar of burnt offering with all his vessels, and the laver and his foot.

29 And thou shalt sanctify them, that they may be most holy: whatsoever toucheth them shall be holy.

30 And thou shalt anoint Aaron and his sons, and consecrate them, that they may minister unto me in the priest's office.

31 And thou shalt speak unto the children of Israel, saying, This shall be an holy anointing oil unto me throughout your generations.

32 Upon man's flesh shall it not be poured, neither shall ye make any other like it, after the composition of it: it is holy, and it shall be holy unto you.

33 Whosoever compoundeth any like it, or whosoever putteth any of it upon a stranger, shall even be cut off from his people.

34 And the LORD said unto Moses, Take unto thee sweet spices, stacte, and onycha, and galbanum; these sweet spices with pure frankincense: of each shall there be a like weight:

35 And thou shalt make it a perfume, a confection after the art of the apothecary, tempered together, pure and holy:

36 And thou shalt beat some of it very small, and put of it before the testimony in the tabernacle of the []{#c3JN.xhtml_page_689}congregation, where I will meet with thee: it shall be unto you most holy.

37 And as for the perfume which thou shalt make, ye shall not make to yourselves according to the composition thereof: it shall be unto thee holy for the LORD.

38 Whosoever shall make like unto that, to smell thereto, shall even be cut off from his people."

We shall take this passage up before examining verses 11--21 . The [spices ]{.class_s4N4-0}are gums and parts of shellfish (["onycha,"]{.class_s4N2-0} vs. 34); they can be found in any Bible dictionary. ["Stacte"]{.class_s4N2-0} comes from trees that produce ["myrrh."]{.class_s4N2-0} The onycha is also the source of the ["purple"]{.class_s4N2-0} color mentioned earlier (see 25:4 and comments). ["Galbanum"]{.class_s4N2-0} is a cream-colored resin that exudes from the ["Bubon Gummiferum"]{.class_s4N4-0} or African ["Ferula."]{.class_s4N4-0} (Ain't that cool?!)

This perfume (with ["frankincense"]{.class_s4N2-0}) is mixed in equal weights (vs. 34) and ["tempered together"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 35). Since it represents the [prayers of the saints,]{.class_s4N4-0} it is to be ["pure"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 Tim. 5:22) ["and]{.class_s4N2-0} [holy"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 35), and when some of it is beaten to pieces (cf. Isa. 53:1--5), it is ["most holy" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 36), for it matches the broken heart and contrite spirit of the penitent sinner (Psa. 51:1--17), whose soul is laid bare before God. These prayers enter into the Holy of Holies (vs. 36) ["before the testimony in the tabernacle of the congregation,"]{.class_s4N2-0} while the ["Hail Marys"]{.class_s4N4-0} and ["Our Fathers"]{.class_s4N4-0} get filed away with ["O Baal, hear us"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 Kings 18:26).

["Ye shall not make to yourselves according to the composition thereof"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 37). The incense burned on this altar had to be God's incense, according to His []{#c3JN.xhtml_page_690}prescription, and His sacrificial fires had to kindle it. Any smoke from another mixture was ["unholy smoke"]{.class_s4N4-0} and would be classified with Winstons, Camels, Chesterfields, L&Ms, etc. Furthermore, the prescribed incense could not be used as common incense within the camp (["ye shall not make to yourselves"]{.class_s4N2-0}). Prayers patterned after the "Sermon on the Mount" and "Our Father which art in heaven" will never come from a regenerated spirit that is engaged in [real ]{.class_s4N4-0}prayer. Prayer wheels and beads may become the trappings of a voodoo witch doctor, but God's people have no business imitating real prayer. [Real prayer]{.class_s4N4-0} comes from the coals off the altar and is sanctified by the Holy Spirit of God as it ascends heavenward.

["Whosoever shall make like unto that, to smell thereto"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 38). Not even the [sound of prayer]{.class_s4N4-0} is to be heard where it is artificial and counterfeit. As you are not to sniff the incense for your own pleasure, you are not to "listen in" to "the greatest prayer ever addressed to an audience" (to quote a news reporter reporting on the opening of the Democratic Convention in a large city one year) .

30:11 "And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

12 When thou takest the sum of the children of Israel after their number, then shall they give every man a ransom for his soul unto the [LORD]{.class_s4ND}, when thou numberest them; that there be no plague among them, when thou numberest them.
