13 This they shall give, every one that passeth among them that are numbered, half a shekel after the shekel of the sanctuary: (a shekel is twenty gerahs:) an half shekel shall be the offering of the [LORD]{.class_s4ND}.

14 Every one that passeth among them that are numbered, from twenty years old and above, shall give an offering unto the LORD.

15 The rich shall not give more, and the poor shall not give less than half a shekel, when they give an offering unto the LORD, to make an atonement for your souls.

16 And thou shalt take the atonement money of the children of Israel, and shalt appoint it for the service of the tabernacle of the congregation; that it may be a memorial unto the children of Israel before the [LORD]{.class_s4ND}, to make an atonement for your souls."

["A ransom"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 12) must accompany any numbering of the people, and a plague follows if the ransom does not accompany the numbering. Observe how exactly the Lord fulfills this word in the case of David (2 Sam. 24:15).

None of the commentators seem to be able to tell us why this tax was put on the people at the time of "numbering." It is taken for granted by all that this is some arbitrary thing thought up to emphasize their sinfulness or to remind them of Egypt (or some other spiritual generality that sheds about as much light on the text as Kenneth Wuest does on 2 Thess. 2). To make sure that you don't get any connection between 2 Samuel chapter 24 and this passage, the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} assures us that "there was [no thought]{.class_s4N4-0} of such a plague as was provoked by David's numbering."

Well, then, why was God against "numbering" to start with? You say, "He wasn't." [Then why did He demand a RANSOM when it was done? ]{.class_s4N4-0}We are already back at the place where we must abandon Keil, Delitzsch, DeRossi, Kennicott, Hengstenberg, Kalisch, Clarke, Ellicott, []{#c3JN.xhtml_page_692}Lange, Bullinger, and the other 3,000 commentators, if we are to learn the Bible. So said---so done.

Now, "numbers" are so important that one book in the Bible bears that title; furthermore, the Bible lists many, many numerical rosters of different groups (1 Chron. 11--12---the mighty men; 1 Chron. 26---workmen; 1 Chron. 23---Levites; 1 Chron. 25---musicians; Psa. 147:4---stars; Ezek. 4:4--8---days; Rev. 13:18---the Antichrist; Acts 4:4---converts; Rev. 5:11---heavenly hosts; Acts 1:15---church members; 1 Chron. 2--9---tribes; etc.).

It is God who keeps the numbers. The "numbers racket" and "safety in numbers" with the zip code, Social Security, bank account, street, phone, license, and registration numbers are manmade for purposes of domination and control. God had told Israel, through Abraham (Gen. 13:16), that their seed would be so great that it could not ["be numbered nor counted"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 Kings 3:8), and throughout the Old Testament, the Lord is constantly trying to teach Israel the lesson that [one man and God is a majority. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Notice how the "numbers" failed to win the battle against the Midianites (Judg. 7:3); notice how the "numbers" failed to whip the little tribe of Benjamin (Judg. 20:21, 25); notice how the "numbers" failed to floor Samson (Judg. 15:16); notice how the "numbers" failed to overcome Ahab (1 Kings 20:10--29); notice how the "numbers" failed to capture Jerusalem (2 Kings 19:35); notice how the "numbers" failed in 2 Chronicles 16:8; 2 Chronicles 24:24; 2 Kings 7:6; and, above all,["there is no king saved by the multitude of an host"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Psa. 33:16). Evidently our major linguists and commentators "don't read much Bible" (as they say down South), even though they [criticize ]{.class_s4N4-0}a great deal of it.

Unless God commands the leader to number His people (as in Num. 1:2), he is to trust God for their enlargement, and this is what David did NOT do. He wanted the number so he could say: "We have fourteen activated divisions, forty standby divisions, with 500 Nike missiles, seven atomic-powered submarines, and a store of ten-megaton bombs in a stockpile that\.... " Sounds familiar, doesn't it?

You see, human nature, being what it is, will be tempted to exalt in NUMBERS [ONLY ]{.class_s4N4-0}if left to itself. This is so apparent in modern-day Christianity that no comment really needs to be made in regard to the matter. We may point with pride at a Sunday School that matches Acts chapters 2--5 (8,000 in attendance at the First Baptist Church of Jerusalem), but when one reads of the things that accompanied this attendance---whipping, imprisonment, miracles, no prizes, no busses, people dropping dead for lying about money, and invitations where 2,000 people got saved at one altar call---one is compelled to confess that the "numbers" do not tell the whole story. The statistical tables in the yearly almanacs and [Information Please]{.class_s4N4-0} magazines show us the temperature of things exactly: [the whole world has gone bugs on numbers. ]{.class_s4N4-0}The Communists in Russia brag about the number of people who make the yearly pilgrimage to Lenin's tomb; the Catholics brag about the people who have come to Rome; the depraved juveniles of America brag about the number of records "Superstar" sold; pastors and evangelists brag about the number of "decisions" recorded during a service or meeting; "packing the pew" has to be added up into totals to see who has the greatest number; while the number of rapes, divorces, dope addicts, assaults, thefts, []{#c3JN.xhtml_page_694}suicides, murders, and abortions outstrips the number of missionaries on the field, off the field, by the field, or aimed at the field. Everybody is in on the "numbers racket."

How the major commentators missed verse 12 is amazing. It is the MEN who are numbered, and the ransom is taken because the men are going into battle and they may get killed. Numbers 31:50--54 is a perfect comment on the text and shows that the ransom money was---among other things---security against dying in sins in an unclean condition. (Keil and Delitzsch's lengthy remark on the ["ransom,"]{.class_s4N2-0} reproduced in the [Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} \[pg. 81\], is about as big a gust of hot air as ever blew out of a six-ply tire on the rear end of a semi.) The telltale statement ["from twenty years old and above"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 14) clearly ties the verse to Numbers 1:3, where the host is mustered for battle. The Israelites had already won a battle (17:13) [without numbering the host,]{.class_s4N4-0} and they won it by PRAYER (see 17:12 and comments). Any future roll call would be accompanied by a reminder that:

1\. They might die in battle.

2\. They were not worthy to fight God's battles.

3\. They better hadn't brag about numerical strength.

This is why the ransom shows up at this time, and the pitiful conjectures of Keil and Delitzsch on the passage (along with "the majority of Conservative" flukes) is distressing indeed to a Bible believer, though he will learn sooner or later to search for truth at the Fountain instead of the hand pumps. All of the commentators were greatly misled by the expression ["When thou takest the sum of the children of Israel" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 12) and forgot that the sum given is always the MALE sum:[ "about six []{#c3JN.xhtml_page_695}hundred thousand on foot that were men"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Exod. 12:37), ["eight hundred thousand valiant men that drew the sword"]{.class_s4N2-0} (2 Sam. 24:9), ["and the men of Judah were five hundred thousand men"]{.class_s4N2-0} (2 Sam. 24:9). The ["six hundred thousand and three thousand and five hundred and fifty"]{.class_s4N2-0} of Numbers 2:32 could not possibly have any reference to women or children, for this would be only 3,000--4,000 more people than those who left Egypt.

Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown saw the truth of the exemption of minors, women, and old men; but then they could make no application of this truth. Clarke tells us that "each of the Israelites" had to offer the money, but this is nonsense, as can be seen from the above. Notice ["atonement for your souls"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 15--16), not "THEIR souls." The army was defending Israel.

["Half a shekel after the shekel of the sanctuary"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 13). The sum comes to somewhere between thirty to sixty cents, depending upon various modes of figuring (one shilling-sixpence, according to Clarke, who gives the English equivalent). Every man is worth the same price,[ "for there is no respect of persons with God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rom. 2:11).

Furthermore, God decides the price ["after the shekel of the sanctuary"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 13). If the sum is sixty cents, it would represent about the equivalent value of the chemicals found in the body if they were sold on a flat-rate basis. By the instructions given, Teddy Kennedy is no more valuable than Charles Manson; Elizabeth Taylor is worth no more than Rahab; and Abraham Lincoln and Lee Harvey Oswald are in the same boat. When it comes to [sin, ]{.class_s4N4-0}["all have sinned,]{.class_s4N2-0} [and come short"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rom. 3:23) The atonement money goes for the []{#c3JN.xhtml_page_696}support of the ministry (vs. 16), exactly as the New Testament minister (l Cor. 9:13--14) is supported by the gifts of people whose sins have been atoned for and whose souls have been "ransomed" (Matt. 20:28).

30:17 "And the [LORD ]{.class_s4ND}spake unto Moses, saying,

18 Thou shalt also make a laver of brass, and his foot also of brass, to wash withal: and thou shalt put it between the tabernacle of the congregation and the altar, and thou shalt put water therein.

19 For Aaron and his sons shall wash their hands and their feet thereat:

20 When they go into the tabernacle of the congregation, they shall wash with water, that they die not; or when they come near to the altar to minister, to burn offering made by fire unto the LORD:

21 So they shall wash their hands and their feet, that they die not: and it shall be a statute for ever to them, even to him and to his seed throughout their generations."

Among other things, the [Brazen Laver]{.class_s4N4-0} was made out of mirrors contributed by the women who assembled at the door of the "tent of meeting" (38:8); its exact form is not described. Since it pictures the ["WORD"]{.class_s4N2-0} which washes the saint's feet (John 15:3; Eph. 5:25--26), it is boundless in measurement, so no circumference or diameter is mentioned here in Exodus. Aaron and his sons had to wash their hands and feet in this Laver, or they would die (vss. 20--21). The sanctification which it represents (John 17:17) is well illustrated by David in Psalm 26:6 and Psalm 73:13. Since the truth represented by the Laver is the truth of John 15:3, we should not be surprised to find the [Septuagint]{.class_s4N4-0} manuscript of the [New []{#c3JN.xhtml_page_697}American Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} perverting this truth into the ancient heresy of "baptismal regeneration." The so-called [Septuagint ]{.class_s4N4-0}has this time used ["loutara"]{.class_s4N4-0} for verse 18 so that it will match the ["loutron"]{.class_s4N4-0} of Titus 3:5. By doing this, one can teach baptismal regeneration from Titus 3:5 by reading "the LAVER of regeneration" instead of ["the washing of regeneration."]{.class_s4N2-0} The Hebrew word (["kiyyo"]{.class_s4N4-0}) cannot be translated as a verb or participle; it stands only for an OBJECT. So this subtle "choice of words" on the part of Origen, Symmachus, Theodotian, Aquila, Eusebius, Pamphilus, and others---many years after the death of the Apostle John---shows real "spiritual insight," if one is as blind as a one-eyed mole. Clarke suggests we read "LAVER of regeneration" in Titus 3:5. [Don't.]{.class_s4N4-0}

Since washing of the hands (vs. 19) is considered to be essential for service, the Babylonian Talmud put the imposition on everyone in Israel (see Matt. 15:2). That is why the Pharisees got so upset when the Lord Jesus sat down to eat with dirty fingernails (!)---(Luke 11:38).

The ["foot"]{.class_s4N2-0} referred to (vs. 8) would be a trough or shallow basin under the Laver into which water flowed from "spigots" in the bottom of the Laver. A good picture of it is on page 48 of Moorehead's work.

In Leviticus 16:4, we are told that the initial washing was all over the body (see 29:4 and notes); after that, the "ablutions" were confined to the [hands ]{.class_s4N4-0}(service) and the [feet ]{.class_s4N4-0}(the walk of the believer).

Since the Laver stood between the altar of burnt offering and the door of the Tabernacle, we learn two more great spiritual truths for the believer. No believer is fit for service until he has been cleansed by the word of []{#c3JN.xhtml_page_698}God (John 17:17), and he must "take a bath in it [daily]{.class_s4N4-0}" to stay clean (John 15:3).

The believer may have already noticed two peculiar things about the Tabernacle: it had no [chairs ]{.class_s4N4-0}and no [floor]{.class_s4N4-0}. This meant that the high priest was never through with his work, and he could never "sit down at the right hand" of the Ark as long as ["the first tabernacle was yet standing"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Heb. 8:8). The priest who offered "daily sacrifices" was said to "STAND" (Heb. 10:11). He could not sit on the Candlestick, he could not sit on ["the golden altar"]{.class_s4N2-0} or the Brazen Altar or the Table of Showbread, and he certainly couldn't rest on the brazen grate in the middle of the altar of burnt offerings. There is no rest until the cry ["IT IS FINISHED!"]{.class_s4N2-0} Then the veil splits in two from top to bottom (Matt. 27:50--51), and the Great High Priest sits down for awhile (Heb. 12:2).

In the meantime (Moses to Christ), it is up and down, in and out, back and forth for the priest; and it is dirt, sand, dirt, mud, sand, gravel, and dirt 24 hours a day as long as God abides in a TENT (2 Sam. 7:6). Washing of feet was a daily thing, and so we learn that as long as we are "in this earthly tabernacle" (2 Pet. 1:13), we must bathe our feet while we look in the ["mirror"]{.class_s4N2-0} (James 1:23--25). The mirrors would reflect up through the water, so the stooping priest could see his face while he bathed his hands and feet. We still ["see through a glass darkly"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 Cor. 13:12), but the time will come when our sanctification will be complete (1 Thess. 5:23).

The instructions concerning the ["holy anointing oil" ]{.class_s4N2-0}are similar to those concerning the incense in regards to two particulars: both had to be according to [prescription ]{.class_s4N4-0}(vss. 23--24, 34--35), and neither could be [counterfeited ]{.class_s4N4-0}or used by the "layman" (vss. 32--33, 37--[]{#c3JN.xhtml_page_699}38). (We have already noted the connection between the oil of the candlestick \[type of the Holy Spirit\] and the incense of ["the golden altar"]{.class_s4N2-0} \[type of prayer\] by observing the time of the "dressing" of the Candlestick \[30:7 and comments\].)

["Calamus"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 23) is called ["sweet cane"]{.class_s4N2-0} in Jeremiah 6:20; ["myrrh"]{.class_s4N2-0} is inseparably connected with the earthly ministry of Christ (Song of Sol. 1:13; Matt. 2:11; Mark 15:23; John 19:39). The ["hin"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 24) is defined in 29:40.

Christ's anointing (Acts 10:38) is mentioned many times in the Scriptures (cf. Luke 4:18; Heb. 1:9; Acts 4:27, etc.). The ["holy ointment"]{.class_s4N2-0} anoints all of the vessels of the ministry (vss. 27--28), the Tabernacle itself (vs. 26), and Aaron and his sons (vs. 30) with their garments (29:21).

The reader will observe the striking occurrence of the word ["sweet"]{.class_s4N2-0} in verse 23 (in regards to the oil) and in verse 34 (in regards to the incense). [Real prayer has the bitterness gone from it. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Job's ["complaint"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Job 9:27, 10:1, 21:4, 23:2) in the bitterness of his spirit (like Hannah's prayer---1 Sam. 1:10) is not quite like the Holy Spirit's "[groanings"]{.class_s4N2-0} for His charges (Rom. 8:26). Even where the Lord Jesus is in great agony of soul (Luke 22:44), there is no BITTERNESS in His prayer. There is bitterness in the vinegar and the cross and the cry of anguish (["my God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Matt. 27:46), but this was not a prayer. The PRAYER on the cross was: ["Father, forgive them; for they know not what they do"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Luke 23:34). No sweetness was allowed in the ["meat offering,"]{.class_s4N2-0} but when dealing with oil and incense that speaks of the risen Saviour and the indwelling Spirit in the praying saint, []{#c3JN.xhtml_page_700 .class-1}["There is Something Mighty Sweet About the Lord."]{.class_s4N4-0} (If you don't believe it, ask Lester Roloff.)

["Upon man's flesh shall it not be poured"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 32), not until the Millennium at least (Joel 2:29; Acts 2:18), and then it is poured out on "all the house of Israel" (Acts 2:22). Even in the act of spiritual circumcision (Col. 2:11), the Holy Spirit does not contact the flesh (John 3:6); the flesh and the Spirit are eternal antagonists (see 17:8 and comments) and have nothing in common (Rom. 8:1--3; Gal. 5:19, 22).

["Neither shall ye make any other like it"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 32). The Holy Spirit is counterfeited by the Devil (Rev. 16:13), and consequently, his ministers are transformed as ["apostles of Christ"]{.class_s4N2-0} (2 Cor. 11:13--14). We do not have to guess about these matters, ["for we are not ignorant of his devices"]{.class_s4N2-0} (2 Cor. 2:11). We know of three situations where someone is trying to get control of the "anointing" by a composition "like unto it."

1\. [Those who claim apostolic succession by ]{.class_s4N4-0}["laying on of hands" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(Heb. 6:2) are trying desperately to continue Acts 8:17 beyond Acts 28:31. This "giving of the Holy Ghost" (Acts 8:18) was stolen from the apostles (Rev. 2:2) by men who were no more apostolic in power than Walt Disney or Jack Nicklaus. The ["signs, and wonders"]{.class_s4N2-0} (2 Cor. 12:12) which are supposed to attend men like Peter and John are so woefully absent from the hierarchy of Popes that it is still a marvel of the ages to think that people like the Luces, Buckleys, Shrivers, and Kennedys could be stupid enough to follow such leadership. The passing on of the Holy Spirit through the hands of [unconverted sinners,]{.class_s4N4-0} in [political favor]{.class_s4N4-0} with ["the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[god of this world" ]{.class_s4NC}(2 Cor. 4:4), is an outrage to the Holy Spirit; you can be certain He has never []{#c3JN.xhtml_page_701}"anointed" one of them from Pope Innocent I to Pope Ivan 666.

2\. The mumbo jumbo and gibberish stuttered at some "Holiness" meetings, under the pretense that someone has the "gift of tongues," is no more the anointing (or baptism) of the Holy Spirit than it is the anointing of Sinclair Oil or the baptism of 2 Kings chapter 5. It is not enough for some people to blame the "loss of their salvation" on the Holy Spirit (after all, they claim verses to prove this, [and the Holy Spirit WROTE the verses]{.class_s4N4-0}), they must go the second mile and claim that some insane, senseless flap-doodle is an "unknown tongue" given to them by the Holy Spirit. (I have never met one of these people who could prove the claim by the Scripture. The only Scripture to which they could point was one that said if a man spoke ["in an unknown tongue,"]{.class_s4N2-0} [no one would understand him ]{.class_s4N4-0}\[1 Cor. 14:2\], Well, who didn't know that? If you tune in a short wave band, you will find thousands of people talking and [none of them can be understood]{.class_s4N4-0} if they speak ["in an unknown tongue."]{.class_s4N2-0} How does this common, ordinary, everyday act---applicable in [any age,]{.class_s4N4-0} on any continent, to [any unsaved sinner]{.class_s4N4-0}--- suddenly become PROOF that the Holy Ghost has "baptized a [Christian"]{.class_s4N4-0}? All of the Corinthians were baptized by the Holy Ghost \[1 Cor. 12:13\], [and one of them was going to bed with ]{.class_s4N4-0}["his father's wife "]{.class_s4N2-0}\[1 Cor. 5:1\]!)

3\. People who claim "the Lord led me" or "the Lord is leading us to do such and such" often blame the Holy Spirit for what was brought about by a dollar bill or property taxes. (Compounding spirits ["like"]{.class_s4N2-0} the anointing oil \[vs. 33\] is quite common among day-school, PTA, cultured Christians who have learned how []{#c3JN.xhtml_page_702}to counterfeit the FRUIT of the Holy Spirit without the ROOT.)

We have in America today a peculiar sort of day school-"Intervarsity"-Gospel Fellowship-Wheaton-Campus Crusade type of "devotional study" for the "prayer partners" that is aimed primarily at rich widows and businessmen about to make out wills. What all of this has to do with the "leadership of the Holy Spirit" is a little hard to imagine. We may properly call it a "green lead." (Lettuce doesn't always grow in the garden!) These phony "Christians" clutter the FM stations with a ghastly counterfeit Christianity. It is marked by:

1\. Effeminate radio announcers.

2\. Women missionaries who teach interdenominational "Bible classes."

3\. Whispering strings and moaning saxophones which convert the militant Christian tunes into a sort of saloon or cocktail-lounge atmosphere.

4\. Constant appeals for "prayer partners" to help with the "widow's mite" for this "great ministry."

5\. A total lack of straight, hard, plain preaching against sin.

6\. [One Bible version per rich widow.]{.class_s4N4-0}

7\. Smooth, syrupy bass voices which modulate like a newscaster and talk all around the Bible without once applying it.

["Whosoever compoundeth any like it\...shall even be cut off from his people"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 33). It is a good thing we are not still under the Law---for more than one reason.

[]{#c3P4.xhtml}

# Chapter 31 {.heading_s4G-0}

31:1 "And the [LORD ]{.class_s4ND}spake unto Moses, saying,

2 See, I have called by name Bezaleel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah:

3 And I have filled him with the spirit of God, in wisdom, and in understanding, and in knowledge, and in all manner of workmanship,

4 To devise cunning works, to work in gold, and in silver, and in brass,

5 And in cutting of stones, to set them, and in carving of timber, to work in all manner of workmanship.

6 And I, behold, I have given with him Aholiab, the son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan: and in the hearts of all that are wise hearted I have put wisdom, that they may make all that I have commanded thee;

7 The tabernacle of the congregation, and the ark of the testimony, and the mercy seat that is thereupon, and all the furniture of the tabernacle,

8 And the table and his furniture, and the pure candlestick with all his furniture, and the altar of incense,

9 And the altar of burnt offering with all his furniture, and the laver and his foot,

10 And the cloths of service, and the holy garments for Aaron the priest, and the garments of his sons, to minister in the priest's office,

11 And the anointing oil, and sweet incense for the holy place: according to all that I have commanded thee shall they do."

We have already discussed the incense (see 30:1--9 and comments), so we shall skip the last five verses in []{#c3P4.xhtml_page_704}chapter 30 and take up the narrative in chapter 31, where the workers are called to do the work of the Tabernacle and a warning is inserted about the Sabbath. This order is absolutely correct, since much work is about to take place and the Sabbath rest is liable to be overlooked. As if to reinforce this truth, the Holy Spirit shows us in the next chapter (chap. 32) how quickly Israel was to break God's commandments. Having experienced signs, wonders, and miracles (chaps. 4--15), and divine provision for daily sustenance (chaps. 15--17), plus having received a supernatural revelation from Heaven (chaps. 19--23), it is "back to his vomit" (2 Pet. 2:22) for the firstborn ["son"]{.class_s4N2-0} of Jehovah (4:22).

Judah and Dan are the tribes honored with the selection of "straw bosses" for the work. This is significant in view of the fact that ["the Lion of the tribe of Juda"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rev. 5:5), who has the scepter (Gen. 49:10), is the real Messiah, while the "Lion" of Dan (Deut. 33:22), who bites like a serpent (Gen. 49:17), is connected with the Roman Catholic religion (cf. extended comments in the[ Commentary on Revelation,]{.class_s4N4-0} Rev. 2:20).

The "master builders" are Bezaleel (["in the shadow of God"]{.class_s4N4-0}) and Aholiab (["the father is my tabernacle"]{.class_s4N4-0}). Bezaleel is descended from Judah through Pharez, Caleb, and Hur (1 Chron. 2:5---20). Their commissioning by God is: ["I have filled"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 3), "[I have given" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 6), and[ "I have put"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 6). The Holy Spirit is placed in the believer, He is given as a gift, and the believer is exhorted to ["be filled with the Spirit"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Eph. 5:18). All of the "gifts" of verse 3 are mentioned in Isaiah 11:2 (["wisdom \... understanding \... knowledge"]{.class_s4N2-0}).

The real believer should notice that these "arts and handicrafts" come from God exactly as musical talent []{#c3P4.xhtml_page_705}and linguistic ability come from God (James 1:5). This means that even if the dead- orthodox linguists use their talents to destroy the authority of the Bible, it is no discredit to God's goodness. The fact that thousands of commercial artists debase their talents to photographing nude women, painting teenagers with beer bottles in their hands, decorating Catholic cathedrals with spooks, demons, goblins, and antichrists, and making a living scribbling nonsense (Picasso) on canvass is still no testimony against God. God has been known to give "talents" to people who buried them in the dirt. (Matt. 25:25)

The word ["cunning"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 4) is the archaic English for "skillful," as in Psalm 137:5 and Exodus 26:1, 31.

Facing the Bible believer is the peculiar statement ["And I have filled him with the spirit of God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 3). Since this goes against the grain of the Lockman Foundation and the Scofield Board of Editors, Brother Bullinger helps them out by saying that he was not [really ]{.class_s4N4-0}["filled with the spirit of God"]{.class_s4N2-0}---although that is what the Bible text said---but rather "Put by Fig. (Metonymy)\...for the GIFTS of the Holy Spirit," which, of course, [the text does NOT SAY.]{.class_s4N4-0}

Now the reason for blatantly denying this clear statement of the Holy Bible (by a fundamental, Premillennial Conservative---Bullinger) is the same reason that John R. Rice gave for altering Acts 8:37 and Revelation 22:14: i.e., ["I cannot understand it;]{.class_s4N4-0} therefore, there is something [wrong ]{.class_s4N4-0}with it." From time immemorial "Conservatives" have taught that the Holy Spirit only came "ON" certain select individuals in the Old Testament as he came "ON" Samson (Judg. 14:6). This coming "on" the individual was supposed to be []{#c3P4.xhtml_page_706}limited to the individual's "anointing" for a particular job (e.g., Saul in 1 Sam. 11:6). The expression used to teach this is found in the [New Scofield Reference Bible,]{.class_s4N4-0} page 975, footnote 1, "Holy Spirit \[Old Testament\] Summary." The careful student will observe that the work of the Spirit has been limited by the [NSRB ]{.class_s4N4-0}to "coming UPON men," and not one word is said about the explicit statement of the Holy Spirit Himself that He was IN men (Gen. 41:38; Exod. 35:31; Num. 27:18; Neh. 9:30; Isa. 63:11; 1 Pet. 1:11).

In the average "fundamental school" these clear references are distorted in the courses called ["Biblical Theology"]{.class_s4N4-0} and ["Biblical Introduction."]{.class_s4N4-0} Both courses are used to teach the student that the ["spirit"]{.class_s4N2-0} referred to in the passages above was only the general term used to express the general work of God moving, generally, in a way which can best be described as "related" to a spirit. That is, rank infidelity is often taught in "Bible-believing bastions of orthodoxy" just like it is taught at the University of Chicago, Wake Forest, Emory, and Rochester "Divinity" School.

The truth of the matter is (and the truth is so inconvenient in this case that the Lockman Foundation and the New Scofield Board of Editors would just as soon forget the whole mess) is:

l\. The Holy Spirit came on men and left them TEMPORARILY ([Samson]{.class_s4N4-0}).

2\. The Holy Spirit came in men and left them PERMANENTLY ([Saul]{.class_s4N4-0}).

3\. The Holy Spirit refused to leave when He could have left ([David]{.class_s4N4-0}).

4\. He was IN Moses, Joseph, David, Daniel, Isaiah, Jeremiah, Ezekiel, et al., [but was NOT INDWELLING PERMANENTLY ]{.class_s4N4-0}(John 14:16).

Rather than believe these truths and teach them, the average Fundamentalist (as Bullinger) simply adopts the cliché that: "The Spirit came ON men in the Old Testament, but IN men in the New Testament."

["I have called by name"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 2), as the Lord called Peter (John 1:43), Samuel (1 Sam. 3:6), Saul (Acts 9:4), Moses (Exod. 3:4), and Lazarus (John 11:43). Observe also that where God commands (vs. 6), He equips: ["I have put wisdom, that they may make all that I have commanded thee."]{.class_s4N2-0} (We have commented on vss. 7--11 under chaps. 27--30.)

31:12 "And the [LORD ]{.class_s4NE-1}spake unto Moses, saying,

13 Speak thou also unto the children of Israel, saying, Verily my sabbaths ye shall keep: for it is a sign between me and you throughout your generations; that ye may know that I am the LORD that doth sanctify you.

14 Ye shall keep the sabbath therefore; for it is holy unto you: every one that defileth it shall surely be put to death: for whosoever doeth any work therein, that soul shall be cut off from among his people.

15 Six days may work be done; but in the seventh is the sabbath of rest, holy to the [LORD]{.class_s4NE-1} whosoever doeth any work in the sabbath day, he shall surely be put to death.

16 Wherefore the children of Israel shall keep the sabbath, to observe the sabbath throughout their generations, for a perpetual covenant.

17 It is a sign between me and the children of Israel for ever: for in six days the [LORD ]{.class_s4NE-1}made heaven and earth, and on the seventh day he rested, and was refreshed.

18 And he gave unto Moses, when he had made an end of communing with him upon mount Sinai, two tables of testimony, tables of stone, written with the finger of God."

As we have remarked earlier, with all of the work going on (vs. 4, ["to work"]{.class_s4N2-0}; vs. 5, ["to work"]{.class_s4N2-0}), the people may get so enthusiastic (see 36:5--7) that they ignore the Sabbath instructions.

["Verily my sabbaths ye shall keep: for it is a sign between me and you\...that ye may know that I am the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[LORD ]{.class_s4NE-0}[that doth sanctify you"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 13).

This is the classic passage used by Mary Ellen White (along with Ezek. 20) to kid the Christian into thinking that he cannot "know the LORD" unless he goes to church on Saturday instead of Sunday---see any literature by the Review and Herald Publishing Co., Washington, D.C. In spite of the one syllable words used to indicate that the Sabbath is a sign to ISRAEL (see also Ezek. 20:12, 20), the Seventh-day Adventists go right on with their cockeyed foolishness year in and year out, pretending (as the Jehovah's Witnesses) that "Israel" is [the Body of Christ. ]{.class_s4N4-0}(But Augustine and Origen were on this kick long before the Popes officially adopted it as their "theology." Adventists and Russellites are a lot closer to Rome than their followers would imagine.)

["Wherefore the children of Israel shall keep the sabbath" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 16); not "the children of God," not "the spiritual Jew" of Romans 2:29, not ["the Israel of []{#c3P4.xhtml_page_709}God"]{.class_s4N2-0} in Galatians 6:15, and not "the Christian." The statement says ["the children of Israel,"]{.class_s4N2-0} and if you are not one of Jacob's children (his name was ["Israel,"]{.class_s4N2-0} remember? See Gen. 32:28), you have no more business keeping the Sabbath than keeping the Black Mass (see extensive comments on the "Sabbath" under Gen. 2:1--3 in the [Commentary on Genesis]{.class_s4N4-0}).

["It is a sign between me and the children of Israel"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 17). Again, there is a clear, distinct, absolute, definitive sentence that locates the Sabbath exactly for any sane man who is searching for truth. This sign is an eternal sign (["for ever,"]{.class_s4N2-0} vs. 17), and it extends beyond the Millennium into ["the new heavens and the new earth"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Isa. 66:20--24), not withstanding the objections of "the majority of Conservative scholars." The Jew seeks for a sign and rightly so (see comments under 4:28). In Exodus, the Lord refers him back to the EVENTS that took place in Genesis 2:1--3, not the REVELATION that God gave in Genesis 2:1--3. Remember, Genesis 2:1--3 was written by a JEW who had THE SIGNS Israel sought (4:8--9)! Observe! ["For in six days the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord ]{.class_s4N9-0}[made heaven and earth, and on the seventh day he rested, and was refreshed"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 17). This revelation is given in 20:11 around 1491 B.C. Adam, Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, Joseph, and Noah knew no more about it than O. J. Simpson knows about the Syrian Peshitta.

[Capital punishment]{.class_s4N4-0} is ordained for the Sabbath breaker (vs. 14), exactly as it is for the man who curses (Lev. 24:15--16), commits adultery (Lev. 20:10), steals men (Exod. 21:16), or practices witchcraft (Exod. 22:18). The Seventh-day Adventists have evidently a law [with no God to back it up,]{.class_s4N4-0} for "Sabbath breakers" are NOT put to death, even though the Lord of the Sabbath, who []{#c3P4.xhtml_page_710}gave the Sabbath, said: ["every one that defileth it shall surely be put to death"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 14). Observe the wording: it matches Genesis 2:17, where God told the man he would ["surely die."]{.class_s4N2-0} The Seventh-day Adventist, then, has a unique religion: he is obeying an Old Testament sign given to Israel, [without the power and authority of the God who gave it]{.class_s4N4-0} (see Num. 15:32--36). ["The sabbath of rest"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 15) marks out the regular seventh day as ["the sabbath"]{.class_s4N2-0} in distinction from ["my sabbaths"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 13), which come on various days (see John 19:31, for example).

["He had made an end of communing with him upon mount Sinai"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 18). SEVEN brings anything to an end in the ["Seven System,"]{.class_s4N4-0} so Moses' "dialogue" on the "summit" ends with a mention of the Sabbath. Before shoving the tables of stone into Moses' hands (vs. 18), the Lord "breaks the news to him gently" (32:7--10) that while He (i.e., the Lord) has been giving the instructions for acceptable worship, the whole nation has gone off on acid rock and "group sensitivity therapy" and are worshipping the fifth cherub in the form of an Egyptian "Osiris"---horns and all! (It is most significant \[to quote A. W. Pink\] that every time ["the finger of God"]{.class_s4N2-0} \[vs. 18\] writes something, nobody can understand the writing. The writing in the sky is [ignored ]{.class_s4N4-0}\[Psa. 8:3, 19:1--4\], the writing from life was [rejected ]{.class_s4N4-0}by Pharaoh \[Exod. 8:19\], the writing on the wall was [incomprehensible ]{.class_s4N4-0}to the best brains of Belshazzar's day \[Dan. 5:5\], and the masses of Jerusalem have [stepped ]{.class_s4N4-0}on Jesus' handwriting \[John 8:8\] so many times you couldn't even find where He ["stooped"]{.class_s4N2-0} to write. No sooner has the ["finger of God"]{.class_s4N2-0} \[vs. 18\] delivered an absolute, supernatural, divine revelation of holiness than---POW! Up shows the []{#c3P4.xhtml_page_711}fifth cherub. \[Ezek. 1:5, 10:15 cf. 28:14\].) All Ten Commandments were broken within 24 hours after they were delivered.

[]{#c3RJ.xhtml}

# Chapter 32 {.heading_s4G-1}

32:1 "And when the people saw that Moses delayed to come down out of the mount, the people gathered themselves together unto Aaron, and said unto him, Up, make us gods, which shall go before us; for as for this Moses, the man that brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we wot not what is become of him.

2 And Aaron said unto them, Break off the golden earrings, which are in the ears of your wives, of your sons, and of your daughters, and bring them unto me.

3 And all the people brake off the golden earrings which were in their ears, and brought them unto Aaron.

4 And he received them at their hand, and fashioned it with a graving tool, after he had made it a molten calf: and they said, These be thy gods, O Israel, which brought thee up out of the land of Egypt.

5 And when Aaron saw it, he built an altar before it; and Aaron made proclamation, and said, To morrow is a feast to the [LORD]{.class_s4ND}.

6 And they rose up early on the morrow, and offered burnt offerings, and brought peace offerings; and the people sat down to eat and to drink, and rose up to play.

The scene is contemporary. This type of "religious happening" or "spiritual experience" begins in America in the early 1950s and grows into such groups as "the Jesus people," "Jesus Christ Superstar," "the Children of God," and other depraved Afro-American passivists. They carry their African music with them (see 12:38), and they consider such symbols as sex music, sex signs, nakedness (or near nakedness) as essentials to "freedom" and "liberation." They paraphrase John 8:32 to be: "You []{#c3RJ.xhtml_page_713}shall know Jesus, and knowing Jesus [will get rid of your inhibitions."]{.class_s4N4-0} The "LORD JESUS CHRIST" rarely appears in their speeches, prayers, songs, dances, sermons, or testimonies. The golden calf is obviously a charismatic symbol (or Christian icon) to "communicate the meaningful relevance" of the physical and the spiritual as a "point of contact," etc. (We know a foul ball when we see one.)

The "love feast" of the "drug culture" (orgy of the dope addicts) is accompanied by ["DANCING"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 19), NAKEDNESS (vs. 25), WORSHIP (vs. 5), OFFERINGS (vs. 6), and ["PLAY"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 6). This is exactly how the Egyptians "worshipped" the 400 (or 215) years that Israel was in Egypt. It is AFRICAN from start to finish, and the "finish" (as Belshazzar's feast) is in a river of blood.

["The people gathered themselves together"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 1), as they did in Genesis chapter 11 (2247 B.C.), and as they did in 1918 (League of Nations), and as they did in 1945 (United Nations), and as they do today (ecumenical councils).

["Make us gods"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 1), since they showed up in Genesis 6:1--4 and will show up in Revelation 12:4--9. (The [King James]{.class_s4N4-0} 1611 Bible states they will come from [Jupiter]{.class_s4N4-0} \[Acts 14:12--13, 19:35\], and even though the [New Scofield Reference Bible]{.class_s4N4-0} and the [NASV]{.class_s4N4-0} have suggested the substitution ["ZEUS,"]{.class_s4N4-0} the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}\[1611\] still gives the advanced revelation which the scientists at Cape Canaveral cannot discover.)

["And Aaron said unto them, Break off the golden earrings, which are in the ears of your wives"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 2). This is the sign of the dissatisfied servant (21:6), whose ear is not ["opened"]{.class_s4N2-0} (see Psa. 40:6) to hear the commands of his master. Observe []{#c3RJ.xhtml_page_714}that all those listed (["wives\...sons\...daughters"]{.class_s4N2-0}) are individuals who were told to be in subjection to the male head of the house (Col. 3:18; Eph. 5:22--23, 6:1). Aaron's action forces the people to renounce their allegiance to their "FATHER" (see 4:23 and comments) before any "communal spiritual experience" can be set up by "TASK FORCE 76."

The ["molten calf"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 4) is engraved after the mold has hardened, thus violating both of the first two commandments (2,--5, 34:17). The "priest" winds up with all of the gold (as usual!), and this gold was gold that God gave to the children of Israel in 12:35. (Sounds like Cortez, Balboa, Pizarro, and Ponce de Leon all over again, doesn't it!?) ["These be thy gods, O Israel"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 4) contains three elements:

1\. The gold.

2\. The calf.

3\. The earrings.

Observe that it is the PEOPLE who say ["These be thy gods, O Israel."]{.class_s4N2-0} Aaron merely says ["To morrow is a feast to the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[LORD]{.class_s4NE-0}["]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 5). This again fits Cortez, Balboa, Pizarro, and Ponce de Leon perfectly. The priest knows the true God and desires to pay him a certain amount of tribute on "holy days" and "special occasions" (see 12:14 and comments), but he is perfectly willing to let the heathen populace worship any IMAGE they want to. Just connect the IMAGE with a "Christian tradition," and everything stays in its place---HELL. The failure of the major commentators to see this obvious and perennial truth is one more testimony to their inability to learn any Bible after thirty years in "the original languages."

At this time, the Lord was angry enough at Aaron to kill him on the spot (Deut. 9:20), and if Moses had not interceded (vss. 11--13), the "mass" would have been a "mess" before Moses got back in the camp. The careful student of church history observes that at the first major apostasy of a nation from supernatural oracles delivered by God Himself, the following phenomena are apparent:

1\. [Africans ]{.class_s4N4-0}mixed up with the chosen people.

2\. [African sex music]{.class_s4N4-0} used in a worship service.

3\. Literal sacrifices instead of spiritual ones.

4\. A [priest ]{.class_s4N4-0}in charge who has neither been sanctified nor anointed by God.

5\. Money worshipped as the supreme god under the pretense of holding ["a feast to the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[LORD]{.class_s4NE-0}[."]{.class_s4N2-0}

6\. The priest taking advantage of his position and the ignorance of the common man in order to placate the people.

7\. The demand for a visible representative or an "aid to worship" which---by sheer accident---turns out to represent Satan.

Nothing could be clearer from the text, and the failure of every one of the major commentators to inform the reader of this material is a tragedy. Dummelow tells us that it was a "living bull" (not an IMAGE) that was worshipped in Egypt, but images of winged bulls and golden bulls and spotted bulls (Rev. 13:2, ah, yes!) and bulls with moons between their horns and bulls with halos over their heads are so common in Asia and Africa that we may safely overlook Dummelow's comment. (The god [Apis]{.class_s4N4-0} in Egypt was always pictured as a bull.)

Clarke, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown nearly bust in their indignant outcries about the apostasy; and one []{#c3RJ.xhtml_page_716}would think to read them that such apostasy after such a history was not merely unthinkable, but absolutely impossible. However, this all smells of "over--painting Esau" (see the[ Commentary on Genesis,]{.class_s4N4-0} Gen. 33:4).

It didn't take Eve more than fifteen minutes to drop Paradise. It didn't take David more than an hour to ruin his testimony. It didn't take Peter more than two hours to deny the One of whom he had said ["Thou art the Christ, the Son of the living God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Matt. 16:16). And the distance between the "prayer altar" in a Bible-believing church and the boob tube at home can be covered in ten minutes by some people. Thirty days is not too short a time for a church to abandon the true God and the God-chosen leader sent to it. (Clarke, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown must have had very little practical experience in dealing with people \[see Gen. 33:11--17\].)

Most of the commentators get rid of the ["gods,"]{.class_s4N2-0} since they cannot understand the expression ["these be thy gods."]{.class_s4N2-0} The [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}has "[God]{.class_s4N4-0}" in the margin; Clarke, Dummelow, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, Bullinger, Williams, Ellicott, Lange, et al., all tell us that the text should have read "GOD." However, the word ["these"]{.class_s4N2-0} is plural in Hebrew or Greek (see Exod. 20:3 and comments).

Notice the instinctive "flinching" that goes on every time a scholar crosses the line of revelation [dealing with the second advent of "HUMANOIDS" from outer space. ]{.class_s4N4-0}The commentators protect themselves instinctively from ridicule by "scientists" when, often, they do not even know why they insist on changing the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}text.

["Up, make us gods"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. l). This is the cry of the ages. Man must have something to worship. It must be able to foresee the future and feed mankind; []{#c3RJ.xhtml_page_717}it must be something he can SEE (cf. John 4:11, 24). Rome accommodates herself to this universal cry. The Communists of China and Russia are no different; they simply select a different "god." Gods come in many sizes (see 12:12 and comments), and political leaders run "bargains" on them regularly. ["Which shall go before us"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 1) is the demand that the "god" know the future and be able to lead safely into the "unknown." Jeane Dixon, Edgar Cayce, science, the HEW Department, the Popes, etc., make their predictions; but one salient characteristic of all of them marks them as sinners---not "gods." [THEY ALL PROPHESY PEACE]{.class_s4N4-0} (Jer. 23:16--22 cf. Deut. 18:22).

With the three biggest wars still yet in the future (Rev. 6:3--4, 19:11--21, 20:7--9), we don't have to be very smart to know which "god" ["shall go before us."]{.class_s4N2-0} First Corinthians 10:7 shows what kind of ["play"]{.class_s4N2-0} is going on; it is the same ["play"]{.class_s4N2-0} showing daily in the theaters by the American federal school system via the philosophy of Mary Calderone, Isidore Reubin, the Fabian Society, John Dewey, Bertrand Russell, Giovanni Gentile, the NEA, William James, and other flesh-crazy apostates (2 Pet. 2:18). The modern "church" in America is rapidly moving in this direction, and there seems to be the feeling that unless you have a rock-and-roll band, you can't "stir up the young people." But there are still some old bull moose who can "stir 'em up" with a [King James ]{.class_s4N4-0}Bible and get them so torn up in thirty minutes that they don't know whether to pop pills or go on "the mainline." (It depends upon what kind of a reaction you desire.)

32:7 "And the [LORD ]{.class_s4NE-1}said unto Moses, Go, get thee down; for thy people, which thou broughtest out of the land of Egypt, have corrupted themselves:

8 They have turned aside quickly out of the way which I commanded them: they have made them a molten calf, and have worshipped it, and have sacrificed thereunto, and said, These be thy gods, O Israel, which have brought thee up out of the land of Egypt.

9 And the [LORD ]{.class_s4NE-1}said unto Moses, I have seen this people, and, behold, it is a stiffnecked people:

10 Now therefore let me alone, that my wrath may wax hot against them, and that I may consume them: and I will make of thee a great nation.

11 And Moses besought the [LORD ]{.class_s4NE-1}his God, and said, [LORD]{.class_s4NE-1}, why doth thy wrath wax hot against thy people, which thou hast brought forth out of the land of Egypt with great power, and with a mighty hand?

12 Wherefore should the Egyptians speak, and say, For mischief did he bring them out, to slay them in the mountains, and to consume them from the face of the earth? Turn from thy fierce wrath, and repent of this evil against thy people.

13 Remember Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, thy servants, to whom thou swarest by thine own self, and saidst unto them, I will multiply your seed as the stars of heaven, and all this land that I have spoken of will I give unto your seed, and they shall inherit it for ever.

14 And the [LORD ]{.class_s4NE-1}repented of the evil which he thought to do unto his people."

The reader cannot help but observe the sarcastic ["thy people, which thou broughtest out"]{.class_s4N2-0} in verse 7. [The Lord has disowned His own people!]{.class_s4N4-0} In chapter 8, the Lord calls them ["my people"]{.class_s4N2-0} five times (8:1, 20, 21, 22, 23) In 15:13, it is explicitly stated that the Lord brought them out---not Moses. Note, further, how Moses throws this back in the Lord's face in verse 11 (["wax hot against thy people")]{.class_s4N2-0} and verse 12 (["repent of this evil against thy people"]{.class_s4N2-0}). It would seem from the conversation that Israel is ["persona non grata"]{.class_s4N4-0} at this moment, and neither the Father nor the stepfather wants to claim kinship. As Jack Hyles has said, "If God and Moses had ever gotten into a killing mood at the same time, there wouldn't have been anybody left but God and Moses." When Moses is threatening, God is merciful, and when God is threatening (as here---vs. 10), Moses is interceding.

["Thy people\...have corrupted themselves"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 7), exactly as Romans 1:21--32 states the matter for the entire human race. There is no "confusing of Jehovah with a calf" (see Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown Commentary, pg. 79). Israel has ["turned aside quickly out of the way\...and have worshipped it"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 8). ["]{.class_s4NC}[It"]{.class_s4N2-0} is not [God ]{.class_s4N4-0}(see 1 Sam. 4:3). Clarke also alibis for Aaron and tells us that the mention of the offerings in verse 6 is proof that Aaron "[intended ]{.class_s4N4-0}that the true God should be the object of their worship." But when did the Roman Catholic Church ever handle it any differently? [Aaron is a liar.]{.class_s4N4-0}

There was no ["feast to the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[LORD]{.class_s4NE-0}["]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 5) in the fourth or fifth month of the Jewish year at this time. This is about 43 days after a date given ["in the THIRD MONTH" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(see 19:1), and there is no [intention ]{.class_s4N4-0}whatsoever on Aaron's part of honoring the Lord God who brought them out of Egypt. Clarke, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown are "over-painting Jacob" again (see Gen. 48:10--14 in the[ Commentary on Genesis]{.class_s4N4-0}).

["Behold, it is a stiffnecked people"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 9 cf. Zech. 7:11; Hos. 4:16). The bull neck matches the papal bull as the ["cowboy"]{.class_s4N4-0} tends bulls for Baal. (It's a lot of "bull" any way you look at it. Those noted for "bullheadedness" are Germans and Irishmen. In the Bible, however, the Jew outdoes both of these races. The term[ "stiffnecked"]{.class_s4N2-0} occurs eight times in the Old Testament \[Exod. 32:9, 33:3, 5, 34:9; Deut. 9:6, 13, 10:16; 2 Chron 30:8\] and one time in the New Testament \[Acts 7:51\]; it is aimed at Israel all nine times.)

At verse 7, Clarke finally wakes up to the Roman Catholic possibilities of the text and stops long enough to mention that the idolatry of Rome matches the image worship of chapter 32; however, his comment amounts to less than one-eighth of a page.

["Now therefore let me alone" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 10). Moses is in the same position that Jacob was in about 300 years earlier (Gen. 32). It is almost as though Moses had gone forward at verse 9 and seized the Angel of the Lord as Jacob seized him.

["And Moses besought the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[LORD ]{.class_s4NE-0}[his God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 11). Moses is in fellowship, like Daniel (Dan. 6:10). He is on "praying ground," as the old saints used to say, and his ["effectual fervent prayer\...availeth much"]{.class_s4N2-0} (James 5:16). As a matter of fact, it prevented a nation of []{#c3RJ.xhtml_page_721}2,000,000 people from dying on the spot (cf. 2 Sam. 24: 17 for a similar case). The faith that prevails throws God's promises back in His face (see Matt. 15:27, for example); it appeals to God's faithfulness (["to whom thou swarest by thine own self,"]{.class_s4N2-0} vs. 13), and grows bolder and bolder till it claims the promise. In Moses' prayer, we see God condescending to man, but this was not the last time it happened. The Lord Jesus Christ Himself was "delivered to man's will" (Luke 23:25), and Mary had the incarnate Son of God at her mercy until He was a young boy. This last picture is unsearchable, and it so confounds the rational mind that it is called ["the mystery of godliness"]{.class_s4N2-0} in 1 Timothy 3:16. God [dependent on a Jewish woman for His food, cleanliness, and clothing?]{.class_s4N4-0} You cannot fathom it. So the cowed and cowardly translators of the [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}and the [NASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}all blanched at the text (1 Tim. 3:16) and ducked out as best they could while saving face.

Moses, as Paul (see Rom. 10:1), would rather see his people saved than anything, so Moses foregoes the opportunity to become a second Abraham ["and I will make of thee a great nation,"]{.class_s4N2-0} vs. 10). There is no doubt about God's ability. He had raised up 2,000,000 people from a 99-year-old man and a 90-year-old woman, and He could have raised up twice that many from a man whose ["natural force"]{.class_s4N2-0}[ ]{.class_s4N4-0}was not[ ]{.class_s4N4-0}["abated"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Deut. 34:7) when he was 120 years old.

Again, we see the peculiar trail of the serpent winding through the narrative; he is still after the seed (Gen. 3:15 cf. Rev. 12:4, 13). Having failed to drown out the male babies (chaps. 1--2), he tried to keep the whole nation integrated with Africans (Exod. 13--14). Having failed again, he now tries to get the Lord to kill all of them and []{#c3RJ.xhtml_page_722}thereby contradict His word, for the word plainly said that the specter would come from JUDAH (Gen. 49:10). If everybody died on the spot in chapter 32, only a LEVITE would have been left.

["And repent of this evil against thy people"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 12). ["God is not a man, that he should lie; neither the son of man, that he should repent" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(Num. 23:19), and yet this repentance is repentance from something God has definitely promised would come to pass. When the Lord "repented" in Genesis 6:6, He repented as anyone repents---[He changed His mind. ]{.class_s4N4-0}That is the exact meaning here (see also 1 Sam. 15:11 cf. 1 Sam. 15:29, 35). The peculiar notation of Keil, Delitzsch, Clarke, Lange, Henry, and others that God did not "change His [purpose]{.class_s4N4-0}" is rather stupid. It is so stupid that the "Cambridge Bible" tells us that God does not really change his purpose, "[but\...]{.class_s4N4-0}He is obliged to make a corresponding CHANGE in the purpose\.... " Good, if you can do it!

["Of the evil he thought to do unto his people" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 14). In this sense, God is the "author of evil," and this is the sense in which Job uses the expression without "sinning with his lips" (Job 2:10). Compare also Isaiah 45:7 and Lamentations 3:38. God can "do no evil" in the sense that He can do no WRONG, but "evil" covers more than a wrong action. In the Bible, God is spoken of as killing people and slaughtering till the blood bath is like an overdose of wine (Deut. 32:29--35). If you think that men would not consider this to be "evil" (from [their ]{.class_s4N4-0}standpoint), just watch the Liberal have a "kanniption" over the ["God of the Old Testament."]{.class_s4N4-0}

Moses prays God out of doing something He might have done otherwise. Furthermore, this is the clear []{#c3RJ.xhtml_page_723}teaching of Matthew 24:22 and Jonah 3:4--10. The Calvinist will always have the last say and insist that God led Moses to pray since He was not going to slay Israel. This Calvinistic song and dance is as nauseating as a duet between Tiny Tim and Vaughn Monroe. Why "lead" Moses to pray [anything ]{.class_s4N4-0}if He wasn't going to kill them anyway? The only possible answer would be so that Moses could go down the mount deceived into thinking he was a prayer warrior when actually he had been [duped]{.class_s4N4-0}. Furthermore, Moses gets in a killing mood when he "lands on the strip" (vss. 26--27), and he probably repented of making [the Lord]{.class_s4N4-0} repent. (Observe how 13:17 brings out the meaning of the word.)

Moses' second strong point in prayer is reminding God of His own enemies ("[Wherefore, should the Egyptians speak, and say\...?"]{.class_s4N2-0} vs. 12). David resorts to this "pressure point" all the time (Psa. 83:2, 89:42, etc.), and the old refrain says: "Why should I wander an alien from thee, or cry in the desert for bread? THY FOES will rejoice when MY SORROW they see, and smile at the tears I have shed!" To "tell it like it is," one would have to imagine the Lord saying:

"Get outta my way, Moses---[let me at 'em!"]{.class_s4N4-0}

"No, Lord, no---don't do it! [Stop!]{.class_s4N4-0} Hold the phone!"

"Stand aside, buddy. I've had all I can take. I'm gonna cream the whole lot, and when I get through, there won't be enough people around to play ping-pong!"

"But Lord, remember your promises?!"

"[What promises?]{.class_s4N4-0}"

"Abraham! Isaac! Jacob! You said you'd give that land to them and they'd inherit it forever, and you said\.... "

"Well, I changed my mind. Enough is enough. [Move over!]{.class_s4N4-0}"

"But Lord! Think about Pharaoh!"

"Pharaoh? He's crab food, forty fathoms straight down."

"Yeah, but Lord! His people! They're still back there, and you know how gossip spreads! Why, they'll hear about all your people getting killed out here, and they'll start the old scuttlebutt! You know: 'Some God! Well, [they ]{.class_s4N4-0}sure had the wrong God. Well, back to Isis and Osiris.'"

"Hmmmm. That does present a problem, doesn't it?"

"Sure! Sure! And look, Lord, they'll call you a liar, and they'll say that you promised us liberty when you intended to kill us. They'll say you're not a God of your word, and\.... " )

"[Okay!]{.class_s4N4-0} Forget it."

(Now we realize, gentle reader, that such "good news for modern man" is so "modern" that no "up-to-date" translation is going to try it, but you will do just as well reading this kind of commentary as one that says: "In the magnanimous aspects of God's absolute sovereignty, one is impressed with the veracity and faithfulness of the anthropomorphic descriptions which inform the herald of Israel that\.... ")

"[Okay!]{.class_s4N4-0} Forget it."

["And the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[LORD ]{.class_s4NE-0}[repented of the evil which he thought to do unto his people"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 14 cf. Jer. 18:10).

32:15 "And Moses turned, and went down from the mount, and the two tables of the testimony were in his hand: the tables were written on both their sides; on the one side and on the other were they written.

16 And the tables were the work of God, and the writing was the writing of God, graven upon the tables.

17 And when Joshua heard the noise of the people as they shouted, he said unto Moses, There is a noise of war in the camp.

18 And he said, It is not the voice of them that shout for mastery, neither is it the voice of them that cry for being overcome: but the noise of them that sing do I hear."

Four times we find a reference to ["writing"]{.class_s4N2-0} in the passage (twice in vs. 15 and twice in vs. 16). The writer was GOD, and the writing was an engraving by ["the finger of God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (see 31:18 and comments). Our word [tablet]{.class_s4N4-0} is associated with these ["tables"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 16), and they are written on both sides showing that the culture of the Chaldeans had survived the Egyptian bondage. Egyptian tablets were only written on [one side,]{.class_s4N4-0} while the Assyrian and Babylonian tablets had inscriptions on [both sides. ]{.class_s4N4-0}The God of Moses is obviously the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.

Joshua was resting halfway up the mount (vs. 17---if you can recall 24:13 after all of the details of the Tabernacle). And having only been in one engagement (17:9--10) since enlisting, he fancies that the rock opera is a [battle]{.class_s4N4-0}. The mistake would be quite easy to make as anyone could testify who has ever been to a rock concert or a rock festival. Many of the elements of a battlefield are present, which may explain why the music has such a fascination for young people looking for excitement; certainly the confusion, hysteria, rage, []{#c3RJ.xhtml_page_726 .class-1}[cursing]{.class_s4N4-0}, obscenity, and noise are "all present and accounted for."

Joshua cocks his ear and says: ["There is a noise of war in the camp"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 17). Moses corrects him with: ["It is not\...neither is it\...but the noise of them that sing do I hear."]{.class_s4N2-0} But modern "Afro-American" singing is SHOUTING and SCREAMING; where the singer cannot shout and scream, the microphone and the P.A. systems amplify his sensual gargling into a bawling, howling, hideous, shock-producing, incoherent roar that could just as easily come from a man shot in the lower intestines with a hollow point 7mm. Those who have the ["mastery"]{.class_s4N2-0} are those who have access to the "mikes," and those who ["cry for being overcome"]{.class_s4N2-0} range from twelve year olds to twenty-seven year olds. Their slobbering and screaming will match the scream of any bayonet assault you ever heard on any drill field since 1861. We can sympathize with Joshua's error in judgment.

What has happened since 24:15 is very simple. The ancient veteran of foreign wars already had fifth columns in the camp, so as soon as the pastor left for the prayer retreat, he went to work. Mrs. Obadiah doesn't like the way Mrs. Jehudiah plays the piano. Mrs. Sheshan was in the hospital two days, and Aaron didn't come and see her. Mrs. Obed and Mrs. Eleasah had a baby shower for Mrs. Shammai and didn't invite Mrs. Jerahmeel and Mrs. Appaim. A committee of elders came to Aaron with a complaint about there not being enough bulletins to go around for the Lenten services. Mrs. Tappuah almost came to blows with Mrs. Rekem over what kind of flowers to put on the rostrum during Easter, and Mr. and Mrs. Sheber quit giving because Mr. Siphah was made head []{#c3RJ.xhtml_page_727}of the building committee instead of Mr. Eshton. The congregation is threatened with achurch split if a rallying point is not found, and the rallying point for any religious person is a God they can SEE! So\...!

"Now Aaron we gotta do something about this mess\...." After 39 days of harassment, Aaron gives in---contrary to his better judgment, and the "worship service" begins. Nobody has to [wash ]{.class_s4N4-0}before offering. Nobody has to sprinkle the [blood ]{.class_s4N4-0}on anything they use. Nobody is [anointed ]{.class_s4N4-0}with anything but [unclean spirits. ]{.class_s4N4-0}No "holy garments" are donned by anyone; to the contrary, clothes are taken [off]{.class_s4N4-0}. No "holy of holies" is recognized, and you couldn't find the "fear of the Lord" in the camp if you had crawled through it on your hands and knees with a magnifying glass.

"Hot Lips Haraziah" has his licorice stick tuned up, "Liver Lips Megolith" is reaching for C above high C, Malchiram has the gut buckets tuned, Shealtiel is on the "dog house," some Egyptian waiter has loosened up the slide pump, another African is tickling the ivories, and a "cultural exchange" is about to happen. The young people stream down the streets between the tents with the usual: "Oh what's wrong with it, mother? Jahath is going to be there, and he sings in the choir!" and "Well, I don't see anything wrong with it; my conscience doesn't convict me," and "Hodiah is there and she's a preacher's daughter," and "But why be so negative; you just see evil in everything, but ['unto the pure all things are pure,'"]{.class_s4N2-0} etc., etc. (Human nature doesn't change any; the newspapers and TV just give you that impression.)

Some old Watusi begins to wobble back and forth like a rubber duck on stilts, and pretty soon the "chosen people" are doing the bumps and grinds like any \$2 a []{#c3RJ.xhtml_page_728}night burlesque stripper. As the clothes go off, the steam picks up.

"Com'on deah, honey chile! Le's have a little mo' integration heah!" "Yeah, dats what we needs---a love-in. Too much hate, man. Like all dem cats rapping wid dat blackout and dem plagues and all dat drownin' mess; like man, we is serving a God of love." "Ah's sick and tired of all dat mess, honey chile; it done bugged de life outta me. Les cool it, sugah; how 'bout a little  \...." (Don't put on airs; it goes on in the high schools from sunup to sundown. If you are a Christian and believe in the [truth]{.class_s4N4-0}, the [whole truth,]{.class_s4N4-0} and [nothing but the truth,]{.class_s4N4-0} you know there is no more "positive [good]{.class_s4N4-0}" in integrating the races \[any kind of "positive good"\] than there is in pouring sugar into a gas tank.)

When Moses began to descend, the party was in full swing, all the stops out, hi-fi stereo, "full sound spectrum," religious choreography, rhythmical figures "expressive of the joyous relationship between the Creator and, etc., etc." That is---[a naked sex orgy set to music.]{.class_s4N4-0}

Those of us who believe the Bible usually talk a great deal plainer about these matters than the crudest, rudest, most pornographic and blatant of the Twentieth-Century sensualists, like Norman Mailer and James Baldwin. Godless people have always used "double talk" when trying to explain their religious devilment, and the more educated they are, the more double talk they can conjure up. (After all, if you know the dictionary, you have access to more hypocrisy than those who do not.)

32:19 "And it came to pass, as soon as he came nigh unto the camp, that he saw the calf, and []{#c3RJ.xhtml_page_729}the dancing: and Moses' anger waxed hot, and he cast the tables out of his hands, and brake them beneath the mount.

20 And he took the calf which they had made, and burnt it in the fire, and ground it to powder, and strawed it upon the water, and made the children of Israel drink of it.

21 And Moses said unto Aaron, What did this people unto thee, that thou hast brought so great a sin upon them?

22 And Aaron said, Let not the anger of my lord wax hot: thou knowest the people, that they are set on mischief.

23 For they said unto me, Make us gods, which shall go before us: for as for this Moses, the man that brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we wot not what is become of him."

Old Moses comes stomping down the mount, and the vein in his forehead begins to stick out like a knotted rope. He wheezes and puffs in fury, and his breath is now coming through his teeth instead of his nose. His eyes bulge when they land on ["the calf,]{.class_s4N2-0} [and the dancing"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 19). That old temper that got him into trouble some forty years ago (2:12) boils up in his system again, and in a rage of anger, the old man raises the stone tables over his head and smashes them to bits on the bottom of the mountain (vs. 19). (This is the ["Be ye angry, and sin not"]{.class_s4N2-0} of Eph. 4:26, although today such righteous indignation would be called "Phariseeism" or "mid-Victorian prudery" or "false modesty." Moses evidently didn't have the same attitude about mixed []{#c3RJ.xhtml_page_730}dancing that the HEW, SPA, SIECUS, SPS, and the NEA has.)

A Christian psychiatrist once said that mixed dancing was: "the mother of harlots, the incubator of adultery, the sister of the white slave traffic. It keeps company with late hours, illicit embraces, jungle music, and promiscuous petting. Its fruits are jaded emotions, unnatural affections, debased morals, and loss of modesty and purity." There is nothing "Christian" about the [music ]{.class_s4N4-0}played at a mixed dance; there is nothing "Christian" about the [musicians ]{.class_s4N4-0}who play the dance; and there is certainly nothing "Christian" about the [conversations ]{.class_s4N4-0}on and off the dance floor or the [movements ]{.class_s4N4-0}of the dancers seated, standing, or dancing.

(The author has "beat it out" on a set of "Slingerlands" in three-piece combos and twenty-six-piece dance orchestras over a period of nearly ten years, in every place from juke joints and oyster bars to sorority houses and USO halls. The only difference between the junior-senior prom \[or cotillion or spring "hop"\] and Club Bali \[French Quarter in New Orleans\] is that the former is covered with a veneer of money, manners, and social graces, and the latter is the real thing. When the rugs were rolled up for the "rug cutters" of 1940--1950, they were the prelude to fornication and adultery; when the portable AM-FMs go out on the beach today, they play the same opening number calling for fornication and adultery. "Making out" is just more of the same old godless hypocrisy we mentioned earlier. They may call it "making out" in HIGH SCHOOL and COLLEGE \[education is a wonderful thing, no?\], but in the back alleys of the asphalt jungles and in the latrines and the barracks they call it something else, [don't they]{.class_s4N4-0}? You []{#c3RJ.xhtml_page_731}see, unsaved people cannot even tolerate the truth where their own crowd "calls a spade a spade.")

In the old days we said, "If she'll dance, she'll SMOKE; if she'll smoke, she'll DRINK; and if she'll drink, she'll do ANYTHING." (["The children of this world are in their generation wiser than the children of light"]{.class_s4N2-0} \[Luke 16:8\]. Some of you stupid Christians will still go to mixed dances.)

[Would Jesus dance if He were here?]{.class_s4N4-0} Chapter and verse? Would you like to have HIM find you at a mixed dance when He comes?

Name one thing that ever happened at a mixed dance that made you feel like praying, tithing, witnessing, praising God for His power, winning souls, or thanking God for SPIRITUAL blessings. Some of you have blistered your feet dancing; did you ever blister them for God doing "visitation"? Explain (in 500,000 words or less) why Dwight Moody, John Wesley, Billy Sunday, Bob Jones Sr., George Whitefield, R.A. Torrey, M. R. DeHaan, and Theodore Epp never danced after they got saved? Too [old]{.class_s4N4-0}? Why didn't Jack Wyrtzen, Bob Gray, and Percy Crawford when they were [young]{.class_s4N4-0}? Why didn't Hugh Pyle dance? Name one great soulwinning Bible believer from A.D. 100 to A.D. 1980 that ever fooled with a mixed dance unless he went there to break it up.

["He saw the calf, and the dancing: and Moses' anger waxed hot"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 19). You say this was "religious dancing" (like the choric dances of the Dorians or the pyrrhic dances at Sparta), or it was like the military dances of the whirling dervishes or the Bacchanalian dance for the "purgation of souls in the underworld," etc. But whether it is a scalp dance after a massacre, a green-corn dance before the rain, a festal dance before Mardi []{#c3RJ.xhtml_page_732}Gras, or a ballroom dance before a coronation, every great preacher from Paul (1 Cor. 15:33--34; Eph. 5:15--16) to Billy Sunday has passed judgment on it where it has been used for anything except the glorifying of the Lord God and Father of the Lord Jesus Christ (Luke 16:15; Exod. 15:20--21). Chrysostom (A.D. 345--407) said of the dance (more than fourteen centuries ago): "The feet were not given for dancing but to walk modestly, not to leap impudently like a camel." The church has said of the dance: "A dance is the Devil's possession, and he that entereth into a dance entereth into his possession. As many paces as a man makes in dancing, so many paces does he make to Hell\...when the Devil calleth one woman to sing in the dance or to play on some musical instrument, presently all the dancers gather together\...." (The last saying was given in the context of a hog-caller calling a bunch of hogs together when they had strayed.)

Sam Jones said a young lady was killed at a dance during a fight between some jealous young men. As the Devil took her soul down to the pit, he was accosted by Michael, who said: "Let go of that young lady; she belongs to the Lord."

"No, she don't," snarled the Devil, "She's one of [mine!]{.class_s4N4-0}"

"She is NOT," persisted Michael. "She's a saved young woman, and I am here to carry her to the third heaven."

"She's one of mine," said the Devil.

"She's NOT; she's the Lord's," said Michael.

"Oh no, she's not!" said the Devil. "She's mine, and I'm going to damn her to Hell right now."

"Open your hand and take another look," said Michael.

The Devil opened his hand and looked at the soul; [it was a Christian!]{.class_s4N4-0}

Reluctantly, Satan handed her over to Michael, and as he disappeared seeking another victim, he said, "Well, you couldn't have blamed me for making the mistake; [she was with MY CROWD"]{.class_s4N4-0} (Eph. 5:6--7).

Among many unusual opportunities which the Lord has given me in over 54 years of ministry was a chance to hold a two-hour "bull session" with about twenty teenagers in a private high school in Pensacola. The students and their teacher were "mod" in the extreme, and there wasn't enough clothing on the average girl student to make "a pair of pajamas for a jay bird"---as the expression goes. Eventually, the questions came up on the dance; I referred them to Exodus 15:20--21; 2 Samuel 6:14; Ecclesiastes 3:4; Luke 15:25; Colossians 3:16; and Exodus32:19. The response I got from the TEACHER (naturally!), who was encouraging nudity and semi-nudity to the girls, was that ONE single isolated incident found in an old Jewish book surely couldn't be accepted as a fixed authoritative moral guide for modern Americans in the Twentieth Century.

This response is quite typical of the modern "Christian" of any profession, and naturally, this teacher was a baptized "church member" who took "the sacraments." Her objection reveals a salient truth, which the reader will find mentioned over and over again in this series of commentaries: Conservative scholars, preachers, and teachers have destroyed the AUTHORITY of the Scriptures in the thinking of three generations of Americans (1900-1930, 1930--1960, and 1960--1990).

You see, this high school teacher [was ]{.class_s4N4-0}a "FUNDAMENTALIST," but she didn't believe that []{#c3RJ.xhtml_page_734 .class-1}[wherever the Bible spoke on any subject,]{.class_s4N4-0} that it was to be taken as authoritative. It was only to be taken seriously on the Virgin Birth, the Resurrection, the Incarnation, etc. Where the Bible recorded [detailed incidents]{.class_s4N4-0}---which the Holy Spirit selected out of literally millions of incidents---to show how God felt about certain particular practices, the Bible was to be pushed aside without any consideration at all. This "mod" teacher, who was leading young ladies into a lifestyle of fornication and adultery, had the same APPROACH to the [Authorized Version]{.class_s4N4-0} (when it attacked her IDOL) as Wuest, Thieme, Sanday, Trench, Wilbur Smith, Culbertson, Weniger, Nestle, Aland, Metzger, Harnack, Westcott, Hort, Ellicott, John R. Rice, Neal, Custer, Origen, Jerome, Bob Jones III, the Popes, Lightfoot, Rendall, and Dr. A. T. Robertson have toward the [Authorized Version]{.class_s4N4-0} of 1611 where it attacked their idol---"SCHOLARSHIP." Birds of a feather flock together!

32:24 "And I said unto them, Whosoever hath any gold, let them break it off. So they gave it me: then I cast it into the fire, and there came out this calf.

25 And when Moses saw that the people were naked; (for Aaron had made them naked unto their shame among their enemies:)

26 Then Moses stood in the gate of the camp, and said, Who is on the [Lord's]{.class_s4N9-1} side? let him come unto me. And all the sons of Levi gathered themselves together unto him.

27 And he said unto them, Thus saith the [Lord ]{.class_s4N9-1}God of Israel, Put every man his sword by his side, and go in and out from gate to gate throughout []{#c3RJ.xhtml_page_735}the camp, and slay every man his brother, and every man his companion, and every man his neighbour.

28 And the children of Levi did according to the word of Moses: and there fell of the people that day about three thousand men.

[29 For Moses had said, Consecrate yourselves to day to the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord]{.class_s4N9-0}[, even every man upon his son, and upon his brother; that he may bestow upon you a blessing this day.]{.class_s4N2-0}"

Now what do you suppose happens when a "priest" (vs. 22) gets caught redhanded in a sex orgy with a bunch of drunken Africans dancing around an idol? Get the scene?! Many of the people are ["naked"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 25), in the sense that the fugitives were naked in 2 Chronicles 28:15. (Observe how the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}uses the word in two senses, in spite of Clarke's bungling \[[Clarke's Commentary,]{.class_s4N4-0} pg. 25\]. It is used in the sense of NUDE \[Gen. 3:7; Ezek. 16:22; Job 1:21\] and in the sense of IMPROPERLY CLOTHED \[James 2:15; Matt. 25:36; Isa. 20:3, 4, etc.\])

The "dance" of chapter 32---like any mixed dance in America from 1910--2010---is attended by half-dressed women, and "half-dressed" is giving the flesh more than "the benefit of the doubt." Dances since 1950 have found white women less than one-fifth dressed; many African women in the Congo wear more clothes to a tribal dance than College-educated "coeds" wear when "twisting" at a "beach love-in."

Well, what happens? The congregation is [drunk ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [half nude. ]{.class_s4N4-0}The first two commandments and the last one have been broken---exactly as the Roman church eliminated the second commandment and made two out []{#c3RJ.xhtml_page_736}of the last one. The priest has led the people into idolatry. They've thrown away their money. They have been deceived into thinking that Ash Wednesday and Good Friday (see vs. 5) were "Christian festivals." They have been caught in the middle of a "meaningful, relevant, religious happening" by a God-fearing, Bible-believing, archaic, Puritanical, blue-nosed, bigoted, mid-Victorian, right wing, Southern extremist, etc.; and now, with the fur about to hit the fan, what excuse is offered?

[HEREDITY AND ENVIRONMENT. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Aaron blames the trouble on the "collective society."

["So they gave it me; then I cast it into the fire, and there came out this calf" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 24).

"It was an accident."

"I had nothing to do with it."

["The woman whom THOU gavest me\..."]{.class_s4N2-0} (Gen. 3:12).

"Mathematical chances of statistical probability would indicate\...."

"Products of the ghetto and social ostracizement cannot be expected to be useful citizens because\...."

"Woman is the [victim]{.class_s4N4-0} of male chauvinism and needs to be liberated so that she\...."

"I couldn't help myself! After all, where I was raised\...."

"But if you just knew what it is like to be called [nigger]{.class_s4N4-0}. How else do you expect us to act when\...."

HELLO KARL MARX, when did YOU join the dance? And I see you brought Jung, Pavlov, Menninger, the HEW, SIECUS, and the UNITED NATIONS with you. Aren't we going to have a ball now!

["So they gave it me\...and there came out this calf"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 24). What about the fire to ["molten"]{.class_s4N2-0} the gold []{#c3RJ.xhtml_page_737}(vs. 4)? Did it engrave itself automatically (vs. 4)? Who collected the stuff to start with (vs. 3)? Who built the ALTAR (vs. 5)? Did the altar "come out of the fire" also?

Would you be in the "ghetto" if you had stayed down South on [the farm]{.class_s4N4-0}?
