Would you need a "rat control" bill if you had not tried to jam two things together that didn't fit and pretended they were "equal"? Would your high schools and junior high schools be in the criminal condition they are in today if YOU had "left well enough alone" and not tried to jam [dissimilar elements]{.class_s4N4-0} together in a mass?

"Where do YOU enter into the picture, Mr. Congressman?" You see, Aaron, Charles Darwin, Giovanni Gentile, Bertrand Russell, William James, and John Dewey (llike ADAM) all sought to "pass the buck." They passed it on to "heredity, environment, and society," blaming their [individual sins]{.class_s4N4-0} on the [collective sins]{.class_s4N4-0} of the environment. All Communists and evolutionists reason in this fashion, for any real Communist or evolutionist is a SELF-RIGHTEOUS rejector of Biblical truth where it deals with his personal accountability to God. They are all "[victims ]{.class_s4N4-0}of circumstances," so they believe in their fantastic credulity---that by "altering circumstances" (reshaping society), the "ills and evils" will disappear. But alas, the "ills and evils" lie resident [in the very bosom]{.class_s4N4-0} (Job 31:33) [of these "do gooders"]{.class_s4N4-0} (Rom. 3:9--20), and the only possible fruit of their attempts to integrate dissimilar elements and unite the world for peace is [murder ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Red China), [torture ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Pakistan), [looting ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [burning ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Catholics in North Ireland), [civil war]{.class_s4N4-0} (Vietnam), and eventually [World War III]{.class_s4N4-0} (Rev. 6), [World War IV]{.class_s4N4-0} (Rev. 19), and [World War V]{.class_s4N4-0} (Rev. 20).

["And there came out this calf"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 24). You just bet it did. Jesus Christ was born in a stable, but He didn't have to rob camels and break into synagogues to make a living. Abraham Lincoln was born in a house that would make a government housing project look like the Taj Mahal, but Lincoln's family didn't urinate in the hallway and pile up garbage on the stairs. When Job was reduced to sores and ashes---after total bankruptcy---he didn't even write a letter to the Welfare Department or apply for Medicare.

You are a blank fool if you think that altering "social conditions" will alter the [human heart. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Judas would have sold Jesus Christ out if both of them had been working on Wall Street as brokers for the Chase National Bank, and Paul would have laid down his life for Jesus Christ if he (Paul) had been thrown in jail for "demonstrating" (which he was! Acts 16:19). A man is good because he [is ]{.class_s4N4-0}good, and if he is no good in the [ghetto, ]{.class_s4N4-0}he is no good in the [White House]{.class_s4N4-0} (editor's note: this was originally written in 1976, [32 years]{.class_s4N4-0} before Obama became "President")[. ]{.class_s4N4-0}A man is bad because he is [bad]{.class_s4N4-0}; and if he is not bad in a Hart, Schaffner, and Marx, he will behave himself in a Robert Hall's (Matt. 7:18).

Karl Marx would have been a good-for-nothing, lazy bum in jail or in the Kremlin, and Martin (Michael) Luther "King" Jr. would have been a rabble-rousing "Red" whether he drove a Cadillac or a mule. You do not change men by changing society; [you change society by changing men]{.class_s4N4-0} (John 3:3--6). This infallible, absolute, fixed, permanent Divine order has never been altered, adjusted, compromised, or "related" to anything any man (or nation) has ever done in 6,000 years of recorded history. The fruit of changing "society" in this century []{#c3RJ.xhtml_page_739}(after blaming it collectively for your individual sins) is the political intrigues of [Communism ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [Catholicism. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Both systems are ironclad dictatorships where a handful of egomaniacs control literally millions of people through a Police (or priest) State.

["There came out this calf"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 24). Moses knows what to do with the automatic calf that automatically appeared from nowhere and was responsible for the terrible "social condition."

["He took the calf which they had made, and burnt it in the fire, and ground it to powder, and strawed it upon the water, and made the children of Israel drink of it"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 20).

Evidently, Moses thought the trouble was with the people as well as the CALF. Evidently, the Creator of the universe (Psa. 148) thinks the trouble is with the [religious philosophers]{.class_s4N4-0} (Matt. 23:1--36), not the "environment." Poverty doesn't make criminals; [criminals ]{.class_s4N4-0}produce [poverty]{.class_s4N4-0}.

Deuteronomy 9:21 explains verse 20. It is a well-known fact that when the great French brain Voltaire (1694--1778) was saying that verse 20 was an impossible operation, there were fifty guilders' shops in Paris that were holding gold "in solution." Very often the "best brains of the day" are so out of touch with reality (see any major Bible commentator from 1880--2010) that they live and die in a smog of misinformed makebelieve (see the[ Commentary on Proverbs,]{.class_s4N4-0} Prov. 30:28).

Quite typical of this erroneous nonsense is the translation of John 1:18 made by the Lockman Foundation, reputedly the "best Conservative scholarship" of 1960--1970, with total disregard for every major statement on the Deity of Christ by orthodox []{#c3RJ.xhtml_page_740}Christianity in every major church council from A.D. 325 to the present. The "Lockman Foundation," as Voltaire, lives in a dream world where the nebulous clouds of scholarly ether waft paralyzing aromas across their minds 24 hours a day. John 1:18 in the [NASV]{.class_s4N4-0} is as blatant a piece of God-dishonoring trash as ever came from the pen of Nels Ferre, Bishop Pike, Eugene Carson Blake, Harry Emerson Fosdick, or Norman Vincent Peale. The fact that this wretched falsehood is recommended by nearly every "Fundamental" scholar in America means that the greatest apostasy the world has ever seen is now taking place within the body of regenerated believers (see Hills, [Believing Bible Study,]{.class_s4N4-0} pg. 136).

["So great a sin"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 21). Idolatry, covetousness, blasphemy, and sacrilege are all present at the first "American" dance. ["The PEOPLE\...are set on mischief"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 22). Shades of Saul (1 Sam. 15:21). This indefinite third person plural is still used (without let up) in every army, navy, business establishment, and school on this earth: "THEY forgot to bring the lister bags," "THEY haven't set up the schedule this year," "THEY didn't move \$10,000 worth of receipts last year," "THEY haven't got the new cars out yet," "THEY never get this schedule right," "THEY don't let us off till June of this year," "THEY got a thing going where, etc., etc."

["The people were naked"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 25). Dummelow (always good for a laugh) tells us that this is the [metaphorical sense]{.class_s4N4-0} of "unruly" and should be translated "were broken loose." The apostate [Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} sides with this and spiritualizes the passage to mean "loose from all restraint and from allegiance to God." (What blockhead didn't know that already from reading vss. 1--23?)

The senile [American Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} (any edition) reproduces this Alexandrian fatuity so the modern reader would lose the connection between the mixed African dance of 1500--1400 B.C. and the mixed African dances of A.D. 1900--2010. Strangely enough, the famous [Septuagint]{.class_s4N4-0}---which the [New American Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} used to correct the New Testament in 2,000 places---now reads ["scattered"]{.class_s4N4-0}; and yet, the [RV, ASV, NASV,]{.class_s4N4-0} and [RSV ]{.class_s4N4-0}refused to accept it as the "final authority." (We know with what we are dealing: the Lockman Foundation has set itself up as the final authority, and it will take any reading EXCEPT the [Authorized]{.class_s4N4-0} reading of the Protestant Reformation.)

Observe how the singular translation of ["para"]{.class_s4N4-0} (Heb.) as ["naked"]{.class_s4N2-0} in the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}text sheds light on the context: ["for Aaron had made them naked unto their shame among their enemies."]{.class_s4N2-0} The trite and sterile translation of ["para"]{.class_s4N4-0} as "turn loose" or "scattered" is obviously the wrong rendering by the context. What is the SHAME of being "turned loose" or "scattered"? But observe how the highly accurate and scientific [King James]{.class_s4N4-0} text translates within the confines set by the Holy Spirit Himself (see Rev.3:18, 16:15). (When in doubt, throw the Hebrew scholars out.)

["Who is on the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[L]{.class_s4NE-0}[ord\'s]{.class_s4N9-0}[ side"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 26)? This is the question that Elijah asks Israel (1 Kings 18:21); this is the proposition that Joshua puts to Israel (Josh. 24:18--19); and this is the dividing line that Jesus Christ drew in the New Testament (Matt. 6:24; Luke 16:13). ["The L]{.class_s4N2-0}[ord's]{.class_s4N9-0}[ SIDE"]{.class_s4N2-0} is clearly:

1\. A position opposite some [other ]{.class_s4N4-0}side (John 17:9).

2\. A position the world has never taken nor ever will take (1 John 2:15--17).

3\. A position that only a [minority ]{.class_s4N4-0}will take in any age (Matt 24:10--12).

4\. A [controversial side]{.class_s4N4-0} (John 7:40--43).

5\. A[ "despised and rejected"]{.class_s4N2-0} side (Isa. 53:1--6).

6\. An antagonistic position that assumes a NEGATIVE stance towards Socialism, paganism, Communism, Catholicism, Santa Claus, commerce, world peace, science, integration, psychology, wealth, fame, public theatricals, position, popularity, and worldly influence (Luke 16:15; Heb. 13:13).

7\. A side which will lead its "team" into a head-on collision with every teaching of science, religion, philosophy, education, and history where these teachings cross the revelation of the Book.

Observe, in what follows, the fulfillment of Luke 14:26 in a LITERAL sense so bloody that to this day, unsaved religious leaders like to think of the "God of the Old Testament" and the "God of the New Testament" as two separate gods---[exactly]{.class_s4N4-0} as the [New American Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} (John 1:18) pictures them for Dr. Neal and Dr. Custer (see [The Sword of the Lord,]{.class_s4N4-0} Sept. 17, 1971).

["Put every man his sword by his side\...and slay every man his brother, and\...his companion, and\...his neighbour" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 27). Neither near relatives nor close friends nor neighbors are to prevent the child of God from keeping the commandments, and if relatives (Zech. 13:3--4) or close friends or neighbors (Deut. 17:2--5) undertake to lead you away from the true God (John 4:24; 1 John 5:20) and into pagan idolatry, they are to be brushed aside and ignored completely. The fact that the world will look upon this action as ["hate"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Luke 14:26) is immaterial to the Lord, and it should be immaterial []{#c3RJ.xhtml_page_743}to you. Three thousand bloody corpses are strewn on the ground where God first illustrates this divine principle. They were not killed (as the [Waldensians]{.class_s4N4-0}) for preaching the Bible, they were not killed (as the [Vaudois]{.class_s4N4-0}) for preserving the [Textus Receptus,]{.class_s4N4-0} they were not killed (as the [Reformers]{.class_s4N4-0}) for criticizing Roman Catholicism, they were not killed (as the [Ulstermen]{.class_s4N4-0}) for trying to rid their State government of Roman Catholic political control, and they were not killed (as Russian [Baptists]{.class_s4N4-0}) for trying to win sinners to Christ. All attempts to justify "religious crusades" on the grounds of the passage are nonsense.

The people who carry out bloody "crusades" (500 B.C.-A.D. 2010) to enforce their religious and political prejudices areNEVER the Bible-believing bunch. They are either the bunch who [PROFESS to believe the Bible]{.class_s4N4-0} (Puritans and Roman Catholics) or the bunch who have openly [ABANDONED belief in the Bible]{.class_s4N4-0} (Communists and Moslems). You can bet on that like you can bet on the temperature of the sun being hotter than 500 degrees Fahrenheit.

Furthermore, such remarks appearing in a "commentary" are not in the least to be considered out of order; they are absolutely essential to a clear exposition of the text. A commentator who refuses to comment on the text in that fashion (plainly, [pointedly, ]{.class_s4N4-0}and practically) is a traitor to his calling.

All totalitarian systems (Communism, Socialism, Catholicism, Fascism, Nazism, and Islam) are ruthless and without moral scruples when it comes to eliminating the opposition. ["Slay every man his brother"]{.class_s4N2-0} is as good Catholic and Communist (and Moslem) doctrine as it is Old Testament Biblical doctrine, so no one should get agitated about the text. A Darwinian anthropoid who []{#c3RJ.xhtml_page_744}believes in the "survival of the fittest," in a framework of [scientific materialism,]{.class_s4N4-0} can hardly find fault with Moses for cutting out a cancer.

["And all the sons of Levi"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 26). This is the most natural reaction, for the Levites are Moses' "kinfolk" (see 2:1). The suddenness with which they volunteer also indicates that even if Aaron (a Levite!) was the religious "sahib" of the orgy, he was bushwhacked into the job without his consent; not all of his kindred were "committed to social involvement" in a "meaningful exchange of cultures" (to quote the current braying of the leaders of UNESCO, HEW, the NEA, and others). (What modern newspapers and magazines refer to as "cultural exchanges through interracial involvement" are usually little more than Caucasian TEENAGERS getting acquainted with lying, stealing, drug abuse, machetes, razors, bestiality, homosexuality, filthy language, and incest.)

["And there fell of the people that day about three thousand men"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 28). By providential design, we are given the same number in Acts 2:41, where the Holy Spirit inaugurates this present dispensation. The contrast is striking. Three thousand are slain at the giving of the Law (Rom. 7:10--11), whereas three thousand are quickened to new life (Eph. 2:1--5) at the giving of the Comforter.

["Upon his son, and upon his brother"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 29). This verse is obviously connected with verse 27. Moses is telling them that the price demanded of consecration, in this case, is to fall upon their own relatives and slay them.

The word ["upon"]{.class_s4N2-0} is used exactly as it is used in the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}1611 in other places, meaning to ["fall upon"]{.class_s4N2-0} a man and slay him (see 2 Sam. 1:15; 1 Kings 2:32; Job []{#c3RJ.xhtml_page_745}1:17, etc.), and we have no need for the changing of this word in the [American Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} (any edition)---which matches the [Revised Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} of the National Council of "Christian" Churches (see [Satan's Masterpiece! The New ASV, ]{.class_s4N4-0}1972).

32:30 "And it came to pass on the morrow, that Moses said unto the people, Ye have sinned a great sin: and now I will go up unto the [Lord]{.class_s4N9-1}; peradventure I shall make an atonement for your sin.

31 And Moses returned unto the [Lord]{.class_s4N9-1}, and said, Oh, this people have sinned a great sin, and have made them gods of gold.

32 Yet now, if thou wilt forgive their sin---; and if not, blot me, I pray thee, out of thy book which thou hast written.

33 And the [Lord ]{.class_s4N9-1}said unto Moses, Whosoever hath sinned against me, him will I blot out of my book.

34 Therefore now go, lead the people unto the place of which I have spoken unto thee: behold, mine Angel shall go before thee: nevertheless in the day when I visit I will visit their sin upon them.

35 And the [Lord ]{.class_s4N9-1}plagued the people, because they made the calf, which Aaron made."

The word ["atonement"]{.class_s4N2-0} has already appeared in 29:33, 36, 37, 30:10, 15, 16; and other places, but at this point, we find the word connected with the [Ark of the Covenant. ]{.class_s4N4-0}You see, the Holy Spirit (who [used ]{.class_s4N4-0}the translators of the [Authorized Version]{.class_s4N4-0}) often plays jokes on Hebrew and Greek scholars. Knowing that []{#c3RJ.xhtml_page_746}these destructive critics (Conservative scholars included) would waste reams of paper correcting ["atonement"]{.class_s4N2-0} to "cover"---so as to emphasize the covering over the Mercy Seat (see 37:9)---the Holy Spirit has preserved the correct emphasis in the English [without any reference to the Hebrew. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Observe, in Deuteronomy 10:1--5, the ARK which Moses made and carried up into the mount at the very time of his saying ["peradventure I shall make an atonement for your sin"]{.class_s4N2-0}! This associates the ark with the blood atonement [before the Tabernacle is erected,]{.class_s4N4-0} before the materials for the Holy of Holies have been gathered, and even before the [real Ark]{.class_s4N4-0} (37:1--9) was fashioned.

Now, it is not by an accidental oversight that all of the Conservative scholars missed the connections; they were so busy substituting their own learning for the authority of the Holy Bible that they forgot the great principle of Ezekiel 14:1--11. This great Biblical truth, found in either Testament (see 2 Thess. 2:3--12, for example), shows that where any man seeks to keep his idols while he is seeking the truth, he cannot find the truth. This principle holds throughout any period of history, in any of God's dealings with men of any race or nation. For Christians, it simply means that profession of orthodoxy, belief in the "fundamentals of the faith," 25 years of formal education, a "godly" reputation, mastery of the original languages, success in building buildings, and an IQ of 170 doesn't equip ANY saved sinner to find the truth if that is all the equipment he has. Where the Christian scholar refuses to [submit to the authority of the word,]{.class_s4N4-0} God refuses to show him anything that is not already written in a book by some other man. Bible revelations cease where []{#c3RJ.xhtml_page_747}"Christian" education usurps the position of the Holy Spirit.

The "covering" (Heb., ["kaphar"]{.class_s4N4-0}) of the sinner's sins is so apparent in all things that deal with the Ark that no alteration of the word ["atonement"]{.class_s4N2-0} is necessary. The Ark is covered with the [Mercy Seat,]{.class_s4N4-0} the Mercy Seat is covered with the [cherubim]{.class_s4N4-0}, and the cherubim were covered by the veil (40:3), which was covered by the [Tabernacle ]{.class_s4N4-0}itself. The ARK is first connected with atonement BEFORE any Tabernacle is there (cf. Rev. 13:8), and although Moses does not make any atonement when he goes up, his intention at this time is so clearly the intention of the Lord Jesus Christ in John 10:11 (and the intention of Paul in Rom. 9:1--3) that no mistake could be made by anyone who believed the text as it stood in the "English." ["And if not, blot me, I pray thee, out of thy book which thou hast written"]{.class_s4N2-0}! It takes blood to atone for sin, and the lengthy discussions of the Hebrew word ["kaphar"]{.class_s4N4-0} mean nothing at all.

The greatest instruction which can be received from the text is that Jesus had to die before His people could enter the Promised Land. Moses, likewise, perished outside of Canaan before his people could go in. No wonder the Scriptures speak of the Lord Jesus as the "prophet like unto Moses" (see Acts 3:22--26).

Verse 31 is self-explanatory; the words ["god"]{.class_s4N2-0} and ["gold"]{.class_s4N2-0} only differ by one letter, and in making the ["gods of gold,"]{.class_s4N2-0} the nation had violated the first two commandments and the last one. This is exactly what the rich young ruler of Luke 18:18--23 did, and the careful reader will observe that Jesus baits the young man with the commandments he could keep before springing the trap on him.

Moses offers to become [accursed ]{.class_s4N4-0}(see Rom. 9:1--3) in Israel's place if that is the only way the Lord will forgive them for their sin. The ["book which thou hast written"]{.class_s4N2-0} is certainly NOT the muster or roll-call of the tribes which Moses had written (see Adam Clarke's Commentary, pg. 467). ["Thy book"]{.class_s4N2-0} is a book, or collection of books, found many times in the Bible, and in every place where it occurs, it is a reference to a supernatural book which God Himself wrote.

1\. Wherever other books are mentioned, their authors are mentioned (Exod. 17:14; Num. 5:23; Deut. 17:18; Josh. 24:26; etc.), and where no author is mentioned in the immediate context (2 Sam. 1:18; Gen. 5:1; Matt. 1:1; 2 Kings 14:15, 18, 28; etc.), a human author can be found for the writing.

2\. ["The book of life"]{.class_s4N2-0} is said to have material in it that only God could have known (see Phil. 4:3; Rev. 13:8). If this book is anywhere on this earth today, it would have to be the Bible itself (Heb. 4:12), for this word is also said to be ["settled in heaven"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Psa. 119:89), exactly as the incarnate Word was in heaven and on earth at the same time (see John 3:13). (The proof for this last statement---John 3:13---has been carefully removed from the [New American Standard "Bible,"]{.class_s4N4-0} promoted by Bob Jones University and other dead-orthodox institutions. [It is also missing from all the Bibles translated by Liberals and Modernists]{.class_s4N4-0} \[[New English, Good News, RSV,]{.class_s4N4-0} etc.\]; "Birds of a feather\...etc.")

3\. The Old Testament saints are written and listed in a book which is God's book (see Dan. 12:1; Mal. 3:16), and this is called ["the book of the living"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Psa. 69:28). Its content is in contrast to those whose names []{#c3RJ.xhtml_page_749}are ["written in the earth"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Jer. 17:13). God seems to keep a census roll, and in keeping with His nature, He carefully segregates, by discrimination, the "earthly" and the "heavenly" (cf. 1 Cor. 15:39--49; John 3:3--8).

With all of this information available, Clarke, Lange, Ellicott, Johnson, Keil, Delitzsch, Williams, and Bullinger slam across the passage like a hockey team headed for the goal posts; and if one expects any light from them on the passage, he will have to be quicker than Bobby Hull or Bobby Orr to pick it up.

Now the Bible is plainly "a Book of Life," whether it is[ "THE book of life" ]{.class_s4N2-0}or not. Furthermore, the Bible is composed of "BOOKS" (plural) which could constitute the ["books"]{.class_s4N2-0} of Revelation 20:12 and Daniel 7:10. Furthermore, the Bible gives more room to a roll call (or census) of men's NAMES than it does to the creation of the universe. Yet further, the names listed are often associated with numbers running into three and four figures, and sometimes five figures (Num. 1, for example). But finally, names written in this book (Luke 10:20) can be [removed ]{.class_s4N4-0}from it (vs. 33), and so the last warning in the Bible is not to mess with the WORDS in it (Rev. 22:18--19).

On this note, we probably should close the composition, for the "nasty turn of events" that are implied in the exegesis above is far beyond the mental or spiritual grasp of the greatest Hebrew and Greek scholars who ever lived. You see, English, Chinese, German, French, Russian, Spanish, and Japanese names have [meanings]{.class_s4N4-0}; and these meanings are also found in the [Hebrew words]{.class_s4N4-0} which make up the composition of the Hebrew names listed in Genesis, Exodus, Numbers, 1 Chronicles, etc. Wouldn't it be shocking to find men []{#c3RJ.xhtml_page_750}standing at the White Throne Judgment, being judged by the "revisions" they produced, and discovering that those "revisions" had removed their own names from the Book of Life? There is only one safe position to take when dealing with such matters (Isa. 66:2): [DON'T MESS WITH THE BOOK.]{.class_s4N4-0}

["Mine Angel shall go before thee"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 34). This is the Angel of the Lord, which is apparent from 33:11, 14. Bullinger says that this angel "is probably an inferior angel," but Bullinger (as the revision committee of the ["New" Scofield Reference Bible]{.class_s4N4-0}) has simply stumbled at the indefinite article in 33:2, and we have been through these matters before (see comments on Gal. 4:14 in the [Commentary on Galatians---Colossians]{.class_s4N4-0}). Besides this, the cross-reference in English, which the Holy Spirit thought fit to use (Isa. 63:9), corrects Bullinger's error.

["In the day when I visit I will visit their sin upon them"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 34). This is explained by what follows---["And the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord ]{.class_s4N9-0}[plagued the people\...."]{.class_s4N2-0} Although the expression ["in the day when I visit"]{.class_s4N2-0} shows a future time for punishment, the reference is probably to the judgments pronounced in Numbers chapter 14. However, the old law of sowing and reaping (Gal. 6:7) is apparent in every letter of ["their sin." "Their sin"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 34) was the sin of idolatry (Acts 7:42), and it was at this place---not later---that ["God turned, and gave them up"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Acts 7:41--42). Notice how clearly the Holy Spirit brings this out in the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}1611 without any benefit of "accredited scholarship." The plagues that follow (Num. 11:33, 16:49) are the logical outcome of violating the first two commandments.

Further, the serious student of Scripture should observe that the expression ["mine Angel"]{.class_s4N2-0} is equated []{#c3RJ.xhtml_page_751}with ["an angel"]{.class_s4N2-0} both here (33:2) and in 23:20, 23. The peculiar statement of Phillip Johnson ([Wycliffe Commentary,]{.class_s4N4-0} pg. 83) to the effect that there was a modification of Jehovah's nature, at this time, is a fluke. The same Angel of the Lord who went ["before"]{.class_s4N2-0} Moses and Israel in verse 34 is the same Angel of the Lord mentioned in 23:20, 23. The difference is that this Angel no longer appears in the middle of the camp (33:7--9), but appears at the Tabernacle placed "outside the camp."

Most of the elements of New Testament Christianity are present. "The face of the Lord" (Exod. 33:11; 2 Cor. 4:6) is to be found ["without the camp"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Exod. 33:7; Heb. 13:11--13), and the Lord's NAME is in Him (23:21), [exactly as the Angel of the Lord whom Paul acknowledges to be his Master ]{.class_s4N4-0}(see Acts 27:23). (Ah, the deadly accuracy of the archaic English. How excruciating are its judgments on scholarship and its infallible ways past finding out.)

There has been no change in God's person or essential presence; the only change has been in His geographical location in regards to the encampment. (When in doubt, throw the majority of "accredited Conservatives" out.)

In closing the chapter, it should be observed that ["the people"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 35) share an equal guilt with Aaron, for although he[ "fashioned it with a graving tool"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 4), it was [the people]{.class_s4N4-0} who demanded it and brought him the material from which to make it. Stephen is quick to lay hold of this truth, so he says in his message to the Sanhedrin: ["And they made a calf in those days\...and rejoiced in the work of their own hands"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Acts 7:41).

[]{#c3XE.xhtml}

# [Chapter 33]{.class_s4NN} {.heading_s2E2-0}

33:1 "And the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}said unto Moses, Depart, and go up hence, thou and the people which thou hast brought up out of the land of Egypt, unto the land which I sware unto Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, saying, Unto thy seed will I give it:

2 And I will send an angel before thee; and I will drive out the Canaanite, the Amorite, and the Hittite, and the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite:

3 Unto a land flowing with milk and honey: for I will not go up in the midst of thee; for thou art a stiffnecked people: lest I consume thee in the way."

We have commented already on the passage in discussing the angel of 32:34. Notice the substitution of ["an"]{.class_s4N2-0} angel for ["mine Angel,"]{.class_s4N2-0} who appeared to Moses on Mt. Sinai (Acts 7:30) and claimed the name of ["I AM"]{.class_s4N2-0} (see 3:2, 6, 14). "AN angel of the Lord" can be any angel of God, but it can also be a reference to the Theophany---["THE angel of the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord]{.class_s4N9-0}["]{.class_s4N2-0} (cf. Gal. 4:14).

Clarke with the majority of commentators ([Wycliffe Commentary,]{.class_s4N4-0} Dummelow, [Pulpit Commentary,]{.class_s4N4-0} etc.) still cannot straighten out the mess they have gotten themselves into, so they all insist that an inferior angel shows up in verse 2, in spite of the clear promise of 32:34. That is, when a destructive critic---John R. Rice, for example ([Sword of the Lord,]{.class_s4N4-0} Feb 23, 1973, pg. 4)---goes about messing up the word, a two-letter article (["an"]{.class_s4N2-0}) can throw him further than Nicklaus can drive a golf ball.

For[ ]{.class_s4N2-0}"the land of milk and honey," see comments under 3:8.

["Thou art a stiffnecked people"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 3). See comments under 32:9.

33:4 "And when the people heard these evil tidings, they mourned: and no man did put on him his ornaments.

5 For the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}had said unto Moses, Say unto the children of Israel, Ye are a stiffnecked people: I will come up into the midst of thee in a moment, and consume thee: therefore now put off thy ornaments from thee, that I may know what to do unto thee.

6 And the children of Israel stripped themselves of their ornaments by the mount Horeb.

7 And Moses took the tabernacle, and pitched it without the camp, afar off from the camp, and called it the Tabernacle of the congregation. And it came to pass, that every one which sought the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}went out unto the tabernacle of the congregation, which was without the camp.

8 And it came to pass, when Moses went out unto the tabernacle, that all the people rose up, and stood every man at his tent door, and looked after Moses, until he was gone into the tabernacle.

9 And it came to pass, as Moses entered into the tabernacle, the cloudy pillar descended, and stood at the door of the tabernacle, and the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}talked with Moses.

10 And all the people saw the cloudy pillar stand at the tabernacle door: and all the people rose up and worshipped, every man in his tent door.

11 And the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}spake unto Moses face to face, as a man speaketh unto his friend. And he turned again []{#c3XE.xhtml_page_754}into the camp: but his servant Joshua, the son of Nun, a young man, departed not out of the tabernacle."

Jacob and company follow the procedure of verse 4 in Genesis 35:4, so there is no doubt about the typology. To strip oneself of extraneous adornments (see 1 Pet. 3:3) is to indicate that the soul is appearing before God in its unvarnished and unembellished condition. The "grace notes" are no longer in the music, and the ornate spires and cornices (see mid-Victorian architecture) are missing from the eaves. Isaiah 3:16--24 gives a beautiful picture of the excess baggage which society people carry when trying to impress someone. [No outward adorning]{.class_s4N4-0} is the way to stand before God (Gen. 35:2--4), so off go the ornaments.

The corrupt [Septuagint ]{.class_s4N4-0}manuscript, used for the [New American Standard "Bible,"]{.class_s4N4-0} has added 58 words to the correct text. Adam Clarke is so anxious to justify this spurious addition that he writes 750 words in its defense, citing everything from Saul's demonic stripping (1 Sam. 19:24) to Mohammedan pilgrimages. Why the worthy doctor should waste so much time justifying this depraved addition is rather mystifying. Any man who knew the Hebrew language as well as Dr. Clarke certainly should have smelled the dry rot of Origen and Eusebius in the passage when it showed up. But as we have mentioned elsewhere, adeptness in foreign languages and training in the "original tongues" are rarely any safeguard against monstrous bungling where the scholar has approached the Bible with the air of a dentist about to pull teeth.

["I will come up into the midst of thee in a moment, and consume thee"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 5). This is exactly what happens in Numbers 11:1, 33, 16:46.

The entire passage is a picture of the backslidden saint and the saint who is in fellowship with God. Furthermore, this photo is reproduced clearly for us in 1 Corinthians 10:6--10. There can be no mistake about the types.

1\. The idolatrous Christian is the Christian who COVETS (Col. 3:5).

2\. The wrath of God can call on this Christian when he partakes of [idolatry ]{.class_s4N4-0}with ["the children of disobedience"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Eph. 5:6).

3\. To rid himself of covetousness, the Christian must be reduced to an appreciation of ["FOOD and RAIMENT"]{.class_s4N2-0} only (1 Tim. 6:8). ["Ornaments" ]{.class_s4N2-0}[are OUT.]{.class_s4N4-0}

4\. It is the Law that strips the Christian (vs. 6---["by the mount Horeb"]{.class_s4N2-0}---and this is why Paul insists---even after salvation---that the law is spiritual, holy, just, and good (Rom. 7:12, 14).

5\. The saint in fellowship deals with his Lord ["face to face"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 11) as a ["friend"]{.class_s4N2-0} (cf. John 15:15),[ ]{.class_s4N2-0}but to do this, he must go[ "without the camp" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(cf. Heb. 13:11--13).

6\. ["The cloudy pillar"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 10) is a type of the Holy Spirit, and it is through Him that we converse with God. Above all(!), the devout student cannot help but note that Jesus is present (vs. 11) and that He stays IN THE TABERNACLE. (["Joshua\...departed not out of the tabernacle."]{.class_s4N2-0} This tabernacle is not the one built later, but the one on which we commented in 18:7--11, which see.)

Formal sacramentalists will immediately recognize their [church liturgy]{.class_s4N4-0} in verses 8 and 10. Since both Catholic and Episcopal communions usurp the Old Testament priesthood and try to force the believer back into a pre-Christian ecclesiasticism, we often find their leaders "parading" up and down the aisle headed for the "holy of holies" in the middle of a split chancel while the congregation stands; it is quite like "Here Comes the Bride."

["And the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord ]{.class_s4N9-0}[spake unto Moses face to face"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 11 cf. Deut. 34:10). This is the manner in which he spoke to Abraham (see Acts 7:1--3), so the term ["friend" ]{.class_s4N2-0}is applied to Moses exactly as in Abraham's case (James 2:23). However, Moses and Abraham are ["face to face"]{.class_s4N2-0} with the Angel of the Lord only, and this is apparent because none of the glistening aura of God's presence rubs off on their faces until the mountain top experience of the next chapter.

["Joshua\...a young man"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 11). Joshua's age at this time is around forty to 42 years old, depending upon the amount of time one assigns to the battles of Joshua chapters 1--24. However, one must consider the fact that Joshua, Moses, Joseph, and Abraham all live far beyond the allotted "threescore and ten" of Psalm 90:10; and when one lays forty years alongside 110, it comes to a little more than a third of a man's life. One-third of the standard "threescore and ten" is 23 years and four months. By such a standard, Joshua is [25 years old]{.class_s4N4-0} and, hence, is referred to as a young man.

33:12 "And Moses said unto the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}, See, thou sayest unto me, Bring up this people: and thou hast not let me know whom thou wilt send with me. Yet thou []{#c3XE.xhtml_page_757}hast said, I know thee by name, and thou hast also found grace in my sight.

13 Now therefore, I pray thee, if I have found grace in thy sight, shew me now thy way, that I may know thee, that I may find grace in thy sight: and consider that this nation is thy people.

14 And he said, My presence shall go with thee, and I will give thee rest.

15 And he said unto him, If thy presence go not with me, carry us not up hence.

16 For wherein shall it be known here that I and thy people have found grace in thy sight? is it not in that thou goest with us? so shall we be separated, I and thy people, from all the people that are upon the face of the earth.

17 And the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}said unto Moses, I will do this thing also that thou hast spoken: for thou hast found grace in my sight, and I know thee by name.

18 And he said, I beseech thee, shew me thy glory.

19 And he said, I will make all my goodness pass before thee, and I will proclaim the name of the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}before thee; and will be gracious to whom I will be gracious, and will shew mercy on whom I will shew mercy.

20 And he said, Thou canst not see my face: for there shall no man see me, and live.

21 And the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}said, Behold, there is a place by me, and thou shalt stand upon a rock:

22 And it shall come to pass, while my glory passeth by, that I will put thee in a clift of the rock, and will cover thee with my hand while I pass by:

23 And I will take away mine hand, and thou shalt see my back parts: but my face shall not be seen."

Again, we find beautiful pictures of New Testament salvation peering out at us through every verse. In verse 12, Moses, as the Christian, has ["found grace"]{.class_s4N2-0} in God's sight (Eph. 2:8), and the Lord knows Him ["by name"]{.class_s4N2-0} (cf. John 10:3). The Christian seeks God's way (vs. 13), not his own, and Ananias is on hand to tell Paul about these ways as soon as Paul enters Damascus (Acts 22:12--16). Furthermore, God's presence (vs. 14) goes with the Christian (Heb. 13:6), and through it he has peace (John 14:27; Rom. 5:1). The ["place"]{.class_s4N2-0} by God (vs. 21) is the second place in the Trinity, and it is a ["Rock"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 Cor. 10:2--4). The Christian and his sins are "covered" (vs. 22) in this presence; in such a position he, as Moses, is a spiritual priest in charge of mediating for a nation (1 Pet. 2:5--9).

Moses begins the great passage by asking for human help to lead the people out (["thou hast not let me know whom thou wilt send with me,"]{.class_s4N2-0} vs. 12). Compare this with the same business on Mt. Sinai much earlier (4:13--14).

The mistaken notion of Dummelow, Clarke, and others that Moses forgot the angel of vs. 2, or wanted a specific angel like 32:34, is just more of the same old nonsense we run into---["immer wieder, immer wieder"]{.class_s4N4-0} (on and on)---when dealing with the proud, critical, destructive Conservatives of the Nineteenth and Twentieth Centuries. Moses is careful to ask for someone to go ["with"]{.class_s4N2-0} him, not "AHEAD" of him. (The reader will always do well to believe the [Authorized]{.class_s4N4-0} text as established and preserved by the Holy Spirit and not waste any time trying to get "light" from the "majority" of Conservative infidels.)

["Shew me now thy way"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 13) quickly progresses to ["I beseech thee, shew me thy glory"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 18). It seems that when Moses and Jacob get at "close quarters" with the Angel of the Lord that their appetite is whetted for more and more revelation (cf. Gen. 32:25--30), and they take advantage of God's grace like a child takes advantage of an affectionate parent. Observe further the double statement on grace in verse 13:

1\. Moses [has ]{.class_s4N4-0}["found]{.class_s4NC} \[past tense\] [grace"]{.class_s4N2-0} in God's sight.

2\. He wants to know God's way so that he ["may]{.class_s4N2-0} \[future tense\] [find grace"]{.class_s4N2-0} in His sight.

3\. Notice how neatly this matches the statements on grace in Hebrews 4:16.

Moses realizes (as any Christian knows it to be) that without God's presence it is a hopeless battle. Joshua the warrior knew this, for he stayed at the tabernacle where the "presence" was manifested. This presence was ["the cloudy pillar"]{.class_s4N2-0} that accompanied them through the wilderness wanderings (40:35--37), and in typology, it stood for the indwelling Comforter who[ "IS"]{.class_s4N2-0} the Lord (2 Cor. 3:17). Separation (vs. 16) and power are both connected with the presence of God, as well as grace.

Verses 17 and 18 are self explanatory. However, the next passage (vss. 19--23) causes such a scramble among the commentators (of any hue) that we are alerted to the fact that there must be something in the passage on the Deity of Christ. (Observe how the Lockman Foundation did this in giving you the Liberal translation of Luke 2:33 in the [New American Standard "Bible"]{.class_s4N4-0} promoted by some of the apostate faculty members at Dallas Theological Seminary.)

Once again, Keil, Driver, Kennicott, Bullinger, Clarke, Dummelow, Ellicott, the [Wycliffe Commentary,]{.class_s4N4-0} Bantzch, and the [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_s4N4-0}(and the rest of the "Scholars' Union") go into their periodic spasm in an effort to cover up the revelation about to be revealed in the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}text[.]{.class_s4N4-0}

1\. The highly advertised [American Standard]{.class_s4N4-0} (any edition) has refused to translate ["achori"]{.class_s4N4-0} (Heb.) in the plural although it is in the plural, and this is done after half of century (1900--1950) of complaining about "inconsistent translating" on the part of the [King James]{.class_s4N4-0} translators. The same serving of apostate garbage has translated a plural word singular (which was a plural word) in 32:3, and then translated another plural word singular in 32:23. This is all done with the air of the most injured innocence, while criticizing the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}for its failure to translate number, tense, gender, etc., properly.

2\. The [Cambridge Bible]{.class_s4N4-0} suggests that only the "afterglow" of God, which he left "behind Him," was what Moses was about to see.

3\. Kalisch tells us that ["back parts"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 23) means God's works.

4\. There was no "VISIBLE APPEARANCE" on the mountain in the next chapter according to Adam Clarke, so the words ["see"]{.class_s4N2-0} and ["shew"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 13, 18, 20, 23) are to be "spiritualized." But those of us who studied our church history and history of Christian doctrine with a little more diligence than the Lockman Foundation, Ellicott, Westcott and Hort, Seeberg, Robertson, Schaff, Keil, Sanday, Delitzsch, Harnack, Gesenius, and the "Conservative orthodox commentators," have already smelled the rat: it is an [agnostic rat. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Somebody like Marcion, Valentinus, Philo, or Origen is messing with []{#c3XE.xhtml_page_761}our Bible again. Somebody doesn't want God to show up with arms and legs; at least not the Jehovah God of the passage! Someone like Dr. Neal or Dr. Custer (Bob Jones University) wants us to believe that a ["begotten God"]{.class_s4N4-0} revealed God the Father (see John 1:18 in the [New American Standard Crapout]{.class_s4N4-0}).

So for the 400th time, we shall lay all the conjectures of the Fundamental and Conservative scholars in the incinerator (with the Liberals and Neo-orthodox works) and take a long, prayerful, believing look at the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}text to locate the gold mine which these men in their arrogant pride could not find.

1\. Moses [had already beheld]{.class_s4N4-0} the glory of the Lord---in a sense (19:21, 16:7).

2\. Abraham, Jacob, and others [had already seen]{.class_s4N4-0} the "face of the Lord" in the sense of the Theophany (Gen. 32:30).

3\. [So had the elders of Israel]{.class_s4N4-0} (24:10).

4\. Therefore, the "Angel of the Lord" cannot be the apparition who ["descended" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(34:5) and ["passed by"]{.class_s4N2-0} (34:6). These two verbs destroy any theory of any Conservative or Liberal who ever lived who presumed that SOMEBODY did not pass across in front of Moses' two eyeballs.

5\. Since the appearance Moses beheld could not have been the essence of God's Soul (see vs. 20) in the sense of 1 Timothy 6:16, we are shut up to only one conclusion: Moses was granted the privilege of seeing the Lord Jesus Christ Himself [in His pre-incarnate state]{.class_s4N4-0}, which matched the glorified, resurrected Christ of Revelation 1:16---glorified flesh [with no blood]{.class_s4N4-0} (Luke 24:37--39).

The Lord Jesus Christ IS the glory of God (John 17:24; 2 Cor. 3:18), and He was "better" than the Angel of the []{#c3XE.xhtml_page_762}Lord (Heb. 1:4), even though He was made ["lower than the angels"]{.class_s4N2-0} [in His incarnate state]{.class_s4N4-0} (Heb. 2:7, 14, 16).

That is, Moses beheld (around 1500 B.C.) what Peter, James, and John beheld around A.D. 32 (Matt 17:1--5). Moses standing ["by"]{.class_s4N2-0} the Lord (vs. 21) answers to Elijah standing ["before"]{.class_s4N2-0} the Lord (1 Kings 17:1) in Revelation 11:3--6, exactly as Peter, James, and John saw them. (Ah, the unsearchable riches of the scholar-despised text. How profound are its revelations and its infallible English past correction with the "originals").

We have only located the mine; let us now dig for the nuggets which Wuest, Thieme, Lange, Robertson, Bantzch, Keil, DeWette, Davidson, Olhausen, Delitzsch, Raschi, Gray, Clarke, Dummelow, Adams, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown all missed in their mad desire to destroy the revelations of the Reformation text.

A. A sinner must experience God's GOODNESS (vs. 19) [before ]{.class_s4N4-0}he can understand a proclamation of the[ "name which is above every name"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 19 cf. Phil. 2:9).

B. Jesus Christ IS God's [goodness ]{.class_s4N4-0}and ALL of His goodness (Col. 2:9).

C. Jesus Christ is God's graciousness (vs. 19---["I will be gracious"]{.class_s4N2-0}), and it is this grace that saves the sinner.

D. Jesus Christ is the manifestation of God's [MERCY ]{.class_s4N4-0}(vs. 19), and this is the mercy spoken of in Luke 1:50, 54, 58, 72, and 78.

E. Jesus Christ is God's FACE (vs. 20) according to 1 John 1:1--2 and 2 Corinthians chapter 3.

F. Jesus Christ is the ROCK (vs. 21), according to 1 Peter 2:6--8 and 1 Corinthians 10:2--4.

G. Jesus Christ was CLEFT (vs. 22) to hide the sinner (see "Rock of Ages" by Toplady), and this cleft []{#c3XE.xhtml_page_763}rock is spoken of distinctly in 17:6 as a Rock that was[ "SMITTEN" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(see Isa. 53:4).

The briefest reading of the passage, therefore (with the most meager knowledge of the New Testament), should have revealed the Lord Jesus Christ [immediately ]{.class_s4N4-0}to every saved sinner who [professed ]{.class_s4N4-0}to be a Conservative scholar. These very men who prate (or bellow) about the "Lord Jesus" (or the "Word") being the final authority---[in order to get rid of the despised Authorized Version]{.class_s4N4-0}---cannot even find Him in the "original Hebrew" which they profess to believe. (Some of you are stupid enough to waste money on a "Christian education" when you have a Bible on your living room table.)

Jesus is the truth (John 14:6), so the truth pops up in the middle of Moses' revelation (34:6). Jesus forgives sin (Mark 2:7), so ["forgiving iniquity"]{.class_s4N2-0} pops up in the middle of the revelation (34:7). And with a dozen appearances of the word ["LORD"]{.class_s4N2-0} for Jehovah (see Clarke's lengthy explanations, pp. 474--475), Moses pulls out the word for Jesus: ["O Lord, let my Lord, I pray thee"]{.class_s4N2-0} (34:9 cf. Psa. 110:1).

That is, what is perfectly [plain ]{.class_s4N4-0}(and this time it is a devotional and spiritual revelation of [spiritual ]{.class_s4N4-0}truth) in the [King James 1611 AV,]{.class_s4N4-0} the greatest Conservative scholars of four centuries could not find in Latin, English, German, Hebrew, or Greek. But we have come by this route before (see 18:13--24); and we have memorized the highway signs, road markers, fills, curves, overpasses, intersections, and rest stops. We are no longer interested; we are no longer even amused. We know a dead end when we see one; and the scholastic line that begins with Origen, Eusebius, Jerome, and Augustine, and ends with Thieme, Wuest, Robertson, []{#c3XE.xhtml_page_764}and the Lockman Foundation, is as God-forsaken and desolate as the Irish Republican Army in the back alleys of burnt-out Belfast.

Nor does any amount of rebuke or correction ever straighten out these types of "Christians." The few things they [know ]{.class_s4N4-0}about the word of God that are TRUE were picked up from books written by men whom they despised in their age, and thus, they keep a backlog of information which gives the next generation the impression that THEY are the real Bible scholars. [They are not. ]{.class_s4N4-0}They are the greatest bungling amateurs who ever confounded the revelation of God Almighty. As we have said many times before, all of them---including every Conservative translator on the Lockman Foundation, with every man who taught him---has one great bond of affinity with Bloody Mary, Mary Calderone, George Santayana, Madalyn Murray O'Hair, Joseph Stalin, Thomas Huxley, Jerry Rubin, Sigmund Freud, Friedrich Engels, Karl Marx, Charles Darwin, and Pope Paul VI. [None of them can stand the authority of the Authorized Bible of the English-speaking people.]{.class_s4N4-0}

With the majesty and authority of the pre-incarnate Word staring them smack in the face, accompanied by a [detailed description]{.class_s4N4-0} of His [attributes ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [earthly ministry,]{.class_s4N4-0} the Bible-despising Conservative and Fundamental commentators join the ranks of Valentinus, Philo, Marcion, and Basilides, and "chicken out" of discussing verses 21--23 like Richard Nixon backing out of an open forum on "federal school bussing to enforce race mixing."

[]{#c3ZZ.xhtml}

# [Chapter 34]{.class_s4NN} {.heading_s2E2-0}

34:1 "And the LORD said unto Moses, Hew thee two tables of stone like unto the first: and I will write upon these tables the words that were in the first tables, which thou brakest.

2 And be ready in the morning, and come up in the morning unto mount Sinai, and present thyself there to me in the top of the mount.

3 And no man shall come up with thee, neither let any man be seen throughout all the mount; neither let the flocks nor herds feed before that mount."

Clarke wastes nearly two pages trying to prove that both Moses and God wrote the same thing. Clarke is deeply disturbed by what he imagines is an apparent contradiction between Moses writing the Ten Commandments (vs. 27--28) and God doing it (Deut. 10:4). Goethe, Hitzig, and Ewald were the originators of this ["double decalogue theory,"]{.class_s4N4-0} which Clarke reproduces for us (Commentary pp. 473--474) to solve a "contradiction." (The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} has well said that it is "absolutely without foundation in fact.") If the reader has had little or no training in Hebrew, he might be interested to observe that he can discern for himself the truth that the ["words"]{.class_s4N2-0} of verse 27 are the ones found between verses 10 and 26; they have no reference to the Decalogue whatsoever. God wrote the Decalogue again (vs. 1, cf. 32:16), while [Moses ]{.class_s4N4-0}wrote verses 10--26 for the people. (The fact that Ewald, Hitzig, and Clarke were three of the greatest Hebrew scholars who ever lived means absolutely nothing at all, as we have remarked before, and their confusion at this point painfully []{#c3ZZ.xhtml_page_766}emphasizes what we have said many times before in this series of commentaries: the bigger the belfry the more room for the bats.)

Since "heaven and earth shall pass away before a jot or tittle of the law shall fail" (Matt. 5:18; Luke 16:17), and since ["the scripture cannot be broken"]{.class_s4N2-0} (John 10:35), God merely replaces the busted tablets word for word (["I will write upon these tables the words that were in the first tables,"]{.class_s4N2-0} vs. 1).

Furthermore, the spiritual application is beautiful, for Moses' vision of the pre-incarnate Saviour cannot come before he is aware of [broken commandments]{.class_s4N4-0} (cf. chap. 32 and Rom. 7:5--10). If this were not enough, we see further that the Law is to be [magnified ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Isa. 42:21) and [restored ]{.class_s4N4-0}when the Saviour shows up (cf. vss. 5--6 and Luke 1:46). The words ["which thou brakest"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 1) must have sunk deeply into Moses' heart as he prepared for an intimate revelation of God, which, up to this time, had been denied to any man: note ["thou brakest"]{.class_s4N2-0} (cf. John 7:19).

Everything about the passage is deeply [personal]{.class_s4N4-0}. Observe: ["present thyself there to me"]{.class_s4N2-0} in verse 2. Paul says, ["I beseech you therefore, brethren, by the mercies of God, that ye present your bodies"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rom. 12:1). Mountain top experiences (cf. 1 Kings 19:8--18) are times of individual heart searching, individual confession, individual self-judgment, and individual communion with God (Matt. 14:23).

34:4 "And he hewed two tables of stone like unto the first; and Moses rose up early in the morning, and went up unto mount Sinai, as the [LORD ]{.class_s4ND}had commanded him, and took in his hand the two tables of stone.

5 And the [LORD ]{.class_s4ND}descended in the cloud, and stood with him there, and proclaimed the name of the LORD.

6 And the [LORD ]{.class_s4ND}passed by before him, and proclaimed, The LORD, The [LORD ]{.class_s4ND}God, merciful and gracious, longsuffering, and abundant in goodness and truth,

7 Keeping mercy for thousands, forgiving iniquity and transgression and sin, and that will by no means clear the guilty; visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children, and upon the children's children, unto the third and to the fourth generation.

8 And Moses made haste, and bowed his head toward the earth, and worshipped.

9 And he said, If now I have found grace in thy sight, O Lord[,]{.class_s4ND} let my Lord, I pray thee, go among us; for it is a stiffnecked people; and pardon our iniquity and our sin, and take us for thine inheritance."

Here again, we come upon one of those climactic scenes in the Bible that is so loaded with spiritual and doctrinal truth that it seems like a whole Bible in miniature (for similar passages see Gen. 3:1--8, 4:1--2, 12:1--3, 22:1--14, and 40:1--23 in the[ Commentary on Genesis]{.class_s4N4-0}). When approaching these concentrations of doctrinal revelation and spiritual truth, we should be doubly on guard against any of the "traditions of men" (Col. 2:8) or the writings of the "majority of Conservtive hijackers" who might steal the gold and silver of God's word from us. Since the rejectors of the [Authorized Version]{.class_s4N4-0} have already been shelved by God Himself in these matters of revelation, they are often Satanically led when they near a passage like the one we are about to study. Because of this, we shall dispense with []{#c3ZZ.xhtml_page_768}all of them, briefly, and then go "prospecting" into the unsearchable riches of the Elizabethan English.

Adam Clarke, in his zeal to impress us with his knowledge of Hebrew, sails over the greatest truth in verse 7 as though he were waterskiing over a sunken treasure chest. Running first to the Latin[ Vulgate]{.class_s4N4-0} and then to the corrupt [Septuagint]{.class_s4N4-0} and then to the Coptic[,]{.class_s4N4-0} the good Doctor massacres the verse till it resembles Wyatt, California in 1966. In so doing, he obliterates one of the greatest revelations on BLOOD REDEMPTION found in either Testament.

The [Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} dodges the passage like a batter getting away from an inside fast ball, while the Hyperdispensationalist Williams flees to the mountains of rhetoric and produces two paragraphs of vaporous spiritualizing that a cat couldn't see through in broad daylight. Dummelow cannot comment intelligently on verse 6 or 7, and the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} (after siding with the [King James]{.class_s4N4-0} reading, supported by Keil, Kalisch, Gesenius, and Rosenmuller) is unable to tell the reader what the text means or how it is to be applied. Only Bullinger guesses at the great truth in the passage, but his marginal note fails to guide the reader to Matthew 26:28; Mark 1:4; Romans 3:25; and Hebrews 9:15, and this is exactly what we are dealing with in the text.

The pity of all this is the fact that if the commentators had given an honest appraisal of the text, they might have turned a multitude of "Campbellite" preachers from heresy to the truth, for the great truths of verse 7 unravel the expression ["for the remission of sins"]{.class_s4N2-0} found in the New Testament. We shall say more about that later.

Now setting aside all of the destructive critics of the [Authorized text]{.class_s4N4-0} (Conservatives foremost), let us see what God intended for us to learn from "the King's English."

["And the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[LORD ]{.class_s4NE-0}[descended"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 5), as He did at His [First]{.class_s4N4-0} Advent (1 Tim. 3:16) and as He will at His [Second ]{.class_s4N4-0}Advent (Zech. 14:1--4).

["And stood with him there"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 5), as He did in His incarnation (Phil. 2:5--8; Heb. 2:9) and as He will at His return (Rev. 20:1--6).

["And proclaimed the name"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 5), which is always connected with MEN (Phil. 2:7--10) and, when truly ["proclaimed,"]{.class_s4N2-0} carries with it the following attributes:

1\. ["Merciful"]{.class_s4N2-0}: This, naturally, is the first attribute mentioned, for it is the first and foremost, prime, essential thing about God that must be true if He has ANY dealings with men. Without [mercy, ]{.class_s4N4-0}the entire race of mankind is doomed and damned without hope. When God's mercy gives out (Gen. 6:6--7), babies drown, old men burn (Gen. 7:21--23, 19:25--26), women are raped (Lam. 5:11), and men are nailed to trees (Luke 23:33). His "[mercy endureth forever]{.class_s4N2-0}" (Psa. 136) in the sense that it is [available ]{.class_s4N4-0}to every generation that is born, and always has been. It is His mercy that often takes the life of little children (1 Kings 14:12--13), and it was mercy that Pharaoh drowned (Psa. 136:15). (What if he had gotten through the sea? Do you suppose he would have embraced Moses as a son-in-law, with "timbrels and dances"?) It is only by the mercy of God that you breathe and eat, and that goes for every "Red," "Black," or "Pinko" on the face of this earth (Psa. 136:25). [No man can live without God's mercy]{.class_s4N4-0} (Acts 17:25), and no nation []{#c3ZZ.xhtml_page_770}can survive without God's mercy (Rom. 11:32), so "[his tender mercies are over all his works]{.class_s4N2-0}" (Psa. 145:9)

God is merciful to the just and the unjust (Matt. 5:45), so He will deal hardly with a man who shows no mercy (Matt. 18:33--34), for this is the FIRST and OUTSTANDING thing about God in His relationship to men. In the final analysis, it is [mercy ]{.class_s4N4-0}that keeps the unregenerate sinner out of Heaven, for Heaven would be worse than Hell to a man who trampled on God's mercy and rejected the love of One who loved that man enough to die in his place. ["It is of the LORD'S mercies that we are not consumed"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Lam. 3:22) says Jeremiah; and that saying holds true for every man, woman, and child who breathes on this earth. How the commentators could brush by this great central truth of the revelation of "[THE NAME OF THE LORD]{.class_s4N2-0}" with a bare mention of [Hebrew roots]{.class_s4N4-0} is beyond comprehension. A. W. Pink does considerably better than most of them here; but he stumbles with Gesenius, Kalisch, Keil, Dummelow, et al., on verse 7.

2\. ["Gracious"]{.class_s4N2-0}: The word goes beyond [mercy]{.class_s4N4-0}, which is negative in many cases (see Psa. 136:10--20). [Graciousness ]{.class_s4N4-0}grants privileges and favors that mercy alone would not allow. [Mercy ]{.class_s4N4-0}can spare the life of a man condemned to death, but where "graciousness" accompanies mercy, the criminal can be released or even---as the case of 2 Chronicles 28:11--15---be supplied with good things.

David was ["merciful" ]{.class_s4N2-0}to Saul (1 Sam. 24:10), but he was ["GRACIOUS"]{.class_s4N2-0} to Mephibosheth (2 Sam. 9:7). God was ["merciful" ]{.class_s4N2-0}to Lot (Gen. 19:29), but He was ["GRACIOUS"]{.class_s4N2-0} to Abraham (Gen. 18:17--19). There can be mercy without [grace]{.class_s4N4-0}, but no grace without [mercy]{.class_s4N4-0}.

3\. ["Longsuffering"]{.class_s4N2-0}: Of the attributes listed, this one sticks the most forcibly in Moses' mind, which is evident by his speech in Numbers 14:18. Burdened with 2,000,000 whining, complaining, grumbling church members (see Acts. 7:38), Moses knew by experience what a tremendous adjective this was---["longsuffering."]{.class_s4N2-0} Peter uses it to describe the years before the Flood (1 Pet. 3:20), when men tested God's patience to the breaking point. He also mentions it in connection with God's desire to save "[all men]{.class_s4N2-0}" (2 Pet. 3:9). Although the word (as [mercy]{.class_s4N4-0}) is a negative word, it is the word that has kept the unsheathed wrath of God from blasting the whole solar system into atoms (2 Pet. 3:10--13). God has put up with lying, stealing, killing, fornication, adultery, bestiality, perversion, murder, torture, embezzlement, fraud, rebellion, lust, cheating, and oppression for more than five millennia; and He has put up with it on a global scale with every kind of man, woman, boy, or girl of every color, age, race, or upbringing. If it were not His [longsuffering]{.class_s4N4-0} (Eccl. 8:11), there wouldn't be a human being alive on the face of this earth today. The eyes of Holy Righteousness have beheld the Gestapo torture chambers of Paris and Berlin; the holocausts of Treblinka, Auschwitz, and Buchenwald; the racks and pincers of the Spanish Inquisition; the funeral pyres of Smithfield, England; the slave camps of Siberia; the starving children of Africa and India; the drug traffic of France, Turkey, and Mexico; the Catholic terrorists of North Ireland; the lying Popes and Cardinals; the blasphemous rantings and ravings of Darwin, Dewey, and Marx; the inane drivel of Einstein, Huxley, Haeckel, and James; the monstrous perversions of the Holy Bible; the ghastly deaths of millions on the battlefields of Europe []{#c3ZZ.xhtml_page_772}and Asia; the lying fraud and deceit of international politics; and the screams and cries of ravished girls and grandmothers through 6,000 years of recorded history. If God were not ["longsuffering,"]{.class_s4N2-0} [you would not be looking at this print.]{.class_s4N4-0}

4\. ["Goodness and truth"]{.class_s4N2-0}: The personal attributes of the Lord Jesus Christ step out of the [Old]{.class_s4N4-0} [Testament ]{.class_s4N4-0}and demand our attention. We know of God's graciousness from Noah's case, we know of His mercy and longsuffering from Lot's case, but now ["goodness and truth"]{.class_s4N2-0} confront us. Eliezer (Gen. 24:27)---a type of the Holy Spirit---mentions this "[truth]{.class_s4N2-0}" in passing, and Jacob mentions it (Gen. 32:10), although neither one connects it with God as an [attribute]{.class_s4N4-0}. Exodus 18:9 mentions the "[goodness" ]{.class_s4N2-0}of the Lord, but only in the sense of what He had done for Israel. Now the words come out as a part of the proclamation of "[the name]{.class_s4N2-0}."

["Goodness"]{.class_s4N2-0} is one of the fruits of the Holy Spirit (Gal. 5:22), and the word is coupled (as here) with the word ["truth"]{.class_s4N2-0} in Ephesians 5:9. In both cases, the connection is with the Lord Jesus Christ, and this is emphasized in Ephesians 5:9 by the insertion of the word "[RIGHTEOUSNESS]{.class_s4N2-0}" between the two words. It is "[the goodness of God]{.class_s4N2-0}" that leads the unsaved man to repentance (Rom. 2:4); the word is the antonym of the word "[severity]{.class_s4N2-0}" (Rom. 11:22). No man is "good" unless he is a vessel in whom the Holy Spirit dwells (Rom. 3:10 cf. Acts 11:24).

["Truth,"]{.class_s4N2-0} again, is directly connected with the Lord Jesus Christ (John 14:6), and when Jesus used the word in John 8:32, He equated it with [Himself ]{.class_s4N4-0}(John 8:36). This "[truth]{.class_s4N2-0}" is so closely connected with the [written words of the Holy Bible]{.class_s4N4-0} that it is applied to []{#c3ZZ.xhtml_page_773}the Bible (John 17:17) as Christ applies it to Himself (cf. Psa. 138:2). (See detailed analysis under Prov. 30:5, 17--21 in the Commentary on Proverbs.) [Hatred for the Bible is symptomatic of a subconscious hatred for Jesus Christ,]{.class_s4N4-0} and no amount of blabbering about "Bibliolatry" will camouflage this God-defying sin. The scholars who mouth the longest and loudest about "godly translators" and the "verbally inspired originals" are usually the men who bare the most hatred for the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}text[,]{.class_s4N4-0} which the Holy Spirit elevated to the seat of absolute authority---without consulting the "godly" scholars about the matter.

5\. ["Forgiving iniquity\...transgression\...sin."]{.class_s4N2-0} The first word indicates perverseness of nature, the second a rebelliousness that seeks to overthrow law and order, while the third covers any "[unrighteousness]{.class_s4N2-0}" of any kind (1 John 5:17). Joseph forgave his brethren for their trespasses (Gen. 50:17); David forgave Abigail (1 Sam. 25:28); Christ's earthly ministry is filled with stories about forgiveness (Luke 7:42, 47; Matt. 18:21, etc.).

The word is kin to "[remission]{.class_s4N2-0}" in the New Testament, as we find it in John 20:23, and it includes the idea of [sending a thing away]{.class_s4N4-0} out of one's sight where it is not noticed, or [passing by a thing]{.class_s4N4-0} without noticing it. Hebrews 10:17 states the matter exactly, just preceding the word ["remission"]{.class_s4N2-0} in the next verse (Heb. 10:18). God freely forgave thousands of people in the Old Testament long before Jesus Christ showed up "in the flesh" (see Psa. 78:38; Num. 14:18--19; Psa. 25:18, 85:2, 99:8, etc.), but this was done on the basis of the "blood of bulls and goats" (Heb. 9:19). You see, the statement in Hebrews 10:4 said nothing at all about the "remission of []{#c3ZZ.xhtml_page_774}sins"; the plain statement was that "[the blood of bulls and of goats" ]{.class_s4N2-0}could not ["take away sins."]{.class_s4N2-0}

This explains the next clause in our text: ["and that will by no means clear the guilty"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 7). The Old Testament saints were [forgiven ]{.class_s4N4-0}without being "cleared." Their sins were overlooked without being [removed]{.class_s4N4-0}, and their sins were not REDEEMED until Christ showed up. Moreover, the unity of Scripture on this important subject is so preponderant that it is a miracle of miracles that every commentator but Bullinger missed the passage thumbs down.

A. ["For the redemption of the transgressions that were under the first testament"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Heb. 9:15) clearly tells you that although these transgressions were [forgiven]{.class_s4N4-0}, the transgressor had not yet been "cleared."

B. ["To declare his righteousness for the remission of sins that are past, through the forbearance of God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rom. 3:25) plainly shows you that God put up with sins in the Old Testament and "overlooked" them (Acts 14:16) on the grounds of Old Testament blood atonement; however, the "[propitiation]{.class_s4N2-0}" (Rom. 3:25) has to show up before the sins can be done away with (Col. 2:14) .

C. ["For this is my blood\...which is shed\... for the remission of sins"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Matt. 26:28). Now the water gets boiling. You see, the Conservatives ran this verse to Hebrews 10:4 and implied that no sins were "remitted" until Calvary; the Campbellite has capitalized on this error for 150 years by tying Matthew 26:28 with Acts 2:38 [("be baptized every one of you\...for the remission of sins\..."]{.class_s4N2-0}). Neither the Conservative nor Campbellite had any more idea of what he was talking about than the Associated Press giving a report on North Ireland.

["For the remission of sins,"]{.class_s4N2-0} in every passage where the expression occurs, [is a reference to the fact that God had already been forgiving sins for centuries before Christ showed up]{.class_s4N4-0} (cf. Mark 1:4). Being a just and holy God, He had to have a legal BASIS on which to forgive men, and that BASIS does not appear [in history]{.class_s4N4-0} until Matthew chapters 26--27 at the crucifixion of His Son (Isa. 53:3--9). Until that basis showed up, the temporary basis was ["the blood of bulls and of goats,"]{.class_s4N2-0} and this explains why the Old Testament saints had to go to an intermediate state ("Abraham's Bosom") at death instead of going directly (Phil. 1:23) into the presence of the Father (2 Cor. 5:1--8) .

Christ sheds His blood BECAUSE God had forgiven sins and had to have a basis on which to forgive them. Peter baptizes BECAUSE God had forgiven Israel (see Luke 23:34), and they are told to manifest their belief if they believe He has forgiven them (cf. Acts 3:19).

There isn't one case in either Testament, in any set of Greek, Latin, Hebrew, or Syriac manuscripts, where the expression ["for the remission of sins"]{.class_s4N2-0} ever meant "[in order]{.class_s4N4-0} to get your sins forgiven." The teaching of Alexander Campbell and company at this point (with the [Westminster Catechism, Episcopal Thirty-Nine Articles, Methodist Handbook,]{.class_s4N4-0} and [Roman Catholic Catechism]{.class_s4N4-0}) is as non-biblical and unscriptural as the charter of the United Nations. There is no case in either Testament where water baptism is said to have [any effect]{.class_s4N4-0} whatsoever on FORGIVENESS OF SINS. They were "forgiven" [before ]{.class_s4N4-0}they were baptized, or else they had no business in the water. Not even Ananias makes the mistake of telling Paul to get baptized "in order to be forgiven." He uses the term ["wash"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Acts 22:16) in []{#c3ZZ.xhtml_page_776}the ritual sense of [purification]{.class_s4N4-0}, and he is then speaking to a man who was saved, born again, and completely FORGIVEN [three days before he got into anybody's pool]{.class_s4N4-0} ( 1 Cor. 15:8 cf. 4:14).

An Old Testament saint was saved but not [eternally sanctified. ]{.class_s4N4-0}He was a saint without the [New Birth. ]{.class_s4N4-0}He was accepted by God on a "promissory" basis. He was forgiven but not [redeemed]{.class_s4N4-0}; he was passed over (12:13) without a complete [regeneration]{.class_s4N4-0}. Because of this, Jesus Christ had to show up and offer a perfect offering (Heb. 10:8--10); ["for the remission of sins"]{.class_s4N2-0} refers to this need every time the expression shows up. And the marvel of marvels is that all of this is starkly apparent and vividly clear to [any Christian]{.class_s4N4-0} without any reference to [any set]{.class_s4N4-0} of Hebrew or Greek manuscripts ever discovered [by anyone. ]{.class_s4N4-0}(So naturally, Keil, Kalisch, Delitzsch, Gesenius, Clarke, Ginsburg, Raschi, Ewald, Kennicott, Green, Dummelow, Williams, Schaff, DeRossi, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown missed it.)

The thing that threw the "majority of Conservative jackrabbits," this time, was the maddening insertion (in italics) of ["the guilty," ]{.class_s4N2-0}which was placed in the verse by the [King James]{.class_s4N4-0} translators. Now, scholastic tradition forbids the majority of Conservative backsliders from taking ANY italicized passages seriously, because it is taken for granted that God could not possibly have had anything to do with them since they were not in the "God-breathed original et ceteras." Rather than accept the italicized words (["the guilty"]{.class_s4N2-0}) as being a contribution to the revelation [which God Himself guided and allowed]{.class_s4N4-0} in the hands of the Reformation translators, the majority of Conservative jugheads simply [omitted the words]{.class_s4N4-0} and then tried to make sense out of the passage. []{#c3ZZ.xhtml_page_777}God rewarded their efforts to "preserve the original" by blinding them to the doctrinal implications of the passage. Moral: [where the "majority of Conservative scholars"]{.class_s4N4-0} (1880--2010)[ ignore the italics supplied by the AV translators, ignore them.]{.class_s4N4-0}

6\. ["Visiting the iniquity"]{.class_s4N2-0} (cf. 20:5). This retribution (which seems to contradict the statement of 2 Kings 14:6) is the same type of thing found in 20:2--5. It is dealing with the sin of [idolatry]{.class_s4N4-0}. This is manifest by the immediate reference to the matter in the next passage (see vss. 10--16), and it is this emphasis on the iniquity of idol worship that separates the Jew from all other people. The heathen at this time were ["winked at"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Acts 17:30) in regards to this particular sin. God never would wink at an [Israelite ]{.class_s4N4-0}(2 Kings 12:25--33) no matter who he was (1 Kings 13:1--6). (See comment on Matt. 1:23 in the [Commentary on Matthew]{.class_s4N4-0}.)

["And Moses made haste\...And he said"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 8--9). Moses' abrupt change of pace is apparent in every line of the text. The careful reader---who has regard for the world's greatest book (the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}1611)---will notice ["pardon our\...sin"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 9), which is quite a change from ["Ye have sinned a great sin"]{.class_s4N2-0} (32:30). ["This people have sinned a great sin"]{.class_s4N2-0} (32:31), and ["if thou wilt forgive their sin"]{.class_s4N2-0} (32:32). Moses has suddenly switched from second and third person plurals to the [first person]{.class_s4N4-0} plural. He is now accusing [himself!]{.class_s4N4-0}

But this is the logical consequence of a head-on collision with Deity. From the subject matter of the Bible, the stupidest Christian can see at a glance that Freud, Huxley, Haeckel, Darwin, Einstein, Weishaupt, Lenin, Marx, Jung, and Churchill never had [any ]{.class_s4N4-0}revelation from God. The outstanding mark of a man who has contacted []{#c3ZZ.xhtml_page_778}the Creator of the universe is a smitten conscience and a "guilt complex" that throws him to the ground on his face.

1\. This is what happened to Paul (Acts 9:4).

2\. This is what happened to Isaiah (Isa. 6:5).

3\. This is what happened to Job (Job 42:1--6).

4\. This is what happens to Moses (the text).

Peter cries out, ["Depart from me; for I am a sinful man, O ]{.class_s4N2-0}[L]{.class_s4N9-0}[ord"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Luke 5: 8), and David---who could give Richard the Lion-Hearted a run for his money anytime---became David the Chicken Hearted when the Angel of the Lord manifested Himself (1 Chron. 21:30). Men who talk blithely about "the Divine Architect" and "the Creator and Sustainer of the universe" and "the First Cause" and "the Energizing Presence," but never cry out, ["I have SINNED AGAINST HEAVEN\...make me as one of thy HIRED SERVANTS]{.class_s4N2-0}["]{.class_s4N4-0} (Luke 15:18--19), have no more encountered the One about whom they talk than a peacock has encountered a red snapper. The "guilt gomplex" always accompanies [salvation;]{.class_s4N4-0} it always accompanies the [New Birth;]{.class_s4N4-0} and it always---without fail---accompanies any [self-revelation of God]{.class_s4N4-0} to an individual (see 20:20). There may be phony "guilt complexes," and there may be false "convictions"; but no man who ever experienced the New Birth by the Holy Spirit of God got away from the Divine "Maternity Ward" without knowing that he was a bankrupt business man, a flunking student, a crippled athlete, a captured soldier, a moral leper, an unsuccessful farmer, a 4-F draftee, a religious wipe-out, and a shame and disgrace to his God and Creator. (If you have never seen that, friend, we Bible-believing people know one thing for certain about you---[whoever you are]{.class_s4N4-0} and whatever you do. We know that you are just one more self-righteous, unregenerate sinner, and the fires of ["the []{#c3ZZ.xhtml_page_779}second death"]{.class_s4N2-0} are already kindled in your bosom \[Rev. 20:14\].)

Even before Moses asked for forgiveness (vs. 9), he mentions GRACE: ["If now I have found grace in thy sight\...." ]{.class_s4N2-0}Now Moses has "come clean," as the expression goes. He is now praying like Daniel (Dan. 9:3--19). As a true deliverer, he has at last identified himself with his own people.

["And take us for thine inheritance"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 9). Up until here, the word ["inheritance" ]{.class_s4N2-0}has always been associated with inheriting LAND (Gen. 31:14, 48:6, 15:7--8, etc.)---not PEOPLE. In spite of this "first usage" of the word, none of the major scholars are able to open their mouths or dip their pens to exposit the expression. This in itself is peculiar, for the ["Law of First Mention"]{.class_s4N4-0} is given in every textbook on [hermeneutics ]{.class_s4N4-0}as one of the basic rules for interpreting Scripture. Now, how is it that men who taught the men who wrote the textbooks cannot apply their own rules? Again, we are at the place---it should look familiar to the reader by now(!)---where we would do well to ignore "the majority" of atheists, Liberals, agnostics, Neo-evangelicals, and "Modernists."

1\. The Lord Himself was LEVI'S inheritance (Deut. 10:9).

2\. The land of Palestine is ISRAEL'S inheritance (Deut. 12:9, 20:16).

3\. The Millennial reign will be the CHRISTIAN'S inheritance (Col. 3:24).

4\. Israel (Jacob) is the LORD'S inheritance (Deut. 32:9; Psa. 78:71; Isa. 19:25; Jer. 10:16, 51:19; Ezek. 48:29).

Into these four main brackets, the Holy Spirit has placed---in the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}text---several other details. []{#c3ZZ.xhtml_page_780}For example, ["JUDAH"]{.class_s4N2-0} is the Lord's inheritance in Zechariah 2:12 at the Second Advent (cf. Isa. 65:9). Again, the Millennial reign is also [Israel's inheritance,]{.class_s4N4-0} as Israel is restored [before the Advent]{.class_s4N4-0} (see Ezek. 47:14; Psa. 105:11; Isa. 54:3, etc.). This explains why the Christian is a "JOINT heir" (Rom. 8:17), and it also explains why the "near kinsman" of Ruth 4:6 could not redeem Ruth (a type of the Body of Christ), because he would have "marred" [his own inheritance,]{.class_s4N4-0} which, in type, was ISRAEL. Observe how the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}text of the Holy Bible (1611) lays these two great bodies of saints alongside each other with similitudes and contrasts that are beyond explaining aside from a supernatural Author.

1\. The Church and Israel are both addressed as ["sons" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(John 1:12--13; Isa. 60:9), yet Israel is called a SON in the singular as a corporate body (4:22). Nowhere in the Old Testament is it ever said of any member of this [corporate body]{.class_s4N4-0} that the [individual ]{.class_s4N4-0}was "born again" or became a "son" through BIRTH. [This is only true of the Body of Christ]{.class_s4N4-0} (Rom. 8:1--15).

2\. The Church and Israel are both BRIDES (Rev. 21:2; Eph. 5:24--32; Hos. 2:19--20; Jer. 3:14), but one bride is ["a CHASTE VIRGIN" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(2 Cor. 11:2--3; Song of Sol. 6:9), and the other is an [adulterous wife]{.class_s4N4-0} who has been restored(!) (Hos. 1--3).

3\. The Church and Israel are both addressed as a [SINGLE MAN,]{.class_s4N4-0} although both are treated as BRIDES (Hos. 6:4, 11:1, 12:2; Amos 7:2--5; Mic. 1:5, 3:8; Eph. 4:13).
