4\. The Church and Israel both have one title in common[---"an holy nation"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 Pet. 2:9; Exod. 19:6), and this fixes the real meaning of the ["inheritance,"]{.class_s4N2-0} for []{#c3ZZ.xhtml_page_781}the passage on the nations in Deuteronomy 32:43 defines what Moses requested.

Deuteronomy 32:8--9 states that God took Israel as an[ "inheritance"]{.class_s4N2-0} in the sense that He selected that one nation out of the other twelve nations on this earth to be His own people. ["When the Most High divided to the nations their inheritance\...he set the bounds of the people\...Jacob is the lot of his inheritance."]{.class_s4N2-0}

Furthermore, in the "unsearchable riches" of the [King James]{.class_s4N4-0} text, we notice that this adoption did NOT take place until after the events of chapters 32--34, for the next verse in Deuteronomy 32 (Deut. 32:10) states that God did NOT find Jacob in Egypt (!) when he was called His ["firstborn"]{.class_s4N2-0} (4:22). God ["found him in a desert land and in the waste howling wilderness."]{.class_s4N2-0} Israel was not taken to be God's ["inheritance"]{.class_s4N2-0} until Moses himself places the petition in verse 9.

Lessons like these should be tucked away deep in the heart of the Bible-believing Christian, and he should reinforce himself again and again with the truth of the [absolute certainty of the words of the Scripture]{.class_s4N4-0} as they are found in the [Authorized Version]{.class_s4N4-0} of the Reformation. Let him never forget for a minute that every Conservative scholar who altered words (or even "a word") in the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}text by appealing to Hebrew and Greek "originals" was unable to follow his own rules of exegesis in a showdown. Go by THE Book and let the scholars stew in their own juices. You see, Israel was NOT taken for an inheritance in 20:21 because the scholars forgot God's comment at that time---found only in [Deuteronomy 5:24--29.]{.class_s4N4-0}

In 19:5--6, God merely laid down the [conditions]{.class_s4N4-0} for taking Israel as His own ["people."]{.class_s4N2-0} In chapter 34, []{#c3ZZ.xhtml_page_782}the Lord answers Moses' prayer, and even though the conditions of chapter 19 are repeated (see vss. 12--15), this time, an absolute affirmation is made that God WILL work with Israel in a covenant relationship.

34:10 "And he said, Behold, I make a covenant: before all thy people I will do marvels, such as have not been done in all the earth, nor in any nation: and all the people among which thou art shall see the work of the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}: for it is a terrible thing that I will do with thee.

11 Observe thou that which I command thee this day: behold, I drive out before thee the Amorite, and the Canaanite, and the Hittite, and the Perizzite, and the Hivite, and the Jebusite.

12 Take heed to thyself, lest thou make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land whither thou goest, lest it be for a snare in the midst of thee:

13 But ye shall destroy their altars, break their images, and cut down their groves:

14 For thou shalt worship no other god: for the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}, whose name is Jealous, is a jealous God:

15 Lest thou make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land, and they go a whoring after their gods, and do sacrifice unto their gods, and one call thee, and thou eat of his sacrifice;

16 And thou take of their daughters unto thy sons, and their daughters go a whoring after their gods, and make thy sons go a whoring after their gods.

17 Thou shalt make thee no molten gods."

Here, the Palestinian covenant given to Abraham (Gen. 15:18--21) is confirmed after an intermission of four centuries (Gen. 15:13). Notice, in particular, the national reference: ["nor in ANY NATION"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 10), []{#c3ZZ.xhtml_page_783}hearkening back to the remarks we have just made under the first occurrence of the word ["inheritance."]{.class_s4N2-0} The ["terrible thing"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 10) which God intends to do with the nation of Israel is described in what follows, and no amount of etymological juggling, modernistic soft-soaping, or historical theorizing can lessen the impact of the ["terrible thing."]{.class_s4N2-0} It is [negative ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [destructive ]{.class_s4N4-0}from start to finish.

1\. ["Behold, I drive out"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 11).

2\. ["Ye shall destroy their altars"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 13).

3\. ["Break their images"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 13).

4\. ["Cut down their groves"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 13).

5\. ["Lest thou make a covenant"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 15).

Additional information on this covenant is even more terrifying. ["Cast out many nations before thee\...thou shalt smite them, and utterly destroy them; thou shalt make no covenant with them, nor shew mercy unto them\...ye shall destroy their altars\...burn their graven images with fire"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Deut. 7:1--5). ["Ye shall utterly destroy all the places\...and break their pillars\...and destroy the names of them out of that place"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Deut. 12:1--3).

Now this is the kind of thing that sets the teeth of the Modernist or "Liberal" on edge. Strangely enough, most Modernists or Liberals are [international Socialists,]{.class_s4N4-0} and international Socialists have never failed to ["utterly destroy"]{.class_s4N2-0} every Bible-believing Christian on whom they could get their hands once they controlled a country (Romania, Yugoslavia, Russia, Red China, and Hungary, for example). That is, the usual rule is: those who abhor the "God of the Old Testament," who was "vicious" and "cruel" as a "tribal deity," and prefer rather the "lowly Nazarene" or the "man of Galilee," are the []{#c3ZZ.xhtml_page_784}very first ones to harass Fundamental radio broadcasts, sue independent churches, revise the Bible, stop tract distribution, object to street preaching, tax Bible Institutes, arrest evangelists, and take sides with the most brutal, depraved political systems of this century: Roman Catholicism and Soviet Communism. The "wolf in sheep's clothing" is still a WOLF.

Now this paradox is not without a proper explanation. You see, "the flesh" naturally rebels against [any ]{.class_s4N4-0}form of self-denial or self-crucifixion. "The flesh" in Eugene Carson Blake, Pope Paul VI, Eleanor Roosevelt, Bishop Pike, A. J. Cronin, Mick Jaggers, Fulton Oursler, Kahlil Gibran, Alice Cassidy, Rod Kuenen, Ricky Nelson, the Apostle Paul, St. Francis, Johnny Carson, and Toyohiko Kagawa is no different than "the flesh" in Charles Starkweather, Baby Face Nelson, Bruno Hauptmann, Dutch Schultz, John Dillinger, Lee Harvey Oswald, Count Dracula, or Charles Manson. When any directions in the Bible are given for the destruction or elimination of fleshy things (whether they be pleasures, sports, books, family affections, popularity, or RELIGION), the unregenerate flesh of the unsaved man rises in open rebellion and cries: ["My punishment is greater than I can bear"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Gen. 4:13). All unsaved theologians resent Samuel telling Saul to ["utterly destroy"]{.class_s4N2-0} (1 Sam. 15:3). All unsaved theologians wish for[ "Ishmael"]{.class_s4N2-0} to ["live before thee"]{.class_s4N2-0} (see the [Commentary on Genesis,]{.class_s4N4-0} Gen. 17:18).

The most unpopular part of the New Testament for a Liberal---outside of the passages on the Second Coming and Hell---is Romans chapters 6 and 7 along with Galatians chapters 5 and 6. The subject matter of these four chapters is the death and crucifixion of fleshy "lusts, []{#c3ZZ.xhtml_page_785}affections, desires, motions, and motives." Penance and self-inflicted suffering are NOT the subjects under discussion, so don't pay a bit of attention to some Liberal idiot who tries to make a "flagellant" out of Paul. The "flagellants" (A.D. 800--1400) were all members of the [Roman Catholic]{.class_s4N4-0} communion; Paul was a [Bible-believing,]{.class_s4N4-0} Jewish Christian.

1\. ["Destroy"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 13) old books (Acts 19:19), old magazines (Eph. 5:13), old pictures (Num. 33:52!), and old phonograph records (Col. 3:16).

2\. ["Break"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 13) old fellowships (Eph. 5:11), old yokes (2 Cor. 6:14), and old habits (Eph. 4:25--32).

3\. ["Cut down"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 13) idols (2 Cor. 10:5), on smoking and drinking (2 Cor. 7:1), and the sacred cows of science and education (Col. 2:8).

By contrast with the Old Testament combat, Ephesians 6:10--18 is a [spiritual ]{.class_s4N4-0}combat with [spiritual ]{.class_s4N4-0}weapons (2 Cor. 10:4), but the typology (1 Cor. 10:6--13) is beautiful. The Hamites of verse 11, as the Amalekites of 17:8--16 (which see), represent the believer's warfare against ["the god]{.class_s4N2-0} \[and the gods\] [of this world"]{.class_s4N2-0} (2 Cor. 4:4 cf. 1 Cor. 8:5), so the commandment is ["destroy\...break\...cut down"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 13). The worldly Christian cannot tolerate this militant aggressive attack on the world system in which he has "settled down," and therefore, he will side with the Liberal and the Modernist every time [where NEGATIVE preaching is demanded]{.class_s4N4-0} (2 Tim. 4:2--5). That is, the average Christian in America today (Fundamentalists included) is LIBERAL in his approach to [applied Christianity,]{.class_s4N4-0} although he is Fundamental in his profession of faith where it deals with basic doctrines.

The warnings of verses 16--17 are as "relative" to the history of Israel as the warnings Jesus Christ gave in Matthew chapter 24 and Mark chapter 13. The book of Judges and the books of 1 & 2 Kings show exactly what happened when these warnings were ignored (but there is no time to comment on the matters here). The greatest comments on verses 13--16 are found in Judges 2:17; Numbers 25:2; Psalm 106:28; 1 Kings 11:2; Ezra 9:2; and Nehemiah 13:25--26. The thing that is demanded by God is SEPARATION and NONCONFORMITY (see Rom. 12:1--2), and these two things seem to be the hardest demands that are made on the Twentieth-Century Christian by the "God of the NEW Testament".

All Popes (A.D. 550--2010) ["make a covenant]{.class_s4N2-0} [with the inhabitants of the land"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 15), and these "concordats" are the "hire of the whore" (Rev. 17:1--5) for committing ["fornication"]{.class_s4N2-0} with ["the kings of the earth"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Rev. 17:2). The Catholic Church has [always ]{.class_s4N4-0}compromised with political leaders to gain control, and if you are waiting for the Pope to make one move to stop any of his vassals from overthrowing the government of North Ireland, you are going to wait until ["heaven and earth\...pass away" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(Matt. 24:35) and ["the elements melt with a fervent heat"]{.class_s4N2-0} (2 Pet. 3:12). The Latin church, originating in North Africa (Carthage and Alexandria), is the perfect counterpart for the Hamite population mentioned in our text (vs. 11), and the universal testimony of history is that where Rome succeeds in making ["a covenant with the inhabitants of the land"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Philippines, Spain, Mexico, Italy, South America, etc.), the pagan practices continue without interruption; they only change in nomenclature and []{#c3ZZ.xhtml_page_787}setting (see the [Commentary on Revelation,]{.class_s4N4-0} comments on Rev. 17 and 2:12--15).

["I will do marvels"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 10), and so they are done:

a\. The miraculous drying up of the River Jordan (Josh. 3:15--17).

b\. The complete disintegration of the walls of Jericho (Josh. 6:20).

c\. The supernatural plague of hailstones (Josh. 10:11).

d\. Joshua's "Long Day" (Josh. 10:13).

["Go a whoring after their gods"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 16) is described in Numbers 25:1--2, which see. Students of ancient history are well aware of the immoral and licentious character of the Assyrian and Babylonian religions that passed their rituals on to Phoenicia, Greece, and Rome. ["The hire of a whore"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Deut. 23:18) was always considered good for a "vow in the temple," and the whore was hired for that purpose---to raise money for the "building fund" and the "expansion of the new facilities" to "meet the need of today's youth, etc." The vestal virgins (?) usually fulfilled this function, but to satisfy the depraved appetites of the "social [misfits]{.class_s4N4-0}" (sex perverts), homosexuals and sodomites were hired (see 1 Kings 14:24) to make the service "meaningfully relevant" to "contemporary needs," etc. That is, the ["sacrifice"]{.class_s4N2-0} was accompanied by sex orgies (see vss. 15--16). This will be the coming style of "worship service" in the future preceding the advent of the Man of Sin---the Son of Perdition (2 Thess. 2:3). The ["virgins"]{.class_s4N2-0} of Revelation 14:4 have to be [physical virgins]{.class_s4N4-0} as well as [spiritual virgins,]{.class_s4N4-0} for the "worship service" of the Tribulation will incorporate fornication and sex perversion as part of "man's freedom of conscience to express his divinely given [creative ]{.class_s4N4-0}powers and God-given [sexuality ]{.class_s4N4-0}in an []{#c3ZZ.xhtml_page_788}adoration of life," etc., etc. (Oh, yes, [education ]{.class_s4N4-0}can equip you to word it any way you jolly well please.)

The ["groves"]{.class_s4N2-0} of verse 13 are groves of living trees, in spite of the continual (and violent) effort of all of the "Conservative scholars" to prove that they were totem poles (see any commentary written by anybody). Occasionally, a commentator will "flub the dub" and tell the truth about it (see Dummelow's slip on pg. 83 and the [Wycliffe]{.class_s4N4-0} commentator's slip on pg. 84), but still they prefer changing the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}text to leaving it the way it is.

["For the ]{.class_s4N2-0}[Lord]{.class_s4N9-0}[, whose name is Jealous, is a jealous God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 14). Again, the Lord has ruined His testimony with the National Council of Churches, the NAACP, the HEW, and the Phi Beta Kappa Society. A supreme God is not allowed to have such petty feelings as "jealousy" in the "theology" of scientists and educators. He is supposed to be able to put up with anything (and anybody) on no other basis than He is just too "big hearted" to hold a grudge. This great new Santa Claus god must kiss the brow of the infuriated sinner and murmur, ["Blessed are the poor in spirit"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Matt. 5:3), or embrace the writhing soul of a blaspheming atheist and say,[ "Suffer the little children to come unto me" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(Mark 10:4). He is sort of a moral leper who looks at sin, suffering, lying, shame, stealing, filth, purity, obscenity, chastity, vulgarity, truth, falsehood, honor, murder, integrity, and perversity with the same degree of tolerance and would no more think of damning a soul to Hell than Jussi Bjoerling would think of singing with the "Rolling Stones."

But the testimony of the saints, prophets, apostles, martyrs, and BOTH TESTAMENTS is a different record.

1\. James says the Holy Spirit resents competition from the world and the flesh (James 4:5).

2\. Paul says the Holy Spirit doesn't want anybody messing with His "VESSEL" (1 Cor. 3:17).

3\. Paul says that ["godly jealousy"]{.class_s4N2-0} is a manifestation of love (2 Cor. 11:2), and every Liberal professes to believe that[ "GOD IS LOVE"]{.class_s4N2-0} (see 1 John 4:8)!

4\. Moses says that God would be moved to jealousy by His own people (Deut. 32:21--42), and as surely as a husband has a right to resent another man messing with his wife (Hos. 1--3), God had a right to resent the world messing with His ["beloved"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Hos. 3:1). If you [belong ]{.class_s4N4-0}to somebody (1 Cor. 6:20) and they belong to [you ]{.class_s4N4-0}(Eph. 5:24--32), can you honestly say that feelings of jealousy are not justified where someone else enters to take one of you from the other? (Read Num. 5:14--30.)

What we are saying amounts to this: if God is God, then such things as love, jealousy, hatred, sorrow, grief, etc., are going to be magnified a million times in His attributes. They are not going to be eradicated simply because He is not HUMAN. The human being got these attributes from God, according to the Bible, and in the Creator, they are magnified and revealed to their fullest extent.

1\. [The Lord hates evil. ]{.class_s4N4-0}(This means that it affects His holiness---see Gethsemane---in a way that you could never possibly grasp. Christ's grief over Jerusalem in A.D. 33 \[Matt. 23:37\] was as great as God's grief over Jerusalem in A.D. 600 \[Jer. 9:1--2\], and it is instructive to note that the only men God said he would spare in Jerusalem when the killing started \[Ezek. 9:4\] were the men who cried and sighed ["for all the abominations\...thereof"]{.class_s4N2-0}!)

2\. The Lord is [jealous]{.class_s4N4-0} over rivals who seek to steal His people, His Book, or His nation from Him. (This means that God will punish His people or His nation when they "flirt" with the rivals \[1 Cor. 3:17; Judg. 1--10\], and God will leave a nation in dirt, superstition, and poverty that does away with His Book. Our God ["is a jealous God."]{.class_s4N2-0} And if YOUR God doesn't mind you making passes at the world, the flesh, the Devil, and infidels, you don't have a "God" with enough sense to grow okra, let alone create a universe.)

["Thou shalt make thee no molten gods"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 17). This is the final checkout for Aaron. The gods that are ["molten"]{.class_s4N2-0} today out of molten metal are, of course, stoves, cars, refrigerators, planes, boats, etc. This way no one recognizes "the Golden Calf" until he has accomplished his purpose.

34:18 "The feast of unleavened bread shalt thou keep. Seven days thou shalt eat unleavened bread, as I commanded thee, in the time of the month Abib: for in the month Abib thou camest out from Egypt.

19 All that openeth the matrix is mine; and every firstling among thy cattle, whether ox or sheep, that is male.

20 But the firstling of an ass thou shalt redeem with a lamb: and if thou redeem him not, then shalt thou break his neck. All the firstborn of thy sons thou shalt redeem. And none shall appear before me empty.

21 Six days thou shalt work, but on the seventh day thou shalt rest: in earing time and in harvest thou shalt rest.

22 And thou shalt observe the feast of weeks, of the firstfruits of wheat harvest, and the feast of ingathering at the year's end."

The first of the three feasts, which mark the three raptures of the first resurrection (see Deut. 16:16), is given in verse 18, and the other two appear in verse 22. The ["thrice in the year"]{.class_s4N2-0} mentioned in verse 23 refers to these three feasts which match:

1\. [The rapture of the Old Testament saints]{.class_s4N4-0} following the Feast of Unleavened Bread (Matt. 27:50--53; Eph. 4:8--10).

2\. [The rapture of the Body of Christ,]{.class_s4N4-0} which is likened to Pentecost (["the feast of weeks"]{.class_s4N2-0}), showing that the Body may leave on the day on which it began (cf. Song of Sol. 2:8--13).

3\. [The rapture of Tribulation saints]{.class_s4N4-0} at the Feast of Tabernacles, or shortly before it (["the feast of ingathering at the year's end,"]{.class_s4N2-0} vs. 22).

(We have commented on vss. 19--20 at length under 13:12--13, which see.)

The shadow of the Lord Jesus Christ is thrown across the passage, and we see the outline of His person at every turn. He was ["the bread of God\...from heaven"]{.class_s4N2-0} (John 6:33), completely unleavened in nature (1 Cor. 5:6--7), and He was slain ["in the month Abib"]{.class_s4N2-0} (see vs. 18). Furthermore, he was the ["firstling"]{.class_s4N2-0} of verse 19 (Acts 4:27, 30---altered in the [New American Standard "Bible,"]{.class_s4N4-0} recommended in [The Sword of the Lord]{.class_s4N4-0}), and since He was ["a lamb"]{.class_s4N2-0} (John 1:29), He was born in a manger with the ["cattle"]{.class_s4N2-0} and the ["ox"]{.class_s4N2-0} of verse 19. Again, He was ["the firstborn"]{.class_s4N2-0} of verse 20 (cf. Col. 1:15), and He was the Redeemer who caused all of His []{#c3ZZ.xhtml_page_792}sons (Heb. 2:10) to bring forth fruit unto God (John 15:1--5).

We have commented on verse 21 under 20:8--10.

["And none shall appear before me empty"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 20). The statement is repeated from 23:15 and indicates that when the male representatives of the populace come to the central place of worship (see 20:24 and 23:17), they are to bring offerings of grain, cattle, etc.

34:23 "Thrice in the year shall all your men children appear before the Lord [God]{.class_s4N8-0}, the God of Israel.

24 For I will cast out the nations before thee, and enlarge thy borders: neither shall any man desire thy land, when thou shalt go up to appear before the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}thy God thrice in the year.

25 Thou shalt not offer the blood of my sacrifice with leaven; neither shall the sacrifice of the feast of the passover be left unto the morning.

26 The first of the firstfruits of thy land thou shalt bring unto the house of the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}thy God. Thou shalt not seethe a kid in his mother's milk.

27 And the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}said unto Moses, Write thou these words: for after the tenor of these words I have made a covenant with thee and with Israel.

28 And he was there with the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}forty days and forty nights; he did neither eat bread, nor drink water. And he wrote upon the tables the words of the covenant, the ten commandments."

For the feasts observed in verse 23, see verse 22 and comments.

Verse 24 is self-explanatory and simply means that during these three feasts, when the male populace has gathered at Jerusalem, the Lord will hold back enemy []{#c3ZZ.xhtml_page_793}nations from coming in and ravaging the land in their absence. Proverbs 16:7 is an excellent comment on the passage. Verse 25 has been commented on under 23:18 and 12:10. Verse 26 is a repetition of 23:19, which see.

["Write thou these words\...I have made a covenant with thee and with Israel"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 27). The ["words"]{.class_s4N2-0} are those from verse 10 to the present text. They constitute a national covenant with Israel (as Israel is related to the land of Palestine); the boundaries have been set already in Genesis 15:18. Moses is plainly the "clincher" in this covenant (as we remarked under vs. 9), for the Lord has added: ["I have made a covenant with thee and with Israel." "After the tenor"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 27) is like saying "according to the gist of" or "according to the sound of" or "according to the way I just said these words."

The ["he"]{.class_s4N2-0} of ["And he wrote upon the tables"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 28) is a reference to the Lord---not Moses.

34:29 "And it came to pass, when Moses came down from mount Sinai with the two tables of testimony in Moses' hand, when he came down from the mount, that Moses wist not that the skin of his face shone while he talked with him.

30 And when Aaron and all the children of Israel saw Moses, behold, the skin of his face shone; and they were afraid to come nigh him.

31 And Moses called unto them; and Aaron and all the rulers of the congregation returned unto him: and Moses talked with them.

32 And afterward all the children of Israel came nigh: and he gave them in commandment all that the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}had spoken with him in mount Sinai.

33 And till Moses had done speaking with them, he put a vail on his face.

34 But when Moses went in before the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}to speak with him, he took the vail off, until he came out. And he came out, and spake unto the children of Israel that which he was commanded.

35 And the children of Israel saw the face of Moses, that the skin of Moses' face shone: and Moses put the vail upon his face again, until he went in to speak with him."

The ["wist"]{.class_s4N2-0} of verse 29 is the archaic English from the Germanic root---[wissen, weiss,]{.class_s4N4-0} etc., meaning; "to know" (cf. ["wot"]{.class_s4N2-0} in 32:1).

["The skin of his face shone while he talked with him"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 29). This is the genuine account of the "halo" or ["nimbus"]{.class_s4N4-0} which the ancient artists always placed around the head of a god or goddess. It is like electrical impulses radiating out from the head and naturally can be counterfeited by the "[angel of light"]{.class_s4N2-0} (2 Cor. 11:13--14), whose main job is ["imitating"]{.class_s4N4-0} Christ, according to the mistranslation of that word in the [New American Standard "Bible"]{.class_s4N4-0} of Bob Jones University and the [Revised Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} of the ecumenical council of Rome (l Cor. 11:1).

["The skin of his face shone"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 30). The phenomenon matches the shining face of the transfigured Christ of Matthew 17:1--2, and the shining face of the martyr Stephen as it disappeared in a hail of rocks (Acts 6:15 cf. 7:55--56). The New Testament uses this phenomenon as an illustration of the difference between the Law and Gospel (2 Cor. 3:1--15). It clearly indicates that the INSIDE of the Christian "vessel" has been []{#c3ZZ.xhtml_page_795}"turned on" (2 Cor. 4:6--7) by the same light that lit up the face of Moses (2 Cor. 3:7). ["THE FACE OF JESUS CHRIST"]{.class_s4N2-0} (2 Cor. 4:6) reflects God's glory (Heb. 1:1--3), and we behold this glory ["with open face"]{.class_s4N2-0} (2 Cor. 3:18).

["The ministration"]{.class_s4N2-0} of the Holy Spirit (2 Cor. 3:8) in the heart of the believer takes the inner man ["from glory to glory"]{.class_s4N2-0} (2 Cor. 3:18), and this explains why many unsaved people never look a Christian straight in the eye when they are talking with him (see 2 Cor. 3:7). (Christians who are experienced in personal work know the meaning of this statement far beyond the comprehension of the type of men who make Bible "revisions" and sit around tearing up the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}with a lexicon. There is something about the FACE of a born-again man, who has been "wallowing in the Bible," that deeply disturbs an unsaved man and often mildly upsets a dead-orthodox "scholar." Some of ["the glory of the Lord"]{.class_s4N2-0} \[2 Cor. 3:18\] has rubbed off.)

It is [oil,]{.class_s4N4-0} a picture of the Holy Spirit,[ ]{.class_s4N4-0}that causes a man's face to SHINE (Psa. 104:15), according to David. Where the Holy Spirit is having His way in the heart and life of the believer, there is an ["epistle\...known and read of all men"]{.class_s4N2-0} (2 Cor. 3:2), whether they ever open the covers of the Holy Spirit's Book or not.

Verses 30--32 are self-explanatory.

The reader will observe that a tabernacle is present (vs. 34) which is NOT the Tabernacle we have been studying in chapters 25, 26, 27, 28, 29, 30, and 31. ["But when Moses went in before the LORD\...until he came out "]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 34).

Now, there are several interesting things about ["the vail."]{.class_s4N2-0} Since one veil is said to be a type of Christ's FLESH (Heb. 10:20), and this flesh was transfigured []{#c3ZZ.xhtml_page_796}with Moses present (Matt. 17:1--3), we should expect a flurry among the faculty members at Dallas, BIOLA, Columbia, Hyles-Anderson, Moody, Southwestern, Bob Jones University, Tennessee Temple, Northwestern, and other "orthodox" institutions as they try to insert some gnostic depravation into the passage that would please Origen, Philo, Theodotian, Pantaenus, Valentinus, Clement, and the faculty of the University of Alexandria (A.D. 40--200). The insertion chosen (in desperation) is the fantastic reading that Moses put the veil on his face [when he had FINISHED speaking with the children of Israel. ]{.class_s4N4-0}This ghastly blunder is found in the [ASV]{.class_s4N4-0} and [NASV,]{.class_s4N4-0} recommended by Bob Jones III, Archer Weniger, John R. Rice, Wilbur Smith, William Culbertson, and the Lockman Foundation. The reading is naturally taken from the God-forsaken [LXX ]{.class_s4N4-0}([Septuagint]{.class_s4N4-0}), which Origen and his followers constructed many years after the death of Christ.

The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0}---trying to justify this colossal boner---has said that "when Moses had [done speaking]{.class_s4N4-0}\...he put a veil on his face" agrees "with the sense of verses 34 and 35." However, any fool could see, at one look, that verses 34 and 35 were talking about the only time Moses did NOT wear the veil. Furthermore, every Hebrew manuscript extant says in verse 35 that Moses ["put the vail upon his face again, UNTIL he went in to speak with him"]{.class_s4N2-0}! The ridiculous [New American Standard "Bible"]{.class_s4N4-0} (1963), along with the [RSV, RV]{.class_s4N4-0}, and other unholy corruptions, has simply called the Holy Spirit a liar. According to the [ASV]{.class_s4N4-0}, recommended by 98% of the "Fundamental" scholars, [Moses took his veil off while he was preaching]{.class_s4N4-0}---BEFORE he got back into the tabernacle ["to speak with him."]{.class_s4N2-0} Albeit, the []{#c3ZZ.xhtml_page_797}God-honored text---in any edition of any set of Hebrew manuscripts---says ["UNTIL he went in to speak with him."]{.class_s4N2-0}

Moral: where 98% of the "born again," saved, soulwinning, "Premillennial," Fundamental scholars recommend the [NASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}over the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}1611, [disregard their scholarship as quickly as you would drop a hot poker.]{.class_s4N4-0}

In a desperate effort to stop the fanaticism of these "Conservative" crackpots, the Greek New Testament---any edition---has told us that the veil over the face was a type of what took place ["in the reading of the old testament"]{.class_s4N2-0} (2 Cor. 3:14). Yet these deluded nuts would have us think that when Moses was READING the words God gave him (vs. 32), [he had the veil OFF!]{.class_s4N4-0}

As we have said on a hundred occasions before, this is very typical of "the majority of Conservative loggerheads" who make their living attacking the text of the [Authorized Version. ]{.class_s4N4-0}You are a blank fool if you take their scholarship seriously, and you are a double blank fool if you waste any money trying to get any wisdom from them. [They are bankrupt.]{.class_s4N4-0}

1\. A veil pictures Christ's flesh (Heb. 10:20).

2\. A veil pictures the clothing of the universe which God made (Heb. 1:10--12).

3\. When Christ dies and arises, the glory can be manifest because we no longer know Him after the FLESH (2 Cor. 5:16).

4\. When the veil is [removed, ]{.class_s4N4-0}the glory is manifest (2 Cor. 3:14--16).

5\. When the veil of the [universe ]{.class_s4N4-0}is removed (Rev. 20:11), God's naked glory will be revealed (Prov. 20:8).

Exactly as the Purple Curtain and the Iron Curtain and the Bamboo Curtain hide the machinations and workings of Catholicism, Socialism, and Communism, so the curtain of the universe hides the glory of God from view (Job 26:9), but when that curtain is "folded up like a garment and changed like a vesture," [then ]{.class_s4N4-0}you'll see something.

Moses is taking off the veil as he enters the Tabernacle, and he is putting it on after walking from the tabernacle (33:7) back to the camp. These are the times that the children of Israel observe the ["nimbus";]{.class_s4N4-0} and the fantastic translation recommended by Dallas, Moody, Columbia, Fuller, Biola, Trinity, Clearwater, and other "Conservative schools" to the effect that Moses had the veil off when he spoke to Israel can only be classified with such irrational nonsense as [Good News,]{.class_s4N4-0} the [New English Bible,]{.class_s4N4-0} [My Daily Missal,]{.class_s4N4-0} [The Big Fisherman, ]{.class_s4N4-0}the [New American Standard "Bible,"]{.class_s4N4-0} "The Robe," and Berkhof's [Systematic Theology.]{.class_s4N4-0}

[]{#c44Y.xhtml}

# Chapter 35 {.heading_s4G-0}

35:1 "And Moses gathered all the congregation of the children of Israel together, and said unto them, These are the words which the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}hath commanded, that ye should do them.

2 Six days shall work be done, but on the seventh day there shall be to you an holy day, a sabbath of rest to the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}: whosoever doeth work therein shall be put to death.

3 Ye shall kindle no fire throughout your habitations upon the sabbath day.

4 And Moses spake unto all the congregation of the children of Israel, saying, This is the thing which the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}commanded, saying,

5 Take ye from among you an offering unto the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}: whosoever is of a willing heart, let him bring it, an offering of the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}; gold, and silver, and brass,

6 And blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine linen, and goats' hair,

7 And rams' skins dyed red, and badgers' skins, and shittim wood,

8 And oil for the light, and spices for anointing oil, and for the sweet incense,

9 And onyx stones, and stones to be set for the ephod, and for the breastplate.

10 And every wise hearted among you shall come, and make all that the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}hath commanded;

11 The tabernacle, his tent, and his covering, his taches, and his boards, his bars, his pillars, and his sockets,

12 The ark, and the staves thereof, with the mercy seat, and the vail of the covering,

13 The table, and his staves, and all his vessels, and the shewbread,

14 The candlestick also for the light, and his furniture, and his lamps, with the oil for the light,

15 And the incense altar, and his staves, and the anointing oil, and the sweet incense, and the hanging for the door at the entering in of the tabernacle,

16 The altar of burnt offering, with his brasen grate, his staves, and all his vessels, the laver and his foot,

17 The hangings of the court, his pillars, and their sockets, and the hanging for the door of the court,

18 The pins of the tabernacle, and the pins of the court, and their cords.

19 The cloths of service, to do service in the holy place, the holy garments for Aaron the priest, and the garments of his sons, to minister in the priest's office."

We have printed the passage at length since very little comment will need to be made until the last chapter of Exodus. Everything in chapters 35, 36, 37, 38, and 39 has been covered in our study of chapters 25--31. We shall make comment on the few passages that vary from the former account, but chapter 34 is an excellent place to stop the book of Exodus for NEW material, because the newest thing God ever revealed in the way of REDEMPTION (and that is what the book of Exodus is about!) was the risen, transfigured Redeemer so vividly described in the last chapter. His incarnation is hinted at in 33:23; His earthly ministry is indicated in 33:11; His attributes are listed in 33:19; His name is found in 33:21. The consecration of His disciples is found in 34:2, their service in 34:25, and their growth in grace in 34:29, 35. With [three raptures ]{.class_s4N4-0}indicated in chapter 34 (vs. 22), []{#c44Y.xhtml_page_801}there is not much sense in going on with "advanced revelation," at least in the book of Exodus, for the word [Exodus]{.class_s4N4-0} indicates the "going out" of the Church from this world, as we remarked in our introduction.

Chapters 35--40, then, are largely [review matters. ]{.class_s4N4-0}They deal with the carrying out of the instructions given to Moses before his advanced revelation and before Aaron's "lovein."

["Ye shall kindle no fire\...upon the sabbath"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 3). This passage---with the one in Numbers 15:32---makes a shambles of the Seventh-day Adventist cult. Various ingenuous alibis have been worked out by the cult to explain it, but as we remarked under 20:8--10, all of them have the sound of somebody who doesn't have any idea about what he is talking. [Capital punishment]{.class_s4N4-0} is to be meted out to the "Seventh-day Adventist" who lights his stove on Saturday or who make [any effort]{.class_s4N4-0} to light it (see Num. 15:32--33). Changing the wood stove to a gas range doesn't alter the seriousness of the offence, and the appeal to Mark 2:27, in the face of Ellen G. White's profession, is just a little too much for an honest man to stomach.

["Whosoever is of a willing heart"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 5). This freewill offering is mentioned many times in the Bible (vss. 21--22, 25--26, 29; Psa. 54:6; Ezra 7:13; Lev. 22:18, 21, 23, 23:38; Num. 15:3; Deut. 12:6, 17; Ezra 1:4, 3:5, 8:28, etc.). With all due respect to Calvin's limited knowledge of the Bible and the work which he did in his day, the "free will" of man stands out so clearly in these passages that any attempt to make Total Depravity extend to AN ACT OF THE WILL becomes a non-scriptural operation.

["And every wise hearted among you shall come"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 10), plainly indicating that the Lord's work---and the Tabernacle is the Lord's work in this book---requires a certain heart attitude. [Intellectuality]{.class_s4N4-0}, [talent]{.class_s4N4-0}, [gifted]{.class_s4N4-0}, [witty]{.class_s4N4-0}, and [knowledgeable]{.class_s4N4-0} are not the words used to describe the Lord's workers; they are to be ["wise hearted"]{.class_s4N2-0} (cf. Eph. 1:19, 5:10, 17).

We have already commented abundantly on the materials found in verses 5--19 under chapters 25--31, which see. ["The pins"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 18) are mentioned here for the first time and would refer to the tent pegs to which cords would be attached in strengthening the Tabernacle.

35:20 "And all the congregation of the children of Israel departed from the presence of Moses.

21 And they came, every one whose heart stirred him up, and every one whom his spirit made willing, and they brought the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}'S offering to the work of the tabernacle of the congregation, and for all his service, and for the holy garments.

22 And they came, both men and women, as many as were willing hearted, and brought bracelets, and earrings, and rings, and tablets, all jewels of gold: and every man that offered offered an offering of gold unto the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}.

23 And every man, with whom was found blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine linen, and goats' hair, and red skins of rams, and badgers' skins, brought them.

24 Every one that did offer an offering of silver and brass brought the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}'S offering: and every man, with whom was found shittim wood for any work of the service, brought it.

25 And all the women that were wise hearted did spin with their hands, and brought that which they had spun, both of blue, and of purple, and of scarlet, and of fine linen.

26 And all the women whose heart stirred them up in wisdom spun goats' hair.

27 And the rulers brought onyx stones, and stones to be set, for the ephod, and for the breastplate;

28 And spice, and oil for the light, and for the anointing oil, and for the sweet incense.

29 The children of Israel brought a willing offering unto the [Lord]{.class_s4N8-0}, every man and woman, whose heart made them willing to bring for all manner of work, which the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}had commanded to be made by the hand of Moses."

The alteration of verse 22 in the corrupt [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}(and the more corrupt [NASV]{.class_s4N4-0}) is not to be taken seriously. The altering of ["tablets"]{.class_s4N2-0} to ["armlets"]{.class_s4N4-0} means absolutely nothing, since Gesenius,  Kalisch, and Knobel say that it means "necklace." (You see, the enemies of God's word can never agree on [how much]{.class_s4N4-0} should [go]{.class_s4N4-0} and how much should be [left;]{.class_s4N4-0} they only agree that the [AV ]{.class_s4N4-0}is not to be tolerated.)

["Every one whose heart stirred him up"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 21). In the Old Testament, you stir yourself up; notice the same type of thing in ["humble yourselves"]{.class_s4N2-0} (James 4:10). God stirred no one up here and made no one ["willing" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(cf. 1 Chron. 28:9). Isolated cases like Ezra 1:1 were used by John Calvin to prove that God always does the "stirring" in anyone, but the simplest mind can tell that Ezra 1:1 is a case of God acting on a ruler to fulfill []{#c44Y.xhtml_page_804}a Scriptural destiny which had been prophesied (Isa. 44:28). This is an entirely different thing from our text.

In our text, God supplies the MATERIAL (12:35--36), gives the knowledge of workmanship (vs. 31), and the ability (vs. 32) with the ["wisdom and understanding"]{.class_s4N2-0} (36:1); but there is not one word about God WILLING for anyone or [deciding ]{.class_s4N4-0}for anyone what to do with the materials, knowledge, ability, wisdom, or understanding. To force this Calvinistic meaning on the passage, the ridiculous Calvinists of our day are running around quoting Philippians 2:13. If Philippians 2:13 means that God's will is ALWAYS done in us---with God "willing" every move we make---then God certainly is responsible directly for the "sins of the saints." You will observe that God gave none a ["wise heart"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 10) [until they stirred themselves up]{.class_s4N4-0} (vs. 21).

Several times the expression ["men and women"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 22, 29) occurs to illustrate the great truth of Acts 1:14, 8:3, where women share in the labor and work of the ministry. ["The rulers"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 27) have the precious stones which go into the work of the breastplate and the ephod, and the reader should observe the word order given in verses 8--9 in regards to these ["onyx stones."]{.class_s4N2-0} The materials are so listed here that we have ["light"]{.class_s4N4-0} first (OIL in vs. 8), then the [rewards ]{.class_s4N4-0}(precious stones, vs. 9), and [guidance ]{.class_s4N4-0}(vs. 8). One must never forget that the Breastplate, fastened to the high priest's shoulders by the onyx stones (28:9--12), was to "inquire at" for [guidance]{.class_s4N4-0} (see comments under "Urim and Thummim," 28:30).

35:30 "And Moses said unto the children of Israel, See, the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}hath called by name Bezaleel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah;

31 And he hath filled him with the spirit of God, in wisdom, in understanding, and in knowledge, and in all manner of workmanship;

32 And to devise curious works, to work in gold, and in silver, and in brass,

33 And in the cutting of stones, to set them, and in carving of wood, to make any manner of cunning work.

34 And he hath put in his heart that he may teach, both he, and Aholiab, the son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan.

35 Them hath he filled with wisdom of heart, to work all manner of work, of the engraver, and of the cunning workman, and of the embroiderer, in blue, and in purple, in scarlet and in fine linen, and of the weaver, even of them that do any work, and of those that devise cunning work."

We have commented on these two workmen before under 31:1--6, which see. They are ["filled\...with the spirit of God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 31)---not "anointed." The first man (Bezaleel) is an artificer and engraver in metals, and the second (Aholiab) is a weaver and an embroiderer. Both men are able to ["teach"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 34) the men who work under them in these two branches of the arts.

[]{#c46J.xhtml}

# [Chapter 36]{.class_s4NN} {.heading_s2E2-0}

36:1 "Then wrought Bezaleel and Aholiab, and every wise hearted man, in whom the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}put wisdom and understanding to know how to work all manner of work for the service of the sanctuary, according to all that the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}had commanded.

2 And Moses called Bezaleel and Aholiab, and every wise hearted man, in whose heart the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}had put wisdom, even every one whose heart stirred him up to come unto the work to do it:

3 And they received of Moses all the offering, which the children of Israel had brought for the work of the service of the sanctuary, to make it withal. And they brought yet unto him free offerings every morning.

4 And all the wise men, that wrought all the work of the sanctuary, came every man from his work which they made;

5 And they spake unto Moses, saying, The people bring much more than enough for the service of the work, which the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}commanded to make.

6 And Moses gave commandment, and they caused it to be proclaimed throughout the camp, saying, Let neither man nor woman make any more work for the offering of the sanctuary. So the people were restrained from bringing.

7 For the stuff they had was sufficient for all the work to make it, and too much."

From here on, the [Pulpit Commentary,]{.class_s4N4-0} with the two standard corruptions of the Twentieth Century's worst corruptions, the [American Standard Version]{.class_s4N4-0} of 1901 (Dr. Green and Dr. Schaff) and the [New American Standard []{#c46J.xhtml_page_807}"Bible"]{.class_s4N4-0} of 1963 (the Lockman Foundation), suggests about two changes per verse. For example:

1\. ["For the sides"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 27, 32) should be[ "for the BACK."]{.class_s4N4-0}

2\. ["Equally distant"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 22) should be ["set in order one against."]{.class_s4N4-0}

3\. ["Middle bar to shoot through the boards"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 33) should be ["to reach from end to end of the boards."]{.class_s4N4-0}

4\. ["The tabernacle door"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 37--38) should have been ["the door of the tent."]{.class_s4N4-0}

5\. ["Their chapiters and their fillets"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 38) should have been ["capitals and rods."]{.class_s4N4-0}

6\. ["Snuffers"]{.class_s4N2-0} (37:23) should have been ["tongs."]{.class_s4N4-0}

7\. ["Their fillets"]{.class_s4N2-0} (38:10--12, 17, 19, etc.) should have been ["their connecting rods."]{.class_s4N4-0}

8\. ["Coats"]{.class_s4N2-0} (39:27) should have been ["tunics."]{.class_s4N4-0}

9\. ["The hanging for the door"]{.class_s4N2-0} (35:15, 17), should have been ["the SCREEN for the GATE" (!).]{.class_s4N4-0}

10\. ["Cunning work"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 8, 35) should have been ["skillful workmanship."]{.class_s4N4-0}

11\. The ["taches"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 13, 18) should have been ["clasps."]{.class_s4N4-0}

12\. ["The loops held one curtain to another"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 12) should have been ["the loops were opposite ONE TO ANOTHER." (!)]{.class_s4N4-0}

Now it is a fact that both editions of the [ASV]{.class_s4N4-0}, recommended by Bob Jones University and Hyles-Anderson College (before Hyles "got right" on the [KJV]{.class_s4N4-0}), depart from the Received Text 30,000 times (8,000 in the New Testament alone), so we have this twiddle-twaddle to put up with, and it goes on throughout every edition of the [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}ever published. It only spells one thing []{#c46J.xhtml_page_808}to the man of God (or the woman of God) who intends to be faithful to the revelation of God: [deliberate rejection of the authority of the Holy Scriptures and the power of the Holy Ghost.]{.class_s4N4-0}

Nothing is gained by most of the changes, while a great deal is often lost. Not a single one of them was necessary, and where any real problem arose (like ["wot"]{.class_s4N2-0} and ["wist"]{.class_s4N2-0} and ["taches"]{.class_s4N2-0}), the definitions could have easily been printed in the MARGIN of the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}editions of the text.

The Bible believer may as well settle it now, and settle it once and for all before he is raptured out of this solar system, that he is dealing with [deliberate ]{.class_s4N4-0}and [intentional ]{.class_s4N4-0}rejection of the Holy Bible by men who profess to believe in "verbal, plenary, God-breathed something or others" that no one has ever seen or had their hands on. The Seminary faculties of the modern Christian schools have [no authority]{.class_s4N4-0} but their own noodles (and there are not many things any more nauseating than a lukewarm [wet noodle!]{.class_s4N4-0}). When these wet noodles speak from "THE Greek text" (see any publication by Bob Jones University written by Dr. Custer or Dr. Neal), they speak with the same authority with which Angela Davis or Adolf Hitler spoke. [They are their OWN authority,]{.class_s4N4-0} and their authority proceeds from [themselves ]{.class_s4N4-0}as they sit in judgment on the word of God.

We have commented on the matters of "free will" (vs. 3) previously (see comments on 35:22). God counts a man to be wise (vs. 4) when he is ["wise hearted"]{.class_s4N2-0} (35:25). The wise heart is the "willing heart" (35:22), and a willing heart will ["stir a man up"]{.class_s4N4-0} (35:21) to do something with the ["wisdom and []{#c46J.xhtml_page_809}understanding"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 1) that God has already deposited within him.

The reader should not surmise for a moment that simply because God deposits ["understanding to know how to work" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 1) in a man, that the man will automatically "rise to the occasion." Bing Crosby and Mario Lanza had a great deal deposited within them by their Creator, yet there is no record in a lifetime that either of them glorified the Word of God or the words of God, even in their "religious" musical moments. Norman Rockwell and Paul Detlefsen had enough artistic talent invested in them by their Creator to illustrate the life and work of every man that God ever used in church history. There is no evidence in a lifetime that either of them used their talents for anything the Bible mentions---directly or indirectly---where the passages deal with the glorification of the Lord Jesus Christ. Brahms and Wagner had musical ["wisdom and understanding"]{.class_s4N2-0} like few men ever had, but there is not a sign in their symphonies or operas that either man was interested in glorifying anything but [pagan beauty]{.class_s4N4-0} or his [own subconsciousness]{.class_s4N4-0} or the glory of his [nationality. ]{.class_s4N4-0}Anything "religious" Beethoven turned out ran a poor "fourth" to any one of his nine symphonies. With the powers of oratory given him (accompanied by his psychological insights), Adolf Hitler could have started a Reformation in Europe that would have made Luther's Reformation look like "child evangelism," but Hitler's oratorical powers and his psychological genius were dedicated to National Socialism and the overthrowing of Germany's enemies.

Unless the heart is willing to do [God's]{.class_s4N4-0} will (John 7:17), the ["wisdom and understanding"]{.class_s4N2-0} amount to less than nothing. They are negative, and they amount to []{#c46J.xhtml_page_810}a debit and a deficit which will bankrupt the owner in due time.

["The people bring much more than enough"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 5). Is it not "highly instructive" (commentator's cliché) to note that the people gave better here with no instructions on "tithing" (Mal. 3:10)? Isn't it strange how much New Testament doctrine is floating around Mt. Sinai [after ]{.class_s4N4-0}the golden calf and the breaking of the Law? If ["God loveth a cheerful giver"]{.class_s4N2-0} (2 Cor. 9:7), He certainly reversed His feelings toward Israel 24 hours after Moses began to take up the collection. The people gave according to 2 Corinthians 9:6--7, and ["the stuff they had was sufficient for all the work to make it, and too much'']{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 7).

[Grace always goes beyond the commandment. ]{.class_s4N4-0}The people had seen the veiled glory of God (chap. 34) when Moses came down from the mount. They knew Moses had been in the "presence" without dying (33:20), and this grace had produced a conviction that miraculous supplies of water and bread had failed to bring forth.

(For a real redundant excursion into Schmaltzville, every Bible believer should read the comments of Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, and Clarke on the passage. This illustrates very vividly the "style" of the traditional commentary and explains why a preacher or teacher can read so long in a commentary without learning ANYTHING. )

36:8 "And every wise hearted man among them that wrought the work of the tabernacle made ten curtains of fine twined linen, and blue, and purple, and scarlet: with cherubims of cunning work made he them.

9 The length of one curtain was twenty and eight cubits, and the breadth of one curtain four cubits: the curtains were all of one size.

10 And he coupled the five curtains one unto another: and the other five curtains he coupled one unto another.

11 And he made loops of blue on the edge of one curtain from the selvedge in the coupling: likewise he made in the uttermost side of another curtain, in the coupling of the second.

12 Fifty loops made he in one curtain, and fifty loops made he in the edge of the curtain which was in the coupling of the second: the loops held one curtain to another.

13 And he made fifty taches of gold, and coupled the curtains one unto another with the taches: so it became one tabernacle.

14 And he made curtains of goats' hair for the tent over the tabernacle: eleven curtains he made them.

15 The length of one curtain was thirty cubits, and four cubits was the breadth of one curtain: the eleven curtains were of one size.

16 And he coupled five curtains by themselves, and six curtains by themselves.

17 And he made fifty loops upon the uttermost edge of the curtain in the coupling, and fifty loops made he upon the edge of the curtain which coupleth the second.

18 And he made fifty taches of brass to couple the tent together, that it might be one.

19 And he made a covering for the tent of rams' skins dyed red, and a covering of badgers' skins above that."

We have commented on each item at length in the comparative passages given earlier (chap. 26).

36:20 "And he made boards for the tabernacle of shittim wood, standing up.

21 The length of a board was ten cubits, and the breadth of a board one cubit and a half.

22 One board had two tenons, equally distant one from another: thus did he make for all the boards of the tabernacle.

23 And he made boards for the tabernacle; twenty boards for the south side southward:

24 And forty sockets of silver he made under the twenty boards; two sockets under one board for his two tenons, and two sockets under another board for his two tenons.

25 And for the other side of the tabernacle, which is toward the north corner, he made twenty boards,

26 And their forty sockets of silver; two sockets under one board, and two sockets under another board.

27 And for the sides of the tabernacle westward he made six boards.

28 And two boards made he for the corners of the tabernacle in the two sides.

29 And they were coupled beneath, and coupled together at the head thereof, to one ring: thus he did to both of them in both the corners.

30 And there were eight boards; and their sockets were sixteen sockets of silver, under every board two sockets.

31 And he made bars of shittim wood; five for the boards of the one side of the tabernacle,

32 And five bars for the boards of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the boards of the tabernacle for the sides westward.

33 And he made the middle bar to shoot through the boards from the one end to the other.

34 And he overlaid the boards with gold, and made their rings of gold to be places for the bars, and overlaid the bars with gold."

We have commented on these verses under chapter 26, which see.

36:35 "And he made a vail of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen: with cherubims made he it of cunning work.

36 And he made thereunto four pillars of shittim wood, and overlaid them with gold: their hooks were of gold; and he cast for them four sockets of silver.

37 And he made an hanging for the tabernacle door of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen, of needlework;

38 And the five pillars of it with their hooks: and he overlaid their chapiters and their fillets with gold: but their five sockets were of brass."

We have commented on these verses at length under 26:31--33. The reader may well ask himself the question: "Is it absolutely necessary for the Holy Spirit to repeat, in detail, what He already has covered in detail in earlier chapters?" The answer to this question is threefold.

1\. In the first place, the Tabernacle is a type of [the universe:]{.class_s4N4-0} see remarks under 25:1--9. What God chose to "skip over" in Genesis chapters 1--2, He now takes time with and discusses the "height, length, breadth, []{#c46J.xhtml_page_814}and depth" (see Eph. 3:18 in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Galatians---Colossians]{.class_s4N4-0}) in detail.

2\. If a thing is gone over twice, it is ["because the thing is established by God"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Gen. 41:32), so there is not one important doctrine or teaching in Scripture that is not repeated in some form or another. The Millennial reign of Christ is mentioned [six times]{.class_s4N4-0} in one chapter (Rev. 20:1--7), and yet, in spite of this triple confirmation by God that ["the thing is established,"]{.class_s4N2-0} the majority of theologians, scientists, and philosophers in the world today reject the Millennial reign of Christ thumbs down, with "no holds barred," and fancy in their blind conceit (Rom. 11:25) that the passage is to be "spiritualized."

3\. The whole force of the instructions which dealt with the Tabernacle was to impress on Moses' mind (and the minds of his people) that when God says a thing it is to be done EXACTLY as God says it is to be done (note Heb. 9:23 and Exod. 25:40). The material given in chapters 36--39 is a record for posterity, given to show future generations of Israelites that Moses was "faithful in all of God's house" (Heb. 3:1--6) as an obedient servant; he turned not to the right hand or the left in doing what God told Him to do in these matters. The ["book of the law"]{.class_s4N2-0} was to be kept by the Kings of Israel and read daily (Deut. 17:18--19). What King (who could read the Old Testament at all) could have failed to notice the emphasis the Holy Spirit laid on the FIDELITY of Moses to the instructions given to him by God (see Joshua 1:1--3, 5, 7, 13--15, 17, 4:10, 12, 14, 8:31--35, 11:12, 15, 20, 23, 13:8, 12, 15, 21, 24, 29, 32--33, etc.)

A [servant ]{.class_s4N4-0}is to be obedient to his master (Eph. 6:5; 1 Tim. 6:1--4) and carry out the letter of the commandment to the last "jot and tittle." Chapters 36--39 prove that []{#c46J.xhtml_page_815}Moses---a type of the [Servant of Jehovah]{.class_s4N4-0} (!) (Isa. 52:13)---didn't muff the ball when it came to [carrying out orders]{.class_s4N4-0} in regards to the Tabernacle.

[]{#c48V.xhtml}

# [Chapter 37]{.class_s4NN} {.heading_s2E2-0}

37:1 "And Bezaleel made the ark of shittim wood: two cubits and a half was the length of it, and a cubit and a half the breadth of it, and a cubit and a half the height of it:

2 And he overlaid it with pure gold within and without, and made a crown of gold to it round about.

3 And he cast for it four rings of gold, to be set by the four corners of it; even two rings upon the one side of it, and two rings upon the other side of it.

4 And he made staves of shittim wood, and overlaid them with gold.

5 And he put the staves into the rings by the sides of the ark, to bear the ark.

6 And he made the mercy seat of pure gold: two cubits and a half was the length thereof, and one cubit and a half the breadth thereof.

7 And he made two cherubims of gold, beaten out of one piece made he them, on the two ends of the mercy seat;

8 One cherub on the end on this side, and another cherub on the other end on that side: out of the mercy seat made he the cherubims on the two ends thereof.

9 And the cherubims spread out their wings on high, and covered with their wings over the mercy seat, with their faces one to another; even to the mercy seatward were the faces of the cherubims."

We have commented on the articles mentioned here under 25:10--20.

Bezaleel---one of the few "Bs" in Scripture connected with righteousness---has a great deal to do with the building of the furniture in the Tabernacle. One will []{#c48V.xhtml_page_817}notice the occurrence of the third person singular (["he"]{.class_s4N2-0}) in verses 2--7, 10--13, 15--17, 23, etc. Bezaleel means "God is protection" or "God is a covering shadow." Again, the reader should notice that when the Lord starts the construction of the furniture within the Tabernacle, the ARK is the first thing that shows up; salvation begins with God and proceeds outward to the sinner (see 25:10 and comments).

37:10 "And he made the table of shittim wood: two cubits was the length thereof, and a cubit the breadth thereof, and a cubit and a half the height thereof:

11 And he overlaid it with pure gold, and made thereunto a crown of gold round about.

12 Also he made thereunto a border of an handbreadth round about; and made a crown of gold for the border thereof round about.

13 And he cast for it four rings of gold, and put the rings upon the four corners that were in the four feet thereof.

14 Over against the border were the rings, the places for the staves to bear the table.

15 And he made the staves of shittim wood, and overlaid them with gold, to bear the table.

16 And he made the vessels which were upon the table, his dishes, and his spoons, and his bowls, and his covers to cover withal, of pure gold."

We have commented on this table under 25:23--29, which see.

37:17 "And he made the candlestick of pure gold: of beaten work made he the candlestick; his shaft, and his branch, his bowls, his knops, and his flowers, were of the same:

18 And six branches going out of the sides thereof; three branches of the candlestick out of the one side thereof, and three branches of the candlestick out of the other side thereof:

19 Three bowls made after the fashion of almonds in one branch, a knop and a flower; and three bowls made like almonds in another branch, a knop and a flower: so throughout the six branches going out of the candlestick.

20 And in the candlestick were four bowls made like almonds, his knops, and his flowers:

21 And a knop under two branches of the same, and a knop under two branches of the same, and a knop under two branches of the same, according to the six branches going out of it.

22 Their knops and their branches were of the same: all of it was one beaten work of pure gold.

23 And he made his seven lamps, and his snuffers, and his snuffdishes, of pure gold.

24 Of a talent of pure gold made he it, and all the vessels thereof."

We have commented on the Candlestick abundantly under 25:31--39. The amount of gold that goes into the building of these objects has long been debated. Clarke (Commentary, pp. 486--488) goes into considerable detail in trying to arrive at an accurate figure. The long and short of it is the gold in the Tabernacle came to over 4,000 pounds, and the combined weight of brass, gold, and silver came to over 20,000 pounds. That is, when the Holy Spirit said that the Jews ["spoiled the Egyptians"]{.class_s4N2-0} (12:36), He wasn't speaking "evangelistically." They couldn't have been spoiled much []{#c48V.xhtml_page_819}worse if Attila the Hun had passed through, with Baibars in charge of "requisitioning."

37:25 "And he made the incense altar of shittim wood: the length of it was a cubit, and the breadth of it a cubit; it was foursquare; and two cubits was the height of it; the horns thereof were of the same.

26 And he overlaid it with pure gold, both the top of it, and the sides thereof round about, and the horns of it: also he made unto it a crown of gold round about.

27 And he made two rings of gold for it under the crown thereof, by the two corners of it, upon the two sides thereof, to be places for the staves to bear it withal.

28 And he made the staves of shittim wood, and overlaid them with gold.

29 And he made the holy anointing oil, and the pure incense of sweet spices, according to the work of the apothecary."

We have commented on the Incense Altar under 30:1--6, which see. The [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_s4N4-0}happily informs us that "this time" the Incense Altar "occurs in its [right ]{.class_s4N4-0}place\...not as in chapter XXX, 1--6, out of place."

Aren't we lucky to have "competent" Conservative scholars with us who can straighten out God's Book after He has put things in the "wrong place"? Isn't it too bad that some Conservative scholar has not yet arisen to put Romans chapter 10 in the "[right ]{.class_s4N4-0}place" and Acts chapter 8 in the "[right ]{.class_s4N4-0}place" and Revelation chapter 11 in the "[right ]{.class_s4N4-0}place"? How about Mark first, then 1 Thessalonians, then 1 & 2 Corinthians, then Acts, then Matthew, then Hebrews, then Revelation, then John, and then Philemon? Better still, why not place John 1:1--[]{#c48V.xhtml_page_820}9 before Matthew 1:1, and then place Luke 3:23--38 between them, and then place 1 Chronicles chapters 3--4 between Matthew 1:2 and Matthew 1:16?

The reader should review the remarks made under 30:1--6 on the Altar of Incense and reestablish himself in the "present truth" that where the [King James ]{.class_s4N4-0}Bible states a thing to be so, its authority easily outweighs the opinion of any 500,000 Conservative "scholars" who resent it. The fact that 500,000 saved sinners may agree on the correcting of the [Authorized ]{.class_s4N4-0}text is really immaterial. "Saved scholars," these days, have had nineteen centuries of corruption to assimilate, and they have been riding Hebrew and Greek "hobbyhorses" for so long in their fanatical efforts to overthrow the Reformation text, that we should never consider their opinions seriously---[charitably,]{.class_s4N4-0} yes; but [seriously]{.class_s4N4-0}, NO! (See [Satan's Masterpiece, The New ASV,]{.class_s4N4-0} 1972.)

The many corrections on this chapter, offered by the "majority of Conservative junk dealers," only adds to the accumulated pile of claptrap for which such gentlemen are noted. Bantzch, Keil, Bullinger, Delitzsch, Gesenius, Schaff, Dillmann, Rawlinson, Clarke, Sanday, Lange, Ellicott, and the [New Bible Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} all have little "nuggets" they would like to give us in exchange for the infallible authority of the preserved text, but to trade with them at such a bargain counter would be quite similar to swapping a brand new Porsche for a used Edsel.

1\. ["Tongs"]{.class_s4N2-0} (25:38) should have been ["snuffers."]{.class_s4N4-0}

2\. ["Beaten out of one piece"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 7) should have been ["of beaten work."]{.class_s4N4-0}

3\. ["Places"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 14, 27) should have been ["receptacles."]{.class_s4N4-0}

4\. ["Spoons"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 16) should have been ["pans."]{.class_s4N4-0}

5\. ["Covers"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 16) should have been ["bowls."]{.class_s4N4-0}

6\. ["Sides"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 26--27) should have been "[walls."]{.class_s4N4-0}

7\. ["Top"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 26) should have been ["roof."]{.class_s4N4-0}

8\. ["Crown"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vss. 2, 11--12, 26--27) should have been "moulding."

9\. ["Shaft" ]{.class_s4N2-0}(vs. 17) should have been "stem."

10\. ["Spices"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 29) should have been "scents" (!).

And I suppose if the Holy Spirit were not still in the world today restraining ["the mystery of iniquity"]{.class_s4N2-0} (2 Thess. 2:7), these conceited asses would probably have gone right on with:

1\. ["Christ"]{.class_s4N2-0} should have been ["St. Christopher."]{.class_s4N4-0}

2\. ["God"]{.class_s4N2-0} should have been ["Baal."]{.class_s4N4-0}

3\. The ["resurrection"]{.class_s4N2-0} should have been ["the resuscitation."]{.class_s4N4-0}

4\. The ascension should have been ["the blast off."]{.class_s4N4-0}

5\. The "[devil"]{.class_s4N2-0} should have been ["Senator Goldwater."]{.class_s4N4-0}

6\. The "[Red sea"]{.class_s4N2-0} should have been ["the Bay of Pigs."]{.class_s4N4-0}

7\. ["Paul"]{.class_s4N2-0} should have been ["Dr. A. T. Robertson."]{.class_s4N4-0}

8\. And ["born again"]{.class_s4N2-0} should have been ["intellectually refined."]{.class_s4N4-0}

All in all, the destructive critics of the Holy Bible (Conservatives foremost) have made 30,000 alterations in the two Testaments in the last fifty years. For a record of this inane drivel (in its worst state), see the [New American Standard "Bible"]{.class_s4N4-0} (recommended by leading "Fundamentalists"), which is no more a "Bible" than the [Shooter's Bible]{.class_s4N4-0} or the [Fisherman's Bible]{.class_s4N4-0} or [The Act of Supremacy ]{.class_s4N4-0}(A.D. 1534).

[]{#c4AU.xhtml}

# [Chapter 38]{.class_s4NN} {.heading_s2E2-0}

38:1 "And he made the altar of burnt offering of shittim wood: five cubits was the length thereof, and five cubits the breadth thereof; it was foursquare; and three cubits the height thereof.

2 And he made the horns thereof on the four corners of it; the horns thereof were of the same: and he overlaid it with brass.

3 And he made all the vessels of the altar, the pots, and the shovels, and the basons, and the fleshhooks, and the firepans: all the vessels thereof made he of brass.

4 And he made for the altar a brasen grate of network under the compass thereof beneath unto the midst of it.

5 And he cast four rings for the four ends of the grate of brass, to be places for the staves.

6 And he made the staves of shittim wood, and overlaid them with brass.

7 And he put the staves into the rings on the sides of the altar, to bear it withal; he made the altar hollow with boards.

8 And he made the laver of brass, and the foot of it of brass, of the lookingglasses of the women assembling, which assembled at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation."

We have commented on the Altar and the Laver under 27:1--8 and 30:18, which see. We mentioned the women's ["lookingglasses"]{.class_s4N2-0} at that time.

38:9 "And he made the court: on the south side southward the hangings of the court were of fine twined linen, an hundred cubits:

10 Their pillars were twenty, and their brasen sockets twenty; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets were of silver.

11 And for the north side the hangings were an hundred cubits, their pillars were twenty, and their sockets of brass twenty; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets of silver.

12 And for the west side were hangings of fifty cubits, their pillars ten, and their sockets ten; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets of silver.

13 And for the east side eastward fifty cubits.

14 The hangings of the one side of the gate were fifteen cubits; their pillars three, and their sockets three.

15 And for the other side of the court gate, on this hand and that hand, were hangings of fifteen cubits; their pillars three, and their sockets three.

16 All the hangings of the court round about were of fine twined linen.

17 And the sockets for the pillars were of brass; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets of silver; and the overlaying of their chapiters of silver; and all the pillars of the court were filleted with silver.

18 And the hanging for the gate of the court was needlework, of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen: and twenty cubits was the length, and the height in the breadth was five cubits, answerable to the hangings of the court.

19 And their pillars were four, and their sockets of brass four; their hooks of silver, and the overlaying of their chapiters and their fillets of silver.

20 And all the pins of the tabernacle, and of the court round about, were of brass."

We have commented on these matters under 27:9--19.

38:21 "This is the sum of the tabernacle, even of the tabernacle of testimony, as it was counted, according to the commandment of Moses, for the service of the Levites, by the hand of Ithamar, son to Aaron the priest.

22 And Bezaleel, the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah, made all that the [Lord ]{.class_s4N8-0}commanded Moses.

23 And with him was Aholiab, son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan, an engraver, and a cunning workman, and an embroiderer in blue, and in purple, and in scarlet, and fine linen.

24 All the gold that was occupied for the work in all the work of the holy place, even the gold of the offering, was twenty and nine talents, and seven hundred and thirty shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary.

25 And the silver of them that were numbered of the congregation was an hundred talents, and a thousand seven hundred and threescore and fifteen shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary:

26 A bekah for every man, that is, half a shekel, after the shekel of the sanctuary, for every one that went to be numbered, from twenty years old and upward, for six hundred thousand and three thousand and five hundred and fifty men.

27 And of the hundred talents of silver were cast the sockets of the sanctuary, and the sockets of the vail; an hundred sockets of the hundred talents, a talent for a socket.

28 And of the thousand seven hundred seventy and five shekels he made hooks for the pillars, and overlaid their chapiters, and filleted them.

29 And the brass of the offering was seventy talents, and two thousand and four hundred shekels.

30 And therewith he made the sockets to the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, and the brasen altar, and the brasen grate for it, and all the vessels of the altar,

31 And the sockets of the court round about, and the sockets of the court gate, and all the pins of the tabernacle, and all the pins of the court round about."

The Levites now appear for the first time as servicing the Tabernacle (Num. 3--4 goes into the matter in great detail). The Levites replace the ["firstborn"]{.class_s4N2-0} mentioned under 13:13. Ithamar (vs. 21) is in charge of "counting" the contributions to the Tabernacle and seeing to it that the work was carried out according to the pattern. He was the youngest son of Aaron (see 6:23, 28:1).

We have mentioned the money matters before. According to the estimate of Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, the gold worth comes to \$450,000, and the silver worth comes to \$301,775. However. ten tons of precious metal (see comments under 37:17--24) comes to far more than \$700,000. The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} suggests 200,000 pounds (English equivalent) for the gold and 40,000 pounds for the silver. The reader may waste several hours tracking the ["bekah"]{.class_s4N2-0} and the []{#c4AU.xhtml_page_826 .class-1}["shekel"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 26) and the ["talent"]{.class_s4N2-0} (vs. 27) through various experts who claim to know the "drams," "sterling money," drachmas, Troy weight, and[ "avoirdupois."]{.class_s4N4-0} The ridiculous [ASV ]{.class_s4N4-0}of 1901 (likened to "Joseph" by the Lockman Foundation, 1963) has written ["beka"]{.class_s4N4-0} instead of ["bekah,"]{.class_s4N2-0} to make the reading CLEARER. (I am sure that is a great help to the student.)

The most Scriptural thing we can say about the passage (that is clear) is that it was a "mess of money and materials." But after all, there were 2,000,000 people there from which to "lift an offering," and there were 603,550 grown men present to offer money (vs. 26).

This last statistic gives the serious student of the Scripture a remarkable opportunity to study (again) the mental processes of "Conservative scholars" who deride and despise the [King James ]{.class_s4N4-0}Bible[. ]{.class_s4N4-0}You see, the figure given in verse 26 matches the figure given in Numbers 1:46. When figures or words MATCH in the corrupt [LXX]{.class_s4N4-0}---supposedly written in 250 B.C.---and the Greek New Testament, it is taken for granted BY EVERY MAJOR CONSERVATIVE SCHOLAR, of any persuasion, that the New Testament writer is [copying ]{.class_s4N4-0}or [quoting ]{.class_s4N4-0}from the Old Testament in Greek. This rule holds for every major "Conservative" scholar from Origen to the Lockman Foundation, bar none.

Now what do you suppose happens to the MENTAL PROCESSES of these men when they find a number in Exodus that matches one in Numbers? If these men were rational men who thought honestly and consistently, would they not assume that Moses (or someone else) first wrote verse 26 and gave an accurate record of those who ["went to be numbered."]{.class_s4N2-0} Then, nearly eleven months later (see Exod. 19:1; Num. 1:1), Moses (or someone else) []{#c4AU.xhtml_page_827}COPIED this number out to number the mustering of the hosts for going to war?

Now, if this is not the thinking on the matter, one must have to assume immediately that the same men who were numbered to go to war (Num. 1), [after ]{.class_s4N4-0}the events of chapter 38, were the same men who gave the ["bekah for every man"]{.class_s4N2-0} in verse 26. That is, the same men who were ["able to go forth to war"]{.class_s4N2-0} (Num. 1:3) were the men who were numbered in verse 26, [without the addition or]{.class_s4N4-0} [subtraction of ONE man.]{.class_s4N4-0}

One can see at a glance that this last alternative is too much to demand of a Bible-rejecting "Conservative" who believes in "the Fundamentals of the Faith," while abhorring [the Book]{.class_s4N4-0} from which they were [extracted. ]{.class_s4N4-0}So to circumvent this phenomenon, the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s4N4-0} has written: "Perhaps the number was lost in this place and restored, from Numbers 2:32, WITHOUT ITS BEING RECOLLECTED that the LEVITES were not included in that reckoning" ([Pulpit Commentary,]{.class_s4N4-0} pp. 391--392). Again, Dummelow chimes in with: "The computations recorded here were made not exactly at THIS TIME, but after the erection of the tabernacle" (Commentary, pg. 84).

Isn't it amazing that it never occurs to these critics to simply accept the two figures as being correct and matching each other? After all, that is how the critics look at any similarity between the Old Testament "[LXX]{.class_s4N4-0}" and the New Testament Greek where quotations match each other! Isn't it peculiar---if not [fantastic]{.class_s4N4-0}---how irrational the brethren get when faced with a matching of texts which tests their BELIEF in the accuracy of the Biblical record?

I. [THE PULPIT COMMENTARY.]{.class_s4N4-0}

Reverend Rawlinson has made the blind assumption that Moses had a lapse of memory ("without its being recollected") in regards to the tribe of Levi, and that he also was careless with his written paper, so he [lost ]{.class_s4N4-0}some of it ("the number was lost in this place").

a\. How does Moses "forget" that Levi was not numbered when he himself was a [Levite?!]{.class_s4N4-0}

b\. How does Moses "forget" that Levi was not numbered when the Levites were [given ]{.class_s4N4-0}money from the other tribes (Num. 3:51) [instead of CONTRIBUTING?]{.class_s4N4-0}

c\. How does Moses "forget" that Levi was not numbered when the context of the first numbering (vs. 26) specifically states that the tribe of Levi had been separated (vs. 21) [for the work of the Tabernacle?]{.class_s4N4-0}

Is it not much more probable---if not absolutely certain (!)---that Reverend Rawlinson lost his own senses momentarily and forgot to "recollect" that the word of God was to judge HIM, not vice versa?!

II . [DUMMELOW.]{.class_s4N4-0}

Dummelow---referred to by irreverent divinity students as "Dumbelow"---has assumed that the numbers in verse 26 occur eight to eleven months after the Tabernacle is finished. If it was finished, why is it that the money (["bekah"]{.class_s4N2-0} in vs. 26) given is listed as COMPOSING the 100 talents and 1,775 shekels of silver (vs. 25) that [made ]{.class_s4N4-0}the silver sockets and silver hooks for the Tabernacle (vss. 27--28)? Observe how the total amount of verse 25 is broken down in verses 27 and 28 into the [exact items. ]{.class_s4N4-0}How then does one suppose that verse 26---between the two accounts (!)---is a reference to something that happened [after the sockets and hooks were made?]{.class_s4N4-0}
